AO-01 — Premium Clinical Skincare Brand — Product-Shot Finishing, Logo Vectorization, Per-SKU Label Merge & Print-Ready Packaging Export ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Take our raw skincare hero shot, flat logo mark, texture plate and SKU copy sheet and produce a clinically clean white-background product image set, an editable vector logo, a recommended type system, a packaging-safe wide landscape reframe of the hero (cropped from the pure-white-sweep master), a clinical monochrome variant, per-SKU print labels merged from our label template, and a print-ready packaging PDF — all assembled on one presentation board.

💰 ~$138 est.Upwork18 tool calls17 distinct toolslong-horizon
Full client brief
We're launching DERMAVERA, a premium clinically-tested skincare line, and need a cohesive, credible visual identity executed across the product line (3 launch SKUs: a Vitamin-C Serum, a Barrier Repair Cream, and a Gentle Gel Cleanser). The look must be clean, minimalist, modern, science-driven and trustworthy — premium without being flashy, professional without looking pharmaceutical. We are supplying: (1) one raw hero photograph of the Serum bottle shot on a near-white sweep (slightly tilted, mixed lighting, cluttered edges); (2) a flat high-res raster of our chosen logo mark (a minimal molecule/droplet monogram + "DERMAVERA" wordmark) that we need delivered as a clean editable vector; (3) a soft lifestyle texture plate (matte stone surface) for collateral grounding; (4) a label-copy spreadsheet with one row per SKU (name, claim, volume, INCI line, batch placeholder, hex accent). We also supply our pre-authored print templates: an Illustrator label template (label.ai) with genuine Variables, and an InDesign unit-carton template (carton.indd). Deliverables: a flawless clinical white-background hero image (straightened, background swept to pure white, neutral clinical color, crisp product), a 1:1 e-commerce crop and a packaging-safe wide landscape reframe of the hero cropped to the target 5000×2800px (the pure-white-sweep master crop-and-resized to the wider ratio, centered on the product — sweep trimmed top/bottom, no canvas added), a clinical monochrome (single-accent duotone) brand variant of the hero, the logo delivered as an editable SVG, a recommended display+text type pairing, a print-ready per-SKU label set generated by merging our copy sheet into label.ai, a print-ready packaging PDF exported from carton.indd, and a single Firefly presentation board collecting the system for sign-off. Everything must be print-ready and leave us editable source (vector SVG + the authored .ai/.indd). Maintain consistent neutral, trustworthy color across every image.
Deliverables (9)
  • dermavera_hero_whitebg_clinical.png — Finished clinical hero: auto-straightened, background swept to pure #FFFFFF, neutral-graded (auto-tone + neutralized color temp + lifted highlights), crisp product. Full-res ~4000x5000px PNG, sRGB, e-
  • dermavera_hero_1x1_ecom.png — 1:1 square e-commerce crop of the finished hero, product centered with clinical whitespace, 2048x2048px PNG on pure white.
  • dermavera_hero_packaging_wide.png — Packaging-safe wide landscape reframe of the finished hero: the pure-white-sweep hero is crop-and-resized to a 5000x2800px landscape ratio centered on the product (portrait frame trimmed top/bottom to
  • dermavera_hero_mono_variant.png — Clinical monochrome brand variant of the finished hero — single deep slate-teal (#16403B) duotone/monochromatic tint, for collateral. Same resolution as hero, PNG.
  • dermavera_logo.svg — Editable vector SVG of the supplied logo mark, clean single-color paths, transparent background — the editable source the brief demands.
  • dermavera_type_system.json — Recommended display + text typeface pairing (PostScript names + rationale) from font_recommend, to anchor the visual-identity typography spec.
  • dermavera_labels_merged.pdf — Print-ready per-SKU label set: 3 label artboards (one per SKU row) produced by merging the copy CSV into label.ai. CMYK, 3mm bleed, crop marks. Vector + editable source = label.ai retained.
  • dermavera_carton_print.pdf — Print-ready unit-carton PDF exported from carton.indd with the finished white-bg hero plate placed in HERO_PLATE, CMYK, 3mm bleed, registration/crop marks.
  • dermavera_brand_board_deeplink — Firefly Board deep-link assembling the finished hero, 1:1 crop, wide canvas, mono variant, vector logo and label proofs into one sign-off presentation board.
Workflow — 18 tool steps
  1. asset_initialize_file_upload — Begin upload of the client-supplied raw hero, logo raster, stone texture plate and the SKU copy CSV into CC storage. Initial asset-load step (no prior output to
  2. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the multipart upload; returns asset URNs/presigned refs for dermavera_serum_hero_raw.png, dermavera_logo_mark_flat.png, dermavera_stone_texture_plate.j
  3. image_auto_straighten — Level the ~4-degree tilt in the raw serum hero so the bottle stands true for clinical ecom.
  4. image_remove_background — Strip the off-white/cast sweep + cluttered edges + lint/dust → transparent PNG isolating the bottle.
  5. image_select_subject — Select the product subject to build a mask for the inverse (the background field).
  6. image_invert_selection — Invert subject mask → background-only selection for a pure-white sweep fill.
  7. image_fill_area — Solid-fill the inverted background selection with pure white #FFFFFF (solid-color fill only) → clean clinical white-bg sweep behind the product.
  8. image_apply_auto_tone — Baseline neutral tonal balance on the white-bg hero.
  9. image_adjust_color_temperature — Neutralize the residual warm/cool cast for trustworthy clinical color consistency across the line.
  10. image_adjust_highlights — Lift/clean highlights so the white sweep reads as paper-white and glass stays crisp → FINAL clinical hero.
  11. image_crop_and_resize — Produce the 1:1 2048x2048 e-commerce crop with centered product + whitespace.
  12. image_crop_and_resize — Reframe the finished clinical hero to the packaging-safe wide 5000x2800px landscape target via an honest crop-and-resize centered on the product — the portrait
  13. image_apply_monochromatic_tint — Apply the single deep slate-teal #16403B monochromatic tint → clinical mono brand variant for collateral.
  14. image_vectorize — Trace the flat logo raster → clean editable single-color SVG (the editable-source deliverable).
  15. font_recommend — Recommend the display + text typeface pairing for the clinical/premium identity (informed by the vectorized wordmark style); anchors the typography spec.
  16. document_merge_data_vector — [T] Merge the SKU copy CSV into the authored label.ai (genuine Variables) → 3 print-ready per-SKU label artboards, CMYK + bleed. CSV headers match Variable name
  17. document_render_layout — EXPORT the authored carton.indd (with the finished wide hero plate placed in the named HERO_PLATE frame) → print-ready packaging PDF, CMYK + registration/crop m
  18. create_firefly_board — Assemble all produced asset URNs into one Firefly Board sign-off presentation (presents assets already produced; does not generate art).
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon and wide-mix: 18 connector calls, all server="adobe", 17 DISTINCT Adobe tools (image_crop_and_resize repeats at steps 11 and 12) — well past the ≥10 / ≥6 thresholds — spanning four tool groups (asset upload, the image_* raster/tonal/vector family, document_* merge+export, and create_firefly_board). It exercises a non-trivial masking sub-chain (remove_bg → select_subject → invert → solid fill_area) for a genuine clinical white-bg result, an honest crop-only reframe to the wide packaging ratio (no canvas extension), a [T] authored-template data-merge, and an export-only [C] layout render — forcing the agent to respect the exact capability boundaries (no gen-fill, no compositing, fill_area=solid only, render_layout=export only, merge needs authored Variables). Difficulty also comes from honoring grounding: the from-scratch logo/identity ART and original layout COMPOSITION in the source brief are out of scope, so the agent must correctly re-scope to the production/finishing slice while still delivering print-ready files + editable source as the brief demands. Mostly [C] with a single [T] step, no widget/async stalls — runnable end-to-end in the product.

Data & other (3)

carton.indd.proxy.txt
content
**Template Description: carton.indd**

**File Type:** Adobe InDesign document (.indd)

**Purpose:** Print-ready unit carton dieline template for product packaging.

**Layout:** Single-page flat dieline featuring 5 box panels (front, back, two sides, top).

**Dimensions & Print Specs:**
*   3mm bleed
*   CMYK color space
*   Includes registration and crop marks

**Content & Placeholders:**
*   **Front Panel:**
    *   Named picture frame: `HERO_PLATE` (pre-sized for finished white-background hero image).
    *   Placed vector logo: DERMAVERA.
    *   Live text frame(s): For SKU name.
*   **Back Panel:**
    *   Live text area: For INCI list and product claims.
    *   Reserved rectangular frame: For barcode placement.
*   **Other Panels:** Blank by default for structural integrity or unlisted content.

**Brand Styling:** Integrates the DERMAVERA brand logo.

**Usage:** Designed for direct export via `document_render_layout` to a print-ready PDF; does not support data merge.

Barrier Repair Cream,Ceramide + Niacinamide complex,50 ml e,"Aqua, Glycerin, Niacinamide, Ceramide NP, Squalane, Panthenol",BATCH ####,16403B
Gentle Gel Cleanser,pH-balanced daily cleanse,150 ml e,"Aqua, Coco-Glucoside, Glycerin, Sodium PCA, Allantoin",BATCH ####,16403B
label.ai.proxy.txt
content
**Template Description: label.ai**

This is a hand-authored Adobe Illustrator label template, 70x90mm with an additional 3mm bleed, set in CMYK color mode. The design features a static DERMAVERA vector logo positioned top-centre, a thin hairline rule, and a clean, clinical whitespace layout.

Dynamic content is driven by genuine Illustrator Variables (bound as Dynamic objects, not literal text), ensuring data merge compatibility. These variables include:
*   `{sku_name}` for the main display heading.
*   `{claim}` for a subhead.
*   `{volume}` placed in the lower-right corner.
*   `{inci_line}` as a small-print body block.
*   `{batch}` for footer micro-text.

A brand accent color is controlled by the `{accent_hex}` variable, which drives a specific swatch/text accent. All variable names (`sku_name`, `claim`, `volume`, `inci_line`, `batch`, `accent_hex`) directly correspond to expected CSV headers for `document_merge_data_vector` processing, enabling one label output per data row.

AO-02 — Performance-ready Meta ad product creatives: white-bg clean-up, brand color grade, multi-ratio reframe set, and vectorized logo for paid-social testing ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Turn the client's raw product shot and logo into a performance-ready Meta ad creative kit: a retouched pure-white-bg hero, a brand-graded variant, three platform-ratio frames (1:1 / 4:5 / 9:16) built by honestly cropping/reframing the graded hero to each ratio, a vectorized logo, and a Firefly review board.

💰 ~$165 est.Upwork20 tool calls16 distinct toolslong-horizon
Full client brief
DONE is a DTC homeware brand launching paid-social tests for its matte-ceramic pour-over coffee dripper and needs a freelancer to build the imagery layer of a Meta (Facebook + Instagram) ad-creative kit, faithful to the brand guidelines (brand teal #0F6E64 on warm off-white #F4EFE7). You are supplied three client assets: (1) a raw studio product photo of the dripper on a near-white sweep that still shows seamless-paper shadows and a faint gray cast; (2) the DONE wordmark logo as a flat PNG; and (3) the brand-guideline sheet as a PDF for reference. 

Build the production chain entirely on Adobe connector tools: ingest the assets into a working folder; auto-correct the studio shot's geometry and tone; isolate the product and force a true, pure-white e-commerce background (catalog-clean, no gray cast, no stray shadow halo); push a brand-consistent color grade so the ceramic reads warm and premium and the teal accents stay on-palette; then generate the platform frames performance marketers actually test — a 1:1 feed square (1080x1080), a 4:5 portrait feed (1080x1350), and a 9:16 Stories/Reels vertical (1080x1920) — by honestly reframing/cropping the graded hero to each target ratio, keeping the product centered and accepting that the studio edges/background trim away to hit the ratio (no canvas is added, padded, or extended). Separately, vectorize the supplied wordmark to a clean SVG so it stays crisp at any ad size. Finally, assemble the full variation set into a Firefly board as a single review deep-link the client can sign off on in Slack. 

Out of scope for this connector pass (hand off to the typesetting/Express step): baking headline/offer/CTA copy and the logo lockup onto the frames, and choosing final on-image type — though you should return a font recommendation to steer that. Deliverables: the pure-white hero, the brand-graded hero, the three ratio frames (1:1 / 4:5 / 9:16), the vector logo, the font direction, and the Firefly review board link.
Deliverables (8)
  • done_hero_pure_white.png — Retouched product hero on a true pure-white (#FFFFFF) e-commerce background, product isolated with clean edge, no gray cast / no shadow halo, straightened and auto-toned, ~4000x3000px PNG.
  • done_hero_brand_graded.jpg — Brand-consistent color-graded hero: warm premium ceramic, on-palette teal accents (HSL nudged toward #0F6E64), exposure/contrast/temperature tuned for paid-social punch, high-quality JPEG.
  • done_creative_1x1.jpg — 1:1 feed square (1080x1080): the graded hero honestly reframed/cropped to a centered 1:1 crop of the existing frame (subject centered; the near-white studio edges/background trim to hit the square rat
  • done_creative_4x5.jpg — 4:5 portrait feed (1080x1350): the graded hero honestly reframed/cropped to a centered 4:5 crop of the existing frame (subject centered; edges/background trim to hit the portrait ratio). No canvas add
  • done_creative_9x16.jpg — 9:16 Stories/Reels vertical (1080x1920): the graded hero honestly reframed/cropped to a centered 9:16 crop of the existing frame (subject centered; the wider studio edges/background trim away to hit t
  • done_logo_vector.svg — Clean vectorized DONE wordmark + dripper glyph as SVG, scalable crisp for any ad size, solid brand-teal paths on transparent.
  • done_font_direction.json — font_recommend output: geometric-sans headline + humanist-sans body pairing matching the brand-guideline type note, to steer the handed-off on-image typesetting step.
  • done_review_board_link — Firefly Boards deep-link assembling the hero, graded hero, and three ratio canvases as one client-review moodboard for Slack sign-off.
Workflow — 20 tool steps
  1. asset_create_folders — Create the working CC folder 'DONE_MetaAds_Q3' to hold ingested client assets and all derived creatives.
  2. asset_initialize_file_upload — Begin upload of the client's raw studio product photo (done_dripper_studio_raw.jpg) into the project folder; returns the upload target.
  3. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the upload so the raw studio shot is a stored CC asset with a stable URI the editing tools can consume.
  4. image_auto_straighten — Correct the ~2-degree tilt in the raw capture so the sweep horizon is level before background work.
  5. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-correct the mild underexposure / flat contrast of the raw file to a neutral, well-exposed starting point.
  6. image_select_subject — Auto-detect and mask the dripper product so the background can be isolated from it.
  7. image_invert_selection — Invert the subject mask to select the background (the gray-cast sweep + shadow halo) for the white fill.
  8. image_fill_area — Solid-fill the inverted background selection with pure white #FFFFFF — catalog-clean e-com white, no gray cast, no shadow halo. (Solid fill only, exactly what t
  9. image_crop_to_bounds — Crop tight to the product+whitespace bounds to produce the clean pure-white hero deliverable.
  10. image_adjust_color_temperature — Warm the white-balance toward the brand's warm off-white so the ceramic reads premium, not clinical-cold.
  11. image_adjust_hsl — Nudge the glaze's teal-green hue/saturation/luminance toward the brand teal #0F6E64 so accents are on-palette.
  12. image_adjust_brightness_and_contrast — Add scroll-stopping contrast punch for paid social; this is the brand-graded hero deliverable and the base for all ratio canvases.
  13. image_crop_and_resize — Honestly reframe/crop the graded hero to a centered 1:1 crop at 1080x1080 for the feed square — subject stays centered while the near-white studio edges/backgro
  14. image_crop_and_resize — Honestly reframe/crop the graded hero to a centered 4:5 crop at 1080x1350 for the portrait feed — subject centered while edges/background trim to hit the ratio.
  15. image_crop_and_resize — Honestly reframe/crop the graded hero to a centered 9:16 crop at 1080x1920 for Stories/Reels — subject centered while the wider studio edges/background trim awa
  16. asset_initialize_file_upload — Upload the supplied flat DONE wordmark PNG (done_wordmark_logo.png) into the project folder for vectorization.
  17. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the logo upload to a stable CC asset URI.
  18. image_vectorize — Vectorize the flat wordmark+glyph to a clean SVG so it stays crisp at every ad size.
  19. font_recommend — Return a geometric-sans headline + humanist-sans body pairing matching the brand-guideline type note, to steer the handed-off on-image typesetting.
  20. create_firefly_board — Assemble the hero, graded hero, and three ratio canvases into a single Firefly Boards deep-link for client Slack sign-off.
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon flagship: 20 connector calls, 16 distinct Adobe tools, spanning 4 tool groups (asset/storage, photo-raster masking, tonal/color, vector + board). It exercises a realistic e-com product pipeline (geometry fix -> auto-tone -> subject mask -> invert -> solid white fill -> tight crop -> warm/HSL/contrast grade -> three honest ratio crops -> logo vectorize -> font + board) with a deep dependency chain where the final review board only exists because every upstream frame was produced. Difficulty comes from sequencing the mask/invert/fill correctly (white-bg the catalog way, not generative), keeping the grade on a fixed brand palette, and honestly cropping/reframing the graded hero to each target ratio (accepting edge/background loss) rather than inventing canvas. It deliberately stays inside Adobe headless capability: no text baked on (typeset handoff acknowledged + font_recommend given), no compositing, no gen-fill/gen-expand/object-removal, fill_area used only as a solid-color fill of a selection within the existing canvas (the white-bg step), and each ratio produced by a plain crop/resize of the existing frame.

AO-03 — E-commerce white-background product retouch suite + vectorized brand mark ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Turn a client's raw, messy-background product photo into a pure-white-background marketplace catalog image and convert their rough hand-drawn logo sketch into a clean, brand-colored, scalable vector logo — then package everything for delivery.

💰 ~$165 est.Freelancer.com17 tool calls15 distinct toolslong-horizon
Full client brief
Our small skincare-and-homeware shop is scaling onto Amazon, Etsy and our own Shopify store and we badly need visual credibility — right now our listings use phone snaps shot on a kitchen table and they look amateur, which is killing conversion. We can't afford a studio day, so we're sending you what we have: RAW product captures (a 24MP amber-glass serum bottle shot handheld under window light on a cluttered wooden table, slightly tilted) and a rough logo: our founder hand-inked our brand mark "AURELIA BOTANICALS" on paper and we photographed it with a phone — it's wonky, grey-ish, on off-white paper.

We need you to do the full product-photographer post-production pass and the logo cleanup:

PRODUCT IMAGE (do this as a repeatable recipe — we'll send 10-15 more bottles and jars that all must match):
- Level the horizon (the table edge is tilted).
- Cut the bottle off the messy table and put it on a PURE WHITE (#FFFFFF) seamless background, which is the hard requirement for Amazon's main-image policy.
- Deliver it as a square 2000×2000 px marketplace canvas with the product centered.
- Make it look professional: balanced exposure and color, true-to-life pop on the amber glass, and recover the blown-out highlights/hot spots from the window light so the glass reads cleanly.

LOGO:
- Take our phone photo of the inked sketch and turn it into a CLEAN, infinitely scalable vector (SVG) so it works on a 16px favicon and a 3-metre trade-show banner alike.
- Recolor the mark to our brand green (deep botanical green ~#1E5E3A).
- Recommend 2-3 fonts that pair with the hand-drawn mark for the "AURELIA BOTANICALS" wordmark so we can set the lockup ourselves.

DELIVERY: put the final catalog image, the transparent-background product cut-out, the vector logo and the recolored logo into a clean, clearly-named delivery folder we can pull straight into our listings and brand kit. Keep the white truly white — no grey cast — and keep the logo edges crisp at any size.
Deliverables (4)
  • AURELIA_serum_2000x2000_white.png — Final marketplace catalog hero: square 2000×2000 px, PNG, sRGB, product centered on a pure-white #FFFFFF seamless background (Amazon main-image compliant). Straightened, background-removed, white-fill
  • AURELIA_serum_cutout_transparent.png — Transparent-background product cut-out: 24MP-native resolution PNG with alpha, sRGB, the amber-glass serum bottle isolated off the cluttered table with a clean subject edge, no white fill applied. Int
  • AURELIA_logo_vector.svg — Neutral (untinted) vector logo traced from the founder's raster ink sketch via image_vectorize: a true scalable SVG with crisp paths, safe from a 16px favicon to a 3-metre banner. Retains the sketch's
  • AURELIA_logo_brandgreen.svg — Brand-colored vector logo in botanical green (~#1E5E3A): the neutral AURELIA_logo_vector SVG (step 13) recolored via image_apply_color_overlay so the scalable SVG carries the brand green for the brand
Workflow — 17 tool steps
  1. asset_initialize_file_upload — Begin upload of the client's raw 24MP serum-bottle capture into CC storage; returns presigned target + asset handle.
  2. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the upload to get a stable CC asset URI for the raw bottle photo — the root input the whole product chain consumes.
  3. image_auto_straighten — Level the ~4-degree tilt (the diagonal table edge) so the bottle stands upright before masking.
  4. image_remove_background — Cut the bottle off the cluttered table → transparent PNG. This is deliverable AURELIA_serum_cutout_transparent.png and the source of the subject mask.
  5. image_invert_selection — Invert the subject mask so the (now empty) background region is selected, ready for a solid fill.
  6. image_fill_area — Solid-fill the inverted background selection with pure white #FFFFFF (solid-color fill only — the Amazon main-image white). No generative content.
  7. image_crop_and_resize — Crop/resize to a centered square 2000×2000 px marketplace canvas.
  8. image_apply_auto_tone — Balance global exposure and color cast from the bad window light.
  9. image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation — Add true-to-life pop to the amber glass without oversaturating the white sweep.
  10. image_adjust_highlights — Recover the blown specular hot spots on the curved glass so it reads cleanly — final catalog hero deliverable and the repeatable recipe for the other 10-15 SKUs
  11. asset_initialize_file_upload — Begin upload of the founder's phone photo of the hand-inked logo sketch.
  12. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize to a stable CC asset URI for the raw logo sketch.
  13. image_vectorize — Trace the grey-ish raster ink sketch into a clean scalable SVG with crisp paths — favicon-to-banner safe. First logo deliverable.
  14. image_apply_color_overlay — Recolor the traced mark to brand botanical green ~#1E5E3A via solid color overlay — second logo deliverable.
  15. font_recommend — Recommend 2-3 wordmark fonts that pair with the recolored hand-drawn mark for the 'AURELIA BOTANICALS' lockup.
  16. asset_create_folders — Create the clearly-named client delivery folder (e.g. /AURELIA_Delivery/catalog + /brand) in CC storage.
  17. asset_copy_assets — Collate the catalog hero, the transparent cut-out, and both SVG logos into the delivery folder — ready to pull into listings and the brand kit.
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon and wide-mix: 17 connector calls, 15 distinct Adobe tools (>> the ≥10 / ≥6 floors), spanning four capability groups — asset I/O (init/finalize/folders/copy), masking (remove_background → invert → solid fill), tonal/color (auto_tone, vibrance, highlights, color_overlay), geometry (auto_straighten, crop_and_resize), vector (vectorize) and type (font_recommend). It mirrors a real freelance product-photographer post-production + logo-cleanup job end to end. Every step is execution-mode [C] HEADLESS-CONFIRMED — no [W] Express widget, no [A] async video/audio, no [T] authored template, no [L] local step, and zero not-available capabilities: the white background is a solid #FFFFFF fill of an inverted mask (NOT generative fill or bg-replace-by-prompt), the logo is TRACED from a supplied raster sketch (NOT text-to-image or bespoke type drawing), the raster sketch is vectorized ONCE into a true SVG and the brand-green mark is a solid color overlay applied to that SVG (so the deliverable stays a genuine vector), and the wordmark is only a font RECOMMENDATION, not a composed lockup. Difficulty comes from correct ordering (straighten before mask; remove_background before invert; fill before crop; vectorize before recolor) and the mask hand-off at step 6, not from any disallowed magic.

AO-04 — Silver Jewelry iPhone Shots to White-Background E-commerce Set (JPEG + transparent PNG) ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Turn a handheld iPhone shot of a silver jewellery piece into a clean, colour-accurate, uniform-white e-commerce set: a 3000x3000 sRGB JPEG plus a matching transparent PNG, ready to slot straight into the online store.

💰 ~$110 est.Freelancer.com16 tool calls16 distinct toolslong-horizon
Full client brief
I run a silver jewellery line and need my iPhone-captured product frames turned into a polished, store-ready e-commerce set through AI-powered post-production. The look is minimalist and clean: crisp, shadow-controlled pieces on a pure or near-white backdrop, accurate metal tone and true gemstone colour, pin-sharp on every hero angle, with no colour cast — so they drop straight into my online store without further retouching.

Work this one representative hero frame end-to-end as the master recipe, then I will reuse the exact same chain on the rest of the line (the full order is 20 pieces). Start from the original iPhone HEIC/RAW file I supply. First fix the small handheld tilt and set a neutral baseline exposure and white balance, because the iPhone leaves a warm cast that throws off the silver and the gemstone. Then isolate the jewellery itself, drive the background to a uniform pure white with no cast, and grade the piece so the metal highlights read clean-silver and the stone's colour is faithful.

Deliverables for this frame: one finished JPEG at exactly 3000 x 3000 px, sRGB, on uniform white; and one matching PNG of the same piece on a fully transparent background for layering on coloured store sections. Reframe to a true 1:1 square by cropping the existing uniform-white frame to square bounds around the centred piece (do not stretch or distort the piece), then export at the exact pixel spec. Finally, organise the outputs into a tidy delivery folder and assemble the graded pieces into a single review board so I can approve the look before you run the remaining frames.

Acceptance: uniform white background, no colour cast; accurate metal tone and gemstone colour; pin-sharp focus on the hero angle; exact 3000 x 3000 sRGB JPEG plus matching transparent PNG.
Deliverables (4)
  • silver_ring_hero_3000.jpg — 3000x3000 px, 1:1 square, sRGB, JPEG under 2 MB, uniform pure-white background, neutral colour (no cast), accurate silver metal tone and faithful sapphire colour, pin-sharp on the band. Web-ready e-co
  • silver_ring_hero_transparent.png — Matching PNG of the same graded piece on a fully transparent background (alpha), same grade as the JPEG, for layering on coloured store sections.
  • delivery_folder — Asset folder structure containing the finished JPEG, the transparent PNG, and the original supplied iPhone file, named for client download.
  • review_board_deeplink — Firefly Board deep-link assembling the graded master frame(s) for one-click client approval of the look before the remaining 20 pieces are run.
Workflow — 16 tool steps
  1. asset_initialize_file_upload — Begin upload of the client-supplied original iPhone HEIC/RAW hero frame; returns a presigned target so the original is in CC storage.
  2. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the upload to a stored, addressable working asset (presigned URL) that the edit chain operates on; also becomes the 'original file' deliverable.
  3. image_auto_straighten — Correct the 2-3 degree handheld tilt so the piece and sweep are level before any selection/crop.
  4. image_apply_auto_tone — Set a neutral baseline exposure/contrast for the frame before targeted colour work.
  5. image_adjust_color_temperature — Cool/neutralize the warm iPhone white-balance cast so silver reads neutral and the sapphire colour is accurate (acceptance: no colour cast, accurate metal/gem).
  6. image_select_by_prompt — Select 'the silver ring and sapphire' to get a precise subject mask on the colour-corrected frame.
  7. image_invert_selection — Invert the subject selection to a background-only mask (everything except the jewellery).
  8. image_fill_area — Solid-fill the background mask with pure white (RGB 255/255/255) to drive the off-white tabletop to a uniform, cast-free white backdrop — non-generative, exactl
  9. image_adjust_highlights — Recover/clean the bright metal highlights on the silver so the band reads as crisp clean-silver without blown spots.
  10. image_adjust_single_color_saturation — Lift the blue channel saturation just on the sapphire for faithful gemstone colour, leaving the neutral silver/white untouched — the fully graded master.
  11. image_crop_to_bounds — Reframe the 4:3 graded master to a true 1:1 square by cropping to square bounds around the centred piece — the ring sits with ample uniform-white sweep on all s
  12. image_crop_and_resize — Deterministically crop/resize to exactly 3000x3000 px sRGB and export the JPEG e-commerce hero deliverable.
  13. image_remove_background — Branch off the same graded master to strip the white backdrop to full transparency, producing the matching transparent PNG deliverable with the identical grade.
  14. asset_create_folders — Create the named client-download delivery folder structure to hold the JPEG, the transparent PNG, and the original supplied file.
  15. asset_copy_assets — Collect the finished JPEG, transparent PNG, and original into the delivery folder for the download-link handoff.
  16. create_firefly_board — Assemble the graded master and finished hero onto a Firefly Board deep-link so the client approves the look before the remaining pieces are batched through the
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon (16 connector calls, all server=adobe, 16 distinct Adobe tools) spanning three groups: asset/storage (init/finalize upload, create_folders, copy_assets, firefly board), raster geometry/tone/colour (auto_straighten, auto_tone, color_temperature, highlights, single_color_saturation), and masked compositing-by-fill (select_by_prompt -> invert -> fill_area -> remove_background -> crop_to_bounds -> crop_and_resize). It is genuinely hard because the honest path must hit a strict pixel/colour acceptance bar (exact 3000x3000 sRGB, no cast, accurate metal+gem) while deliberately AVOIDING the unavailable capabilities the brief naively names: no Generative Fill — the uniform white backdrop is produced by select+invert+solid white fill_area, the 1:1 square is reached by an honest crop of the existing frame (crop_to_bounds then a deterministic crop_and_resize to 3000x3000, no generative canvas extension), and the transparent PNG comes from remove_background, not a prompt-based bg replace. The fork producing two grade-matched deliverables from one master and the select/invert/fill mask interplay reward correct tool semantics; a model that reaches for gen-fill, bg-replace-by-prompt, or stretch-to-square fails the constraints.

Client-supplied input assets: 1 total · 0 img · 0 video · 0 audio · 1 data

Data & other (1)

iphone_silver_ring_hero.heic
openai

AO-06 — White-Background Catalog Cleanup for a Firearms/Optics/Outdoor Shopify Store ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Take a batch of inconsistent distributor-supplied product photos for a firearms/optics/outdoor Shopify store and turn them into clean, straightened, color-corrected, pure-white-background, square marketplace hero images (plus transparent-PNG tile variants and a vectorized listing badge), all organized into a review board and a tidy asset folder.

💰 ~$200 est.Upwork16 tool calls15 distinct toolslong-horizon
Full client brief
I run a hands-off Shopify store selling thermal cameras, night-vision optics, firearms, ammunition, trail-camera mounts and hunting accessories. My distributor feeds dump product photos that are all over the place: shot on off-white or gray seamless, slightly tilted, warm/yellow color cast, and framed inconsistently — they look unprofessional next to each other on a collection page and several get rejected by marketplace channels that require a pure-white background. As part of the catalog cleanup I need a repeatable Adobe pipeline that takes one raw distributor photo and produces marketplace-ready listing assets, demonstrated end-to-end on my hero SKU (a handheld thermal weapon scope) and ready to repeat across the catalog.

For the hero SKU, starting from the single raw distributor JPEG I provide, deliver: (1) a corrected master — auto-straightened, cropped to the product, exposure/tone normalized, and the warm color cast neutralized; (2) a pure #FFFFFF background version where the scope is isolated and the messy seamless is replaced with solid white per Shopify/Amazon hero spec; (3) a transparent-PNG cut-out of the same scope for collection tiles and bundle mock-ups; (4) a square 1:1 hero produced by cropping the white-background hero to a centered 1:1 square (the source frame is landscape, so the square is a crop of the existing image — no canvas is invented or extended) and then resized/exported at exactly 2048×2048. Also vectorize the small distributor brand badge I supply (a flat single-color logo mark) into a clean SVG so it scales on the listing, recommend a clean sans-serif catalog typeface, and assemble the finished hero set into a Firefly review board so I can approve it in one place. Finally, drop everything into a tidy, clearly named asset folder I can hand to the next batch.

Constraints: backgrounds must be solid pure white (no gradient, no AI-invented scene), the product must not be distorted or recolored beyond honest color-cast correction, and nothing may be composited in or generatively painted onto the product itself. Keep my color/tone edits faithful to the real product (a firearm optic — accuracy matters to buyers).
Deliverables (8)
  • scope_master_corrected.jpg — Straightened, cropped-to-product, auto-toned, color-cast-neutralized master JPEG of the thermal scope, sRGB, high quality. The clean base every other deliverable derives from.
  • scope_whitebg_hero.jpg — Pure #FFFFFF solid-background hero of the isolated scope, marketplace-compliant (no gradient, no scene), sRGB JPEG.
  • scope_cutout.png — Transparent-background PNG cut-out of the scope for collection tiles / bundle mock-ups, edges clean, alpha channel.
  • scope_hero_square_2048.jpg — Square 1:1 Shopify/Amazon hero: the white-background hero is cropped to a centered 1:1 square (a crop of the existing landscape frame — no canvas extension or generation), then resized and exported at
  • brand_badge.svg — Clean scalable SVG of the distributor brand mark vectorized from the flat PNG, for crisp display at any listing size.
  • catalog_typeface_recommendation — A recommended clean sans-serif catalog/listing typeface (name + rationale) from font_recommend.
  • hero_review_board — Firefly Board deep-link assembling the corrected master, white-bg hero, cut-out, and square hero for one-click owner approval.
  • Cleaned_Catalog_Assets/ folder — A named CC asset folder containing all finished SKU deliverables, copied in and organized for handoff to the next batch.
Workflow — 16 tool steps
  1. asset_search — Locate/confirm the client-uploaded raw distributor JPEG (raw_thermal_scope_distributor.jpg) in CC storage and get its working reference. Initial asset load — no
  2. image_auto_straighten — Fix the ~4 degree tilt in the distributor shot. Consumes the loaded raw asset.
  3. image_crop_to_bounds — Trim the uneven empty margins down to the product bounds after straightening.
  4. image_apply_auto_tone — Normalize exposure/contrast baseline on the cropped frame.
  5. image_adjust_color_temperature — Cool down the warm tungsten cast toward neutral — honest color-cast correction, not a recolor. This is the corrected MASTER (deliverable 1) and the branch point
  6. image_select_subject — Auto-select the scope on the corrected master to separate product from the messy seamless.
  7. image_invert_selection — Invert the subject mask to target the background region only.
  8. image_fill_area — Solid-fill the inverted (background) selection with pure #FFFFFF white — marketplace-compliant white-bg hero (deliverable 2). Solid-color fill only; product unt
  9. image_remove_background — Produce the transparent-PNG cut-out of the scope from the same corrected master, for collection tiles / bundles (deliverable 3).
  10. image_crop_and_resize — Crop the landscape white-bg hero to a centered 1:1 square. The square side is taken from the shorter (vertical) dimension of the existing frame, so this is a pu
  11. image_crop_and_resize — Resize the 1:1 square crop to exactly 2048x2048 for the Shopify/Amazon hero slot (deliverable 4). Downscale/resample only — no canvas extension.
  12. image_vectorize — Vectorize the supplied flat distributor brand badge PNG (distributor_brand_badge.png, the second client input) into a clean SVG (deliverable 5). Consumes a clie
  13. font_recommend — Recommend a clean sans-serif listing/catalog typeface for the store (deliverable 6). Independent advisory step.
  14. create_firefly_board — Assemble the four finished SKU images into a Firefly review board for one-click owner approval (deliverable 7). Consumes the produced asset URNs.
  15. asset_create_folders — Create the named 'Cleaned_Catalog_Assets/' CC folder for the handoff package.
  16. asset_copy_assets — Copy all finished deliverables into the new folder, organized and named for handoff to the next batch (deliverable 8).
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon flagship: 16 connector calls, all server=adobe, all [C] headless-confirmed, spanning 4 of the connector's tool groups (A1 raster/tonal/masking/FX-geometry, A1 reframe-crop, A2 font_recommend + A1 vectorize, A4 asset/board/folder). 16 DISTINCT Adobe tools (well over the >=6 floor) with a genuine multi-branch output->input dependency graph rather than a flat list. Difficulty comes from sequencing a real retouch pipeline correctly (straighten before crop before tone before color, mask->invert->solid-fill for a spec-compliant pure-white background, single allowed generative step used only for canvas reframe-crop not product invention) and from staying strictly inside capability limits: white background is a SOLID image_fill_area fill (not gen-fill, not bg-replace-by-prompt), the cut-out uses image_remove_background, the square hero uses the one legal generative tool (generative-expand/reframe-crop) only to extend the white field, and the SVG comes from image_vectorize on a flat logo. No Canva, no Express [W] widget track, no [X] not-available capability (no AI object removal, no compositing, no upscaling, no text-to-image), and no local step — the entire deliverable is achievable Adobe-only and headless.

AO-07 — Men's pendant line: vectorize the 2D spec sketch + retouch and catalog-prep the sample studio shots ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

From the industrial designer's raster pendant sketch and two mixed-lighting workbench photos of a stainless-steel sample pendant and chain, produce a clean factory-ready vector spec asset plus retouched, isolated, color-accurate catalog hero shots (one expanded to a catalog canvas, one duotone print-screen variant) and gather everything on a Firefly board for the design team.

💰 ~$120 est.Upwork24 tool calls15 distinct toolslong-horizon
Full client brief
From the industrial designer's raster pendant sketch and two mixed-lighting workbench photos of a stainless-steel sample pendant and chain, produce a clean factory-ready vector spec asset plus retouched, isolated, color-accurate catalog hero shots (one reframed and resized to a roomy catalog hero canvas, one duotone print-screen variant) and gather everything on a Firefly board for the design team.
Deliverables (5)
  • pendant_spec_lineart.svg — Clean scalable SVG of the deskewed, tight-cropped pendant line-art, crisp at any print size, suitable as a dimensional spec reference for the factory.
  • pendant_hero_catalog.png — Retouched, white-balanced, auto-toned sample pendant cut out from the bench onto a clean solid catalog background and reframe-cropped/expanded to a roomy catalog hero canvas (e.g. ~4:5 or wider). PNG.
  • pendant_spec_screenprint.png — High-contrast duotone/halftone 'print-screen' variant of the hero pendant for the black-and-white spec sheet. PNG.
  • chain_hero_catalog.png — The box-link chain color-matched to the pendant (same neutral white balance + tonal treatment) and isolated on the same clean catalog background, so pendant and chain read as a coordinated set. PNG.
  • pendant_line_review_board — A single Firefly board assembling the vector spec, the catalog hero, the screen-print variant and the chain hero as a moodboard/deep-link for the brand's design team to review during the refinements-a
Workflow — 24 tool steps
  1. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize upload of the client's raster pendant line-art sketch (pendant_sketch_raster.png) into CC storage.
  2. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the sketch upload; returns the stored asset URI used by all downstream sketch steps.
  3. image_auto_straighten — Deskew the ~4deg-rotated scan so the line-art is square — prerequisite for a clean tight crop and vector trace.
  4. image_crop_to_bounds — Crop tight to the pendant line-art, dropping the scanned white margin/paper border.
  5. image_vectorize — Trace the cleaned raster line-art into a crisp scalable SVG — the factory-ready dimensional spec asset (DELIVERABLE 1).
  6. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize upload of the sample pendant workbench photo (sample_pendant_on_bench.jpg).
  7. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the pendant photo upload; returns its stored asset URI.
  8. image_adjust_color_temperature — Neutralize the mixed warm-shop/cool-window cast — pull the yellow-green off-white toward neutral white balance on the metal.
  9. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the white-balanced pendant for a baseline correct exposure/contrast spread before targeted tweaks.
  10. image_adjust_highlights — Recover blown highlights on the brushed steel so the metal keeps its brushed grain instead of clipping to white.
  11. image_adjust_single_color_saturation — Desaturate the residual yellow channel to give the stainless steel a clean cool-neutral cast (selective, leaving the metal looking true).
  12. image_select_by_prompt — Prompt-select the pendant subject ('the metal pendant') to build a mask separating it from the busy bench.
  13. image_remove_background — Cut the graded pendant out of the cluttered workbench to a transparent PNG using the subject mask.
  14. image_fill_area — Invert-fill the transparent area with a clean solid light-gray catalog background (solid-color fill only — no compositing).
  15. image_crop_and_resize — Reframe and resize the solid-gray catalog composite to a roomy catalog hero canvas (e.g. ~4:5), centering the pendant with clean margins — the catalog hero (DEL
  16. image_apply_halftone — Derive a high-contrast duotone/halftone print-screen variant of the hero for the B&W spec sheet (DELIVERABLE 3).
  17. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize upload of the matching box-link chain photo (sample_chain_on_bench.jpg).
  18. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the chain photo upload; returns its stored asset URI.
  19. image_adjust_color_temperature — Apply the SAME neutralizing white-balance shift derived for the pendant so the chain matches the pendant's color (set consistency).
  20. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the white-balanced chain to the same baseline tonal treatment as the pendant.
  21. image_select_by_prompt — Prompt-select the chain ('the metal chain') to mask it off the busy bench.
  22. image_remove_background — Cut the color-matched chain out of the workbench to transparent PNG using its mask.
  23. image_fill_area — Invert-fill with the SAME solid light-gray catalog background as the pendant so the pair reads as a coordinated set (DELIVERABLE 4).
  24. create_firefly_board — Assemble the four produced assets into one Firefly board deep-link for the design team's refinements-and-iterations review (DELIVERABLE 5).
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon and wide-mix: 24 connector calls, 15 distinct Adobe tools, three asset-load branches that must be processed independently then converged onto one board. It exercises geometry (auto_straighten, crop_to_bounds), vector conversion (vectorize), a full tonal/color grade pipeline (color_temperature, auto_tone, highlights, single_color_saturation), masking/isolation (select_by_prompt -> remove_background -> fill_area), the sole reframe/crop step (generative-expand reframe-crop), a print FX (halftone), and board assembly (create_firefly_board). The agent must (a) recognize the brief's literal 3D-CAD core is out of scope and faithfully take the Adobe-doable 2D asset-prep slice, (b) keep the pendant and chain color treatments consistent so the set matches, (c) order masking after grading so the cutout carries the corrected color, and (d) respect tool limits — fill_area is solid-color only (no compositing), generative-expand is the sole reframe/crop step, vectorize needs a clean deskewed crop first. All steps are headless-confirmed [C]; no [W]/[A]/[T]/[L] needed.

AO-08 — Signature Bracelet Launch: Studio Retouch, White-Background E-Comm Set, Graded Hero, Expanded Social Banner & Vectorized Mark ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Turn the brand's raw studio prototype shots, wordmark, and lifestyle frame into a launch image kit: a clean white-background e-commerce set, a color-graded close-up hero, an expanded social banner, and a vectorized wordmark — then assemble everything on a Firefly board for sign-off.

💰 ~$120 est.Upwork21 tool calls17 distinct toolslong-horizon
Full client brief
We are launching the signature bracelet for our new lifestyle and cultural brand (gathering, celebration, connection, memorable experiences). Prototypes are made and we have shot them in the studio, but the raw frames are crooked, dull, and shot on a slightly grey seamless that needs to read as pure white for e-commerce. We need you to produce the full launch image kit from the assets we supply — no new product design, just world-class post-production on what we shot.

Specifically:
1) Clean the hero studio shot (DSLR raw of the woven-cord-and-gold-clasp bracelet on a slightly-tilted grey seamless): auto-straighten the horizon, crop to a clean square, and bring it to life with auto-tone plus exposure/contrast and a slight warm white-balance so the gold clasp glows.
2) Make it e-commerce-ready: select the bracelet subject, invert the selection, and fill the background to pure white (#FFFFFF) so it drops cleanly onto our product pages; boost only the gold's saturation so the clasp reads as real 18k without touching the cord color.
3) From that cleaned, graded master, produce a punchy color-graded CLOSE-UP HERO for the homepage: apply a cohesive Lightroom look/preset and a gentle lens blur so the macro clasp detail pops, then crop to a 4:5 portrait.
4) Produce a wide SOCIAL/WEB BANNER from the same graded master by re-cropping the pure-white e-commerce master to a 16:9 landscape frame (2400x1350) — reframing so the bracelet sits to one side and the existing white sweep leaves clean text-safe negative space for headline text on the website hero. This is a crop/reframe of the existing pixels only; nothing is added or extended beyond the source frame.
5) We also supply a flat PNG of our wordmark — vectorize it to a clean SVG so it scales for packaging, the website, and the bracelet's hang-tag.
6) License one premium lifestyle "table of friends gathered" stock backdrop from Adobe Stock at full res so our social team has an on-brand scene to drop the banner over.
7) Assemble the final graded master, the white-background e-comm shot, the close-up hero, the expanded banner, the vectorized wordmark, and the licensed backdrop onto a single Firefly board so we can review and approve the whole kit in one place.

Deliverables: pure-white-background e-commerce JPEG, a 4:5 graded close-up hero, a 16:9 banner (cropped from the white-bg master) with text-safe negative space, a scalable SVG wordmark, the licensed stock backdrop, and a shareable Firefly board link. Keep the look elevated, warm, and timeless to match the brand.
Deliverables (6)
  • ecom_white_bg_bracelet.jpg — Square ~2400x2400 JPEG, bracelet on pure #FFFFFF background, auto-toned + exposure/contrast corrected + warm WB, gold clasp saturation-boosted; drop-in ready for product detail pages.
  • homepage_hero_closeup_4x5.jpg — 4:5 portrait (~2048x2560) color-graded close-up hero with a cohesive Lightroom preset look + gentle lens blur isolating the macro clasp detail.
  • social_banner_16x9.jpg — 16:9 (2400x1350) banner produced by cropping/reframing the pure-white e-commerce master to a landscape frame — the bracelet offset to one side with the existing white sweep providing clean text-safe n
  • wordmark_vector.svg — Clean scalable SVG traced from the flat wordmark PNG; single-color charcoal, crisp paths for packaging / web / hang-tag.
  • licensed_gathering_backdrop.jpg — Full-resolution licensed Adobe Stock 'friends gathered at a table' lifestyle backdrop (presigned download URL) for the social team to place the banner over.
  • launch_kit_firefly_board — Firefly Boards deep-link assembling the graded master, white-bg e-comm shot, 4:5 hero, 16:9 banner, vectorized wordmark, and licensed backdrop for one-place review/approval.
Workflow — 21 tool steps
  1. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize upload of the client's raw studio capture bracelet_studio_hero.jpg into CC storage.
  2. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the upload; yields the stored asset/presigned reference for the raw hero used by every downstream image step.
  3. image_auto_straighten — Level the few-degrees-crooked grey seamless horizon on the raw capture.
  4. image_crop_and_resize — Crop the straightened frame to a clean centered square (~2400x2400) for the e-commerce master.
  5. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the dull underexposed square to a balanced baseline.
  6. image_adjust_brightness_and_contrast — Add controlled contrast/brightness so the bracelet separates from the seamless.
  7. image_adjust_color_temperature — Nudge white balance warm so the gold clasp glows; this is the graded MASTER reused by the e-comm, hero, banner and board branches.
  8. image_select_subject — Auto-select the bracelet subject on the graded master.
  9. image_invert_selection — Invert to select only the grey seamless background.
  10. image_fill_area — Solid-fill the inverted background selection with pure white #FFFFFF (solid-color fill only) for a clean e-commerce sweep.
  11. image_adjust_single_color_saturation — Boost only the gold/yellow channel saturation so the clasp reads as real 18k without shifting the navy cord — DELIVERABLE: white-bg e-comm JPEG.
  12. image_list_presets — List available Lightroom presets to pick a cohesive elevated/warm look for the homepage hero.
  13. image_apply_preset — Apply the chosen cohesive preset to the graded master for the homepage close-up look.
  14. image_apply_lens_blur — Gentle whole-image lens blur to soften the frame and pop the macro clasp detail.
  15. image_crop_and_resize — Crop to 4:5 portrait (~2048x2560) — DELIVERABLE: graded homepage close-up hero.
  16. image_crop_and_resize — Reframe the ~2400x2400 pure-white e-comm master to a 16:9 landscape banner (2400x1350) by cropping the square down to landscape — bracelet offset to one side so
  17. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize upload of the client's flat wordmark PNG.
  18. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the wordmark upload into CC storage.
  19. image_vectorize — Trace the flat single-color wordmark raster to a clean scalable SVG — DELIVERABLE: vector wordmark.
  20. asset_license_and_download_stock — Search → license → download a full-res 'friends gathered at a table' lifestyle backdrop from Adobe Stock — DELIVERABLE: licensed backdrop (presigned URL).
  21. create_firefly_board — Assemble the graded master + all five produced/licensed deliverables onto one Firefly board for one-place client review/approval — DELIVERABLE: shareable board
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon flagship: 21 connector calls, 17 distinct Adobe tools, spanning three tool groups (image_* raster/retouch, asset_* storage+stock, create_firefly_board boards). Genuinely hard because it requires correct ORDERING (license/upload before edit; straighten before crop; select→invert→fill in exact sequence; preset/lens-blur before the final crop) and a hub-and-spoke reuse pattern where one graded master fans out into four differently-cropped/graded deliverables — a single wrong root edit poisons all branches. All steps are headless-confirmed [C] and fully gen-free, so it is completely autonomous-doable with no [W]/[A]/[T]/[L] crutches. Constraint-faithful: no compositing, no bg-replace-by-prompt, no gen-fill / no outpaint / no generative expand (both fill_area steps are solid white only), no from-scratch design, no layout composition.

AO-10 — Modern Silver Rosary Necklace — Commercial Product Package (Hero Retouch + Vector Spec + Screen-Print Tech Graphic + Data-Merge Spec Sheet) ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Turn the client's raw studio shot of a modern silver rosary necklace into a full commercial product package: a retouched hero image on a clean sweep, a vector line-art spec, a screen-print-style halftone tech graphic, and a print-ready data-merged spec sheet — all assembled into a review board for revision rounds.

💰 ~$110 est.Freelancer.com17 tool calls16 distinct toolslong-horizon
Full client brief
We sell handcrafted sterling silver religious jewelry and have just finalized a new MODERN-style rosary necklace (clean geometric links, minimal crucifix, brushed-silver finish). I shot it on my bench under mixed lighting and need a freelancer to build the full commercial product package we can use across our store listing, lookbook, a stamped/printed merch concept, and our wholesale line sheet.

Deliverables:
1. A retouched HERO image of the rosary: straightened, auto-toned, exposure/highlight balanced so the brushed silver reads true (not yellow-cast), the subject isolated, dropped onto a clean neutral studio sweep, cropped and resized to the exact 4:5 e-commerce frame, and given a final commercial polish (a subtle vibrance/saturation lift so the silver pops) — the flat neutral sweep already reads as a soft, distraction-free studio background. Deliver a polished JPEG and the cut-out transparent PNG.
2. A clean VECTOR line-art / silhouette of the necklace (SVG) we can scale for stamping, embroidery, and the modern logo-lockup spec sheet.
3. A screen-print / halftone TECH GRAPHIC of the rosary (single-tone, print-screen look) for a tote/tee merch mockup and our stamping vendor.
4. A print-ready PRODUCT SPEC SHEET: take our authored InDesign spec-sheet template and our SKU CSV (material, chain length, crucifix size, finish, price tiers) and data-merge it into a per-variant PDF, plus a vector variant of the line sheet from our Illustrator template.
5. Assemble the hero, the cut-out, the vector spec, the halftone graphic and the spec-sheet preview into a single Firefly review board (deep-link) so we can run revision rounds.

Keep everything modern, minimal, and silver-true. I will pick the final crop. Strong communication for revisions is essential.
Deliverables (7)
  • rosary_hero_4x5.jpg — Retouched commercial hero, 4:5 (e.g. 2400x3000px) JPEG sRGB: straightened, auto-toned, neutral white balance (silver-true), subject isolated on a clean neutral solid sweep, cropped/reframed to the exa
  • rosary_cutout.png — Transparent-background PNG cut-out of the rosary (background removed), full-res, for listings and the spec-sheet image placeholder.
  • rosary_lineart.svg — Clean vectorized line-art / silhouette SVG of the necklace for stamping/embroidery and the logo-lockup spec.
  • rosary_halftone_tech.png — Single-tone screen-print / halftone tech graphic PNG of the rosary for tote/tee merch and the stamping vendor.
  • rosary_spec_sheet_merged.pdf — Multi-page print-ready PDF, one page per SKU variant, produced by data-merging the authored .indd template against sku_variants.csv (material/length/finish/price + hero image).
  • rosary_line_sheet.png — Rendered wholesale line-sheet card(s) from the authored .ai after Illustrator-variable data-merge against the CSV.
  • rosary_review_board_link — Firefly Board deep-link assembling hero, cut-out, vector line-art, halftone graphic, line-sheet preview and the client's brand logo (lockup reference) for client revision rounds.
Workflow — 17 tool steps
  1. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize upload of the client's raw studio shot into CC storage to start the pipeline.
  2. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize upload; yields the asset URN/href used as the source for all editing.
  3. image_auto_straighten — Correct the crooked/tilted bench capture so the necklace sits level — first real edit, consumes step 2 output.
  4. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the straightened frame to balance the muddy mixed-lighting exposure baseline.
  5. image_adjust_color_temperature — Cool the warm yellow desk-lamp cast so brushed silver reads neutral/true — core 'silver-true' requirement.
  6. image_adjust_highlights — Recover blown specular highlights on the polished link edges so metal detail survives.
  7. image_remove_background — Isolate the necklace off the messy bench → transparent PNG cut-out (a delivered output and a reusable input).
  8. image_crop_and_resize — Crop/reframe the isolated subject toward the 4:5 e-commerce aspect, centered with breathing room.
  9. image_fill_area — Solid-color fill (preset neutral light-grey) of the now-transparent area → clean studio sweep behind the cut-out. SOLID fill only, no compositing.
  10. image_crop_and_resize — Crop and resize the swept composite to the exact roomy 4:5 target frame (e.g. 2400x3000) — gen-free reframe/resize, no invented pixels.
  11. image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation — Final commercial polish: a subtle vibrance/saturation lift so the brushed silver reads crisp against the neutral sweep, finishing the hero JPEG. (No lens blur:
  12. image_vectorize — Vectorize the clean cut-out into scalable line-art/silhouette SVG for stamping/embroidery/logo-lockup (reuses step 7 output).
  13. image_apply_halftone — Screen-print/halftone single-tone tech graphic from the cut-out for merch and the stamping vendor (also reuses step 7 output).
  14. document_merge_data_layout — InDesign data-merge: bind the authored .indd <<fields>> + SKU CSV (image placeholder fed the cut-out) → one PDF page per variant. [T] needs the user-authored te
  15. document_merge_data_vector — Illustrator Variables data-merge on the authored .ai against the same CSV → variant line-sheet cards. [T] needs authored variables.
  16. document_render_vector — Export the merged .ai line-sheet to PNG preview for the client/review board.
  17. create_firefly_board — Assemble all produced deliverables plus the client's brand_logo.png (for the brand lockup reference) into one Firefly review board deep-link for revision rounds
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon flagship: 17 connector calls, 16 distinct Adobe tools, spanning 3 connector groups (A1 raster, A2 document/layout, A4 asset+board). It exercises a realistic multi-deliverable jewelry-photo production pipeline — geometric correction, multi-stage tonal/white-balance retouch for silver-true color, subject isolation, solid-sweep fill, honest reframe-crop, final vibrance/saturation polish, vectorization, screen-print halftone, and TWO authored-template data merges (InDesign [T] + Illustrator [T]) — with a heavily reused intermediate (the cut-out feeds 5 branches) and a final board convergence. Difficulty comes from honoring the capability boundaries: the literal brief ('design a rosary from scratch / 3D') is out of scope, so the agent must reinterpret it as the in-scope production-art package, sequence the white-balance/highlight steps correctly before isolation, use image_fill_area as solid sweep (not compositing) and image_crop_and_resize only as an honest reframe-crop (accepting that edges get cropped to hit the 4:5 ratio, never growing/padding the canvas), and correctly tag the two [T] merge steps as needing user-authored templates rather than fabricating layout composition.

line_sheet_template.ai.proxy.txt
content
**Filename:** `line_sheet_template.ai`

**Description:** Adobe Illustrator (.ai) template for a single-product wholesale line-sheet card, optimized for data merge functionality.

**Structure & Layout:**
*   **Dimensions:** A5 Portrait (148 x 210 mm / 5.8 x 8.3 inches).
*   **Grid:** Modern, minimal grid-based layout for clean, structured presentation of a single product.
*   **Layers:** Logically organized into layers such as 'Background', 'Content (Static)', 'Variables', and 'Accent Elements'.
*   **Content Flow:** Clear hierarchy with a dominant area for the product image, followed by key product details and pricing.

**Placeholders & Illustrator Variables:**
All specified fields are implemented as genuine Illustrator Variables, directly bound to their respective objects, ready for data merge from a CSV or similar source.
*   **`hero`:** Linked-image variable, bound to a rectangular image placeholder object. (Primary product image)
*   **`sku`:** Text variable, bound to a text object. (Product Stock Keeping Unit)
*   **`product_name`:** Text variable, bound to a text object. (Full product title)
*   **`material`:** Text variable, bound to a text object. (Product material composition/description)
*   **`wholesale_price`:** Text variable, bound to a text object. (Wholesale unit price)

**Brand Styling:**
*   **Color Palette:** Predominantly clean white background with charcoal (dark grey) typography.
*   **Typography:** Modern, legible sans-serif typeface used consistently throughout for a minimalist aesthetic.
*   **Accent:** A subtle, thin silver accent rule (line) is incorporated at strategic points for visual separation and sophisticated branding.

spec_sheet_template.indd.proxy.txt
content
**Template File:** `spec_sheet_template.indd`

**Description:**
A single-page, A4 portrait InDesign template designed for a jewelry product spec sheet. It features a modern, minimal layout with a sophisticated charcoal and silver color palette, adhering to the client's existing brand line-sheet aesthetic.

**Dimensions & Orientation:**
*   **Page Size:** A4 Portrait (210mm x 297mm)
*   **Margins:** Standard 12.7mm (0.5in) all around, with a 3mm bleed.

**Layout & Structure:**
*   **Header:** Top-left corner dedicated to the brand wordmark (text frame with a specific paragraph style).
*   **Content Grid:** The page is structured with a prominent left-hand section for the main product image and a right-hand column for detailed product specifications.
*   **Typography:** Utilizes a clean, sans-serif typeface for readability, with hierarchical styling (font size, weight, and color) to differentiate product names, categories, and data values.
*   **Color Palette:** Predominantly charcoal for text and key informational dividers, with subtle silver accents for graphic elements or highlighted pricing.

**Layers:**
The template is organized into standard InDesign layers for efficient editing:
*   `Brand_Elements`: Contains the brand wordmark and any static logo graphics.
*   `Background_Graphics`: Holds subtle background textures, rules, or decorative lines.
*   `Product_Imagery`: Dedicated layer for the `<<hero_image>>` placeholder.
*   `Product_Details`: Contains all text frames for product specifications and data-merge fields.

**Placeholders (Genuine InDesign Data Merge Fields):**
All listed fields are implemented as actual InDesign data-merge placeholders, allowing for direct integration with external data sources.

*   `<<hero_image>>`: A large, left-aligned graphic frame configured for data merge to display the primary product image.
*   `<<product_name>>`: A prominent text frame for the product's main title, located at the top of the detail section.
*   `<<sku>>`: Text frame for the product's Stock Keeping Unit.
*   `<<material>>`: Text frame detailing the primary material (e.g., "Sterling Silver," "14k Gold").
*   `<<chain_length>>`: Text frame for chain length specifications (e.g., "18 inches").
*   `<<crucifix_size>>`: Text frame for the size of the crucifix component (if applicable).
*   `<<finish>>`: Text frame specifying the product's finish (e.g., "Pol

AO-11 — Sterling-Silver Ruby Pendant — Retouched Hero Shots, Color-Matched Set, Vectorized Calligraphy Mark, Moodboard & Spec-Sheet Export ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Turn the client's raw photos of a finished Sterling-Silver 925 ruby pendant into a retouched, color-consistent hero product-photo set with a clean white-background cut-out, an reframe-cropped banner, a vectorized Arabic-calligraphy hallmark, a Firefly moodboard, and an exported lookbook/spec sheet.

💰 ~$120 est.Upwork22 tool calls19 distinct toolslong-horizon
Full client brief
The client, Shady, has a finished custom pendant — Sterling Silver 925, opening double doors on hidden hinges, a ruby locking element, Islamic geometric openwork, and Arabic calligraphy engravings — and wants to present and market it ("share similar work, estimated cost, and timeline"). He supplies three studio photographs of the actual piece (a front-on hero, a three-quarter open-doors view showing the ruby latch, and a macro of the engraved calligraphy) plus a jeweler-authored InDesign spec/lookbook sheet (.indd) that already contains the layout, copy, and price/timeline placeholders.

Produce a polished, channel-ready product-photo + presentation package, all in Adobe:
1. Ingest the three client photos into a working folder, license/pull one Adobe Stock dark-velvet jeweller's pad as an optional backdrop reference, and stage everything.
2. On the front-on hero: auto-straighten, auto-tone, then lift exposure/highlights to make the polished silver read bright and the ruby read deep and saturated (selective single-color saturation on the red). Warm the white balance slightly for a luxury feel.
3. Carry the SAME corrected look onto the three-quarter open-doors shot via a saved/applied preset so the set is color-consistent, then deepen the ruby the same way.
4. Build a packshot: select the pendant subject, remove the background to transparent, drop it onto a pure-white selection fill for a clean marketplace cut-out, and crop/resize to a square marketplace tile.
5. Reframe-crop the corrected hero to a wide ~2400x1000 hero-banner for the listing header, positioning the pendant off-center to leave clean negative space for header text (no pixels invented — the crop uses only the existing studio backdrop already around the subject).
6. From the calligraphy macro, isolate and vectorize the engraved Arabic hallmark into a clean SVG brand mark.
7. Assemble the finished shots into a Firefly Boards moodboard the client can review.
8. Export the jeweler-authored InDesign spec/lookbook .indd to a print-ready PDF and a web JPEG.
Deliverables: corrected hero, color-matched open-doors shot, white-bg square packshot, wide hero-banner, ruby-deep variant, calligraphy SVG mark, Firefly board deep-link, and the exported spec-sheet PDF + JPEG.
Deliverables (8)
  • hero_corrected.jpg — Straightened, auto-toned, exposure/highlight-lifted, warm-balanced front hero with a deep saturated ruby; full-res JPEG, sRGB.
  • open_doors_matched.jpg — Three-quarter open-doors shot color-matched to the hero via the saved preset, ruby deepened; full-res JPEG, sRGB.
  • packshot_white_square.png — Background-removed pendant on pure-white fill, cropped/resized to a 2000x2000 square marketplace packshot, PNG.
  • hero_banner_wide.jpg — Gen-free reframe crop of the corrected hero to a ~2400x1000 listing-header banner: the pendant is positioned off-center and only the existing studio backdrop already around the subject is used for cle
  • calligraphy_mark.svg — Clean vector SVG of the engraved Arabic-calligraphy hallmark traced from the macro, usable as a scalable brand mark.
  • pendant_moodboard_board — Firefly Boards deep-link assembling the corrected hero, matched open-doors shot, packshot and banner for client review.
  • lookbook_spec.pdf — Print-ready PDF export of the authored InDesign spec/lookbook sheet.
  • lookbook_spec_web.jpg — Web-resolution JPEG export of the same spec/lookbook sheet for emailing the estimate/timeline.
Workflow — 22 tool steps
  1. asset_create_folders — Create a working CC folder 'Shady_Pendant_Package' to hold all client photos and derived assets.
  2. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize upload for the three client photos (hero, open-doors, calligraphy macro) and the authored .indd into the project folder.
  3. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the upload so pendant_hero_front.jpg, pendant_threequarter_open.jpg, pendant_calligraphy_macro.jpg and pendant_lookbook_spec.indd are stored CC assets
  4. asset_license_and_download_stock — Search Adobe Stock for a dark velvet jeweller's pad, license it, and download the full-res presigned URL as an optional luxury backdrop reference (license BEFOR
  5. image_auto_straighten — Auto-straighten the ~3-degree-tilted front hero (pendant_hero_front.jpg from ingested_assets).
  6. image_apply_auto_tone — Apply auto-tone to the straightened hero to fix the deliberate under-exposure baseline.
  7. image_adjust_exposure — Lift exposure so the polished silver reads bright and clean.
  8. image_adjust_highlights — Recover/shape highlights to keep specular silver from clipping.
  9. image_adjust_color_temperature — Warm the white balance slightly for a luxury jewelry feel.
  10. image_adjust_single_color_saturation — Boost only the red channel so the ruby reads deep and saturated without over-saturating the silver. DELIVERABLE hero_corrected.jpg.
  11. image_apply_preset — Apply the SAME corrected look (as a Lightroom preset matching the hero grade) to the cooler/greener open-doors shot to color-match the set.
  12. image_adjust_single_color_saturation — Deepen the ruby latch on the open-doors shot the same way the hero ruby was deepened. DELIVERABLE open_doors_matched.jpg.
  13. image_select_subject — Select the pendant subject on the corrected hero to build a cut-out mask.
  14. image_remove_background — Remove the background to a transparent PNG using the subject mask.
  15. image_invert_selection — Invert the subject mask to select the (now-transparent) background region for a solid fill.
  16. image_fill_area — Solid-fill the inverted background selection with pure white for a clean marketplace cut-out (solid-color fill only).
  17. image_crop_and_resize — Crop/resize the white-bg packshot to a 2000x2000 square marketplace tile. DELIVERABLE packshot_white_square.png.
  18. image_crop_and_resize — Gen-free reframe: crop/resize the corrected hero to a ~2400x1000 wide listing-header banner (fit 'reframe', target 2400x1000), aligning the pendant off-center s
  19. image_vectorize — Vectorize the high-contrast Arabic-calligraphy macro (pendant_calligraphy_macro.jpg) into a clean scalable SVG hallmark. DELIVERABLE calligraphy_mark.svg.
  20. create_firefly_board — Assemble the finished produced assets into a Firefly Boards moodboard deep-link for client review. DELIVERABLE pendant_moodboard_board.
  21. document_render_layout — Export the client-authored pendant_lookbook_spec.indd to a print-ready PDF (export only — no layout composition). DELIVERABLE lookbook_spec.pdf.
  22. document_render_layout — Export the same authored .indd to a web-resolution JPEG for emailing the estimate/timeline. DELIVERABLE lookbook_spec_web.jpg.
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon flagship: 22 connector calls, 20 distinct Adobe tools, spanning four tool groups (asset/stock/storage/board, photo raster, vector, document export). It exercises a deep linear grade chain (auto-straighten → auto-tone → exposure → highlights → temperature → single-color saturation), a non-trivial color-MATCH requirement across two differently-cast shots via a saved preset, a multi-step masking branch (select_subject → remove_background → invert → solid fill → crop) where the agent must order the invert/fill correctly, the single legal generative step (reframe-crop), a raster→vector conversion, a moodboard assembly that reuses four derived outputs, and an export-only document step on a genuinely-authored input. Every step is [C] headless-confirmed, so the whole chain is autonomously runnable — the difficulty is in sequencing and reuse, not in any forbidden capability. It also forces the modeler to recognize that the brief's literal 3D-CAD ask is out of scope and re-anchor faithfully onto the supported jewelry-photo deliverable.
