pendant_lookbook_spec.indd.proxy.txt
content
**Template Description: pendant_lookbook_spec.indd**

This is a single-page Adobe InDesign (.indd) document designed as a luxury pendant lookbook and specification sheet.

**Structure & Layout (Typical A4/US Letter orientation):**

*   **Header:** A dedicated top-aligned area for a `[Brand Wordmark / Logo]` placeholder.
*   **Hero Image:** A prominent, large rectangular `[Hero Pendant Image]` frame dominating the upper-mid section of the page.
*   **Detail Images:** Three smaller, equally-sized `[Pendant Detail Image]` frames positioned horizontally below the hero image, for showcasing specific features or angles.
*   **Specification Table:** A clearly structured table area with styled text fields:
    *   `Material:` Placeholder for `Sterling Silver 925`
    *   `Stone:` Placeholder for `Natural Ruby`
    *   `Mechanism:` Placeholder for `hidden-hinge opening doors with ruby latch`
    *   `Motifs:` Placeholder for `Islamic geometric openwork, Arabic calligraphy engraving`
*   **Logistics Block:** A compact section at the bottom containing distinct text frames for `[Estimated Cost]` and `[Lead Time]`.

**Layers & Placeholders:**

The template utilizes genuine InDesign image frames for all visual content (1 large hero, 3 small detail). All text elements, including the brand wordmark, spec table entries, and logistics block, are implemented as styled InDesign text frames, allowing for easy content updates while maintaining consistent typography and layout.

**Dimensions:**
Optimized for standard print dimensions (e.g., A4 or US Letter equivalent), ensuring balanced margins and clear presentation.

**Brand Styling:**
The design embodies a luxury aesthetic, characterized by clean lines, precise alignment, and a sophisticated typographic hierarchy. It employs carefully chosen InDesign paragraph and character styles to convey a premium brand identity, with an emphasis on clarity for product specifications and an elegant presentation for visual elements.

AO-12 — Bright-and-Airy Lightroom Grade for a 38-Shot Food Photography Set (Flat-Lay, 45°, Side) ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Take a client's 38-frame food shoot (flat-lay / 45° / side of one dish) that is close to the mark and grade it to a cohesive bright-and-airy look — white balance, exposure, highlight/shadow, gentle color — then deliver straightened, correctly-sized final files.

💰 ~$138 est.Upwork20 tool calls15 distinct toolslong-horizon
Full client brief
A food-photography client (single hero dish, shot three ways: an overhead flat-lay, a 45-degree three-quarter, and a side/profile shot, 38 frames total) hands you a set that is "close to the goal but needs tweaking." The goal is a consistent BRIGHT AND AIRY look across the whole set: clean neutral-to-slightly-warm white balance, lifted exposure, bright but not blown highlights on the plate and window light, open (not muddy) shadows, and gentle, appetising color — applied cohesively so the 38 frames read as one shoot. Quick turnaround / ASAP.\n\nConcretely, working in the Adobe connector entirely on the client's own pixels (no compositing, no generative fill, no background swap):\n1. Ingest the three representative hero frames the recipe is tuned on.\n2. On the flat-lay, square it up (auto-straighten the overhead so the plate edges are true) and crop to a clean composition.\n3. Build the bright-and-airy base grade on that straightened flat-lay: warm the white balance toward a clean daylight, lift exposure, pull the brightest highlights back so the window light and the plate rim are bright-not-blown, open the dark portions so the shadows are airy, then a restrained vibrance/saturation bump and a subtle color-temperature confirmation.\n4. Selectively brighten the dish itself: prompt-select the food, invert to protect the background, and give the food a small targeted highlight/exposure lift so the hero pops without crushing the airy backdrop.\n5. Carry the identical look onto the 45-degree frame and the side frame (auto-tone to seat them, then the same warm-WB + highlight-recovery + shadow-lift recipe) so all three — and by extension the remaining 35 — match.\n6. Produce delivery-ready files: crop/resize the graded frames to the client's delivery dimensions, and size one of them for an Instagram social teaser.\n\nDeliverables: the three fully-graded hero frames at delivery resolution, the selective-food-pop master, an Instagram-sized teaser, and a short edit recipe note describing the cohesive look so the client can see exactly how the set was unified.
Deliverables (6)
  • flatlay_bright_airy.jpg — Straightened, cropped, fully bright-and-airy-graded flat-lay at client delivery resolution (3000px long edge, sRGB JPG).
  • food_pop_master.jpg — The flat-lay grade with a selective targeted lift on the dish (food prompt-selected, background protected) — the hero master, full resolution sRGB JPG.
  • angle45_bright_airy.jpg — 45-degree frame matched to the same bright-and-airy look (auto-tone + warm WB + highlight recovery + shadow lift), delivery resolution sRGB JPG.
  • side_bright_airy.jpg — Side frame matched to the same look, delivery resolution sRGB JPG.
  • ig_teaser_1080.jpg — Instagram teaser crop/resize of the food-pop master at 1080x1350 (4:5) sRGB JPG.
  • edit_recipe_note.txt — Short plain-text note documenting the cohesive bright-and-airy recipe (the ordered list of adjustments and their intent) so the client can see how the set was unified; compiled locally from the connec
Workflow — 20 tool steps
  1. asset_initialize_file_upload — Open an upload session for the client's three hero frames (flatlay_raw.jpg, angle45_raw.jpg, side_raw.jpg). Initial asset load — the only step with no upstream
  2. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the upload; yields stored asset references/URLs for the three raw frames that every edit step downstream consumes.
  3. image_auto_straighten — Square up the overhead flat-lay so the plate edges and table grain are true — the slight 1-2 degree tilt is corrected before composition.
  4. image_crop_to_bounds — Crop the straightened flat-lay to a clean composition (trim the rotation margins, tighten around the plate).
  5. image_adjust_color_temperature — Warm the white balance off the faintly-cool import toward a clean daylight — the core of the bright-and-airy look.
  6. image_adjust_exposure — Lift overall exposure ~half a stop to brighten the frame (airy base).
  7. image_adjust_highlights — Pull the brightest highlights back so the window light and honey/plate-rim are bright-not-blown after the exposure lift.
  8. image_adjust_dark_portions — Open the dark portions (shadows under the plate) so they read airy, not muddy — completes the bright-and-airy tonal base.
  9. image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation — Restrained vibrance/saturation bump for appetising-but-not-garish color. This is the finished flat-lay base grade (deliverable flatlay_bright_airy.jpg) and the
  10. image_select_by_prompt — Prompt-select the food (the toast and figs) on the graded flat-lay to build a mask for a targeted pop.
  11. image_invert_selection — Invert the food mask so the next lift protects the airy background and acts only on the dish (background-protected).
  12. image_adjust_light_portions — Apply a small targeted lift to the food's light portions through the food mask so the hero dish pops without brightening the protected background. Deliverable f
  13. image_apply_auto_tone — Seat the 45-degree frame with auto-tone as a consistent starting point before matching the reference recipe.
  14. image_adjust_color_temperature — Apply the same warm white-balance shift used on the flat-lay so the 45-degree frame matches the set.
  15. image_adjust_highlights — Recover highlights + (with the matching exposure/shadow intent baked from auto-tone) finish the 45-degree frame to the bright-and-airy look. Deliverable angle45
  16. image_apply_auto_tone — Seat the side/profile frame with auto-tone before applying the matching recipe.
  17. image_adjust_dark_portions — Open the shadows on the side frame to match the airy base (its bright background window already handled by auto-tone highlight roll-off), producing the matched
  18. image_crop_and_resize — Crop/resize the food-pop master to 1080x1350 (4:5) for the Instagram teaser deliverable ig_teaser_1080.jpg.
  19. create_firefly_board — Assemble the graded deliverables (already produced above) into a Firefly board deep-link so the client can review the cohesive set in one place — presents exist
  20. local_write_recipe_note — Single unavoidable local step: compile the ordered connector adjustments into edit_recipe_note.txt so the client has a written record of the cohesive look. No c
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon flagship: 21 steps, 16 distinct Adobe tools, spanning three connector groups (asset/stock, the raster photo family, and Firefly boards) plus one allowed local packaging step. It is genuinely hard because the agent must (a) sequence a real Lightroom bright-and-airy recipe in the correct tonal order (straighten/crop before grading; WB→exposure→highlight recovery→shadow lift→vibrance, not arbitrary), (b) build and correctly invert a prompt-mask to pop the dish while protecting the airy background, and (c) MATCH that look across two more angles via auto-tone-seated sub-chains so the set reads cohesive — the core ask of the brief. All steps are headless-confirmed [C] except one unavoidable [L] note-writing step; no [W]/[A]/[T] tools, no generative fill, no compositing, no background replacement, and image_fill_area is deliberately not used. Faithful to the real Upwork brief (bright-and-airy, white balance/exposure/color correction, flat-lay/45°/side, ASAP).

Client-supplied input assets: 3 total · 3 img · 0 video · 0 audio · 0 data

flatlay_raw.jpg
openai
side_raw.jpg
openai

AO-13 — Taco Tuesday & Lunch-Special Meta Ad Photo-Asset Prep — Retouch, Grade, Cutout, Multi-Ratio reframe-crop & Delivery Pack for a West Palm Beach Mexican Restaurant ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Prep and deliver the campaign-ready photo + brand asset pack (retouched, warm/bold-graded hero food shots, color-splash and subject-pop scroll-stop variants, cut-out dishes, three Meta ad ratios via reframe-crop, a licensed backdrop texture, and a vectorized logo) for a West Palm Beach Mexican restaurant's Taco Tuesday & Lunch-Special Meta ad mockups.

💰 ~$138 est.Upwork20 tool calls17 distinct toolslong-horizon
Full client brief
You are the production designer prepping the photo and brand assets that will be dropped into 12 static Meta (Facebook/Instagram) ad mockups for a family-owned, authentic Mexican restaurant in West Palm Beach, FL. The brand is warm, bold, family-oriented and community-focused — a local neighborhood favorite, NOT a corporate chain. The campaign covers two offers: Taco Tuesday (scroll-stop, urgent, recurring-promo feel) and Lunch Specials. The client has supplied three raw phone/DSLR food photos (a hero al-pastor taco platter, a carne-asada lunch plate, a margarita-and-chips table scene), plus their logo as a flat PNG.

Your job, entirely within the Adobe connector:
1) Load the three supplied food photos and the logo PNG into the project.
2) Straighten and tonally fix the hero taco shot (auto-straighten the slightly tilted handheld frame, then auto-tone), so it reads clean and appetizing.
3) Push a warm, bold restaurant grade on the corrected hero: lift color temperature toward warm, boost vibrance/saturation so the salsa reds and lime greens pop, and recover highlight detail on the glossy meat — the "appetizing and bold" look the brand wants.
4) Build a scroll-stop "color-splash" variant from the graded hero: select the taco/food by prompt, invert the selection so everything BUT the food is selected, and desaturate that background so the food is the only color on screen (eye-catching scroll-stop power).
5) Apply an Adaptive "Subject Pop" preset to the graded hero to create a second high-contrast scroll-stop variant with the food made to stand forward.
6) Create a clean cut-out of the lunch plate: remove the background to a transparent PNG so the dish can float on any colored ad background the layout team chooses.
7) Reframe the graded hero into the three Meta ad canvases the campaign needs — square 1:1 feed, 4:5 portrait feed, and 9:16 story — by cropping and resizing the existing shot to each ratio/size (keep the food in frame, use the surrounding table/scene as the reframe margin; no scene is invented).
8) License one on-brand backdrop texture (warm woven Mexican textile / rustic wood) from Adobe Stock at full res for the layout team to use behind cut-outs.
9) Vectorize the supplied logo PNG into a clean SVG so it scales crisply across every ad size.
10) Assemble all produced assets into a Firefly board as a labeled review deck for the client, and copy the final delivery set into a clearly named project folder.

Deliverables: the corrected + warm-graded hero JPEG; the color-splash hero; the Subject-Pop hero; the transparent-PNG lunch-plate cut-out; three ratio-correct hero canvases (1:1, 4:5, 9:16); one licensed full-res backdrop texture; the logo SVG; a Firefly review board link; and a named delivery folder containing the set.
Deliverables (10)
  • hero_warm_graded.jpg — Straightened + auto-toned + warm/bold-graded hero taco platter; full-res JPEG, sRGB, the appetizing campaign hero.
  • hero_color_splash.jpg — Scroll-stop variant: food in full color, background desaturated via inverted-mask saturation; full-res JPEG, sRGB.
  • hero_subject_pop.jpg — Adaptive Subject-Pop preset applied to the graded hero; high-contrast scroll-stop variant; full-res JPEG, sRGB.
  • lunch_plate_cutout.png — Carne asada lunch plate with background removed; transparent PNG, edges clean enough to float on any ad background.
  • hero_1x1_feed.jpg — Hero reframed to square 1080x1080 (1:1) Meta feed canvas by cropping/resizing the existing shot to the 1:1 ratio; food kept in frame, surrounding table/scene trimmed as crop margin (no pixels invented
  • hero_4x5_feed.jpg — Hero reframed to 1080x1350 (4:5) portrait feed canvas by cropping/resizing the existing shot to the 4:5 ratio; food kept in frame (no pixels invented); JPEG.
  • hero_9x16_story.jpg — Hero reframed to 1080x1920 (9:16) story/Reels canvas by cropping/resizing the existing shot to the 9:16 ratio; food kept in frame (no pixels invented); JPEG.
  • backdrop_texture_fullres.jpg — One licensed Adobe Stock warm woven-textile/rustic-wood backdrop texture, full-res, with license; for layout team to place behind cut-outs.
  • logo_vector.svg — Clean SVG vectorization of the supplied logo PNG; scales crisply across all ad sizes.
  • ElVecino_MetaAd_AssetPack/ — Named project folder containing the full delivery set, plus a Firefly review board deep-link assembling all produced assets as a client-approval deck.
Workflow — 20 tool steps
  1. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize upload of the four client-supplied files (3 food photos + logo PNG) into the project.
  2. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the upload; yields asset handles for hero_taco_platter.jpg, lunch_carne_asada_plate.jpg, margarita_chips_table.jpg, restaurant_logo_flat.png. These are
  3. image_auto_straighten — Straighten the ~4-degree handheld tilt on the hero taco platter (uses hero_taco_platter.jpg from loaded_assets).
  4. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the straightened hero for a clean balanced base before the creative grade.
  5. image_adjust_color_temperature — Lift color temperature toward warm for the inviting neighborhood-restaurant feel.
  6. image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation — Boost vibrance/saturation so salsa reds and lime greens pop (bold brand direction).
  7. image_adjust_highlights — Recover blown highlight detail on the glossy meat — final appetizing campaign hero. PRIMARY graded deliverable.
  8. image_select_by_prompt — Select the taco/food region by prompt on the graded hero (start of the color-splash variant).
  9. image_invert_selection — Invert so everything BUT the food is selected.
  10. image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation — Drive saturation down on the inverted (background) mask of the graded hero → color-splash scroll-stop variant. Food stays full color. DELIVERABLE.
  11. image_apply_preset — Apply Adaptive 'Subject Pop' preset to the graded hero for a second high-contrast scroll-stop variant. DELIVERABLE.
  12. image_remove_background — Remove background on lunch_carne_asada_plate.jpg → transparent-PNG floating cut-out for flexible ad backgrounds. DELIVERABLE.
  13. image_crop_and_resize — Reframe the graded hero to 1080x1080 (1:1) Meta feed by cropping/resizing the existing shot to the square ratio — keep the food in frame, trim surrounding table
  14. image_crop_and_resize — Reframe the graded hero to 1080x1350 (4:5) portrait feed by cropping/resizing the existing shot to the 4:5 ratio — food kept in frame.
  15. image_crop_and_resize — Reframe the graded hero to 1080x1920 (9:16) story/Reels by cropping/resizing the existing shot to the tall ratio — food kept in frame.
  16. asset_license_and_download_stock — Search → license → download one full-res warm woven-textile/rustic-wood backdrop texture from Adobe Stock for layout backgrounds. (Stock search input is the cam
  17. image_vectorize — Vectorize the flat logo PNG (restaurant_logo_flat.png from loaded_assets) into a clean scalable SVG. DELIVERABLE.
  18. asset_create_folders — Create the named delivery folder 'ElVecino_MetaAd_AssetPack' to hold the final set.
  19. asset_copy_assets — Copy all produced deliverables into the named delivery folder.
  20. create_firefly_board — Assemble the copied delivery set into a Firefly review board (deep-link) as the client-approval deck. FINAL deliverable bundle.
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon flagship: 20 connector calls, 17 distinct Adobe tools, spanning four tool groups (A1 raster/photo, A1 vector, A4 asset/stock/board, plus upload/storage). All steps are [C] headless-confirmed — no [W]/[A]/[T]/[X] dependencies, so it runs autonomously on Adobe alone. The chain is genuinely deep (a 6-step linear grade feeding a 5-way branch) and reuses the graded hero many times, exercising masked selective editing (select_by_prompt → invert → masked saturation for color-splash), an Adaptive preset, background removal, the sole reframe/crop step (image_crop_and_resize, which crops/resamples existing pixels only and invents none) across three real Meta ad ratios, stock licensing, vectorization, and a folder+board delivery package. It deliberately stays inside the constraints: NO layout composition (the 12 finished mockups' typesetting is explicitly out of scope and handed off), NO text-to-image, NO compositing, NO bg-replace-by-prompt, image_fill_area is not abused (saturation, not fill, does the desaturate), and the single local-packaging escape hatch is not needed (folder+board are real Adobe asset tools). Grounded faithfully in the real Upwork restaurant brief.

Client-supplied input assets: 4 total · 4 img · 0 video · 0 audio · 0 data

AO-14 — Brickyard retro one-page menu: food-photo grade + logo vectorize + InDesign data-merge to print PDF/JPEG ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Grade four raw burger photos, vectorize the Brickyard wordmark, source a chalkboard texture, and run the client's authored InDesign menu template through a data-merge to produce a print-ready PDF and high-res JPEG.

💰 ~$138 est.Freelancer.com25 tool calls16 distinct toolslong-horizon
Full client brief
Brickyard, a retro street-food burger joint, needs its one-page menu finished for print. The owner has handed off an authored InDesign menu template (menu_template.indd) laid out in a rustic chalkboard style with four photo frames and real data-merge fields (<<section>>, <<item>>, <<desc>>, <<price>> across Classic Burgers, Specialty Burgers, Sides, Beverages), the brand wordmark as a flat PNG, four raw phone-shot food photos (a double Brickyard, a specialty bacon-jalapeño burger, a basket of fries, a craft cola), and a CSV of placeholder item names / descriptions / prices. The four food photos are unappetizing as-shot: slightly tilted, dull/flat, cool white-balance, busy backgrounds. The job: (1) make every food photo look vibrant and appetizing — give the hero double-smash shot the full grade (straighten, auto-tone, warm the white balance toward a hot street-food look, push vibrance, apply a food-pop preset, and throw the cluttered background out of focus so the burger reads as hero), and bring the other three to a matching appetizing look through their own lighter auto-tone / warm-up (plus a background lens-blur where the background is busy) sub-chains; consistently crop all four to the same frame ratio the template expects; (2) lift the hero double-smash shot cleanly onto a transparent background so it can sit over the chalkboard without a hard photographic edge; (3) vectorize the flat wordmark PNG into a clean SVG so it stays razor-sharp at large print size; (4) license a rustic chalkboard/blackboard texture from Adobe Stock to back the layout; (5) upload all the prepped assets, then run the client's authored .indd through InDesign data-merge against the placeholder-prices CSV so each section/item/price binds into the template, and export the bound document to a print-ready PDF and a high-resolution JPEG; (6) assemble the produced assets onto a Firefly board so the owner can sign off on the photo treatment and final menu in one deep-link. Every section/price stays placeholder per the brief.
Deliverables (5)
  • Brickyard_Menu_print.pdf — Print-ready one-page A4 portrait PDF exported from the data-merged InDesign document; placeholder copy bound from CSV, graded food photos + vectorized wordmark + chalkboard texture placed; ~300 DPI pr
  • Brickyard_Menu_highres.jpg — High-resolution JPEG of the same bound one-page menu (A4 portrait, max-quality) for digital/social sharing.
  • graded_food_photos_x4 — Four color-graded, straightened, consistently 4:5-cropped food photos (classic burger / specialty burger / fries / cola) matching the template frames; the classic hero also delivered as a transparent-
  • brickyard_wordmark.svg — Clean vector SVG of the Brickyard wordmark for crisp scaling at large print size.
  • Brickyard_Menu_review_board — Firefly board deep-link assembling the graded photos, vectorized wordmark, and final menu render for one-click owner sign-off.
Workflow — 25 tool steps
  1. asset_search — Search Adobe Stock for a rustic chalkboard/blackboard texture to back the menu (low-res candidates).
  2. asset_license_and_download_stock — License and download the chosen chalkboard texture at full-res (license BEFORE use); becomes the layout background asset.
  3. image_auto_straighten — Level the tilted horizon on the hero double-smash shot.
  4. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the straightened hero to fix the flat/dull exposure.
  5. image_adjust_color_temperature — Warm the cool white balance toward a hot street-food look.
  6. image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation — Push vibrance so the cheese/bun/sauce read as appetizing.
  7. image_apply_preset — Apply a food-pop Lightroom preset to lock a consistent appetizing look (same preset reused for the other three).
  8. image_apply_lens_blur — Throw the cluttered diner background out of focus so the burger reads as hero.
  9. image_crop_and_resize — Crop/resize to the 4:5 frame the template expects (same target applied to all four photos).
  10. image_remove_background — Lift the finished hero burger onto a transparent background so it can sit over the chalkboard without a hard photo edge.
  11. image_apply_auto_tone — Run the specialty-burger photo through the same grade pipeline starting with auto-tone (fixes underexposure).
  12. image_adjust_color_temperature — Warm the specialty shot to match the hero's white balance.
  13. image_apply_lens_blur — Blur the busy kitchen-pass background behind the specialty burger.
  14. image_crop_and_resize — Crop the specialty photo to the matching 4:5 template frame.
  15. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the fries side shot to the consistent look.
  16. image_crop_and_resize — Crop the fries photo to the 4:5 template frame.
  17. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the muddy/flat cola beverage shot.
  18. image_crop_and_resize — Crop the cola photo to the 4:5 template frame so all four match.
  19. image_vectorize — Vectorize the flat wordmark PNG into a clean SVG for razor-sharp large-print scaling.
  20. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize an upload session for all prepped menu assets (graded photos, vector wordmark, texture).
  21. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the upload; returns asset URNs the InDesign template/data-merge will reference.
  22. document_merge_data_layout — InDesign data-merge: bind the placeholder section/item/desc/price rows into the client's authored .indd (which carries real <<fields>>); prepped photos + textur
  23. document_render_layout — Export the merged InDesign document to a print-ready ~300 DPI A4 PDF deliverable.
  24. document_render_layout — Export the same merged document to a high-resolution JPEG deliverable.
  25. create_firefly_board — Assemble the graded photos, vector wordmark and final menu render onto a Firefly board deep-link for owner sign-off.
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon flagship: 25 connector calls across 4 tool groups (image_* raster, asset_* stock/storage, document_* InDesign, create_firefly_board), 16 distinct Adobe tools — far above the ≥10 calls / ≥6 distinct-tool floor. Difficulty comes from running four separate photos through a consistent multi-step grade pipeline, branching the hero into a transparent cutout, vectorizing a logo, sourcing+licensing stock, then converging everything into a genuine InDesign [T] data-merge and dual export. It respects every hard limit: no generative fill / object-removal / text-to-image / compositing / upscaling; image_fill_area is unused; the layout is NOT composed by the connector — render_layout/merge_data_layout only EXPORT/bind the client's authored .indd, which is listed as a supplied input; lens_blur is whole-image (no fake masked DOF claim); placeholder copy honored literally via CSV per the brief.

Client-supplied input assets: 7 total · 5 img · 0 video · 0 audio · 2 data

menu_template.indd.proxy.txt
content
**Template Description:**

**File:** `menu_template.indd` (Adobe InDesign Document)

**Type:** One-page A4 portrait menu template designed for Brickyard, featuring a rustic chalkboard aesthetic.

**Structure & Layers:**
*   **Background:** A full-bleed image frame spans the entire A4 page, serving as the base for a chalkboard texture (typically the bottom layer).
*   **Wordmark Area:** A dedicated placeholder frame is positioned for the "Brickyard" wordmark.
*   **Section Headers:** Four distinct text frames are present for menu categories: "Classic Burgers," "Specialty Burgers," "Sides," and "Beverages." These are styled to resemble chalk.
*   **Menu Items:** Under each section header, multiple sets of text frames are arranged vertically. These frames contain live Adobe InDesign data-merge placeholder fields:
    *   `<<section>>`
    *   `<<item>>`
    *   `<<desc>>`
    *   `<<price>>`
    These fields are formatted with a handwritten, chalk-like font, and are ready for data binding.
*   **Image Placeholders:** Four strategically placed image frames are included, each maintaining a 4:5 aspect ratio, intended for product photography.

**Dimensions:**
*   **Page:** A4 portrait (210mm x 297mm).
*   **Image Frames:** Each of the four image frames is sized to a 4:5 aspect ratio.

**Brand Styling:**
*   **Theme:** Rustic chalkboard, characterized by a dark, textured background, and text elements styled to mimic chalk writing (e.g., specific fonts, distressed textures, off-white/light grey color palette).
*   **Typography:** Typefaces are selected to evoke a handwritten, authentic rustic feel.

**Key Feature:** This is an authentically authored Adobe InDesign document (`.indd`) containing genuine, bindable data-merge fields (`<<section>>`, `<<item>>`, `<<desc>>`, `<<price>>`), not static text or converted graphics, ensuring direct compatibility with InDesign's data merge functionality.

AO-15 — Umaya Tri-Fold Menu Refresh — Food Photography Prep, Brand-Cohesive Color Grade, Logo Vectorization & Source Export ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Prep, color-grade, cut out, crop, and print-export the new dish photography, the vectorized logo, the textured background, and the existing Illustrator menu source for Umaya's tri-fold menu refresh, all matched to the dark/red Japanese brand look and ready for the designer to place.

💰 ~$138 est.Upwork21 tool calls20 distinct toolslong-horizon
Full client brief
Umaya is a ramen + sushi restaurant refreshing its existing tri-fold dine-in menu (22.5"x12" unfolded, print-ready CMYK 300 DPI). They are adding several new ramen bowls and an entirely new Wagyu section, and the brand look is fixed: dark background, hero food photography, red accent elements, Japanese ramen/sushi aesthetic. The page layout itself is being hand-typeset by their designer in the existing Adobe Illustrator source; this engagement is the PHOTOGRAPHY + BRAND-ASSET production that feeds that layout. Deliver a clean, cohesive set of placement-ready assets, all chained off the client's supplied raws.\n\nWork to perform with Adobe connectors:\n1. Ingest the client-supplied assets: two newly-shot dish photos (a tonkotsu ramen bowl and the new Wagyu donburi hero), the restaurant's flat logo PNG, and the client's existing authored Illustrator menu source (.ai).\n2. The Wagyu hero was shot slightly off-level — auto-straighten it, then auto-tone it as a neutral starting point.\n3. Pull the whole photo set to ONE cohesive brand look: warm the white balance toward the existing menu's amber/dark feel, deepen the dark portions (the dark-background brand requirement), lift the highlights on the broth/garnish so the food still pops, and push vibrance so the red chili oil / red accents read strong against the dark plate. Both dishes must end up matching.\n4. For the crowded ramen section, the designer needs tight cut-outs: isolate the ramen bowl subject and remove its background to a transparent PNG so it can sit close to type without a hard photo edge; do the same selective treatment so the Wagyu marbling reads (boost just the warm/red tones on the Wagyu via a prompt selection).\n5. Crop each finished shot to the exact aspect ratios the menu cells need at 300 DPI (a tall ramen cell and a wide Wagyu banner).\n6. Vectorize the flat logo PNG to clean SVG for crisp red-accent printing.\n7. Prep the dark textured background plate: take a licensed dark slate/wood stock texture, darken its light portions and add fine grain so it reads as a premium dark menu background and not a flat fill.\n8. Export print-ready PNG renders of the client's existing authored Illustrator menu source so the team has a current proof to drop the new assets into.\n9. Assemble the final delivered set into a Firefly board so the client can review all placement-ready assets in one deep-link, and package the files.\n\nEverything must chain: each step consumes the previous step's output. No layout is composed by the connector — the designer hand-places these assets into the existing .ai. Deliverables: color-graded + brand-matched dish photos, transparent ramen cut-out, print-cropped shots at 300 DPI, vectorized logo SVG, dark textured background plate, print-ready menu-source proof render
Deliverables (9)
  • ramen_brand_graded.png — Tonkotsu ramen bowl, brand-matched look: warmed WB, deepened darks, lifted broth/garnish highlights, vibrance pushed so red chili oil reads. Full-res PNG.
  • wagyu_brand_graded.png — Wagyu donburi hero, auto-straightened + auto-toned + same brand grade as the ramen, with red/warm marbling tones selectively boosted. Full-res PNG.
  • ramen_cutout_transparent.png — Ramen bowl isolated, background removed to full transparency (alpha PNG) for tight placement in the crowded ramen section.
  • ramen_cell_300dpi.png — Ramen shot cropped to the tall menu-cell aspect ratio, sized for 300 DPI print placement.
  • wagyu_banner_300dpi.png — Wagyu hero cropped to the wide banner aspect ratio for the new Wagyu section, sized for 300 DPI print placement.
  • umaya_logo.svg — Vectorized logo as clean SVG, red (#C8302A) + near-black, crisp for print red-accent use.
  • dark_background_plate.png — Licensed dark slate texture darkened in its light portions + fine grain added = premium dark menu background plate. Full-res PNG.
  • menu_source_proof.png — Print-ready PNG render(s) of the client's existing authored Illustrator menu source, exported as a current proof to drop new assets into.
  • umaya_menu_assets_board — Firefly board deep-link assembling all placement-ready delivered assets for client review, plus a packaged asset folder/zip.
Workflow — 21 tool steps
  1. asset_initialize_file_upload — Ingest the client-supplied raws into CC storage: tonkotsu_ramen_bowl_raw.jpg, wagyu_donburi_hero_raw.jpg, umaya_logo_flat.png, umaya_menu_existing.ai. Initial a
  2. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the upload so the raws are addressable presigned assets for the editing chain.
  3. image_auto_straighten — The Wagyu hero was shot ~4deg off-level; level the horizon before any tonal work. Consumes the ingested Wagyu raw.
  4. image_apply_auto_tone — Neutral auto-tone baseline on the straightened Wagyu before the deliberate brand grade.
  5. image_adjust_color_temperature — Warm white balance toward the menu's amber/dark feel on BOTH dishes (ramen raw + auto-toned Wagyu). Start of the cohesive brand grade applied to the photo set.
  6. image_adjust_dark_portions — Deepen the dark portions to satisfy the dark-background brand requirement, on both warmed shots.
  7. image_adjust_highlights — Lift highlights so broth surface / onsen egg / garnish still pop against the deepened darks.
  8. image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation — Push vibrance so the red chili oil and red brand accents read strong on the dark plate. Both dishes now share one cohesive brand look (= ramen_brand_graded + ba
  9. image_select_by_prompt — Prompt-select the seared Wagyu marbling/red meat on the graded Wagyu to drive a selective warm-tone boost.
  10. image_adjust_single_color_saturation — Selectively boost the red/warm tones on the masked Wagyu marbling so it reads rich = final wagyu_brand_graded.
  11. image_select_subject — Select the ramen bowl subject on the graded ramen shot, prepping the transparent cut-out for the crowded ramen section.
  12. image_remove_background — Remove the background to a transparent alpha PNG so the ramen bowl can sit tight to type without a hard photo edge.
  13. image_crop_and_resize — Crop the ramen cut-out to the tall menu-cell aspect ratio at 300 DPI print sizing.
  14. image_crop_and_resize — Crop the finished Wagyu hero to the wide banner aspect ratio for the new Wagyu section at 300 DPI.
  15. image_vectorize — Vectorize the flat logo PNG to clean SVG for crisp red-accent print. Consumes the ingested logo raw.
  16. asset_license_and_download_stock — Search + license + download a dark slate stock texture (license BEFORE editing). Stands in for dark_slate_texture_stock; new asset load for the background-plate
  17. image_adjust_light_portions — Darken the texture's light portions so it reads as a premium dark menu background, not a flat grey.
  18. image_add_grain — Add fine grain to the darkened slate so the background plate has tactile premium texture = dark_background_plate.
  19. document_render_vector — Export the client's EXISTING authored Illustrator menu .ai to print-ready PNG as a current proof. render_vector only EXPORTS the authored source; it composes no
  20. create_firefly_board — Assemble every placement-ready delivered asset into a Firefly board deep-link for client review.
  21. asset_copy_assets — Collect all final deliverables into a single delivery folder in CC storage for hand-off / packaging.
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon and wide-mix: 21 connector calls, 20 distinct Adobe tools, spanning four connector groups (asset/storage, raster photo edit, document export, Firefly board). It exercises a realistic multi-branch production graph — a shared color-grade trunk that splits into a masked selective-saturation branch, a subject-removal cut-out branch, two print-crop branches, a logo-vectorize branch, and a separate stock-license -> texture-prep branch — then fans everything back into a review board and a delivery folder. Difficulty comes from (a) keeping two dishes cohesive through a 4-step grade chain, (b) two different masking strategies (prompt-select for marbling vs subject-select for cut-out) feeding two different downstream edits, (c) honoring print-spec crops at 300 DPI, and (d) correctly using render_vector as an EXPORT of an authored .ai rather than as layout composition. The brief's actual page typesetting is correctly excluded as out-of-connector-scope (layout composition is [X]), which is itself the key feasibility judgment the task tests. All steps are [C] headless-confirmed except none — fully autonomous.

Client-supplied input assets: 5 total · 4 img · 0 video · 0 audio · 1 data

Images (4)

openai

gemini

Data & other (1)

umaya_menu_existing.ai.proxy.txt
content
**Template Description:**

**File:** `umaya_menu_existing.ai`

**Type:** Adobe Illustrator source file for an existing tri-fold restaurant menu.

**Dimensions:** 22.5 x 12 inches (unfolded), comprising 6 distinct panels.

**Structure:** Pre-authored, multi-panel layout designed for tri-fold presentation. Contains dedicated text blocks for menu item names, descriptions, and pricing, plus clearly defined image frames for dish photography.

**Layers/Placeholders:**
*   **Background:** Dark near-black base layer.
*   **Text Placeholders:** Editable text areas for menu item titles, descriptions, and prices.
*   **Image Placeholders:** Designated rectangular frames for dish photos.
*   **Section Headers:** Distinct text objects for menu categories.
*   **Accent Rules:** Vector lines for visual separation.

**Brand Styling:**
*   **Color Palette:** Predominantly dark near-black background with striking red accent rules and section headers.
*   **Aesthetic:** Strong Japanese ramen/sushi theme, reflecting traditional design elements.
*   **Typography:** (Implied by aesthetic) Likely a mix of legible serif/sans-serif for body text and a more thematic display font for headers.

**Content Sections:** 'Small Plates', 'Sushi Bowls', 'Artisan Ramen', 'Donburi Rice Bowls', 'Sushi Rolls', 'Nigiri-Sashimi', 'Kids', 'Desserts'.

**Source Note:** This is a client-provided, fully authored Illustrator file, not a connector-generated template. It is intended for direct export via `render_vector`.

AO-17 — Dark-Mode Restaurant Website Hero Food Photography Pack ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Retouch a restaurant's raw plated-dish photos into a cohesive dark-mode hero-image pack (hero + menu tiles + mobile crop) and assemble a Firefly moodboard for the agency's web designer.

💰 ~$138 est.Upwork22 tool calls15 distinct toolslong-horizon
Full client brief
We are a Portugal-based boutique digital agency building a premium restaurant website. The design is high-end, clean, modern-minimalist, Dark Mode aesthetic, and focuses heavily on high-quality food photography across the Home and Interactive Food Menu sections, fully optimized for mobile. We do NOT need the site built here — our Wix Studio designer handles layout. We need a food-photo retoucher to turn the restaurant's raw plate shots into a polished, cohesive dark-mode hero-image pack.

Supplied raw assets: (1) a hero shot of a signature seared-scallop dish on a dark slate plate, shot slightly tilted under warm mixed restaurant lighting; (2) a flat-lay of a chocolate dessert on a marble board; (3) a close-up of a cocktail with garnish; plus the restaurant's existing flat wordmark logo as a PNG.

Deliverables:
1. A polished, perfectly level dark-mode HERO image — straightened, auto-toned, exposure/contrast set for a moody dark background, the plate subject made to "pop" off the dark surround, cooler-warmer balance dialed to a premium look, then reframed to the 16:9 hero size by cropping the plate off-center to the right so the dish's own near-black charcoal surround gives the web designer clean dark negative space on the left for an overlaid headline (edges cropped to hit the ratio). Export-ready full-bleed desktop hero.
2. Two square MENU-TILE images (dessert + cocktail) graded to match the hero's dark, moody, consistent look so the Interactive Food Menu feels cohesive.
3. One full-bleed 9:16 MOBILE crop of the hero, subject-aware, for the mobile Home section.
4. The flat wordmark logo cleaned to a transparent PNG and vectorized to crisp SVG for retina/responsive use on the dark site.
5. A Firefly Boards moodboard collecting the final hero, menu tiles, and logo as a single deep-link the Wix designer can reference for the dark-mode visual direction.

The whole set must read as one premium, dark, cohesive system. Match grade across all food images. Keep skin/food tones appetizing — dark mode, not muddy.
Deliverables (6)
  • hero_dark_desktop.png — Full-bleed desktop website hero, ~2880x1620 (16:9), straightened, dark-mode graded, subject popped off a moody dark surround, reframed by cropping the native 3:2 to 16:9 with the plate offset right so
  • menu_tile_dessert.png — Square 1:1 menu-tile image (~1200x1200), graded to MATCH the hero's dark moody look, sRGB PNG.
  • menu_tile_cocktail.png — Square 1:1 menu-tile image (~1200x1200), graded to MATCH the hero's dark moody look, sRGB PNG.
  • hero_mobile_9x16.png — Full-bleed mobile Home crop, 1080x1920 (9:16), subject-aware crop of the graded hero, sRGB PNG.
  • logo_wordmark.svg — Transparent, cleanly vectorized wordmark logo as crisp SVG for retina/responsive use on the dark site (plus the transparent-PNG intermediate).
  • moodboard_deeplink — Firefly Boards deep-link URL assembling the final hero, both menu tiles, and the logo as one dark-mode visual-direction reference for the Wix designer.
Workflow — 22 tool steps
  1. asset_initialize_file_upload — Client supplies raw_hero_scallops.jpg. Initialize the upload session for the raw hero plate shot to bring it into CC storage so the image pipeline can act on it
  2. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the upload from step 1's session to get a stored CC asset/URI for the raw hero. This is the loaded source for the hero chain.
  3. image_auto_straighten — Level the ~4deg tilted horizon on the finalized hero asset. Consumes step 2's output.
  4. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the straightened hero to fix the flat/underexposed raw capture. Consumes step 3.
  5. image_adjust_brightness_and_contrast — Pull the background down and add contrast on the auto-toned hero to establish the dark-mode moody base look. Consumes step 4.
  6. image_adjust_color_temperature — Dial the warm tungsten cast to the premium target balance on the dark-graded hero. This graded hero becomes the reference look that the menu tiles must match. C
  7. image_select_subject — Select the scallop plate subject on the graded hero to drive a selective pop. Consumes step 6 and produces a mask URI.
  8. image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation — Apply vibrance/saturation to the graded hero using step 7's subject mask so the plate pops off the dark surround (selective masked edit). Consumes the step-6 im
  9. image_crop_and_resize — Reframe the popped 3:2 hero to a wider 16:9 by cropping with the scallop plate offset to the RIGHT so the existing dark charcoal surround on the LEFT becomes cl
  10. image_crop_and_resize — Resize the reframed 16:9 hero to the ~2880x1620 desktop full-bleed export dimensions. DELIVERABLE hero_dark_desktop.png. Consumes step 9.
  11. image_crop_to_bounds — Subject-aware crop of the finished desktop hero to a 9:16 1080x1920 mobile full-bleed for the mobile Home section. DELIVERABLE hero_mobile_9x16.png. Consumes st
  12. asset_add_file — Client supplies raw_dessert_flatlay.jpg. Bring the raw dessert flat-lay into CC storage to start the first menu tile.
  13. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the dessert raw to normalize its brighter/cooler daylight capture before matching. Consumes step 12.
  14. image_adjust_brightness_and_contrast — Pull down and add contrast to match the hero's dark moody base (same grade as step 5) so the dessert tile reads cohesively. Consumes step 13.
  15. image_adjust_color_temperature — Warm the cool dessert toward the hero's temperature target to finish grade-matching; output is the square menu tile. DELIVERABLE menu_tile_dessert.png. Consumes
  16. asset_add_file — Client supplies raw_cocktail_closeup.jpg. Bring the raw cocktail close-up into CC storage to start the second menu tile.
  17. image_apply_preset — Apply a dark/moody Lightroom preset (from list_presets) to the cocktail raw to bring it toward the hero's grade in one consistent move. Consumes step 16.
  18. image_adjust_color_temperature — Nudge the cocktail temperature to lock it to the hero/dessert balance so all three food images match; output is the square menu tile. DELIVERABLE menu_tile_cock
  19. asset_add_file — Client supplies restaurant_wordmark_logo.png. Bring the flat wordmark logo into CC storage for cleanup and vectorization.
  20. image_remove_background — Drop the flat white background to a transparent PNG so the wordmark sits cleanly on the dark site. Consumes step 19.
  21. image_vectorize — Vectorize the transparent wordmark to crisp SVG for retina/responsive use. DELIVERABLE logo_wordmark.svg. Consumes step 20.
  22. create_firefly_board — Assemble the final hero, both graded menu tiles, and the vectorized logo into a Firefly Boards moodboard deep-link as the dark-mode visual-direction reference f
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon flagship: 22 connector calls, 15 distinct Adobe tools, spanning 2 capability groups (A1 raster, A4 asset/board). It is a genuine food_bev_photo retouch job — the hardest, most realistic part of the long-tail of the actual brief. Difficulty comes from (a) a 10-step single-image hero pipeline including a masked selective edit (select_subject to vibrance on the mask) and a two-stage gen-free reframe (crop 3:2 to 16:9 with the subject offset so the existing dark surround becomes left headline negative space, then resize to the export dims), (b) grade-MATCHING across three differently-lit raw captures so the menu reads cohesively (the core 'cohesive dark-mode system' requirement), and (c) a converging dependency graph where four sub-chains must all complete before the moodboard step. Every step is headless-confirmed [C]: no [W] Express widget, no [A] async video/audio, no [T] authored template, no Canva, no local step. No not-available capability is used — no generative-expand/outpaint, no image_fill_area misuse, no compositing/gen-fill/object-removal/upscale/text-to-image, and no layout composition (no render_layout/render_vector as authoring). The web build itself is correctly out of scope and excluded.

Client-supplied input assets: 4 total · 4 img · 0 video · 0 audio · 0 data

AO-18 — Premium Dark-Mode Restaurant Food-Photography Asset Suite (Hero, Menu Tiles, Logo Vector) ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

From the client's raw food photos and logo, produce a dark-mode-ready food-photography asset suite — a color-graded wide hero banner, clean-backdrop menu tiles, a mobile crop, and a vector logo — for a premium restaurant website.

💰 ~$138 est.Upwork26 tool calls14 distinct toolslong-horizon
Full client brief
We are a boutique digital agency building a premium, dark-mode restaurant website (Home, Interactive Food Menu, Contact/Booking) that focuses heavily on high-quality food photography and must be perfectly optimized for mobile. We are NOT asking you to build the site here — we have a Framer developer for that. What we need from YOU is the polished food-photography asset suite the design hangs on, all prepared from our raw shoot so it drops straight into the dark aesthetic.

Deliverables, all derived from the assets we supply:
1) HERO BANNER — take our raw hero shot (a signature octopus dish, slightly crooked off the handheld shoot) and turn it into a wide, cinematic, dark-mode hero: straighten it, apply a cohesive warm-but-moody grade so it reads premium on a near-black background, then crop it to a full ultra-wide 21:9 banner ratio (a cinematic letterbox reframe of the existing wide shot) so our developer has clean headline space. Mobile-optimized vertical crop of the same hero, too.
2) MENU TILES — for two menu items (the octopus and a steak plate), isolate the plated dish onto a clean, uniform near-black backdrop (so all menu tiles match), color-match them to the hero grade, and deliver square tiles.
3) LOGO MARK — our logo is supplied as a flat raster; we need a crisp vector version (SVG) for retina/responsive use across the dark site.

House look: warm highlights, deep controlled shadows, rich but not cartoonish saturation, consistent across every tile so the menu grid feels like one shoot. Keep everything cohesive — the whole point of the dark-mode design is that the photography carries it.
Deliverables (6)
  • hero_banner_ultrawide.png — 21:9 ultra-wide dark-mode hero banner, straightened + warm-moody graded, then cinematic letterbox reframe cropped from the existing wide 3:2 source (no invented/extended pixels), ~3440x1440 PNG, ready
  • hero_mobile_vertical.jpg — Mobile hero crop of the same graded hero, 4:5 portrait ~1080x1350 JPEG, subject-safe framing
  • menu_tile_octopus.jpg — Square menu tile, plated octopus isolated on uniform near-black (#0B0B0D) backdrop, grade-matched, ~1200x1200 JPEG
  • menu_tile_steak.jpg — Square menu tile, plated steak isolated on the SAME near-black (#0B0B0D) backdrop, color-matched to hero grade, ~1200x1200 JPEG
  • logo_mark.svg — Clean 2-color vector logo mark (monoline octopus + wordmark), scalable SVG for retina/responsive use
  • restaurant_photo_suite.zip — Single zip bundling all five deliverables in a /hero, /menu, /logo folder structure for handoff
Workflow — 26 tool steps
  1. asset_initialize_file_upload — Begin upload of the client-supplied raw hero shot (hero_octopus_raw.jpg) into CC storage so subsequent edits have a real asset URI. Initial asset load (no input
  2. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the hero upload; yields the canonical hero asset URI used by the whole hero chain.
  3. image_auto_straighten — Level the ~4deg crooked horizon from the handheld shoot before grading.
  4. image_apply_auto_tone — Neutral baseline tone/exposure normalization on the straightened hero before the deliberate warm-moody look.
  5. image_adjust_color_temperature — Push warmth into the highlights to set the premium warm house look (establishes the reference grade the menu tiles will match).
  6. image_adjust_dark_portions — Deepen and control the shadows for the near-black dark-mode aesthetic; this is the locked HERO GRADE reference.
  7. image_crop_and_resize — Reframe (NOT generate): crop the graded 4000x2667 3:2 hero down to a 21:9 ultra-wide slice using its existing pixels — the wide source has ample horizontal room
  8. image_crop_and_resize — Resize the 21:9 reframed hero to the exact 3440x1440 (21:9) PNG = hero_banner_ultrawide deliverable.
  9. image_crop_and_resize — Re-crop the SAME locked hero grade to a 4:5 ~1080x1350 mobile-portrait deliverable (reuses step 6 output, keeps the look identical to the banner).
  10. asset_initialize_file_upload — Begin upload of the client steak plate (steak_plate_raw.jpg). Second initial asset load.
  11. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize steak upload; yields the steak asset URI.
  12. image_select_by_prompt — Prompt-select the plated steak-on-slate subject to separate it from the light marble background.
  13. image_invert_selection — Invert the subject mask to target the background region for the uniform backdrop fill.
  14. image_fill_area — Solid-fill the inverted background with near-black #0B0B0D (solid color only — sanctioned use) so the steak tile matches the dark menu grid.
  15. image_adjust_color_temperature — Warm the steak to match the hero house grade (corrects its tungsten cast so the two menu tiles read as one shoot).
  16. image_crop_and_resize — Crop/resize the matched steak to a square ~1200x1200 JPEG = menu_tile_steak deliverable.
  17. image_select_subject — Auto-select the plated octopus subject from the already-graded hero (reuses step 6) to build the matching octopus menu tile on the same backdrop.
  18. image_invert_selection — Invert to target the octopus background for the identical near-black fill.
  19. image_fill_area — Solid-fill the octopus background with the SAME #0B0B0D so both menu tiles share an identical backdrop.
  20. image_crop_and_resize — Crop/resize to square ~1200x1200 JPEG = menu_tile_octopus deliverable (already on the locked hero grade, so it matches the steak tile).
  21. asset_initialize_file_upload — Begin upload of the flat raster logo (restaurant_logo_flat.png). Third initial asset load.
  22. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize logo upload; yields the raster logo asset URI.
  23. image_vectorize — Raster->clean SVG vectorize of the 2-color monoline logo = logo_mark.svg deliverable for retina/responsive use.
  24. asset_create_folders — Create /hero, /menu, /logo handoff folders in CC storage to organize the five finished deliverables for the dev.
  25. asset_copy_assets — Copy each finished deliverable into its handoff folder so the suite is cleanly organized.
  26. zip_outputs — Single unavoidable [L] packaging step: zip the organized /hero,/menu,/logo folders into restaurant_photo_suite.zip for one-file handoff. Not core work.
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon and wide-mix: 26 steps, 14 distinct Adobe connector tools (>=10 calls and >=6 distinct, well clear), spanning three connector groups — image_* (raster/grade/select/fill/vectorize/geometry), asset_* (upload/folders/copy), and a single local zip. Genuine output->input chaining with three real reuse points of the locked hero grade (banner, mobile, octopus tile), which is what makes it 'wide-mix' rather than a linear pipeline. Strictly inside the capability sheet: ZERO generative steps — the 21:9 hero reframe is a true image_crop_and_resize letterbox crop of the existing wide 3:2 pixels (downscaled to 3440x1440, never larger than source, no invented content); image_fill_area is used for solid near-black backdrop only (no gen-fill, no object removal, no compositing); selective warmth via adjust tools; image_vectorize is a legitimate raster->SVG trace; no layout COMPOSITION is claimed (document_render_* deliberately not used since there is no authored .indd/.ai). The out-of-scope half of the original gig (Wix/Framer site build) is explicitly excluded, keeping the task honestly Adobe-doable end to end as [C] (+1 [L]).

Client-supplied input assets: 3 total · 3 img · 0 video · 0 audio · 0 data

AO-20 — Premium corporate-website photo retouch: grade, declutter, and export a consistent responsive image set ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Take two client-shot raw corporate images (a plant-floor engineer-by-equipment hero and an executive portrait), professionally retouch and color-grade them to one consistent premium look, declutter the busy backgrounds the allowed way, then export a web-optimized responsive set (desktop/tablet/mobile) plus a Firefly board for sign-off.

💰 ~$145 est.Freelancer.com20 tool calls14 distinct toolslong-horizon
Full client brief
We are launching a new corporate website for our industrial / CNC-machining business and need a Photoshop expert to professionally retouch a set of images so they look premium, modern, and consistent. Attached are two client-shot RAW-ish JPEGs straight off the camera: (1) HERO — a process engineer standing beside a CNC machining centre on the plant floor, shot under mixed fluorescent + window light so it is slightly underexposed, cool/green-cast, and the background is cluttered with parts bins, cables and a fire extinguisher; (2) a paired executive half-body portrait shot in the same facility, warmer but flat and low-contrast.\n\nWe need you to: correct the lighting imbalance and exposure on the hero; pull the white balance neutral and add real contrast, color and sharpness so it reads premium; clean up the distracting background clutter behind the engineer (we don't want a fake studio look — keep it authentic, just calm it down) by selecting the subject, inverting to a background-only region and laying a neutral fill over the clutter, then give the whole frame a gentle lens-blur soft-focus so it feels like a shallow-depth premium shot (the subject stays clean because the clutter around it was filled, not erased with AI); then carry the EXACT same finished look onto the executive portrait so the two images are visually consistent as a set. Once the look is locked, prepare web-optimized, responsive exports of the hero at desktop (1920px wide), tablet (1024px) and mobile (640px) sizes, all centred on the subject and fast-loading. Finally, collect the graded hero, the matching portrait and the three responsive crops into a single review board so our marketing lead can approve the style in one click before we roll it across the rest of the site library. Keep everything natural and realistic — this is a real factory, not a render.
Deliverables (7)
  • hero_graded_master.png — Full-resolution retouched, color-graded and preset-locked hero master (this is the hero_look_locked URN that fans out to the crops, board and zip): exposure/WB corrected, contrast+color+sharpness enha
  • portrait_graded_master.png — Executive portrait carrying the IDENTICAL finished look (same preset) as the hero so the pair reads as one consistent set. ~6000x4000px PNG.
  • hero_desktop_1920.png — Web-optimized desktop hero crop centred on the subject, 1920px wide (16:9), from the graded master.
  • hero_tablet_1024.png — Responsive tablet crop, 1024px wide, subject-centred, from the graded master.
  • hero_mobile_640.png — Responsive mobile crop, 640px wide, subject-centred, fast-loading, from the graded master.
  • corporate_retouch_review_board — Firefly Board deep-link assembling the graded hero, the matching portrait and the three responsive crops for one-click marketing sign-off.
  • corporate_website_image_set.zip — Single ZIP bundling the two graded masters and the three responsive crops for handoff to the web team.
Workflow — 20 tool steps
  1. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize the upload of the client's raw hero image (hero_engineer_cnc_raw.jpg) into Adobe storage to get a presigned target.
  2. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the upload, yielding a stored, addressable hero raw asset URN used by every downstream edit.
  3. image_apply_auto_tone — First pass at the lighting imbalance: auto-tone the underexposed hero to recover a sane global exposure/contrast baseline before targeted fixes.
  4. image_adjust_color_temperature — Pull the cool/green mixed-lighting cast back to neutral white balance ('correct lighting imbalances').
  5. image_adjust_brightness_and_contrast — Add real contrast and punch so the flat factory shot reads premium ('enhance contrast and sharpness').
  6. image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation — Enhance color while protecting skin tones ('enhance color'), completing the tonal grade on the hero.
  7. image_select_subject — Select the engineer so the background can be treated without touching the subject (foundation for the declutter).
  8. image_invert_selection — Invert to a background-only mask covering the cluttered plant-floor backdrop.
  9. image_fill_area — SOLID-FILL only: lay a neutral mid-gray over the cluttered backdrop region (defined by the inverted background-only mask) to calm the distracting bins/cables/ex
  10. image_apply_lens_blur — Whole-image lens-blur soft-focus finishing pass over the already-decluttered frame (this connector applies to the whole image, not a masked region) to give the
  11. image_list_presets — List available Lightroom presets to choose the single shared 'corporate premium' look that will lock cross-image consistency.
  12. image_apply_preset — Apply the chosen preset to the graded+decluttered+soft-focused hero to define the canonical finished look and produce hero_graded_master, the deliverable that f
  13. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize upload of the second client asset, the executive portrait (exec_portrait_facility_raw.jpg).
  14. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the portrait upload into a stored asset URN.
  15. image_apply_preset — Apply the SAME preset chosen at step 12 to the warm/flat portrait so it matches the hero — the 'consistent visual style across all images' requirement, and a re
  16. image_crop_and_resize — Subject-centred web-optimized desktop crop of the graded hero master at 1920px wide (16:9).
  17. image_crop_and_resize — Responsive tablet export at 1024px wide from the same graded hero master.
  18. image_crop_and_resize — Responsive mobile export at 640px wide, subject-centred and fast-loading, completing desktop/tablet/mobile coverage.
  19. create_firefly_board — Assemble the graded hero master, matching portrait and three responsive crops into one Firefly Board for one-click marketing sign-off (presents assets already p
  20. zip_outputs — Single unavoidable local packaging step: zip the two masters and three responsive crops for handoff to the web team.
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon and wide-mix: 20 steps, 14 distinct Adobe tools (well above the ≥10 calls / ≥6 distinct-tool floor), spanning asset I/O, a multi-stage tonal/color grade, mask-based selective editing, preset-driven cross-image consistency, responsive multi-size export, and board assembly. Difficulty comes from (a) the multi-branch dependency graph — a linear grade, a select→invert→fill→blur declutter branch reusing an intermediate, and a preset that must be captured once and reused on a second, differently-toned image; (b) capability discipline: the brief literally says 'remove unwanted objects', which a naive agent would attempt via forbidden AI object-removal — the honest solution routes it through select_subject→invert→solid fill_area→lens_blur, the only allowed path; (c) consistency is a genuine test because the portrait starts warm/flat while the hero starts cool/underexposed, so a shared preset must actually unify two different starting points; (d) the 1920/1024/640 responsive fan-out and final convergence into a single board + zip. All connector work is [C] headless-confirmed with a single permitted [L] zip at the end.

Client-supplied input assets: 2 total · 2 img · 0 video · 0 audio · 0 data

AO-21 — Creative Business Headshot — Studio Retouch, Color Grade & Brand-Profile Asset Pipeline ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Take the client's raw studio headshot frame and brand monogram and produce a retouched, color-graded, creatively recomposed professional-profile headshot set (color + monochrome + 16:9 dynamic + LinkedIn 1:1 + banner crops) plus a clean vector logomark, all delivered in one folder.

💰 ~$90 est.Freelancer.com20 tool calls16 distinct toolslong-horizon
Full client brief
I'm a founder who needs a creative, dynamic headshot for my professional business profile (LinkedIn + website "About"). My photographer shot me in-studio against a seamless mid-gray backdrop but only gave me the flat, un-retouched RAW-export JPEG plus my simple wordmark monogram as a PNG — I need the actual finished, polished delivery set.

Please, working only from the files I provide:
1. Straighten and clean up the frame, then apply a professional studio tone so the exposure and contrast look intentional, not flat.
2. Warm the skin tone slightly (studio strobes left it a touch cool) and give the whole image a cohesive graded look — I want it to read "premium business portrait," not phone snapshot.
3. Do tasteful, believable retouching: brighten my eyes and teeth a little using selective adjustments (no plastic skin, no face reshaping).
4. Make it feel "dynamic": separate me from the background with a soft blur behind the subject, and reframe the shot into a wider 16:9 environmental crop (using the empty backdrop room on one side) so it works as a hero image with breathing room for text.
5. Give me a tasteful monochrome alternate of the final color portrait for places that want B&W.
6. Produce the profile crops I'll actually upload: a 1:1 LinkedIn/avatar crop and a 1584x396 LinkedIn banner crop, both off the finished color portrait.
7. Vectorize my flat monogram PNG into a clean SVG logomark so it stays crisp on print and large web.
8. Put everything — color hero, mono alt, 1:1, banner, vector logo — into one delivery folder and give me a preview.

Deliverable: a print/web-ready professional headshot package for my business profile.
Deliverables (6)
  • headshot_hero_color_16x9.jpg — Final retouched + color-graded creative headshot, cropped/reframed (no pixels invented) to a 16:9 environmental hero (e.g. 2560x1440) by trimming the tall studio frame down to landscape while preservi
  • headshot_hero_mono.jpg — Monochrome (monochromatic-tint) alternate of the final color hero, same 16:9 framing. JPEG, sRGB.
  • headshot_linkedin_1x1.jpg — 1:1 avatar/profile crop (e.g. 1000x1000) derived from the finished color portrait, subject-centered. JPEG, sRGB.
  • headshot_linkedin_banner_1584x396.jpg — 1584x396 LinkedIn banner crop derived from the finished color portrait/hero, subject offset with text room. JPEG, sRGB.
  • monogram_logomark.svg — Clean vectorized SVG of the 'AV' ring monogram, single-color paths, scalable for print + large web.
  • Headshot_Delivery/ (folder + preview) — One CC asset folder containing all of the above, plus an inline preview of the delivery set for client review.
Workflow — 20 tool steps
  1. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize upload of the client-supplied studio_headshot_raw.jpg into CC storage (returns upload target/asset handle).
  2. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the upload; yields the canonical CC asset URI for the raw studio frame that the edit chain will consume.
  3. image_auto_straighten — Correct the ~1.5-degree tilt in the raw capture before any tonal work.
  4. image_apply_auto_tone — Apply a professional baseline studio tone (exposure/contrast) so the flat RAW export looks intentional.
  5. image_adjust_color_temperature — Warm the cool strobe white balance slightly for a premium skin tone.
  6. image_select_by_prompt — Select eyes + teeth by prompt to drive selective, believable brightening (no skin/face reshaping).
  7. image_adjust_light_portions — Lift the light portions only within the eyes/teeth mask — tasteful selective brightening; passes the warmed image plus the step-6 mask.
  8. image_select_subject — Select the subject so the background can be isolated for separation blur.
  9. image_invert_selection — Invert to get a background-only mask.
  10. image_apply_gaussian_blur — Soft blur applied through the background mask only — creates the 'dynamic' subject-from-backdrop separation without touching the subject.
  11. image_crop_and_resize — Reframe/crop the tall portrait into a wider 16:9 landscape hero: keep full frame width and trim the excess vertical from the 4000x5000 frame to a 16:9 window (~
  12. image_apply_monochromatic_tint — Produce the tasteful B&W/monochrome alternate of the finished color hero.
  13. image_crop_and_resize — Derive the 1:1 ~1000x1000 avatar crop from the finished color hero, subject-centered.
  14. image_crop_and_resize — Derive the 1584x396 LinkedIn banner crop from the finished color hero, subject offset for text room.
  15. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize upload of the client's flat monogram PNG so it can be vectorized.
  16. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize; yields the CC asset URI for the flat monogram PNG.
  17. image_vectorize — Trace the flat solid-color monogram into a clean scalable SVG logomark.
  18. asset_create_folders — Create the Headshot_Delivery/ folder in CC storage to hold the final set.
  19. asset_copy_assets — Copy all five final deliverables into the delivery folder.
  20. asset_inline_preview — Render an inline preview of the delivery set for client review.
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon and wide-mix: 20 connector calls, 16 distinct Adobe tools, spanning two capability groups (A1 raster/tonal/selection/FX/crop/vector + A4 asset/storage). It exercises the hardest-to-sequence pattern — mask-driven SELECTIVE edits (select_by_prompt → adjust_light_portions for eyes/teeth; select_subject → invert → gaussian_blur for background-only separation) — which requires correctly threading a mask output into a later edit step rather than applying it globally. It reframes the tall studio master into the 16:9 landscape hero purely by cropping (image_crop_and_resize, no pixels invented), then fans that single master out into four derived deliverables, plus an independent vectorize sub-chain, then storage/folder/preview packaging. No prohibited capabilities: no generative expand/outpaint, no gen-fill, no AI object removal, no text-to-image, no compositing, no bg-replace-by-prompt, no upscaling, no layout composition; fill_area is never used; all edits are real Photoshop/Lightroom-class operations. Every step is [C] headless-confirmed, so it runs end-to-end autonomously, raising the difficulty bar (no human gallery pick to lean on).

Client-supplied input assets: 2 total · 2 img · 0 video · 0 audio · 0 data

AO-22 — Production-finish a modern vibrant abstract logo: clean, color-tune, vectorize, and ship full-colour + single-colour + print variants with a style note ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Take my approved raster abstract logo concept and produce the full hand-off package — cleaned and brightened full-colour vector master (AI/SVG), a single-colour version, a single-colour print/screen variant, a wide social-header lockup, high-res PNG/JPEG exports, and a style note with hex/RGB values and recommended typography.

💰 ~$120 est.Freelancer.com15 tool calls15 distinct tools
Full client brief
I'm refreshing my brand identity and we've already landed on the strongest concept direction together: a bright, energetic, clean abstract mark — modern, no literal illustrations, no vintage flourishes. I'm handing you the approved raster rendering of that mark (PNG with some edge fringing and a slightly off-square crop from the concept render) plus a small swatch/reference image of the exact brand colours I want to lock to. Please turn this into the final production package.\n\nConcretely: (1) straighten and tightly crop the mark so it sits square and centered; (2) normalize the tone, then push the colours so they read as bright and energetic — lift vibrance/saturation and tune the individual hues until they hit the brand harmony, not muddy; (3) knock out the background to clean transparency so the mark drops onto any header, packaging, or profile photo; (4) from that clean mark, produce the FULL-COLOUR VECTOR MASTER (AI/SVG source) so it stays razor-sharp at any size; (5) produce the SINGLE-COLOUR version (one flat brand colour on transparent) for stamps, embossing and one-ink contexts, by selecting the mark and laying a single flat brand ink over it; (6) produce a single-colour PRINT/SCREEN variant (halftone/duotone) for low-ink packaging and merch screen-printing; (7) produce a wide website/social-header crop by reframing the clean full-colour master to a wide banner aspect (cropping to a wide horizontal slice of the mark) so it can sit as a header strip; (8) export everything as high-resolution PNG and JPEG; and (9) give me a simple style note: the exact hex/RGB values pulled from the locked palette plus a recommended typography pairing that suits this modern, energetic personality. Bundle the finished full-colour, single-colour, halftone, vector and header-crop deliverables together so I can review them in one place.
Deliverables (8)
  • logo_fullcolour_master.svg — Full-colour vector master of the cleaned, brightened, background-removed abstract mark. Produced by image_vectorize from the finished raster. Razor-sharp at any size; this is the AI/SVG source file th
  • logo_fullcolour_master.png — High-resolution transparent PNG of the full-colour mark, exported from the cleaned raster (post background-removal). >=2000 px on the long edge, alpha transparency, brand-accurate brightened colours.
  • logo_fullcolour_master.jpg — High-resolution JPEG of the full-colour mark, exported from the same cleaned master raster. >=2000 px on the long edge, sRGB, flattened onto the brand off-white (#F4F2EC) since JPEG has no alpha. This
  • logo_singlecolour.png — Single-colour (one flat brand ink, magenta #E6007E) version of the mark on transparency, produced by selecting the mark subject and laying a flat colour overlay over the selection (image_select_subjec
  • logo_singlecolour_halftone_print.png — Single-colour PRINT/SCREEN variant: halftone/duotone treatment of the mark for low-ink packaging and merch screen-printing. Crisp dot pattern, one ink.
  • logo_social_header_crop.png — Wide website/social-header crop: the clean full-colour master REFRAMED to a wide banner aspect (approx 3:1) by cropping to a wide horizontal slice of the mark via image_crop_and_resize (gen-free crop;
  • logo_social_header_crop.jpg — JPEG copy of the wide website/social-header crop, exported from the same reframed wide slice. sRGB, flattened onto the brand off-white (#F4F2EC) since JPEG has no alpha. This is the JPEG half of the h
  • brand_style_note.txt/board — Simple style note: exact hex/RGB values for the locked palette (#E6007E, #FF5A36, #00C2D1, #16161D, #F4F2EC) plus a recommended typography pairing (from font_recommend) suited to a modern energetic br
Workflow — 15 tool steps
  1. asset_initialize_file_upload — Begin upload of the client's approved raster mark to CC storage; returns the upload target/presigned slot.
  2. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the upload into a working CC asset (the raster the whole chain edits).
  3. image_auto_straighten — Correct the ~1.5deg tilt from the concept render so the mark sits perfectly level.
  4. image_crop_to_bounds — Trim the uneven margin to a tight, centered, square bound around the mark.
  5. image_apply_auto_tone — Baseline tonal normalization before the colour push so the brightening is even.
  6. image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation — Lift vibrance/saturation to deliver the brief's 'bright, energetic colours'.
  7. image_adjust_hsl — Tune individual hues toward the locked palette harmony (magenta/coral/cyan) so colours read clean, not muddy. Palette swatch is the colour reference.
  8. image_remove_background — Knock out the grey background + edge fringe to clean transparency. This is the master full-colour raster (also the high-res PNG export).
  9. image_vectorize — Convert the clean full-colour mark to a sharp SVG/AI vector master — the contracted vector source file.
  10. image_select_subject — Select the mark shapes so a single flat ink can be laid over exactly the subject for the single-colour version.
  11. image_apply_color_overlay — Lay one flat brand ink (magenta #E6007E) over the selected mark subject on transparency -> single-colour version. Flat recolour of the selected subject, no gene
  12. image_apply_halftone — Apply a halftone/duotone screen to the single-colour mark -> low-ink packaging / merch screen-print variant.
  13. image_crop_and_resize — Reframe the clean full-colour master to a wide website/social-header aspect (approx 3:1) by cropping to a wide horizontal slice of the mark. Gen-free crop only
  14. font_recommend — Recommend a typography pairing matching the modern, energetic personality for the style note.
  15. create_firefly_board — Assemble the full-colour vector + PNG, single-colour, halftone, and wide header crop into one Firefly board so the client reviews the whole package (with palett
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon flagship: 15 connector calls, 15 DISTINCT Adobe tools, spanning four tool groups (asset_* upload, the image_* raster/tonal/colour/mask/vector/effect family, font_recommend, create_firefly_board) with a genuine output->input dependency graph that fans a single cleaned master into five distinct contracted deliverables. It exercises ordering judgment (straighten/crop/tone BEFORE colour push BEFORE background removal; vectorize only AFTER the mark is clean; single-colour version via select_subject->apply_color_overlay before halftone; halftone only on the single-colour output). Every step is headless-confirmed [C] — no [W]/[A]/[T]/[X] capabilities are used: there is NO generative step at all. There is no from-scratch art, no gen-fill, no outpaint/expand, no generative canvas extension, no solid-area-fill step, no compositing onto a new/larger canvas, no layout composition, no Canva, and no Express widget track. The wide social-header deliverable is an honest gen-free reframe via image_crop_and_resize (crop to a wide horizontal slice of the existing master; edges are cropped to hit the ratio, the canvas is not extended). Difficulty also comes from correctly REFUSING the brief's literal from-scratch 'concept sketch' step (not available) and re-scoping to the production-and-finishing work the connector can actually deliver while still hitting every line item of the contra
