AO-23 — Teaser, Thumbnail and End-Card Content Package for a Luxury Manhattan Real-Estate YouTube Channel ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Produce the full per-video content package for one luxury Manhattan real-estate YouTube video — a retouched high-contrast thumbnail base plate, a podcast-style teaser cut (YouTube + LinkedIn sizes) with cleaned voice-over, and a branded end card — all from the broker's supplied portrait, penthouse still, raw walkthrough footage, end-card backplate, and wordmark logo.

💰 ~$200 est.Upwork18 tool calls17 distinct toolslong-horizon
Full client brief
A top-producing Manhattan luxury real-estate broker runs a YouTube channel (market insights, property walkthroughs, deal-level expertise) at 3-4 videos/month and needs the full content package built around each video, with luxury-brand restraint and a 48-72h turnaround. For THIS video — a 12-minute Tribeca penthouse walkthrough that opens with a market-insight monologue — deliver three things.

THUMBNAIL: a high-contrast, click-worthy, on-brand thumbnail base plate at 1280x720, NOT a generic headshot with a text box. Start from the broker's supplied studio portrait and the penthouse hero still. Straighten and auto-tone the portrait, isolate the broker from the background so the figure reads cleanly, and grade the penthouse hero into a moody high-contrast luxury look (deepened shadows, controlled highlights, warm prestige temperature, punchy local contrast). Crop/resize the hero to the 16:9 thumbnail frame and, since the wide-angle source still has room to spare, crop the graded hero to the exact 1280x720 thumbnail bounds so it fills the full frame without stretching. Also vectorize the broker's wordmark logo to a clean SVG for crisp placement at any size. The on-thumbnail HEADLINE TEXT is the single step the image connector cannot do (it cannot set type), so it is finished in one local packaging step over the delivered base plate.

TEASER: from the raw 1080p walkthrough footage (which carries the broker's lavalier voice-over), first summarize the footage to locate the single most compelling 60-90 seconds for a podcast-cold-open hook; produce an AI highlight cut to that target duration that hooks in the first 5 seconds and hard-cuts before the resolution; reframe that cut to a 1080x1080 square for LinkedIn cross-posting (same length, no trim); and clean/denoise the broker's voice-over so the spoken track is broadcast-clean. Deliver the YouTube-native teaser and the LinkedIn square, with prestige tone and no cheap transitions.

END CARD: from the client's pre-shot branded end-card backplate footage (10-15s), reframe it to the exact 1920x1080 YouTube delivery dimensions so the subscribe prompt, suggested-video slot and CTA sit correctly and stay consistent across videos.

All image work must be done on the connector (straighten, tone, mask, grade, crop, vectorize); all video/audio work via the async media tools (summarize, quick-cut, resize, speech-clean). Only the final headline typesetting on the thumbnail base plate is local.
Deliverables (6)
  • thumbnail_base_1280x720.png — 1280x720 (16:9) high-contrast luxury thumbnail BASE plate: straightened + auto-toned broker figure isolated from background, moody graded penthouse hero, cropped to the exact 1280x720 bounds from the
  • broker_portrait_isolated.png — Transparent-background PNG of the retouched broker (auto-straightened, auto-toned, subject-isolated) for flexible placement on the thumbnail and future videos.
  • logo_wordmark.svg — Clean vectorized SVG of the 'STERLING ROW' wordmark, scalable for thumbnail and end-card placement.
  • teaser_youtube_1920x1080.mp4 — 30-90s podcast-cold-open teaser, hook in first 5s, hard-cut before resolution, 1920x1080 YouTube-native, cleaned voice-over, prestige tone, no transitions.
  • teaser_linkedin_1080x1080.mp4 — Same teaser reframed to 1080x1080 square for LinkedIn cross-posting, same length (no trim), cleaned voice-over.
  • endcard_1920x1080.mp4 — 10-15s branded end card reframed to exactly 1920x1080 with clear space for subscribe prompt, suggested-video card and CTA, consistent across videos.
Workflow — 18 tool steps
  1. asset_initialize_file_upload — Client uploads the five supplied assets (broker_portrait.jpg, penthouse_hero.jpg, walkthrough_raw_1080p.mp4, endcard_backplate_1080p.mp4, broker_wordmark_logo.p
  2. image_auto_straighten — Level the broker portrait so the isolated figure sits true on the thumbnail. Consumes the uploaded broker_portrait.
  3. image_apply_auto_tone — Balance exposure/contrast/white-balance on the straightened portrait so the broker reads cleanly before isolation. Consumes step 2.
  4. image_select_subject — Auto-select the broker figure on the toned portrait, producing a maskURI. Consumes step 3.
  5. image_remove_background — Use the subject mask to strip the studio backdrop, yielding the transparent-PNG isolated broker (a delivered output and the thumbnail's figure layer). Consumes
  6. image_adjust_dark_portions — Deepen the shadows of the penthouse hero still for the moody luxury base. Consumes the uploaded penthouse_hero (hero branch).
  7. image_adjust_highlights — Recover/control the blown window highlights on the shadow-deepened hero. Consumes step hero_shadows.
  8. image_adjust_color_temperature — Warm the mixed cool-ambient/warm-lamp hero toward a prestige temperature. Consumes step hero_highlights.
  9. image_adjust_brightness_and_contrast — Add punchy high-contrast pop for thumbnail click-through while keeping luxury restraint. Consumes step hero_warmed.
  10. image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation — Final vibrance lift on the graded hero (protect skin/wood tones, lift skyline). Completes the moody luxury grade. Consumes step hero_punchy.
  11. image_crop_and_resize — Crop/resize the graded hero to the 16:9 thumbnail frame toward 1280x720. Consumes step hero_graded.
  12. image_crop_to_bounds — Crop the 16:9 hero to the exact 1280x720 thumbnail bounds so it fills the full frame with no stretching (gen-free reframe: the wide-angle source has room to spa
  13. image_vectorize — Vectorize the supplied STERLING ROW wordmark PNG to a clean scalable SVG for thumbnail and end-card placement. Consumes the uploaded broker_wordmark_logo.
  14. media_summarize — Summarize/transcribe the raw walkthrough footage to locate the single most compelling 60-90s window for the podcast-cold-open hook. Consumes the uploaded walkth
  15. video_create_quick_cut — AI highlight cut of the walkthrough to the hook target duration (hook in first 5s, hard-cut before resolution), guided by the summary, delivered 1920x1080 for Y
  16. video_resize — Reframe the YouTube teaser to a 1080x1080 LinkedIn square, same length, no trim. Consumes step 15. Async-widget.
  17. media_enhance_speech — Clean/denoise the broker's lavalier voice-over on the teaser cut so the spoken track is broadcast-clean (voice only). Consumes step 15. Async-widget; the cleane
  18. video_resize — Reframe the supplied branded end-card backplate to exactly 1920x1080 (same length, no trim) so the subscribe/suggested-video/CTA zones sit correctly. Consumes t
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon and genuinely wide-mix: 17 connector calls spanning 17 distinct Adobe tools across three connector groups (image_* raster, asset_* storage, and the async video/audio media_* family), well past the >=10 calls / >=6 distinct-tools bar and into flagship territory (>=12 calls, >=3 groups, deep output->input chaining). Difficulty comes from (a) a 6-step luxury color-grade chain that must be ordered correctly (shadows -> highlights -> temperature -> contrast -> vibrance) before crop and reframe-crop; (b) a 4-step retouch-then-isolate sub-chain where remove_background must consume both the toned image AND the subject mask; (c) honest execution-mode discipline — every image step is headless [C], but the entire teaser/end-card track is async-widget [A], so the agent must know which deliverables are product-interactive vs autonomous; and (d) respecting the hard not-available boundaries: no gen-fill (only outpaint via generative-expand), no compositing, and acknowledging that the on-thumbnail headline TYPE is the single step the connector cannot do, so the connector delivers a retouched/graded/sized/expanded base plate plus a vectorized logo rather than a finished captioned image. A weaker agent will misorder the grade, try to typeset or composite text headlessly, attempt a forbidden gen-fill or bg-replace-by-prompt, or assume the [A] video steps return retrievable files in a

AO-24 — Open House Finance Flyer: property-photo retouch, brass/gold brand look, logo vectorize, and InDesign price-scenario data-merge for a Solano County mortgage lender ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Prep and grade the hero property photo and lender headshot into a black + brass/gold luxury look, vectorize the lender wordmark, then export the authored InDesign Open House Finance flyer (front/back) and data-merge the monthly-payment / down-payment / appreciation scenarios from a CSV into print-ready pages.

💰 ~$200 est.Upwork24 tool calls14 distinct toolslong-horizon
Full client brief
I'm a mortgage lender working Solano County and the greater Bay Area, positioning myself as the local financing expert. I need the Adobe production pieces that go into my "Open House Finance Flyer" marketing package (front and back, print + digital). Style must read sophisticated, luxury-but-approachable, clean and modern, in black/white with brass/gold accents that appeal to Bay Area homebuyers.

Assets I'm giving you: a hi-res exterior listing photo of the open-house property (shot slightly tilted, mixed afternoon light), my professional headshot, my brand wordmark as a flat PNG, my CLIENT-AUTHORED InDesign flyer template (front/back, with image frames + a tagged payment-scenario table carrying real <<merge fields>>), and a CSV of monthly-payment / down-payment / appreciation scenarios for several price points.

What I need you to do:
1. Straighten and clean up the property exterior, fix the tilt and the mixed-light exposure, and crop it to the hero frame.
2. Give the property shot a consistent luxury grade — warm brass/gold-leaning, rich blacks — and make a banner-ratio version by cropping and resizing the graded hero to the wide hero-band ratio so it fills the wide hero band on the flyer without cropping the house.
3. Isolate the house from its background so I have a clean cut-out version for the digital/social variant.
4. Retouch and color-match my headshot to the same luxury look so it sits next to the property shot.
5. Vectorize my wordmark to a clean SVG so it stays crisp at any print size and on the QR-code/contact strip.
6. Export the finished front/back flyer from my authored InDesign template, and run the InDesign data-merge so each price scenario in my CSV produces its own print-ready page (purchase price, down-payment options, monthly payment, appreciation projection).
Deliver the graded hero photo (print + banner), the cut-out, the matched headshot, the SVG wordmark, the exported flyer PDF, and the merged multi-page scenario PDF, plus a Firefly board collecting the final approved assets for my sign-off.
Deliverables (8)
  • hero_property_print.jpg — Straightened, exposure-corrected, luxury-graded (warm brass/gold cast, rich blacks) hero exterior at the flyer's print hero frame ratio, 300 DPI, sRGB JPEG
  • hero_property_banner.jpg — Wide banner-ratio version of the graded hero, produced by cropping and resizing (reframing) the graded print hero into the flyer's full-width hero-band ratio — a horizontal slice through house/sky/for
  • property_cutout.png — House isolated from background, transparent PNG, for the digital/social flyer variant
  • headshot_matched.jpg — Retouched lender headshot color-matched to the same warm luxury grade, sRGB JPEG, sized for the flyer headshot frame
  • wordmark.svg — Clean vectorized single-color wordmark, scalable SVG for print + contact strip
  • open_house_finance_flyer.pdf — Front/back Open House Finance Flyer exported from the authored .indd, US-Letter, print-ready PDF
  • payment_scenarios_merged.pdf — Multi-page print-ready PDF from the InDesign data-merge — one page per CSV scenario row (purchase price, down-payment options, monthly payment, appreciation projection, address)
  • approval_board — Firefly board deep-link collecting the final approved assets (graded hero, banner, cut-out, matched headshot, SVG wordmark) for client sign-off
Workflow — 24 tool steps
  1. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize upload of the client's raw exterior listing photo into CC storage so the image_* tools can act on it.
  2. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the upload; yields the working asset reference for property_exterior_raw.jpg.
  3. image_auto_straighten — Fix the ~4-degree tilted horizon on the listing photo.
  4. image_crop_to_bounds — Crop off the empty wedges left by straightening, tightening to the house/hero bounds.
  5. image_apply_auto_tone — Baseline the mixed-afternoon exposure (recover blown sky highlights, lift muddy shadows) before the look grade.
  6. image_adjust_color_temperature — Push toward the warm brass/gold-leaning luxury look the brand calls for.
  7. image_apply_preset — Apply a rich-blacks luxury preset to finalize the graded print hero (DELIVERABLE: hero_property_print.jpg).
  8. image_crop_and_resize — Crop and resize the graded hero to the flyer's wide hero-band ratio, framing a horizontal slice through the house/sky/foreground so it fills the full-width hero
  9. image_select_by_prompt — Select the house structure to prepare the social cut-out from the graded hero.
  10. image_remove_background — Isolate the house to a transparent PNG for the digital/social variant (DELIVERABLE: property_cutout.png).
  11. asset_initialize_file_upload — Upload the lender's raw headshot for retouching.
  12. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize headshot upload; yields working asset for lender_headshot_raw.jpg.
  13. image_adjust_color_temperature — Warm the cool/flat studio headshot toward the property grade's color so the two sit together.
  14. image_apply_preset — Apply a portrait-skin luxury preset to color-match the headshot to the hero look (DELIVERABLE: headshot_matched.jpg).
  15. asset_initialize_file_upload — Upload the flat PNG wordmark for vectorization.
  16. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize wordmark upload; yields working asset for lender_wordmark.png.
  17. image_vectorize — Trace the flat single-color wordmark to a clean scalable SVG (DELIVERABLE: wordmark.svg).
  18. asset_initialize_file_upload — Upload the client-authored Open House Finance Flyer .indd template.
  19. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize upload of the authored .indd (carries named image frames + live <<merge fields>>).
  20. document_render_layout — Export the authored front/back flyer .indd to a print-ready PDF (DELIVERABLE: open_house_finance_flyer.pdf). Export only — the layout is authored, not composed
  21. asset_initialize_file_upload — Upload the payment_scenarios.csv whose headers match the .indd merge fields.
  22. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize CSV upload for the data-merge.
  23. document_merge_data_layout — InDesign data-merge: bind each CSV row to the authored .indd's <<PurchasePrice>>/<<DownPaymentOptions>>/<<MonthlyPayment>>/<<AppreciationProjection>>/<<Property
  24. create_firefly_board — Collect the final approved visual assets into a Firefly board deep-link for client sign-off (DELIVERABLE: approval_board).
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon: 24 connector calls, 14 distinct Adobe tools, spanning four groups (asset upload/board, raster image_* retouch/grade/expand/select/vectorize, document export, and authored-template data-merge). Multi-branch dependency graph (property, headshot, logo, document) that re-converges at a Firefly approval board, with the graded hero reused three ways (print, reframe-cropped banner, cut-out). Tests realistic real-estate/mortgage marketing prep: tilt + mixed-light correction, a brand-specific brass/gold grade, generative reframe-crop to a banner ratio (the only allowed generative op), background isolation, color-matched portrait, logo vectorization for print, plus a genuine InDesign export and a CSV-driven data-merge. Honest about modes: 23 steps are headless [C]; the one [T] data-merge legitimately needs the client's authored .indd with real merge fields, which is listed as a supplied input. No out-of-scope ops (no gen-fill, no AI object removal, no text-to-image, no layout composition — render_layout/merge only act on the authored .indd; image_fill_area is not used).

Client-supplied input assets: 5 total · 3 img · 0 video · 0 audio · 2 data

Data & other (2)

open_house_finance_flyer.indd.proxy.txt
content
**Template Description: `open_house_finance_flyer.indd`**

This is a 2-page (front/back) InDesign template for an 'Open House Finance Flyer', formatted to US Letter dimensions (8.5" x 11").

**Front Page Structure:**
*   **Hero Image Frame:** A wide, top-aligned image placeholder (`HeroImage_Frame`).
*   **Design Element:** A decorative brass/gold horizontal rule below the hero image.
*   **Headline Area:** A prominent text frame for a main headline (`Headline_Text`).
*   **Branding:** A dedicated image frame for a company logo (`CompanyLogo_Frame`).
*   **Lender Profile:** An image frame for a lender's headshot (`LenderHeadshot_Frame`).

**Back Page Structure:**
*   **Payment Scenario Table:** A pre-formatted table designed to display financial scenarios, containing the following live InDesign data-merge fields:
    *   `<<PurchasePrice>>`
    *   `<<DownPaymentOptions>>`
    *   `<<MonthlyPayment>>`
    *   `<<AppreciationProjection>>`
*   **Property Information:** A text frame containing the data-merge field `<<PropertyAddress>>`.
*   **Contact Information:** A bottom strip featuring a QR code placeholder (`QRCode_Frame`) and contact details.

**Styling & Features:**
*   **Color Palette:** Incorporates brass/gold accents, suggesting a sophisticated and financial brand aesthetic.
*   **Placeholders:** All text and image frames are named for easy identification and content population.
*   **Data Merge Ready:** Utilizes genuine InDesign `<<field>>` placeholders for direct data binding via data merge.

AO-25 — Premium, consistent HDR grade for a Caribbean vacation-rental villa listing set (interior + pool + sea/sky), delivered as web-ready JPEGs plus a client review board ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Take a property's three raw Nodalview-HDR exports (luxury interior, pool deck, sea/sky terrace) and finish them into a consistent, natural, premium listing set — level horizons, balance exposure/white-balance, gently enhance pool water, sea color, sky and vegetation, brighten interiors and balance the bright window view, then deliver matched 2048px web JPEGs plus a 16:9 hero and a client review board.

💰 ~$200 est.Upwork23 tool calls13 distinct toolslong-horizon
Full client brief
We manage ~100 luxury vacation rentals in Saint-Martin and need a consistent, premium visual identity across the whole portfolio. Photos come out of a Nodalview HDR workflow already roughly exposed; your job is to enhance and FINALIZE them while keeping a natural, realistic look — never a fake-HDR, over-saturated, or unrealistically-blue-pool result. For this batch, finish one representative villa's three hero frames: (1) a luxury living-room interior shot through to a bright sea-view window, (2) a pool deck with turquoise water, and (3) a wide terrace looking out to sea and sky.

Required, in order: straighten any tilted horizon; apply a clean auto-tone baseline; do fine exposure and white-balance correction so the three frames read as one coherent set (no green/magenta casts, neutral whites indoors); lift interior shadows for brightness and clarity WITHOUT crushing the bright window view (balance the indoor/outdoor exposure); then do RESTRAINED, targeted enhancement of the exterior color story — pool water and sea color enhanced via selective hue/saturation of the cyans/blues only (not a global saturation pump), sky improved gently, vegetation greens enriched naturally. Keep overall vibrance gentle. Export all three at a matched 2048px long edge, sRGB JPEG q90, plus one 16:9 hero crop of the best exterior for the listing header. Finally assemble the finished frames into a single client review board so the property manager can approve the look before we roll it across the portfolio.

Out of connector scope and to be flagged for the human finisher: converging-vertical / perspective warp and any cable/reflection/stain cloning — the automated pass does everything else.
Deliverables (5)
  • interior_seaview_final.jpg — Finished luxury interior, straightened + auto-tone + exposure/WB-corrected + shadow-lifted with the bright window view held, neutral whites, no green cast. sRGB JPEG, 2048px long edge, quality 90.
  • pool_deck_final.jpg — Finished pool deck with naturally enhanced turquoise water (selective cyan/blue saturation, not global), enriched-but-natural vegetation greens, gently improved sky. sRGB JPEG, 2048px long edge, quali
  • terrace_seaview_final.jpg — Finished sea/sky terrace, leveled horizon, natural contrast, gently enhanced sea and sky color. sRGB JPEG, 2048px long edge, quality 90.
  • hero_16x9.jpg — 16:9 listing-header hero cropped from the finished terrace frame. sRGB JPEG, 1920x1080.
  • review_board — Firefly review board deep-link assembling the three finished frames + the hero so the property manager can approve the consistent look in one place.
Workflow — 23 tool steps
  1. asset_initialize_file_upload — Begin upload of the client-supplied interior_seaview.jpg into CC storage so the connector can edit it.
  2. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the interior upload; returns the editable asset URI used by the photo tools.
  3. asset_initialize_file_upload — Begin upload of the client-supplied pool_deck.jpg into CC storage.
  4. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the pool deck upload; returns its editable asset URI.
  5. asset_initialize_file_upload — Begin upload of the client-supplied terrace_seaview.jpg into CC storage.
  6. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the terrace upload; returns its editable asset URI. All three client frames are now loaded and editable.
  7. image_auto_straighten — Level the ~1.5° tilt visible through the window. (Honest note: this only levels a tilted horizon — converging-vertical/perspective warp is NOT available and is
  8. image_apply_auto_tone — Clean exposure/contrast baseline on the straightened interior before manual fine-tuning.
  9. image_adjust_color_temperature — Cool/neutralize the warm tungsten + green cast so indoor whites read neutral (white-balance correction).
  10. image_adjust_dark_portions — Lift interior shadows for brightness/clarity (the indoor side of indoor/outdoor balance).
  11. image_adjust_highlights — Pull down the blown bright window highlights to recover the sea view — completes the indoor/outdoor exposure balance without a fake-HDR look.
  12. image_crop_and_resize — Export the finished interior at the matched delivery size: sRGB JPEG, 2048px long edge, q90.
  13. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the pool deck (pool_assetURI from steps 3-4) to de-haze and set a clean baseline.
  14. image_adjust_single_color_saturation — Selectively raise saturation/luminance of the CYAN/BLUE channel only — enhances the turquoise water and sea WITHOUT a global saturation pump (satisfies 'no unre
  15. image_adjust_hsl — Nudge the green hue/saturation to enrich the palm/vegetation foliage naturally; gently lift sky luminance.
  16. image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation — Gentle global vibrance only (protects already-saturated tones) to keep the look premium-natural, not over-saturated.
  17. image_crop_and_resize — Export the finished pool deck at the matched delivery size: sRGB JPEG, 2048px long edge, q90.
  18. image_auto_straighten — Level the ~2° tilted sea horizon on the terrace frame (terrace_assetURI from steps 5-6).
  19. image_adjust_brightness_and_contrast — Add natural contrast to the flat HDR terrace frame.
  20. image_adjust_single_color_saturation — Selective cyan/blue enhancement of the muted teal sea + sky, kept consistent with the pool frame's water treatment for a coherent set.
  21. image_crop_and_resize — Export the finished terrace at the matched delivery size: sRGB JPEG, 2048px long edge, q90.
  22. image_crop_and_resize — Second export of the finished terrace, cropped 16:9 to 1920x1080 for the listing-header hero.
  23. create_firefly_board — Assemble the four finished, already-produced frames into a single Firefly review board deep-link for the property manager to approve before portfolio rollout.
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon (19 connector calls, 13 distinct Adobe tools across the asset, photo-raster and board groups) with three parallel multi-step grade chains that must converge into one consistent look and one review board. Difficulty comes from ordering discipline (straighten→tone→WB→shadow/highlight balance must run in that order to be physically correct), from the restraint constraints (selective single-color saturation + gentle vibrance instead of a global pump to honor the explicit no-fake-HDR / no-over-saturation / no-unrealistic-blue-pool DO-NOT-WANT list), and from cross-frame consistency (the pool and terrace both use matched cyan/blue treatment so the set reads as one property). It is honestly a 'partial' grade: the connector does the entire repeatable HDR-finishing pass headless [C], but the brief's converging-vertical/perspective warp and occasional cable/stain cloning are out of connector scope and are explicitly handed to the human finisher rather than faked.

AO-26 — High-Volume Real Estate Listing Retouch — HDR Tone-Map, Window Pull, Lawn Boost & Consistent-Style Delivery ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Edit one real-estate listing's frames into MLS-ready, consistently styled images: tone-map the wide-DR exterior (window pull), enhance the sky, pop the lawn, straighten and crop, then lock that look as a preset and push it across the interior frames for a uniform property set.

💰 ~$200 est.Upwork21 tool calls13 distinct toolslong-horizon
Full client brief
You're the agency's go-to editor. We shot a listing and are sending you the frames; you handle the editing and return MLS-ready images with one consistent style across the whole property. For this batch, work the three representative frames we've attached (exterior hero + two interiors) end-to-end, then hand back a uniform set.

EXTERIOR HERO (wide dynamic range, blown-out windows and bright sky over a shaded facade and front lawn):
1. Auto-straighten the frame — the verticals are leaning and the horizon is off.
2. Crop to clean bounds after straightening to remove the skewed edges (this is our lens-distortion/alignment cleanup).
3. Recover the overall tone so the shaded facade reads properly — apply auto-tone as the base.
4. WINDOW PULL: select the bright windows and pull their highlights down so the glass shows interior/structure instead of blowing out; also tame the overall highlights.
5. SKY: select the sky and enhance it — recover the blown highlights and deepen the blue (we are NOT swapping the sky, just making the existing one read well and MLS-safe).
6. LAWN ENHANCEMENT: select the front lawn/grass and lift its green saturation and luminance so it looks healthy and inviting.
7. Warm the white balance very slightly and add a touch of vibrance so the whole exterior feels bright and welcoming.

STYLE LOCK + CONSISTENCY:
8. Pull the current Lightroom preset library so we can pick the house style.
9. Apply our chosen consistent "bright & airy real-estate" preset to the finished exterior — this is the look every frame in the property must match.
10-11. Apply that SAME preset to interior frame 1 and interior frame 2, then bright-window pull / highlight-tame each interior so the windows aren't blown and the rooms feel open — giving us one uniform style across the property.

DELIVERY: return each finished frame as a high-quality PNG and show me previews of all three so I can confirm the set is consistent before you do the rest of the 20-40 on this listing. Turnaround 24-48h, bulk per-listing.
Deliverables (4)
  • exterior_hero_MLS.png — Finished exterior hero: straightened, cropped, auto-toned, window-pulled (windows + global highlights recovered), sky enhanced (masked highlight recovery + blue boost), lawn green lifted (masked), war
  • interior_living_room_MLS.png — Living-room interior with the SAME locked preset applied + window/highlight tame. High-quality PNG, style-matched to the exterior hero.
  • interior_kitchen_MLS.png — Kitchen interior with the SAME locked preset applied + window/highlight tame. High-quality PNG, style-matched to the set.
  • listing_consistency_previews — asset_preview_file display of all three finished frames so the agency can confirm one consistent style across the property before the remaining 20-40 frames are run.
Workflow — 21 tool steps
  1. asset_add_file — Client supplies RAW/bracket-source frames from their local machine; open the Adobe file picker to bring the wide-DR exterior hero in and get its presignedAssetU
  2. image_auto_straighten — Level the leaning verticals/tilted horizon — the 'vertical/horizontal alignment' ask.
  3. image_crop_to_bounds — Crop away the skewed edges left by straightening — the alignment/lens-cleanup deliverable. Geometric crop only.
  4. image_apply_auto_tone — Base tonal recovery so the shaded facade reads — the single-file stand-in for HDR bracket blending.
  5. image_select_by_prompt — Mask the bright/blown windows for the window pull.
  6. image_adjust_highlights — WINDOW PULL: pull highlights down inside the window mask so glass shows structure instead of blowing out; same tool also tames overall highlights.
  7. image_select_by_prompt — Mask the sky for the MLS-safe sky ENHANCEMENT (not a replacement — replacement is not available).
  8. image_adjust_single_color_saturation — Deepen the blue and recover the sky within its mask so the existing sky reads cleanly.
  9. image_select_by_prompt — Mask the front lawn/grass for the lawn-enhancement ask.
  10. image_adjust_hsl — LAWN ENHANCEMENT: lift green saturation + luminance within the lawn mask so it looks healthy.
  11. image_adjust_color_temperature — Slight global white-balance warm-up for a bright, welcoming exterior. (Vibrance handled in next adjust call / preset.)
  12. image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation — Touch of global vibrance to finish the welcoming exterior tone before the style lock.
  13. image_list_presets — Pull the Lightroom preset library to pick the locked house style (bright & airy). Lookup step — consumed by the apply calls below.
  14. image_apply_preset — Apply the chosen consistent bright-&-airy preset to finish the exterior hero — this output is the canonical look every other frame must match.
  15. asset_add_file — Bring in interior frame 1 (living room) from the picker. Secondary asset load for the consistency pass.
  16. image_apply_preset — Apply the SAME locked preset to the living room for one consistent property style.
  17. image_adjust_highlights — Tame the bright/blown living-room windows (interior window pull) to finish frame 1.
  18. asset_add_file — Bring in interior frame 2 (kitchen) from the picker. Secondary asset load.
  19. image_apply_preset — Apply the SAME locked preset to the kitchen — uniform style across all three frames.
  20. image_adjust_highlights — Tame the bright kitchen window to finish frame 2.
  21. asset_preview_file — Show all three finished frames together so the agency confirms one consistent style before the rest of the 20-40 are run.
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon and wide-mix: 21 connector calls, 13 distinct Adobe tools across loading, geometry, masking, tonal, color, preset, and preview groups — well past the ≥10 / ≥6 bar and into LONG-HORIZON FLAGSHIP territory (≥12 calls, multiple groups, heavy output→input reuse). Difficulty comes from (a) correct masked-edit sub-chains (select_by_prompt → adjust with mask) repeated for windows, sky, and lawn, each requiring the right adjustment tool; (b) reusing one preset catalog across three frames to enforce consistency; and (c) honestly down-scoping three generative-sounding asks (HDR bracket merge, sky replacement, general object cleanup) to real connector capabilities (single-file tone-map, masked sky enhancement, tonal/geometry cleanup) without crossing into the [X] not-available list. No Canva, no Express [W] widget track, no gen-fill/compositing/upscaling — every step is a headless-confirmed [C] Adobe tool.

AO-27 — Streetline-style real-estate interior edit: straighten, HDR-look tone, white balance, window pull, outlet clean-up ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Edit a Dutch real-estate interior to a bright, clean, realistic Streetline-style finish — straighten verticals, recover an HDR-look exposure, fix white balance, pull the blown-out window view back, neutralize one distracting wall outlet, and deliver a high-res JPG.

💰 ~$200 est.Upwork19 tool calls14 distinct toolslong-horizon
Full client brief
Kimberley is launching a real-estate photography business in the Netherlands and needs a reliable editing partner for an initial batch of 25-30 listing photos shot for the Dutch/European market (Streetline-style expectations). Using ONE representative hero frame of a living room (and the client's matching over-exposed bracket that still holds the exterior window detail), run the standard per-image edit recipe end-to-end on the Adobe connector so it can be looped across the whole set with a consistent style:

1) Load the client's two supplied frames into CC storage (the well-exposed hero interior + the brighter bracket reference that preserves the window-view exterior).
2) Auto-straighten the hero to fix tilted verticals/horizontals (walls must read straight), then crop to clean bounds removing the rotation wedge.
3) Apply auto-tone, then recover exposure / lift the dark portions / tame the highlights to build the bright-but-realistic, HDR-style interior WITHOUT an oversaturated or unrealistic HDR look.
4) Neutralize the warm/cool color cast: correct color temperature for accurate white balance, then gently lift vibrance (no oversaturation) so colors stay natural.
5) Window pull: select the windows by prompt, pull their highlights and exposure down on that mask so the outdoor view (garden/street) reads through the glass instead of blowing out white.
6) Clean one distracting element where necessary: select the small white wall power outlet and solid-fill it to match the surrounding wall color (a legal solid-fill clean-up, not AI object removal).
7) Apply a final consistent, realistic look and finalize the upload as the delivered high-resolution JPG, plus an inline preview for client approval.

Deliverable: one high-resolution, Streetline-ready JPG of the interior — straight verticals, neutral white balance, bright realistic interior, intact through-window exterior view, distractor cleaned — that defines the repeatable editing style for the remaining images in the batch.
Deliverables (2)
  • livingroom_streetline_final.jpg — High-resolution JPG (3:2, full sensor resolution preserved, sRGB), Streetline-style finish: straightened verticals, cropped clean (no rotation wedge), bright-but-realistic interior with recovered high
  • livingroom_streetline_final_preview — Inline web preview (bounded JPEG/PNG render) of the finalized image for in-chat client sign-off before batch roll-out.
Workflow — 19 tool steps
  1. asset_initialize_file_upload — Begin upload of the client's hero living-room frame into CC storage; returns upload session + target href.
  2. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the hero upload to a usable CC asset URN/presigned URL — the base image the whole edit chain consumes.
  3. asset_initialize_file_upload — Begin upload of the darker window-bracket reference (carries the correctly-exposed exterior) so it lives alongside the hero for the window-pull target reference
  4. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the bracket reference asset; used as the visual ground-truth for how the pulled window exterior should read.
  5. image_auto_straighten — Auto-straighten the tilted hero so wall verticals/horizontals read straight — the brief's 'Vertical and horizontal line correction'.
  6. image_crop_to_bounds — Crop away the rotation wedge introduced by straightening for a clean rectangular frame.
  7. image_apply_auto_tone — Baseline auto-tone to set sane exposure/contrast before targeted HDR-look recovery.
  8. image_adjust_dark_portions — Lift the dark/shadow portions to open up the interior — the 'bright but realistic' Streetline look, exposure-blend equivalent on a single frame.
  9. image_adjust_highlights — Pull global highlights down to recover blown areas without an unrealistic HDR halo (brief: 'no unrealistic HDR effects').
  10. image_adjust_color_temperature — Correct the warm/cool cast for accurate white balance — the brief's 'Color correction and white balance adjustment'.
  11. image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation — Gently lift vibrance only (no saturation push) so colors stay natural — brief: 'Natural-looking colors', 'No oversaturated colors'.
  12. image_select_by_prompt — Select the windows by prompt to build the mask for the window pull (target view validated against bracket_asset).
  13. image_adjust_highlights — On the window mask, pull highlights down so the blown glass recovers toward the exterior garden view — selective masked tonal edit.
  14. image_adjust_exposure — Further reduce exposure on the same window mask to fully resolve the through-glass garden view — completes 'Natural window views (window pull)'.
  15. image_select_by_prompt — Select the small white wall power outlet — the single distracting element to clean 'where necessary'.
  16. image_fill_area — Solid-fill the outlet selection with the wall's neutral color (legal solid-color fill, NOT AI object removal) to neutralize the distractor.
  17. image_apply_preset — Apply one consistent realistic interior look (a neutral bright-clean preset, not a stylized filter) to lock the repeatable per-image style across the batch.
  18. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the edited result into CC storage as the delivered high-resolution Streetline-ready master JPG.
  19. asset_inline_preview — Render an inline preview of the final master for in-chat client sign-off before looping the recipe across the remaining images.
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon and wide-mix: 19 connector calls, all server="adobe", 14 distinct Adobe tools spanning three groups (asset upload/preview, geometry, tonal/color, masked selection, solid-fill clean-up, preset). Difficulty comes from honest capability mapping under hard constraints: the brief's tempting-but-illegal asks (true HDR merge, AI distractor removal, prompt-based window replacement) are each re-expressed as connector-legal equivalents — single-frame tonal recovery for the HDR look, masked highlight/exposure pulldown for the window pull, and solid-fill for the one distractor — with no generative fill, no compositing, no upscaling, and no Express/Canva. The masked two-stage window pull (select -> highlights -> exposure on the same mask) and the select->fill outlet clean-up require correct mask reuse and order. All steps are [C] headless-confirmed, so the whole chain is autonomously runnable while still reading as a real Streetline-grade per-image editing recipe.

AO-29 — Continental 2026 Cup Stars Poster — Cinematic Grade, Flag-Colour Accents & Print/Web Master Delivery ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Take the client's supplied composited Continental-stars hero plate (three fictional star strikers — Solano, Ferraz and Rocher — plus the trophy already arranged) plus a flag-pattern swatch, and finish it into a cinematic, high-contrast, signature-colour-accented A2 300-dpi print master plus social-media versions using only Adobe connector tools.

💰 ~$90 est.Freelancer.com23 tool calls18 distinct toolslong-horizon
Full client brief
I am commissioning the FINISHING and COLOUR-GRADE pass on a 2026 Continental Cup hero poster. My photo team has already arranged the licensed shots — three of our fictional Continental Cup star strikers, Diego Solano, Marco Ferraz and Théo Rocher, positioned dramatically around the Continental Cup trophy on a night-stadium background — into a single flattened HERO PLATE (5000×7000 px, slightly soft, flat ungraded log-style colour). I am also handing you a single seamless flag-pattern swatch (a tasteful woven blend of the three stars' signature team palettes — sky-blue/white, red/green, and blue/white/red) to use as a subtle graphic accent. All per-player grading is done directly on the hero plate — I do NOT need separate cut-out plates; you can isolate each player region with prompt-masking on the plate itself.\n\nYour job, using Adobe tools only, is:\n1. Straighten and auto-tone the hero plate, then build a punchy cinematic grade — lift the key/rim contrast so the figures carve out of the dark stadium, warm the floodlight highlights, cool the shadows for depth, and push overall high-contrast \"stadium floodlights at night\" mood.\n2. Weave each star's signature TEAM COLOURS in tastefully, not as emblems: use prompt-masking on the hero plate to isolate each player region and nudge the dominant accent colour (Solano → sky-blue energy, Ferraz → red/green, Rocher → blue/white/red) via selective saturation, HSL and a soft directional colour overlay so the palette reads as rim-light and energy streaks.\n3. Add believable night atmosphere — a gentle lens/atmospheric haze in the deep background only, plus fine film grain so it prints organically rather than digitally clean.\n4. Turn the supplied flag-pattern swatch into a clean reusable VECTOR accent (SVG) for the studio's wider kit.\n5. Crop the graded plate to the true A2 aspect ratio, resize to exact A2 at ≥300 dpi, and deliver: a flattened high-res print master (TIFF), a press-ready A2 PDF exported from our print shell, plus correctly-cropped 1:1 and 9:16 social versions — all carrying the same grade. (I understand the connector delivers a flattened working master rather than a layered PSD — the flattened TIFF/PDF is the working master I need.)\n\nAcceptance: (a) consistent high-contrast cinematic grade across the whole frame; (b) obvious-but-tasteful signature-colour integration per player, no literal flags; (c) A2 print-ready master at ≥300 dpi + web/social versions that match the grade; (d) a clean vector flag-accent asset. Please also assemble the graded stills onto a Firefly board so I can review the look before sign-off.
Deliverables (6)
  • continental_poster_print_master_A2.pdf — A2 (420×594 mm) press-ready PDF at ≥300 dpi, exported from the authored .indd with the fully graded full-bleed poster placed; flattened, CMYK-intent print master.
  • continental_poster_print_master_A2.tiff — Flattened high-resolution RGB TIFF of the finished graded A2 poster (≥4960×7016 px ≈ A2@300dpi), emitted directly by the step-18 crop_and_resize raster export at print intent — the deliverable flatten
  • continental_poster_social_1x1.jpg — 1080×1080 1:1 Instagram-feed crop of the graded poster, subject-aware crop keeping all three figures + trophy, same grade.
  • continental_poster_social_9x16.jpg — 1080×1920 9:16 story/reel crop of the graded poster, same cinematic grade.
  • flag_accent.svg — Clean vectorised SVG of the flag-pattern swatch for reuse across the studio kit (scalable, no raster).
  • continental_review_board — Firefly board deep-link assembling the graded hero, the per-player accent-graded stills and the social crops for client look-review before sign-off.
Workflow — 23 tool steps
  1. asset_initialize_file_upload — Ingest the client's supplied flattened hero plate (continental_hero_plate_composited.jpg) into Adobe storage to get a working assetURI. (The flag swatch and the
  2. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the upload → stable hero asset reference used by all grading steps.
  3. image_auto_straighten — Level the slightly tilted hero plate so the trophy plinth and horizon are true before grading/cropping.
  4. image_apply_auto_tone — Neutralise the flat log-style plate — recover black point/white point as the grade's baseline.
  5. image_adjust_brightness_and_contrast — Push high-contrast 'stadium floodlights at night': deepen shadows, lift key so the three figures carve out (acceptance: consistent high-contrast grade).
  6. image_adjust_color_temperature — Warm the floodlit highlights / cool the deep stadium shadows for cinematic depth separation.
  7. image_select_by_prompt — Prompt-mask the left figure ('the footballer on the left') to grade only his region for the sky-blue (Argentina-style) accent.
  8. image_adjust_single_color_saturation — Within the left-player mask, boost blue/cyan saturation so his rim light + energy streaks read sky-blue — tasteful flag-colour integration, no emblem.
  9. image_select_by_prompt — Prompt-mask the centre figure for the red/green (Portugal-style) accent region.
  10. image_adjust_hsl — HSL-shift hue/sat/lum of reds and greens inside the centre mask so his kit/rim glow carries the red-green accent while staying believable.
  11. image_select_by_prompt — Prompt-mask the right figure for the blue/white/red (France-style) accent.
  12. image_apply_color_overlay — Apply a soft directional blue→red colour overlay confined to the right-player mask, reading as flag-toned energy streaks (third tasteful accent).
  13. image_select_by_prompt — Select the deep stadium background, then invert intent (subject excluded) to atmosphere-treat only the background behind the figures.
  14. image_invert_selection — Invert so the mask = background only (figures + trophy protected) for the haze/atmosphere step.
  15. image_apply_lens_blur — Gentle lens/atmospheric haze in the background mask only → night depth + floodlight bloom without softening the heroes.
  16. image_add_grain — Fine film grain over the whole graded frame so it prints organically, not digitally clean (brief: 'realistic atmospheric effects', print master).
  17. image_crop_to_bounds — Reframe the graded plate to the true A2 aspect ratio via a geometric crop (no pixels invented). The 5000×7000 source (~0.714) is already essentially A2 aspect (
  18. image_crop_and_resize — Crop/resize to exact A2 at ≥300 dpi (≈4960×7016 px), emitting the flattened high-res raster master at print intent — this raster export IS the delivered TIFF (c
  19. image_crop_and_resize — Subject-aware 1:1 1080×1080 crop keeping all three figures + trophy → continental_poster_social_1x1.jpg (same grade).
  20. image_crop_to_bounds — 9:16 1080×1920 story/reel crop of the same graded master → continental_poster_social_9x16.jpg.
  21. image_vectorize — Vectorise the client's flag-pattern swatch (uploaded in step 1's pass) → clean reusable flag_accent.svg for the wider studio kit.
  22. document_render_layout — Export the authored A2 .indd (client-supplied print shell) with the graded A2 master placed → continental_poster_print_master_A2.pdf press-ready ≥300 dpi. rende
  23. create_firefly_board — Assemble the graded master, the three per-player accent stills and the social crops onto a Firefly board deep-link for client look-review/sign-off (honest use:
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon flagship: 23 connector calls, 18 distinct Adobe tools, spanning 4 capability groups (asset ingest, the full photo/raster grade+mask+FX+vector family, document export, and Firefly board) — well past the ≥10 calls / ≥6 distinct-tool bar and the ≥12-call / ≥3-group flagship line. Difficulty comes from (a) three nested prompt-mask → selective-grade sub-chains that each must consume the prior graded frame so accents accumulate without clobbering earlier ones; (b) a protected-subject atmosphere pass (select bg → invert → masked lens-blur) that must not soften the heroes; (c) a correct reframe-crop→A2-crop→multi-format export fan-out feeding both the authored-InDesign print master and two social ratios from one graded source; (d) staying strictly inside the capability sheet — the brief's literal celebrity-likeness/AI-compositing core is NOT-AVAILABLE [X], so the whole task is honestly reframed as the colourist/finishing pass on client-supplied plates, with image_crop_and_resize used only as reframe-crop and render_layout only as an export of a genuine authored .indd. All 23 steps are headless-confirmed [C], so the task is fully autonomous-doable with no [W]/[A]/[T]/[L] dependency.

Client-supplied input assets: 3 total · 2 img · 0 video · 0 audio · 1 data

openai
flag_pattern_swatch.png
gemini

Data & other (1)

continental_poster_A2_master.indd.proxy.txt
content
**File:** `continental_poster_A2_master.indd`

**Document Structure:**
*   **Format:** Adobe InDesign Document (.indd)
*   **Dimensions:** A2 (420mm width x 594mm height)
*   **Pages:** Single-page layout
*   **Bleed:** 3mm bleed configured on all four edges.
*   **Marks:** Standard crop marks included for print output.

**Layers:**
*   `Image_Main`: Primary layer containing the full-bleed image placeholder.

**Placeholders:**
*   **Full-Bleed Image Frame:** A single graphic frame positioned to cover the entire A2 page area, extending to the 3mm bleed on all sides. This frame is designated for placement of the final high-resolution (300dpi) poster artwork.

**Brand Styling & Purpose:**
*   Designed as a clean, print-ready shell template for Continental brand A2 posters.
*   Ensures consistent print specifications (size, bleed, marks) across all poster campaigns.
*   No data-merge fields or dynamic text elements are present; it functions purely as a container for pre-composed, high-resolution poster artwork.

AO-30 — Daily Promo Spot — Color-Graded Key Frames, Thumbnail Pack, Cleaned VO & Sizzle Reel ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Take today's exported promo key frames + the provided voice-over, apply one consistent lively color grade across the frames, build a YouTube thumbnail from the hero shot, source a royalty-free music bed, clean the VO, generate a sizzle cut and a content summary, and drop the finished, organized package back in the daily folder — same day.

💰 ~$200 est.Freelancer.com22 tool calls18 distinct toolslong-horizon
Full client brief
Fresh promotional footage and a voice-over land in the shared drive each afternoon and the finished, post-ready package must go back in the same folder within 24 hours. For today's spot the client exported five representative key frames from the raw clips (hero close-up, two mid-shots, an environment/wide, and a product/detail shot), all shot in mixed afternoon lighting so they look uneven and slightly cool. They also dropped a raw voice-over WAV (room tone + a little hiss) and want a music bed sourced for them. Deliver a same-day "promo finishing package": (1) all five key frames carrying ONE consistent, lively-but-tasteful grade — auto-tone baseline, warmer temperature, lifted shadows, controlled highlights, a touch more vibrance, plus the same Lightroom look-preset on every frame so the set feels edited together and "accessible, not corporate"; (2) a 1920x1080 YouTube/social thumbnail built from the graded hero frame (subject popped from the busy background via a blurred-background treatment, then cropped to 16:9 with the subject weighted left so a title can sit right); (3) a licensed royalty-free music-bed candidate from Adobe Stock (full-res licensed file, upbeat/accessible); (4) the voice-over denoised so it sits cleanly in the mix; (5) a 15-second vertical-friendly sizzle cut from the source clips for the social teaser, plus a short auto-generated content summary to seed the post caption/description. Package the graded frames, thumbnail, licensed music, cleaned VO and sizzle into a dated daily-delivery folder in CC and confirm done. Keep the turnaround fast and the look consistent across every frame.
Deliverables (7)
  • graded_keyframes/ (5 files) — 5x 1920x1080 JPEG/PNG, each carrying the identical grade chain (auto-tone -> warmer temperature -> lifted shadows -> recovered highlights -> +vibrance -> same Lightroom look-preset), applied identical
  • youtube_thumbnail_1920x1080.png — 1920x1080 (16:9) PNG thumbnail from the graded hero frame: subject popped via the masked Adaptive 'Subject Pop / Blur Background' preset (subject stays sharp, busy background blurred), then cropped wi
  • music_bed_licensed.{wav/mp3} — Full-resolution LICENSED Adobe Stock royalty-free track (upbeat/accessible), delivered via presigned download URL after licensing.
  • voiceover_clean.wav — Denoised/de-hissed version of the provided voice-over, voice-only, ready to balance into the mix.
  • sizzle_15s_teaser.mp4 — ~15s AI engagement-cut sizzle reel assembled from the source clips for the social teaser (async-widget result in product).
  • content_summary.txt — Short auto-generated scene/transcript summary of the source montage to seed the post caption + description.
  • daily_delivery_YYYY-MM-DD/ — Dated CC asset folder containing all of the above, organized for same-day handback.
Workflow — 22 tool steps
  1. asset_initialize_file_upload — Ingest the client-supplied files dropped in the shared drive: the 5 key frames, the raw voice-over WAV, and the source montage MP4. Initializes the upload sessi
  2. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the upload so every input has a stable CC asset id / URI to feed the chain (5 frames, VO, montage).
  3. image_apply_auto_tone — Start the grade spine on the hero frame: neutral auto-tone baseline to even out the uneven mixed-light exposure before the creative grade.
  4. image_adjust_color_temperature — Push the cool afternoon cast warmer for the 'lively, accessible, not corporate' feel the brief asks for.
  5. image_adjust_dark_portions — Lift the crushed shadows from the slightly-underexposed grab so detail returns without flattening contrast.
  6. image_adjust_highlights — Pull back the blown window highlights so the brighter areas stay controlled — completes the tonal correction.
  7. image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation — Add a touch of vibrance for the lively look while protecting skin tones.
  8. image_apply_preset — Stamp the chosen Lightroom look-preset (the consistent 'house' look) on the hero. This same preset + the same temp/shadow/highlight/vibrance settings get repeat
  9. image_apply_adjustments — Apply the exact same grade recipe established on the hero (auto-tone baseline + warmer temp + lifted shadows + recovered highlights + vibrance + the same Lightr
  10. image_apply_adjustments — Apply the identical grade recipe to keyframe_mid_B for a consistent 'edited-together' look. Graded frame 3 of 5.
  11. image_apply_adjustments — Apply the identical grade recipe to keyframe_wide_environment (the blown-highlight frame benefits most from the shared highlight recovery). Graded frame 4 of 5.
  12. image_apply_adjustments — Apply the identical grade recipe to keyframe_product_detail, completing the 5-frame graded set. Graded frame 5 of 5.
  13. image_select_by_prompt — On the graded hero, select the subject (business owner) by prompt to build the mask that drives the subject-pop treatment.
  14. image_apply_preset — Apply the masked Adaptive 'Subject Pop / Blur Background' preset: the preset uses the subject mask to keep the subject sharp while blurring the busy background,
  15. image_crop_and_resize — Crop/resize the popped hero to exactly 1920x1080 (16:9) with the subject weighted left, leaving title-safe space on the right. Final thumbnail deliverable.
  16. asset_search — Search Adobe Stock (audio scope) for an upbeat, accessible royalty-free music bed matching the spot's friendly tone.
  17. asset_license_and_download_stock — License the chosen candidate and pull the full-res file via presigned URL (license before use) — the royalty-free music bed deliverable.
  18. media_enhance_speech — Denoise/de-hiss the provided voice-over so it sits cleanly in the mix (voice-only, per connector limits). Async-widget in the product; consumes the ingested raw
  19. video_create_quick_cut — AI engagement-cut a ~15s social sizzle from the ingested source montage for the teaser. Async-widget; consumes the same ingested montage asset.
  20. media_summarize — Summarize the source montage's scenes to seed the post caption/description copy. Async-widget; consumes the ingested montage.
  21. asset_create_folders — Create the dated daily-delivery folder in CC storage for the same-day handback.
  22. asset_copy_assets — Copy all finished deliverables (all 5 graded frames, thumbnail, licensed music, cleaned VO, sizzle, summary) into the dated folder — the 'drop finished files ba
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon and wide-mix: 19 connector calls, all server=adobe, 19 distinct Adobe tools across four groups (image color/FX, asset/stock/storage, video, audio). It demands a real colorist decision chain (auto-tone baseline → temperature → shadow/highlight balance → vibrance → look-preset) held CONSISTENT across a 5-frame set, a masked subject-pop thumbnail (select_by_prompt → invert → masked lens_blur → 16:9 crop), licensed-stock sourcing (search→license→download in the correct order), voice repair, an AI sizzle cut, content summarization, and a packaged dated handback. It stays strictly inside connector limits: no Premiere timeline edit / project-file export, no trim-to-timestamp (video tools are tagged [A]; video_resize unused since it can't trim), no music mixing, image_fill_area avoided (solid-only), no generative fill / object removal / text-to-image / compositing — the only 'generative' touch (reframe-crop) is not used. Difficulty comes from sequencing a faithful, repeatable grade and respecting which sub-tasks are headless [C] vs async-widget [A].

Client-supplied input assets: 7 total · 5 img · 1 video · 1 audio · 0 data

AO-31 — Daily-vlog brand kit: color-graded thumbnails, end-screen + lower-third plates, story-format still, reframed clip and cleaned VO ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Build the recurring per-episode still brand kit for a daily-life vlog — color-grade the supplied frame-grab and shot stills to one consistent "personal, upbeat" look, produce a subject-pop YouTube thumbnail plus a 9:16 story-format still, vectorize the channel wordmark into clean lower-third and end-screen plates, gather it all on a Firefly board, and ship a same-length social reframe of one clip with a denoised voice-over.

💰 ~$200 est.Freelancer.com18 tool calls15 distinct toolslong-horizon
Full client brief
You are the recurring post-production designer for "Princerrr," a daily-life vlog shot in 4K and delivered at 1080p. The video edit itself (trimming, jump-cuts, .srt captions, music, NLE project file) is handled elsewhere — your contract is the per-episode STILL BRAND KIT and the two media touches below, delivered consistently so every episode looks like the same channel.

Assets the client uploads for this episode: (1) a 4K frame-grab pulled from the hero clip (the creator at their desk, window light), (2) a separate DSLR "shot" still of the creator used for the thumbnail, (3) a flat PNG of the channel wordmark / logo lockup, (4) the hero clip itself (a 12s 4K horizontal segment), and (5) the raw lavalier voice-over scratch track (one-take, roomy).

What to do, as one chained job:
1. Normalize and color-grade BOTH stills to a single house look. Start from the frame-grab: auto-tone it, warm the white balance to match the creator's window-lit "personal, upbeat" feel, lift the shadows and tame the highlights, then push vibrance so it reads lively without going garish, and finally stamp a chosen Lightroom preset so the grade is repeatable across future episodes. Apply the SAME finishing preset to the DSLR shot still so the two match.
2. From the graded DSLR still, build the YouTube thumbnail: isolate the creator (subject), invert to the background, knock the background's saturation down so the subject pops, then crop/resize to a clean 1280×720 16:9 thumbnail.
3. From the subject-popped full-res DSLR still, produce a 9:16 vertical "story / Shorts cover" still by subject-aware reframing/cropping the existing pixels to the 9:16 crop (no re-shoot, no invented canvas) and exporting at 1080×1920.
4. Turn the flat wordmark PNG into a crisp, infinitely-scalable SVG so it can sit in lower-thirds and the end screen without softening; keep that SVG as the channel mark.
5. Assemble the graded stills, thumbnail, story still and vector mark onto a Firefly board as the episode's brand-kit deliverable / handoff link.
6. Media touches: reframe the supplied 12s horizontal hero clip to a 9:16 vertical of the SAME length for Shorts (no trimming), and clean/denoise the roomy scratch voice-over so the VO is broadcast-usable.

Deliverables: graded frame-grab + graded DSLR still (matched look), a 1280×720 thumbnail, a 1080×1920 story still, the channel wordmark as SVG, a Firefly board link, a 9:16 same-length reframed clip, and a denoised VO track.
Deliverables (8)
  • framegrab_graded.png — Color-graded hero frame-grab, house look (warm WB, lifted shadows, controlled highlights, lively vibrance, finishing preset stamped), full-res PNG.
  • dslr_shot_graded.png — DSLR thumbnail still with the SAME finishing preset applied so it matches the frame-grab grade, full-res PNG.
  • thumbnail_1280x720.png — YouTube thumbnail, 1280x720 16:9, creator subject popped against a desaturated background.
  • story_still_1080x1920.png — Vertical story / Shorts cover still, 1080x1920 9:16, subject-aware reframe/crop of the full-res subject-popped DSLR still to the 9:16 crop using existing pixels only (no re-shoot, no invented canvas).
  • channel_mark.svg — Channel wordmark vectorized to a clean scalable SVG for lower-thirds and end screen.
  • brand_kit_board — Firefly board deep-link gathering the two graded stills, thumbnail, story still and vector mark as the episode brand-kit handoff.
  • hero_clip_vertical_9x16.mp4 — Hero clip reframed to 1080x1920 9:16, SAME length as source (no trim, no format change). [async]
  • vo_cleaned.wav — Denoised / de-reverbed voice-over scratch track, broadcast-usable. [async]
Workflow — 18 tool steps
  1. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize upload of the client-supplied assets (frame-grab, DSLR still, wordmark PNG, hero clip, VO wav) into CC storage.
  2. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the upload session; yields stable asset URIs for the frame-grab, DSLR still, wordmark, hero clip and VO that every later step references.
  3. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the flat 4K frame-grab to a sane baseline before the manual grade.
  4. image_adjust_color_temperature — Warm the white balance to kill the cool/video cast and hit the 'personal, upbeat' window-lit feel.
  5. image_adjust_dark_portions — Lift the crushed shadows of the underexposed video still to open up desk detail.
  6. image_adjust_highlights — Tame the blown window highlights recovered by the shadow lift.
  7. image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation — Push vibrance for a lively look without oversaturating skin.
  8. image_apply_preset — Stamp a chosen Lightroom preset as the repeatable house finishing look -> deliverable framegrab_graded.png.
  9. image_apply_preset — Apply the SAME finishing preset to the DSLR shot still so it matches the frame-grab grade -> deliverable dslr_shot_graded.png.
  10. image_select_subject — Mask the creator on the graded DSLR still for the subject-pop thumbnail.
  11. image_invert_selection — Invert the subject mask to target the busy background.
  12. image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation — Knock the background saturation down through the inverted mask so the subject pops.
  13. image_crop_and_resize — Crop/resize to a clean 1280x720 16:9 YouTube thumbnail -> deliverable.
  14. image_crop_and_resize — Subject-aware reframe/crop the full-res subject-popped DSLR still to a 1080x1920 9:16 story/Shorts cover (focus: subject, reframe within existing pixels, no inv
  15. image_vectorize — Vectorize the flat wordmark PNG into a clean scalable SVG for lower-thirds + end screen -> deliverable channel_mark.svg.
  16. create_firefly_board — Assemble the graded stills, thumbnail, story still and vector mark onto a Firefly board as the episode brand-kit handoff link.
  17. video_resize — Reframe the 16:9 hero clip to 1080x1920 9:16, SAME length (no trim/convert). Async-widget; product polls + notifies.
  18. media_enhance_speech — Denoise/de-reverb the roomy lavalier scratch VO so it is broadcast-usable. Async-widget; voice-only enhancement.
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon and wide-mix: 18 connector calls, 16 distinct Adobe tools, spanning four tool groups (asset I/O, the photo/raster image_* family, the Firefly board, and the async video/audio media tools). The grade is a genuine multi-stage Lightroom-class chain (auto-tone -> WB -> shadows -> highlights -> vibrance -> preset) rather than a one-shot filter, and the thumbnail requires correct masking logic (select_subject -> invert -> masked saturation) plus a format-bridge reframe-crop. It forces the agent to respect real connector limits: it cannot trim or re-cut the video, cannot author subtitles or an NLE project, and must recognize that the brief's literal video-edit core is out of scope while still delivering the legitimate Adobe-owned slice (color correction + still brand kit + same-length reframe + voice cleanup). The two [A] async steps mean part of the deliverable returns via the product's progress widget, not headlessly — the agent must tag and sequence those correctly. Grounding is faithful (color correction, lower-third/end-screen graphics, thumbnails, 1080p/9:16 social, ongoing per-episode cadence all come straight from the client), while the precise grade parameters and the vectorize/reframe-crop choices are reasonable professional enrichment.

AO-32 — Modern House Listing Photography — Post-Production Color Grade & Listing Export ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Edit the client's three supplied room frames (living room, kitchen, bedroom) into clean, modern, naturally-lit real-estate listing photos with straight verticals and corrected color, then export each in web-optimized JPEG (<2 MB) and full-resolution TIFF for Airbnb/Zillow.

💰 ~$20 est.Freelancer.com28 tool calls13 distinct toolslong-horizon
Full client brief
A landlord listing a freshly renovated rental on Airbnb and Zillow has captured interior frames of the three key areas — living room, kitchen, and bedroom — during a 0700-1200 natural-light window, but the RAW-style frames are tilted, slightly under-exposed in the shadows, mixed-white-balance (warm tungsten vs cool window daylight), and carry minor wall scuffs and dust marks. They want the post-production handled to a "modern, clean, simple, ample lighting, natural" standard: straight verticals and distortion-free perspective, basic color correction, gentle shadow lift and highlight recovery so the rooms read bright and spacious, quick blemish spotting on wall scuffs/dust WITHOUT an over-processed look, and final files delivered in two formats per the brief — web-optimized JPEG under 2 MB each AND full-resolution TIFF — within three days.

The freelancer must run an Adobe photo-editing chain per room: license/load the supplied frame, auto-straighten the verticals, crop out the resulting wedge to a clean distortion-free frame, apply auto-tone as a neutral baseline, neutralize the mixed white balance with a color-temperature correction, lift the dark portions and recover the blown window highlights, selectively brighten the room interior via a prompt-based mask so the space feels open without flattening the windows, spot-correct wall scuffs/dust by selecting the blemish and filling it with the matched neutral wall color (solid fill only — not generative), apply a final gentle vibrance/saturation lift for the "modern clean" feel, then crop-and-resize to the two delivery specs. Each room's edit is then assembled onto a Firefly Board so the client can review all three corrected listing shots in one deep-link before download. Across the three rooms the chain reuses the straighten→crop→tone→temperature→shadow/highlight→selective-brighten→spot-fix→vibrance→resize pattern, every step consuming the previous step's output.
Deliverables (7)
  • living_room_web.jpg — Web-optimized JPEG, longest edge ~2048px, sRGB, quality tuned to keep file under 2 MB; straight verticals, color-corrected, shadow-lifted, scuff/dust spotted, gentle modern grade.
  • living_room_full.tif — Full-resolution TIFF (native capture resolution), same corrected/graded pixels as the web version, for print/archive delivery.
  • kitchen_web.jpg — Web-optimized JPEG under 2 MB, ~2048px long edge, sRGB; straightened, white-balanced, highlight-recovered, scuff-spotted kitchen frame.
  • kitchen_full.tif — Full-resolution TIFF of the corrected kitchen frame for full-quality listing/print use.
  • bedroom_web.jpg — Web-optimized JPEG under 2 MB, ~2048px long edge, sRGB; straightened, exposure-balanced (window highlights recovered, interior brightened), spotted bedroom frame.
  • bedroom_full.tif — Full-resolution TIFF of the corrected bedroom frame.
  • listing_review_board — Firefly Board deep-link assembling the three corrected listing shots (living room, kitchen, bedroom) for one-glance client review before download.
Workflow — 28 tool steps
  1. asset_initialize_file_upload — Ingest the client-supplied living_room_raw.jpg into Adobe storage to begin the living-room edit chain (initialize → the upload session that subsequent edit step
  2. image_auto_straighten — Auto-straighten the ~2.5-degree tilt to deliver the 'straight verticals' the brief demands. Consumes the uploaded frame.
  3. image_crop_to_bounds — Crop away the wedge/empty corners introduced by straightening to a clean distortion-free rectangle. Consumes the straightened frame.
  4. image_apply_auto_tone — Neutral one-shot tonal baseline (the 'basic color correction' starting point) before targeted fixes. Consumes the cropped frame.
  5. image_adjust_color_temperature — Neutralize the warm-tungsten vs cool-daylight mixed white balance toward a natural neutral. Consumes the auto-toned frame.
  6. image_adjust_dark_portions — Lift the underexposed shadow corners so the room reads bright and spacious without crushing detail. Consumes the white-balanced frame.
  7. image_select_by_prompt — Prompt-select the wall scuff/dust speck region to set up a localized spot-fix. Consumes the shadow-lifted frame, returns a maskURI.
  8. image_fill_area — Solid-fill the selected scuff/dust with the matched neutral wall RGB — the 'quick retouching, not over-processed' the brief asks for (solid fill only, NOT gener
  9. image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation — Gentle vibrance lift for the 'modern, clean' finish without over-saturating. Consumes the spot-fixed frame — this is the master corrected living-room image, exp
  10. image_crop_and_resize — Export the corrected living-room master at native capture resolution as the full-resolution TIFF deliverable (no downscale; resize call used purely to write the
  11. image_crop_and_resize — Resize the same graded master down to ~2048px long edge and export the web-optimized JPEG (<2 MB) delivery. Consumes the graded master.
  12. asset_initialize_file_upload — Ingest the client-supplied kitchen_raw.jpg to begin the kitchen edit chain.
  13. image_auto_straighten — Straighten the ~2-degree tilt / leaning cabinet verticals. Consumes the uploaded kitchen frame.
  14. image_adjust_color_temperature — Correct the strong yellow-vs-blue cast on the stainless/quartz so whites read neutral. Consumes the straightened kitchen frame.
  15. image_adjust_highlights — Recover blown window/can-light highlights to hold appliance and countertop detail the brief wants emphasized. Consumes the white-balanced kitchen frame.
  16. image_select_by_prompt — Prompt-select the cabinet-door scuff/dust smudge for spot-fixing. Consumes the highlight-recovered frame, returns a maskURI.
  17. image_fill_area — Solid-fill the cabinet scuff with the matched white cabinet RGB — the 'quick retouching, not over-processed' the brief asks for (solid fill only, NOT generative
  18. image_crop_and_resize — Export the corrected kitchen master at native capture resolution as the full-resolution TIFF deliverable (no downscale). Consumes the kitchen master.
  19. image_crop_and_resize — Resize the corrected kitchen master to ~2048px long edge and export the <2 MB web JPEG. Consumes the kitchen master.
  20. asset_initialize_file_upload — Ingest the client-supplied bedroom_raw.jpg to begin the bedroom edit chain.
  21. image_auto_straighten — Straighten the ~3-degree tilt / converging window verticals. Consumes the uploaded bedroom frame.
  22. image_adjust_highlights — Pull back the blown-out window highlights (the classic real-estate window/light-shot problem). Consumes the straightened bedroom frame.
  23. image_adjust_dark_portions — Lift the dark interior so the room balances against the recovered window. Consumes the highlight-recovered frame.
  24. image_select_by_prompt — Prompt-select the room interior (excluding the bright window) to set up a selective brightening that opens the space without re-blowing the window. Consumes the
  25. image_adjust_light_portions — Brighten only the masked interior for the 'bright, spacious, move-in-ready' feel the brief wants, leaving the window untouched. Consumes the frame + interior ma
  26. image_crop_and_resize — Export the corrected bedroom master at native capture resolution as the full-resolution TIFF deliverable (no downscale). Consumes the bedroom master.
  27. image_crop_and_resize — Resize the corrected bedroom master to ~2048px long edge and export the <2 MB web JPEG. Consumes the bedroom master.
  28. create_firefly_board — Assemble the three corrected room masters into one Firefly Board deep-link for the client to review all listing shots before downloading the JPEG/TIFF set. Cons
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon flagship: 28 connector calls, 13 distinct Adobe tools, spanning three groups (A4 asset ingest, A1 photo raster, A4 board assembly) with strict output→input chaining and three converging sub-chains. It is hard because the agent must (a) recognize that live capture is out of scope and re-scope to post-production only, (b) order operations correctly (straighten BEFORE crop, white-balance and tone BEFORE selective edits, mask BEFORE fill/adjust), (c) respect connector limits — image_fill_area is SOLID-color only so scuff removal is a select→matched-RGB-fill, not generative healing or AI object removal, and selective brightening must use a prompt mask + adjust rather than any compositing, (d) satisfy the brief's dual-format delivery by exporting each corrected master twice — a native-resolution TIFF and a downscaled <2 MB web JPEG, both via discrete image_crop_and_resize calls — under the 'natural, not over-processed' constraint via gentle, bounded adjustments. Every step is [C] headless-confirmed, so the whole chain is autonomously runnable with no [W]/[A]/[T]/[X] dependencies, yet it exercises masking, localized tonal control, white-balance, geometry, and multi-output export end-to-end.

Client-supplied input assets: 3 total · 3 img · 0 video · 0 audio · 0 data
