AO-34 — Informative explainer video: brand-graded sizzle cut, cleaned VO, and on-brand lower-third / call-out graphic plates ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Turn my raw informative-explainer footage, scratch voiceover, brand logo, and color-reference still into a polished short cut: clean the VO, build a brand-locked color look plus a graded end-card, and produce on-brand lower-third, call-out, and vectorized-logo graphic plates — then deliver a 1080p sizzle and a vertical reframe, all assets organised on a board for the Premiere project.

💰 ~$85 est.Freelancer.com25 tool calls21 distinct toolslong-horizon
Full client brief
I have raw footage for an informative explainer video that needs to be shaped into a clear, engaging cut, plus the supporting brand assets to dress it. Source material I'm supplying: (1) a bundle of raw clips — a ~3-minute talking-head segment intercut with product/process b-roll; (2) a separate scratch voiceover WAV recorded in a noisy room that needs cleaning before it can carry the edit; (3) my company logo as a flat PNG; (4) a single "color reference" photograph that represents the warm, slightly cinematic brand look I want the whole piece graded toward; and (5) a loose narrative outline.

I need the freelancer to handle the full edit and the supporting graphics, mapped to Adobe Creative Cloud connector tools, with every step honestly tagged for execution mode:

A. AUDIO — clean my scratch voiceover so it can drive the cut: denoise / de-room the voice track (voice-only; this is not music mastering).

B. VIDEO — (1) summarize the raw footage so we agree on the key talking points and a logical flow before cutting; (2) produce a punchy ~60-second highlight/sizzle cut from the raw clips that surfaces those key points; (3) reframe that cut to a 9:16 vertical version for social teasers, same length (no trimming).

C. BRAND COLOR LOOK — from my color-reference still, build the locked "brand LUT" look the editor will match the master grade to: straighten and crop it clean, then run the full Lightroom-class grade (auto-tone baseline, exposure, highlights, dark-portion lift, warm color-temperature push, HSL + vibrance/saturation shaping, a gentle preset and fine grain) so I have one authoritative graded reference frame.

D. GRAPHIC PLATES — the on-brand overlays the editor lays onto the timeline (delivered as standalone transparent PNG / SVG plates, NOT composited onto the video): (1) vectorize my logo to a clean SVG so it scales for the end-card and corner bug; (2) build a lower-third bar plate — solid brand-color bar generated by selecting a region and filling it; (3) build a call-out badge plate the same way; (4) grade the end-card hero frame to match the brand look.

E. PACKAGING — gather the graded reference, the plates, the cleaned VO, and the video deliverables onto a Firefly board so the whole linked-asset set is organised for easy future tweaks, exactly as I want the Premiere project handed off.

Deliverables on approval: cleaned voiceover; a ~60s 1080p H.264 sizzle cut and a 9:16 reframe (same length); one authoritative graded brand-reference frame; logo SVG; lower-third and call-out PNG plates; a graded end-card frame; and a Firefly board linking the whole organised asset set. Two rounds of feedback should be plenty.
Deliverables (9)
  • cleaned_voiceover.wav — Denoised / de-reverbed voiceover track, voice-only, suitable as the edit's narration bed (media_enhance_speech output).
  • sizzle_cut_1080p.mp4 — ~60s AI highlight/sizzle cut of the raw clips surfacing the key talking points, 1920x1080 (H.264 target on export).
  • sizzle_cut_vertical_9x16.mp4 — 9:16 (1080x1920) reframe of the sizzle cut, SAME length (no trimming), for social teasers.
  • brand_look_reference_frame.jpg — One authoritative graded brand-reference frame: straightened, cropped, and run through the full Lightroom-class grade — the locked look the editor matches the master grade to.
  • logo_vector.svg — Clean vectorized SVG of the company logo for scalable end-card and corner-bug use.
  • lower_third_bar_plate.png — Transparent-background lower-third bar plate — solid brand-amber bar region filled via selection-fill, ready to drop on the Premiere timeline.
  • callout_badge_plate.png — Transparent-background call-out badge plate — solid brand-color badge region, same selection-fill technique, for on-screen fact call-outs.
  • graded_endcard_frame.jpg — End-card hero frame graded to match the locked brand look, with right-side negative space for the logo lockup.
  • northloop_asset_board — Firefly board deep-link gathering the graded reference, plates, logo SVG, cleaned VO, and video deliverables as one organised linked-asset set for the Premiere handoff.
Workflow — 25 tool steps
  1. asset_initialize_file_upload — Begin uploading the five client-supplied assets (raw clips bundle, scratch VO wav, logo png, brand color reference jpg, end-card hero jpg) into CC storage to st
  2. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the multipart uploads; returns the asset IDs/URIs for clips, VO, logo, color-reference, and end-card used by every downstream step.
  3. media_enhance_speech — Denoise/de-room the scratch voiceover (voice-only) so it can drive the cut. [A] async-widget.
  4. media_summarize — Summarize the raw clips bundle to agree on key talking points / logical flow before cutting. Consumes the uploaded video asset. [A].
  5. video_create_quick_cut — Build the ~60s highlight/sizzle cut from the raw clips, prompted by the agreed key points from step 4. [A] async-widget.
  6. video_resize — Reframe the sizzle cut to 9:16 vertical for social teasers — SAME length, no trimming. Consumes the step-5 cut. [A].
  7. image_auto_straighten — Straighten the deliberately-tilted brand color-reference still (consumes the uploaded color-reference). [C].
  8. image_crop_to_bounds — Crop the straightened reference clean to remove the loose headroom. Consumes step-7 output. [C].
  9. image_apply_auto_tone — Lay down an auto-tone baseline on the cropped reference before manual grading. Consumes step-8. [C].
  10. image_adjust_exposure — Set overall exposure for the brand look. Consumes step-9. [C].
  11. image_adjust_highlights — Roll off highlights for the soft film-like top end. Consumes step-10. [C].
  12. image_adjust_dark_portions — Lift the dark portions for a lifted-shadow cinematic base. Consumes step-11. [C].
  13. image_adjust_color_temperature — Push warm color temperature to establish the amber brand tone. Consumes step-12. [C].
  14. image_adjust_hsl — Shape the amber/teal hue-sat-lum split that defines the look. Consumes step-13. [C].
  15. image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation — Final vibrance/saturation balance on the reference grade. Consumes step-14. [C].
  16. image_apply_preset — Apply a gentle Lightroom preset to seal the cinematic character. Consumes step-15. [C].
  17. image_add_grain — Add subtle film grain to finalize the authoritative locked brand-look reference frame. Consumes step-16. [C].
  18. image_vectorize — Vectorize the flat logo PNG to a clean scalable SVG for end-card and corner bug. Consumes the uploaded logo. [C].
  19. image_select_by_prompt — On the vectorized logo's already-transparent canvas (reused as the plate canvas, NOT the opaque graded frame), select a horizontal lower-third bar region so the
  20. image_fill_area — Solid-fill the selected region with brand amber (#E8A13A) to produce the lower-third bar plate (solid-color fill only). Consumes step-19. [C].
  21. image_select_by_prompt — On the same plate canvas, select a rounded badge region for the call-out. Consumes step-20. [C].
  22. image_fill_area — Solid-fill the badge region with the deep-navy accent to produce the call-out badge plate. Consumes step-21. [C].
  23. image_apply_auto_tone — Begin grading the end-card hero frame: auto-tone baseline on the uploaded ungraded end-card still. [C].
  24. image_adjust_color_temperature — Warm the end-card to MATCH the locked brand-look reference frame from step 17, finishing the graded end-card. [C].
  25. create_firefly_board — Gather every produced deliverable (cleaned VO, both video cuts, graded reference, logo SVG, both plates, graded end-card) into one Firefly board deep-link as th
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon flagship: 25 connector calls, 21 distinct Adobe tools, spanning four groups (asset/storage, video/audio [A], photo/raster grade [C], vector + board). It exercises an 11-link linear color-grade dependency chain (each adjust_* consuming the previous frame), a video sub-chain where a summary prompts a sizzle cut that is then reframed, a selection→solid-fill plate sub-chain reused twice, and a cross-branch match (end-card grade references the locked look frame). Difficulty comes from (a) honoring the [A] async vs [C] headless split correctly — the video/audio deliverables are async-widget, the stills/plates fully headless; (b) respecting the hard limits (no compositing of text onto video, no gen-fill, fill_area solid-only, video_resize same-length) while still satisfying the brief's 'motion graphics / text overlays' intent via standalone graphic plates; and (c) keeping every post-load step strictly output→input chained into a single coherent Premiere-handoff package.

AO-35 — Restore a 1910 magician-troupe postcard: clarify the dark front photo and make the handwritten back legible, delivered as two book/social-ready files ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Digitize and restore one 1910 magician-troupe postcard — clarify and lighten the dark sepia front photo and sharpen the contrast of the handwritten back so the description is legible — and deliver each side as its own clean, deskewed, print-and-social-ready file.

💰 ~$20 est.Upwork18 tool calls14 distinct toolslong-horizon
Full client brief
A family-history client is digitizing ~24 antique postcard photographs from 1910 documenting a famous stage magician and his traveling company touring Asia and abroad; the client's grandmother and aunt performed in the troupe and wrote a description of each scene on the BACK of the postcards. The client needs every postcard delivered as two separate restored files — the photographic FRONT as one picture and the handwritten BACK as another — that are clean enough to be pasted into a printed memorial book and posted to social media. Many of the front photos are dark, low-contrast, and tilted from flatbed scanning; the handwriting on the backs is faint cursive pencil/ink that is hard to read. This benchmark uses ONE representative postcard as the worked end-to-end example. The client supplies two raw flatbed scans: (1) the postcard front (a dark, slightly rotated sepia-toned albumen-style photograph of the magician and company on a theatre stage, with scanner-bed border) and (2) the postcard back (faded cursive handwriting plus a postmark and divided-back postcard printing, also slightly skewed). Required restoration pipeline, per side: ingest both raw scans into the workspace; auto-straighten each skewed scan and crop away the scanner-bed margins to the postcard edge; on the FRONT, recover the dark midtones and shadows, lift exposure and overall brightness/contrast, rein in any blown highlight on the bright stage lights, gently recover the crushed dark portions where the company stands in shadow, neutralize the scanner's cool cast and re-warm to a consistent archival sepia, and finish with a vintage-photo preset look that keeps the period feel while making faces and stage detail readable; on the BACK, push brightness and contrast hard and convert to a clean high-legibility monochrome/sepia so the faint cursive description reads clearly. Deliver a high-resolution restored FRONT and a high-resolution restored BACK, each deskewed and edge-cropped, color-consistent, copy-paste-ready for the book layout and for social posts. Separately document (out-of-band, from the parameters recorded during the run) the exact per-card operation sequence so the same recipe can be re-run across the remaining 23 postcards.
Deliverables (3)
  • Magician_Postcard_01_FRONT_restored.tif — Restored postcard FRONT: deskewed and edge-cropped to the card, dark midtones/shadows recovered, exposure and brightness/contrast lifted, highlights tamed, re-warmed to a consistent archival sepia wit
  • Magician_Postcard_01_BACK_legible.tif — Restored postcard BACK: deskewed and edge-cropped, brightness/contrast pushed and converted to a clean high-legibility monochrome/sepia so the faint cursive description and postmark read clearly; long
  • restoration_recipe_notes.txt — Out-of-band plain-text log (compiled by the operator from the parameters recorded during the connector run, NOT emitted by any Adobe connector step) documenting the exact per-card operation sequence a
Workflow — 18 tool steps
  1. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize upload of the two raw client scans (front + back) into the Adobe workspace so every later step has real source URIs.
  2. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize both uploads; returns the canonical asset URIs for the raw front and back scans that seed the two restoration chains.
  3. image_auto_straighten — Auto-level the ~4-degree-rotated FRONT scan so the postcard edges are square before cropping. (The same call is re-run on the back at step 13, which begins the
  4. image_crop_to_bounds — Crop away the gray scanner-bed margin to the postcard edge, giving a clean FRONT frame.
  5. image_apply_auto_tone — First-pass automatic tonal balance on the dark FRONT — establishes a baseline before targeted manual recovery.
  6. image_adjust_exposure — Lift overall exposure to brighten the dark photo as the client explicitly asked ('lightened and clear').
  7. image_adjust_dark_portions — Selectively recover the crushed shadow areas where the company stands in stage shadow, pulling detail out of the darks.
  8. image_adjust_highlights — Rein in the blown bright stage-light spot so brightening doesn't clip the highlights.
  9. image_adjust_brightness_and_contrast — Set final overall brightness and contrast so faces and stage detail are clearly readable ('clear').
  10. image_adjust_color_temperature — Neutralize the cool/greenish scanner cast and re-warm toward a consistent archival sepia.
  11. image_apply_preset — Apply a vintage/period-look Lightroom preset (chosen from image_list_presets) to lock a consistent archival aesthetic across the 24 cards while keeping period f
  12. image_crop_and_resize — Derive the 1080 px social-media JPEG variant of the restored FRONT from the same master (book uses the full-res master, social uses this).
  13. image_auto_straighten — Begin the BACK chain: auto-level the ~3-degree-rotated back scan before cropping.
  14. image_crop_to_bounds — Crop the scanner margin off the BACK to the postcard edge.
  15. image_adjust_brightness_and_contrast — Push brightness and contrast HARD to separate the faint cursive ink from the yellowed paper so the description becomes legible (core client ask: 'writing on the
  16. image_apply_monochromatic_tint — Convert to a clean high-legibility monochrome/sepia tint, killing the foxing color noise so the handwriting and postmark read clearly. Finished restored BACK ma
  17. image_crop_and_resize — Derive the 1080 px social variant of the legible BACK from its master, matching the FRONT social output.
  18. create_firefly_board — Assemble the four produced deliverables (FRONT master, BACK master, and the two social variants) into one Firefly Board deep-link as the client review/handoff s
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon and wide-mix: 18 connector calls, 14 distinct Adobe tools, spanning asset ingest, geometry (auto_straighten, crop_to_bounds, crop_and_resize), a full tonal-recovery stack (auto_tone, exposure, dark_portions, highlights, brightness_and_contrast), color (color_temperature, monochromatic_tint), a preset look, and a Firefly Board handoff. It is genuinely hard because it requires correct ORDERING of a real restoration workflow — deskew and crop must precede tonal work; shadow/exposure recovery must precede highlight protection and the final brightness/contrast; color-cast neutralization must precede the period preset — and it splits into two divergent sub-recipes (rich tonal+sepia recovery for the photographic front vs. aggressive contrast+monochrome legibility for the handwritten back) that nonetheless share the ingest and the deliverable-packaging stages. Every step is headless-confirmed [C] per the capability sheet (no [W]/[A]/[T]/[X] steps, no generative fill, no compositing, no colorization, no upscaling), so it is a fully autonomous, faithfully-grounded restoration benchmark.

AO-37 — Restore Three Faded, Scratched Family Photo Scans for A4 Wall Prints ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Restore three damaged old photo scans — correct color fading, conceal scratches/tears over plain areas, and maximize clarity — then output each as a print-ready A4 (2480x3508 px, 300 DPI) file.

💰 ~$100 est.Freelancer.com20 tool calls14 distinct toolslong-horizon
Full client brief
I have several old family photo scans that I want to restore and print at A4 to mount on my wall. They suffer from three problems: color fading (the older color shots have gone yellow/orange and flat), scratches or tears (white scratch lines and a torn corner, mostly running across plain backdrops and sky), and blurriness — and the MAIN priority is improving overall clarity so they look crisp on a large print.

Please work from the three supplied scans:
1. faded_color_portrait_1968.png — a colour studio portrait from the late 1960s, heavily yellowed, low contrast, slightly tilted on the scanner bed.
2. scratched_bw_studio_1955.png — a black-and-white studio photo with several fine white scratch lines crossing the plain grey studio backdrop behind the subject.
3. torn_outdoor_snapshot_1972.png — a colour outdoor snapshot with a torn top-left corner exposing white paper and a couple of scratches across the open sky.

For each photo I need you to: straighten any scanner tilt and crop off the ragged scan border; correct the colour cast and faded contrast so skin tones and colours look natural again; lift overall clarity and local contrast so detail reads on a big print; conceal the scratches and the torn corner ONLY where they cross plain, uniform regions (the studio backdrop and the open sky) by colour-matching and filling those marks so they disappear; add a very light film grain so the restored areas don't look plastic against the original photographic texture; and finally produce a clean, true-A4 file at 2480x3508 px / 300 DPI ready to send to the print lab.

Deliverables: three restored, A4 print-ready image files (one per scan), plus a single before/after contact-board so I can approve the restoration at a glance before printing.
Deliverables (4)
  • portrait_1968_restored_A4.png — Restored late-1960s color portrait: tilt-straightened, border-cropped, color-cast neutralized, faded contrast and clarity recovered, light grain re-added. Final A4 print size 2480x3508 px at 300 DPI,
  • bw_studio_1955_restored_A4.png — Restored 1955 B&W studio photo: backdrop scratches concealed by matched solid-grey fill, tonal range and clarity recovered, light grain re-added. Final A4 print size 2480x3508 px at 300 DPI, PNG.
  • snapshot_1972_restored_A4.png — Restored 1972 outdoor snapshot: torn corner and sky scratches concealed by matched solid sky-blue fill, color cast corrected, contrast/clarity recovered, light grain re-added. Final A4 print size 2480
  • restoration_before_after_board — A single Firefly Board deep-link assembling the three original scans next to their three restored A4 outputs as a before/after approval contact-board for the client to sign off before printing.
Workflow — 20 tool steps
  1. asset_initialize_file_upload — Client supplies the scans, not Adobe Stock, so ingest the three provided files (faded_color_portrait_1968.png, scratched_bw_studio_1955.png, torn_outdoor_snapsh
  2. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the upload to obtain stable asset URNs/URLs for the three scans that the editing steps will reference. PHOTO 1 (portrait) is processed in steps 3-8; PH
  3. image_auto_straighten — PHOTO 1: correct the 2-3 degree scanner tilt on the faded color portrait so the crop in the next step is clean.
  4. image_crop_to_bounds — PHOTO 1: crop off the ragged uneven white scan border revealed/levelled by straightening, leaving only the photographic image area.
  5. image_apply_auto_tone — PHOTO 1: auto-tone as the first global recovery pass on the flat, faded C-print to re-spread the tonal range before targeted color work.
  6. image_adjust_color_temperature — PHOTO 1: cool the strong yellow-orange age cast (shift temperature toward blue) to neutralize the color and bring skin tones back to natural. Consumes the auto-
  7. image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation — PHOTO 1: gently restore the faded color saturation/vibrance after the cast is gone, so muted fabrics and skin regain life without oversaturating.
  8. image_adjust_brightness_and_contrast — PHOTO 1: final clarity/contrast lift — raise local contrast to recover the perceived sharpness the brief calls the MAIN priority (no sharpen tool exists; clarit
  9. image_select_by_prompt — PHOTO 2 (B&W): select the plain grey studio backdrop region that carries the white scratch lines, so the scratches over a UNIFORM area can be concealed with a m
  10. image_fill_area — PHOTO 2: solid-color fill the selected backdrop with the matched medium-grey of the seamless, covering the white scratch lines so they vanish into the uniform b
  11. image_apply_auto_tone — PHOTO 2: auto-tone the now-clean B&W to recover the silver-gelatin tonal range flattened by fading. Consumes the scratch-concealed image.
  12. image_apply_preset — PHOTO 2: apply a B&W clarity/contrast Lightroom preset (from image_list_presets) to deliver the crisp, high-clarity monochrome look for the A4 print. Produces t
  13. image_crop_to_bounds — PHOTO 3 (snapshot): crop off the crooked thin white scan border so only the image content remains before damage concealment and grading.
  14. image_select_by_prompt — PHOTO 3: select the plain pale-blue sky region that contains both the torn white corner triangle and the sky scratches — a uniform area where a matched solid fi
  15. image_fill_area — PHOTO 3: solid-fill the selected sky with the matched sky-blue, covering the torn-corner white triangle and the scratch lines so they disappear into the uniform
  16. image_adjust_color_temperature — PHOTO 3: correct the aged magenta/cyan color shift of the faded C-print toward neutral. Consumes the concealed image.
  17. image_adjust_brightness_and_contrast — PHOTO 3: recover faded contrast and lift clarity/local contrast for the print. Produces the fully restored snapshot.
  18. image_add_grain — Add a very light, uniform film grain to all three restored images so the solid-filled concealment patches and re-graded areas don't look plastic against the ori
  19. image_crop_and_resize — Crop-and-resize each grained image to true A4 print dimensions 2480x3508 px at 300 DPI (run per image), yielding portrait_1968_restored_A4.png, bw_studio_1955_r
  20. create_firefly_board — Assemble the three original scan URNs alongside the three restored A4 outputs into a Firefly Board before/after approval contact-board (deep-link) for client si
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon and wide-mix: 20 connector calls and 14 distinct Adobe tools across 3 groups (asset ingest, the image_* raster family, and the Firefly board), with three independent per-photo restoration sub-chains that converge into a shared grain+A4+board tail — far above the >=10 calls / >=6 distinct tools bar. Difficulty comes from sequencing damage-type-specific fixes correctly (straighten before crop; conceal scratches before re-toning; neutralize cast before recovering saturation; grain last so it overlays the solid-fill patches) and from honest scoping: the agent must recognize that the connector has NO generative heal/clone/object-removal and NO sharpen, so 'scratches/tears' are concealed only where they cross uniform regions via select-by-prompt + solid fill_area, and 'clarity' is delivered through tonal/local-contrast shaping and a clarity preset rather than an invented sharpen op. Every step is [C] headless-confirmed, so the whole workflow is autonomously runnable, but a naive agent that reaches for gen-fill, object-removal, upscaling, or a sharpen tool would violate the capability sheet — making correct tool discipline the core challenge.

AO-38 — Logo + Four Challenge-Coin Marks: Concept Rasters to Coin-Ready Vector Artwork with Black/White/Gold/Silver Variants ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Turn the client's five supplied concept rasters (one logo + four challenge-coin references) into clean, coin-ready vector marks, each delivered as transparent-PNG and SVG in black, white, gold, and silver.

💰 ~$145 est.Upwork19 tool calls14 distinct toolslong-horizon
Full client brief
A challenge-coin / branding client (automotive-industrial sector) supplies five flat reference images: one primary logo concept and four symbolic coin-face concepts. They are pixelated mock-ups that cannot go to a mint. The task is to convert each into production-grade vector art and deliver a four-color family per mark.

Pipeline per the connector chain: (1) upload all five client rasters to CC storage and preview them; (2) on the primary logo, remove the busy background so only the mark survives on transparency; (3) auto-straighten the de-bagged logo so the mark sits square (coins are minted on a true axis); (4) crop the straightened logo tight to its own bounds so the trace has no dead margin; (5) reframe/resize that cropped mark to a fixed 2000x2000 px square proof canvas via image_crop_and_resize (a gen-free crop-and-scale to the target aspect — no pixels are invented), establishing the exact resolution every variant export inherits; (6) vectorize that clean, square, transparent master into a scalable SVG — this is the editable master vector that also seeds EPS/PDF on packaging; (7) select the mark's silhouette on the 2000x2000 master, then fill it with solid true-black to mint the BLACK variant; (8) on the same selected silhouette, fill it with solid white to mint the WHITE variant; (9) apply a flat color overlay in gold #C8A24B to mint the GOLD variant; (10) apply a monochromatic silver/gray tint to mint the SILVER variant. Then the four coin references are run through the same remove-background -> crop -> vectorize spine so all five marks are coin-ready vector. Finally, assemble every produced vector + variant asset into a single Firefly Board so the client can review the full logo+four-coin family in black/white/gold/silver before mint submission.

Hard constraints honored: no from-scratch illustration (every mark is traced from a client reference), no generative fill / outpaint / object removal / compositing / upscaling; image_crop_and_resize only crops-and-scales the existing mark to the square proof canvas (it never grows or invents canvas), image_fill_area is used only for SOLID color minting (black and white) of an isolated mask, and no generative tool is used at any step. Deliverables map 1:1 to the brief: transparent PNGs, SVG vector source, and black/white/gold/silver color versions, coin-ready and readable at small sizes.
Deliverables (8)
  • logo_vector_master.svg — Clean scalable SVG traced from the cropped, background-free, straightened, 2000x2000-normalized primary logo; editable master vector (seeds EPS / vector-PDF on packaging); coin-ready, readable at <=15
  • logo_variant_black.png — Transparent PNG, 2000x2000 px, logo silhouette filled solid true-black #000000.
  • logo_variant_white.png — Transparent PNG, 2000x2000 px, logo silhouette filled solid white #FFFFFF (the on-light / reversed variant).
  • logo_variant_gold.png — Transparent PNG, 2000x2000 px, logo flat gold #C8A24B color overlay.
  • logo_variant_silver.png — Transparent PNG, 2000x2000 px, logo silver/gray #AAAAAA monochromatic tint.
  • coin_vectors_x4.svg — Four clean SVG coin-face vectors (eagle, gear-globe, anvil, tractor), each traced from its background-removed reference, coin-ready for mint review.
  • coin_review_board — Firefly Board deep-link assembling the logo master + four coin vectors + black/white/gold/silver variant proofs into one client-review moodboard.
  • ironline_coin_package.zip — Single ZIP bundling all SVG vector sources, transparent PNG exports, and the black/white/gold/silver variant set, organized per-mark for mint/manufacturer handoff.
Workflow — 19 tool steps
  1. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize a CC-storage upload session for the five client-supplied concept rasters (logo + 4 coin refs).
  2. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the upload; returns stored asset handles/URLs for all five rasters.
  3. asset_preview_file — Preview the primary logo raster to confirm the mark and its busy background before processing (inspect only).
  4. image_remove_background — Strip the cluttered workshop background from the primary logo -> transparent PNG of the bare mark.
  5. image_auto_straighten — Auto-straighten the isolated mark so it sits on a true axis (coins mint on-axis).
  6. image_crop_to_bounds — Crop tight to the mark's own bounds, removing dead transparent margin so the trace is clean.
  7. image_crop_and_resize — Reframe/scale the cropped mark to a fixed 2000x2000 px square proof canvas (gen-free crop-and-scale to target aspect; no pixels invented). Establishes the exact
  8. image_vectorize — Trace the clean, square, transparent logo master into a scalable SVG -> the editable master vector deliverable.
  9. image_select_subject — Select the logo silhouette on the 2000x2000 master to drive the solid-color minting of the black and white variants.
  10. image_fill_area — Solid-fill the selected silhouette with true-black #000000 -> BLACK variant (image_fill_area = solid color only), 2000x2000 px.
  11. image_fill_area — Solid-fill the same selected silhouette with white #FFFFFF -> WHITE / reversed variant (image_fill_area = solid color only), 2000x2000 px.
  12. image_apply_color_overlay — Apply a flat gold #C8A24B color overlay to the master -> GOLD variant, 2000x2000 px.
  13. image_apply_monochromatic_tint — Apply a silver/gray #AAAAAA monochromatic tint -> SILVER variant, 2000x2000 px.
  14. image_remove_background — Run the four coin references through background removal (eagle / gear-globe / anvil / tractor), one call per image -> transparent coin faces.
  15. image_crop_to_bounds — Crop each coin face tight to bounds before tracing (one call per image).
  16. image_vectorize — Trace the four cropped coin faces into clean coin-ready SVG vectors (one vectorize call per coin; four outputs collected under this handle).
  17. create_firefly_board — Assemble the logo master, four coin vectors, and black/white/gold/silver variant proofs (all CC assets) into one Firefly Board for client review.
  18. asset_copy_assets — Copy all produced vectors + variant PNGs into a structured per-mark deliverables folder in CC storage.
  19. local_zip — Single unavoidable local packaging step: zip the organized SVG/PNG/variant family into one mint-handoff archive.
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon (17 steps, 13 distinct Adobe tools) spanning asset I/O, raster cleanup, geometry, raster-to-vector, masked solid minting, and board assembly. Difficulty comes from the two parallel chained spines (logo + four coins) plus a three-way variant fan-out from a single cropped master, all of which must stay on-axis and crop-tight so the trace yields mint-grade vectors readable at small sizes. It is faithful to the brief (refine provided concepts into true vector art; black/white/gold/silver; SVG + transparent PNG; coin-ready) while respecting every capability limit: vectorize is the genuine raster->vector path, fill_area mints only solid color on an isolated mask, color_overlay/monochromatic_tint do the metallic tints, and no banned capability (gen-fill, object removal, compositing, text-to-image, upscaling, layout composition) appears. Only one unavoidable [L] step (final zip); all 16 core steps are headless-confirmed [C].

Client-supplied input assets: 1 total · 1 img · 0 video · 0 audio · 0 data

Images (1)

logo_concept_primary.png
gemini

AO-39 — Apparel Shirt Isolation, Background Cleanup and Marketplace-Ready E-commerce Set ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Turn a messy worn-shirt hero photo plus a flat-lay reference shirt into a clean, colour-true, marketplace-ready apparel product set (cleaned studio backdrop, isolated transparent packshot, vectorized logo, square + catalog crops).

💰 ~$110 est.Freelancer.com21 tool calls15 distinct toolslong-horizon
Full client brief
We sell a small line of cotton shirts and our marketplace listings look amateur. I'm sending you three things: (1) HERO_WORN.jpg — a model wearing our flagship olive-green button-down in a cluttered home studio (laundry, cables and a coat rack are visible behind her, and the shot is slightly tilted and a touch under-exposed); (2) SHIRT_FLATLAY.jpg — a clean-ish flat-lay of "the similar shirt" (the same garment laid flat on a light table) that we want isolated as a drop-in packshot for the listing thumbnail; (3) BRAND_LOGO.png — our small wordmark logo we want as crisp vector for the woven label and the listing banner. Please: straighten and tone-correct the hero so it looks professionally lit; cut the model cleanly off the messy background and put her on a clean solid-white studio backdrop (the clutter must be gone); make sure the olive-green of the shirt reads true and rich, not muddy; reframe the cleaned hero to a wide catalog banner shape centered on the model (a horizontal crop of the same cleaned frame — I understand the top and bottom of the shot get trimmed to hit the wide ratio); isolate the flat-lay shirt to a transparent PNG packshot; vectorize the logo to SVG; and hand back the hero as both a 1:1 Instagram square and a clean catalog frame, plus the transparent packshot and the SVG logo. Attention to detail on the edge of the collar and sleeves is essential.
Deliverables (5)
  • hero_square_1x1.jpg — 1:1 Instagram square, 2048x2048 px, JPEG, sRGB. The master cleaned/graded hero (straightened, toned, white-balanced, subject isolated on solid-white studio backdrop, olive shirt colour corrected) cent
  • hero_catalog_banner.jpg — Wide catalog/banner crop, 2400x1200 px (~2:1), JPEG, sRGB. An HONEST horizontal reframe of the same master cleaned hero via crop_and_resize — the existing frame is cropped to the wide ratio centered o
  • shirt_packshot.png — Transparent-background PNG packshot of the isolated olive flat-lay shirt (RGBA, alpha channel), pale-grey light-table background removed with clean collar/sleeve edges, native flat-lay resolution (~20
  • northgrove_logo.svg — Vector SVG of the NORTHGROVE 2-color wordmark (charcoal type + olive leaf glyph) traced from the raster logo, scalable crisp paths for woven-label and banner use. DELIVERABLE 4 (step 19).
  • apparel_set.zip — Single CC folder / ZIP bundle gathering the four leaf deliverables (hero_square_1x1.jpg, hero_catalog_banner.jpg, shirt_packshot.png, northgrove_logo.svg) for marketplace handoff. DELIVERABLE 5 (step
Workflow — 21 tool steps
  1. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize the upload of the client's HERO_WORN.jpg into CC storage so the connector can operate on it. (Initial asset load — the only step with no upstream cha
  2. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the upload begun in step 1; yields the stored hero asset URI that every hero step downstream consumes.
  3. image_auto_straighten — Fix the ~4deg tilt on the uploaded hero. Consumes the asset URI from step 2.
  4. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the straightened hero to fix the flat under-exposure. Consumes step 3 output.
  5. image_adjust_color_temperature — Neutralize the mixed tungsten/window white balance so colours read true. Consumes step 4 output.
  6. image_select_subject — Auto-select the model (subject) on the colour-corrected hero, producing a maskURI. Consumes step 5 output. Sets up the background cleanup.
  7. image_invert_selection — Invert the subject mask to target everything BEHIND the model (the messy coat-rack/cables/laundry). Consumes the mask from step 6 against the step-5 image.
  8. image_fill_area — Solid-fill the inverted background region with preset white — this is the honest, in-scope 'remove background mess': clutter is covered by a clean studio-white
  9. image_select_by_prompt — Prompt-select just the olive shirt region on the cleaned hero so the next step can correct only the garment colour. Consumes step 8 output.
  10. image_adjust_single_color_saturation — Boost the green channel saturation on the masked shirt so the muddy olive reads rich and true (selective, mask-driven). Consumes the shirt mask from step 9 over
  11. image_crop_and_resize — Honestly reframe the master cleaned hero to the wide ~2:1 banner shape by CROPPING the existing frame horizontally, centered on the model (gen-free: a crop of t
  12. image_crop_and_resize — Crop/resize the master cleaned hero to a 1:1 2048x2048 Instagram square. DELIVERABLE 1. Consumes step 10 output.
  13. image_crop_and_resize — Resize the wide-reframed hero to the ~2:1 2400x1200 catalog banner deliverable size (JPEG). No canvas extension — this exports the honest horizontal crop from s
  14. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize upload of the client's SHIRT_FLATLAY.jpg packshot reference.
  15. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the flat-lay upload; yields its stored asset URI.
  16. image_remove_background — Cut the olive shirt off the pale-grey light-table background -> transparent PNG packshot with clean collar/sleeve edges. DELIVERABLE 3. Consumes step 15 output.
  17. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize upload of the client's BRAND_LOGO.png wordmark.
  18. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the logo upload; yields its stored asset URI.
  19. image_vectorize — Vectorize the flat 2-color NORTHGROVE wordmark raster to a clean SVG for label + banner. DELIVERABLE 4. Consumes step 18 output.
  20. create_firefly_board — Assemble the four produced deliverables into a Firefly Boards moodboard/contact-sheet for client review (honest use: presenting assets already produced). Consum
  21. asset_copy_assets — Gather the four deliverables into a single CC folder for ZIP handoff (apparel_set.zip). DELIVERABLE 5. Consumes steps 12, 13, 16, 19.
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon flagship: 21 connector calls, 15 distinct Adobe tools, spanning 3 capability groups (A1 raster/photo, A4 asset/board/storage, plus the upload/copy asset plumbing) with three converging input branches. It exercises the full headless photo pipeline (geometry -> tone -> white balance -> mask-driven selective edits -> solid-fill background cleanup -> honest multi-ratio crop/reframe export), plus isolation (remove_background), vectorization, and asset assembly. Difficulty comes from the masking discipline (select_subject -> invert -> fill, then a second select_by_prompt -> selective saturation on the SAME image) and from honestly re-casting a brief whose literal asks (compositing a shirt in, AI-erasing clutter) are both out of scope — the agent must recognize the [X] capabilities and deliver the in-scope equivalent. All steps are [C] headless-confirmed; no [W]/[A]/[T] dependencies, so it runs end-to-end autonomously. There is no generative step: the wide catalog banner is an honest horizontal crop of the master frame (image_crop_and_resize), which trims the top/bottom to hit the ~2:1 ratio rather than inventing canvas; the only solid-color fill is the in-scope background cleanup (step 8), and no gen-fill, canvas extension, object-removal, text-to-image, compositing, or layout-composition is used.

Client-supplied input assets: 3 total · 3 img · 0 video · 0 audio · 0 data

AO-41 — Batch-clean and productize the Maple Ridge Home Services logo concepts into a scalable transparent/one-color/badge/vector brand-asset set ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Take the client's three raster logo-concept renders and turn them into a clean, scalable brand-asset set: background-removed transparent masters, a color-corrected primary, a one-color (single-ink) version, a circular badge crop, and a vectorized SVG — all assembled on a Firefly board for sign-off.

💰 ~$120 est.Upwork16 tool calls13 distinct toolslong-horizon
Full client brief
Maple Ridge Home Services is launching a premium-but-warm home-services brand (tagline: "Clean. Caring. Consistent.") and has early concept artwork it needs elevated into a polished, scalable asset system. The client supplies three raster files: (1) a photographed full-color primary lockup (maple-leaf icon + wordmark) shot on a cream studio sweep, (2) the maple-leaf-only icon photographed on a cluttered wooden workshop desk (busy background that must be stripped), and (3) a circular-badge concept render. The freelancer must NOT redraw the mark — the work is to clean, color-correct, and productize the existing concepts into the exact deliverables the brief lists.\n\nPipeline: ingest the three client files into a working folder (finalizing them into three per-file CC asset URNs); straighten and tightly crop the primary lockup; auto-tone then warm/normalize its color toward the requested warm olive-sage / muted-terracotta / cream palette and lift the cream background to a clean even ivory; isolate the maple-leaf icon from its busy desk shot via prompt-driven subject selection and background removal to a transparent PNG; from that clean transparent icon, derive a one-color (single-ink terracotta) version using a flat color overlay for use on uniforms/yard signs where only one ink is available; build the circular badge by removing the badge render's background and cropping it square-to-round bounds; vectorize the cleaned icon to a scalable SVG for trucks and large-format signage; and finally assemble all the produced variants (color primary, transparent icon, one-color icon, badge, SVG preview) onto a Firefly board as the review/sign-off deliverable. Deliverables: transparent-PNG master set, a color-graded primary, a one-color version, a circular badge, an SVG, and the board deep-link.
Deliverables (6)
  • primary_logo_graded.png — Color-corrected, straightened, tightly cropped primary lockup; warm olive-sage/terracotta/ivory palette, even ivory background; high-res PNG for web headers and invoices.
  • maple_icon_transparent.png — Maple Ridge icon isolated from the busy desk shot, background fully removed, transparent PNG master for flexible placement.
  • maple_icon_onecolor.png — Single-ink (muted terracotta) flat version of the transparent icon for one-color application on uniforms, yard signs and stamps; transparent PNG.
  • circular_badge_transparent.png — Background-removed circular badge, cropped to its round bounds; transparent PNG for social avatars and truck doors.
  • maple_icon.svg — Clean vectorized SVG of the maple-leaf icon for infinite-scale signage and embroidery (the source/scalable deliverable the brief requests alongside AI/EPS).
  • maple-leaf_brand_board — Firefly board deep-link assembling the color primary, transparent icon, one-color icon, badge and vector preview for client review/sign-off.
Workflow — 16 tool steps
  1. asset_create_folders — Create a /Maple Ridge-and-Co working folder in CC storage to hold the three client uploads and all derived variants.
  2. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize uploads for the three client raster concepts (primary_lockup_concept.png, maple_icon_on_desk.jpg, circular_badge_concept.png) into the project folder
  3. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the uploads so each client file becomes its own CC asset URN usable by the image_* tools: primary_urn (primary_lockup_concept.png), icon_urn (maple_ico
  4. image_auto_straighten — Straighten the off-angle photographed primary lockup so the wordmark baseline is level.
  5. image_crop_to_bounds — Tightly crop the straightened primary lockup to the artwork, dropping the surplus cream sweep.
  6. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the cropped primary to fix the dull photographed exposure/contrast as a neutral starting point.
  7. image_adjust_color_temperature — Warm the white balance toward the requested cozy olive-sage/terracotta/ivory palette.
  8. image_select_by_prompt — Select the off-white background area of the warmed primary (prompt: 'cream paper background') to isolate it for cleanup.
  9. image_fill_area — SOLID-fill the selected cream-paper-background region with a clean even ivory RGB swatch, removing the uneven paper blotches -> final graded primary.
  10. image_select_by_prompt — On the busy desk shot, prompt-select the maple-leaf icon card ('maple-leaf logo icon on white card') to separate it from the cluttered wood-desk background.
  11. image_remove_background — Remove the desk background using the subject mask -> transparent-PNG maple-leaf icon master.
  12. image_apply_color_overlay — Flatten the transparent icon to a single muted-terracotta ink via a solid color overlay -> one-color version for uniforms/yard signs.
  13. image_remove_background — Remove the gray-tabletop background from the circular badge concept render -> transparent badge.
  14. image_crop_to_bounds — Crop the transparent badge to its tight round bounds -> square-framed transparent circular badge.
  15. image_vectorize — Vectorize the clean transparent maple-leaf icon into a scalable SVG for large-format signage/embroidery.
  16. create_firefly_board — Assemble all produced variants into a Firefly board deep-link for client review and sign-off.
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon (16 connector calls, 13 distinct Adobe tools) spanning three groups — asset/storage, photo/raster, and board — with three parallel sub-chains (primary grade, icon isolation, badge) converging on one Firefly board. It is honestly scoped to the connector's real ceiling: no mark is drawn from scratch (A6 [X] illustration is avoided), image_fill_area is used only as a solid ivory fill of an inverted selection (not generative/removal), and the SVG comes from image_vectorize rather than a render_vector export of a nonexistent authored .ai. The hard parts are genuine: prompt-isolating the maple-leaf card from a deliberately cluttered wood-desk photo, deriving a one-color ink version via flat overlay, and producing transparent + vector masters — exactly the 'bg_removal_batch' work this candidate is categorized as. Every step is [C] headless-confirmed, so the whole chain is autonomously runnable with no [W]/[A]/[T] dependency.

AO-42 — OVERDRIVE merch-brand logo finishing: variant set, transparent PNGs, SVG vector source, palette + font kit, usage board ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Take the client's supplied OVERDRIVE raster logo lockup and productionize it into the full merch brand-kit deliverable set — transparent primary/stacked/icon/avatar PNGs, social-PFP crop, black/white/mono variants, a clean SVG vector source, an extracted palette reference, font recommendations, and a usage moodboard — chaining every step in Adobe.

💰 ~$200 est.Upwork19 tool calls12 distinct toolslong-horizon
Full client brief
OVERDRIVE is a new scholarship-focused merchandise brand (education support via merch sales + scholarship-style campaigns); the look must read bold, modern, youthful, collegiate and merch-ready — premium streetwear, not charity/nonprofit — with the name shown as OVERDRIVE in all caps and a clean, scalable graduation-cap element (no government/university seal, lottery, or generic-education-clipart vibe). The client has approved a rendered raster logo lockup (primary horizontal version on a flat background) and supplies a hoodie mock photo for usage context. Your job, executed entirely with the Adobe connector, is to finish that concept into the production brand kit the client requested: (1) upload the supplied logo lockup into Adobe storage; (2) strip its background to a clean transparent primary logo PNG; (3) derive a tight-cropped icon/avatar from the cap mark and a square social-profile-picture crop sized for PFPs; (4) generate the merch-critical single-color variants — a full BLACK version and a full WHITE version (for dark/light garments, stickers, and watermarks) using subject selection + solid fills, plus one monochromatic brand-tint alt; (5) vectorize the transparent logo into a clean, scalable SVG source for hoodies/hats/large print; (6) produce a flat palette/swatch reference for the color-palette deliverable; (7) recommend brand fonts that match the collegiate-streetwear tone; and (8) assemble the finished variants into a Firefly usage moodboard the client can review, then package everything as the delivered kit. Hard exclusions per Adobe capability ground truth: no from-scratch logo generation, no AI object removal, no compositing, no upscaling — the supplied lockup is the source of truth and every deliverable is derived from it by chaining. The brief's requested AI/EPS source and multipage PDF, and the written originality/commercial-rights confirmation, are noted as human/format-export finishing items layered on top of the connector-produced SVG + PNG masters.
Deliverables (11)
  • OVERDRIVE_primary_logo_transparent.png — Primary horizontal lockup, background removed, RGBA transparent, full source resolution (~2400x1400).
  • OVERDRIVE_icon_avatar_transparent.png — Compact icon/avatar version of the mark: the transparent primary lockup tight-cropped to its content bounds (removes the surrounding empty margin so the whole wordmark+cap lockup sits edge-to-edge as
  • OVERDRIVE_social_pfp_1080.png — Square social profile-picture asset derived from the transparent icon via image_crop_and_resize to 1080x1080, transparent RGBA. The icon is fit centered into the square frame with transparent margins
  • OVERDRIVE_logo_black.png — Full BLACK (solid #000000) single-color version of the lockup on transparency — for light garments, stickers, watermarks.
  • OVERDRIVE_logo_white.png — Full WHITE (solid #FFFFFF) single-color version of the lockup on transparency — for dark garments and headers.
  • OVERDRIVE_logo_mono_navy.png — Monochromatic navy-tinted single-color brand alt of the lockup.
  • OVERDRIVE_logo_source.svg — Clean vectorized SVG source of the transparent primary logo — scalable master for hoodies/hats/large print (stands in for the AI/EPS source deliverable).
  • OVERDRIVE_palette_swatches.png — Flat two-tone color-palette reference built honestly from real regions of the cropped icon canvas: the transparent background field is solid-filled with deep brand navy and the mark's own pixels are s
  • OVERDRIVE_font_recommendations.json — Recommended brand font set (collegiate-streetwear display + supporting text) with names/rationale.
  • OVERDRIVE_usage_moodboard_url — Firefly Boards deep-link assembling all produced variants + the hoodie usage tile for client review.
  • OVERDRIVE_brand_kit.zip — Packaged delivery bundle of all PNG/SVG/JSON masters above (the multipage PDF + AI/EPS export + originality/commercial-rights letter are human finishing items layered on this kit).
Workflow — 19 tool steps
  1. asset_initialize_file_upload — Begin upload of the client's supplied raster logo lockup into Adobe storage; returns an upload target/session.
  2. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the logo upload; yields the stored logo asset URN/URL used as the source of truth for the whole chain.
  3. asset_initialize_file_upload — Begin upload of the supplied hoodie mock photo so it has a referenceable Adobe asset URN for the usage board tile.
  4. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the hoodie upload; yields the stored hoodie asset URN consumed later by the Firefly board as the contextual usage tile.
  5. image_remove_background — Strip the flat background -> clean transparent PRIMARY logo PNG. Deliverable: OVERDRIVE_primary_logo_transparent.png.
  6. image_crop_to_bounds — Tight-crop the transparent logo to its content bounds -> compact icon/avatar version (the whole wordmark+cap lockup with the empty margin trimmed off, edge-to-e
  7. image_crop_and_resize — Reframe/resize the transparent icon to a 1080x1080 square social profile-picture asset, fitting the mark centered with transparent margins on the short axis (ho
  8. image_select_subject — Select the logo marks (wordmark + cap) on the transparent primary to drive single-color fills.
  9. image_fill_area — Solid-fill the selected marks with #000000 -> full BLACK version (solid color only, not generative). Deliverable: OVERDRIVE_logo_black.png.
  10. image_fill_area — Re-fill the same selected marks with #FFFFFF -> full WHITE version on transparency. Deliverable: OVERDRIVE_logo_white.png.
  11. image_apply_monochromatic_tint — Apply a single-hue navy monochromatic tint -> brand mono alt. Deliverable: OVERDRIVE_logo_mono_navy.png.
  12. image_vectorize — Vectorize the clean transparent primary into a scalable SVG source master (hoodies/hats/large print). Deliverable: OVERDRIVE_logo_source.svg.
  13. image_select_subject — Palette build step 1: select the mark (the actual wordmark+cap pixels) on the cropped icon canvas. This selects a region that genuinely exists on the canvas — s
  14. image_invert_selection — Palette build step 2: invert the subject selection to get the surrounding (background) region as a mask, so the two real regions of the canvas can each take one
  15. image_fill_area — Palette build step 3: solid-fill the background region with deep brand navy. image_fill_area lays exactly ONE solid color into ONE selected region (solid fill o
  16. image_fill_area — Palette build step 4: solid-fill the mark region (the reused subject mask from step 13) with varsity gold, yielding the flat navy+gold two-tone color-field refe
  17. font_recommend — Recommend collegiate-streetwear display + supporting text fonts matched to the lockup. Deliverable: OVERDRIVE_font_recommendations.json.
  18. create_firefly_board — Assemble all produced variant URNs + the uploaded hoodie usage tile (hoodie_asset_urn from steps 3-4) into a Firefly Boards usage moodboard deep-link for client
  19. zip_package — Single unavoidable local step: zip all connector-produced PNG/SVG/JSON masters into the delivery bundle. Deliverable: OVERDRIVE_brand_kit.zip.
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon (19 steps, 12 distinct Adobe tools, well past the ≥10 calls / ≥6 tools bar) and a genuine wide mix across A1 raster (remove_background, select_subject, invert_selection, fill_area×4, monochromatic_tint, crop_to_bounds, crop_and_resize, vectorize), A2 document/typography (font_recommend), and A4 asset/board (init/finalize upload, create_firefly_board). It is a real freelancer production job: derive an entire merch brand-kit deliverable set from one approved logo lockup. Difficulty comes from disciplined masking-and-fill reuse (one subject selection drives two single-color logo variants, and a select+invert pair drives the two-tone palette tile), correct ordering (background must be removed before crop/vectorize/board so edges are clean), and staying strictly inside Adobe ground truth — the brief's tempting from-scratch-logo and AI-removal asks are explicitly out of scope, so the spec is honestly scoped to the productionizable half and notes the AI/EPS+PDF+rights-letter as human finishing items. Exactly one unavoidable local step (zip packaging); all core work is connector-headless [C].

AO-45 — Logo Vectorization + Brand Identity Kit — HaulAway (junk removal / vehicle-wrap-ready) ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Vectorize and clean up HaulAway's existing raster logo to print-ready quality, then build the full set of brand variations (master vector, horizontal, stacked, icon-only HA mark, one-color knockouts, a lime-on-black trailer-wrap hero) plus a font + color-usage reference and a presentation board — all wrap/shirt/print ready.

💰 ~$145 est.Upwork19 tool calls16 distinct toolslong-horizon
Full client brief
HaulAway (junk removal & cleanout, Maitland FL / North Orlando) supplies ONE existing logo they love but only as a flat, low-resolution raster PNG (a bold "HA" monogram lockup with the company wordmark beneath, in their black / lime-green / gray palette). They need it turned into a professional, print-ready brand identity kit usable on t-shirts, vehicle & trailer wraps, car magnets, and print.

The freelancer must drive the Adobe Creative Cloud connector end-to-end:
1) Ingest the client's raster logo and get a clean working copy (auto-tone + straighten the slightly skewed scan so edges are true).
2) Clean the color: neutralize any off-cast and crisp up the lime green and the blacks so the trace is sharp.
3) Clean-knock the background to transparent so the trace has no halo.
4) Vectorize the cleaned full lockup into a clean SVG = the MASTER vector (basis for AI/EPS/SVG/PNG exports).
5) Isolate the "HA" monogram for the ICON-ONLY mark: select the wordmark text by prompt, invert to keep just the monogram, solid-fill the wordmark region away with a solid white fill, then vectorize the isolated mark.
6) Produce the ONE-COLOR versions: a black-on-light knockout (solid black overlay) and a white-on-dark knockout (white fill) of the mark.
7) Build the layout LOCKUPS by cropping the cleaned master to its true bounds for the horizontal version and a tighter square for the stacked/vertical and the magnet.
8) Create the creative bonus "trailer-wrap hero": recolor the mark to a high-impact lime treatment, then set the surrounding canvas to solid black so the mark reads lime-on-black across a trailer side.
9) Identify/recommend the brand font family + a usage pairing (font_recommend) as the typography guide.
10) Assemble all produced variations into a Firefly Board as the client-facing presentation / brand sheet (carrying the hex/CMYK/Pantone callouts in the board notes).

Hard rules honored: ABSOLUTELY no generative fill, no AI object removal, no text-to-image, no compositing, no layout composition; image_fill_area is solid-color only; the master export step only EXPORTS genuine vector — the deliverable vector emerges from image_vectorize, not from an authored .ai. Every step after ingest consumes a prior step's output.
Deliverables (9)
  • haulaway_master_logo.svg — Clean vectorized master lockup (full HA monogram + wordmark + tagline) as scalable SVG with crisp paths, transparent background; the print-ready master from which AI/EPS/PNG exports derive. Black/lime
  • haulaway_icon_mark.svg — Icon-only 'HA' monogram, isolated (wordmark removed via select→invert→solid-white-fill) and vectorized; transparent background; for app icons, magnets, embroidery.
  • haulaway_mark_oneclr_black_on_light.png — One-color knockout: solid black HA mark on transparent/light, hi-res PNG for single-color print, stamps, light-shirt screen-print.
  • haulaway_mark_oneclr_white_on_dark.png — One-color knockout: solid white HA mark on transparent (for dark backgrounds), hi-res PNG for dark-shirt print and dark wrap panels.
  • haulaway_horizontal_lockup.png — Horizontal layout version, cropped to true content bounds, transparent hi-res PNG sized for vehicle door / business-card horizontal use.
  • haulaway_stacked_lockup.png — Stacked/vertical (square-ish) layout version cropped tight, transparent hi-res PNG for car magnet and crew-shirt left-chest.
  • haulaway_trailerwrap_hero.png — Creative bonus: high-impact lime HA mark on a solid black canvas (lime-on-black 'trailer wrap hero'), hi-res PNG meant to splash large across a trailer side. Lime achieved via color_overlay on the mar
  • haulaway_font_usage_guide.json — Typography reference: recommended brand font family + heading/body pairing and usage notes from font_recommend, to accompany the kit.
  • haulaway_brand_board — Firefly Board deep-link assembling all produced variations as the client presentation / brand sheet; board notes carry the black/lime/gray hex, CMYK and Pantone callouts and the font guidance.
Workflow — 19 tool steps
  1. asset_initialize_file_upload — Ingest the client's supplied raster logo into CC storage and get a working asset reference/URI.
  2. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the upload to a stable presigned asset URI that the image_* tools can consume.
  3. image_auto_straighten — Correct the ~2.5° scan skew so the logo edges are true before tracing — straight paths trace cleaner.
  4. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the straightened logo to normalize exposure/contrast and recover clean black/white separation.
  5. image_adjust_single_color_saturation — Boost only the lime-green channel so the brand green is punchy and clean-edged for the trace (other colors untouched).
  6. image_remove_background — Knock the near-white #F4F4F2 background to true transparent so the vectorize has no halo/fringe.
  7. image_vectorize — Vectorize the cleaned full lockup → MASTER print-ready SVG (deliverable; basis for AI/EPS/PNG).
  8. image_select_by_prompt — Select the 'HaulAway' wordmark + tagline text by prompt so it can be removed to isolate the monogram.
  9. image_invert_selection — Invert the wordmark selection so the kept region is just the 'HA' monogram mark.
  10. image_fill_area — Solid-fill the wordmark area with solid white (solid color only) leaving the isolated HA monogram.
  11. image_vectorize — Vectorize the isolated monogram → ICON-ONLY mark SVG (deliverable).
  12. image_apply_color_overlay — Apply a solid black color overlay to the isolated mark → one-color black-on-light knockout (deliverable).
  13. image_fill_area — Solid-fill the mark region pure white → white-on-dark one-color knockout for dark shirts/wraps (deliverable).
  14. image_crop_to_bounds — Crop the cleaned master to its true content bounds → tight horizontal layout lockup (deliverable).
  15. image_crop_and_resize — Re-crop/resize to a square-ish frame → stacked/vertical layout version for magnet + left-chest (deliverable).
  16. image_apply_color_overlay — Creative bonus (step 1 of 2): apply a solid high-impact lime color overlay to the isolated mark → lime HA mark on transparent.
  17. change_background_color — Creative bonus (step 2 of 2): set the transparent surrounding canvas to solid black → the final lime-on-black 'trailer wrap hero' (deliverable).
  18. font_recommend — Recommend the brand font family + heading/body pairing referencing the wordmark style → typography usage guide (deliverable).
  19. create_firefly_board — Assemble every produced variation into a Firefly Board presentation/brand sheet (board notes carry hex/CMYK/Pantone + font guidance) (deliverable).
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon and wide-mix: 18 connector calls, 15 distinct Adobe tools across three groups (asset ingest, the image_* raster/vector family, and font/board). Requires correct ordering (straighten + tone + background removal BEFORE vectorize for a clean trace), a non-trivial isolate-the-monogram sub-chain (select_by_prompt -> invert -> solid fill_area -> re-vectorize) exercising masking semantics exactly as the sheet allows, and disciplined reuse of one cleaned hub asset to spawn seven downstream variations. Stays strictly inside connector limits: no generative fill, no object removal, no compositing, no layout composition; image_fill_area as solid-color only; vector deliverables from image_vectorize (not an authored .ai); one-color knockouts via solid fill + color overlay. The hex/CMYK/Pantone color sheet and font guide are honestly delivered as font_recommend output + Firefly Board notes rather than a composed page. All steps are [C] headless-confirmed, so the task is fully autonomous-doable while remaining a demanding, faithful real-client brief."

AO-46 — Recreate a Sports Jersey Font (A–Z, 0–9) as Clean Vector SVG / EPS / AI from a Reference Image ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Turn two photographed jersey-font reference sheets (letters A–Z and numbers 0–9) into clean, print-ready vector artwork and deliver SVG + EPS + AI for both sets.

💰 ~$110 est.Upwork24 tool calls14 distinct toolslong-horizon
Full client brief
We run a custom football/soccer kit shop and need our signature jersey numbering font rebuilt as proper vector artwork for DTF, Flex and Flock production. We are sending two reference images shot off one of our printed jerseys: one sheet showing the full uppercase alphabet A–Z, and a second sheet showing the digits 0–9. The phone shots are slightly tilted, unevenly lit, and have a faint fabric texture and color cast from the jersey weave, so they need cleanup before tracing. We also include a licensed close-up of the jersey collar so you can match the exact ink tone. Deliverables: clean vector recreations of the complete glyph set with consistent stroke widths and even spacing, exported as SVG + EPS + AI for the letter set AND separately for the number set. The vector paths must be tight enough to cut on a Flex/Flock plotter and to separate cleanly for DTF. We hold commercial rights to the original printed artwork; the recreation must be original vector geometry, not extracted from any proprietary font file. Please process the letters and the numbers as two parallel passes off the same cleanup recipe so the two sheets stay visually identical in weight and proportion, and hand back a single organized package.
Deliverables (6)
  • letters_AZ_clean.svg — Clean traced SVG of the full uppercase A–Z glyph sheet, pure black geometry on transparent ground, tight continuous paths suitable for plotter cutting and DTF separation. Produced by image_vectorize.
  • numbers_09_clean.svg — Clean traced SVG of the 0–9 digit sheet, matched stroke weight and proportion to the letters SVG, transparent ground. Produced by image_vectorize.
  • letters_AZ.ai — Adobe Illustrator (.ai) master of the letter set, re-exported by document_render_vector from the authored input letters_AZ_master.ai (whose geometry was carried verbatim from letters_AZ_clean.svg). Si
  • numbers_09.ai — Adobe Illustrator (.ai) master of the number set, re-exported by document_render_vector from the authored input numbers_09_master.ai (geometry from numbers_09_clean.svg).
  • letters_AZ.eps / numbers_09.eps — EPS exports of each set for legacy DTF/Flex/Flock RIP workflows. Emitted by document_render_vector, which lists application/eps as a supported output media type, from the two authored .ai masters.
  • jersey_font_vector_package.zip — Single organized package: /letters and /numbers folders each containing the SVG + EPS + AI plus a contact-sheet preview PNG.
Workflow — 24 tool steps
  1. asset_license_and_download_stock — License + download the collar ink-tone close-up (collar_ink_reference) as a color-fidelity reference the operator eyeballs, per the license-before-edit rule. It
  2. asset_initialize_file_upload — Open an upload session to bring the two client reference sheets into Adobe storage.
  3. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize upload of jersey_letters_sheet.png and jersey_numbers_sheet.png into the working folder; yields stored asset handles for both reference sheets.
  4. image_auto_straighten — LETTERS BRANCH: correct the ~3 degree phone tilt on the letters sheet so glyph baselines are level before tracing.
  5. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone to even out the uneven window lighting across the letters sheet — establishes the shared cleanup recipe step 1.
  6. image_adjust_color_temperature — Neutralize the cool color cast (cleanup recipe step 2) via a global white-balance shift so the white ground reads true white. The operator eyeballs collar_ink_r
  7. image_adjust_brightness_and_contrast — Push contrast to crush the faint knit-fabric texture and drive the glyphs toward a clean high-contrast silhouette (cleanup recipe step 3) — critical so vectoriz
  8. image_select_by_prompt — Select the letter glyphs (prompt: the white block letters) to isolate the shapes from the fabric ground for a uniform fill.
  9. image_invert_selection — Invert to target the background ground so it can be flattened to pure white, removing every last trace of weave/lighting around the glyphs.
  10. image_fill_area — Solid-fill the inverted (background) selection with pure white — a clean, traceable two-tone plate. Solid-color fill only, within the existing canvas; no genera
  11. image_vectorize — CORE DELIVERABLE 1: raster→clean SVG trace of the A–Z plate, tight continuous paths ready for plotter/DTF.
  12. image_auto_straighten — NUMBERS BRANCH (parallel pass, same recipe): straighten the digits sheet.
  13. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the numbers sheet — identical recipe step so the two sets stay matched in weight/value.
  14. image_adjust_color_temperature — Neutralize the same cool cast on the numbers sheet with the same global white-balance shift; operator again eyeballs collar_ink_ref_licensed as the visual targe
  15. image_adjust_brightness_and_contrast — Match the letters' contrast push to crush fabric texture on the digits — keeps stroke weight identical across the family.
  16. image_select_by_prompt — Select the white digit glyphs to isolate them from the ground.
  17. image_invert_selection — Invert to the background ground for flattening.
  18. image_fill_area — Solid white fill of the digit-sheet background within the existing canvas — clean two-tone plate matched to the letters plate. No generation, no resize.
  19. image_vectorize — CORE DELIVERABLE 2: raster→clean SVG trace of the 0–9 plate, weight-matched to the letters SVG.
  20. document_render_vector — Export the authored letters master letters_AZ_master.ai (a declared input whose geometry was carried verbatim from letters_AZ_clean.svg) to SVG/PDF/PNG and to t
  21. document_render_vector — Export the authored numbers master numbers_09_master.ai (declared input, geometry from numbers_09_clean.svg) to its .ai/EPS/SVG/PDF/PNG masters, matching the le
  22. asset_create_folders — Create the /letters and /numbers delivery folders in Adobe storage to organize the final SVG+EPS+AI package.
  23. asset_copy_assets — Copy both clean SVGs and both export sets into their respective delivery folders, assembling the final organized package.
  24. zip_package — Single unavoidable local step: zip the organized /letters and /numbers folders (SVG+EPS+AI + contact-sheet PNG) into one downloadable package for handoff.
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon and wide-mix: 24 steps, 14 distinct Adobe tools across 3 connector groups (image_* raster cleanup, document_* vector export, asset_* stock/upload/storage). It demands real planning — a shared deterministic cleanup recipe applied identically across two parallel branches for cross-set consistency, correct ordering (straighten before tone before select, license before edit), and honest handling of the connector's limits: vectorize is the only path to the SVG, fill_area is used strictly as a solid two-tone flattener (not generative), and the .ai/EPS masters are EXPORTED via document_render_vector from authored vectors rather than composed from scratch. Difficulty comes from glyph-edge fidelity (crushing fabric weave without eroding stroke weight) and keeping the letter and number sets a coherent family, not from any single hard step.
