letters_AZ_master.ai.proxy.txt
content
**Template Description: `letters_AZ_master.ai`**

This Adobe Illustrator template contains a single artboard featuring the complete A-Z glyph set. The content consists solely of vector paths, which are a direct, unaltered re-wrap of the traced letter geometry originally sourced from `letters_AZ_clean.svg`.

*   **Structure:** Single artboard. All A-Z glyphs are grouped together as a single vector object.
*   **Layers:** No complex layer structure; the glyph set resides as a primary grouped object.
*   **Placeholders:** None. The template *is* the final artwork for the A-Z glyphs.
*   **Dimensions:** Inherited directly from the original `letters_AZ_clean.svg` source file.
*   **Brand Styling:** None applied. The template contains raw, traced vector geometry only, with no colors, strokes, effects, or custom fonts introduced within Illustrator. Its purpose is to provide a pure `.ai` container for existing vector paths.
numbers_09_master.ai.proxy.txt
content
**Template Description: `numbers_09_master.ai`**

This Adobe Illustrator master template (`numbers_09_master.ai`) provides the complete 0-9 numeral glyph set, meticulously prepared for vector output and full family coherence with the brand's letterforms.

**Structure:**
*   **Artboards:** A single artboard is present, optimized to contain all ten numeral glyphs (0-9). The glyphs are typically arranged in a clear, sequential layout (e.g., horizontal row or grid) on a common baseline for easy visual inspection and export.
*   **Layers:** A primary layer, likely named "Glyphs" or "Artwork," holds all vector path data for the numerals. No additional layers for guides, text placeholders, or interactive elements are expected.

**Placeholders:**
*   None. This file contains the finalized vector geometry for the number set, not editable text fields or dynamic content placeholders. The glyphs themselves are the static content.

**Dimensions:**
*   **Artboard:** Sized to comfortably encompass the entire 0-9 glyph set, allowing for minimal internal padding.
*   **Glyphs:** Individual numeral glyphs are precisely designed to a consistent height, width, and baseline. Their specific dimensions (e.g., cap height, x-height, stroke width, aspect ratio) are directly derived from and perfectly matched to the `letters_AZ_master.ai` asset to ensure visual consistency across the entire alphanumeric family.

**Brand Styling:**
*   **Typeface/Glyph Style:** This template *defines* the numeric component of the brand's custom typeface. The geometry is a direct re-wrap of the traced vector outlines from `numbers_09_clean.svg`.
*   **Consistency:** The core styling principle is absolute family coherence. All numeral glyphs are meticulously **weight- and proportion-matched** to the `letters_AZ_master.ai` asset. This ensures identical stroke thickness, character width ratios, and overall visual balance, creating a unified typographic voice.
*   **Fill/Stroke:** Glyphs are composed of clean, closed vector paths, typically with a solid fill (e.g., black) and no stroke, ready to receive brand-specific coloring or styling upon export.

AO-47 — Redraw Swimming-Pool Photos into Clean Web-Ready Vector Graphics ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Turn three supplied swimming-pool photos into clean, detailed, flat-color vector graphics (SVG + flat-white-background web PNG) on a uniform canvas, packaged and shown on a moodboard for the client's website.

💰 ~$90 est.Upwork34 tool calls16 distinct toolslong-horizon
Full client brief
A pool-design / pool-builder studio needs vector artwork of its swimming pools for the gallery and "Our Builds" pages of a new website. The client supplies three real photographs of completed pools — a freeform lagoon pool, a rectangular infinity-edge pool, and a kidney-shaped residential pool. Each must be "redrawn" as a high-quality, detailed vector image that accurately represents the pool, is visually appealing, and meets the site's clean, flat, modern standard.

The freelancer's job per photo: (1) straighten the shot so the waterline and coping are level; (2) crop to a clean composition; (3) isolate the pool-and-deck subject from the busy backyard background; (4) knock the background out to a solid flat field so it does not bleed colored noise into the trace; (5) flatten exposure and contrast and normalize the water/coping/decking colors (so the blues read as one or two clean blues, not a gradient of speckled tones) — this is what lets the vectorizer emit clean, detailed shapes instead of a thousand tiny noisy paths; (6) crop-and-resize the cleaned raster to the shared uniform 4:3 gallery aspect so all three pieces match, no invented pixels; (7) vectorize the cleaned raster into a crisp SVG; (8) export the genuine vector to web PNG + SVG. The three finished vectors are uploaded to a project folder and assembled onto a Firefly board so the client can approve the set in one view before it goes on the website.

The deliverable emerges entirely from the connector chain: one supplied photo is straightened → cropped → subject-selected → background-inverted → solid-filled → tonally flattened → color-normalized → cropped-and-resized to the uniform gallery canvas → vectorized → exported, with the same recipe applied to the freeform-lagoon hero piece, and the two remaining pools loaded and pushed through the equivalent prep so the whole gallery is consistent. Output is a uniform set of three flat-color pool vectors (SVG + flat-white-background web PNG) plus an approval moodboard.
Deliverables (5)
  • pool_freeform_lagoon_vector.svg — Clean flat-color SVG of the freeform lagoon pool, detailed but path-economical (water, coping, decking, rock feature as distinct flat fills), on a uniform 4:3 canvas, web-optimized, scalable for the g
  • pool_infinity_rectangular_vector.svg — Flat-color SVG of the rectangular infinity pool, same uniform 4:3 canvas and flat-fill style as the hero, grey+deep-blue palette preserved. Vectorized from the solid-white-knocked raster, so it carrie
  • pool_kidney_residential_vector.svg — Flat-color SVG of the kidney-shaped pool, same uniform 4:3 canvas and style, light-blue+brick palette preserved. Vectorized from the solid-white-knocked raster, so it carries a flat-white backing fiel
  • pool_vectors_web_png_set.zip — Three matching flat-white-background web PNGs (one per vector), exported from the cleaned, background-knocked rasters at a uniform 4:3 canvas size for direct drop-in on the website gallery. The backgr
  • pool_gallery_approval_board — Firefly Boards deep-link assembling the three finished pool vectors (their uploaded asset URNs) into a single moodboard so the client can approve the gallery set in one view.
Workflow — 34 tool steps
  1. asset_initialize_file_upload — Begin upload of the client's freeform-lagoon hero photo into CC storage to get a working asset handle.
  2. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the upload; yields the stored raster asset (URN + presigned URL) that the edit chain consumes.
  3. image_auto_straighten — Level the ~2deg horizon so the waterline and coping are true before any cropping or tracing.
  4. image_crop_and_resize — Crop out the cluttered fence/rooftop edges to a clean composition centered on the pool.
  5. image_select_subject — Auto-select the pool-and-deck subject, producing a mask of what to keep.
  6. image_invert_selection — Invert to target the busy backyard background instead of the pool.
  7. image_fill_area — Solid-fill (preset white) the background selection so no colored backyard noise bleeds into the vector trace. Solid color only — not generative.
  8. image_apply_auto_tone — Even out exposure/contrast as a base so the subsequent flat-color normalization is predictable.
  9. image_adjust_brightness_and_contrast — Reduce contrast / lift midtones to flatten the water and coping into broad even tonal regions (fewer noisy paths after vectorize).
  10. image_adjust_hsl — Pull the speckled turquoise water into one or two clean blues and unify the travertine tones so the trace yields crisp, on-brand flat fills.
  11. image_crop_and_resize — Crop-and-resize the cleaned, background-knocked raster to the shared uniform 4:3 gallery canvas (reframe/resize only — no invented pixels; the surrounding solid
  12. image_vectorize — Trace the cleaned, flattened raster into a clean detailed flat-color SVG — the core 'redraw as vector' deliverable for the hero piece.
  13. asset_initialize_file_upload — Load the second client photo (rectangular infinity pool) to apply the same recipe for a consistent gallery.
  14. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the infinity-pool upload; stored raster feeds its prep chain.
  15. image_apply_auto_tone — Fix the uneven exposure on the infinity shot before isolation and color cleanup.
  16. image_select_by_prompt — Prompt-select the hillside/sky background (different framing than the hero) to target it for knockout.
  17. image_fill_area — Solid-white knock out the background so only the pool/deck remains for a clean trace.
  18. image_adjust_hsl — Normalize the grey coping and deep-blue water into clean flat fills consistent with the gallery style.
  19. image_crop_and_resize — Crop-and-resize the cleaned, background-knocked infinity raster to the same shared uniform 4:3 gallery canvas as the hero (reframe/resize only — no invented pix
  20. image_vectorize — Vectorize the second cleaned pool into a matching flat-color SVG.
  21. asset_initialize_file_upload — Load the third client photo (kidney-shaped pool) to complete the set.
  22. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the kidney-pool upload for its prep chain.
  23. image_adjust_highlights — Recover blown highlights from the hard midday sun so the water/plaster detail survives the trace.
  24. image_select_by_prompt — Prompt-select the lawn/fence background to isolate the kidney pool and brick coping.
  25. image_fill_area — Solid-white knockout of the backyard so the trace stays clean at the pool edges.
  26. image_adjust_hsl — Unify the light-blue plaster and brick-red coping into clean flat colors matching the gallery look.
  27. image_crop_and_resize — Crop-and-resize the cleaned, background-knocked kidney raster to the same shared uniform 4:3 gallery canvas (reframe/resize only — no invented pixels).
  28. image_vectorize — Vectorize the third cleaned pool, completing the three-piece flat-color SVG set.
  29. image_crop_and_resize — Export the hero's uniform-canvas cleaned raster to a flat-white-background web PNG at the shared gallery size (outputFileType=png, same 4:3 canvas — format-conv
  30. image_crop_and_resize — Export the infinity pool's uniform-canvas raster to a matching flat-white-background web PNG at the same gallery size (outputFileType=png, same 4:3 canvas). Its
  31. image_crop_and_resize — Export the kidney pool's uniform-canvas raster to the matching flat-white-background web PNG at the same gallery size (outputFileType=png, same 4:3 canvas). Its
  32. asset_create_folders — Create the delivery project folder that will hold the three finished pool vectors and their matching web PNGs for the website handoff.
  33. asset_copy_assets — Collect the three vector outputs and their three matching web PNGs into the delivery folder, yielding the asset URNs needed for the moodboard and the PNG set.
  34. create_firefly_board — Assemble the three finished pool-vector asset URNs onto a single Firefly board (deep-link) so the client approves the gallery set in one view before it ships to
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon and wide-mix: 34 connector calls and 16 distinct Adobe tools, well past the ≥10 / ≥6 bar, spanning three groups (asset I/O, photo/raster edit, board). It is genuinely hard because each of the three photos has a different geometry, palette, and defect profile (tilt+clutter; uneven exposure+vanishing edge; blown highlights+leaves), so the agent must adapt the isolation and normalization sub-chain per image rather than blindly replaying one macro — choosing select_subject vs select_by_prompt, and auto_tone vs highlights recovery, based on the shot. The strict requirement that fill_area receive BOTH a raster and the correct inverted/prompted mask, and that vectorize only succeed on a properly flattened, color-normalized, background-knocked raster, makes the ordering load-bearing: skip the tonal flatten or the HSL normalization and the trace degrades into noisy paths, failing the 'clean, detailed, visually appealing' bar. Everything stays inside the v2.1 capability sheet — image_fill_area is solid-only, image_crop_and_resize is reframe-only, image_vectorize is the core deliverable — with no generative fill, object removal, compositing, reframe-crop, or from-scratch illustration.

AO-48 — Convert an AI-Generated Logo into a Clean, Multi-Platform Vector Kit ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Take the client's AI-generated raster logo, clean and de-skew it, isolate the mark on transparency, trace it into a crisp scalable SVG, and derive a full multi-platform vector + raster export kit that stays high-quality at every size and format.

💰 ~$100 est.Upwork20 tool calls17 distinct toolslong-horizon
Full client brief
Northwind Analytics generated their logo in AI and only has a flat 1024x1024 PNG of it — a teal-and-charcoal wordmark-plus-icon (a stylized wind/data-flow glyph) sitting on a faint off-white textured card, with the soft anti-aliased edges, slight noise, and a baseline that tips a couple of degrees off level that AI image tools typically produce. They cannot use this raster on their website header, app icon, pitch deck, sign vinyl, or one-color merch because it does not scale and has no transparency. The freelancer must deliver a production vector kit: (1) auto-straighten and tonally clean the raster so edges are sharp and the background is even; (2) tighten the crop to the artwork; (3) isolate just the logo mark from its background card and flatten the background to pure white so the trace is clean; (4) produce a transparent cut-out of the mark; (5) VECTORIZE the cleaned mark into a crisp full-color SVG that scales to any size; (6) derive a single-color / knockout vector (logo silhouette filled solid for one-color print, vinyl, embroidery and small favicons) by selecting the mark, inverting, solid-filling the field, and re-vectorizing; (7) produce a halftone "print-screen" treatment of the mark for merch/poster use; (8) recommend a brand-safe font pairing that matches the wordmark so the client can extend the identity in decks and docs; and (9) export platform-sized PNG rasters (a 1024 app icon, a web header bug, a 512 social avatar) from the cleaned mark and assemble all generated deliverables into a single shareable presentation board with an organized asset folder. Everything must remain high-quality across formats — vectors stay editable/scalable, rasters are crisp at their target sizes.
Deliverables (9)
  • northwind_logo_fullcolor.svg — Transparent full-color vector trace of the cleaned logo mark, teal+charcoal, clean paths, scalable to any size; primary multi-platform deliverable.
  • northwind_logo_onecolor_knockout.svg — Single-color knockout/silhouette vector of the mark (solid fill on flattened field) for one-color print, vinyl, embroidery and small-size favicons.
  • northwind_mark_transparent.png — PNG, transparent background, isolated logo mark with edges trimmed to bounds; source for raster exports.
  • northwind_mark_halftone.png — Halftone / print-screen duotone treatment of the mark for merch and poster use.
  • northwind_icon_1024.png — 1024x1024 square app-icon PNG, transparent background, crisp at target size; reframed from the cleaned transparent mark via crop/resize (edges cropped to hit the 1:1 icon ratio, centered on the mark).
  • northwind_header_bug.png — 480x120 (4:1 landscape) web header 'bug' PNG, transparent background, crisp at target size; honestly reframed from the cleaned transparent mark via crop/resize to the wide header ratio (top/bottom of
  • northwind_avatar_512.png — 512x512 square social-avatar PNG, transparent background, crisp at target size; reframed from the cleaned transparent mark via crop/resize (edges cropped to hit the 1:1 avatar ratio, centered on the m
  • font_pairing_recommendation — Recommended brand-safe font pairing matching the wordmark for extending the identity in decks/docs.
  • Northwind_brand_kit_board + asset folder — Firefly Board presentation deep-link assembling all generated deliverables, plus an organized CC asset folder containing every output for client hand-off.
Workflow — 20 tool steps
  1. asset_initialize_file_upload — Begin uploading the client-supplied raster northwind_ai_logo_raw.png into Creative Cloud storage to start the pipeline.
  2. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the upload to get a stored CC asset/URI for the raw AI PNG. This is the single initial asset load; every later step consumes a prior output.
  3. image_auto_straighten — Correct the ~2.5deg tilt baked into the AI export so the wordmark baseline is level — essential before any clean trace.
  4. image_adjust_brightness_and_contrast — Boost contrast and even out the mild AI lighting so the teal/charcoal artwork separates cleanly from the off-white card — sharper edges for the vectorizer.
  5. image_crop_to_bounds — Trim the surrounding card so the canvas hugs the artwork, removing wasted background area before isolating the mark.
  6. image_select_by_prompt — Select just the logo mark (the teal/charcoal icon + wordmark) via prompt, producing a maskURI separating artwork from the textured background.
  7. image_invert_selection — Invert the selection so the background card (everything but the mark) is now selected — sets up a clean solid-white flatten of the field.
  8. image_fill_area — Solid-fill the inverted (background) selection with pure white, removing the faint texture/noise so the field behind the mark is perfectly even for tracing. Sol
  9. image_remove_background — Knock out the now-uniform white field to produce northwind_mark_transparent.png — the isolated mark on transparency (a named deliverable and the source for rast
  10. image_vectorize — Trace the cleaned transparent mark into northwind_logo_fullcolor.svg — the primary full-color, infinitely scalable vector deliverable.
  11. image_apply_color_overlay — Flatten the isolated mark to a single solid charcoal color (color overlay over the transparent cut-out) to build the one-color/knockout source.
  12. image_vectorize — Vectorize the solid-color mark into northwind_logo_onecolor_knockout.svg for one-color print, vinyl, embroidery and tiny favicons.
  13. image_apply_halftone — Apply a halftone/print-screen treatment to the isolated mark for merch and poster use — northwind_mark_halftone.png.
  14. image_crop_and_resize — Crop/resize the transparent mark to a 1024x1024 (1:1) app-icon PNG — first of the platform-sized raster exports; edges cropped to hit the square icon ratio, cen
  15. image_crop_and_resize — Reframe the same transparent mark to a 480x120 (4:1 landscape) web header 'bug' PNG — second platform raster export. Honest crop/resize to the wide header ratio
  16. image_crop_and_resize — Crop/resize the same transparent mark to a 512x512 (1:1) social-avatar PNG — third platform raster export, derived from the cleaned mark for crispness; edges cr
  17. font_recommend — Recommend a brand-safe font pairing matching the cleaned wordmark so the client can extend the identity into decks and documents.
  18. asset_create_folders — Create an organized CC asset folder (Northwind_Vector_Kit) to collect all hand-off deliverables.
  19. asset_copy_assets — Copy every generated vector + raster deliverable into the hand-off folder so the client receives one tidy package.
  20. create_firefly_board — Assemble all produced asset URNs into a single Firefly Board presentation deep-link — the shareable multi-platform vector kit overview for client review.
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon and wide-mix: 20 connector calls, 17 distinct Adobe tools, spanning 3 groups (A1 photo/raster, A2 document/font, A4 asset/board). It is genuinely hard because the order matters — you must straighten, contrast-clean, crop, isolate via select->invert->solid-fill->remove-background, and only THEN vectorize, or the trace inherits the AI noise/tilt; a naive agent that vectorizes the raw PNG fails the 'high-quality across formats' bar. It also tests honest tool boundaries: image_fill_area is used correctly as a solid-color flatten (not generative), the platform PNGs are honest crop/resize reframes to their target ratios (not canvas extensions), the from-scratch vector comes only from image_vectorize (not document_render_vector, which can't author a vector), and the one-color variant is correctly built via color_overlay->re-vectorize rather than any unavailable gen step. The branch-and-converge topology (one cleaned mark fanning into seven deliverables, then collected into a folder and a board) exercises multi-output reuse end to end.

Client-supplied input assets: 1 total · 1 img · 0 video · 0 audio · 0 data

Images (1)

northwind_ai_logo_raw.png

AO-49 — Evergreen Cannabis Apparel Capsule — Screen-Print Separation & Vector Production Package ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Turn the client's hand-painted hero graphic and brand logo into a production-ready, screen-print-separable vector + halftone package locked to the Evergreen purple/black/pink palette, plus an exported tech-pack mockup, bundled for the decorator.

💰 ~$222 est.Upwork21 tool calls15 distinct toolslong-horizon
Full client brief
Evergreen (a CT/OH/MO cannabis retailer) is producing a Fall/Spring 2027 elevated-streetwear capsule sold in-store and via loyalty redemption, and needs the hero front-print graphic handed off to a screen-print decorator in production-ready form. The client supplies two assets: (1) a high-res scan of a hand-painted "Evergreen crest" hero illustration (mountain + lotus motif) shot on textured paper, slightly rotated and with an off-white/warm cast from the scanner; and (2) the Evergreen wordmark logo as a flat bitmap PNG. The contractor must: straighten and tightly crop the scanned hero, correct the warm scanner cast and lift it to clean neutral tone, force the artwork onto the locked brand palette (purple #4B2E83 dominant, black #0B0B0B, intentional accent pink #E8669E only) by collapsing stray colors, isolate the crest from its paper background to a clean transparent subject for separation, convert the cleaned crest to crisp print-ready vector art (the spot-color "separations" a screen shop burns), give a second halftone-screened raster variant of the crest and add a subtle grain/distress pass to it for the worn, premium hand-feel and distressed-overlay look the brand references (Higher Stitch / Supreme direction), vectorize the supplied wordmark to scalable line art, and finally export a genuine authored tech-pack mockup (.ai garment template) showing placement so the decorator sees print size/position. Everything is bundled into one handoff package for the manufacturer. Output must read as premium and confident, on-palette across all three states with no rework, never cartoonish or cheap.
Deliverables (5)
  • crest_vector_seps.svg — Clean print-ready vector of the hero crest (transparent), spot-color-friendly flat shapes locked to #4B2E83 / #0B0B0B / #E8669E — the screen-print separation art the decorator burns.
  • crest_halftone_distress.png — Halftone/duotone-screened raster variant of the crest (purple/black) for the distressed-overlay look, transparent PNG, print resolution.
  • wordmark_vector.svg — Vectorized Evergreen wordmark, scalable solid-purple line art for embroidery/print handoff.
  • evergreen_la_techpack_mockup.pdf — Exported tech-pack/placement mockup from the authored .ai garment template, showing chest print placement and color callouts.
  • Evergreen_Capsule_PrintPackage.zip — Single decorator handoff bundle containing the two vectors, the halftone PNG, the cleaned transparent crest, and the tech-pack PDF.
Workflow — 21 tool steps
  1. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize upload of the client's hand-painted hero scan (hero_crest_scan.png) into CC storage.
  2. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the upload → addressable CC asset URI for the hero scan. This is the only raw asset-load step; everything downstream chains from it.
  3. image_auto_straighten — Auto-straighten the ~3-degree-rotated scan so the crest sits level for cropping and vectorization.
  4. image_crop_to_bounds — Crop off the uneven cream paper margins, tight to the artwork bounds.
  5. image_adjust_color_temperature — Cool the warm/yellow scanner cast back to neutral so brand purples read true, not muddy.
  6. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone to clean, even contrast — lifts the off-white toward a printable clean base before palette enforcement.
  7. image_select_by_prompt — Select the stray off-palette accents (the hand-mixed magenta/teal brush marks) by prompt so they can be collapsed off the locked palette.
  8. image_fill_area — Solid-fill the selected stray-color region with brand purple #4B2E83, forcing the artwork onto the locked palette (image_fill_area = solid color only, used corr
  9. image_remove_background — Knock out the paper background → clean transparent crest subject, the prerequisite for clean separations.
  10. image_vectorize — Vectorize the transparent crest into crisp flat spot-color shapes → the screen-print separation art (crest_vector_seps.svg). DELIVERABLE 1.
  11. image_apply_halftone — Branch from the same transparent crest: apply a purple/black halftone screen for the distressed-overlay print variant. Intermediate — a grain/distress pass foll
  12. image_add_grain — Add a subtle grain/distress pass to the halftone variant for the worn, premium hand-feel the brand references (crest_halftone_distress.png). DELIVERABLE 2. This
  13. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize upload of the client wordmark bitmap (evergreen_la_wordmark.png).
  14. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize wordmark upload → CC asset URI.
  15. image_vectorize — Vectorize the rasterized wordmark into scalable solid-purple line art (wordmark_vector.svg) for print/embroidery handoff. DELIVERABLE 3.
  16. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize upload of the authored garment tech-pack template (evergreen_la_techpack_template.ai).
  17. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the authored .ai upload → CC asset URI. (Authored template input; the connector only EXPORTS it, does not compose layout.)
  18. document_render_vector — Export the authored .ai tech-pack template → placement-mockup PDF showing chest print position + color callouts (evergreen_la_techpack_mockup.pdf). DELIVERABLE
  19. asset_create_folders — Create the decorator handoff folder in CC storage to collect all final deliverables.
  20. asset_copy_assets — Copy all final outputs (two vectors, halftone PNG, cleaned transparent crest, tech-pack PDF) into the handoff folder.
  21. local_zip_package — Single unavoidable local step: zip the collected handoff folder into one manufacturer package (Evergreen_Capsule_PrintPackage.zip). DELIVERABLE 5.
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon (21 steps, 15 distinct Adobe tools across A1 photo/raster, A2 document export, and A4 asset/storage groups), well past the ≥10 calls / ≥6 distinct-tool bar. Difficulty comes from honest sequencing: straighten/crop/temperature/auto-tone must precede palette work; the select_by_prompt → fill_area mask handoff enforces brand-palette discipline using fill_area in its ONLY legal mode (solid color, not generative); remove_background must precede vectorize for clean separations; and the chain forks correctly so one cleaned crest feeds both the vector seps and the halftone-then-grain distress variant (halftone → add_grain produces the worn hand-feel in-chain, no external stock texture). The tech-pack uses an AUTHORED .ai exported via document_render_vector (no layout composition by the connector). Every step is [C] except a single unavoidable [L] zip — no [W]/[A]/[T] dependencies, no not-available capabilities (no gen-fill, no AI object removal, no upscaling, no compositing, no bg-replace-by-prompt). Faithful to the brief's explicit 'vectors that hand cleanly to manufacturers and decorators' deliverable for the screenprint_seps category.

Client-supplied input assets: 3 total · 2 img · 0 video · 0 audio · 1 data

evergreen_la_techpack_template.ai.proxy.txt
content
**Template Description:**

**File:** evergreen_la_techpack_template.ai (Adobe Illustrator)

**Type:** Client-authored garment tech-pack template.

**Structure:** Features a flat front view of a premium heavyweight crewneck tee on a neutral artboard.

**Key Elements:**
*   **Print Placeholder:** Centered chest print-placement box, approximately 11 inches wide, clearly labelled.
*   **Spec Callouts:** Includes dedicated callouts for print location and maximum print size.
*   **Brand Styling:** A color-callout strip referencing brand colors: Purple (#4B2E83), Black (#0B0B0B), and Pink (#E8669E).

**Ready for Export:** Genuine .ai file with defined artboard and placed art, designed for direct export.

AO-51 — Vanguard Athletic Logo — Spot-Color Separation, Vectorization & Multi-Format Screen-Print Package ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Refine my ChatGPT-made "Vanguard" athletic badge into clean, separated spot-color screen-print artwork (olive drab / khaki / black / dark grey + digital camo), vectorize it, and deliver it in multiple file formats with spot-color info.

💰 ~$90 est.Upwork25 tool calls15 distinct toolslong-horizon
Full client brief
I run a youth/athletic "Vanguard" team and I already mocked up our logo set in ChatGPT, but the files are messy raster PNGs I can't actually use. I need them refined and turned into real production artwork I can hand to a screen printer and also post on social. The combined badge has several elements — the "VANGUARD" wordmark (UC), a subdued American flag, a star icon, chevrons, a soldier head mark, and the outer badge ring. For screen printing each of these has to print as a FLAT spot color, so I need every element reduced to a single clean ink from my military palette — olive drab, khaki, black, and dark grey — with the digital-camo swatch I made available as a fill/accent. Right now the ChatGPT export is dull, slightly crooked, has anti-aliased fuzzy color edges, and the colors aren't consistent enough to separate. So: straighten and tidy it, auto-tone/level it so the palette reads true, then go element-by-element and lock each named mark to one solid spot ink (so my printer gets clean separations), pull a true black plate and a knockout, vectorize the cleaned art into scalable vector, and give me a digital-camo accent swatch as a separate vector too. Final delivery: a genuine layered vector source (.ai) exported to PDF/PNG/SVG for the press and for social, plus a recommendation for a military-style font for the wordmark, plus a spot-color reference sheet so the printer knows exactly which inks to load. I want to be able to re-edit outline/stroke/fill colors later, so keep the elements separable.
Deliverables (7)
  • vanguard_separations_source.ai — AUTHORED (human designer) input, NOT connector-generated — listed here because it is part of the client handoff package. Genuine layered Adobe Illustrator (.ai) vector source with each named mark (VAN
  • vanguard_badge_vector.svg — Clean scalable SVG of the cleaned/spot-reduced badge (from image_vectorize), suitable for social and re-coloring.
  • vanguard_press_export.pdf — Press-ready PDF exported from the genuine .ai via document_render_vector (all artboards).
  • vanguard_social.png — High-res transparent-or-flat PNG of the finished badge for social media.
  • vanguard_black_plate.png — Single-color black knockout/plate raster (badge filled flat black on transparent) for the press black separation.
  • digital_camo_accent.svg — Vectorized digital-camo accent swatch (from image_vectorize) for use as a repeatable fill/accent.
  • spot_color_reference.txt — Spot-color reference note: which solid ink each named element is locked to + recommended military font, packaged with the deliverables.
Workflow — 25 tool steps
  1. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize upload of the client's ChatGPT raster badge (vanguard_badge_chatgpt_raster.png) into CC storage to get a working asset handle.
  2. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the upload; yields the badge asset URI used by all subsequent edits.
  3. image_auto_straighten — Correct the faint ~2deg tilt in the AI export so element edges are square before separation.
  4. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone/level the dull low-contrast export so the olive/khaki/black/grey palette reads true before color locking.
  5. image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation — Push saturation/vibrance so the muddy AI fills converge toward consistent, separable hues.
  6. image_select_by_prompt — Select the outer badge ring element by prompt to isolate it for spot-color locking.
  7. image_fill_area — Solid-fill the ring selection with olive drab RGB (flat spot ink) — no generative content, just a flat spot separation.
  8. image_select_by_prompt — Select the 'VANGUARD' wordmark lettering by prompt on the ring-locked art to isolate it next.
  9. image_fill_area — Solid-fill the wordmark with khaki RGB — locks the lettering to one flat spot ink.
  10. image_select_by_prompt — Select the chevrons + star icon by prompt on the progressively-locked art.
  11. image_fill_area — Solid-fill the chevrons/star with dark grey RGB — another flat spot separation, fully chained on prior output.
  12. image_select_subject — Select the central soldier-head subject to produce a true black plate from the spot-locked art.
  13. image_invert_selection — Invert to the background so we can knock it out and pull a clean single-color black plate.
  14. image_fill_area — Solid-fill the inverted background flat black to produce the black knockout/plate separation (deliverable).
  15. image_remove_background — Remove the off-white background of the fully spot-locked badge -> transparent PNG for social + vectorization.
  16. image_crop_to_bounds — Crop tight to the badge bounds to produce the clean high-res social PNG deliverable.
  17. image_vectorize — Vectorize the cleaned, spot-reduced, cropped badge into a clean scalable SVG (deliverable).
  18. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize upload of the client's digital-camo swatch (digital_camo_swatch.png).
  19. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the camo swatch upload -> asset URI.
  20. image_vectorize — Vectorize the digital-camo swatch into a repeatable vector accent fill (deliverable).
  21. font_recommend — Recommend a military/stencil-style font for the VANGUARD wordmark, per the brief's 'military type font' request.
  22. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize upload of the AUTHORED layered .ai source (vanguard_separations_source.ai) — a genuine human-designer file supplied as an input, since render_vector
  23. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the authored .ai upload -> asset URI for the render_vector export step.
  24. document_render_vector — Export the GENUINE authored layered .ai source (uploaded in steps 22-23) to a press-ready PDF across all artboards. render_vector only EXPORTS the authored .ai;
  25. asset_create_folders — Create a delivery folder and gather all deliverables (SVG, PDF, social PNG, black plate, camo SVG) plus the spot-color/font reference for handoff.
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon (25 connector calls, 15 distinct Adobe tools) across three groups: raster cleanup/tonal (auto_straighten, auto_tone, vibrance), iterative masked spot-color separation (select_by_prompt/select_subject/invert + fill_area solid repeated per named element — the genuinely hard, error-prone part where each fill must target exactly one element and chain onto the last), and vector/document export + asset packaging. It exercises the honest non-generative path for screen-print seps: fill_area is solid-color ONLY (no gen-fill, no object removal), image_vectorize for raster->SVG, render_vector EXPORTS a genuine authored .ai (never composes one), and font_recommend for the type ask. Difficulty comes from maintaining the spot-lock chain element-by-element without bleeding fills, and from respecting that the .ai must be supplied as an authored input and uploaded rather than generated.

vanguard_separations_source.ai.proxy.txt
content
**Template Description: `vanguard_separations_source.ai`**

**Structure:** Genuine, hand-authored Adobe Illustrator (.ai) vector file with clearly defined, separable layers.

**Layers:**
*   `VANGUARD` (wordmark)
*   `Subdued Flag`
*   `Star`
*   `Chevrons`
*   `Soldier Head`
*   `Outer Badge Ring`
Each named mark is a distinct, editable vector layer.

**Placeholders:** The template features pre-drawn vector elements for each badge component (wordmark, flag, star, chevrons, soldier head, outer ring). These are not generic placeholders but fully editable vector paths and text objects.

**Dimensions:** Multiple artboards are included, specifically optimized for:
*   `Press` (print-ready output)
*   `Social` (digital media applications)

**Brand Styling:**
*   **Color Palette:** Spot inks restricted to {olive drab, khaki, black, dark grey}. Each vector element is locked to one of these specific spot colors.
*   **Editability:** All vector paths (outlines, strokes, fills) remain fully re-editable, allowing precise adjustments while maintaining color integrity.
*   **Purpose:** Designed for accurate separation and export, mirroring the structure and styling of the cleaned, spot-locked raster badge.

AO-52 — 20th-Anniversary folk-art poster screen-print separation pack — approved master vectorized, white underbase + 4 spot-colour halftone plates with dot-gain choke, a grain-textured proof, the authored registration sheet rendered out of Illustrator, and a campus-reference Firefly board ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

From the approved folk-art 20th-Anniversary master, produce a print-ready 5-screen separation pack (white underbase + four spot-colour halftone plates with a dot-gain choke), a vectorized hard-edge lettering/line plate, a grain-textured client proof, the registration/separation sheet rendered out of the supplied Illustrator template (PDF, PNG and SVG as three separate render passes), and a Firefly board of the campus reference photos for the screen printer.

💰 ~$120 est.Upwork22 tool calls17 distinct toolslong-horizon
Full client brief
Bayou Cross Classical Academy (a classical Christian school in the fictional town of Sainte-Colombe, Louisiana) has APPROVED the final folk-art / festival-poster illustration celebrating the school's first twenty years — a handcrafted, story-driven composition weaving faith, academics, student life, community traditions, and campus landmarks, rich with discoverable details. The illustration phase is complete and signed off; this engagement is the screen-print PRODUCTION phase. You are given: (1) the approved master artwork as a high-resolution flattened RGB PNG (~24x36 in @300dpi, full-bleed), in which the figurative folk-art scene carries soft tonal passages and the commemorative title/year lettering is hard-edge solid; (2) the campus reference photos that accompanied the creative brief (chapel, oak quad, schoolhouse facade, founding-class photo); and (3) the print shop's authored Illustrator separation/registration sheet template (separations.ai) carrying real artboards, registration marks, and ink-name slots.\n\nDeliver a 5-screen spot-colour separation pack for a manual screen print on natural/cream stock: a WHITE UNDERBASE plate (solid fill behind all art), and four spot-ink plates — Marian Blue, Antique Gold, Brick Red, and Ink Black — where the continuous-tone folk-art passages are converted to a registered Ben-Day halftone dot field and CHOKED (a light blur pass on the dot layer tightens the dot edges) so dots do not plug on press, while the title lettering and key line-work stay hard-edge solid via a vectorized hold-out plate. Because image_vectorize traces a whole raster and has no mask input, first knock the figurative art field out to flat with a solid fill (leaving only the masked lettering/line-work on the canvas), then vectorize that lettering-only raster to the crisp hold-out SVG. Provide a single flattened grain-textured client proof that previews the screen-print \"tooth\" on cream stock, the registration/separation sheet rendered out of the supplied Illustrator template as three separate export passes (PDF, PNG and SVG — document_render_vector emits one media type per call), and a Firefly Boards moodboard collecting the campus reference photos so the printer can match landmark colours. Every separation must share identical trim and registration so the five screens line up on the press.
Deliverables (11)
  • plate_white_underbase.png — White underbase separation, 24x36in @300dpi, solid fill behind all printable art on transparency — first screen down on cream stock.
  • plate_marian_blue_halftone.png — Marian Blue spot plate: Ben-Day halftone dot field over the tonal folk-art passages, dot-gain choked, lettering held out solid; trim/registration identical to all plates.
  • plate_antique_gold_halftone.png — Antique Gold spot plate, same registered halftone geometry, choked, on its colour build.
  • plate_brick_red_halftone.png — Brick Red spot plate, same registered halftone geometry, choked.
  • plate_ink_black_lettering.png — Ink Black plate carrying the vectorized hard-edge title/year lettering and key line-work as solid (no dots) plus the darkest tonal dots.
  • lettering_lineart.svg — Vectorized clean SVG of the hard-edge commemorative lettering and key line-work — traced from a lettering-only raster (art field knocked flat by a solid fill first, since image_vectorize has no mask i
  • client_proof_grain.png — Single flattened client proof of the assembled look with screen-print grain/tooth on cream stock, for sign-off before burning screens.
  • separation_registration_sheet.pdf — Registration/separation sheet rendered from the authored separations.ai (all five labelled artboards with crop/reg marks + ink names) to print-ready PDF. Matching PNG and SVG exports are produced as t
  • separation_registration_sheet.png — Registration/separation sheet rasterized to PNG (all five labelled artboards with crop/registration marks + ink names), rendered from separations.ai at step 20 — a screen-viewable proof of the plate l
  • separation_registration_sheet.svg — Registration/separation sheet exported to editable SVG (vector, same five labelled artboards with crop/registration marks + ink names), rendered from separations.ai at step 21 — hands the print shop a
  • campus_reference_board — Firefly Boards deep-link moodboard collecting the four campus reference photos so the printer can colour-match landmarks (chapel brick, oak greens, gold).
Workflow — 22 tool steps
  1. asset_initialize_file_upload — Open an upload session for the client drop: approved_master_poster.png, the four campus reference photos, and the authored separations.ai registration template.
  2. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize uploads; returns stable presigned URLs (master, campus photos, separations.ai) that seed every downstream chain.
  3. asset_preview_file — Inline-preview the approved master to confirm it landed intact and to read the live print extent / lettering vs tonal regions before separating (inspect only).
  4. image_crop_to_bounds — Trim to the true live print area so screen budget and registration use the real extent — the shared canvas every plate forks from.
  5. image_apply_auto_tone — Normalize overall tone of the approved master so blacks read true and the spot-ink builds separate cleanly — the master raster all five plates derive from.
  6. image_select_by_prompt — Mask 'the title and year lettering and banner text' to isolate the hard-edge type/line-work that must stay solid (never dotted).
  7. image_invert_selection — Invert the lettering mask to get the figurative folk-art field — the region that becomes the halftone dot field (lettering held out).
  8. image_fill_area — Solid flat fill of the art-field region on the master so only the masked lettering/line-work remains on the canvas — a lettering-only raster to vectorize (image
  9. image_vectorize — Trace the lettering-only raster (single imageURI, no mask) to a clean SVG hold-out plate — crisp type that scales to wall size and prints as solid on the Ink Bl
  10. image_apply_halftone — Ben-Day halftone (blackAndWhite, registered dot geometry) INSIDE the art-field mask only — the shared screen-print dot field all four spot plates share.
  11. image_apply_gaussian_blur — Dot-gain CHOKE — a light gaussian pass on the dot layer tightens dot edges so they don't plug shadows on absorbent cream stock — the press-safe master dot field
  12. image_select_subject — Select all printable art off the cream ground; invert-ready mask whose inverse is the underbase footprint.
  13. image_fill_area — Solid white fill (preset:white) of the art footprint -> WHITE UNDERBASE plate, the first screen laid down under the colours on cream stock. Solid-fill within th
  14. image_apply_monochromatic_tint — Tint the choked dot field to the Marian Blue spot build -> MARIAN BLUE plate proof (registration identical to all plates).
  15. image_apply_color_overlay — Re-key the same registered dot field to the Antique Gold spot build -> ANTIQUE GOLD plate (registration identical to all plates).
  16. image_apply_monochromatic_tint — Tint the same registered choked dot field to the Brick Red spot build -> BRICK RED plate (same halftone geometry and registration as all plates).
  17. image_apply_color_overlay — Build the INK BLACK plate: key the choked dot field to the ink-black spot build AND overlay the vectorized hard-edge lettering/line-work from lettering_lineart.
  18. image_add_grain — Add screen-print grain/tooth to the assembled choked field for a flattened cream-stock CLIENT PROOF to sign off before screens are burned.
  19. document_render_vector — Render the authored separations.ai (five labelled spot artboards + crop/reg marks + ink-name slots) to print-ready PDF (outputMediaType=pdf). Needs the genuine
  20. document_render_vector — Second render pass of the same separations.ai to PNG (outputMediaType=png) — separate call because render_vector emits one media type per call.
  21. document_render_vector — Third render pass of the same separations.ai to SVG (outputMediaType=svg) for the printer's scalable copy.
  22. create_firefly_board — Assemble the four uploaded campus reference photos (chapel, oak quad, schoolhouse, founding class) into a Firefly Boards moodboard deep-link so the screen print
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon and wide-mix: 22 connector calls, all server="adobe", 17 distinct Adobe tools across the asset, image, and document groups — well beyond the >=10 calls / >=6 distinct-tools bar. It is a genuine screenprint_seps production job mirroring two already-validated reference tasks (the 4-colour screen-print separation pack and the VOIDRUNNER variant covers): masked halftone dot field, dot-gain choke, five spot-colour plates each produced by an explicit step from one shared choked master, a vectorized hard-edge hold-out that also composites into the Ink Black screen, a grain proof, and an authored-.ai registration sheet exported via render_vector. Difficulty comes from correct mask polarity (lettering held out of the dot field; subject-inverse drives the underbase), keeping all five separations on identical trim/registration, and respecting tool limits: image_fill_area is solid-fill only (white underbase, not generative), document_render_vector only EXPORTS the genuine separations.ai, create_firefly_board only assembles existing assets, and no step uses generative fill, object removal, compositing-from-scratch, text-to-image, or upscaling. The original brief's from-scratch folk-art illustration is intentionally out of connector scope and is supplied as the approved client master, keeping the task honest and fully [C] headless-runnable.

Data & other (1)

separations.ai.proxy.txt
content
This Adobe Illustrator template, named "separations.ai," is a standard registration and separation sheet designed for screen printing. It's structured as a single document containing five distinct artboards, each dedicated to a specific spot colour separation.

**Template Description:**

*   **Structure:** A single Adobe Illustrator (.ai) document featuring five vertically arranged artboards.
*   **Artboards:** Each artboard measures 24x36 inches (trim size) and includes a 0.125-inch bleed around all edges, resulting in an effective canvas size of 24.25x36.25 inches per artboard.
*   **Labels:** Each artboard is clearly labelled at the top with its respective spot colour: "WHITE UNDERBASE," "MARIAN BLUE," "ANTIQUE GOLD," "BRICK RED," and "INK BLACK."
*   **Marks:** All artboards feature comprehensive crop and registration marks. These include standard crosshair registration marks at all four corners of the trim box, at the midpoint of each side of the trim box, and a central registration mark, totaling nine marks per artboard.
*   **Placeholders:** Below each artboard's trim area, there is a dedicated text slot placeholder: "INK NAME + MESH COUNT," allowing for easy input of specific ink details and mesh specifications for each separation.
*   **Styling:** The template adheres to a functional, high-contrast print shop standard, utilizing clear black vector lines for all marks and text, ensuring readability and accuracy on film positives.

---

```xml
<svg width="25in" height="200in" viewBox="0 0 25 200" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">
  <title>Adobe Illustrator Separation Sheet Template</title>
  <desc>A multi-artboard SVG structure mimicking an Adobe Illustrator separation sheet for screen printing.</desc>

  <defs>
    <!-- Reusable group for registration marks and guides -->
    <g id="artboard-elements">
      <!-- Bleed Guide (outer rectangle) -->
      <rect x="0" y="0" width="24.25" height="36.25" fill="none" stroke="#D3D3D3" stroke-width="0.01in" stroke-dasharray="0.05in 0.05in" />
      <!-- Trim Guide (inner rectangle, representing 24x36in) -->
      <rect x="0.125" y="0.125" width="24" height="36" fill="none" stroke="#808080" stroke-width="0.01in" />

      <!-- Registration Marks (relative to trim box edges) -->
      <!-- Top-Left Corner -->
      <line x1="0.125" y1="-0.1in" x2="0.125" y2="0.35in"

AO-54 — LaneLoom — Branded Top-Down Traffic Shorts: Reusable Asset Kit, Color-Graded Frames, Vertical Cover + VO/Reel Prep ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Build the LaneLoom branded source-asset kit for the top-down traffic shorts: vectorize reusable road/vehicle/arrow icons to clean SVG, apply one consistent color grade across the reference frames, reframe the graded junction frame to a 9:16 vertical cover/end-card and produce a stock-backed road texture, then clean the scratch VO and reframe the rough preview clip to vertical — all chained so the deliverable kit emerges from the supplied plates.

💰 ~$145 est.Upwork17 tool calls17 distinct toolslong-horizon
Full client brief
LaneLoom is a modern driving-education brand (beginner, nervous, teen, and defensive drivers) producing mobile-first vertical shorts (1080x1920) of clean, top-down animated traffic explainers — lane changes, turns, parallel parking, highway merging, 4-way stops, hazard recognition. We are standing up a 10-template content system and need the reusable, brand-consistent SOURCE-ASSET PACKAGE that our After Effects animator will build the templates from. (We are NOT asking the connector to animate — keyframing stays with our AE artist. We need the upstream art kit + downstream delivery prep.)

Supplied by us: (1) a flat-rendered top-down reference frame of a 4-lane road junction (PNG, on a neutral asphalt background); (2) a separate "icon plate" PNG holding our reusable top-down car body, turn-arrow, and yield/stop-sign glyphs laid out on white; (3) our LaneLoom wordmark/logo PNG (transparent); (4) a scratch voice-over WAV (a presenter explaining a 4-way-stop, recorded on a noisy laptop mic); (5) one rough ~12s After Effects preview render (16:9, 1920x1080) of a lane-change move that we want test-reframed to the vertical short canvas.

We need, as one chained job:
- A clean, scalable SVG icon set (cars, arrows, signs) extracted from the icon plate so the AE artist can drop infinitely-scalable vectors into every template — this is the heart of the "reusable" requirement.
- ONE locked brand color grade (cool, high-clarity, slightly desaturated asphalt, punchy lane-line yellow) applied identically to the reference junction frame AND the cover frame so all 10 templates read as one brand.
- A 9:16 vertical COVER / END-CARD frame: reframe (crop) the graded junction frame to the 9:16 short aspect so the road sits centered with clean headroom, then seat a solid LaneLoom-brand charcoal title band into the upper region for the title and the logo lockup, with the wordmark color-matched in.
- A licensed, subtly-grained seamless asphalt texture (from stock) the animator can tile as the road base.
- The scratch VO denoised to broadcast-clean voice.
- The rough AE preview reframed to 1080x1920 (same length) so we can sanity-check the vertical crop before committing the template.
- Everything assembled onto a single shareable Firefly board as the kit hand-off, and a font recommendation for the on-road label/caption type.
Deliverables (7)
  • laneloom_icons.svg — Clean auto-traced SVG icon set (top-down cars, turn-arrows, road signs) with closed solid-fill paths, transparent background, scalable for the AE template library.
  • junction_frame_graded.png — The top-down junction reference frame with the locked LaneLoom brand grade (cool temp, reduced asphalt saturation, lifted clarity/contrast, punchy lane-yellow), 1024x1024 PNG.
  • cover_endcard_9x16.png — Vertical 9:16 cover / end-card: the graded 1024x1024 junction frame reframed (cropped) to the 9:16 short aspect (576x1024, a crop of the source — no upscaling, no invented pixels), with a solid brand-
  • asphalt_texture_grained.png — Licensed seamless asphalt stock with subtle film grain added, tileable road-base texture for the templates. PNG/JPEG full-res from the licensed presigned URL.
  • vo_4way_stop_clean.wav — Denoised broadcast-clean voice-over (hiss/hum/reverb removed by media_enhance_speech), same content/length as the scratch take.
  • ae_preview_1080x1920.mp4 — The rough AE lane-change preview reframed to 1080x1920 vertical, SAME ~12s length, for vertical-crop sanity check.
  • laneloom_kit_board — Firefly board deep-link assembling the produced kit assets (icons SVG preview, graded frame, cover, texture) as the hand-off moodboard, plus the font recommendation noted alongside.
Workflow — 17 tool steps
  1. asset_initialize_file_upload — Begin ingest of the 5 client-supplied source assets (junction frame, icon plate, wordmark, scratch VO, AE preview) into CC storage.
  2. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize upload; yields stable asset URNs/URIs for junction_frame, icon_plate, wordmark, scratch_vo, ae_preview that all later steps consume.
  3. image_remove_background — Strip the white background off the icon plate so the glyphs sit on transparency before vectorizing (clean separation = clean traced paths).
  4. image_vectorize — Auto-trace the transparent glyph plate to a clean scalable SVG icon set — the core reusable-asset deliverable. DELIVERABLE.
  5. image_apply_preset — Apply a clarity/contrast Lightroom preset to the top-down junction frame to establish the crisp explainer base look.
  6. image_adjust_color_temperature — Cool the white balance for the brand's clean/cool asphalt read.
  7. image_adjust_single_color_saturation — Pull asphalt grays down while keeping lane-line yellow punchy — locks the LaneLoom brand grade. DELIVERABLE (graded reference frame).
  8. image_crop_and_resize — Reframe the graded 1024x1024 square to the 9:16 vertical short aspect by CROPPING the width to 576px (keep full 1024px height) — a crop of the source, no upscal
  9. image_select_by_prompt — Select the top headroom region of the vertical crop to receive a solid brand title band.
  10. image_fill_area — Solid-fill the selected band with the brand charcoal RGB within the existing canvas (fill_area = solid color only) to seat the title/wordmark lockup. DELIVERABL
  11. asset_search — Search Adobe Stock for a seamless top-down asphalt/road texture for the road base.
  12. asset_license_and_download_stock — License (before editing) and download the full-res asphalt texture via presigned URL.
  13. image_add_grain — Add subtle film grain to the licensed asphalt for a believable tileable road base. DELIVERABLE (road texture).
  14. media_enhance_speech — Denoise the noisy scratch VO to broadcast-clean voice (speech-only enhance). Async-widget. DELIVERABLE (clean VO).
  15. video_resize — Reframe the 1920x1080 AE preview to 1080x1920 vertical, SAME length (no trim/convert). Async-widget. DELIVERABLE (vertical preview).
  16. font_recommend — Recommend an on-road caption/label typeface matching the brand wordmark and the supplied caption strings.
  17. create_firefly_board — Assemble the produced kit assets into one shareable Firefly board as the hand-off package (font rec noted alongside). DELIVERABLE (kit board).
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon (17 connector calls, 17 distinct Adobe tools, well past the ≥10/≥6 bar) spanning four groups — raster/vector edit, document/stock asset ops, async video/audio, and board/font — with genuine multi-branch output→input chaining (a 5-step grade→reframe-crop→select→fill pipeline, a 2-step bg-removal→vectorize pipeline, a search→license→grain stock pipeline). It is honest about connector limits: the original from-scratch After Effects 2D animation is NOT claimed (A6 [X] — no text-to-image, no keyframing, no compositing); the task is reframed to the real upstream source-asset kit + downstream delivery prep that this exact content system needs. the sole reframe/crop step is image_crop_and_resize (reframe-crop) only; image_fill_area is used strictly as solid-color fill; video_resize keeps the same length; media_enhance_speech is voice-only. Mode mix is explicit: fifteen [C] headless steps plus two [A] async-widget media steps, every step tagged.

Client-supplied input assets: 6 total · 3 img · 1 video · 1 audio · 1 data

AO-55 — Multi-Channel Meal-Prep Ad Package: Product-Showcase Video + Social Cutdowns + Retouched Hero Still + Vectorized Merch Logo ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

From the client's raw chicken-bowl hero photo, raw 4K product-showcase footage, raw phone-recorded voiceover, and flat logo PNG, produce a retouched website-banner hero still, a vertical + square + landscape cutdown set of the product-showcase video, a denoised voiceover, a screen-print-ready vector logo, and a moodboard of the package.

💰 ~$138 est.Upwork26 tool calls16 distinct toolslong-horizon
Full client brief
Scale My Meal Prep has delegated this week's creative package for the client "Fuel'd Kitchen", who is launching a High-Protein Chicken Bowl. The client uploaded four raw assets: (1) a hand-held studio hero photograph of the plated chicken bowl (slightly tilted, mixed lighting, flat color, shot wider than the banner crop), (2) ~70 seconds of raw 4K horizontal product-showcase b-roll of the bowl being assembled and steaming, (3) a phone-recorded founder voiceover ("Real food, prepped for real life…") with room hiss and HVAC hum, and (4) a flat full-color PNG of the Fuel'd Kitchen wordmark logo for the meal-prep container labels.

Deliverables for the package:
- ONE retouched, on-brand hero still for the website banner and Facebook/Instagram feed ad: leveled horizon, cleanly cropped, auto-toned and color-corrected, warmer appetizing temperature, the bowl made to pop against a softened background, then placed on a solid brand-colour (charcoal) canvas at a tall hero-banner size so it can also run as a tall hero banner.
- ONE supporting steam/texture still licensed from Adobe Stock, color-matched to the hero look, for the email header and landing-page background.
- A THREE-format social cutdown of the product-showcase video — 9:16 for Reels/TikTok, 1:1 for the feed, and the original 16:9 — all the same runtime (reframe only, no trimming) plus an AI sizzle/quick-cut highlight version for the paid short-form ad.
- A cleaned founder voiceover (denoise/de-hum) and a short text content-summary of the showcase footage for the post caption / scheduling sheet.
- A screen-print-ready vectorized (SVG) version of the wordmark logo for the branded meal-prep container labels.
- A Firefly Board deep-link assembling the finished package for client review.

Work fast and to spec — the agency measures this role on turnaround. Use only Adobe Creative Cloud connector tools; every editing step must build on the previous one.
Deliverables (10)
  • hero_banner_still.png — Retouched website/feed hero still: auto-straightened, cropped to banner framing, auto-toned + exposure/contrast corrected, warmer color temperature, bowl popped against a softened (lens-blurred) backg
  • stock_steam_texture.jpg — Licensed Adobe Stock steam/food-texture still, color-temperature matched to the hero look, for the email header / landing-page background. Full-res licensed JPG.
  • showcase_9x16.mp4 — Reels/TikTok reframe of the product-showcase video to 1080x1920, SAME runtime as source (reframe only, no trim).
  • showcase_1x1.mp4 — Feed reframe to 1080x1080, same runtime as source.
  • showcase_16x9.mp4 — Landscape 16:9 delivery cut of the product-showcase video, normalized to 1920x1080, SAME runtime as source (same-ratio resize only, no reframe crop, no trim). Completes the three-format cutdown set (9
  • showcase_sizzle.mp4 — AI quick-cut highlight/sizzle version (~15s target) of the product-showcase footage for the paid short-form ad.
  • founder_vo_clean.wav — Denoised/de-hummed founder voiceover, voice-only enhancement, ready to lay under the cutdowns.
  • showcase_summary.txt — Short text content-summary of the showcase footage (scene/transcript summary) for the post caption and scheduling sheet.
  • fueld_logo_vector.svg — Screen-print-ready vectorized wordmark with clean closed paths for the meal-prep container labels.
  • fueld_package_board — Firefly Board deep-link assembling the finished package (hero still, stock texture, vector logo, cutdown thumbnails) for client review.
Workflow — 26 tool steps
  1. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize upload of the client's raw hero photo chicken_bowl_hero_raw.jpg into CC storage to get an asset handle for the photo chain.
  2. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the upload, yielding the stored hero asset URN consumed by the retouch chain.
  3. image_auto_straighten — Level the ~4-degree tilted horizon of the hero photo.
  4. image_crop_and_resize — Crop the loosely-framed straightened still down to the website-banner framing and target width.
  5. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the cropped still to fix the flat, under-exposed base.
  6. image_adjust_color_temperature — Warm the mixed-white-balance auto-toned still for a more appetizing food look; this warmed look becomes the reference for stock color-matching.
  7. image_select_by_prompt — Select the chicken bowl subject by prompt on the warmed still, producing a mask to isolate it from the background.
  8. image_invert_selection — Invert the bowl mask to target the background only for the pop/blur step.
  9. image_apply_lens_blur — Apply lens blur through the inverted background mask so the bowl pops against a softened backdrop.
  10. image_crop_and_resize — Reframe/crop the popped hero to the final website hero-banner ratio and target width (bowl centered; the softened lens-blurred background reads as a clean studi
  11. asset_license_and_download_stock — Search Adobe Stock for a steaming-food / texture still and license + download full-res for the email header / landing background.
  12. image_adjust_color_temperature — Warm the licensed stock still to match the hero look (reference temperature from step 6/10) so the package is color-consistent. FINAL stock deliverable.
  13. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize upload of the client's flat logo PNG fueld_kitchen_logo_flat.png for vectorization.
  14. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the logo upload to a stored asset URN.
  15. image_vectorize — Vectorize the flat raster logo into clean closed SVG paths for screen-print container labels. FINAL vector deliverable.
  16. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize upload of the raw 4K product-showcase footage product_showcase_4k_raw.mp4 to get assetIds for the video tools.
  17. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the footage upload to a stored video assetId consumed by all video steps.
  18. video_resize — Reframe the horizontal showcase footage to 1080x1920 for Reels/TikTok, same runtime (no trim). Async-widget. FINAL deliverable.
  19. video_resize — Reframe the same footage to 1080x1080 for the feed, same runtime. Async-widget. FINAL deliverable.
  20. video_resize — Normalize the same horizontal footage to the canonical 1920x1080 16:9 delivery cut, same runtime (same-ratio resize, no reframe crop, no trim). Completes the th
  21. video_create_quick_cut — Generate an AI quick-cut highlight/sizzle (~15s) from the showcase footage for the paid short-form ad. Async-widget. FINAL deliverable.
  22. media_summarize — Summarize the showcase footage's content into text for the post caption / scheduling sheet. Async-widget. FINAL deliverable.
  23. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize upload of the raw founder voiceover founder_voiceover_raw.wav for speech enhancement.
  24. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the voiceover upload to a stored audio asset URN.
  25. media_enhance_speech — Denoise / de-hum the founder voiceover (voice-only enhancement). Async-widget. FINAL audio deliverable.
  26. create_firefly_board — Assemble the finished package assets (hero still, color-matched stock, vector logo, a cutdown thumbnail) into a Firefly Board deep-link for client review. FINAL
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon flagship: 26 connector calls spanning 5 connector groups (asset upload/storage, image raster, stock licensing, video, audio) and 16 distinct Adobe tools — well past the ≥10 calls / ≥6 distinct-tool bar. It exercises a deep photo retouch chain (straighten→crop→auto-tone→temperature→mask-select→invert→masked-lens-blur→banner reframe-crop), where the final banner step is image_crop_and_resize (an honest reframe-crop of the popped hero to the banner ratio — crops existing pixels only, invents nothing, extends no canvas), cross-asset color-matching (hero look → stock still), vectorization for screen-print, three async same-runtime video reframes to 9:16 / 1:1 / 16:9 (reframe only, no illegal trim) plus an AI quick-cut sizzle, voice-only speech enhancement (no loudness/music misuse), content summarization, and a Firefly Board assembly that fans in four finished outputs. Mixed execution modes are honestly tagged: image/asset/vectorize/board steps are [C] headless-confirmed; the video/audio steps are [A] async-widget (in-product). No image generation is used anywhere: no generative fill/expand, no text-to-image, no object removal, no compositing, no bg-replace, no upscale, no video trim-to-timestamp, no layout composition. Faithful to the real Upwork meal-prep brief's exact deliverable list (product-showcase video, social resizing/adaptation, FB/IG ads, banners, branding a

AO-56 — Comedy Gameplay YouTube Edit: Sizzle Cut, Reframed Shorts Variant, Meme Cutaway Graphics + Clickbait Thumbnail ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Turn raw comedy gameplay footage plus client notes, custom graphics and a music folder into a punchy 1080p H.264 YouTube cut with a vertical Shorts variant, polished meme/text-callout overlay graphics, a denoised commentary track, and a clickbait thumbnail PNG — all organized into one reusable project package.

💰 ~$200 est.Freelancer.com18 tool calls17 distinct toolslong-horizon
Full client brief
You are editing a comedy gameplay channel's next upload. The client hands you: a ~22-minute raw 1080p gameplay capture (ranked match, lots of dead air between highlights), a voice-memo commentary/ad-lib track recorded on a cheap mic, a time-stamped notes sheet flagging the funny moments ('3:12 clutch fail', '7:48 teammate rage', '14:02 accidental teamkill', etc.), a folder of custom graphics (a raw 'POV:' meme reaction screenshot and a hero gameplay still earmarked for the thumbnail), and a royalty-free music folder.\n\nGoal: a fully edited, fast-paced comedy YouTube piece that lands a laugh every few seconds — rapid reaction zooms, jump cuts, text call-outs, and meme-style cutaways — PLUS a matching reusable project package and a clickbait thumbnail.\n\nConcretely:\n1. Pull the client's footage, commentary, meme screenshot, hero still and music into a managed Creative Cloud project folder, and license one extra royalty-free 'explosion/impact' stock sound or sting if the supplied folder is thin.\n2. Summarize the music folder for content/mood/structure so the right track can be paired with the cut; clean the commentary track so the ad-libs sit cleanly over the gameplay.\n3. Generate the on-screen comedy graphics from the supplied raw assets: straighten + auto-tone + crop the hero frame into a punchy 1280x720 thumbnail base; punch its contrast/saturation and apply a clickbait color overlay; turn the raw 'POV:' meme screenshot into a high-contrast duotone meme tile; and vectorize two meme/callout stills to clean SVGs so they stay crisp when zoomed.\n4. Cut the gameplay down to a tight comedy edit driven by the client's funny-moment notes as free-text prompt steering, then produce a vertical Shorts teaser of the same energy.\n5. Hand back: the edited 1080p H.264 master, the vertical Shorts cut, the thumbnail PNG, the overlay graphic set (duotone tile + 2 SVG callouts), and a project board/folder that ties every source + produced asset together so the client can tweak details later.\n\nStay within YouTube guidelines; humor tasteful; graphics crisp at zoom.
Deliverables (8)
  • comedy_edit_master_1080p_h264.mp4 — Edited fast-paced comedy cut, 1920x1080, H.264/MP4, ~3-4 min target, produced by an AI Quick Cut driven by the funny-moment notes as free-text prompt steering — the YouTube master deliverable.
  • comedy_edit_shorts_1080x1920.mp4 — Vertical reframed teaser of the master cut, 1080x1920, H.264/MP4, SAME length as its source clip (reframe only, no trim) — YouTube Shorts variant.
  • youtube_thumbnail_1280x720.png — Clickbait thumbnail: straightened, auto-toned, contrast/saturation-punched hero frame with clickbait color overlay, cropped/resized to 1280x720 PNG.
  • meme_cutaway_tile.png — High-contrast duotone/halftone meme reaction tile derived from the 'POV:' screenshot, ready to drop in as an on-screen cutaway.
  • text_callout_card_1.svg — Crisp whole-raster vectorization (SVG) of the duotone meme tile for on-screen call-outs, stays sharp under reaction zooms.
  • text_callout_card_2.svg — Second whole-raster vectorization (SVG) of the styled hero frame, matching style, for a different beat.
  • cleaned_commentary.wav — Denoised/cleaned commentary ad-lib voice track (speech-only enhancement) for layering over gameplay.
  • project_package — A CC project folder containing every source + produced asset plus a Firefly Board deep-link that ties them together as the reusable 'project file' reference; plus a music content/mood summary note for
Workflow — 18 tool steps
  1. asset_create_folders — Create a managed Creative Cloud project folder ('Comedy_Gameplay_Edit') to hold all source + produced assets. Root of the deliverable package.
  2. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize upload of the client-supplied source files (gameplay mp4, commentary wav, meme screenshot, hero still, notes csv, music stems) into project_folder.
  3. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the uploads, yielding stored CC asset handles for every source file — the chain's root inputs now live in the project.
  4. asset_license_and_download_stock — Search + license one royalty-free 'explosion/impact' stock sound sting to supplement the thin music folder; full-res presigned URL added to the project. (Licens
  5. media_summarize — Summarize the royalty-free music stems' content/mood/structure (instrumentation, energy character, sections) so the right track can be paired with the cut. Cons
  6. media_enhance_speech — Denoise/clean the cheap-mic commentary ad-lib WAV (speech-only) so it sits cleanly over gameplay. Consumes the ingested commentary track only. Async-widget.
  7. image_auto_straighten — Level the crooked hero gameplay still (the thumbnail base) — first step of the thumbnail chain.
  8. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the straightened hero frame to fix the underexposed/flat capture.
  9. image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation — Punch vibrance/saturation on the toned hero frame for that high-energy clickbait pop.
  10. image_apply_color_overlay — Apply a warm clickbait color overlay (orange-teal push) on the punched hero frame for thumbnail vibe.
  11. image_crop_and_resize — Crop + resize the overlaid hero frame to the 1280x720 YouTube thumbnail spec → thumbnail PNG deliverable.
  12. image_adjust_brightness_and_contrast — Crank contrast on the raw 'POV:' meme screenshot to prep it for a punchy duotone treatment — start of the meme-tile chain.
  13. image_apply_halftone — Apply halftone/duotone to the contrasted meme screenshot → high-contrast meme cutaway tile deliverable.
  14. image_vectorize — Whole-raster vectorize the duotone meme tile into a clean SVG so it stays crisp under reaction zooms → SVG callout #1 deliverable. (Traces the whole image, not
  15. image_vectorize — Whole-raster vectorize the styled hero frame into a second clean SVG callout card (matching look, different beat) → SVG callout #2 deliverable. (Traces the whol
  16. video_create_quick_cut — AI Quick Cut of the raw gameplay into a tight comedy sizzle; prompt driven by the client's funny-moment notes as free-text steering, with music_content_summary
  17. video_resize — Reframe the master cut to vertical 1080x1920 (same length, no trim) for the YouTube Shorts teaser variant. Consumes the master from step 16. Async-widget.
  18. create_firefly_board — Assemble all produced asset URNs (thumbnail, meme tile, 2 SVG callouts, master + shorts cuts, cleaned VO) into a Firefly Board deep-link as the reusable 'projec
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon flagship: 18 connector calls, 17 distinct Adobe tools, spanning four connector groups (A4 asset/storage/board, A5 video/audio, A1 photo/raster, A2 vector) with three parallel sub-chains (thumbnail, meme/callout graphics, video) that converge into one project-package deliverable — well past the ≥12 calls / ≥3 groups / ≥6 distinct tools flagship bar. Difficulty also comes from honest execution-mode mixing: the core video/audio steps (5,6,16,17) are [A] async-widget (product-doable, not headless-retrievable), while the durable creative spine (thumbnail, meme tile, SVG callouts, folder, board) is all [C] headless-confirmed, so a grader can verify most of the deliverable autonomously. Faithful to a real Freelancer.com comedy-gameplay brief while staying strictly inside the capability sheet — no generative fill, no object removal, no text-to-image, no layout composition; image_fill_area avoided; video_resize used as same-length reframe only.
