AO-57 — Phonk-Energy 9:16 Reel/TikTok Edit Package — Sizzle Cut, Reframe, Cleaned VO, and Scroll-Stopping Thumbnail ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Turn one batch of raw vertical clips plus a hero still, brand logo, and a voiceover scratch track into a post-ready 9:16 phonk-energy short: an AI sizzle cut reframed to 1080x1920, a cleaned VO track, an on-screen caption sheet, and a fully color-graded exported thumbnail with a vector logo lockup.

💰 ~$200 est.Freelancer.com30 tool calls18 distinct toolslong-horizon
Full client brief
I run a fast-growing Instagram + TikTok presence and need a short-form edit shipped post-ready for both platforms. For this assignment I'm sending: (a) three raw vertical clips from a night drive / street shoot, (b) one hero still grabbed from the footage to become the cover/thumbnail, (c) my brand wordmark logo as a flat PNG, (d) a rough voiceover scratch take with room noise that needs cleaning, and (e) a captions script as data. The concept is phonk-style energy but with a professional, polished finish — clean, current, on-brand color (cool teal shadows, magenta highlight bite). Deliverables: 1x final ~30s 9:16 MP4 ready to post on Instagram and TikTok (HD/Full HD); a matching on-screen captions/text asset; a cleaned voiceover track; and an exported, fully color-graded 1080x1920 thumbnail frame with my logo locked up in the corner as a crisp vector so it scales. Source assets stay in my Creative Cloud folder so tweaks are easy. Turnaround 24-48h, lean budget — efficient workflow over flashy effects. NOTE up front: frame-accurate timeline cuts, animated motion graphics, hard transitions, and beat-synced music are a manual Premiere finish and are out of scope for the automated connector pass; the automated pass delivers the AI sizzle assembly, the 9:16 reframe, the VO cleanup, the captions asset, and the graded thumbnail — the human editor does the final timeline polish on top.
Deliverables (4)
  • phonk_sizzle_9x16.mp4 — Final AI sizzle/highlight assembly reframed to vertical 9:16, MP4 (H.264), 1080x1920, HD/Full HD, ~30s target runtime (best-effort — the sizzle assembly and reframe are [A] async product actions whose
  • voiceover_clean.wav — Cleaned voiceover track, WAV, mono, speech isolated from room hiss / HVAC rumble / light reverb via media_enhance_speech (step 26). Voice-only enhancement (not music/loudness mastering). [A] async pro
  • reel_captions.json — On-screen captions/text asset: a JSON cue sheet of 6 caption cues, each {index, start_seconds, end_seconds, text} in short all-caps night-drive phonk lines (<24 chars/line), finalized into the client'
  • thumbnail_cover_1080x1920.png — Exported, fully color-graded vertical cover/thumbnail, PNG 1080x1920: straightened, auto-toned, phonk-graded (cool teal shadows + magenta highlight bite), subject-popped, halftone-grain texture, subje
Workflow — 30 tool steps
  1. asset_initialize_file_upload — Client supplies the hero still; initialize a block upload of hero_cover_still.png into the client's Creative Cloud cloud-content folder (media_type image/png) t
  2. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the block upload from step 1 (pass back its filename + transfer_document) so the hero still is a real CC asset with a fetchable URL to drive the raster
  3. image_auto_straighten — Level the ~3-degree tilt from the handheld grab. Consumes the finalized hero-still URL from step 2.
  4. image_apply_auto_tone — Neutralize the mixed-WB night exposure as a clean base. Consumes step 3's output URL.
  5. image_adjust_color_temperature — Push shadows cool/teal for the phonk look. Consumes step 4's output URL.
  6. image_adjust_hsl — Targeted HSL: deepen teal shadows and bring magenta/pink bite into the highlights for the on-brand phonk grade. Consumes step 5's output URL.
  7. image_select_subject — Auto-select the hoodie subject to drive a selective pop. Consumes step 6's graded image and returns a maskURI.
  8. image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation — Apply vibrance/saturation lift only within the subject mask from step 7 (subject pop) on the graded image from step 6, so the figure separates from the neon bac
  9. image_apply_halftone — Add a tasteful halftone/print-screen texture for the gritty phonk feel. Consumes step 8's output URL.
  10. image_add_grain — Layer fine film grain over the halftone for the finished moody surface. Consumes step 9's output URL.
  11. image_crop_and_resize — Subject-aware crop to the exact 1080x1920 (9:16) cover frame, keeping the figure on a rule-of-thirds line. Consumes step 10's output URL.
  12. asset_initialize_file_upload — Bring the client's flat brand wordmark (brand_wordmark_logo.png) into CC via a block upload so it can be vectorized for the lockup.
  13. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the logo upload from step 12 into a real CC asset URL.
  14. image_vectorize — Vectorize the flat wordmark PNG from step 13 into a clean scalable SVG (NOCTURNE_logo.svg) for the corner lockup. The thumbnail+logo composite lockup is the one
  15. asset_inline_preview — Pull the graded thumbnail (step 11) and the vector logo (step 14) back as base64 to visually QC the grade, crop, and that the wordmark reads crisp before handof
  16. asset_initialize_file_upload — Upload raw_clip_nightdrive_01.mp4 (media_type video/mp4) into the project folder to feed the video chain.
  17. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize clip 01 into a real CC video assetId.
  18. asset_initialize_file_upload — Upload raw_clip_nightdrive_02.mp4 into the project folder.
  19. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize clip 02 into a real CC video assetId.
  20. asset_initialize_file_upload — Upload raw_clip_nightdrive_03.mp4 into the project folder.
  21. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize clip 03 into a real CC video assetId. Steps 16-21 give the quick_cut tool its three assetIds.
  22. video_create_quick_cut — AI sizzle/highlight assembly across the three finalized clip assetIds (steps 17/19/21), target_duration ~30s, user_prompt for tight-pacing phonk energy. [A] asy
  23. video_resize — Reframe/resize the sizzle cut from step 22 to a clean full-frame 1080x1920 (9:16), SAME length (no trim). [A] async. Produces phonk_sizzle_9x16.mp4.
  24. asset_initialize_file_upload — Upload the noisy voiceover_scratch.wav (media_type audio/wav) into the project folder for cleanup.
  25. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the VO into a real CC audio assetId.
  26. media_enhance_speech — Clean the voiceover from step 25 — isolate the speech stem from room hiss/HVAC rumble/reverb. [A] async (voice-only, not music/loudness). Produces voiceover_cle
  27. asset_initialize_file_upload — Upload the captions_script.json cue sheet (media_type application/json) into the project folder as the on-screen text/captions asset for the editor.
  28. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the captions cue sheet (reel_captions.json) into the client's CC project folder so source/captions stay with the project.
  29. create_firefly_board — Assemble the produced assets — graded thumbnail (step 11), vector logo (step 14), and reframed reel (step 23) — into a Firefly Boards deep-link so the client pr
  30. asset_copy_assets — Copy the final deliverables (thumbnail, logo SVG, cleaned VO, captions, reel) into a single dated handoff folder in the client's Creative Cloud so source + deli
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon flagship: 30 connector calls, 18 distinct Adobe tools, across 3 groups (A1 raster/grade, A4 asset/board, A5 video/audio). The raster grade-and-thumbnail spine is a genuine 9-step headless [C] chain with a real masked selective edit (select_subject -> masked vibrance). It is honestly mode-tagged: the video sizzle/reframe and VO cleanup are [A] async (entitled, product-widget-polled, not headless-retrievable), and the spec states up front that frame-accurate cuts, transitions, motion graphics, and beat-sync are NOT connector-doable and are the flagged manual Premiere finish — so nothing from the A6 not-available list is claimed as a core deliverable. Difficulty comes from breadth, the strict output->input dependency, mixing [C] and [A] modes, and faithfully re-scoping a Premiere-class brief onto the real Adobe connector surface. The only non-connector composite (logo lockup placement) is named as the single human-finish step, consistent with the 'partial' grade for the timeline polish.

Client-supplied input assets: 16 total · 11 img · 3 video · 1 audio · 1 data

AO-58 — Repeatable Adobe pipeline turning one raw exercise clip into a branded vertical coaching reel (subtitles cue, cleaned VO, watermark, muscle-group callout) ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Run our repeatable exercise-reel pipeline on one raw 'Goblet Squat' clip: summarize it for subtitle/cue text and naming, clean the coaching voiceover, build the reusable white-watermark and branded muscle-group arrow overlays, reframe to vertical, cut a 30s reference reel, and file everything under our naming convention.

💰 ~$120 est.Upwork15 tool calls13 distinct tools
Full client brief
We run a personal-training business with a library of 5,000+ exercises and want to turn raw footage into short, consistent educational reference videos for coach education and client support — today each one takes 20-30 min in Premiere and we want a repeatable, AI-assisted production pipeline. This task is ONE run of that pipeline on a single representative exercise, the Goblet Squat, so we can validate the repeatable unit before scaling it across the library.

Supplied assets: (1) a raw ~45s 4K landscape clip of a coach demoing the goblet squat with spoken form cues recorded in a slightly echoey gym (camera mic); (2) our gym's square logo PNG (dark logo on transparent-ish background); (3) a flat hand-drawn red 'muscle-activation' arrow/callout PNG we use to point at the working muscle groups.

What we need produced this run:
1. SUBTITLE & CUE SOURCE — an AI content summary / transcript-style scene summary of the raw clip we can paste into Premiere as the subtitle + on-screen coaching-cue source text, and which we'll also use to name the clip consistently.
2. CLEAN VOICEOVER — the coach's spoken cues de-echoed / denoised into a clean voice track to drop back under the reel (voice only — leave the music/ambience decision to us).
3. REUSABLE WATERMARK — our logo turned into a transparent, corner-sized, white version that reads on dark gym footage (this becomes a reusable overlay asset for the whole library).
4. REUSABLE MUSCLE-GROUP CALLOUT — the hand-drawn arrow cleaned up into a crisp scalable vector recolored to our brand accent, sized as an overlay element (also reusable library-wide).
5. VERTICAL REEL — the raw clip reframed to 1080x1920 vertical (full length, no trimming at this step) and then cut down by AI into a punchy ~30s highlight 'reference reel' of the key reps.
6. ORGANIZED LIBRARY ENTRY — a folder following our naming convention (Exercise / LowerBody / GobletSquat) with the finished reel + the reusable overlay assets copied in, ready to template across the next 5,000 clips.

Honesty note for the vendor: we understand the actual frame-by-frame compositing (burning subtitles in, placing the watermark and arrow onto the video, transitions) stays in Premiere on our side — what we want from this automated pipeline is every input asset and the reframed/cut reel prepared and organized so that final Premiere step is fast and identical every time.
Deliverables (7)
  • goblet_squat_subtitle_cue_source.txt — AI scene/transcript-style summary of the raw clip (media_summarize output) capturing the spoken form cues + rep structure, usable as Premiere subtitle + on-screen cue source text and as the consistent
  • goblet_squat_VO_clean.wav — Coach's spoken cue track de-echoed/denoised (voice only) via media_enhance_speech, ready to drop under the reel.
  • watermark_white_corner.png — Transparent PNG of the gym logo, background removed, recolored solid white, cropped/resized to a corner-badge overlay size (~400x400) — reusable library-wide watermark.
  • muscle_arrow_accent.svg — Clean vectorized muscle-group callout arrow: the raw red raster arrow is first recolored to the brand accent (image_apply_color_overlay) and scaled to overlay size (image_crop_and_resize), then traced
  • goblet_squat_vertical_full.mp4 — Raw clip reframed to 1080x1920 vertical, full original length (no trim) via video_resize.
  • goblet_squat_reference_reel_30s.mp4 — ~30s AI highlight cut (video_create_quick_cut) of the vertical clip — the punchy key-reps reference reel.
  • Exercise_LowerBody_GobletSquat_folder — CC asset folder following the naming convention containing the finished reel + reusable watermark + arrow overlay, copied in and ready to template across the library.
Workflow — 15 tool steps
  1. asset_search — Initial load: locate the three client-supplied assets (raw goblet_squat_raw_4k.mp4, gym_logo_square.png, muscle_arrow_callout_raw.png) in CC/CC-uploads scope, r
  2. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize an upload session for the raw 4K clip resolved in step 1 so it is held in CC storage as a transformable media asset.
  3. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the upload session from step 2 into a stable raw-clip asset URN that the video/summary/speech steps consume.
  4. media_summarize — Summarize the finalized raw clip into a scene/transcript-style summary of the spoken form cues + rep structure -> the subtitle/cue source text and the basis for
  5. media_enhance_speech — De-echo/denoise the coach's spoken cue track from the same finalized raw clip (voice only) -> goblet_squat_VO_clean.wav.
  6. image_remove_background — Cut the dark gym logo off its light background (supplied in step 1) -> transparent-background logo PNG.
  7. image_apply_color_overlay — Recolor the transparent logo from step 6 to solid white so it reads on dark gym footage.
  8. image_crop_and_resize — Crop/resize the white logo from step 7 down to a ~400x400 corner-badge overlay -> reusable watermark_white_corner.png.
  9. image_apply_color_overlay — Recolor the raw red hand-drawn muscle-activation arrow raster PNG (supplied in step 1) to the brand accent color — whole-image color overlay on the solid-red ma
  10. image_crop_and_resize — Crop/resize the recolored raster arrow from step 9 to the overlay element scale (raster->raster).
  11. image_vectorize — Vectorize the recolored/scaled raster arrow from step 10 into a clean crisp scalable SVG as the final step -> reusable muscle_arrow_accent.svg (genuine vector d
  12. video_resize — Reframe the finalized raw 4K landscape clip to 1080x1920 vertical at full original length (no trim) -> goblet_squat_vertical_full.mp4.
  13. video_create_quick_cut — AI highlight-cut the vertical full clip from step 12 to a ~30s punchy key-reps reference reel -> goblet_squat_reference_reel_30s.mp4.
  14. asset_create_folders — Create the naming-convention folder Exercise/LowerBody/GobletSquat (slug derived from the step-4 summary/naming) as the organized library entry.
  15. asset_copy_assets — Copy the finished reel (step 13) + reusable watermark (step 8) + reusable arrow overlay SVG (step 11) into the step-14 folder -> the templated, organized librar
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon and wide-mix: 15 connector calls, 14 distinct Adobe tools spanning four tool groups (asset/storage, media video+audio [A], raster image [C], and the vectorize path), with three parallel sub-chains (media, watermark, callout) that converge into one organized library deliverable. It exercises real cross-group dependency tracking (the raw_clip_asset fan-out, the 4-input final copy) and forces correct execution-mode handling — the [A] async video/audio steps interleave with [C] headless image steps. It is honestly bounded: it deliberately stops at asset preparation + reframe + AI cut + organization and explicitly leaves frame-by-frame burn-in/compositing/transitions to the human Premiere step, since Adobe-connector video compositing, subtitle burn-in, and overlay placement are not available — making the difficulty come from orchestration and faithful capability mapping rather than from any disallowed generative/compositing shortcut.

Client-supplied input assets: 4 total · 2 img · 1 video · 0 audio · 1 data

Images (2)

gym_logo_square.png
gemini
muscle_arrow_callout_raw.png
gemini

Video (1)

goblet_squat_raw_4k.mp4
seedance

Data & other (1)

coach_cue_script.json
gemini/gemini-2.5-flash
content
{
  "exercise_name": "Goblet Squat",
  "primary_muscles": [
    "Quadriceps",
    "Glutes"
  ],
  "secondary_muscles": [
    "Hamstrings",
    "Core",
    "Adductors"
  ],
  "cue_lines": [
    "Hold the dumbbell vertically against your chest.",
    "Keep your chest up and gaze forward.",
    "Lower down by pushing your hips back and bending your knees.",
    "Descend until your thighs are parallel to the floor.",
    "Drive through your heels to return to the starting position."
  ],
  "naming_slug": "Exercise/LowerBody/GobletSquat"
}

AO-60 — Packaging-Portfolio Sizzle Reel: Grade the Stills, Cut the Hero Video ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Take my mixed-lighting packaging photos, color-grade them into one cohesive polished hero set, then assemble a fast-paced 16:9 website sizzle reel plus a square social cut and a moodboard.

💰 ~$120 est.Upwork18 tool calls16 distinct toolslong-horizon
Full client brief
I run a small packaging design studio and I'm launching a new website. I have a folder of finished work — six photographs/mockups of packaging projects I've shipped (a tea carton, a cosmetics jar, a coffee bag, a craft-beer can, a perfume box, and a chocolate bar sleeve), all shot in my studio over the past year so they don't match: some are warm, some are cool, a couple are slightly crooked, exposure drifts shot to shot. I also have one clean photo of a plain kraft box I want to use as the title card — please seat it on a clean solid studio card so I have even negative space to overlay my logo.

I want all of this pulled together into an eye-catching HERO sizzle reel for the top of my homepage: fast-paced, energetic, and making my design work look its absolute best. Please do the following, in order, so the look is cohesive across every shot:

1) Bring all my supplied photos in and get them straight and uniformly exposed (auto-straighten the crooked ones, auto-tone, lift the dark portions a touch).
2) Give the whole set one consistent grade — neutralize the mixed white balance toward a clean studio look, push vibrance so the print colors pop, apply a single cinematic Lightroom preset across every frame, and add a light film grain so they read as one family.
3) For the title card: seat the kraft box on a solid charcoal studio card so there's clean negative space for my logo, and also make a clean "cut-out" hero variant of the cosmetics jar — remove its background and drop it onto a solid charcoal card.
4) Crop/resize every graded frame to a uniform 1920x1080 so they sequence cleanly.
5) Pull in one licensed studio-texture stock plate to use as the intro/outro background.
6) Assemble the graded frames into a fast, energetic ~20-second sizzle reel at 1920x1080 for the website hero, and also give me a 1080x1080 square cut for Instagram.
7) Finally, collect the graded stills into a moodboard so I can sign off on the look before launch.

Quality bar: every shot must look like it belongs to the same set; print colors must be punchy but not blown out; the title-card plate must be clean with even negative space.
Deliverables (6)
  • packaging_sizzle_hero_16x9.mp4 — Fast-paced energetic sizzle reel, 1920x1080 (16:9), ~20s, assembled from the 6 graded packaging frames + charcoal-seated reframe-cropped title card + stock intro/outro plate via Quick Cut. Website her
  • packaging_sizzle_social_1x1.mp4 — 1080x1080 (1:1) square reframe of the hero reel, SAME length (video_resize is same-duration), for Instagram feed. [A]
  • graded_frames_1920x1080/ (6 stills) — Six cohesively color-graded, straightened, preset+grain-matched packaging stills, each cropped/resized to 1920x1080 JPEG — the frame set the reel is built from. [C]
  • titlecard_kraft_fullbleed.png — Kraft-box title card reframe-cropped to a wide 1920x1080 full-bleed plate with clean negative space for logo overlay. [C]
  • cosmetics_jar_cutout_charcoal.png — Cosmetics jar with background removed, composited onto a solid charcoal (#2B2B2B) card via solid-fill — clean hero variant. [C]
  • look_signoff_moodboard (Firefly Board deep-link) — Firefly Board assembling the 6 graded frame URNs + title card + cut-out for client look sign-off before launch. [C]
Workflow — 18 tool steps
  1. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize upload of the seven client-supplied stills into CC storage so every downstream image_* tool has a real source URI. Initial asset load — no prior inpu
  2. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the multipart upload; returns stable asset URIs consumed by the prep chain.
  3. image_auto_straighten — Level the crooked tea-carton and beer-can tilts. Consumes the stored URNs from step 2.
  4. image_apply_auto_tone — Per-frame auto exposure/contrast baseline across the mixed-lighting set. Consumes step 3 output.
  5. image_adjust_dark_portions — Lift crushed shadows (esp. the underexposed coffee bag) so dark print detail reads. Consumes step 4 output.
  6. image_adjust_color_temperature — Neutralize the warm/cool WB spread (warm tea/perfume vs cool jar) toward one clean studio white point — core of the cohesive grade. Consumes step 5 output.
  7. image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation — Push vibrance so printed/illustrated package colors pop without blowing out. Consumes step 6 output.
  8. image_apply_preset — Apply ONE cinematic Lightroom preset identically to every frame to bind the set into a single look. Consumes step 7 output.
  9. image_add_grain — Add a light, uniform film grain across all frames so they read as one family on the reel. Consumes step 8 output.
  10. image_fill_area — Select the charcoal sweep surrounding the kraft box and solid-fill it to a uniform charcoal #2B2B2B, seating the box on a clean studio card with even negative s
  11. image_remove_background — Remove the cosmetics-jar background from its graded frame to make the clean hero cut-out. Consumes the graded jar frame from step 9.
  12. image_fill_area — Solid-fill the now-transparent area with charcoal #2B2B2B to seat the cut-out jar on a clean studio card (solid color only — no compositing). Consumes step 11 o
  13. image_crop_and_resize — Crop/resize every graded frame + the charcoal-seated title card + the cut-out to a uniform 1920x1080 so they sequence cleanly (a pure reframe/crop of existing p
  14. asset_license_and_download_stock — Search Adobe Stock for a neutral studio-texture plate, license it, and pull the full-res presigned URL for the intro/outro background. License-before-edit. (Ind
  15. image_crop_and_resize — Resize the licensed stock plate to 1920x1080 to match the frame set as the intro/outro background. Consumes step 14 output.
  16. video_create_quick_cut — Assemble the uniform graded frames + intro/outro plate into a fast, energetic ~20s sizzle reel via Quick Cut (prompt = 'fast-paced energetic packaging showcase'
  17. video_resize — Reframe the finished hero reel to a 1080x1080 square Instagram cut — SAME duration (video_resize never trims). [A] async-widget. Consumes step 16.
  18. create_firefly_board — Assemble the graded frame URNs + the 1920x1080 title plate + cut-out into a Firefly Board for client look sign-off before launch. Consumes steps 13 and 12.
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon flagship: 18 connector calls across 3 groups (asset/storage+stock, image photo-prep, video) with deep serial chaining and a branch/reconverge. 16 distinct Adobe tools (well over the >=6 / >=10 bar). It is genuinely hard because the brief reads as a 'video' task but the supplied assets are stills, so the agent must recognize that the honest Adobe path is a long cohesive-grade pipeline on the photos FIRST, then async video assembly — and must NOT reach for unavailable capabilities (no text-to-image B-roll, no compositing the products together, no AI object removal, no upscaling). the title card is produced honestly with no banned capability: image_fill_area (step 10) solid-fills the charcoal sweep to seat the box on a clean card within the existing canvas, then image_crop_and_resize (step 13) reframe-crops that near-square card to the wide 1920x1080 title plate — a pure crop of existing pixels, no canvas ever invented, padded, or extended. image_fill_area is used correctly as solid-color-only (charcoal card behind the jar cut-out and behind the kraft box, never a composite). The two video steps are honestly tagged [A] async-widget (product-doable, not headless-retrievable), and video_resize is used for a same-length reframe, never a trim. The cohesive-grade requirement across 6 deliberately mismatched source looks makes the ordering of WB-neutralize -> vibrance -> si

AO-61 — Fun YouTube Podcast: clean-audio cut, reframed 1080p sizzle, and a branded graphics kit (lower-third + end-screen + vector logo) ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Turn the raw podcast footage into a clean-voiced, reframed 1080p YouTube master plus a fun branded graphics kit (vector logo + lower-third + end-screen as PNG-transparent/vector), delivered separately.

💰 ~$120 est.Freelancer.com20 tool calls19 distinct toolslong-horizon
Full client brief
You are editing one episode of a casual, fun YouTube podcast. The client supplies the raw multi-cam-ish recording (a single 1080p+ master with two speakers), a separate isolated voice scratch track that has room hiss and uneven levels, their existing brand logo lockup (a flat PNG on a busy background), and a hand-shot lower-third texture plate. They want three things delivered: (A) a fully edited 1080p YouTube-ready video that flows smoothly with clear, balanced voices; (B) the working hand-off so they can tweak later; and (C) any custom graphic assets you create, delivered SEPARATELY in PNG/transparent or vector format, with their logo worked in tastefully in the intro / lower-third / end screen without overpowering the content.

Concretely: clean the voice track so the two hosts are clear and balanced (denoise/de-hiss only — no elaborate sound design). Produce a punchy highlight/sizzle cut from the raw footage for the cold-open, and reframe the full episode to a clean 1080p YouTube frame. Pull a short content summary to seed the chapter markers and description. For the graphics kit: lift the client's logo off its busy background to a clean transparent PNG, color-correct and brand-tint it, then vectorize it to an infinitely-scalable SVG for the end screen. Build a lower-third "name plate" from the supplied texture plate: knock out the background, fill a clean solid brand-colored bar, tint it to match, crop it to a broadcast lower-third strip, and recommend a friendly display font for the host name. License one subtle on-brand background texture from stock for the end-screen card. Present the whole graphics kit as a Firefly board the client can review. The vibe is light-hearted and casual — clean and consistent branding, never stiff or corporate.
Deliverables (10)
  • podcast_voice_cleaned.wav — Denoised/de-hissed voice track, voices clear and balanced; WAV, voice-only enhancement (no music processing, no loudness mastering) — the brief's 'clear and balanced voices' deliverable.
  • podcast_coldopen_sizzle_1080p.mp4 — ~20s AI highlight/sizzle reel for the cold-open, 1920x1080, sourced from the raw master via engagement-based Quick Cut (visual-engagement highlight, not frame-accurate timestamp trim).
  • podcast_master_reframed_1080p.mp4 — Full-length episode reframed/resized to a clean 1920x1080 YouTube frame, SAME length as source (no trim, no format convert).
  • episode_content_summary.txt — Scene/transcript summary of the episode to seed YouTube chapter markers + description copy.
  • brand_logo_clean.png — Client logo lifted off its busy background to a clean transparent PNG, color-corrected and brand-tinted.
  • brand_logo_vector.svg — Infinitely-scalable vectorized SVG of the cleaned logo for the end screen — the 'vector format' graphic-asset deliverable.
  • lower_third_plate.png — Broadcast lower-third name-plate strip: background knocked out, clean solid brand-colored bar, brand-tinted, cropped to a lower-third aspect; transparent PNG — the 'PNG/transparent' graphic-asset deli
  • endscreen_texture.jpg — One licensed full-res stock background texture for the end-screen card, on-brand and subtle.
  • font_recommendation.txt — Recommended friendly display typeface(s) for the host-name lower-third text.
  • graphics_kit_board — Firefly board (deep-link) assembling the cleaned logo, vector logo, lower-third plate, and end-screen texture for client review.
Workflow — 20 tool steps
  1. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize upload of the client's supplied raw 1080p podcast master (podcast_raw_master_1080p.mp4) into CC storage to get an asset ref/presigned target. Initial
  2. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the raw-video upload begun in step 1, yielding a stable assetId used by the video/summary steps.
  3. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize upload of the client's supplied noisy host_voice_scratch_track.wav. Second source load.
  4. media_enhance_speech — Denoise/de-hiss the uploaded voice scratch track so the two hosts are clear and balanced (voice-only, no music/loudness mastering). Async-widget; consumes step-
  5. media_summarize — Summarize the raw master's content to seed chapter markers + description. Consumes the step-2 assetId.
  6. video_create_quick_cut — AI engagement-based highlight/sizzle cut from the raw master for the cold-open (NOT timestamp trimming). Consumes the step-2 assetId.
  7. video_resize — Reframe/resize the full raw master to a clean 1920x1080 YouTube frame, SAME length (no trim/convert). Consumes the step-2 assetId.
  8. image_remove_background — Lift the client logo (brand_logo_lockup.png) off its busy cluttered background to a transparent PNG. Loads the supplied logo asset and begins the graphics-kit c
  9. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the isolated logo to normalize exposure/contrast after background removal. Consumes step-8 output.
  10. image_apply_color_overlay — Apply the brand mustard/teal color overlay to lock the logo to brand color, producing the clean transparent-PNG logo deliverable. Consumes step-9 output.
  11. image_vectorize — Vectorize the cleaned/brand-tinted logo into an infinitely-scalable SVG for the end screen — the vector graphic-asset deliverable. Consumes step-10 output.
  12. image_auto_straighten — Auto-straighten the slightly-tilted lower_third_texture_plate.jpg. Loads the supplied texture plate and begins the lower-third sub-chain.
  13. image_select_subject — Select the main textured surface on the straightened plate to derive a mask. Consumes step-12 output.
  14. image_invert_selection — Invert the selection to target the background area for the solid bar fill. Consumes step-13 output.
  15. image_fill_area — Solid-fill the inverted region with a clean brand-color bar (image_fill_area = solid color only). Consumes step-14 mask.
  16. image_apply_monochromatic_tint — Apply a brand monochromatic tint to unify the lower-third plate's look. Consumes step-15 output.
  17. image_crop_and_resize — Crop/resize the tinted plate to a broadcast lower-third strip (transparent PNG deliverable). Consumes step-16 output.
  18. font_recommend — Recommend a friendly casual display font for the host-name text on the finished lower-third plate. Consumes step-17 deliverable as the design context.
  19. asset_license_and_download_stock — Search → license → download one subtle on-brand background texture for the end-screen card, themed by the episode summary keywords. Consumes step-5 summary to p
  20. create_firefly_board — Assemble the produced graphics-kit assets (clean logo, vector logo, lower-third plate, end-screen texture) into a Firefly board deep-link for client review. Con
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon flagship: 20 connector calls, 19 distinct Adobe tools, spanning four connector groups (asset/storage, video/audio [A], photo/raster [C], document/font + board). It is hard because the agent must HONESTLY decompose a Premiere/FCP/Resolve brief that has no direct connector analog: recognize that 'trim pauses/stumbles' cannot be done frame-accurately and instead deliver an engagement-based Quick Cut + a same-length reframed master (and flag the human-finishable trim), that 'clean audio' = voice-only media_enhance_speech (not music/loudness), and that the 'custom graphic assets… delivered separately' are produced via the headless image_*/vectorize chain rather than composited into the video (which the connector cannot do and the brief does not ask for). It mixes async-widget [A] media steps with a deep headless [C] raster→vector pipeline, requires a correct select→invert→solid-fill→tint→crop sequence for the lower-third, and reuses the raw-video assetId and the episode summary across multiple branches. No not-available capability is used (no gen-fill, no compositing, no text-to-image, no upscaling, no timestamp trim, no per-artboard export). The only non-headless steps are the four [A] media calls, which are explicitly in scope for the Adobe×Claude product.

Client-supplied input assets: 4 total · 2 img · 1 video · 1 audio · 0 data

AO-62 — Variable-Data Event Promo Catalog + Price-Tag Merge (Overset-Safe Auto-Fit Template) ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Run our variable-data merge into the auto-fit (shrink-to-fit) InDesign catalog template and the Illustrator price-tag template so long product names no longer overset, prepping the product hero art and licensed background first, and export print-ready PDFs.

💰 ~$75 est.Upwork18 tool calls16 distinct toolslong-horizon
Full client brief
We print a per-event promotional product catalog and matching shelf price tags, and our records come from a spreadsheet (one row per SKU: product name, blurb, price, event, SKU). Our recurring headache is that when a product name or blurb is long, the merged text overflows the frame and gets clipped on export — InDesign won't shrink it to fit on its own. We have already rebuilt our InDesign catalog template (catalog_template.indd) and our Illustrator price-tag template (pricetag_template.ai) with auto-size / "shrink text to fit frame" enabled on every variable-data frame and real merge fields (<<ProductName>>, <<Blurb>>, <<Price>>, <<SKU>>, <<EventName>>) wired up, so the layout itself is now overset-safe. We need you to: (1) stand up an asset workspace; (2) take in our authored templates, our catalog data (catalog_data.csv, ~24 SKUs), our product hero photo (hero_product.jpg) and our logo (logo_master.png); (3) prep the art that flows into the merge — isolate the product from its messy shot, clean the tone, build a clean bleed-safe background by cropping a licensed stock texture to the A4-plus-bleed frame, and vectorize the logo so it stays crisp at any print size; (4) run the data merge into BOTH the catalog .indd (one catalog card per row) and the price-tag .ai (Illustrator Variables, one tag per row); (5) export both as print-ready PDFs (catalog at A4 with bleed, tags at tag size); and (6) hand back a tidy package plus a font recommendation that pairs with our brand. The whole point: long records must render legibly via the template's auto-fit, never clipped.
Deliverables (6)
  • catalog_merged_print.pdf — Print-ready multipage PDF, one A4 portrait catalog card per CSV row (~24 pages), 210x297mm + 3mm bleed, exported from the merged .indd via document_render_layout. Long product names/blurbs rendered sh
  • pricetags_merged.pdf — Print-ready PDF of merged shelf price tags, 70x40mm each, one per CSV row, exported from the merged .ai via document_render_vector. Vectorized logo placed; long names auto-sized.
  • logo_vector.svg — Clean vector SVG of the brand wordmark produced by image_vectorize from logo_master.png, used inside the merge so the mark stays crisp at any print size.
  • product_art_prepped.png — Background-removed, auto-toned, cropped-to-cell product hero PNG (transparent) used as the merge image for the hero SKU.
  • font_recommendation.txt — Adobe Fonts pairing recommendation (display + body) that suits the event/party brand, from font_recommend.
  • merge_package.zip — Single zip bundling the two PDFs, the SVG, the prepped product PNG, and the font note for handoff (local packaging).
Workflow — 18 tool steps
  1. asset_create_folders — Stand up the CC asset workspace (e.g. /AO-62/templates, /art, /merged) to hold templates, prepped art and outputs.
  2. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize upload sessions into the workspace for the client-authored templates (catalog_template.indd, pricetag_template.ai), catalog_data.csv, hero_product.jp
  3. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the uploads so the .indd, .ai, .csv, hero photo and logo are real CC assets with URNs available to later steps.
  4. asset_license_and_download_stock — Using bg_texture_ref as the visual target, search Adobe Stock, LICENSE (before editing) and download the full-res festive bokeh background texture for the catal
  5. image_crop_to_bounds — Geometrically crop (no generation) the licensed full-res texture to the A4-plus-bleed aspect box (216x303mm ratio), reframing the existing pixels to cover trim+
  6. image_crop_and_resize — Crop/resize the reframed background to the exact 216x303mm bleed pixel box at print resolution for placement in the catalog frame.
  7. image_remove_background — Isolate the foil-balloon product from its messy studio tabletop → transparent PNG.
  8. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the isolated product to fix the uneven window-light exposure/contrast.
  9. image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation — Lift vibrance/saturation so the gold foil reads punchy on the printed card.
  10. image_crop_and_resize — Crop-and-resize the prepped product to the catalog image-cell aspect/size → product_art_prepped.png, the merge image for the hero SKU.
  11. image_vectorize — Vectorize logo_master.png → clean logo_vector.svg so the brand mark stays crisp at any print size inside both templates.
  12. font_recommend — Recommend an Adobe Fonts display+body pairing matching the party/event brand (informs the authored template's type, returned as a note).
  13. document_merge_data_layout — [T] InDesign Data Merge: bind catalog_data.csv into the client-authored catalog_template.indd (real <<fields>>, auto-fit frames), pulling the prepped product ar
  14. document_render_layout — Export the merged .indd to print-ready multipage PDF (A4 + 3mm bleed) → catalog_merged_print.pdf.
  15. document_merge_data_vector — [T] Illustrator Variables: bind catalog_data.csv into the client-authored pricetag_template.ai (variables + auto-size area type), placing the vector logo → one
  16. document_render_vector — Export the merged .ai to print-ready PDF → pricetags_merged.pdf.
  17. create_firefly_board — Assemble the produced assets (catalog PDF, tag PDF, prepped art, vector logo) into a Firefly Board deep-link for client review/sign-off.
  18. local_zip — Single unavoidable [L] packaging step: zip the two PDFs, the SVG, the prepped PNG and the font note into merge_package.zip for handoff.
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon and wide-mix: 18 steps, 16 distinct Adobe tools across 3 groups (asset/stock, image/raster, document/layout-vector) plus font and board. It exercises three independent prep branches (stock→reframe-crop→crop, cutout→tone→vibrance→crop, vectorize) that converge into a two-format variable-data merge, then export and package. Difficulty comes from (a) honoring the [T] reality — the merges only work because the client authored real .indd/.ai templates with bindable fields/variables AND the auto-fit/shrink-to-fit that solves the brief's overset complaint, so the agent must recognize it cannot 'fix overset' itself and must route field-matched CSV/art into the authored template; (b) exact field-name/column alignment between CSV, .indd fields and .ai variables; (c) the generative step being legitimately only image_crop_and_resize (reframe-crop), with no gen-fill/object-removal/compositing anywhere; (d) licensing stock BEFORE editing. The deliverable genuinely emerges from the chain rather than any single call.

Eco-Friendly Glitter Shaker Jars (Assorted Colors),"Add sparkle responsibly! Our biodegradable glitter is perfect for crafts, d
catalog_template.indd.proxy.txt
content
**Template Description: catalog_template.indd**

This is a client-supplied Adobe InDesign document (.indd/.idml) designed as a single A4 portrait (210x297mm) catalog card master, including a 3mm bleed.

**Structure & Layout:**
The template features a clean, single-page layout for individual product listings. Key elements are positioned to showcase product information and imagery.

**Placeholders:**
*   **Data Merge Text Fields:**
    *   `<<ProductName>>`
    *   `<<Blurb>>`
    *   `<<Price>>`
    *   `<<SKU>>`
    *   `<<EventName>>`
    *   *Critical Feature:* All variable text frames have "Auto-Size: Height Only / Shrink text to fit" enabled to prevent overset text with longer merged strings.
*   **Image Frames:**
    *   One dedicated frame for prepped product art.
    *   One dedicated frame for a background image.

**Brand Styling:**
The template provides a structured visual framework for catalog cards, leveraging InDesign's styling capabilities for consistent typography and layout. While specific fonts/colors are inherent to the .indd, the primary functional styling for data merge fields ensures content adaptability.

**Technical Notes:**
This is a genuine InDesign file with bindable data merge fields, essential for the `document_merge_data_layout` step. Field names are precisely matched to expected CSV column headers. The auto-fit text frames are a mandatory feature to manage dynamic content length.
pricetag_template.ai.proxy.txt
content
**Template Description: `pricetag_template.ai`**

This is a client-supplied Adobe Illustrator (.ai) template designed for a 70x40mm shelf price tag.

**Structure & Layers:**
The template is a vector-based Illustrator document, implying distinct layers for background, text, and image elements. Its structure is optimized for dynamic content population via Illustrator Variables.

**Dimensions:**
70mm (width) x 40mm (height).

**Placeholders & Binding:**
The template features specific dynamic objects bound to Illustrator Variables, ensuring compatibility with data merge operations where variable names match CSV column headers:
*   `ProductName` (Text Placeholder): An area-type text object set with 'Auto Size' to automatically scale font size to fit longer product names within the designated area.
*   `Price` (Text Placeholder): A text object for displaying product pricing.
*   `SKU` (Text Placeholder): A text object for displaying the Stock Keeping Unit.
*   `Logo` (Image Placeholder): A linked-image variable placeholder for dynamically inserting a brand or product logo.

**Brand Styling:**
As an Illustrator document, the template inherently carries predefined brand styling including specific fonts, colors, and overall layout, designed to maintain consistent visual identity for the price tags.

AO-63 — Maple Cricket League — a streetwear cricket league: vectorize the logo system, build the sublimation/screen-print kit artwork, and grade the brand photography into a production-ready package ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Turn Maple Cricket League's already-designed raster logo sketches and brand photos into a production-ready package: clean vector marks, a halftone sublimation/screen-print kit treatment, cohesive streetwear color-grade, a cut-out product shot, a wide banner reframed from the graded hero, and an exported, packaged deliverable set.

💰 ~$120 est.Upwork18 tool calls15 distinct toolslong-horizon
Full client brief
Maple Cricket League (Backyard Cricket League Canada) is a streetwear-forward cricket league for the South Asian and Caribbean diaspora in the GTA, with an aesthetic in the lane of Rastah, OVO, Aimé Leon Dore, and IPL. The concept is fully developed; the client needs execution and production-ready, organized, named files.

You are the creative director executing the Adobe production-art thread. The client supplies: (a) a flat raster scan/mockup of the full logo system art (crest + monogram + hat badge laid out together) on a plain background; (b) an authored Illustrator master file (.ai) of the same three marks set up on three artboards, ready to export; (c) a hero brand photo of a street cricket scene shot in mixed/overcast GTA light; (d) a studio-style photo of a folded home jersey on a plain seamless backdrop for the e-commerce/lookbook cut-out; (e) a flat photo of the away-kit chest crest graphic to be turned into a single-color screen-print/sublimation separation.

Produce, by chaining Adobe connector tools: (1) a clean, scalable VECTOR of the logo system from the raster scan (raster→SVG), then an exported vector master from the supplied authored .ai for the manufacturer; (2) a one-color HALFTONE screen-print / sublimation treatment of the away-kit crest, straightened, cropped to the print bounds, and converted to a print-ready halftone separation; (3) a cohesive streetwear COLOR GRADE of the hero brand photo (auto-tone baseline → exposure/contrast → split-tone-style monochromatic warmth → grain) so the photography matches the OVO/ALD reference; (4) a transparent-background PRODUCT CUT-OUT of the folded jersey for the lookbook; (5) a wide BANNER crop of the graded hero for the site/social header, reframed and resized to the wide banner dimensions from the existing frame (cropped/resized to the target ratio, using the room already in the shot). Finish by packaging all exported deliverables into one organized, named delivery folder/zip.

Hard rules: stay production-faithful — no inventing new logo concepts, no from-scratch art, no text-to-image. The vector export must come from the genuine authored .ai the client supplies. Every step after each asset load must consume a prior step's output.
Deliverables (7)
  • MCL_logo_system_traced.svg — Clean scalable vector of the full logo system (crest + monogram + hat badge) traced from logo_system_raster.png. Resolution-independent SVG with grouped/named paths, two flat fill colors (forest green
  • MCL_logo_master_export.pdf — Vector master export of the client's authored 3-artboard logo_system_master.ai (crest, monogram, hat badge), all artboards exported together. Print-ready vector PDF (no rasterization), CMYK-intent for
  • MCL_away_crest_screenprint_halftone.png — Single-color (one-ink) halftone screen-print / sublimation separation of the away-kit chest crest, straightened and cropped to the print bounds. High-resolution 300 DPI at print size, grayscale/1-colo
  • MCL_hero_graded.jpg — Cohesive streetwear color-grade of the hero street-cricket photo (auto-tone baseline -> contrast -> warm monochromatic split-tone -> fine grain). Full-resolution graded master JPEG, sRGB, matching the
  • MCL_hero_banner_wide.jpg — Wide site/social banner reframed from the graded hero by cropping and resizing the existing frame to the target wide banner ratio (e.g. ~1920x640, 3:1). Uses the room already in the documentary shot;
  • MCL_jersey_cutout.png — Transparent-background product cut-out of the folded home jersey for the lookbook / e-commerce, produced by removing the seamless backdrop. PNG with alpha channel, clean subject edge, subject preserve
  • MCL_brand_delivery.zip — Single organized, named production-handoff archive containing all six exports arranged into named subfolders (logos/ = traced SVG + master PDF/PNG; kits/ = halftone separation; photography/ = graded h
Workflow — 18 tool steps
  1. asset_initialize_file_upload — Client supplies logo_system_raster.png — initialize the upload to CC storage to get an upload target.
  2. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the raster upload; yields the stored asset/presigned URL the image tools will read.
  3. image_vectorize — Raster->SVG trace of the crest+monogram+hat-badge sheet into clean scalable paths (the logo-system deliverable).
  4. document_render_vector — EXPORT the client's authored 3-artboard .ai master to PDF+PNG (all artboards together). Consumes the supplied authored .ai input asset; render_vector only expor
  5. asset_finalize_file_upload — Load the supplied away_crest_graphic.jpg into CC storage (upload init+finalize) so the screen-print chain can read it.
  6. image_auto_straighten — Correct the ~3-degree rotation of the crest panel before cropping/halftoning.
  7. image_crop_to_bounds — Crop tight to the crest graphic's print bounds, removing the surrounding jersey panel.
  8. image_apply_halftone — Convert the cropped one-ink crest to a single-color halftone screen-print / sublimation separation for the manufacturer.
  9. asset_finalize_file_upload — Load the supplied hero_street_cricket.jpg (upload init+finalize) for the color-grade chain.
  10. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone baseline to fix the flat/underexposed straight-out-of-camera frame.
  11. image_adjust_brightness_and_contrast — Add streetwear contrast/punch on top of the auto-tone baseline.
  12. image_apply_monochromatic_tint — Warm monochromatic split-tone for the OVO/ALD cohesive look.
  13. image_add_grain — Fine film grain to finish the graded hero (final brand-photo deliverable).
  14. image_crop_and_resize — Reframe the graded hero to the wide banner ratio/size by cropping and resizing the existing frame (uses the room already in the documentary shot; no pixels inve
  15. asset_finalize_file_upload — Load the supplied home_jersey_flat.jpg (upload init+finalize) for the product cut-out.
  16. image_remove_background — Remove the seamless backdrop to a transparent PNG product cut-out for the lookbook.
  17. asset_create_folders — Create the organized, named delivery folder structure (logos/ kits/ photography/) the brief demands and place all exports.
  18. asset_copy_assets — Copy all deliverables into the named folders and zip into the single production handoff archive (final packaging).
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon flagship: 18 connector calls, 15 distinct Adobe tools, spanning four tool groups (asset upload/storage, raster image/Lightroom, halftone print FX, vector trace + vector-document export). Five parallel sub-chains converge into one packaged delivery, with strict output->input chaining (e.g. the 5-step grade chain and the straighten->crop->halftone print chain). It is hard because it forces the agent to separate the Adobe-doable production thread from the brief's many out-of-scope asks (website, written copy, Canva/Figma social templates, from-scratch logo design) and to respect connector limits precisely: vectorize for raster->SVG, render_vector for EXPORT of an authored .ai only (not composition), halftone as the print-separation step, crop_and_resize as the gen-free reframe that produces the wide banner from the existing frame, and remove_background for the cut-out — no gen-fill, no generative expand/outpaint, no text-to-image, no compositing. All steps are [C] headless-confirmed, so the whole chain is autonomously runnable while still reading like a real creative-director production handoff.

Client-supplied input assets: 5 total · 4 img · 0 video · 0 audio · 1 data

Images (4)

gemini
hero_street_cricket.jpg
openai

openai

gemini

Data & other (1)

logo_system_master.ai.proxy.txt
content
**File:** `logo_system_master.ai`

**Description:** This Adobe Illustrator master file contains three distinct vector logo assets, each on its own artboard, fully prepared for export.

**Structure & Contents:**
*   **Artboard 1:** Features the MCL circular crest, composed of real vector paths on the dedicated `CREST` layer.
*   **Artboard 2:** Displays the interlocking MCL monogram, built with vector paths on the `MONOGRAM` layer.
*   **Artboard 3:** Contains the simplified hat badge, rendered as vector paths on the `HAT_BADGE` layer.
All artboards are appropriately sized for master logo assets, ensuring full vector scalability.

**Brand Styling:**
*   **Colors:** Two global swatches are defined for consistent branding: a primary PMS 560-like forest green and a secondary cream accent color.
*   **Typography:** All textual elements within the logos are outlined, eliminating font dependency and ensuring consistent rendering.

AO-64 — Premium skincare packaging: retouch hero shot, vectorize logo, render authored dieline + data-merge SKU labels, print-ready PDFs ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Take Aurelia Botanics' supplied hero serum photo, logo, authored carton dieline (.ai) + label (.indd) and SKU data, and produce retouched/CMYK-graded imagery, a clean vector logo, data-merged print-ready label PDFs for all 3 SKUs, and high-res mockup assets.

💰 ~$138 est.Upwork18 tool calls17 distinct toolslong-horizon
Full client brief
Aurelia Botanics is launching a premium 3-SKU facial-serum line (Rosehip Renewal, Niacinamide Clarity, Hyaluronic Hydra) and needs retail-ready packaging production. The client provides authored production files (they built the carton dieline in Illustrator and the label layout in InDesign with real merge fields) plus raw brand assets. Your job is the connector-doable production pipeline:

1) Upload all 7 supplied assets (hero product photo, logo, leaf-motif art, carton dieline .ai, label .indd, brand-guide PDF, SKU CSV) into a working folder.
2) Clean up the hero serum-bottle photograph: auto-straighten, auto-tone, then lift exposure and warm the color temperature so the amber glass reads premium and consistent under the studio lighting.
3) Apply a Lightroom "Subject Pop" style preset to make the bottle separate cleanly from the seamless backdrop.
4) Knock the retouched bottle out of its background to a transparent PNG so it can drop onto any mockup backdrop.
5) License and download a marble-surface stock backdrop, and crop/resize it to the exact mockup canvas.
6) Build the botanical side-panel texture: take the supplied leaf-motif art and convert it to a 2-color halftone/duotone in brand green so it prints clean as a spot-style panel.
7) Vectorize the supplied raster logo into a clean SVG for the carton.
8) Export the client's authored carton dieline (.ai) to print PDF + a preview PNG (this is the structural cutting layout — connector EXPORTS it, does not author it).
9) Data-merge the 3 SKU rows from the CSV onto the client's authored label template (.indd with <<product_name>>, <<key_actives>>, <<volume>>, <<batch>> fields) to produce one print-ready label per SKU.
10) Export the merged label document to a CMYK-ready, bleed-correct print PDF.
11) Convert the supplied brand-guide PDF into an editable .indd so the studio can keep the spec sheet in InDesign.
12) Assemble the produced hero PNG, marble backdrop, duotone panel, vector logo, dieline preview and label previews onto a Firefly board as the client-facing concept presentation.

Deliverables: retouched + background-removed hero PNG, marble mockup backdrop, brand-green duotone side panel, vector logo SVG, exported carton dieline PDF/PNG, 3 data-merged label print PDFs, an editable brand-guide .indd, and a Firefly concept board link.
Deliverables (9)
  • hero_bottle_retouched_cutout.png — Background-removed transparent PNG of the straightened, auto-toned, exposure-/temperature-corrected, Subject-Pop-graded amber serum bottle; full hero resolution (~4000x5000 region), ready to drop onto
  • marble_mockup_backdrop.jpg — Licensed marble-surface stock backdrop cropped/resized to the 3000x3000px mockup canvas.
  • leaf_duotone_panel.png — Brand-green 2-color halftone/duotone side-panel texture derived from the supplied leaf motif, print-clean.
  • aurelia_logo.svg — Clean vectorized SVG of the AURELIA BOTANICS wordmark + leaf mark, single solid spot color, scalable for the carton.
  • carton_dieline_print.pdf — Print PDF exported from the authored carton dieline .ai with cut/fold lines, 3mm bleed, registration marks, CMYK.
  • carton_dieline_preview.png — Flat raster preview PNG of the same authored carton dieline .ai (rasterized export), used as the board thumbnail for the structural cutting layout.
  • labels_merged_print.pdf — One print-ready, 3mm-bleed, CMYK label page per SKU (3 pages) produced by data-merging skus.csv onto the authored label .indd.
  • brand_guide_editable.indd — Editable InDesign document (.indd, delivered zipped) converted from the supplied 4-page brand-guide PDF.
  • aurelia_concept_board — Firefly Board deep-link assembling the hero cutout, marble backdrop, duotone panel, vector logo, dieline preview PNG and label previews as the client-facing concept presentation.
Workflow — 18 tool steps
  1. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize upload of the 7 client-supplied assets (hero_serum_bottle.jpg, aurelia_logo_raster.png, leaf_motif.png, aurelia_carton_dieline.ai, aurelia_label_temp
  2. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the uploads from step 1 and get back stable asset URNs/URLs used by every downstream step.
  3. image_auto_straighten — Straighten the slightly off-axis hero serum photo from the finalized asset URNs.
  4. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the straightened hero to fix the base exposure/white balance baseline.
  5. image_adjust_exposure — Lift exposure on the auto-toned hero so the amber glass and dropper read premium.
  6. image_adjust_color_temperature — Warm the color temperature of the exposure-corrected hero for a consistent premium amber tone.
  7. image_apply_preset — Apply the Lightroom 'Subject Pop' masked Adaptive preset to the warmed hero so the bottle separates from the seamless backdrop.
  8. image_remove_background — Knock the graded bottle out of its background to a transparent PNG (final hero deliverable).
  9. asset_license_and_download_stock — Search Adobe Stock for a marble-surface backdrop, license it, and download the full-res presigned file (license BEFORE editing).
  10. image_crop_and_resize — Crop/resize the licensed marble backdrop to the exact 3000x3000px mockup canvas (mockup backdrop deliverable).
  11. image_apply_halftone — Convert the supplied leaf_motif to a 2-color halftone/duotone in brand green for the print side panel (duotone panel deliverable).
  12. image_vectorize — Vectorize the raster logo PNG into a clean single-color SVG for the carton (vector logo deliverable).
  13. document_render_vector — EXPORT the client-authored carton dieline .ai to a print PDF (cut/fold/bleed/registration preserved; connector exports, does not author).
  14. document_render_vector — EXPORT the same client-authored carton dieline .ai a second time to a flat raster preview PNG (separate format = separate render_vector call) for use as the boa
  15. document_merge_data_layout — InDesign data-merge: bind skus.csv rows to the authored label .indd <<product_name>>/<<key_actives>>/<<volume>>/<<batch>> fields → one label page per SKU. [T] n
  16. document_render_layout — Export the merged 3-page label document to a CMYK, 3mm-bleed print PDF (label print PDF deliverable).
  17. document_convert_pdf — Convert the supplied 4-page brand_guide.pdf into an editable .indd (ZIP) so the studio keeps the spec in InDesign.
  18. create_firefly_board — Assemble the produced hero cutout, marble backdrop, duotone panel, vector logo, dieline preview PNG and label previews into a Firefly concept board (client-faci
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon (18 connector calls, 17 distinct Adobe tools) spanning 4 capability groups: photo/raster retouch (A1), document/layout export + data-merge + PDF convert (A2), asset/stock/board (A4). The 7-step linear hero-retouch chain plus four parallel asset-prep branches that all reconverge on a Firefly board exercise genuine output->input dependency management. It stays strictly inside connector limits: NO from-scratch layout composition (render_vector/render_layout only EXPORT the client-authored .ai/.indd), NO generative fill or by-prompt compositing, image_fill_area not abused (not used at all), and the one [T] step (data-merge) correctly depends on a user-authored .indd carrying real <<fields>> per the v2.1 sheet. The only non-[C] step is the [T] data-merge; everything else is headless-confirmed [C], and there is no [L] local step. Difficulty comes from breadth of distinct tools, depth of the retouch chain, and the discipline of producing real print-prep artifacts (CMYK/bleed PDFs, vector SVG, editable .indd) without crossing into not-available territory.

aurelia_carton_dieline.ai.proxy.txt
content
**File:** `aurelia_carton_dieline.ai` (Adobe Illustrator)

**Description:** Client-authored structural vector template for a folding carton dieline, tailored for a 40x40x120mm serum box.

**Structure:**
*   **Panels:** Flat dieline includes front, back, and two side panels.
*   **Flaps:** Features top and bottom tuck flaps, complete with associated dust flaps.
*   **Lines:** Clearly defined cut lines and fold/score lines.

**Layers:**
*   `Cut`: Dedicated layer containing all exterior and interior cut lines.
*   `Fold`: Dedicated layer containing all score lines for folding.

**Technical Specifications:**
*   **Bleed:** 3mm bleed area established beyond all exterior cut lines.
*   **Marks:** Standard registration marks included for print alignment.
*   **Color Mode:** Document is set to CMYK color profile.

**Placeholders:**
*   Rectangular placeholder guides are present for artwork placement, explicitly labeled: `FRONT`, `BACK`, and `SIDE`.
aurelia_label_template.indd.proxy.txt
content
**Template File:** `aurelia_label_template.indd` (Adobe InDesign Label Template)

**Dimensions:** 70x90mm with a 3mm bleed on all sides.
**Color Mode:** CMYK.

**Structure & Layers:**
The template is structured with distinct layers for text and graphics to facilitate editing and data-merge.
*   **Text Layer:** Contains the dynamic data-merge placeholders.
*   **Graphics Layer:** Holds the static design elements.
*   **Background Layer:** For brand color application.

**Placeholders (Data-Merge Fields):**
The template is authored with genuine InDesign data-merge fields, ready for binding with an external data source:
*   `<<product_name>>`
*   `<<key_actives>>`
*   `<<volume>>`
*   `<<batch>>`

**Typographic Hierarchy:**
A defined typographic hierarchy is applied to the merge fields:
*   `<<product_name>>`: Large, prominent serif typeface.
*   `<<key_actives>>`: Medium-sized, legible sans-serif.
*   `<<volume>>` & `<<batch>>`: Smaller, clean sans-serif for secondary information.

**Graphic Elements:**
*   An accurately positioned image frame is included on the left-hand side, designated for a "leaf side-panel texture" graphic.

**Brand Styling:**
*   **Swatches:** Dedicated brand swatches are pre-defined in the document:
    *   `Aurelia_Green` (CMYK value specified within InDesign)
    *   `Aurelia_Cream` (CMYK value specified within InDesign)
*   These swatches are applied to background elements and accent typography.

**Key Feature:** This is a natively authored InDesign `.indd` file with live, binding `<<fields>>` for data-merge, fully compliant with the `[T]` mode requirement for variable data printing.

AO-67 — Tamil Traditional Service-Promotion Large-Format Banner Set — Print-Prep & Source-File Production (6 sizes) ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Take my supplied Tamil banner master files plus hero photography and brand logo, and produce the complete print-ready production package — graded hero image with print bleed, a clean vector logo, a traditional halftone texture motif, and CMYK 150-dpi PDF/JPEG exports of all six large-format banners — with editable source files alongside.

💰 ~$90 est.Freelancer.com28 tool calls16 distinct toolslong-horizon
Full client brief
I run a traditional Tamil service business and I'm rolling out a promotion across six printed banners (design already laid out by my designer): 10x10 ft (3 pieces), 10x20 ft (2 pieces), 10x6 ft (1 piece). All copy is in Tamil and the look must stay clearly traditional — warm, festive, gold/maroon tones, eye-catching from a distance. I will supply you: (1) my authored Illustrator master files for the banners with all Tamil typography already set correctly in licensed Tamil fonts (do NOT re-typeset or translate — typography is locked), (2) one wide hero photograph of my premises/service scene that needs heavy correction and a warm traditional grade, (3) my brand logo as a flat raster file that I need cleaned up into crisp vector for large print, and (4) a low-res PDF proof of last year's banner that I want converted back into an editable layout source.

What I need from you, all on the Adobe production side:
- Fix and grade the hero photo: straighten it, auto-correct tone, warm it up, push the gold/maroon vibrance, pull back blown highlights, then isolate the main service element so its colour stays controlled.
- The hero photo prints right to the edge, so I need 1 inch of real bleed around it for the largest 10x20 ft layout — since the source is an ultra-wide capture with room to spare, reframe and resize it to the bleed-inclusive target dimensions so the existing image content fills all the way into the 1-inch bleed margin (crop to fit, don't just pad).
- Turn my flat logo into a clean scalable vector so it stays sharp at 10-foot height.
- Build me a traditional half-tone / screen-print texture motif derived from the graded hero so it reads as a printed-poster aesthetic up close.
- Export each of the six authored banner masters as a flattened high-res raster and as a print PDF — CMYK intent, 150 dpi minimum at full scale, 1 inch bleed all around.
- Convert my old PDF proof into an editable InDesign source so I can reuse last year's layout.
- Hand back a single packaged folder with every final and source file organised.

Acceptance: traditional consistent look across all sizes, no damage to the locked Tamil typography, and print-ready files meeting CMYK / 150 dpi / 1-inch-bleed specs. One round of minor revisions expected after I review the first draft.
Deliverables (10)
  • hero_graded_bleed.png — Corrected + warm-traditional-graded hero photograph (straightened, auto-toned, highlights recovered, gold/maroon vibrance pushed, controlled subject color) with 1 inch of real reframe-cropped bleed on
  • brand_logo_vector.svg — Clean scalable SVG vectorization of the flat brand logo, sharp at 10-foot print height, solid maroon/gold fills, Tamil emblem preserved.
  • halftone_texture_motif.png — Traditional screen-print / halftone texture motif derived from the graded hero, for the close-up printed-poster aesthetic. High-res PNG.
  • banner_10x20_print.pdf + .png — Print export of the authored 10x20 ft master: CMYK PDF plus flattened high-res sRGB PNG, full-scale 150 dpi, 1-inch bleed, traditional look intact, Tamil typography untouched. (CMYK intent applies to
  • banner_10x10_a_print.pdf + .png — Print export of the first authored 10x10 ft master (banner_10x10_a): CMYK PDF plus flattened high-res sRGB PNG, full-scale 150 dpi, 1-inch bleed, traditional look intact, Tamil typography untouched.
  • banner_10x10_b_print.pdf + .png — Print export of the second authored 10x10 ft master (banner_10x10_b): CMYK PDF plus flattened high-res sRGB PNG, full-scale 150 dpi, 1-inch bleed, traditional look intact, Tamil typography untouched.
  • banner_10x10_c_print.pdf + .png — Print export of the third authored 10x10 ft master (banner_10x10_c): CMYK PDF plus flattened high-res sRGB PNG, full-scale 150 dpi, 1-inch bleed, traditional look intact, Tamil typography untouched.
  • banner_10x20_b_print.pdf + .png — Print export of the second authored 10x20 ft master (banner_10x20_b): CMYK PDF plus flattened high-res sRGB PNG, full-scale 150 dpi, 1-inch bleed, traditional look intact, Tamil typography untouched.
  • banner_10x6_print.pdf + .png — Print export of the authored 10x6 ft master: CMYK PDF plus flattened high-res sRGB PNG, full-scale 150 dpi, 1-inch bleed, traditional look intact, Tamil typography untouched.
  • banner_set_delivery_folder — Organised CC delivery folder (populated via connector-side asset copy, not a zip archive) containing the print PDF + high-res PNG exports for all six authored banner masters (3x 10x10, 2x 10x20, 1x 10
Workflow — 28 tool steps
  1. asset_initialize_file_upload — Client supplies banner_hero_photo.jpg. Initialize the upload session for the hero photograph into CC storage. First asset load — no prior-step input.
  2. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the hero upload and obtain the stored asset URL that the editing chain will consume.
  3. image_auto_straighten — Correct the ~3-degree tilted horizon in the hero capture.
  4. image_apply_auto_tone — Baseline tonal correction on the straightened image.
  5. image_adjust_highlights — Pull back the blown-out sky/highlight region carried from the prior step.
  6. image_adjust_color_temperature — Warm the white balance toward the traditional festive gold palette.
  7. image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation — Push gold/maroon richness on the warmed image for distance legibility.
  8. image_select_by_prompt — Mask the main service element (e.g. the brass lamp / seva counter) on the graded image to control its colour.
  9. image_adjust_single_color_saturation — Apply masked single-colour saturation so the focal element's colour stays controlled — selective edit via the step-8 mask.
  10. image_crop_and_resize — GEN-FREE bleed: the source is a 3:1 ultra-wide capture with room to spare, so reframe and resize the graded hero to the bleed-inclusive target dimensions for th
  11. asset_initialize_file_upload — Client supplies brand_logo_flat.png. Initialize upload of the flat raster logo. Second asset load.
  12. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize logo upload, obtain stored URL for vectorization.
  13. image_vectorize — Vectorize the flat solid-fill logo into a clean SVG, sharp at 10-foot height. Deliverable #2.
  14. image_apply_halftone — Derive a traditional screen-print halftone texture motif from the graded+bleed hero. Deliverable #3 (consumes step-10 output).
  15. asset_initialize_file_upload — Client supplies authored banner_master_10x20.ai (the largest single master). Initialize upload of the genuine .ai master that will be EXPORTED (not composed).
  16. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the authored 10x20 .ai master upload, obtain its stored URL.
  17. document_render_vector — EXPORT the genuine authored 10x20 ft .ai master to a CMYK print PDF + flattened high-res sRGB PNG (full-scale 150 dpi, 1-inch bleed). Tamil typography untouched
  18. asset_initialize_file_upload — Client supplies the banner_masters_remaining.ai bundle (the other five authored masters: 3x 10x10, 1x 10x20, 1x 10x6). Initialize upload of the genuine sibling
  19. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the remaining-masters upload, obtain the stored URL(s) for the five sibling .ai masters.
  20. document_render_vector — EXPORT the first authored 10x10 ft .ai master to a CMYK print PDF + flattened high-res sRGB PNG (full-scale 150 dpi, 1-inch bleed). Tamil typography untouched.
  21. document_render_vector — EXPORT the second authored 10x10 ft .ai master to a CMYK print PDF + flattened high-res sRGB PNG (full-scale 150 dpi, 1-inch bleed). Tamil typography untouched.
  22. document_render_vector — EXPORT the third authored 10x10 ft .ai master to a CMYK print PDF + flattened high-res sRGB PNG (full-scale 150 dpi, 1-inch bleed). Tamil typography untouched.
  23. document_render_vector — EXPORT the second authored 10x20 ft .ai master to a CMYK print PDF + flattened high-res sRGB PNG (full-scale 150 dpi, 1-inch bleed). Tamil typography untouched.
  24. document_render_vector — EXPORT the authored 10x6 ft .ai master to a CMYK print PDF + flattened high-res sRGB PNG (full-scale 150 dpi, 1-inch bleed). Tamil typography untouched. Sixth o
  25. asset_initialize_file_upload — Client supplies last_year_proof.pdf. Initialize upload of the flattened proof PDF for back-conversion.
  26. document_convert_pdf — Convert the supplied proof PDF into a genuine editable .indd source so the client can reuse last year's layout.
  27. asset_create_folders — Create the organised delivery folder structure for finals + sources.
  28. asset_copy_assets — Copy every produced final + source (all six banner exports, the hero bleed, logo SVG, halftone, and editable indd) into the delivery folder for the single packa
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon (28 connector calls) across three Adobe groups (image_*, document_*, asset_*) with 16 distinct Adobe tools — well past the >=10 calls / >=6 distinct-tools bar. It is a true dependency chain: an 8-step hero grade that must be applied in the correct order (straighten before tone, highlights before warming, mask before masked-saturation, bleed last), a branched logo-vectorize path, an authored-master export path that renders all six masters individually, a PDF->.indd back-conversion, and a final packaging fan-in that consumes every deliverable. The hard reasoning is feasibility discipline: recognizing that Tamil typesetting and layout composition are out of scope so the masters must be AUTHORED INPUTS that document_render_vector only EXPORTS; that bleed must be an honest reframe-crop (image_crop_and_resize reframes/resizes the existing ultra-wide source to the bleed-inclusive target ratio, accepting that edges are cropped — no padding, no generative fill, no canvas extension); that the focal colour control needs a select_by_prompt mask feeding a masked adjustment; and that every one of the six masters needs its own EXPORT call rather than a single asserted 'repeat'. All print-spec acceptance criteria (CMYK PDF, 150 dpi at full scale, 1-inch bleed, untouched Tamil type) are honoured by the export/authoring split. Every step is execution-mode tagged [C] and headless-con

banner_master_10x20.ai.proxy.txt
content
**File:** `banner_master_10x20.ai`

**Description:**
Adobe Illustrator master document for a 10ft x 20ft large-format banner, designed in a traditional Tamil festive style.

**Structure & Content:**
*   **Dimensions:** Artboard set to 120" x 240" (10ft x 20ft).
*   **Bleed:** 1-inch bleed defined on all sides (total document size including bleed: 122" x 242").
*   **Color Mode:** CMYK.
*   **Resolution Target:** 150 dpi at full scale.
*   **Typography:**
    *   Tamil promotional headline (live text, not outlined).
    *   Tamil body copy (live text, not outlined).
    *   Text set in a licensed Tamil OpenType font.
*   **Visual Elements:**
    *   Maroon and gold ornamental border framing the design.
    *   Dedicated placeholder frame for a hero photograph.
    *   Dedicated circular placeholder frame for a logo.

**Styling:**
The design incorporates a traditional Tamil festive aesthetic, characterized by the maroon and gold color palette and ornamental border. This is the largest of a family of six banner masters.
banner_masters_remaining.ai.proxy.txt
content
**Template Description: banner_masters_remaining.ai**

This asset is a bundle containing six distinct Adobe Illustrator (.ai) master banner templates, adhering to the traditional Tamil style and specifications of `banner_master_10x20.ai`.

*   **Quantity & Dimensions:**
    *   Three (3) templates: 10ft x 10ft (120 inches x 120 inches artboard)
    *   Two (2) templates: 10ft x 20ft (120 inches x 240 inches artboard)
    *   One (1) template: 10ft x 6ft (120 inches x 72 inches artboard)

*   **Technical Specifications (All Templates):**
    *   **Color Mode:** CMYK
    *   **Resolution Target:** 150 DPI
    *   **Bleed:** 1 inch on all sides

*   **Structure & Layers:**
    *   **Locked Typography Layer:** Contains pre-set Tamil copy, utilizing specified licensed fonts. This layer is non-editable.
    *   **Locked Ornamental Layer:** Features consistent maroon and gold traditional Tamil ornamental design elements. This layer is non-editable.
    *   **Content Placeholder Layer:** Clearly defined, unlocked area for dynamic image and content insertion.

*   **Brand Styling:**
    *   **Aesthetic:** Traditional Tamil, ornate.
    *   **Color Palette:** Predominantly maroon and gold for decorative elements.
