AO-68 — Roadside Coaching-Institute Banner: brand-graded hero, vector icon set, halftone geometry + print-ready CMYK PDF export ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Prepare every visual asset for the DYNAMIC SOLUTION CLASSES 10ft x 5ft roadside banner — brand-graded student hero, vector subject icons, brand-colour halftone geometry — and export the authored InDesign layout to a print-ready CMYK PDF for large-format flex printing.

💰 ~$200 est.Freelancer.com18 tool calls16 distinct toolslong-horizon
Full client brief
DYNAMIC SOLUTION CLASSES needs a clean, premium, professional roadside banner (10 ft x 5 ft, 2:1) that stays readable from the road and presents the course/contact info without clutter. The brand palette is primary #046CB8 and secondary #02ADEF, headings/key numbers in bold Inter, with subject-hinting icons, a confident-but-minimal photo of students, and understated geometric elements. The freelancer has already authored the layered banner in InDesign with every line of verbatim copy (institute name, tagline \"Where Learning Meets Success\", the Pre-Foundation / 9th-10th / 11th-12th course list, \"ADMISSIONS OPEN\", the three phone numbers with 8103332901 most prominent, the \"Railway Station Road, Multai\" location, and the four feature bullets). That authored layout (.indd) plus a raw, slightly-tilted DSLR photo of students, a flat icon sheet, and the brand-color swatch file are the client inputs.\n\nYour Adobe production job, end to end:\n1. Stand up the project folders and load/preview the raw student photo and the icon sheet.\n2. Fix the photo geometry: auto-straighten the tilted horizon, then crop to the banner hero band aspect.\n3. Correct and grade it to the brand: auto-tone for a clean baseline, then warm/cool the white balance and push the dominant tones toward the #046CB8 / #02ADEF brand blues via a color overlay and a targeted single-colour saturation lift so the photo \"matches the specs\".\n4. Produce a clean placement plate: select the students as the subject and remove the background to a transparent cutout the banner can drop onto its geometric field.\n5. Reframe the graded photo to the full 2:1 banner hero ratio with a gen-free crop/resize (the 4:3 source is wider than tall enough for a 2:1 reframe, so the existing pixels are cropped/resized to the print-scale hero band — no invented pixels, no pixelation at full print size).\n6. Turn the flat icon sheet into crisp, infinitely-scalable print vectors (vectorize → SVG) so the subject icons stay sharp on a 10-foot banner.\n7. Build the \"understated geometric element\": take the brand-blue field and render a halftone/duotone texture in the brand colours for a modern, minimal accent.\n8. Export the client-authored InDesign banner layout to the final print-ready, high-resolution CMYK PDF for large-format flex printing, and also export a JPEG proof.\n9. Assemble the graded hero, the cutout plate, the vector icons, the halftone texture and the PDF/JPEG proof onto a single Firefly board so the client can sign off that the file opens cleanly, the colours match, and the hierarchy is crystal clear at full size.\n\nThe client signs off once the PDF opens cleanly, colours match #046CB8/#02ADEF in CMYK, and the text hierarchy is legible at full 10ft x 5ft size.
Deliverables (7)
  • students_hero_brandgraded_2x1.png — Straightened, cropped, auto-toned, white-balanced and brand-graded student hero photo pushed toward #046CB8/#02ADEF, reframed via a gen-free crop/resize to the full 2:1 (10ft x 5ft) banner hero band,
  • students_cutout_transparent.png — Background-removed transparent-PNG cutout of the student group for clean placement onto the banner's geometric field.
  • subject_icons.svg — Brand-blue subject icon set vectorized to clean scalable SVG paths that stay crisp at 10-foot print size.
  • brand_halftone_texture.png — Modern, minimal halftone/duotone geometric texture in the #046CB8/#02ADEF brand colours for the understated background accent.
  • DYNAMIC_SOLUTION_banner_PRINT.pdf — Print-ready, high-resolution CMYK PDF of the authored banner layout, 10ft x 5ft, exported for large-format flex printing — the primary client deliverable.
  • DYNAMIC_SOLUTION_banner_proof.jpg — Flattened JPEG proof of the banner layout for quick on-screen client review.
  • DYNAMIC_SOLUTION_signoff_board — Firefly board deep-link assembling the graded hero, cutout plate, vector icons, halftone texture and the PDF/JPEG proof for client sign-off.
Workflow — 18 tool steps
  1. asset_create_folders — Stand up /DynamicSolutionBanner with /raw, /graded, /vector, /export subfolders to organize the job.
  2. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize the upload of the client's raw student photo, icon sheet and brand-palette swatch into /raw.
  3. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize ingest of students_raw_photo.jpg, subject_icons_sheet.png and brand_palette_swatch.png; returns asset handles.
  4. asset_preview_file — Preview the ingested raw photo to confirm the tilt and flat tone before processing — establishes the working source.
  5. image_auto_straighten — Auto-straighten the slightly-tilted handheld horizon so the hero sits level on the banner.
  6. image_crop_and_resize — Crop/resize the straightened photo toward the banner hero band framing.
  7. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone for a clean, balanced exposure/contrast baseline before brand color work.
  8. image_adjust_color_temperature — Correct white balance, nudging toward the cool brand-blue feel and removing the classroom-light cast.
  9. image_apply_color_overlay — Apply a subtle #046CB8 brand color overlay so the hero reads on-brand and 'matches the specs'; the ingested brand_palette_swatch.png supplies the exact #046CB8/
  10. image_adjust_single_color_saturation — Lift the blue channel's saturation to lock the dominant tone to the brand palette without oversaturating skin.
  11. image_select_subject — Select the student group as the subject to build a clean placement cutout.
  12. image_remove_background — Remove the background to a transparent-PNG cutout (DELIVERABLE) for dropping onto the banner's geometric field.
  13. image_crop_and_resize — Crop/resize the graded hero to the full 2:1 banner ratio (DELIVERABLE) at print scale. The 4:3 source is wide enough to reframe to 2:1, so existing pixels are c
  14. image_vectorize — Vectorize the flat icon sheet into clean SVG paths (DELIVERABLE) that stay crisp at large-format print size.
  15. image_apply_halftone — Render a brand-colour halftone/duotone texture (DELIVERABLE) from the graded field for the understated geometric accent.
  16. document_render_layout — Export the client-AUTHORED .indd banner layout (whose reserved picture frames hold the hero, cutout, icons and texture) to the print-ready high-res CMYK PDF (PR
  17. document_render_layout — Export the same authored layout to a flattened JPEG proof (DELIVERABLE) for quick on-screen client review.
  18. create_firefly_board — Assemble all produced assets + the proof onto a Firefly board (DELIVERABLE) so the client can sign off that colours match and hierarchy is crystal clear.
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon and wide-mix: 18 connector calls, 16 distinct Adobe tools, spanning four capability groups (asset/storage, photo/raster, vector, document/export, plus board). The photo prep is a genuine 7-stage linear retouch+grade chain (straighten → crop → auto-tone → white-balance → brand overlay → single-color saturation) before fanning into three independent derivatives, which forces correct ordering and reuse of an intermediate (photo_brandgraded reused 3x). It exercises the trickiest grounding distinction in the sheet: layout COMPOSITION is [X], so the banner cannot be typeset by the connector — the authored .indd is supplied as a client input and document_render_layout only EXPORTS it. The reframe to the 2:1 hero band is a gen-free image_crop_and_resize that crops height from the existing pixels — there is no generative step anywhere in the workflow. No Canva, no Express [W] widget track, no generative fill / generative expand / object removal / text-to-image / upscaling / image→PDF. CMYK print-prep, large-format no-pixelation, brand-hex color matching and a multi-asset sign-off board make it a faithful, runnable benchmark of the real freelance brief.

DYNAMIC_SOLUTION_banner_AUTHORED.indd.proxy.txt
content
**Template Description: DYNAMIC_SOLUTION_banner_AUTHORED.indd**

This is a client-authored InDesign banner document (.indd), designed for a 10ft x 5ft print scale with a CMYK color profile. The layout is fully composed and ready for export, featuring a distinct brand aesthetic and comprehensive information.

**Visual Style:**
*   **Background:** A brand field with a gradient transition from #046CB8 to #02ADEF.
*   **Typography:** All text is set in bold Inter font, with size and weight variations establishing a clear visual hierarchy.

**Layout & Content:**
The banner is structured to present key information prominently:

*   **Header/Primary Messaging:**
    *   **"DYNAMIC SOLUTION CLASSES"** (large, primary institute name)
    *   **"Where Learning Meets Success"** (tagline, positioned beneath the institute name)

*   **Central Content Area:**
    *   **Hero Photo Frame:** A large, dominant picture frame is reserved for the main hero image.
    *   **Course List:** Clearly listed and formatted:
        *   "Pre-Foundation 5th-8th All Subjects"
        *   "9th & 10th Maths/Science/Social Science/English"
        *   "11th & 12th PCMB & Commerce"
    *   **Call to Action:** **"ADMISSIONS OPEN"** (prominently displayed)

*   **Contact & Features Section:**
    *   **Phone Numbers:** Displayed with the largest number being "8103332901", followed by "8989871223" and "9589110600".
    *   **Location:** "Railway Station Road Multai".
    *   **Feature Bullets:** Four distinct feature points, likely accompanied by icon frames:
        *   Small Batch Size
        *   Personal Attention
        *   Regular Tests
        *   Doubt Sessions

*   **Graphic Placeholders:**
    *   **Icon Frames:** Multiple small picture frames are reserved for icons, likely associated with the feature bullets.
    *   **Geometric Texture Frame:** A dedicated frame is present for a geometric texture element, providing visual depth.

**Structure:** The document is comprised of live text frames, image placeholders (picture frames), and vector shapes for background and decorative elements, ensuring full editability and high-fidelity output.

AO-69 — TitanSeal Sealants Launch — Logo-System Extension, Swappable SKU Label/Carton Data-Merge, and Print-Ready + Digital Asset Package ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Take the approved TitanSeal master logo plus the client's authored InDesign label .indd and Illustrator carton .ai mechanicals, derive the full logo-variant system, data-merge the three SKUs into swappable print-ready label/carton artwork, and output the print PDFs plus a correctly-sized digital-asset pack.

💰 ~$200 est.Upwork22 tool calls14 distinct toolslong-horizon
Full client brief
TitanSeal is a Dubai-based B2B private-label sealants brand launching three SKUs in January 2027. The master logo is approved and supplied as a high-res transparent PNG; the client also supplies two desktop-authored print mechanicals carrying real merge fields/variables (an InDesign wrap label .indd and an Illustrator carton .ai) and a sku_variants.csv with the three SKUs (600ml MS Polymer grey, 600ml PU Construction grey, 300ml Neutral Silicone white). \n\nUsing ONLY the Adobe connector, the freelancer must: (1) upload the supplied logo and mechanicals into a project workspace; (2) build the LOGO-VARIANT SYSTEM from the one master logo — a clean vector SVG (for scalable lockups), a single-color BLACK mono version and a single-color WHITE reversed/knockout version (for the black-on-cardboard carton and dark backgrounds), and a brand-blue color-overlay version — without redrawing the mark; (3) build the wide HORIZONTAL LOCKUP working canvas by cropping/reframing the square logo composition to a 3:1 banner band (a gen-free crop of the source, no invented pixels); (4) run the swappable-SKU LABEL data-merge: the authored .indd + sku_variants.csv produce one print-ready label mechanical per SKU so SKU codes/volumes swap by editing the CSV, then export those merged labels to press-ready PDF; (5) run the CARTON data-merge from the authored .ai Variables + CSV for the 20-cartridge and 24-tube single-color cartons and export to PDF/SVG; (6) assemble the DIGITAL pack — license a construction/jobsite background from Adobe Stock, color-grade it on-brand, then crop-and-resize it to the exact platform specs (1080x1080 social tile, 1920x1080 web banner, 350x120 email signature, and a 1:1 WhatsApp catalog card); and (7) present the finished logo-variant system and key assets as a Firefly board deep-link for client review. \n\nOUT OF SCOPE for the connector (flag as human/desktop finishing): authoring the 5–8pp brand-standards PDF body copy and typography pages, drawing any new layout from scratch, and the Amazon UAE listing page composition. The connector's job is the asset/variant system, the merged swappable SKU mechanicals, the export-ready print PDFs, and the correctly-sized digital tiles.</full_brief>
<parameter name="outputs">[{"name":"titanseal_logo.svg","kind":"vector","spec":"Clean scalable SVG vectorized from the master logo PNG; solid-fill paths, usable in print lockups at any size."},{"name":"titanseal_logo_mono_black.png","kind":"image","spec":"Single-color solid black version of the mark on transparent background, for the black-on-cardboard carton."},{"name":"titanseal_logo_reversed_white.png","kind":"image","spec":"Single-color solid white reversed/knockout version on transparent background, for dark/photo backgrounds."},{"name":"titanseal_logo_brandb
Deliverables (12)
  • titanseal_logo.svg — Clean scalable SVG vectorized from the master logo PNG; solid-fill paths, usable in print lockups at any size.
  • titanseal_logo_mono_black.png — Single-color solid black version of the mark on transparent background, for the black-on-cardboard carton.
  • titanseal_logo_reversed_white.png — Single-color solid white reversed/knockout version on transparent background, for dark/photo backgrounds.
  • titanseal_logo_brandblue.png — Brand-blue color-overlay version of the mark on transparent background.
  • titanseal_horizontal_lockup.png — 3:1 horizontal-lockup band cropped/reframed from the square logo composition (a horizontal band of the source, no invented pixels, output no larger than the source), centered on the mark; the top/bott
  • titanseal_labels_merged.pdf — Press-ready multi-page PDF, one page per SKU, from the authored .indd + CSV data-merge; 3mm bleed, swappable SKU codes/volumes via CSV.
  • titanseal_cartons_merged.pdf — Single-color black carton artwork PDF for 20-cartridge and 24-tube packs, from the authored .ai Variables + CSV; die-line preserved.
  • titanseal_social_tile_1080.png — 1080x1080 on-brand graded social media tile (jobsite background graded + cropped).
  • titanseal_web_banner_1920x1080.png — 1920x1080 web banner crop of the graded jobsite asset.
  • titanseal_email_signature_350x120.png — 350x120 email-signature crop of the graded jobsite asset.
  • titanseal_whatsapp_card_1x1.png — 1:1 WhatsApp catalog product card crop of the graded jobsite asset.
  • titanseal_review_board — Firefly Board deep-link presenting the logo-variant system and key produced assets for client review.
Workflow — 22 tool steps
  1. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize upload of the client's approved master logo PNG into the project workspace.
  2. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the logo upload to get a stable asset/presigned URL for the chain.
  3. image_vectorize — Vectorize the raster logo into a clean scalable SVG for print lockups — no redrawing.
  4. image_apply_monochromatic_tint — Derive the single-color BLACK mono version for the black-on-cardboard carton.
  5. image_apply_color_overlay — Overlay solid white to produce the reversed/knockout version for dark/photo backgrounds.
  6. image_apply_color_overlay — Overlay brand blue (#0B3D91) to produce the on-brand color variant.
  7. image_crop_and_resize — Reframe the square logo composition to a 3:1 horizontal-lockup band by cropping the existing 3000x3000 source (a gen-free crop, output never larger than the sou
  8. asset_initialize_file_upload — Upload the client-authored InDesign label mechanical carrying real <<merge fields>>.
  9. asset_initialize_file_upload — Upload the sku_variants.csv whose headers bind to both authored templates.
  10. document_merge_data_layout — InDesign data-merge: authored .indd <<fields>> + CSV → one swappable label mechanical per SKU (codes change by editing the CSV).
  11. document_render_layout — Export the merged label .indd to a press-ready multi-page PDF with bleed.
  12. asset_initialize_file_upload — Upload the client-authored Illustrator carton mechanical with named Variables.
  13. document_merge_data_vector — Illustrator Variables data-merge: authored .ai Variables + CSV → single-color carton artwork for 20-cartridge and 24-tube packs.
  14. document_render_vector — Export the merged carton .ai to press-ready PDF (die-line preserved, single-color black).
  15. asset_license_and_download_stock — Search + license a construction/jobsite stock photo and pull the full-res presigned URL (license before editing).
  16. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the licensed jobsite photo as the grading base.
  17. image_adjust_color_temperature — Shift temperature on-brand (cool industrial) to finish the color grade for all digital tiles.
  18. image_crop_and_resize — Crop+resize the graded asset to the 1080x1080 social tile spec.
  19. image_crop_and_resize — Crop+resize the graded asset to the 1920x1080 web banner spec.
  20. image_crop_and_resize — Crop+resize the graded asset to the 350x120 email-signature spec.
  21. image_crop_and_resize — Crop+resize the graded asset to a 1:1 WhatsApp catalog card.
  22. create_firefly_board — Assemble the produced logo-variant system + key assets into a Firefly Board deep-link for client review.
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon flagship: 22 connector calls, 14 distinct Adobe tools, spanning 4 connector groups (asset/storage, image raster/vector, document layout/vector data-merge+render, Firefly board). It demands the agent recognize the honest connector boundary — that label/carton "swappable-element templates" can only be produced via authored-template [T] data-merge (InDesign data-merge + Illustrator Variables) bound to a header-matched CSV, that logo variants must be DERIVED from the one supplied mark (vectorize / monochromatic_tint / color_overlay / crop_and_resize reframe) rather than redrawn, and that brand-standards-PDF body authoring and Amazon listing composition are out of connector scope and must be flagged. Multiple independent sub-chains (logo system, label merge, carton merge, digital pack) reconverge on a single review board, with one graded asset reused across four exact platform crop specs — exercising fan-out, fan-in, [T] template binding, and [C] export in one runnable benchmark.

titanseal_carton_mechanical.ai.proxy.txt
content
**File:** `titanseal_carton_mechanical.ai`

**Description:**
Illustrator template for a corrugated shipping carton mechanical. Designed for single-color (K=100) black print on natural kraft corrugated stock, aligning with cost constraints.

**Structure & Variants:**
The template features two primary size variants – 20-cartridge and 24-tube – dynamically controlled via an Illustrator `CARTON_SIZE` visibility variable. This variable toggles between the respective die-line geometries and associated artwork layouts, ensuring accurate scaling and positioning for each carton type.

**Layers:**
Organized with a dedicated, non-printing, locked `_DIE_LINE` layer (utilizing a spot color, e.g., "Dieline" or "CutContour") and a primary `_ARTWORK` layer for all printable elements.

**Content & Brand Styling:**
Includes the TitanSeal mono mark logo, standardized "Fragile" and "This Way Up" pictograms, and brand-consistent typography for all text elements. All graphics are rendered in solid black.

**Illustrator Variables (Pre-defined):**
The template is authored with Illustrator Named Variables, enabling automated data merge.

*   **Text Variables:**
    *   `SKU_NAME` (e.g., "TitanSeal Silicone Sealant")
    *   `SKU_CODE` (e.g., "TS20CRTG-BLK")
    *   `PACK_COUNT` (e.g., "20", "24")
    *   `BATCH` (e.g., "20231015A")
*   **Visibility Variable:**
    *   `CARTON_SIZE` (Controls which carton variant's die-line and artwork group is active, e.g., "20-Cartridge" or "24-Tube").

**Functionality:**
Configured for direct data binding from a CSV (e.g., `document_merge_data_vector.csv`), allowing rapid generation of specific SKU carton artwork per row.
titanseal_label_mechanical.indd.proxy.txt
content
**Template File:** `titanseal_label_mechanical.indd`

**Type:** Adobe InDesign Data Merge Template

**Purpose:** Client-provided master template for generating TitanSeal brand wrap-around cartridge labels. Designed for automated population of product-specific data via InDesign's native Data Merge feature, linking directly to a CSV data source.

**Dimensions:**
*   **Primary Page Size:** 200mm (width) x 95mm (height)
*   **Bleed:** 3mm applied universally to all edges.
*   **Content Adaptability:** The layout is engineered to accommodate a 297mm tube length variant (maintaining the 95mm height), with flexible text frames and graphic zones designed to scale or re-flow appropriately without manual adjustment.

**Structure & Layers:**
The document utilizes a standard layered structure for print production:
1.  **Die-line/Cut:** Non-printing layer defining the precise label cut contour.
2.  **Base Graphics:** Static brand elements, background colors, and fixed design patterns.
3.  **Dynamic Content:** Contains all data-merged text frames and image placeholders.
4.  **Legal/Fine Print:** Fixed legal disclaimers, compliance text, and registration marks.

**Placeholders (Native InDesign Data Merge Fields):**
The template features the following *real* InDesign data-merge placeholder fields, designed to bind directly to corresponding CSV header columns:
*   `<<SKU_NAME>>`: For the full product name (e.g., "TitanSeal Pro Grout Sealant").
*   `<<SKU_CODE>>`: For the unique product SKU or internal code (e.g., "TSG-5000").
*   `<<VOLUME>>`: For the cartridge volume (e.g., "310ml", "10.5 fl oz").
*   `<<COLORWAY>>`: For the product's color description (e.g., "Clear", "Grey", "White").
*   `<<BARCODE>>`: For the barcode string (e.g., EAN-13, UPC). This field is typically styled with a barcode font or linked to an external barcode image generation process.

**Linked Assets & Zones:**
*   **[TitanSeal Logo Frame]:** A dedicated, locked image frame positioned for the primary brand logo (expected as a linked asset, e.g., `titanseal_logo.ai`).
*   **[Hazard Pictogram Zone]:** A clearly defined area/frame for GHS (Globally Harmonized System) hazard pictograms, typically populated by linked image assets or conditional content based on product data.

**Brand Styling (TitanSeal):**
*   **Aesthetics:** Professional, industrial, and highly functional with an emphasis on clarity and im

AO-72 — Modern Store Graphics Package — Hero Banner, PDP Assets & Store Decoration ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Turn the client's product hero shot, stock backdrop, brand palette, and two authored source templates into a cohesive modern-storefront graphics package: a desktop + mobile hero banner, clean PDP image frames and vector icon/divider assets, and tied-together decoration — delivered as layered source exports plus optimized JPG/PNG.

💰 ~$75 est.Freelancer.com24 tool calls18 distinct toolslong-horizon
Full client brief
I'm refreshing my online shop and need a cohesive set of graphics that instantly tells shoppers they're in a sleek, modern space. Scope is three areas. (1) STORE BANNER — a hero that sets the tone the moment visitors land, sized for both desktop (1920x720) and mobile (1080x1350). I'll give you one clean product hero shot to build it from, framed with generous empty space so you can reframe it to both ratios by cropping (no invented pixels). (2) PDP ASSETS — template-ready image frames plus iconography and typography treatments that make features, specs and CTAs pop without clutter. (3) STORE DECORATION — background elements, section dividers and accent graphics that tie everything together page to page. Art direction: clean lines, bold but balanced color, plenty of negative space — nothing fussy or ornamental. I'm supplying brand colors (deep ink #14233B + warm sand #E7D3B1 accent), the product hero shot, a few supporting product crops, and two layered source templates my team authored in InDesign and Illustrator (the banner layout .indd with our logo + headline frames, and an .ai sheet of our icon/divider library) so you can produce the final layered source files and labeled exports we can tweak later. Please also pull one modern, negative-space lifestyle backdrop from stock for the secondary banner panel. Deliverables: layered PSD/AI/INDD source plus optimized JPG/PNG exports for immediate upload, clearly labeled, with desktop and mobile banner sizes, the PDP frame set, the vector icon + divider pack, and the decoration backgrounds. Lock the direction first, then banner, then PDP, then decoration.
Deliverables (8)
  • hero_banner_desktop.jpg — 1920x720 JPEG, sRGB, optimized for web (<400KB), product on warm-sand reframed to the wide desktop ratio using the source's own empty left third as negative space (cropped from existing pixels, no gen
  • hero_banner_mobile.jpg — 1080x1350 JPEG, sRGB, tall-ratio crop of the same graded hero (vertically reframed from the existing frame, no generation) for mobile storefront, optimized <350KB.
  • secondary_banner_panel.jpg — 1920x720 JPEG built on the licensed stock backdrop, brand-temperature-matched to the hero, negative-space right side for headline overlay.
  • pdp_frame_set.png — Set of 1:1 PDP image frames (1200x1200 PNG) cropped from the product detail macros, consistently toned to match the hero look, clean edges, transparent-safe margins.
  • icon_pack.svg — Clean vectorized icon + divider pack exported from the authored .ai plus one raster-derived accent vectorized to SVG; single-weight deep-ink strokes, transparent, scalable for PDP and decoration.
  • store_banner_source.indd_export — Press/preview export (PDF + PNG) of the authored layered banner .indd source, delivered alongside the original layered .indd so the client team can swap text/images.
  • decoration_backgrounds.png — Two tileable/section decoration backgrounds (2000x1200 PNG) in brand paper + accent tones with subtle grain, plus the exported section-divider graphics; ties pages together.
  • store_graphics_package.zip — Single labeled ZIP bundling all layered source exports and optimized JPG/PNG/SVG deliverables in clearly named folders (01_banner, 02_pdp, 03_decoration, 00_source).
Workflow — 24 tool steps
  1. asset_search — Search Adobe Stock with the client's lifestyle-backdrop query for the secondary banner panel; returns low-res candidates + asset IDs.
  2. asset_license_and_download_stock — License the chosen candidate BEFORE editing and download the full-res presigned image; this becomes the secondary banner base.
  3. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize upload of the client's product hero shot into CC storage so it can be edited headlessly.
  4. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the hero upload; yields the stored hero asset URI used by the photo chain.
  5. image_auto_straighten — Auto-straighten the hero so the backdrop seam and product sit level before any crop/reframe.
  6. image_apply_auto_tone — Apply auto-tone to normalize exposure/contrast as the grading base for the whole package look.
  7. image_adjust_color_temperature — Warm the temperature toward the warm-sand brand feel; this is the master look every other asset gets matched to.
  8. image_crop_and_resize — Reframe the graded 4000x3000 hero to the wide 1920x720 desktop ratio, using the source's generous empty left third as the negative space (crop existing pixels,
  9. image_crop_and_resize — Crop/resize the reframed hero to an exact 1920x720 desktop banner master.
  10. image_crop_and_resize — Reframe the same graded hero to the tall 1080x1350 mobile ratio by cropping the existing frame vertically (crop existing pixels, no generation), reusing the mas
  11. image_crop_and_resize — Crop/resize the vertically reframed hero to exact 1080x1350 mobile banner master.
  12. image_adjust_color_temperature — Match the licensed stock backdrop's temperature to the hero master look so the secondary panel feels cohesive.
  13. image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation — Pull vibrance/saturation toward the balanced, restrained brand palette (bold-but-balanced) for the secondary banner.
  14. image_crop_and_resize — Crop the graded stock backdrop to the 1920x720 secondary banner panel with right-side negative space.
  15. image_apply_preset — Apply a Lightroom preset matching the hero grade to the supporting macro crops so PDP frames look shot together.
  16. image_crop_to_bounds — Crop each graded macro to clean 1:1 PDP image frames (1200x1200) with consistent margins.
  17. document_render_vector — Export the client-authored .ai icon/divider library to PNG/SVG (export only — does not draw the icons).
  18. image_vectorize — Vectorize one graded product-crop accent into a clean SVG decoration motif to extend the icon pack.
  19. image_fill_area — Select the negative-space region and solid-fill it with the brand paper hex (#FAF6EF) to produce a clean decoration background base (solid fill only).
  20. image_add_grain — Add subtle film grain to the solid decoration background for a tactile, modern non-flat finish tying sections together.
  21. document_render_layout — Export the client-authored layered banner .indd to PDF+PNG as the deliverable source preview (export only — the layout was authored by the client).
  22. asset_create_folders — Create labeled CC folders (00_source, 01_banner, 02_pdp, 03_decoration).
  23. asset_copy_assets — Copy every produced deliverable into its labeled folder for clean handoff.
  24. zip_package — Single unavoidable local step: zip the organized folders into one labeled deliverable archive.
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon and wide-mix: 24 steps, 18 distinct Adobe tools spanning four groups (asset/stock, raster photo, vector/document export, organization) plus one allowed local zip. It exercises a realistic agency pipeline — establish a master grade on the hero, propagate it to a stock asset and PDP macros for visual cohesion, fork the same graded hero into two honest crop-only reframes (desktop and mobile ratios), export two genuinely client-authored InDesign/Illustrator sources, vectorize an accent, and build a grain-textured solid decoration background — all chained. Difficulty comes from sequencing dependencies (license-before-edit, straighten/tone before cropping, grade before propagate), reusing inputs across branches, and staying strictly inside real capabilities: the reframe steps are image_crop_and_resize crops of the existing hero (edges cropped away to hit each ratio, no invented pixels), fill_area is solid-color only, and both authored templates are EXPORTED not composed. No not-available capability (generative expand, gen-fill, object removal, text-to-image, compositing, layout composition, upscaling) is used.

Client-supplied input assets: 6 total · 2 img · 0 video · 0 audio · 4 data

icon_divider_sheet.ai.proxy.txt
content
**Template Name:** `icon_divider_sheet.ai`

**Description:** This Illustrator source sheet functions as a self-contained library of thin-line, modern e-commerce graphic elements. It's designed for direct export, providing ready-to-use vector assets rather than content placeholders.

**Dimensions:** A tidy, well-spaced artboard optimized to present all elements with ample negative space, ensuring crisp rendering and easy selection of individual assets.

**Structure & Layers:**
*   The `.ai` file is meticulously layered, with each individual icon, divider rule, and corner flourish residing on its own distinct, clearly named layer. This facilitates precise selection and export of specific elements.

**Content & Placeholders:**
*   **Icons (8):** Truck (free-shipping), Shield (secure), Leaf (eco), Return Arrow, Gift, Headset (support), Ruler (specs), Star (rating).
*   **Dividers (3):** Minimalist section-divider rules.
*   **Accents (2):** Corner flourishes.
*   *Note:* All elements are final graphic assets; there are no text or image content placeholders.

**Brand Styling:**
*   **Aesthetics:** Modern, minimalist, and geometric.
*   **Stroke:** Consistent single-weight, thin uniform strokes.
*   **Color:** Deep-ink `#14233B`.
*   **Background:** Transparent.
*   **Rendering:** Designed for crisp vector output, ensuring scalability and clarity in both vector and downstream raster applications.

store_banner_layout.indd.proxy.txt
content
**Template File Description:** `store_banner_layout.indd`

**Type:** InDesign Desktop Hero Banner Layout Template

**Dimensions:** 1920px (width) x 720px (height)

**Structure & Layers:**
This template is structured with the following named layers, reflecting a clean, grid-based layout with generous margins:

*   **Layer: `logo`**
    *   **Content:** Placed vector logo lockup.
    *   **Position:** Top-left corner of the artboard.
*   **Layer: `headline`**
    *   **Content:** Empty text frame, styled for a bold sans-serif headline.
    *   **Position:** Left negative space, below the logo and above the CTA.
*   **Layer: `cta`**
    *   **Content:** Empty pill-shaped graphic frame, designated for a call-to-action button.
    *   **Position:** Left negative space, below the headline.
*   **Layer: `image-frame`**
    *   **Content:** Empty rectangular image frame, ready for product imagery.
    *   **Position:** Occupies the right side of the artboard.

**Brand Styling & Placeholders:**
*   **Color Palette:**
    *   Primary: Deep-ink (`#14233B`)
    *   Accent: Warm-sand (`#E7D3B1`) - used for CTA button frame.
*   **Typography:** Headline text frame styled with a bold sans-serif font.
*   **Layout:** Utilizes a clean grid system with generous margins for visual breathing room.
*   **Placeholders:**
    *   Headline text placeholder (text frame).
    *   CTA button text/graphic placeholder (pill-shaped frame).
    *   Product image placeholder (rectangular image frame).
    *   Logo is a fixed brand asset.

AO-74 — Crumb & Co. wholesale bakery catalog: prep 12 product photos + InDesign catalog data-merge to a print-ready B2B PDF and hi-res JPEG ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Grade and consistently crop twelve raw bakery product photos, vectorize the Crumb & Co. wordmark, upload the prepped assets, then run the client's authored InDesign catalog template through a data-merge against the wholesale roster CSV to produce a print-ready B2B PDF and a high-resolution JPEG.

💰 ~$120 est.Freelancer.com45 tool calls11 distinct toolseditlong-horizon
grounded in a real brief: Bakery B2B Catalog Development
Full client brief
Crumb & Co., a small-batch wholesale bakery, needs its B2B distributor catalog finished for print and PDF hand-out. The owner has already authored an InDesign catalog template (crumbco_catalog_template.indd) — an 8.5x11 portrait, multi-page grid layout with repeating product cells, each cell holding an image frame plus real InDesign data-merge fields (<<product_name>>, <<sku>>, <<description>>, <<case_pack>>, <<wholesale_price>>) and section headers for Breads, Pastries, Cakes & Slices, and Cookies & Bars, with a masthead frame for the wordmark and a full-bleed cover panel. The owner has also handed off the wholesale product roster as a CSV (12 SKUs across the four sections, each with a keyword-rich product description, case-pack quantity, and B2B wholesale price) and twelve raw product photos shot quickly on the bakery counter — sourdough boules, seeded rye, croissants, cinnamon rolls, a layer cake, brownies, chocolate-chip cookies, and more. The photos are unappetizing as-shot: slightly tilted, flat and dull, cool fluorescent white balance, and cluttered counter backgrounds. The job: (1) make every product photo look like a "high-quality image" — straighten the tilted frames, auto-tone to fix the flat exposure, warm the cool white balance toward a golden bakery look, push vibrance so crust and glaze read appetizing, apply a consistent bakery-warm preset across all twelve so they match, and crop every photo to the identical square (1:1) ratio the template's cells expect; (2) vectorize the flat Crumb & Co. wordmark PNG into a clean SVG so the masthead stays razor-sharp at print size; (3) upload all the prepped assets (twelve graded 1:1 photos, the vector wordmark) to Creative Cloud storage; (4) run the client's authored .indd through an InDesign data-merge against the roster CSV so every product cell binds its name, SKU, description, case pack, and wholesale price, with each graded photo dropping into its matching cell; (5) export the merged catalog to a print-ready ~300 DPI PDF for the print run and a high-resolution JPEG of the cover/spread for emailing to distributors. Deliverable dimensions: US Letter 8.5x11 portrait, print-ready CMYK-quality PDF plus a max-quality JPEG. Brand constraints: keep the warm/golden bakery palette consistent across every photo, the wordmark must stay crisp at masthead scale, and all descriptions/prices bind verbatim from the supplied roster CSV — do not rewrite the copy.
Deliverables (4)
  • CrumbCo_Wholesale_Catalog_print.pdf — Print-ready multi-page US Letter (8.5x11) portrait PDF exported from the data-merged InDesign catalog; every product cell bound from the roster CSV (name/SKU/description/case-pack/wholesale price) wit
  • CrumbCo_Wholesale_Catalog_cover.jpg — High-resolution JPEG of the bound catalog cover/first spread (US Letter portrait, max-quality) for emailing to B2B distributors.
  • graded_product_photos_x12 — Twelve color-graded, straightened, consistently 1:1-cropped bakery product photos matching the template cells, sharing a warm/golden bakery look.
  • crumbco_wordmark.svg — Clean vector SVG of the Crumb & Co. wordmark for crisp scaling in the catalog masthead.
Workflow — 45 tool steps
  1. image_auto_straighten — Level the tilted horizon on the sourdough boule shot (first of the 12-photo grade pipeline).
  2. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the straightened boule to fix the flat/dull counter exposure.
  3. image_adjust_color_temperature — Warm the cool fluorescent white balance toward the golden bakery look.
  4. image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation — Push vibrance so the crust and crumb read appetizing.
  5. image_apply_preset — Apply and lock a bakery-warm Lightroom preset — the SAME preset is reused across the other 11 photos for a consistent look.
  6. image_crop_and_resize — Crop/resize the boule to the 1:1 square the template's product cells expect (same target applied to all 12).
  7. image_apply_auto_tone — Run the seeded-rye photo through the grade pipeline: auto-tone to fix the flat exposure.
  8. image_apply_preset — Apply the locked bakery-warm preset so the rye matches the sourdough's white balance and tone.
  9. image_crop_and_resize — Crop the rye to the matching 1:1 template cell.
  10. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the honey whole-wheat loaf photo.
  11. image_apply_preset — Apply the locked bakery-warm preset to the wheat loaf for a consistent look.
  12. image_crop_and_resize — Crop the wheat loaf to the 1:1 template cell.
  13. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the butter croissant photo (Pastries section).
  14. image_apply_preset — Apply the locked bakery-warm preset to the croissant.
  15. image_crop_and_resize — Crop the croissant to the 1:1 template cell.
  16. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the glazed cinnamon roll photo.
  17. image_apply_preset — Apply the locked bakery-warm preset so the glaze reads warm and consistent.
  18. image_crop_and_resize — Crop the cinnamon roll to the 1:1 template cell.
  19. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the almond danish photo.
  20. image_apply_preset — Apply the locked bakery-warm preset to the danish.
  21. image_crop_and_resize — Crop the danish to the 1:1 template cell.
  22. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the vanilla layer-cake slice (Cakes & Slices section).
  23. image_apply_preset — Apply the locked bakery-warm preset to the layer cake.
  24. image_crop_and_resize — Crop the layer cake to the 1:1 template cell.
  25. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the fudge brownie photo.
  26. image_apply_preset — Apply the locked bakery-warm preset to the brownie.
  27. image_crop_and_resize — Crop the brownie to the 1:1 template cell.
  28. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the lemon bar photo.
  29. image_apply_preset — Apply the locked bakery-warm preset to the lemon bar.
  30. image_crop_and_resize — Crop the lemon bar to the 1:1 template cell.
  31. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the chocolate-chip cookie stack (Cookies & Bars section).
  32. image_apply_preset — Apply the locked bakery-warm preset to the chocolate-chip cookies.
  33. image_crop_and_resize — Crop the chocolate-chip cookies to the 1:1 template cell.
  34. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the oatmeal-raisin cookie photo.
  35. image_apply_preset — Apply the locked bakery-warm preset to the oatmeal-raisin cookie.
  36. image_crop_and_resize — Crop the oatmeal-raisin cookie to the 1:1 template cell.
  37. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the salted-caramel blondie photo (final of the 12).
  38. image_apply_preset — Apply the locked bakery-warm preset to the blondie.
  39. image_crop_and_resize — Crop the blondie to the 1:1 template cell so all 12 photos match.
  40. image_vectorize — Vectorize the flat Crumb & Co. wordmark PNG into a clean SVG for razor-sharp masthead scaling.
  41. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize an upload session for all 12 graded 1:1 photos plus the vector wordmark.
  42. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the upload; returns asset URNs the InDesign template/data-merge will reference for each product cell and the masthead.
  43. document_merge_data_layout — InDesign data-merge: bind each roster row (product_name/sku/description/case_pack/wholesale_price) into the authored .indd's repeating cells (which carry real <
  44. document_render_layout — Export the merged multi-page catalog to a print-ready ~300 DPI US Letter PDF deliverable.
  45. document_render_layout — Export the cover/first spread of the same merged document to a high-resolution max-quality JPEG deliverable for distributor emails.
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon flagship: 45 connector calls, 11 distinct Adobe tools across 3 groups (image_* raster grading, asset_* storage, document_* InDesign merge/export) — far above the >=10 calls / >=6 distinct-tool floor. Difficulty comes from running twelve separate product photos through a consistent multi-step grade-and-crop pipeline where a single locked preset is threaded as a real dependency into all eleven follow-on sub-chains, vectorizing the wordmark, uploading thirteen prepped assets, then converging everything into a genuine InDesign [T] data-merge that binds both copy (name/SKU/description/case-pack/price) AND the matching photo per row, followed by dual print/JPEG export. It respects every hard limit: no generative fill / generative-expand / object-removal / text-to-image / from-scratch layout / upscaling; image_fill_area is unused; image_remove_background is unused; the catalog layout is NOT composed by the connector — document_merge_data_layout binds and document_render_layout only EXPORTS the client's authored .indd, which is listed as a supplied authored input; all descriptions/prices bind verbatim from the supplied roster CSV per the brief.

Client-supplied input assets: 4 total · 1 img · 0 video · 0 audio · 3 data

crumbco_catalog_template.indd.proxy.txt
content
**File:** `crumbco_catalog_template.indd` (Adobe InDesign Document)

**Purpose:** Multi-page wholesale product catalog for Crumb & Co. bakery, designed for data merge automation.

**Dimensions & Orientation:** US Letter (8.5" x 11"), portrait orientation. Document includes a 0.125" bleed and standard half-inch margins.

**Structure & Placeholders:**
*   **Cover Page:** Features a full-bleed panel for hero imagery, with a dedicated masthead text frame positioned for the "Crumb & Co." wordmark.
*   **Master Pages:** Configured for repeating product-cell grids (typically 2x3 or 3x4 cells per spread), ensuring consistent layout.
*   **Product Cell (Repeating Unit):** Each cell contains:
    *   **Image Frame:** A 1:1 (square) aspect ratio frame, mapped for a data merge image column (e.g., `@Product_Image.jpg`).
    *   **Data Merge Text Fields:**
        *   `<<product_name>>`: Prominent, bold heading for the product title.
        *   `<<sku>>`: Smaller text, typically italicized, for the stock keeping unit.
        *   `<<description>>`: Body text frame for product details.
        *   `<<case_pack>>`: Concise text frame for packaging information.
        *   `<<wholesale_price>>`: Large, bold text frame for pricing.
*   **Section Headers:** Dedicated, styled text frames for content organization: "Breads", "Pastries", "Cakes & Slices", and "Cookies & Bars", strategically placed on master pages or as overrideable elements.
*   **Layers:** Organized into logical groups (e.g., "Background Graphics", "Images", "Product Text", "Master Page Elements") for ease of editing.

**Brand Styling (Crumb & Co.):**
*   **Typography:** Employs a clean, legible sans-serif for body copy and data fields (e.g., Montserrat or Lato), complemented by a slightly more decorative serif or script for section headers and the masthead, conveying a crafted, artisanal feel.
*   **Color Palette:** Predominantly warm, earthy tones (creams, light browns, charcoal) with a subtle accent color (e.g., a muted berry or sage green) to reflect a natural, inviting bakery aesthetic.
*   **Overall:** Professional, organized layout with ample white space, prioritizing clear product presentation and ease of readability.

**Technical Notes:** All specified `<<data_merge_fields>>` are correctly defined and bound within the InDesign Data Merge panel, ready for dynamic content population from a CSV/TXT data so

AO-75 — Northgate Dental Congress 2027: delegate name-badge data-merge from roster CSV into authored InDesign template, print PDF + per-badge proof ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Take our approved event wordmark, delegate roster CSV (~1,000 rows) and authored InDesign badge template, prep and vectorize the wordmark plus the four role QR marks, then run an InDesign data-merge to bind every delegate into a personalised name badge and export a print-ready imposed PDF plus a high-res proof.

💰 $30Upwork20 tool calls11 distinct toolseditlong-horizon
grounded in a real brief: Logo Designer — BDSA Conference Newcastle 2027
Full client brief
Northgate Dental Students' Congress 2027 is a two-day event bringing roughly 1,000 delegates from dental schools across the country together for speakers, workshops, a gala dinner and socials. Our brand identity is already signed off, and we now need the physical delegate name badges produced from our attendee list — one personalised badge per delegate. We have handed off everything the job needs: our approved event wordmark as a flat high-contrast PNG (northgate_wordmark.png); an authored InDesign badge template (badge_template.indd) laid out at 90x130mm portrait with 3mm bleed, containing real InDesign Data Merge fields (<<FullName>>, <<School>>, <<Role>>, <<QRPath>>), a placeholder frame for the vectorized wordmark, a full-width role colour-band frame, and a QR image frame; our delegate roster export (delegate_roster.csv) with one row per delegate holding full name, school, role (Delegate / Speaker / Committee / Press) and the matching role-QR filename; and four flat role-band QR marks as PNGs (one per role, each a solid-colour block with the check-in QR). The wordmark PNG is slightly oversized and off-centre for the badge frame and needs cleaning and vectorizing so it stays razor-sharp at print size; the four QR PNGs are inconsistent sizes and must be squared and cropped to the badge's QR frame. The job: (1) crop/clean the wordmark and square-crop the four role QRs to the exact frame ratios the template expects; (2) vectorize the flat wordmark into a clean SVG so it prints crisp on ~1,000 badges; (3) upload all prepped assets to our workspace; (4) run the InDesign data-merge so every roster row binds its name, school, role band and role QR into the authored template — one badge record per delegate; (5) export the merged document to a print-ready imposed PDF (90x130mm + bleed, ~300 DPI, crop marks) for the lanyard printer, plus a high-resolution JPEG proof of the first sheet for our committee to sign off; and (6) gather the produced assets into a review folder deep-link so the committee can approve the badge treatment in one place. Roles, names and schools all come from the roster as-is — nothing is invented; long names must bind into the template's auto-fit name frame without clipping.
Deliverables (5)
  • Northgate_Badges_print_imposed.pdf — Print-ready PDF imposed from the data-merged InDesign document, one 90x130mm portrait badge record per roster row (~1,000 badges), 3mm bleed, crop marks, ~300 DPI; each badge carries the bound FullNam
  • Northgate_Badge_proof.jpg — High-resolution JPEG proof of the first badge sheet from the merged document (max quality) for committee sign-off before the full run prints.
  • northgate_wordmark.svg — Clean vector SVG of the approved event wordmark produced by image_vectorize from the cropped wordmark PNG, placed in the badge template so the mark stays razor-sharp across all ~1,000 printed badges.
  • role_qr_prepped_x4 — Four role-band QR PNGs (delegate/speaker/committee/press) squared and cropped to the badge's QR frame ratio so each binds cleanly via the roster's QRPath image field.
  • Northgate_Badge_review_folder — Review folder deep-link assembling the vectorized wordmark, the four prepped role QRs and the badge proof JPEG (copied into a shared /review folder) for one-click committee approval of the badge treat
Workflow — 20 tool steps
  1. asset_create_folders — Stand up the CC asset workspace (e.g. /L02/templates, /qr, /prepped, /merged, /review) to hold the authored template, roster, QRs, prepped art and outputs.
  2. image_crop_to_bounds — Trim the oversized/off-centre approved wordmark PNG to its tight content bounds so it seats correctly in the badge's wordmark frame.
  3. image_crop_and_resize — Crop/resize the trimmed wordmark to the exact aspect the template's top wordmark frame expects.
  4. image_vectorize — Vectorize the flat approved wordmark into a clean SVG so the mark stays razor-sharp across all ~1,000 printed badges.
  5. image_crop_to_bounds — Trim the non-square Delegate role QR to its colour-block bounds.
  6. image_crop_and_resize — Square-crop/resize the Delegate QR to the badge's square QR frame ratio so it binds cleanly.
  7. image_crop_to_bounds — Trim the non-square Speaker role QR to its colour-block bounds.
  8. image_crop_and_resize — Square-crop/resize the Speaker QR to the matching QR frame ratio so all four role QRs are consistent.
  9. image_crop_to_bounds — Trim the non-square Committee role QR to its colour-block bounds.
  10. image_crop_and_resize — Square-crop/resize the Committee QR to the matching QR frame ratio.
  11. image_crop_to_bounds — Trim the non-square Press role QR to its colour-block bounds.
  12. image_crop_and_resize — Square-crop/resize the Press QR to the matching QR frame ratio so all four QRs bind identically via the roster QRPath field.
  13. font_recommend — Recommend an Adobe Fonts pairing that matches the approved wordmark for the badge name/role type (informs the authored template's fonts; returned as a note).
  14. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize an upload session into the workspace for all prepped merge assets (vector wordmark + four squared role QRs) so the data-merge image field can referen
  15. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the uploads so the vector wordmark and four role QRs are real CC assets with URNs the authored .indd / data-merge will reference by QRPath.
  16. document_merge_data_layout — [T] InDesign Data Merge: bind every delegate_roster.csv row into the client's authored badge_template.indd (real <<FullName>>/<<School>>/<<Role>> fields + <<QRP
  17. document_render_layout — Export the merged InDesign document to a print-ready imposed PDF (90x130mm + 3mm bleed, crop marks, ~300 DPI) — one badge record per roster row for the lanyard
  18. document_render_layout — Export the first badge sheet of the same merged document to a high-resolution JPEG proof for committee sign-off.
  19. asset_inline_preview — Generate an inline preview of the proof so the committee can eyeball the first badge (name binding, role band, QR, wordmark) before the full run.
  20. asset_copy_assets — Copy the vectorized wordmark, the four prepped role QRs and the badge proof into the shared /L02/review folder as a single deep-link so the committee can approv
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon variable-data flagship: 20 connector calls, 11 distinct Adobe tools across three groups (image_* raster prep, asset_* storage/preview/copy, document_* InDesign merge/render) plus font_recommend — above the >=10 calls / >=6 distinct-tool floor. Difficulty comes from normalising five inconsistent supplied raster assets (one wordmark + four differently-sized role QRs) each through its own crop sub-chain, vectorizing the wordmark, then converging all of them into a genuine InDesign [T] data-merge that binds ~1,000 roster rows — one personalised badge per delegate — with a role-mapped image field (QRPath) and an auto-fit name frame for long names, followed by dual export (imposed print PDF + proof JPEG) and a review-folder deep-link. It respects every hard limit: no from-scratch logo/letterform design (the wordmark is a supplied approved input, only cropped + vectorized); no generative fill/expand, no object-removal, no text-to-image, no upscaling, no compositing; image_fill_area is unused; image_remove_background is unused; the layout is NOT composed by the connector — document_merge_data_layout binds and document_render_layout only EXPORTS the client's authored .indd, which is a supplied input; the roster is honored as-is per the brief.

Client-supplied input assets: 7 total · 5 img · 0 video · 0 audio · 2 data

badge_template.indd.proxy.txt
content
**File:** `badge_template.indd`

**Purpose:** Adobe InDesign master template for a single 90x130mm portrait conference delegate name badge, ready for data merge.

**Dimensions:** 90mm (W) x 130mm (H) portrait, with 3mm bleed on all sides.

**Structure & Layers:**
1.  **Top:** Vectorized event wordmark image frame.
2.  **Mid-Upper:** `<<FullName>>` text frame.
3.  **Mid-Lower:** `<<School>>` text frame.
4.  **Lower:** `<<Role>>` text frame.
5.  **Behind <<Role>>:** Full-width rectangular frame for a role-specific color band.
6.  **Lower Right:** Square image frame for QR code.

**Placeholders (Data Merge Fields):**
*   **Text Fields:**
    *   `<<FullName>>`: Configured with Auto-Size 'shrink text to fit' to prevent overset text for long names.
    *   `<<School>>`
    *   `<<Role>>`
*   **Image Field:**
    *   `<<QRPath>>`: Populates the lower-right QR code image frame, wired to a data source column containing image file paths (e.g., `qr_john_doe.png`).

**Brand Styling & Functionality:**
*   Distinct visual separation for delegate role via a full-width color band.
*   Professional event branding via a dedicated wordmark placement.
*   Dynamic, personalized QR codes for each delegate.
*   Robust text handling for `<<FullName>>` ensures legibility regardless of name length.
*   Authored as a genuine InDesign document with live data merge functionality, ensuring proper binding of text and image fields from a CSV data source.

AO-76 — Northlantic Insurance auto-insurance rate card: InDesign data-merge a supplied premium-tier CSV into the client's authored .indd, refresh the disclosure footer, and export a print-ready PDF + high-res JPEG ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Run the client's authored InDesign rate-card template through an InDesign data-merge against a supplied auto-insurance premium-tier CSV, place the on-brand logo and licensed background texture, refresh the compliance disclosure footer, and export a press-ready PDF plus a high-resolution JPEG.

💰 ~$120 est.Freelancer.com15 tool calls11 distinct toolsedit
grounded in a real brief: Fast-Food Menu Illustrator Design
Full client brief
Northlantic Insurance Group, a regional personal-lines insurance broker, needs its quarterly Personal Auto rate card finished for print and for the branch counters. Our design lead has already built and handed off the authored InDesign template (auto_rate_card.indd) — it is fully brand-locked to our style guide (Northlantic Slate #1F2A44 and Signal Amber #F4A82C, headline face Basis Grotesque, body face Source Serif) and carries a single-page A4 portrait rate table with REAL InDesign data-merge fields (<<coverage_tier>>, <<coverage_desc>>, <<annual_premium>>, <<monthly_premium>>, <<deductible>>) laid out across four coverage bands (Liability Only, Standard, Standard Plus, Full Coverage), a logo placement frame, a full-bleed background frame, and a boxed disclosure/footer frame. We are NOT asking you to redesign the layout — the layout is locked. What we need is the production pass: (1) bind our supplied Q3 premium data (auto_premium_tiers.csv) into the template via a proper InDesign data merge so every tier, description, annual/monthly premium and deductible lands in the right cell — accuracy is critical, this is regulated pricing and one wrong number is a reprint; (2) place our brand wordmark crisply — vectorize the flat wordmark PNG we send so it prints razor-sharp at large size, and drop it in the logo frame; (3) back the card with a subtle premium paper/linen texture — license one from Adobe Stock (nothing loud, it must sit behind the table without hurting legibility) and set it as the background; (4) our compliance line changed this quarter — the small disclosure footer PNG we supply (new state-filing language + effective date) must be placed in the footer frame so the printed card carries the current legal text; (5) export the finished, merged document as a press-ready ~300 DPI A4 PDF for the printer AND a high-resolution JPEG for the intranet and the tablet quoting tool; (6) drop the final assets onto a review board so our compliance officer and marketing lead can sign off on the numbers and the disclosure in one link before it goes to press. Keep everything strictly on-brand and do not alter any premium value — bind exactly what the CSV says.
Deliverables (5)
  • Northlantic_Auto_RateCard_print.pdf — Press-ready single-page A4 portrait PDF exported from the data-merged InDesign document; all premium tiers/descriptions/annual+monthly premiums/deductibles bound from auto_premium_tiers.csv, vectorize
  • Northlantic_Auto_RateCard_highres.jpg — High-resolution JPEG of the same bound one-page rate card (A4 portrait, max-quality) for the intranet and the branch tablet quoting tool.
  • northlantic_wordmark.svg — Clean vector SVG of the Northlantic wordmark for crisp scaling at large print size, placed in the template's logo frame.
  • northlantic_linen_texture_fullres — The licensed, full-resolution Adobe Stock cream/linen paper texture used as the rate card's full-bleed background.
  • Northlantic_RateCard_review_board — Firefly board deep-link assembling the vectorized wordmark, the placed disclosure footer, and the final merged rate-card render for one-click compliance + marketing sign-off before print.
Workflow — 15 tool steps
  1. asset_search — Search Adobe Stock for a subtle cream/off-white linen paper texture using the marketing lead's art-direction search terms (low-res candidates).
  2. asset_license_and_download_stock — License and download the chosen linen texture at full-res (license BEFORE use); becomes the rate card's full-bleed background asset.
  3. asset_inline_preview — Inline-preview the licensed texture to confirm it is subtle enough to sit behind the rate table without hurting legibility (the brief's legibility constraint).
  4. image_vectorize — Vectorize the flat Northlantic wordmark PNG into a clean SVG so it prints razor-sharp at large size in the logo frame.
  5. image_crop_to_bounds — Crop the supplied disclosure-footer PNG to its content bounds so it drops cleanly into the template's boxed footer frame with no stray margin.
  6. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize an upload session for all prepped placement assets (vector wordmark, trimmed disclosure footer, licensed linen texture).
  7. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the upload; returns asset URNs the InDesign template/data-merge will reference for the logo, footer and background frames.
  8. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize a second upload session for the client's authored .indd template and the Q3 premium-tier CSV so the merge engine can reach them.
  9. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the template+CSV upload; returns the URNs the data-merge step consumes.
  10. asset_create_folders — Create a Northlantic rate-card project folder in CC storage and organize the uploaded template, CSV and placement assets so the merge references a clean structu
  11. document_merge_data_layout — InDesign data-merge: bind the coverage_tier/coverage_desc/annual_premium/monthly_premium/deductible rows into the client's authored .indd (which carries real <<
  12. document_render_layout — Export the merged InDesign document to a press-ready ~300 DPI A4 portrait PDF with bleed — the printer deliverable.
  13. document_render_layout — Export the same merged document to a high-resolution max-quality JPEG for the intranet and the branch tablet quoting tool.
  14. asset_inline_preview — Inline-preview the exported print PDF as a proof so the bound premiums, placed logo, texture and disclosure can be eyeballed before sign-off (accuracy check per
  15. create_firefly_board — Assemble the vectorized wordmark, the placed disclosure footer, and the final merged rate-card render onto a Firefly board deep-link for compliance + marketing
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon data-merge production task: 15 connector calls across 4 tool groups (asset_* stock/storage/preview, image_* raster prep, document_* InDesign merge+export, create_firefly_board), 11 distinct Adobe tools — above the >=10 calls / >=6 distinct-tool floor. Difficulty comes from correctly staging a genuine InDesign [T] data-merge: sourcing+licensing a legibility-safe background, vectorizing a logo for print, trimming a supplied compliance artwork, running TWO separate upload/finalize sessions (placement assets vs template+data) and organizing them into a project folder before binding a real authored .indd against a regulated premium CSV, then dual-exporting press PDF + high-res JPEG and proofing the result. It respects every hard limit: the layout is NOT composed by the connector — document_merge_data_layout binds and document_render_layout only EXPORTS the client's authored .indd, which is a supplied input; no generative fill / outpaint / text-to-image / from-scratch art / upscaling / object-removal; image_fill_area is unused; the disclosure text is placed as a supplied image (no text authoring claimed); premium values are bound verbatim from the CSV per the brief.

Client-supplied input assets: 5 total · 2 img · 0 video · 0 audio · 3 data

gemini

auto_rate_card.indd.proxy.txt
content
**Template Description: `auto_rate_card.indd`**

This is a single-page A4 (210mm x 297mm) portrait Adobe InDesign document, serving as a Northlantic Insurance Personal Auto rate card. It is fully brand-locked to Northlantic's style guide.

**Brand Styling:**
*   **Colors:** Northlantic Slate (#1F2A44), Signal Amber (#F4A82C).
*   **Typography:** Headlines use Basis Grotesque, body text uses Source Serif.

**Structure & Layers:**
1.  **Background Layer:** Contains a full-bleed graphic frame intended for a textured background image.
2.  **Header Layer:** Includes a dedicated placement frame for the Northlantic Insurance logo at the top.
3.  **Content Layer:**
    *   Features four distinct, styled coverage-band headers: "Liability Only", "Standard", "Standard Plus", and "Full Coverage".
    *   Under each header, content is structured with repeating rows utilizing genuine Adobe InDesign data-merge placeholder fields.
    *   **Data-Merge Fields:**
        *   `<<coverage_tier>>`
        *   `<<coverage_desc>>`
        *   `<<annual_premium>>`
        *   `<<monthly_premium>>`
        *   `<<deductible>>`
    *   These fields are meticulously arranged to bind correctly with external data sources for dynamic content generation.
4.  **Footer Layer:** A clearly defined, boxed frame at the bottom contains placeholder text for legal disclosures and general footer information.

This template is an authentically authored InDesign file, ensuring the data-merge fields are live and functional for direct binding, rather than being flattened text.
northlantic_paper_linen_reference.txt
gemini/gemini-2.5-flash
content
**Art Direction Note: Background Texture for Rate Tables**

For all rate tables across our Northlantic materials, we require a very subtle off-white premium paper / linen texture as the background. This texture must be low contrast and nothing loud; its primary function is to add a touch of sophistication without ever reducing the legibility of the overlaid text. Please think along the lines of high-quality cream stationery with a faint fiber grain—absolutely no distracting pattern motifs or overt designs. The goal is a clean, understated elegance that enhances, not competes with, the content. When searching for options on Adobe Stock, please use terms like: 'subtle cream linen paper texture', 'off-white fine paper grain'.

AO-77 — Willowfen Press KDP picture-book preflight: audit supplied interior + cover .indd/PDF against KDP print specs and emit a marked-up preflight report ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Audit Willowfen Press's supplied 32-page KDP picture-book interior .indd, its exported interior PDF, and the wraparound cover PDF against the KDP spec sheet — checking trim, bleed, margins, spine width, image resolution and caption overset — and emit a marked-up preflight report plus proof renders that says whether the files will pass KDP preflight.

💰 $200Upwork13 tool calls8 distinct toolsanalyze
grounded in a real brief: KDP Formatter Needed for 32-Page Children's Picture Book — Paperback
Full client brief
Willowfen Press is a small independent children's-book publisher about to upload its next paperback, "Pip & the Paper Moon", to Amazon KDP. Our illustrator and layout designer have already built the files, but our last title bounced twice in KDP's preflight (spine width off, one illustration under-resolution, a caption clipped), so before we upload again we want an independent print-QA pass. We are handing off four things: (1) the authored InDesign interior template (pip_interior_template.indd) — a 32-page 8.5x11 in trim book with real InDesign data-merge caption fields (<<page_no>>, <<caption>>, <<alt_note>>) placed on each illustrated spread; (2) the exported interior PDF our designer produced from it (pip_interior_export.pdf, 32 pages); (3) the wraparound paperback cover our designer built to the KDP cover template (pip_cover_wrap.pdf — front + spine + back on one sheet); and (4) the KDP spec sheet we were given (kdp_spec_sheet.pdf) that states the required trim (8.5x11 in), bleed (0.125 in on outside edges), interior safe margin (0.375 in), minimum image resolution (300 DPI), and the spine-width formula for a 32-page white-paper paperback (~0.0725 in). We also include the filled caption data (pip_captions.csv) that maps to the interior template's merge fields. What we need from you: go through the files and tell us, in writing, whether they will pass KDP preflight the first time. Specifically: pull a proof render of the interior template and of the exported interior PDF so we can compare them page for page; inline-preview a representative illustrated spread and the full cover sheet so bleed, safe-margin and spine placement can be eyeballed; read the KDP spec sheet and pull out the exact required numbers; and re-run the caption data-merge into the authored interior template so any long caption that would overset or clip in a text frame is caught (that was one of last time's failures). Then write up a clean, itemized preflight report — one line per check (trim, bleed, margins, spine width, image resolution, caption overset, font embedding, page count) with a PASS / FLAG verdict and a short note, ending with an overall go / no-go and a punch-list of what to fix before upload. Deliver the written report plus the proof renders you pulled. Do NOT redesign or re-lay-out anything — this is a review, not a rebuild; every caption and price stays as supplied.
Deliverables (5)
  • Pip_Preflight_Report.md — The primary deliverable: an itemized KDP preflight findings report (recipe note) — one row per check (trim size, bleed, interior/gutter margins, spine width, image resolution, caption overset/clipping
  • interior_template_proof.pdf — Proof render exported from the authored interior .indd, used as visual evidence in the audit (page-for-page reference against the designer's export).
  • interior_export_proof.pdf — Proof render/normalized copy of the designer's exported interior PDF, annotated in the report where it drifts from the template proof (e.g. missing bleed, downsampled image).
  • spread_and_cover_preview_frames — Inline-preview frames of a representative illustrated interior spread and of the full wraparound cover sheet, captured as the visual basis for the bleed / safe-margin / spine-placement findings.
  • caption_merge_check.pdf — Proof of the caption CSV merged into the authored interior template, rendered so any overset/clipped caption is visible — the evidence for the caption-overset line in the report. Diagnostic only; not
Workflow — 13 tool steps
  1. asset_create_folders — Stand up a CC asset workspace (e.g. /L04/handoff, /L04/proofs, /L04/report) to hold the client-supplied files and the proof renders the audit produces.
  2. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize an upload session for the five client-supplied files (pip_interior_template.indd, pip_interior_export.pdf, pip_cover_wrap.pdf, pip_captions.csv, kdp_
  3. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the uploads so all five hand-off files are real CC assets with URNs the inspection steps can reference.
  4. media_summarize — Read/summarize kdp_spec_sheet.pdf to extract the exact required numbers the audit measures against: trim 8.5x11 in, bleed 0.125 in, safe margin 0.375 in, gutter
  5. document_render_layout — Render the authored interior .indd to a proof PDF (EXPORT-only) so the audit has a page-for-page reference of what the template is supposed to produce.
  6. document_convert_pdf — Normalize/re-render the designer's exported interior PDF into a comparable proof so it can be inspected as the artifact KDP will actually preflight (page count,
  7. asset_inline_preview — Inline-preview a representative illustrated interior spread from the template proof to eyeball full-bleed illustration coverage and the 0.375 in caption/text sa
  8. asset_inline_preview — Inline-preview the full wraparound cover sheet (pip_cover_wrap.pdf) to eyeball bleed on all edges and, critically, whether the spine text sits inside the narrow
  9. asset_inline_preview — Inline-preview the same page from the designer's exported interior PDF to compare it against the template proof and catch export drift (bleed that did not carry
  10. document_merge_data_layout — [T] Re-run the caption data-merge: bind pip_captions.csv into the client-authored interior .indd (real <<page_no>>/<<caption>>/<<alt_note>> fields) so any long
  11. document_render_layout — Render the re-merged interior to a proof PDF so the overset/clipped caption is visible on the page — the evidence for the caption-overset finding. EXPORT-only.
  12. asset_inline_preview — Inline-preview the page carrying the deliberately long caption to confirm and capture the overset/clip visually. INSPECT only.
  13. local_write_recipe_note — Emit the itemized KDP preflight report: one line per check (trim, bleed, margins, spine width, image resolution, caption overset, font embedding, page count) wi
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon analyze/QA task: 13 connector calls, 8 distinct Adobe tools across three groups (asset_* workspace/upload/preview, document_* render/convert/merge, media_summarize), above the >=6 calls / >=6 distinct-tool analyze floor. Difficulty comes from the review being genuinely multi-artifact: the freelancer must inspect THREE different representations of the same book (authored .indd, designer's exported PDF, wraparound cover) and cross-check each against numeric targets extracted from a fourth file (the spec sheet), then re-run a real [T] data-merge purely as a diagnostic to reproduce an overset caption. It respects every hard limit: nothing is composed or redesigned — document_render_layout/document_convert_pdf only EXPORT/normalize supplied artifacts, document_merge_data_layout is correctly [T] against the client's authored .indd (a supplied input carrying real merge fields), and all preview/summarize steps are read-only inspection. No generative fill / expand, no object-removal, no text-to-image, no upscaling, no from-scratch layout. The deliverable is a findings report plus proof renders (the analyze output form), emitted via the single tolerated trailing [L] local_write_recipe_note.

Client-supplied input assets: 8 total · 0 img · 0 video · 0 audio · 8 data

Data & other (8)

kdp_spec_sheet.pdf.txt
content
# KDP Paperback Print Spec Sheet - Pip & the Paper Moon

This document summarizes the key print specifications required for the KDP paperback edition of *Pip & the Paper Moon*.

| Parameter | Requirement |
|---------------------------|-------------------------------|
| Trim size | 8.5 x 11 in |
| Bleed | 0.125 in on all outside edges |
| Interior safe margin | 0.375 in from trim |
| Gutter / inside margin | 0.5 in (for 24-150 pages) |
| Minimum image resolution  | 300 DPI |
| Paper | White |
| Page count | 32 |
| Spine width formula | page_count x 0.002252 in |
| Spine width for 32 pages  | ~0.0725 in |
| Fonts | All fonts embedded |
| Transparency | Flatten all transparency |
| PDF standard | PDF/X compatible (PDF/X-1a, X-3 or X-4) |

Files failing any of these parameters will be rejected by KDP preflight.
pip_cover_wrap.pdf.txt
content
# KDP Print-Ready Cover - Pip & the Paper Moon

## Document Details
**File Name:** pip_cover_wrap.pdf
**Asset Type:** Wraparound Paperback Cover (Single Sheet)
**Book Format:** 8.5" x 11" (US Letter) White Paper, 32 Pages
**Spine Width (as designed for this file):** 0.0725 inches

## Final Print Dimensions (including 0.125" bleed all around)
**Overall Width:** 17.3225 inches (8.5" Back Panel + 0.0725" Spine + 8.5" Front Panel + 0.125" Left Bleed + 0.125" Right Bleed)
**Overall Height:** 11.25 inches (11" Trim Height + 0.125" Top Bleed + 0.125" Bottom Bleed)

## Trim Line Dimensions
**Trim Width:** 17.0725 inches
**Trim Height:** 11 inches

## Layout Structure (Left-to-Right: Back Panel | Spine | Front Panel)

---

### Back Panel Content
**Trim Area:** 8.5" Wide x 11" High
**Bleed:** Background elements extend 0.125" beyond trim on left, top, bottom, and into the spine area.
**Key Elements:**
*   [Placeholder for Author Biography or Book Blurb]
*   [Standard KDP ISBN Barcode Box - positioned in the lower right corner of the back panel within the safe zone]
*   [Willowfen Press logo - positioned in the lower left corner of the back panel within the safe zone]
*   Background illustration continues seamlessly from the spine and front panel.

---

### Spine Content
**Trim Area:** 0.0725" Wide x 11" High
**Bleed:** Background elements extend 0.125" beyond trim on top and bottom, and into adjacent panels.
**Key Elements:**
*   **Title:** PIP & THE PAPER MOON
    *   **Orientation:** Vertical, reading top-to-bottom.
    *   **Font:** [Bold Sans-Serif, e.g., 'Helvetica Neue Bold', White text color]
    *   **Positioning Defect:** The spine text for "PIP & THE PAPER MOON" is slightly misaligned. Characters extend marginally beyond the intended 0.0725" spine boundary, infringing upon the live area of the adjacent front panel. This placement causes the text to sit a hair outside the designated spine safe zone, which is a common reason for KDP preflight rejection.

---

### Front Panel Content
**Trim Area:** 8.5" Wide x 11" High
**Bleed:** All background and design elements extend 0.125" beyond trim on right, top, bottom, and into the spine area.
**Key Elements:**
*   **Main Title:** Pip & the Paper Moon
    *   **Font:** [Decorative Script, e.g., 'Scriptina', Soft Gold text color]
    *   **Position:** Horizontally centered, positioned in the upper-middle section of the

Note: (a) page 17 illustration downsampled to ~180 DPI (below 300 DPI minimum); (b) page 24 caption oversets/clips its caption frame.

Page 1
[full-bleed illustration: Pip, a small mouse, sleeps soundly in a cozy bed.]
Caption: Pip loved to dream.
(alt-text: Pip sleeping peacefully in a small bed)

Page 2

Caption: One day, a special idea sparked.

Page 3

Page 6

Caption: It glowed softly in the room.
(alt-text: Paper moon glowing softly near window)

Page 7
[full-bleed illustration: Pip sits cross-legged, whispering to the hanging paper moon.]
Caption: Pip whispered secrets to it.
(alt-text: Pip whispering to paper moon)

Page 8
[full-bleed illustration: The paper moon illustration with a subtle knowing face.]
Caption: The moon seemed to listen.
(alt-text: Paper moon with a gentle face)

Page 9
[full-bleed illustration: Pip peeks from behind a curtain, the moon behind a chair.]

(alt-text: Pip hiding behind curtain, moon behind chair)

Page 10
[full-bleed illustration: Pip holding the moon, floating upwards in the room.]
Caption: Up, up, up they went!
(alt-text: Pip and moon floating upward)

Page 11
[full-bleed illustration: Pip and the moon soar past shelves of colorful books.]
Caption: Flying past tall bookshelves.
(alt-text: Pip and moon flying by bookshelves)

Page 12
[full-bleed illustration: Pip and the moon gently float above a napping cat.]
Caption: Over the sleepy cat.
(alt-text: Pip and moon floating over sleeping cat)

Page 13
[full-bleed illustration: Pip

page_no,caption,alt_note
1,Pip loved to dream.,"Pip, a small mouse, sleeps soundly in a cozy bed."
2,"One day, a special idea sparked.","Pip, now awake, has a thought bubble with a crescent moon."
