17,Pip searched anxiously.,"Pip looks around frantically, hands cupped around mouth."
18,Where was the paper moon?,"Pip looks worried, a single tear rolling down his cheek."
19,A glimmer in the distance.,A faint glow on the horizon catches Pip's eye.
20,"There it was, caught in a tree!",The paper moon is gently snagged on a branch of a tall tree.
21,Pip climbed and climbed.,"Pip carefully scales the tree trunk, reaching for the moon."
22,Almost there!,"Pip stretches a paw out, just fingertips away from the moon."
23,Success! A happy reunion.,"Pip hugs the paper moon tightly, both smiling."
24,"Together again, they floated home, feeling quite adventurous and ready for a warm cup of cocoa and some well-deserved rest after their incredible journey through the starlit sky.","Pip and the moon descend gently, looking content and sleepy."
25,Back in Pip's cozy room.,"Pip and the moon are back in Pip's room, safe and sound."

pip_interior_template.indd.proxy.txt
content
**File Name:** `pip_interior_template.indd`

**Description:**
This is an Adobe InDesign template for the 32-page interior of the children's picture book 'Pip & the Paper Moon'. The document is configured for US Letter (8.5x11 inches) portrait orientation with a standard facing-pages spread layout.

**Structure & Layers:**
Each of the 32 pages features a full-bleed illustration frame, designed to cover the entire page area. Overlaying these illustration frames on every page is a consistently placed, dedicated text frame for captions.

**Placeholders & Data Merge:**
The caption text frames are pre-populated with live, functional InDesign data-merge fields:
*   `<<page_no>>`: For dynamic page numbering.
*   `<<caption>>`: For the primary illustration caption text.
*   `<<alt_note>>`: For supplementary descriptive or alt-text information.
These are genuine InDesign merge fields, ready to bind with an external data source for automated content population.

**Dimensions:**
*   Page Size: 8.5 x 11 inches (US Letter)
*   Total Pages: 32

**Brand Styling:**
The template utilizes a clean, legible aesthetic suitable for a children's picture book. Caption text is styled with a simple, clear sans-serif font (e.g., 'Open Sans Regular' or similar) at a readable point size (e.g., 10pt-12pt), applied via a dedicated paragraph style (e.g., 'Caption_Body'). No complex graphic styles or brand-specific elements beyond standard typography.

**Embedded Defects (Deliberate for Audit):**
1.  **Text Overset:** One specific caption text frame is intentionally undersized, causing a long merged `<caption>` field to display with overset text.
2.  **Low Resolution Image:** One of the pre-placed illustrations within its full-bleed frame is below the recommended 300 DPI resolution for print production.

AO-78 — Hearthstone Brew Co. café menu: food-photo prep + logo vectorize + InDesign data-merge to print-ready PDF & high-res JPEG ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Grade and consistently crop five raw café food/drink photos, vectorize the Hearthstone Brew Co. wordmark, license a warm kraft-paper texture, then run the client's authored InDesign café-menu template through a data-merge against the menu CSV to produce a print-ready PDF and a high-res JPEG.

💰 $45–$45/hrUpwork29 tool calls17 distinct toolscreatelong-horizon
grounded in a real brief: Menu Design for a Brew cafe
Full client brief
Hearthstone Brew Co., a warm neighborhood café, needs its full food-and-drinks menu finished for print. The owner has handed off an authored InDesign menu template (hearthstone_menu_template.indd) laid out in a warm, cozy, elevated style — clean type, generous margins — with five photo frames and real data-merge fields (<<section>>, <<item>>, <<desc>>, <<price>>) arranged under four section headers (Breakfast, All-Day Plates, Pastries, Coffee & Tea). Alongside it come the brand wordmark as a flat PNG, five raw phone-shot photos (a flat white, a stack of buttermilk pancakes, an avocado toast plate, a butter croissant, a cortado in a glass), and a CSV holding every menu row (section, item name, one-line description, price). The photos are unappetizing as-shot: slightly tilted, dull and flat, shot under cool café-window light with busy table clutter behind them. The job: (1) make every food/drink photo look warm and appetizing — straighten, auto-tone, warm the white balance toward a cozy morning-light look, lift the shadows, push vibrance, apply a warm food-pop preset to lock a consistent look across all five, and throw the cluttered café background out of focus so each dish reads as hero; consistently crop all five to the 4:5 frame ratio the template's photo frames expect; (2) lift the flat-white hero cup cleanly onto a transparent background so it can sit on the kraft texture without a hard photographic edge; (3) vectorize the flat wordmark PNG into a clean SVG so it stays razor-sharp at large menu-board size; (4) license a warm kraft-paper / linen texture from Adobe Stock to back the layout and reinforce the cozy feel; (5) upload all the prepped assets, then run the client's authored .indd through an InDesign data-merge against the menu CSV so each section / item / description / price binds into the template and the prepped photos + wordmark + texture sit in their frames; (6) export the bound document to a print-ready A4-portrait PDF (~300 DPI) and a high-resolution JPEG for the café's social and delivery-app listings; (7) assemble the graded photos, vector wordmark, and final menu render onto a Firefly board so the owner can approve the photo treatment and finished menu from one deep-link. Deliver print-ready files plus the graded photos and vector wordmark as the editable source assets.
Deliverables (5)
  • Hearthstone_Menu_print.pdf — Print-ready one-page A4 portrait PDF exported from the data-merged InDesign document; menu copy bound from CSV, graded food/drink photos + vectorized wordmark + kraft texture placed; ~300 DPI print qu
  • Hearthstone_Menu_highres.jpg — High-resolution JPEG of the same bound one-page menu (A4 portrait, max-quality) for the café's social feeds and delivery-app listings.
  • graded_cafe_photos_x5 — Five color-graded, straightened, consistently 4:5-cropped café photos (flat white / pancakes / avocado toast / croissant / cortado) matching the template frames; the flat-white hero also delivered as
  • hearthstone_wordmark.svg — Clean vector SVG of the Hearthstone Brew Co. wordmark for crisp scaling at large menu-board / print size; provided as an editable source asset.
  • Hearthstone_Menu_review_board — Firefly board deep-link assembling the graded photos, vectorized wordmark, and final menu render for one-click owner sign-off.
Workflow — 29 tool steps
  1. asset_search — Search Adobe Stock for a warm kraft-paper / linen texture to back the menu and reinforce the cozy feel (low-res candidates).
  2. asset_license_and_download_stock — License and download the chosen kraft-paper texture at full-res (license BEFORE use); becomes the layout background asset.
  3. image_auto_straighten — Level the tilted horizon on the flat-white hero cup shot.
  4. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the straightened hero to fix the flat/dull exposure.
  5. image_adjust_color_temperature — Warm the cool café-window white balance toward a cozy morning-light look.
  6. image_adjust_dark_portions — Lift the muddy shadows so the crema and cup detail read cleanly.
  7. image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation — Push vibrance so the coffee and latte-art fern read as appetizing.
  8. image_apply_preset — Apply a warm food-pop Lightroom preset to lock a consistent cozy look (same preset reused for the other four).
  9. image_apply_lens_blur — Throw the cluttered pastry-case background out of focus so the cup reads as hero.
  10. image_crop_and_resize — Crop/resize to the 4:5 frame the template expects (same target applied to all five photos).
  11. image_remove_background — Lift the finished flat-white cup onto a transparent background so it can sit on the kraft texture without a hard photo edge.
  12. image_apply_auto_tone — Run the pancake-stack photo through the same grade pipeline starting with auto-tone (fixes underexposure).
  13. image_adjust_color_temperature — Warm the pancake shot to match the hero's cozy white balance.
  14. image_apply_lens_blur — Blur the busy salt-shaker / menu-card background behind the pancakes.
  15. image_crop_and_resize — Crop the pancake photo to the matching 4:5 template frame.
  16. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the avocado-toast plate to the consistent look.
  17. image_adjust_color_temperature — Warm the avocado-toast shot to match the other dishes.
  18. image_crop_and_resize — Crop the avocado-toast photo to the 4:5 template frame.
  19. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the muddy/flat butter-croissant pastry shot.
  20. image_crop_and_resize — Crop the croissant photo to the 4:5 template frame.
  21. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the flat/cool cortado beverage shot.
  22. image_crop_and_resize — Crop the cortado photo to the 4:5 template frame so all five match.
  23. image_vectorize — Vectorize the flat wordmark PNG into a clean SVG for razor-sharp large-print / menu-board scaling.
  24. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize an upload session for all prepped menu assets (graded photos, vector wordmark, kraft texture).
  25. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the upload; returns asset URNs the InDesign template / data-merge will reference.
  26. document_merge_data_layout — InDesign data-merge: bind the section/item/desc/price rows into the client's authored .indd (which carries real <<fields>>); prepped photos + texture + wordmark
  27. document_render_layout — Export the merged InDesign document to a print-ready ~300 DPI A4 portrait PDF deliverable with bleed.
  28. document_render_layout — Export the same merged document to a high-resolution JPEG deliverable for social / delivery-app listings.
  29. create_firefly_board — Assemble the graded photos, vector wordmark and final menu render onto a Firefly board deep-link for owner sign-off.
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon flagship: 29 connector calls across 4 tool groups (image_* raster grading, asset_* stock/storage, document_* InDesign, create_firefly_board), 17 distinct Adobe tools — far above the >=10 calls / >=6 distinct-tool floor. Difficulty comes from running five separate café photos through a consistent multi-step warm-grade pipeline, branching the flat-white hero into a transparent cutout, vectorizing a logo, sourcing + licensing stock, then converging everything into a genuine InDesign [T] data-merge and dual export. It respects every hard limit: no generative fill / object-removal / text-to-image / compositing / upscaling; image_fill_area is unused; the layout is NOT composed by the connector — document_render_layout / document_merge_data_layout only EXPORT/BIND the client's authored .indd, which is listed as a supplied input; lens_blur is whole-image (no fake masked-DOF claim); the full food+drinks menu copy is supplied via CSV per the brief's 'Full café menu (food + drinks)' requirement.

Client-supplied input assets: 8 total · 6 img · 0 video · 0 audio · 2 data

hearthstone_menu_template.indd.proxy.txt
content
**Template File:** `hearthstone_menu_template.indd`

**Description:**
This is an Adobe InDesign document (.indd) designed as a single-page, A4 portrait menu template for Hearthstone Brew Co. The template embodies a warm, cozy, and elevated aesthetic, utilizing clean serif and humanist typography with generous margins for a refined, non-template-heavy presentation.

**Dimensions:** A4 portrait (210mm x 297mm).

**Structure & Layers:**
*   **Background:** A full-bleed image frame is positioned at the bottom layer, designated for a kraft-paper texture.
*   **Branding:** A dedicated placeholder area at the top of the page is reserved for the "Hearthstone Brew Co." wordmark.
*   **Imagery:** Five distinct image frames, each set to a 4:5 aspect ratio, are strategically placed throughout the layout for visual menu item highlights.
*   **Section Headers:** Pre-formatted text frames delineate four primary menu sections: "Breakfast," "All-Day Plates," "Pastries," and "Coffee & Tea."

**Placeholders & Data Merge:**
The template is configured with genuine Adobe InDesign data-merge placeholder fields, ensuring direct binding for dynamic content population. These fields are arranged under the respective section headers:
*   `<<section>>` (for category or sub-section titles)
*   `<<item>>` (for menu item names)
*   `<<desc>>` (for item descriptions)
*   `<<price>>` (for item pricing)

AO-79 — Marlow & Vane property flyer batch: grade listing photos, vectorize the agency crest, and InDesign data-merge a listings CSV into per-property print flyers (PDF + high-res JPEG) ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Grade four raw property photos, vectorize the Marlow & Vane crest, source a subtle linen texture, then run the client's authored InDesign flyer template through a data-merge against a four-listing roster CSV to batch-produce a print-ready PDF and per-listing high-res JPEGs.

💰 $100Upwork29 tool calls17 distinct toolscreatelong-horizon
grounded in a real brief: Real Estate Flyer Designer Needed
Full client brief
Marlow & Vane Realty, a boutique residential agency, needs its new-listing flyer sheet finished for print and email. Our marketing lead has handed off an authored InDesign flyer template (listing_flyer_template.indd) — a US Letter portrait one-listing-per-page layout in a clean editorial style with a hero photo frame, three thumbnail frames, a crest placement, a full-bleed background frame for a subtle paper/linen texture, and REAL data-merge fields (<<address>>, <<price>>, <<beds>>, <<baths>>, <<sqft>>, <<blurb>>, <<agent_name>>, <<agent_phone>>). We are also handing off a listings roster CSV (four current new listings, one row each with those exact field names), four raw agent-shot property photos (a curb-appeal exterior, a bright living room, a kitchen, a primary bedroom), and the agency crest as a flat PNG. The photos are unappetizing as-shot: slightly tilted, dull/flat, cool white-balance, and slightly under- or over-exposed by window light. The job: (1) make every property photo look bright, warm and inviting — straighten, auto-tone, warm the white balance toward a welcoming daylight look, gently lift shadows, push vibrance a touch (never garish — this is real estate, not a burger), and crop all four consistently to the frame ratios the template expects (a 3:2 hero and matching thumbnails); (2) vectorize the flat crest PNG into a clean SVG so the monogram stays razor-sharp at large print size; (3) license a subtle paper/linen texture from Adobe Stock to back the layout without competing with the photos; (4) upload all the prepped assets, then run the client's authored .indd through InDesign data-merge against the roster CSV so every listing's address / price / beds / baths / sqft / blurb / agent contact binds into its own flyer page, with the graded photos, crest and texture placed; (5) export the bound multi-page document to a print-ready PDF (all four flyers) and also emit a high-resolution JPEG per listing for email and MLS use; (6) write a short recipe note recording the grade settings and the CSV-to-field mapping so the same treatment can be re-run next week when new listings come in. Deliverable dimensions: US Letter portrait (8.5 x 11 in), ~300 DPI print. Brand constraints: keep the editorial/clean look, do not oversaturate, crest must be crisp vector, agent contact block bound from the CSV per row.
Deliverables (5)
  • MarlowVane_Listing_Flyers_print.pdf — Print-ready multi-page US Letter portrait (8.5x11in) PDF exported from the data-merged InDesign document — one flyer page per roster listing; per-row copy (address/price/beds/baths/sqft/blurb/agent) b
  • MarlowVane_Listing_JPEGs_x4 — Four high-resolution JPEGs, one per listing flyer page (US Letter portrait, max quality), for email blasts and MLS upload.
  • graded_property_photos_x4 — Four color-graded, straightened, consistently cropped property photos (exterior 3:2 hero + living room / kitchen / bedroom thumbnails) matching the template frames, with a shared warm/bright editorial
  • marlow_vane_crest.svg — Clean vector SVG of the Marlow & Vane monogram crest for crisp scaling at large print size.
  • flyer_recipe_note.txt — A short recipe note recording the per-photo grade settings (straighten, auto-tone, temperature, shadow-lift, vibrance, crop ratios) and the CSV-column-to-InDesign-field mapping, so the same flyer batc
Workflow — 29 tool steps
  1. asset_search — Search Adobe Stock for a subtle paper/linen background texture to back the flyer without competing with the photos (low-res candidates).
  2. asset_license_and_download_stock — License and download the chosen linen texture at full-res (license BEFORE use); becomes the layout background asset.
  3. image_auto_straighten — Level the tilted horizon on the curb-appeal exterior hero shot.
  4. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the straightened exterior to fix the flat/dull overcast exposure.
  5. image_adjust_color_temperature — Warm the cool/blue overcast white balance toward an inviting daylight look.
  6. image_adjust_dark_portions — Gently lift the crushed shadows so the shaded siding and lawn read cleanly.
  7. image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation — Push vibrance a touch so the lawn/sky read fresh — restrained, not garish (real estate look).
  8. image_crop_and_resize — Crop/resize to the 3:2 hero frame the template expects, trimming the parked car / trash bin at the edge (crop, not object removal).
  9. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the backlit living room to recover the underexposed interior (same grade pipeline).
  10. image_adjust_exposure — Nudge exposure up on the dim interior so the room is bright and welcoming.
  11. image_adjust_color_temperature — Warm the cool interior to match the exterior hero's white balance for a consistent set.
  12. image_crop_and_resize — Crop the living room to the matching 3:2 thumbnail frame, trimming the stray power cable at the baseboard edge.
  13. image_auto_straighten — Straighten the tilted kitchen shot.
  14. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the flat fluorescent-lit kitchen.
  15. image_adjust_color_temperature — Correct the ugly yellow-green fluorescent cast toward neutral daylight.
  16. image_adjust_single_color_saturation — Knock down the lingering green cast by desaturating just the green channel so cabinets read true.
  17. image_crop_and_resize — Crop the kitchen to the 3:2 thumbnail frame, trimming the cluttered dish-rack countertop edge.
  18. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the dim underexposed primary bedroom.
  19. image_adjust_color_temperature — Warm the cool bedroom to the consistent inviting look.
  20. image_crop_and_resize — Crop the bedroom to the matching 3:2 thumbnail frame, trimming the laundry basket at the edge so all four photos share the frame ratio.
  21. image_vectorize — Vectorize the flat crest PNG into a clean SVG for razor-sharp large-print scaling.
  22. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize an upload session for all prepped flyer assets (graded photos, vector crest, texture).
  23. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the upload; returns asset URNs the InDesign template/data-merge will reference.
  24. asset_create_folders — Create a Creative Cloud project folder to hold the flyer batch inputs and outputs for the recurring engagement.
  25. document_merge_data_layout — InDesign data-merge: bind the four roster listings (address/price/beds/baths/sqft/blurb/agent) into the client's authored .indd (which carries real <<fields>>),
  26. document_render_layout — Export the merged multi-page InDesign document to a print-ready ~300 DPI US Letter PDF (all four flyers).
  27. document_render_layout — Export the same merged document to high-resolution per-page JPEGs (one per listing) for email/MLS use.
  28. asset_copy_assets — Copy the exported PDF + JPEGs into the project folder so the whole flyer batch lives together for hand-off.
  29. local_write_recipe_note — Write a recipe note recording each photo's grade settings and the CSV-column-to-InDesign-field mapping so the batch can be re-run for next week's listings.
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon flagship: 29 connector calls across four tool groups (image_* raster grading, asset_* stock/storage/folders, document_* InDesign data-merge+render, local recipe note), 17 distinct Adobe tools — well above the >=10 calls / >=6 distinct-tool floor. Difficulty comes from running four separate property photos through consistent but photo-specific grade pipelines (the kitchen needs an extra fluorescent-cast neutralize + green-desaturate branch; the living room needs an exposure lift for the backlit window), vectorizing the crest, sourcing+licensing stock, then converging everything into a GENUINE InDesign [T] data-merge that paginates one flyer per roster row, dual-exporting a multi-page PDF and per-page JPEGs, filing them into a project folder, and emitting a re-run recipe. It respects every hard limit: no generative fill / expand / object-removal-with-reconstruction / text-to-image / from-scratch layout / upscaling; edge clutter (cars, cables, laundry) is handled by CROPPING, not inpainting; image_fill_area is unused; the layout is NOT composed by the connector — document_merge_data_layout / document_render_layout only BIND/EXPORT the client's authored .indd, which is a supplied input; vibrance is kept restrained per the real-estate brand constraint; real per-listing copy comes from the roster CSV.

Client-supplied input assets: 7 total · 5 img · 0 video · 0 audio · 2 data

Images (5)

openai

listing_flyer_template.indd.proxy.txt
content
**Template File Description:** `listing_flyer_template.indd`

This InDesign template is designed for a single-listing real estate flyer, formatted to US Letter portrait dimensions (8.5x11 inches). It features a clean, editorial aesthetic, prioritizing clear information hierarchy and visual impact for each property.

**Structure & Layout:**

*   **Background Layer:** A full-bleed image frame is positioned at the base, designated for a subtle paper or linen texture to provide a premium feel.
*   **Image Frames:**
    *   **Hero Image:** A prominent 3:2 aspect ratio image frame dominates the upper half, intended for the primary property photo.
    *   **Thumbnail Images:** Three smaller, matching image frames are arranged below the hero, suitable for additional property views.
*   **Crest Placement:** A dedicated, smaller frame is positioned for a brand or agency crest/logo, typically in the header or footer area.
*   **Listing Card:** The core content block is laid out as a distinct "listing card," designed for immediate readability.
*   **Agent Contact Footer:** A dedicated section at the bottom for agent details.

**Data Merge Placeholders (InDesign Data Merge Fields):**

The template is configured with genuine InDesign data-merge fields to facilitate automated population and pagination (one page per listing). These fields are strategically placed within the layout:

*   `<<address>>`: Positioned as the primary headline for the listing card.
*   `<<price>>`: Appears prominently in a price band beneath the address.
*   `<<beds>>`, `<<baths>>`, `<<sqft>>`: Arranged in a feature row, typically with accompanying icons or labels.
*   `<<blurb>>`: A multi-line text frame for a descriptive property overview.
*   `<<agent_name>>`: Located in the agent contact footer.
*   `<<agent_phone>>`: Paired with the agent name in the contact footer.

**Brand Styling & Typography:**

The "clean editorial" style is characterized by:

*   **Typography:** A focus on legible sans-serif and/or classic serif fonts with clear hierarchy for headlines, subheadings, and body text.
*   **Color Palette:** Likely minimal, relying on strong contrasts and possibly a single accent color for branding elements.
*   **Whitespace:** Generous use of whitespace to enhance readability and give the flyer an uncluttered, professional appearance.

This template is authored directly in InDesign, ensuring

AO-80 — Lanternwood Folk Fest 2026 program booklet: sponsor-logo prep + performer-photo grade + InDesign data-merge to press-ready PDF ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Prep the Lanternwood Folk Fest sponsor logos (vectorize / transparent-cut) and grade the performer press photos, source a kraft cover texture, then run the client's authored multi-page InDesign booklet template through two data-merge passes against the schedule and sponsor CSVs to produce a press-ready PDF and a screen-quality PDF program guide.

💰 ~$120 est.Freelancer.com26 tool calls16 distinct toolscreatelong-horizon
grounded in a real brief: Event Program Booklet / Brochure Design
Full client brief
Lanternwood Folk Fest, a fictional two-day roots-and-Americana music festival, needs its official program booklet finished for print and for the event app. The organizer has handed off an authored multi-page InDesign booklet template (folkfest_program_template.indd) that already carries the festival's established warm-cream / lantern-amber colour scheme and type hierarchy, with real InDesign data-merge fields laid out across the interior spreads (<<day>>, <<stage>>, <<set_time>>, <<artist>>, <<bio>>, <<artist_photo>> for the schedule pages, and <<sponsor_name>>, <<sponsor_tier>>, <<sponsor_logo>> for the sponsor grid on the back-cover spread), plus front/back covers with a full-bleed frame for a kraft-paper texture and a placeholder for the festival wordmark. Along with the template the organizer supplies: a schedule CSV (one row per performance set: day, stage, set time, artist, one-line bio, and the filename of that artist's press photo), a sponsor CSV (sponsor name, tier — Presenting / Stage / Community — and the filename of that sponsor's logo), a folder of raw sponsor logo files (some are clean flat rasters, some are photos of the logo on a busy background, some are already reasonable PNGs), and a folder of raw performer press photos that are unappetizing as-shot: slightly tilted, dull and flat, mixed white-balance because they came from different photographers. The job: (1) make every sponsor logo crisp and print-safe per the brief — vectorize the flat raster logos into clean SVGs so they stay sharp at any size, and lift the busy-background logos onto clean transparent PNGs so they sit consistently on the cream page; (2) grade the performer press photos so the artists look their best — straighten the tilted ones, auto-tone the flat exposure, normalize the mixed white balance toward the festival's warm look, push vibrance, and crop each to the uniform portrait ratio the schedule frames expect; (3) license a subtle kraft/recycled-paper texture from Adobe Stock to back the front and back covers; (4) upload all the prepped assets, then run the client's authored .indd through the InDesign data-merge against the schedule CSV (interior schedule/bio pages) and then against the sponsor CSV (back-cover sponsor grid) so every set, bio, artist photo, sponsor name, tier and logo binds into the correct frame; (5) export the merged booklet to a press-ready CMYK PDF with bleed/crop marks for the printer and a lighter RGB screen-quality PDF for the event app; (6) assemble the graded photos, prepped logos and final booklet render onto a Firefly board so the organizer can sign off on the whole program in one deep-link. All copy stays exactly as supplied in the CSVs — the design work is the layout binding, image optimization and logo prep the brief asks for, nothi
Deliverables (5)
  • Lanternwood_Program_press.pdf — Press-ready CMYK PDF of the full multi-page A5 booklet exported from the twice-merged InDesign document, with bleed and crop marks; schedule/bio/sponsor copy bound from the CSVs, graded performer phot
  • Lanternwood_Program_screen.pdf — Lighter RGB screen-quality PDF of the same bound booklet (no crop marks, downsampled images) for the festival event app and email distribution.
  • graded_performer_photos — Three straightened, auto-toned, white-balance-normalized, vibrance-boosted performer press photos, each cropped to the uniform 4:5 portrait ratio the schedule frames expect.
  • sponsor_logos_prepped — Sponsor logos made print-safe: the two flat-raster logos delivered as clean scalable SVGs (vectorized), and the on-signage logo lifted onto a clean transparent-background PNG so all sit consistently i
  • Lanternwood_Program_review_board — Firefly board deep-link assembling the graded performer photos, prepped sponsor logos and final booklet render for one-click organizer sign-off.
Workflow — 26 tool steps
  1. asset_search — Search Adobe Stock for a subtle kraft/recycled-paper texture to back the front and back covers (low-res candidates).
  2. asset_license_and_download_stock — License and download the chosen kraft-paper texture at full-res (license BEFORE use); becomes the cover background asset.
  3. image_vectorize — Vectorize the flat Harbormill raster logo into a clean SVG so it stays razor-sharp at any print size.
  4. image_vectorize — Vectorize the flat single-color Cedarline logo into a clean scalable SVG.
  5. image_select_by_prompt — Select just the Riverbend logo mark+wordmark off the busy storefront-glass background (logo is fully visible/unoccluded).
  6. image_remove_background — Lift the selected Riverbend logo onto a clean transparent PNG so it sits consistently on the cream sponsor grid (no reconstruction — the logo is unoccluded).
  7. image_auto_straighten — Level the tilted horizon on the Copper Kettle Trio press photo.
  8. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the straightened photo to fix the flat/dull overcast exposure.
  9. image_adjust_color_temperature — Warm the cool/greyish white balance toward the festival's warm look.
  10. image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation — Push vibrance so the band and instruments read well on the printed page.
  11. image_crop_and_resize — Crop/resize to the 4:5 portrait frame the schedule template expects (same target for all press photos).
  12. image_apply_auto_tone — Run the Marigold duo photo through the same grade pipeline starting with auto-tone (fixes the underexposed shadows).
  13. image_adjust_color_temperature — Normalize the muddy tungsten cast to match the festival warm white balance.
  14. image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation — Lift vibrance so the duo photo matches the graded look of the others.
  15. image_crop_and_resize — Crop the Marigold photo to the matching 4:5 schedule frame.
  16. image_auto_straighten — Level the tilted horizon on the Hollow Pines golden-hour photo.
  17. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone to pull back the washed/overexposed sky and flat contrast.
  18. image_crop_and_resize — Crop the Hollow Pines photo to the 4:5 frame so all three performer portraits match.
  19. asset_create_folders — Create a Creative Cloud folder to hold all prepped booklet assets before upload/merge.
  20. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize an upload session for all prepped assets (vector logos, transparent logo, graded photos, cover texture) into the folder.
  21. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the upload; returns asset URNs (and presigned URLs) the InDesign data-merge references by filename via additionalImageFiles.
  22. document_merge_data_layout — First data-merge pass: bind the schedule rows (day/stage/set_time/artist/bio + the graded artist_photo images) into the interior schedule frames of the client's
  23. document_merge_data_layout — Second data-merge pass over the schedule-merged .indd: bind the sponsor rows (sponsor_name/sponsor_tier + prepped sponsor_logo SVGs/PNG) into the back-cover spo
  24. document_render_layout — Export the fully-merged booklet to a press-ready ~300 DPI CMYK PDF with bleed/crop marks for the saddle-stitch printer.
  25. document_render_layout — Export the same fully-merged booklet to a lighter RGB screen-quality PDF for the event app and email distribution.
  26. create_firefly_board — Assemble the graded performer photos, prepped sponsor logos and final booklet render onto a Firefly board deep-link for organizer sign-off.
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon flagship: 26 connector calls across 4 tool groups (image_* raster, asset_* stock/storage/folders, document_* InDesign, create_firefly_board), 16 distinct Adobe tools — far above the >=10 calls / >=6 distinct-tool floor. Difficulty comes from prepping a mixed bag of sponsor logos two different correct ways (vectorize the flat rasters vs. select+lift the photographed one), running three separate performer photos through a consistent multi-step grade pipeline to a uniform crop, sourcing+licensing a cover texture, organizing everything into a CC folder, then converging it all into a genuine TWO-pass InDesign [T] data-merge (schedule CSV then sponsor CSV, because the tool binds one dataSourceUrl per call) and dual PDF export (press CMYK + screen RGB). It respects every hard limit: no generative fill / object-removal-with-reconstruction / text-to-image / from-scratch layout composition / upscaling; image_fill_area is unused; the layout is NOT composed by the connector — merge_data_layout/render_layout only BIND/EXPORT the client's authored .indd, which is listed as a supplied input; the on-signage logo is fully visible so remove_background is a clean lift, not inpainting; all copy stays verbatim from the supplied CSVs.

folkfest_program_template.indd.proxy.txt
content
**Template Description:**

**File:** `folkfest_program_template.indd`
**Type:** Adobe InDesign Document (.indd)
**Purpose:** Multi-page program booklet for the Lanternwood Folk Fest.

**Structure & Dimensions:**
*   **Format:** A5 portrait (148 x 210 mm)
*   **Binding:** Saddle-stitched booklet
*   **Layout:** Multi-page document with dedicated spreads for schedule details and a back-cover sponsor grid.

**Brand Styling:**
*   **Color Palette:** Established warm-cream and lantern-amber identity.
*   **Backgrounds:** Front and back covers feature a full-bleed background frame pre-set for a kraft texture.
*   **Wordmark:** Placeholder frame for the festival's wordmark is present on both front and back covers.
*   **Typography:** Pre-defined type hierarchy consistent with the festival's branding.

**Placeholders & Data Merge Fields:**
*   **Interactive Data Merge:** The template is genuinely authored in InDesign with active data merge fields, ensuring proper binding with external data sources.
*   **Interior Schedule Spreads:** Each set's entry includes:
    *   `<<day>>` (Text field)
    *   `<<stage>>` (Text field)
    *   `<<set_time>>` (Text field)
    *   `<<artist>>` (Text field)
    *   `<<bio>>` (Text field)
    *   `<<artist_photo>>` (Image merge frame, 4:5 portrait ratio)
*   **Back Cover Sponsor Grid:** Each sponsor entry includes:
    *   `<<sponsor_name>>` (Text field)
    *   `<<sponsor_tier>>` (Text field)
    *   `<<sponsor_logo>>` (Image merge frame)

**Layers:**
*   Implicitly structured with layers for backgrounds, text content, image frames, and data merge fields to maintain design integrity during content population.

AO-81 — Nimbadesk SaaS product sell sheets: grade product screenshots + vectorize logo + InDesign data-merge one row per product tier into print PDFs + high-res JPEGs ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Clean up and on-brand-tint three raw product-UI screenshots, vectorize the Nimbadesk logo, then run the client's authored one-page sell-sheet InDesign template through a data-merge against a product-tier CSV to produce a print-ready PDF and a high-res JPEG per tier.

💰 ~$120 est.Upwork23 tool calls13 distinct toolscreatelong-horizon
grounded in a real brief: Marketing Collateral & Brand Assets (Legal / MVA Case Management)
Full client brief
Nimbadesk, a B2B SaaS company that sells a customer-support help-desk platform, needs its product sell sheets finished for print and for the sales team's leave-behind kit. Our production designer has already authored the layout: a one-page US-Letter (8.5" x 11" portrait) sell-sheet InDesign template (nimbadesk_sellsheet.indd) built on a clean modern grid, with a branded header, a "Why teams switch to Nimbadesk" company-advantage section, a core-features bullet block, a prominent "Book a demo" CTA, and real InDesign data-merge fields (<<tier_name>>, <<tagline>>, <<price_line>>, <<feature_1>>, <<feature_2>>, <<feature_3>>, <<cta_line>>) plus a hero image frame for the product screenshot and a logo frame. This is a production/finishing job, NOT a fresh branding exercise. We will hand off: the authored .indd template, a brand kit (hex palette #0E1F3D deep navy + #3B82F6 signal blue + #F5F7FA fog-grey, typography notes), three raw product-UI screenshots (the ticket inbox, the analytics dashboard, the automation builder) that were captured on-screen and look dull, slightly tilted and cool/washed-out, the Nimbadesk logo as a flat PNG, and a CSV holding one row per product tier (Starter, Growth, Enterprise) with the tier name, tagline, price line, three feature bullets and CTA line. The job: (1) make each product screenshot look crisp and on-brand — straighten it, auto-tone to fix the washed-out capture, warm/neutralize the cool cast, lift the shadows so dark-UI detail reads, nudge brightness/contrast, and lay a subtle signal-blue monochromatic tint so the three shots feel like one brand family; consistently crop all three to the same 16:10 ratio the hero frame expects; (2) vectorize the flat logo PNG into a clean SVG so the wordmark stays razor-sharp at print size and on the large header; (3) recommend a professional, legible sans-serif body font that pairs with the brand's geometric feel (for the designer's reference note); (4) upload all the prepped assets, then run the client's authored .indd through InDesign data-merge against the tier CSV so each tier's copy binds into its own page of the template with the matching prepped screenshot in the hero frame and the vector logo in the header; (5) export the merged multi-page document to a print-ready PDF and a high-resolution JPEG deliverable. Keep everything on the supplied palette; the copy is exactly what the CSV carries — do not rewrite it. Final files must be print-ready (~300 DPI, US Letter portrait).
Deliverables (5)
  • Nimbadesk_SellSheets_print.pdf — Print-ready multi-page US-Letter (8.5" x 11", portrait) PDF exported from the data-merged InDesign document — one page per product tier (Starter, Growth, Enterprise), tier copy bound from the CSV, pre
  • Nimbadesk_SellSheets_highres.jpg — High-resolution JPEG export of the same bound sell-sheet document (US Letter portrait, max quality) for the sales team's digital leave-behind and email use.
  • graded_screenshots_x3 — Three cleaned, straightened, on-brand-tinted product screenshots (ticket inbox / analytics dashboard / automation builder), consistently 16:10-cropped to match the template hero frame.
  • nimbadesk_logo.svg — Clean vector SVG of the Nimbadesk wordmark for razor-sharp scaling in the sell-sheet header and at large print size.
  • sellsheet_body_font_note.txt — A short recipe note recording the recommended body font (from font_recommend) that pairs with the brand's geometric heading font, for the designer's reference.
Workflow — 23 tool steps
  1. image_auto_straighten — Level the ~2-degree tilt on the ticket-inbox screen capture.
  2. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the straightened inbox shot to fix the flat/washed-out capture.
  3. image_adjust_color_temperature — Warm/neutralize the cool bluish cast so the UI whites read neutral.
  4. image_adjust_brightness_and_contrast — Nudge brightness/contrast so the small UI label text stays crisp.
  5. image_apply_monochromatic_tint — Lay a subtle signal-blue (#3B82F6) monochromatic tint so the screenshot reads on-brand (same tint reused for the other two).
  6. image_crop_and_resize — Crop/resize to the 16:10 hero frame the template expects (same target applied to all three).
  7. image_apply_auto_tone — Run the analytics-dashboard capture through the same grade, starting with auto-tone (fixes underexposure).
  8. image_adjust_dark_portions — Lift the crushed shadows so the dark-theme chart/table detail becomes readable.
  9. image_adjust_color_temperature — Neutralize the cool cast to match the inbox shot's white balance.
  10. image_apply_monochromatic_tint — Apply the same signal-blue tint so the dashboard matches the brand family.
  11. image_crop_and_resize — Crop the dashboard to the matching 16:10 hero frame.
  12. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the automation-builder capture to the consistent look.
  13. image_adjust_color_temperature — Neutralize the cool white balance on the automation-builder shot.
  14. image_apply_monochromatic_tint — Apply the same signal-blue tint so all three screenshots read as one brand family.
  15. image_crop_and_resize — Crop the automation-builder shot to the 16:10 hero frame so all three match.
  16. image_vectorize — Vectorize the flat logo PNG into a clean SVG for razor-sharp large-print and header scaling.
  17. font_recommend — Recommend a legible sans-serif body font that pairs with the brand's geometric heading font, per the brand kit.
  18. local_write_recipe_note — Emit the recommended body font as a short reference note for the designer.
  19. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize an upload session for all prepped sell-sheet assets (three graded screenshots + vector logo).
  20. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the upload; returns asset URNs the InDesign template/data-merge will reference for the hero frame and header logo.
  21. document_merge_data_layout — InDesign data-merge: bind each tier row (Starter/Growth/Enterprise) into the client's authored .indd (which carries real <<fields>>), producing one bound page p
  22. document_render_layout — Export the merged multi-page InDesign document to a print-ready ~300 DPI US-Letter PDF deliverable.
  23. document_render_layout — Export the same merged document to a high-resolution JPEG deliverable for the digital leave-behind.
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon: 23 connector calls, 13 distinct Adobe tools plus one local step — well above the >=10 calls / >=6 distinct-tool floor. Difficulty comes from running three separate product screenshots through a consistent multi-step clean-up-and-tint pipeline (with a dark-UI shadow-lift branch on the dashboard), vectorizing a logo, sourcing a font recommendation, then converging everything into a genuine InDesign [T] data-merge that binds one row per product tier into its own page, plus dual print/JPEG export. It respects every hard limit: no generative fill / generative-expand / object-removal / text-to-image / from-scratch layout / compositing / upscaling; image_fill_area is unused; the layout is NOT composed by the connector — document_merge_data_layout binds and document_render_layout only EXPORTS the client's authored .indd, which is listed as a supplied authored input; the monochromatic tint and crop are whole-image operations; copy is honored literally from the CSV per the brief.

nimbadesk_sellsheet.indd.proxy.txt
content
**Template File:** `nimbadesk_sellsheet.indd`

**Description:**
This is an Adobe InDesign template for a one-page B2B SaaS product sell sheet, formatted for US Letter (8.5" x 11") in portrait orientation. It features a clean, modern grid layout for a professional aesthetic.

**Structure & Layout:**
The template is organized into distinct sections:
1.  **Header:** Branded section containing the company logo frame and key product identifiers.
2.  **Hero Image:** A prominent image frame (16:10 ratio) intended for a product screenshot.
3.  **Company Advantage:** A dedicated block titled 'Why teams switch to Nimbadesk', detailing company benefits.
4.  **Core Features:** A three-bullet list highlighting primary product features.
5.  **Call to Action (CTA) Band:** A prominent footer band for the main call to action.

**Layers:**
The document is typically structured with a 'Background/Graphics' layer for fixed brand elements and a 'Content' layer for editable text and image frames.

**Placeholders:**
The template contains genuine InDesign data-merge placeholder fields for dynamic content population:
*   `<<tier_name>>` (in header)
*   `<<tagline>>` (in header)
*   `<<price_line>>` (within product details)
*   `<<feature_1>>`, `<<feature_2>>`, `<<feature_3>>` (for the core features list)
*   `<<cta_line>>` (in the CTA band)
Additionally, dedicated frames are present for the company logo (header) and the hero product screenshot.

**Brand Styling:**
The design adheres to the Nimbadesk brand guidelines, utilizing a palette of #0E1F3D (navy), #3B82F6 (signal blue), and #F5F7FA (fog-grey) for a consistent and modern appearance. Typography is clean and legible, complementing the grid-based layout.

tier_name,tagline,price_line,feature_1,feature_2,feature_3,cta_line

Growth,Scale your support with advanced features and automation.,$59 / agent / mo,SLA Rules Engine,Live Chat Integration,Automation & Macros,Book a demo
Enterprise,Comprehensive solutions for complex support operations.,Custom Pricing,SSO & SAML,Advanced Analytics,Dedicated CSM,Book a demo

AO-82 — Lumen Quarterly magazine issue: brand-guideline consistency audit of the laid-out pages against the supplied style guide (marked-up findings report) ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Audit every page of the laid-out Lumen Quarterly issue proof against the supplied Lumen brand style guide and return a marked-up, page-referenced brand-consistency findings report listing each colour / logo / typography / imagery drift and the exact rule it breaks.

💰 ~$120 est.Freelancer.com10 tool calls6 distinct toolsanalyze
grounded in a real brief: Graphic Design Aligned with Brand Standards
Full client brief
Lumen Quarterly is a fictional independent arts-and-culture magazine published by Lumen House Press. Our in-house junior designer has finished laying out the Autumn 2026 issue in InDesign and exported it as a print proof PDF, but before it goes to the printer we need a fresh set of eyes to check that the whole issue actually stays inside our brand framework — our board is strict about consistency and the last issue went out with an off-palette pull-quote and a stretched masthead lock-up that we only caught in print. You will receive: (1) lumen_autumn2026_proof.pdf, the full 16-page laid-out issue (cover, masthead, contents, editor's letter, three feature spreads, department pages, back-cover ad); (2) lumen_brand_styleguide.pdf, our complete brand style guide covering the approved colour palette (Lumen Ink #1B2A4A, Signal Coral #FF5A47, Paper #F4F1EA — with the exact hex values and the rule that body text is Lumen Ink only), the masthead / logo lock-up rules (clear-space, minimum size, never stretched or recoloured), the typography system (Freight Display for headlines, Untitled Sans for body, specified sizes and tracking), and the imagery tone rules (warm, editorial, consistent grade; no cold or oversaturated photos); and (3) two flat reference images — the approved palette swatch strip and the correct masthead lock-up — to compare on-page elements against. The job is an audit, not a redesign — do NOT re-lay-out, restyle, recolour or edit anything. Go through every page, compare what is on the page against the guideline, and hand back a brand-consistency audit report that, for each issue found, gives the page number, the on-page region, a plain-language description of the drift, the specific guideline clause it violates, the approved value versus the observed value, and a severity (blocker / warning / nit). We expect you to catch things like: a headline set in the wrong weight or the wrong face, a coral used at the wrong hex, the masthead placed with too little clear-space or subtly stretched, a feature photo that reads cold/blue against the warm-editorial imagery rule, and body copy set in a non-approved colour. Deliver the findings as a clean marked-up report file plus the raw findings as a structured data file, and open with a one-page cover summary giving the total count by severity. Page size is A4 portrait; the report is for the editor and the board, not the printer.
Deliverables (3)
  • Lumen_BrandConsistency_Audit.md — The primary marked-up deliverable emitted by local_write_recipe_note: a clean, human-readable brand-consistency audit report. Opens with a one-page cover summary (total findings by severity: blocker /
  • lumen_audit_findings.json — The raw structured findings companion emitted by local_write_recipe_note: an ordered list of records {page, region, drift, guideline_clause, approved_value, observed_value, severity} plus a summary bl
  • lumen_inspection_previews — The page-preview renders of the proof and the style guide (and the two reference PNGs) produced by the asset preview steps and used as the inspection surface for the audit, referenced by page number i
Workflow — 10 tool steps
  1. asset_create_folders — Stand up the CC asset workspace (e.g. /L09/proof, /styleguide, /references, /findings) to hold the supplied proof, style guide, reference PNGs and the emitted r
  2. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize an upload session for the four client-supplied audit inputs: lumen_autumn2026_proof.pdf, lumen_brand_styleguide.pdf, lumen_palette_swatches.png, lume
  3. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the upload so the proof PDF, style guide PDF and the two reference PNGs are real CC assets with URNs the inspection steps can read.
  4. asset_preview_file — Preview lumen_brand_styleguide.pdf page by page to READ the ground-truth rules the audit checks against: the palette hex values + 'body in Lumen Ink only' rule,
  5. asset_preview_file — Preview the 16-page lumen_autumn2026_proof.pdf page by page so each spread's on-page state (headlines, pull-quotes, body copy colour, masthead placement, photo
  6. asset_inline_preview — Inline-preview the suspect proof pages (contents masthead, second feature pull-quote, editor's-letter body copy, department-page headline, feature photo) at hig
  7. asset_preview_file — Preview the palette-swatch strip and masthead-lock-up reference PNGs so the approved hex chips and the correct un-stretched lock-up proportions are available to
  8. image_select_by_prompt — On the supplied masthead-lock-up reference PNG, select the 'LUMEN QUARTERLY lock-up wordmark' region as an INSPECTION locator so its correct proportions/clear-s
  9. image_select_by_prompt — On the supplied palette-swatch reference PNG, select the individual 'Signal Coral' and 'Lumen Ink' colour chips as an INSPECTION locator so the approved hex val
  10. local_write_recipe_note — Emit the audit deliverables: cross-reference the style-guide rules (read from the guide previews) against the confirmed on-page state (from the proof previews)
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon analyze/audit: 10 connector calls, 6 distinct Adobe tools across two groups (asset workspace/upload/preview and the image_select_by_prompt inspection locator) plus the single [L] recipe-note emit — at the ≥10-call / ≥6-distinct-tool floor for an analyze task and consistent with the analyze band (~6-12 calls). Difficulty is in the analytic structure, not raster grunt-work: the agent must (a) READ the actual brand rules out of a supplied style-guide PDF via page previews rather than assuming them, (b) preview a 16-page proof and inspect every page, drilling into the suspect pages at higher detail, (c) use image_select_by_prompt purely as an INSPECTION locator on the supplied reference PNGs to isolate the approved lock-up proportions and the approved hex chips so on-page colour/proportion can be compared against ground truth, (d) distinguish compliant pages from the planted defects (wrong-hex coral, body copy in coral, wrong-face headline, stretched/under-clear-space masthead, cold-graded photo), and (e) emit a structured, page-referenced, severity-ranked findings report. It respects every hard limit: NOTHING is composed, restyled, edited or rendered — image_select_by_prompt only selects for inspection (no fill, mask edit, recolour or compositing follows it), no image_* edit tool alters any pixels, no document_* tool is used to convert or compose (document_convert_pdf

AO-83 — Halden Sleep mattress catalog pre-merge audit: validate SKU roster against the authored InDesign merge template, proof-render a sample spread, and emit a fix-list report ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Audit Halden Sleep's mattress product roster CSV against the authored InDesign catalog merge template and the mandatory-spec checklist, run a proof data-merge of a sample spread to inspect for blank/overset frames, and emit a per-SKU validation findings report with a prioritized fix-list.

💰 ~$120 est.Freelancer.com14 tool calls10 distinct toolsanalyze
grounded in a real brief: Flipkart and amazon Mattress Listing Setup
Full client brief
Halden Sleep is a boxed-mattress startup about to send its 2026 wholesale catalog to the printer and to its retail-partner intake team. We already have (a) an authored InDesign catalog template (halden_catalog_template.indd) — a 2-up A4 landscape product-spread layout with real InDesign data-merge fields (<<sku>>, <<model>>, <<size>>, <<firmness>>, <<material>>, <<height_in>>, <<weight_lb>>, <<cert>>, <<box_dims>>, <<price>>, plus an <<image>> frame per product) and (b) a product roster CSV (mattress_roster.csv) with one row per SKU that our ops team filled in by hand. Before we spend money on a print run and before the retail intake team rejects us, we need someone to VALIDATE the roster against the template and against the marketplace-style mandatory-spec checklist — the same fields a catalogue approval team checks: size, material, firmness level, certification, weight, and packaging (box) dimensions must be present and non-blank on EVERY SKU, prices must be formatted consistently, firmness must be one of a controlled vocabulary (Soft / Medium / Medium-Firm / Firm), and specs must be consistent across all SKUs (no unit drift, e.g. some rows in cm and some in inches). Deliverable: a plain-English validation findings report (halden_catalog_audit.md/pdf) that lists, per SKU, exactly which mandatory field is missing, mis-formatted, off-vocabulary, or inconsistent, with a prioritized fix-list ranked by blocker vs. warning; plus a proof: run a data-merge of the FIRST sample spread through the authored template and export it so we can eyeball whether any merge frame comes out blank or text oversets. Do NOT redesign the catalog — the .indd layout is final; we only need the data checked and a proof spread rendered. Page size A4 landscape, 300 DPI proof. Please also confirm every referenced product image resolves (no missing/placeholder frames) by previewing the sample.
Deliverables (4)
  • halden_catalog_audit.pdf — Plain-English validation findings report: a per-SKU table of every mandatory-field defect (blank field, off-vocabulary firmness, unit drift, inconsistent price format, duplicate SKU, unresolved image)
  • halden_catalog_audit.md — The same findings + fix-list authored as a Markdown recipe note (source of the PDF), listing each SKU, the failing rule from the checklist, and the exact edit needed.
  • proof_spread_sample.pdf — Proof render of the FIRST sample product spread produced by running the authored template through a data-merge of the sample rows, A4 landscape ~300 DPI, so the client can eyeball blank/overset merge
  • sample_spread_preview.png — Flattened inline preview image of the proof spread used during the audit to visually confirm which frames render empty or overset and whether the referenced packshot resolves.
Workflow — 14 tool steps
  1. asset_create_folders — Create a Creative Cloud working folder to hold the roster, template, images and audit artifacts for this catalog review.
  2. asset_initialize_file_upload — Open an upload session for the supplied inputs (template .indd, roster CSV, checklist, 12 packshots).
  3. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the upload; returns URNs the preview, merge and image-checks reference.
  4. asset_preview_file — Inspect the authored .indd to read its merge-field set and confirm which fields the layout actually expects (<<sku>>,<<model>>,<<size>>,<<firmness>>,<<material>
  5. media_summarize — Summarize/extract the roster CSV contents (columns + all rows) to enumerate every SKU and its filled/blank cells for the field-completeness pass.
  6. media_summarize — Extract the mandatory-spec checklist rules (required fields, firmness vocabulary, unit + price-format rules, uniqueness, image-resolves) that the roster is vali
  7. asset_search — Search the uploaded packshot set for each filename referenced in the roster's image column to detect the unresolved/missing image reference (a hit-or-miss per S
  8. asset_inline_preview — Inline-preview the resolved packshots to confirm they are real product images (not blank/placeholder) so the audit only flags the genuinely-missing one.
  9. document_merge_data_layout — Run a PROOF data-merge of the sample rows through the client's authored .indd (real <<fields>>) so we can observe how the current roster binds — blank cells sur
  10. document_render_layout — Export the first proof spread to an A4-landscape ~300 DPI PDF for visual inspection of blank/overset frames.
  11. asset_inline_preview — Inline-preview the proof spread as a flattened image to visually confirm which merge frames render empty/overset and whether the missing-image SKU shows a place
  12. media_summarize — Summarize the rendered proof spread to capture the visible defects (empty spec cells, overset description, placeholder image) as evidence for the report.
  13. local_write_recipe_note — Author the validation findings: cross-reference every SKU against the checklist (blank mandatory field, off-vocabulary firmness, cm/kg unit drift, inconsistent
  14. document_convert_pdf — Convert the Markdown findings report to a clean PDF deliverable for hand-off to the owner and the retail-intake team.
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon analyze task: 14 connector calls, 10 distinct Adobe tools across three groups (asset_* storage/search/preview, document_* InDesign merge/render/convert, media_summarize inspection) plus one [L] recipe-note emit — above the >=10-call / >=6-distinct-tool floor for an analyze workflow. Difficulty comes from genuinely INSPECTING heterogeneous inputs (an authored .indd's field set, a defect-seeded roster CSV, a rules checklist, a 12-image packshot set) and cross-referencing them: field completeness, controlled-vocabulary firmness, unit drift, price-format consistency, SKU uniqueness, and image resolution, THEN validating the theory by running a genuine [T] proof data-merge and visually reading the rendered spread for blank/overset frames before writing an evidence-backed per-SKU fix-list. It respects every hard limit: no generative fill / object-removal / text-to-image / upscaling / from-scratch layout; document_merge_data_layout binds and document_render_layout only EXPORTS the client's authored .indd (a supplied input); the output is a findings report + proof, exactly matching an ANALYZE deliverable.

halden_catalog_template.indd.proxy.txt
content
`halden_catalog_template.indd` is an Adobe InDesign template configured for a 2-up A4 Landscape mattress catalog product spread.

**Dimensions:** A4 Landscape (297mm W x 210mm H), featuring standard 12mm margins and a 3mm bleed.

**Structure:** Each A4 spread presents two distinct product cards arranged side-by-side. Each product card follows a consistent layout:
*   **Top:** A prominent graphic frame linked to `<<image>>`.
*   **Below Image:** Product title (`<<model>>`) and SKU (`<<sku>>`).
*   **Body:** A structured specification table detailing `<<size>>`, `<<firmness>>`, `<<material>>`, `<<height_in>>`, `<<weight_lb>>`, `<<cert>>`, and `<<box_dims>>`.
*   **Bottom:** A clear price display (`<<price>>`).

**Placeholders:** All specified fields (`<<sku>>`, `<<model>>`, `<<size>>`, `<<firmness>>`, `<<material>>`, `<<height_in>>`, `<<weight_lb>>`, `<<cert>>`, `<<box_dims>>`, `<<price>>`, `<<image>>`) are genuine Adobe InDesign data merge placeholders, correctly formatted and bound within their respective text or graphic frames, ready for data population.

**Layers:** The document is organized into standard layers for clarity and control:
*   `Background & Guides`: Non-printing guides, subtle background elements.
*   `Images`: Contains all `<<image>>` frames.
*   `Text & Data`: Houses all text frames including data merge fields and static copy.
*   `Branding & Logos`: Dedicated for the "Halden" brand logo and any recurring graphic elements.

**Brand Styling (Halden):** The template embodies a clean, modern aesthetic appropriate for premium mattress products. It utilizes a primary sans-serif typeface (e.g., Open Sans or similar) for headings and body text, a neutral color palette (greys, whites) with a subtle brand accent color (e.g., a calming blue or soft green), and a strong grid-based layout ensuring readability and a sophisticated presentation. Ample white space frames the product information.

spec_checklist.txt
gemini/gemini-2.5-flash
content
1.  **Mandatory Fields:** The following fields are required and must not be blank for any product entry: `size`, `material`, `firmness`, `cert`, `weight_lb`, `box_dims`.

2.  **Firmness Controlled Vocabulary:** The `firmness` field must use one of these exact values: `Soft`, `Medium`, `Medium-Firm`, `Firm`. No other values are permitted.

3.  **Unit Consistency:**
    *   All linear dimensions (e.g., product dimensions, `box_dims`) must be specified in `INCHES`.
    *   The `weight_lb` field must be specified in `POUNDS`.
    *   Units `cm` or `kg` are not accepted.

4.  **Price Formatting:** The `price` field must be consistently formatted as `$#,###.##` (e.g., `$1,234.56`, `$999.00`).

5.  **SKU Uniqueness:** Each product `sku` must be unique across the entire catalogue submission.

6.  **Image Resolution:** All image URLs referenced in the `images` field must be valid and resolve to an accessible image file.

AO-84 — Verdafix industrial sealant labels: per-SKU/variant data-merge of a supplied InDesign label template to print-ready CMYK PDFs + hazard-panel photo prep ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Prep the supplied Verdafix product photos and hazard/barcode graphics, then run the client's authored InDesign master label template through a data-merge against a per-SKU/variant CSV to produce one print-ready CMYK-with-bleed PDF per label variant.

💰 ~$120 est.Upwork21 tool calls12 distinct toolseditlong-horizon
grounded in a real brief: Packaging/Graphic Designer for Label Conversion
Full client brief
Verdafix Industrie GmbH, a European maker of construction sealants and adhesives, is converting its product-label artwork from legacy flat PDFs into a maintainable, print-ready system. The team has already rebuilt ONE master label into an authored InDesign template (verdafix_label_master.indd) - a 90 x 130 mm bottle/canister wrap laid out with 3 mm bleed, carrying real InDesign data-merge fields (<<product_name>>, <<variant>>, <<net_content>>, <<sku>>, <<batch_prefix>>, <<h_statements>>, <<p_statements>>, <<usage_de>>) plus image frames for the product shot, the GHS hazard pictogram, and the EAN barcode. What they need now is production, not design: bind the master to the full product range and output one press-ready file per SKU/variant. Supplied assets: a variants CSV (verdafix_label_variants.csv, one row per SKU/variant across the MS-Polymer, Hybrid and Acryl ranges in 290 ml and 600 ml sizes - 12 rows total) whose column headers exactly match the merge fields and which references each variant's product-photo and barcode filenames; a folder of raw product photos shot on the line (slightly tilted, dull, cool-cast studio table shots that need leveling, tone and a consistent crop to the template's photo frame); pre-rendered EAN-13 barcode PNGs and the GHS02/GHS05/GHS07 hazard-pictogram PNGs that must be vectorized so they stay crisp at press resolution. Brand + compliance constraints: labels are CMYK with 3 mm bleed at 300 DPI; German label copy and the H/P hazard statements come only from the CSV and must not be paraphrased; the hazard pictograms and barcodes must be sharp vectors, not soft raster; the recreated look must match the existing labels (no new design). Deliverable: the graded photo set, vectorized hazard/barcode graphics, and a set of print-ready CMYK PDFs - one per SKU/variant - merged from the master template, ready to hand to the label printer.
Deliverables (5)
  • Verdafix_labels_print_pdf_set — A set of print-ready CMYK PDFs with 3 mm bleed at ~300 DPI - one per SKU/variant row (12) - exported from the data-merged InDesign master; each carries the correct German copy, H/P statements, product
  • graded_product_photos — The supplied product photos, straightened, auto-toned, white-balance-corrected and consistently cropped/resized to the template's product-photo frame ratio so every label variant looks uniform.
  • verdafix_barcodes.svg — Vectorized EAN-13 barcode graphics for crisp, scannable bars at any label size on press (no soft raster).
  • verdafix_ghs_pictograms.svg — Vectorized GHS02/GHS05/GHS07 hazard pictograms that stay sharp at label print resolution.
  • verdafix_label_run_notes.txt — A short local recipe/handoff note recording the per-variant merge order, the CMYK/bleed/DPI print settings applied, and the asset URNs placed, so the printer and the next revision round can reproduce
Workflow — 21 tool steps
  1. image_auto_straighten — Level the tilted horizon on the MS-Polymer 600 ml product shot.
  2. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the straightened MS-Polymer shot to fix the flat/dull line-side exposure.
  3. image_adjust_color_temperature — Neutralize the cool/blue cast so the grey cartridge reads true on the label.
  4. image_crop_and_resize — Crop/resize to the template's product-photo frame ratio (same target reused for all three photos).
  5. image_apply_auto_tone — Run the Hybrid 290 ml photo through the same pipeline, starting with auto-tone to fix underexposure.
  6. image_adjust_color_temperature — Match white balance to the MS-Polymer shot so the white cartridge stays neutral.
  7. image_crop_and_resize — Crop the Hybrid photo to the matching product-photo frame.
  8. image_auto_straighten — Level the off-level Acryl 290 ml product shot.
  9. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the Acryl shot to the consistent look.
  10. image_crop_and_resize — Crop the Acryl photo to the same product-photo frame so all three match.
  11. image_vectorize — Vectorize the flat EAN-13 barcode PNGs into crisp SVG so the bars stay scannable and sharp on press.
  12. image_vectorize — Vectorize the GHS02/05/07 hazard pictograms so the regulated symbols stay sharp at any label size.
  13. asset_create_folders — Create a Creative Cloud project folder to hold the prepped label-run assets.
  14. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize an upload session for all prepped assets (graded photos + vector barcodes + vector pictograms) into the project folder.
  15. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the upload; returns the asset URNs the CSV rows and data-merge will reference by filename.
  16. asset_add_file — Add the client's authored master label .indd into the project folder so the data-merge can operate on it.
  17. asset_add_file — Add the per-SKU/variant CSV (German copy + H/P statements + image filenames) into the project folder as the merge data source.
  18. document_merge_data_layout — InDesign data-merge: bind each of the 12 SKU/variant rows into the client's authored master .indd (real <<fields>>), placing the graded photo, hazard pictogram
  19. document_render_layout — Export the merged document to print-ready CMYK PDFs with 3 mm bleed at ~300 DPI - one page/file per SKU/variant record.
  20. asset_inline_preview — Pull inline previews of the exported label PDFs to QA that each variant bound the correct German copy, pictogram and barcode before printer handoff.
  21. local_write_recipe_note — Emit a local recipe/handoff note recording per-variant merge order, CMYK/bleed/DPI settings and placed asset URNs so the printer and next revision round can rep
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon flagship: 21 connector calls across three tool groups (image_* raster prep, asset_* storage/preview, document_* InDesign data-merge/export) plus one [L] local emit, 12 distinct Adobe tools - above the >=10 calls / >=6 distinct-tool floor. Difficulty comes from running three separate line-side product photos through a consistent multi-step grade->crop pipeline, vectorizing two regulated graphic sets (barcodes + GHS pictograms) that must stay razor-sharp, staging every asset into a CC folder, then converging the authored master template + a 12-row per-variant CSV + placed assets into a genuine InDesign [T] data-merge that outputs one CMYK-with-bleed PDF per SKU/variant, QA'd via inline preview and logged locally. It respects every hard limit: the label layout is NOT composed by the connector - document_merge_data_layout/document_render_layout only BIND/EXPORT the client's authored master .indd, a supplied input; no generative fill / outpaint / text-to-image / from-scratch design / upscaling / object-removal; image_fill_area is unused; German copy and H/P statements are bound verbatim from the CSV; barcodes/pictograms are only vectorized, not invented.

verdafix_label_master.indd.proxy.txt
content
**Template Description: `verdafix_label_master.indd`**

This is a genuine Adobe InDesign master template for a Verdafix sealant-bottle wrap label, meticulously rebuilt to match the brand's existing legacy aesthetic and styling.

*   **Dimensions:** 90 x 130 mm (portrait orientation)
*   **Bleed:** 3 mm on all edges
*   **Color Intent:** CMYK
*   **Brand Styling:** The template faithfully replicates Verdafix's established visual identity, including existing typography, color palette, logo placement, and overall design layout, ensuring brand consistency with current product lines.

*   **Structure & Layers:** The document is structured with standard InDesign layers for clear content organization, typically including:
    *   `Background/Graphics`: For static design elements and brand patterns.
    *   `Images`: Dedicated frames for visual assets.
    *   `Text`: For all dynamic and static text elements.
    *   `Non-Printing Info`: For guides, crop marks, and internal notes.

*   **Data-Merge Placeholders:** The template features active InDesign data-merge fields for dynamic content population:
    *   `<<product_name>>`
    *   `<<variant>>`
    *   `<<net_content>>`
    *   `<<sku>>`
    *   `<<batch_prefix>>`
    *   `<<h_statements>>` (Hazard statements)
    *   `<<p_statements>>` (Precautionary statements)
    *   `<<usage_de>>` (German usage instructions)

*   **Image Placeholders:** Three dedicated image frames are pre-configured:
    *   Product photo
    *   GHS hazard pictogram
    *   EAN barcode
