AO-85 — Ironclad parts catalog pre-press audit: merge-proof render, spec-row extraction + CSV cross-check, overset/missing-field findings report ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Render our authored InDesign parts-catalog template against the master parts CSV into a proof, then audit it page by page — read back every printed spec row, cross-check it against the source data, and hand back a flagged-pages findings note (each defective page keyed to its issue) plus a findings report calling out overset/clipped part numbers, blank spec cells, and section-count mismatches before we send ~100 pages to print.

💰 ~$120 est.Upwork14 tool calls9 distinct toolsanalyze
grounded in a real brief: Catalog designer needed — B2B electrical/elevator parts distributor (100+ pages)
Full client brief
Ironclad Elevator & Electrical Supply is a wholesale distributor of electrical and elevator replacement parts selling to service technicians, maintenance supervisors, and electrical contractors. Our buyers open this catalog in the field — often as a phone PDF — when they need to spec a replacement part fast and prepare a quote, so a wrong part number, a blank voltage, or a price that got clipped off the edge of the page is a real problem that costs us an order. We are about to send our new parts catalog to the printer (~100 pages across five sections: Contactors & Relays, Elevator Door Operators, Push-Button Fixtures, Fuses & Breakers, and Cables & Connectors). We already have the InDesign catalog template authored with real data-merge fields (<<part_no>>, <<description>>, <<voltage>>, <<amperage>>, <<list_price>>, <<section>>) laid out as one spec card per part, and our master parts spreadsheet (catalog_parts.csv, ~180 SKUs). Before we spend money on the print run we need a pre-press data-integrity and spec-completeness audit — not a redesign. Specifically: (1) run our authored template through the data merge against the CSV and export a proof PDF so we can see exactly what will print; (2) convert/inspect that proof page by page and read back what actually rendered on each card; (3) extract the printed spec rows and cross-check them against the source CSV to catch part numbers that ran overset (long codes clipped by the frame), any blank/empty voltage-amperage-price cells that would leave a technician guessing, and any section whose printed card count does not match the number of SKUs in the CSV for that section; (4) re-inspect the pages carrying a defect in context and deliver a flagged-pages findings note that keys each defective page number to its issue and part_no so we can find each problem card fast; and (5) deliver a written findings report — a table listing every flagged part number, its page, the field at fault, the CSV value vs. the printed value, and a pass/fail per section — plus a one-line go / no-go recommendation for the print run. Page size is US Letter portrait; keep our section order. Deliver the flagged-pages findings note, the findings report table, the report summary, and the whole audit package as a single zip.
Deliverables (5)
  • Ironclad_Catalog_proof.pdf — Proof PDF exported from the data-merged InDesign template against catalog_parts.csv — one spec card per part across the five sections, US Letter portrait — representing exactly what would print. This
  • catalog_audit_flagged_pages.md — Flagged-pages findings note: a short markdown/text index of every proof page the cross-check identified as carrying a defect (overset-clipped part numbers, blank voltage/amperage/price cells, section-
  • catalog_audit_findings.csv — Findings report table: one row per flagged item with columns part_no, page, section, field_at_fault, csv_value, printed_value, issue_type (overset_clip / blank_cell / count_mismatch), plus per-section
  • catalog_audit_report.txt — Human-readable audit summary: total SKUs checked, count of clipped part numbers, count of blank spec cells, the section whose printed card count did not match the CSV, the explicit acceptance-rule ver
  • Ironclad_catalog_audit_package.zip — Single zip bundling the proof PDF, the flagged-pages findings note, the findings CSV, and the report txt for handoff to the client's pre-press contact.
Workflow — 14 tool steps
  1. asset_create_folders — Stand up the CC asset workspace (e.g. /L12/inputs, /proof, /audit) to hold the authored template, CSV, brand note, logo, and the audit outputs.
  2. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize an upload session for the client-supplied inputs: parts_catalog_template.indd, catalog_parts.csv, brand_kit_note.txt, catalog_logo.png.
  3. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the uploads so the authored .indd, the parts CSV, the brand note, and the logo are real CC assets with URNs the merge/render steps can reference.
  4. document_merge_data_layout — [T] InDesign data-merge: bind catalog_parts.csv (~180 rows) into the client-authored parts_catalog_template.indd (real <<part_no>>/<<description>>/<<voltage>>/<
  5. document_render_layout — Export the merged .indd to a US Letter portrait proof PDF — exactly what would go to the printer. This is the artifact the audit will read back, page by page.
  6. document_convert_pdf — Convert/normalize the proof PDF into a per-page-inspectable form so each catalog page's rendered spec cards can be previewed and read back individually (inspect
  7. asset_preview_file — Generate page previews of the normalized proof so the audit can visually inspect each spec card as it will print (look for clipped part codes at frame edges, em
  8. asset_inline_preview — Inline-preview the proof pages to read back the actual printed text of each spec card (printed part_no / voltage / amperage / price strings), producing the 'pri
  9. media_summarize — Summarize/extract the rendered spec rows across all proof pages and align them against the source catalog_parts.csv — surfacing where a printed part_no is trunc
  10. asset_inline_preview — Re-inspect ONLY the pages the cross-check flagged (from extraction_vs_source's defective-page list) by inline-previewing those specific pages of the existing pr
  11. local_write_recipe_note — Emit the flagged-pages findings NOTE: a short index listing, per defective page, the page number, the part_no(s) on it, and the issue_type (overset_clip / blank
  12. local_write_recipe_note — Emit the findings report TABLE: one row per flagged item — part_no, page, section, field_at_fault, csv_value, printed_value, issue_type (overset_clip / blank_ce
  13. local_write_recipe_note — Emit the human-readable audit summary: SKUs checked, clipped-part-number count, blank-spec-cell count, the section with a card-count mismatch, the acceptance-ru
  14. local_zip — Single trailing [L] packaging step: bundle the proof PDF, the flagged-pages findings note, the findings CSV, and the report txt into one zip for handoff to the
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon analyze task: 13 connector calls, 9 distinct Adobe tools across three groups (asset/storage+preview, document/merge+render+convert, media_summarize), above the analyze floor. Difficulty comes from the two-sided cross-check: the agent must first EXECUTE a genuine [T] data-merge + render to produce the exact proof that would print, then INSPECT it back page by page (convert -> preview -> inline-preview -> summarize) and align the printed strings against the source CSV to catch three distinct defect classes — part numbers that ran overset because the authored frame is narrow, blank voltage/amperage/price cells seeded in the data, and a section whose printed card count does not match its CSV SKU count — before emitting a flagged-pages proof extract and a structured findings table with a go/no-go verdict. It respects every hard limit: it is a review/audit that produces a findings report + a flagged-pages proof extract (true analyze), NOT a redesign; no layout is composed by the connector — document_merge_data_layout is [T] and only binds the client's authored .indd (a supplied input), and document_render_layout/document_convert_pdf only export/normalize that genuine file; the flagged-pages extract only SELECTS existing rendered pages (no new annotations drawn — the page->issue mapping lives in the findings CSV); no generative fill, no generative-expand, no object-remova

Client-supplied input assets: 4 total · 1 img · 0 video · 0 audio · 3 data

Images (1)

catalog_logo.png
gemini

Data & other (3)

brand_kit_note.txt
gemini/gemini-2.5-flash
content
Pre-press Guidelines: Ironclad Elevator & Electrical Supply

This note outlines critical brand and pre-press specifications for all Ironclad Elevator & Electrical Supply print assets. Our official brand colors are Industrial Navy (#1B2A41) and Safety-Orange Accent (#E8621F). Please utilize `Ironclad_Logo_Primary_CMYK.ai` for our primary logo, ensuring it is always used in its original vector format. All print layouts should be designed for US Letter portrait (8.5" x 11") dimensions.

For all product listings, catalogs, and data sheets, the following section order is mandatory: Contactors & Relays; Elevator Door Operators; Push-Button Fixtures; Fuses & Breakers; and Cables & Connectors. This sequence must be strictly adhered to across all materials.

Final print proofs will be audited against the following non-negotiable acceptance criteria: no part number may be clipped, and no voltage/amperage/price cell may be blank on the printed card. Any proof failing these specific checks will be rejected and require immediate correction.

parts_catalog_template.indd.proxy.txt
content
**Template Description: `parts_catalog_template.indd`**

**File Type:** Adobe InDesign Document (.indd)

**Purpose:** This template is designed as a US Letter portrait B2B parts catalog, optimized for data merge to generate individual part specification cards.

**Dimensions:** US Letter (8.5" x 11"), portrait orientation.

**Structure & Layout:**
The template is structured to display multiple "spec cards" per page, with each card detailing a single part. Content is logically grouped under five primary section headers:
1.  Contactors & Relays
2.  Elevator Door Operators
3.  Push-Button Fixtures
4.  Fuses & Breakers
5.  Cables & Connectors

A consistent running header and footer are present across all pages. Each individual part card includes a dedicated, small image frame for a product photograph.

**Layers:**
Standard InDesign layers are employed for organization, typically including:
*   **Text:** For all dynamic and static text elements.
*   **Images:** For product photo frames and any static graphics.
*   **Background/Graphics:** For layout elements, rules, and background colors.
*   **Headers/Footers:** For recurring page elements.

**Placeholders (Data Merge Fields):**
The template is authored with genuine Adobe InDesign data merge fields, ensuring dynamic content population from an external data source. These fields include:
*   `<<part_no>>`
*   `<<description>>`
*   `<<voltage>>`
*   `<<amperage>>`
*   `<<list_price>>`
*   `<<section>>` (used for section grouping/navigation)

**Brand Styling:**
The design follows a clean, professional, and highly functional B2B aesthetic. Emphasis is placed on clear typography, organized presentation of technical specifications, and readability, characteristic of industrial parts catalogs.

**Critical Audit Finding (Intentional Authoring Defect):**
The text frame specifically designated for the `<<part_no>>` data merge field has been deliberately authored with a narrow width. Crucially, auto-fit options (such as "Auto-Fit Frame to Content" or similar) are *not* enabled for this frame. This intentional design flaw will cause text overset and clipping for any alphanumeric part codes that exceed the frame's limited width when data is merged or the document is exported, providing a genuine finding for content overflow during the audit process.

AO-86 — Amberleaf Tea Studio: vectorize two AI-generated tea-brand wordmarks + one ornamental crest into production-ready SVGs and derive a matching stand-up-pouch front-label base panel ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Clean and de-skew the client's three AI-generated tea-brand raster logos, trace them into crisp scalable SVG masters, derive one-color and halftone print variants of the crest, and produce a matching front-facing stand-up-pouch label base panel from the supplied pouch mockup.

💰 ~$120 est.Freelancer.com26 tool calls15 distinct toolseditlong-horizon
grounded in a real brief: Vectorize Logos & Packaging
Full client brief
Amberleaf Tea Studio, a small independent tea-branding consultancy, is prepping two of its house tea brands for a production run and needs the artwork made print-ready. The studio has already generated the concept artwork with an AI image tool but only has flat raster PNGs: a wordmark lockup for 'Golden Chai Co.' (warm amber-and-cream with a subtle gold gradient), a wordmark lockup for 'Silverleaf Green' (cool sage-green with a fine leaf glyph), and one ornamental 'crest' mark (a symmetrical scrollwork badge with a small tea-leaf motif meant to sit on both brands' packaging). All three rasters have the usual AI-export problems: soft anti-aliased edges, a faint off-white textured card behind the art, mild luminance noise, and each is tilted a couple of degrees off level. None of these can go to a printer as-is because they do not scale and have no transparency. The studio also supplies a flat mockup image of a blank stand-up coffee/tea pouch it wants to use as the packaging shape. The job: (1) ingest all three raster logos and get clean working copies; auto-straighten each tilted mark and tonally clean it so edges are true and the near-white card is even; (2) crop each mark tight to its true artwork bounds and knock the background to transparent so the trace has no halo; (3) VECTORIZE each cleaned mark into a crisp, fully-scalable SVG master while preserving every colour and the gold gradient exactly as in the concept — these three SVGs are the primary editable/print-ready vector deliverables (AI/EPS/PDF are the same master exported to those formats); (4) from the ornamental crest, derive a single-color / knockout vector (crest silhouette solid-filled) for one-colour foil, embossing and small favicon use, and a halftone 'print-screen' treatment for merch and kraft-paper packaging; (5) produce the 'matching front-facing product label' the brief asks for: crop the supplied stand-up-pouch mockup to just its flat front panel and lay a clean solid kraft-tone base band on that panel, delivering the print-ready front-label base panel that fits the new vector style — the cleaned Golden Chai wordmark + crest are dropped onto it by the studio in the layered vector files; (6) recommend a brand-safe font pairing that matches the wordmark style so the studio can extend the identity across both brands; (7) upload every produced deliverable into a tidy Creative Cloud hand-off folder and assemble them onto a single Firefly board deep-link so the studio can approve the vectors and the pouch label in one place. Preserve every colour, gradient and detail exactly as in the original concept while improving line sharpness and overall balance; keep the two brands visually distinct.
Deliverables (8)
  • golden_chai_wordmark.svg — Clean full-colour vector trace of the Golden Chai Co. wordmark lockup, gold/amber gradient and cream keyline preserved, transparent background, crisp scalable paths; the primary editable master (AI/EP
  • silverleaf_green_wordmark.svg — Clean full-colour vector trace of the Silverleaf Green wordmark lockup, sage palette + leaf glyph preserved, transparent background, scalable paths; second brand master (AI/EPS/PDF derive from it).
  • amberleaf_crest.svg — Clean vector trace of the ornamental scrollwork crest, filigree sharpened, transparent background, scalable paths; the shared ornamental mark master.
  • amberleaf_crest_onecolor_knockout.svg — Single-colour knockout/silhouette vector of the crest (solid fill on flattened field) for one-colour foil, embossing, small favicon and single-colour print use.
  • amberleaf_crest_halftone.png — Halftone / print-screen treatment of the crest, hi-res PNG for merch and kraft-paper packaging where a dot-screen reads better than solid line art.
  • golden_chai_pouch_front_label_base.png — The 'matching front-facing product label' base panel: the supplied stand-up-pouch mockup cropped to its flat front panel with a clean solid kraft-tone base band laid on it (image_fill_area solid fill
  • amberleaf_font_pairing.json — Recommended brand-safe font family + heading/body pairing matching the wordmark style, from font_recommend, so the studio can extend the identity across both brands.
  • Amberleaf_vector_kit_folder + review_board — Organized Creative Cloud hand-off folder containing every produced deliverable, plus a Firefly board deep-link assembling the three SVG masters, the knockout + halftone crest variants and the pouch fr
Workflow — 26 tool steps
  1. asset_initialize_file_upload — Begin uploading the studio's four supplied rasters (two wordmarks, the crest, the pouch mockup) into Creative Cloud storage to start the pipeline.
  2. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the upload to stable CC asset URIs for the four rasters; every later step consumes a prior output. This is the single initial asset load.
  3. image_auto_straighten — Correct the ~2.5deg clockwise tilt on the Golden Chai wordmark so the baseline is level — straight art traces cleaner.
  4. image_adjust_brightness_and_contrast — Boost contrast and even the mild AI lighting so the amber letterforms separate cleanly from the off-white card — sharper edges for the vectorizer, gradient pres
  5. image_crop_to_bounds — Trim the surrounding card so the canvas hugs the Golden Chai artwork, removing wasted background before knocking it out.
  6. image_remove_background — Knock the faint textured card to true transparency so the trace has no halo/fringe around the letterforms.
  7. image_vectorize — Vectorize the cleaned Golden Chai wordmark into a crisp full-colour SVG master, preserving the gold/amber gradient and cream keyline (deliverable; AI/EPS/PDF de
  8. image_auto_straighten — Straighten the Silverleaf Green wordmark, which is tilted ~3deg the OTHER way, so its baseline is level for a clean trace.
  9. image_adjust_brightness_and_contrast — Contrast-clean the Silverleaf wordmark so the sage green separates crisply from its cool off-white card.
  10. image_crop_to_bounds — Crop the Silverleaf lockup tight to its true artwork bounds.
  11. image_remove_background — Knock the Silverleaf card to transparency for a halo-free trace.
  12. image_vectorize — Vectorize the cleaned Silverleaf Green wordmark into a scalable SVG master, sage palette + leaf glyph preserved (deliverable).
  13. image_auto_straighten — Level the ornamental crest (~2deg off) so the symmetrical scrollwork traces true.
  14. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the crest to maximize the black/amber line-art contrast against the card so the fine filigree traces sharply.
  15. image_crop_to_bounds — Crop the badge tight to its bounds.
  16. image_remove_background — Knock the crest card to transparency; this cleaned crest is the reusable hub for the master trace + the knockout + halftone variants.
  17. image_vectorize — Vectorize the cleaned crest into a scalable SVG master with the filigree sharpened (deliverable).
  18. image_apply_color_overlay — Flatten the isolated crest to a single solid colour (color overlay over the transparent cut-out) to build the one-colour knockout source — a recolor/flatten, no
  19. image_vectorize — Vectorize the solid-colour crest into the one-colour knockout SVG for foil, embossing, single-colour print and small favicons (deliverable).
  20. image_apply_halftone — Apply a whole-image halftone / print-screen treatment to the isolated crest for merch and kraft-paper packaging (deliverable).
  21. image_crop_to_bounds — Crop the supplied stand-up-pouch mockup down to just its flat front panel — the printable label area for the front-facing product label base.
  22. image_fill_area — Lay a clean SOLID kraft-tone base band on the cropped pouch front panel to produce the print-ready front-label base panel that fits the new vector style. Solid-
  23. font_recommend — Recommend a brand-safe font family + heading/body pairing matching the wordmark style so the studio can extend the identity across both brands (deliverable).
  24. asset_create_folders — Create an organized CC hand-off folder (Amberleaf_Vector_Kit) to collect all deliverables neatly for the studio.
  25. asset_copy_assets — Copy every produced vector + raster deliverable into the hand-off folder so the studio receives one tidy, layered, print-ready package.
  26. create_firefly_board — Assemble all produced deliverables onto a single Firefly board deep-link for one-click studio sign-off on the vectors and the pouch label.
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon and wide-mix: 26 connector calls, 15 distinct Adobe tools across three groups (asset ingest/storage, the image_* raster/vector family, and font/board). Ordering matters — each mark must be straightened, tonally cleaned, cropped and background-knocked BEFORE vectorizing or the trace inherits the AI noise/tilt/halo and fails the 'clean, fully-editable, scale without loss' bar; a naive agent that vectorizes the raw PNGs fails. It runs three separate marks through their own consistent clean->trace pipelines (two wordmarks with opposite tilts + one filigree crest), then reuses the cleaned crest as a hub to spawn the one-colour knockout (color_overlay -> re-vectorize) and the halftone variant, and produces the front-facing label base from a crop + solid-fill branch. It respects every hard limit: no generative fill / object-removal / text-to-image / reframe-crop / upscaling / from-scratch layout / image-onto-image compositing; image_fill_area is used solid-colour ONLY (kraft base band, no generated content, no placement of other marks); the vector masters come solely from image_vectorize (raster->SVG), and AI/EPS/PDF are honestly described as export formats of that same master rather than authored by a from-scratch vector tool. The branch-and-converge topology (three cleaned marks fanning into five vector/raster deliverables plus a label base, then collected into a folder

Client-supplied input assets: 4 total · 4 img · 0 video · 0 audio · 0 data

AO-87 — Little Lighthouse charity tee: rebuild two raster concept mockups into clean 3-spot-color screen-print separations (front chest + full back) as print-ready vector ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Rebuild the client's two flat raster tee concepts (front chest + full back) into clean, production-ready screen-print separations: isolate each of the 3 approved brand spot colors and force it to its exact PANTONE hex as a solid flat plate, vectorize every color layer, and export per-color separation vectors plus a combined proof PDF.

💰 $5–$17/hrUpwork36 tool calls10 distinct toolseditlong-horizon
grounded in a real brief: Vector Apparel Designer Needed to Create Screen Print Ready T-Shirt Artwork
Full client brief
Little Lighthouse Fund, a children's charity, is running a fundraising T-shirt campaign and needs its two established concepts turned into production-ready screen-print artwork. The design lead has handed off two flat, high-contrast raster concept mockups exported from their design tool — a front-chest lockup (a stylized lighthouse-and-wave badge with the words LITTLE LIGHTHOUSE) and a full-back design (a larger lighthouse scene with a supporter tagline) — plus a one-page brand-guidelines PDF that pins the campaign palette to exactly three approved spot colors: Beacon Red (PANTONE 186 C, hex #C8102E), Harbor Navy (PANTONE 2758 C, hex #1B2A5B), and Sand Cream (PANTONE 7401 C, hex #F0E6C8). The printer's constraints are strict and non-negotiable: MAXIMUM 3 spot colors per shirt, NO gradients, NO halftones, all lettering already flattened into the raster (no live fonts needed), and each color must sit on its own clean, solid separation so it can be burned to a screen. The as-supplied concept files are muddy: the flat art has slight anti-aliased fringing at the edges, a busy off-white paper-texture background bleeding into the art, and colors that drift a few shades off the approved PANTONE hexes. The job: (1) for BOTH the front and back art, isolate each of the three brand regions by prompt selection and force it to the exact approved spot-color hex as a solid flat fill (no gradient, no tint ramp) so each plate is press-clean; (2) drop the paper-texture background to transparent so only the artwork remains; (3) vectorize each cleaned, single-color layer into crisp scalable vector so the plates hold a hard edge at full front/back print size; (4) deliver one separation vector per color per placement (6 plates total: front R/N/C + back R/N/C), a transparent-background composite vector of each full design, and a single combined proof PDF the printer can check color count and registration against; (5) stage everything into a tidy CC folder and drop a print-shop recipe note listing the PANTONE numbers, placement sizes, and mesh guidance so the printer can run it without a call. Front chest prints ~10cm wide, full back ~30cm wide. Every color stays within the approved 3-spot palette; nothing new is drawn.
Deliverables (10)
  • front_sep_red_vector.svg — Front-chest Beacon Red (PANTONE 186 C, #C8102E) separation plate as a single-color vector SVG on transparent background, sized for ~10cm print width. Solid flat fill only — no gradient, no halftone —
  • front_sep_navy_vector.svg — Front-chest Harbor Navy (PANTONE 2758 C, #1B2A5B) separation plate as a single-color vector SVG on transparent background, sized for ~10cm print width. Solid flat fill only, its own screen.
  • front_sep_cream_vector.svg — Front-chest Sand Cream (PANTONE 7401 C, #F0E6C8) separation plate as a single-color vector SVG on transparent background, sized for ~10cm print width, derived as NOT(red+navy). Solid flat fill only, i
  • back_sep_red_vector.svg — Full-back Beacon Red (PANTONE 186 C, #C8102E) separation plate as a single-color vector SVG on transparent background, sized for ~30cm print width. Solid flat fill only, its own screen.
  • back_sep_navy_vector.svg — Full-back Harbor Navy (PANTONE 2758 C, #1B2A5B) separation plate as a single-color vector SVG on transparent background, sized for ~30cm print width. Solid flat fill only, its own screen.
  • back_sep_cream_vector.svg — Full-back Sand Cream (PANTONE 7401 C, #F0E6C8) separation plate as a single-color vector SVG on transparent background, sized for ~30cm print width, derived as NOT(red+navy). Solid flat fill only, its
  • front_composite.svg — Transparent-background composite vector SVG of the full cleaned three-color front-chest design (all three approved spot colors in registration), for client approval/placement reference at ~10cm print
  • back_composite.svg — Transparent-background composite vector SVG of the full cleaned three-color full-back design (all three approved spot colors in registration), for client approval/placement reference at ~30cm print wi
  • Little_Lighthouse_seps_proof.pdf — Two-page print-ready proof PDF (front composite on page 1, back composite on page 2) rendered from the two full-design composite vectors, each page showing all three approved spot colors in registrati
  • printshop_recipe_note.txt — Plain-text print-shop hand-off note listing the three approved PANTONE numbers + hexes (Beacon Red 186 C #C8102E, Harbor Navy 2758 C #1B2A5B, Sand Cream 7401 C #F0E6C8), per-color separation order map
Workflow — 36 tool steps
  1. asset_create_folders — Create the tidy CC project folder structure (separations + composites + proof + hand-off) so every plate produced downstream has a home for the printer hand-off
  2. image_select_by_prompt — Begin the front placement: select the red region of the front-chest art to isolate the Beacon Red plate.
  3. image_fill_area — Solid-fill the front red region with exact Beacon Red #C8102E to force it to the approved spot color (flat fill, no gradient).
  4. image_select_by_prompt — Select the navy region of the front art for its Harbor Navy plate.
  5. image_fill_area — Solid-fill the front navy region with exact Harbor Navy #1B2A5B.
  6. image_select_by_prompt — Select the cream/sand region of the front art to isolate its third plate.
  7. image_fill_area — Solid-fill the front cream region with exact Sand Cream #F0E6C8 so all three front colors are forced to their approved flat spot hexes.
  8. image_adjust_single_color_saturation — Knock down the saturation of the off-white paper-texture band only (not the three brand colors), making the background a neutral near-white so it lifts cleanly
  9. image_remove_background — Drop the paper-texture background on the front art to transparent so only the three-color artwork remains.
  10. image_select_by_prompt — Isolate the Beacon Red areas on the cleaned front art for its separation plate.
  11. image_vectorize — Vectorize the front Beacon Red layer into a solid vector plate sized for ~10cm print width.
  12. image_select_by_prompt — Isolate the Harbor Navy areas of the cleaned front art.
  13. image_vectorize — Vectorize the front Harbor Navy layer into a solid vector separation.
  14. image_select_by_prompt — Select the two SATURATED brand colors together — 'all the bright-red and dark-navy artwork marks' — a robust high-contrast prompt, rather than trying to pick th
  15. image_invert_selection — Invert the red+navy selection so the remaining opaque artwork pixels — the Sand Cream fill — become the third mask. Deriving cream geometrically as NOT(red+navy
  16. image_vectorize — Vectorize the flat Sand Cream layer (the inverted-mask region) into the third solid front separation plate.
  17. image_vectorize — Vectorize the full cleaned three-color front art into one transparent-background composite SVG for client approval/placement reference.
  18. image_select_by_prompt — Begin the back placement: select its red region to isolate the Beacon Red plate.
  19. image_fill_area — Solid-fill the back red region with exact Beacon Red #C8102E to match the front's approved spot color.
  20. image_select_by_prompt — Select the navy region of the back art for its Harbor Navy plate.
  21. image_fill_area — Solid-fill the back navy region with exact Harbor Navy #1B2A5B.
  22. image_select_by_prompt — Select the cream/sand region of the back art to isolate its third plate.
  23. image_fill_area — Solid-fill the back cream region with exact Sand Cream #F0E6C8 so all three back colors are forced to their approved flat spot hexes.
  24. image_remove_background — Drop the paper-texture background on the back art to transparent so only the three-color artwork remains.
  25. image_select_by_prompt — Isolate the Beacon Red areas on the cleaned back art for its separation plate.
  26. image_vectorize — Vectorize the back Beacon Red layer into a solid vector plate sized for ~30cm print width.
  27. image_select_by_prompt — Isolate the Harbor Navy areas of the back art.
  28. image_vectorize — Vectorize the back Harbor Navy layer into a solid vector separation.
  29. image_select_by_prompt — Select the two SATURATED brand colors together — 'all the bright-red and dark-navy artwork marks' — a robust high-contrast prompt, rather than trying to pick th
  30. image_invert_selection — Invert the red+navy selection so the remaining opaque artwork pixels — the Sand Cream fill — become the third mask, deriving cream geometrically as NOT(red+navy
  31. image_vectorize — Vectorize the back Sand Cream layer (the inverted-mask region) into the third back separation plate.
  32. image_vectorize — Vectorize the full cleaned three-color back art into one transparent-background composite SVG.
  33. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize an upload session for all six separation plates plus the two composite SVGs into the staged project folders.
  34. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the upload; returns CC asset URNs for the plates/composites so they live in the tidy printer hand-off folder.
  35. document_render_vector — Render the two full-design composite vectors (front + back) into one two-page proof PDF — one placement per page — using document_render_vector's native per-art
  36. local_write_recipe_note — Emit the print-shop hand-off note: the three PANTONE numbers+hexes, front ~10cm / back ~30cm sizes, per-color separation order (mapping each uploaded separation
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon flagship: 36 connector calls, 10 distinct Adobe-server tools plus a local recipe emit — well above the >=10 calls / >=6 distinct-tool floor. Difficulty comes from running TWO separate raster concepts through a full spot-color separation pipeline: isolating each of three brand colors by prompt selection, FORCING each to its exact approved PANTONE hex as a SOLID flat fill (not a gradient or tint), desaturating and stripping a paper-texture background, then vectorizing every single-color layer into its own press-clean plate — six separation vectors plus two composites plus a two-page proof PDF. The near-white Sand Cream plate is derived geometrically as NOT(red+navy) — select the two saturated marks then image_invert_selection — rather than by an unreliable prompt-select of near-white on transparency. It respects every hard limit: image_fill_area is used only for SOLID flat fills (the entire point of spot-color seps), image_adjust_single_color_saturation only reduces the paper band's saturation (no recolor of brand art), image_invert_selection only flips an existing mask, image_remove_background only lifts to transparent, image_vectorize is genuine raster->vector, and no color outside the printer-approved 3-spot palette is introduced. document_render_vector exports the two composite vectors into a two-page proof (one placement per page) — no multi-file montage and no

Client-supplied input assets: 4 total · 2 img · 0 video · 0 audio · 2 data

Images (2)

concept_front_chest.png
gemini
concept_full_back.png
gemini

Data & other (2)

brand_guidelines_palette.pdf
printer_specsheet.txt
content
Max 3 spot colors.
Deliver each color as its own solid separation (vector preferred).
No gradients, no halftones, no 4-color process.
Fonts must be outlined/flattened.
Front chest max 10cm wide, full back max 30cm wide.
Provide a combined color proof PDF for registration check.
Mesh: 156 for line art.
Underbase not required on light garments.

AO-88 — Ridgeline Rovers FC crest: clean up a phone-shot patch photo, vectorize the emblem, and build a factory-ready embroidery/patch vector package ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Clean up the club's phone photo of a stitched sample crest, vectorize the emblem into razor-sharp scalable art at 3-6 inch patch size, reduce it to flat spot-color separations, and hand back a factory-ready SVG plus exported vector masters and a stitch-color reference gathered into a shared review folder.

💰 ~$120 est.Freelancer.com27 tool calls17 distinct toolseditlong-horizon
grounded in a real brief: Realistic Textile Embroidery Design
Full client brief
Ridgeline Rovers FC, an amateur mountain-town football (soccer) club, is ordering embroidered crest patches and sleeve badges from a factory and needs its emblem turned into proper production-ready vector artwork first. The only artwork the club has is a phone photo of a sample patch that a previous designer stitched off an early draft (crest_sample_patch.jpg) — a shield-shaped crest with a stylized mountain-ridge silhouette, a football, the letters "RRFC", and the founding year "EST. 2014" on a banner ribbon, stitched in four thread colors (forest green, cream, slate grey, black outline) on a heathered fabric backing. The photo is a real hand-held shot: tilted about 3 degrees, dull and slightly underexposed, a cool/green color cast from the fabric, and the twill weave texture shows across every color so the edges look fuzzy. That texture and cast make it useless for a factory as-is.

The job: (1) clean the reference so it can be traced — deskew it level, auto-tone to fix the flat exposure, neutralize the green fabric cast so the four thread colors read true, then push contrast hard to crush the woven-fabric texture and drive every element toward a clean, flat, high-contrast silhouette; knock the heathered backing to transparent so the trace has no halo. (2) VECTORIZE the cleaned crest into a clean, scalable SVG — this is the master emblem, and it must stay razor-sharp when the factory scales it to the requested medium 3-6 inch patch size. (3) Because embroidery and woven patches need each color as one flat solid thread, reduce the emblem to FLAT SPOT SEPARATIONS: go element by element, select each color region, and lock it to a single solid ink from the club's 4-color palette (no gradients, no anti-aliased blends), then pull a single-color BLACK OUTLINE plate the factory uses for the merrowed-border stitch path, and re-vectorize the flattened art. (4) Deliver a one-color KNOCKOUT (solid emblem silhouette) the factory can use to cut the twill/felt backing. (5) Recommend a clean condensed athletic font for the "RRFC" / "EST. 2014" lettering so the club can extend the wordmark later, and write it up alongside the exact thread colors as a reference sheet. (6) Export the artwork to vector masters (PDF/PNG/SVG) from a genuine authored .ai and gather everything into a shared review folder so the club can approve the crest and the exact thread colors before the factory runs it.

Palette: forest green #2E5A3A, cream #F2E9D0, slate grey #6B7078, black #111111 outline. Deliverables: master crest SVG, flat spot-separated SVG, black-outline plate, one-color knockout, exported vector masters, a stitch-color / thread reference sheet, and a shared review-folder link. Stitch digitizing (.dst/.pes) is handled by the factory from these vector masters — it is not part
Deliverables (7)
  • ridgeline_crest_master.svg — Clean vectorized master crest (full shield: mountain ridge + football + RRFC + EST. 2014 banner) as a scalable SVG with crisp continuous paths and transparent ground, razor-sharp at the requested 3-6
  • ridgeline_crest_spot_separated.svg — Flat spot-separated SVG: every element reduced to a single solid ink from the 4-color palette (no gradients, no anti-aliased blends) so each color prints/stitches as one flat thread — the factory-read
  • ridgeline_crest_black_outline_plate.png — Single-color black outline plate (solid black emblem linework on transparent) giving the factory the merrowed-border and detail-stitch path; hi-res PNG.
  • ridgeline_crest_knockout.png — One-color solid knockout of the whole shield silhouette (flat fill on transparent) the factory uses to cut the twill/felt patch backing to shape.
  • ridgeline_crest_vector_masters — Exported vector masters (PDF + PNG + SVG) rendered from the genuine authored .ai built off the traced SVGs — for the factory press and for club use.
  • ridgeline_thread_color_reference.md — Thread/stitch-color reference sheet: the 4 palette inks with hex values mapped to element (ridge=green, ball/panels=cream+black, RRFC/banner=slate/black outline) plus the recommended condensed athleti
  • ridgeline_crest_review_folder — Shared Adobe CC review folder gathering the master crest SVG, spot-separated version, black-outline plate, knockout, exported vector masters and thread-color reference with an inline preview, so the c
Workflow — 27 tool steps
  1. asset_initialize_file_upload — Open an upload session to bring the club's phone photo of the stitched sample patch into Adobe CC storage.
  2. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the upload to a stable presigned asset URI that the image_* tools can consume as the working reference.
  3. image_auto_straighten — Correct the ~3 degree hand-held tilt so the shield sits level and the banner baseline is true before tracing — straight geometry traces cleaner.
  4. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the dull, mildly underexposed photo so the four thread colors gain clean separation in value.
  5. image_adjust_color_temperature — Neutralize the cool/green fabric color cast so forest green, cream, slate grey and black each read true rather than tinted by the backing.
  6. image_adjust_brightness_and_contrast — Push contrast hard to crush the woven twill/satin texture across every color and drive each element toward a flat high-contrast silhouette — critical so vectori
  7. image_remove_background — Knock the heathered fabric backing to true transparent so the trace has no halo/fringe around the shield edge (->transparent PNG only, no reconstruction).
  8. image_vectorize — CORE DELIVERABLE 1: raster->clean SVG trace of the cleaned crest = MASTER emblem, razor-sharp for the 3-6 inch patch scale; basis for the .ai/PDF/PNG exports.
  9. image_select_by_prompt — SPOT-SEPARATION PASS: select the forest-green mountain-ridge region so it can be locked to one flat ink.
  10. image_fill_area — Solid-fill the selected ridge region with the exact palette green #2E5A3A (SOLID color only) so it becomes one flat embroidery thread with no gradient or anti-a
  11. image_select_by_prompt — Select the cream football/panel region on the partially-flattened art to lock the next thread color.
  12. image_fill_area — Solid-fill the cream region with #F2E9D0 (SOLID color only) — second flat spot ink locked.
  13. image_select_by_prompt — Select the slate-grey RRFC lettering / banner fill so it too reduces to a single flat ink.
  14. image_fill_area — Solid-fill the slate region with #6B7078 (SOLID color only) — third flat spot ink locked; the black outline remains, giving a clean 4-flat-ink plate ready to re
  15. image_vectorize — CORE DELIVERABLE 2: re-vectorize the flattened 4-flat-ink plate -> factory-ready spot-separated SVG where each color is one solid thread.
  16. image_select_by_prompt — Select just the black outline / detail linework of the emblem to build the border-stitch plate.
  17. image_invert_selection — Invert so everything except the black linework is targeted, ready to clear it away and leave only the outline plate.
  18. image_fill_area — Solid-fill the inverted (non-outline) area to clear/transparent leaving the solid black outline plate (SOLID fill only) -> the merrowed-border / detail-stitch p
  19. image_apply_color_overlay — Apply a solid black color overlay to the whole cleaned shield silhouette -> one-color KNOCKOUT the factory uses to cut the twill/felt patch backing to the shiel
  20. font_recommend — Recommend a condensed athletic font pairing for the RRFC / EST. 2014 lettering so the club can extend the wordmark later (returns typeface suggestions only, no
  21. local_write_recipe_note — Write up the thread/stitch-color reference sheet: the 4 palette hex inks mapped to elements (ridge=green, ball/panels=cream+black, RRFC/banner=slate/black outli
  22. asset_initialize_file_upload — Open an upload session to bring in the genuine authored .ai vector master (built off the traced ridgeline_crest_master.svg / spot-separated SVG in Illustrator)
  23. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the authored .ai upload to a stable URI for export; the vector geometry derives entirely from the traced SVGs above (no from-scratch drawing).
  24. document_render_vector — Export the genuine authored .ai to the vector masters (PDF + PNG + SVG) for the factory press and club use. document_render_vector only EXPORTS the supplied .ai
  25. asset_create_folders — Create a shared review folder in Adobe CC storage to gather all the produced deliverables for club sign-off.
  26. asset_copy_assets — Copy the master crest, spot-separated SVG, black-outline plate, knockout, exported vector masters and thread-color reference into the review folder so everythin
  27. asset_inline_preview — Generate an inline preview / deep-link of the populated review folder so the club can approve the emblem and exact thread colors before the factory runs the emb
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon and wide-mix: 27 connector calls, 18 distinct tools (17 Adobe + 1 local) across four groups (asset ingest/export/folder, the image_* raster/vector family, font, and a local recipe note). Difficulty comes from correct ordering (deskew + tone + cast-neutralize + contrast-crush + background-knock all BEFORE vectorize, so the trace follows emblem edges and not the woven twill texture), and from a genuinely non-trivial spot-separation sub-chain — three sequential select_by_prompt -> solid fill_area passes that lock each of three thread colors to one flat ink, then a re-vectorize, plus an invert-based black-outline-plate extraction. It stays strictly inside connector limits: no generative fill, no AI object removal / inpainting, no text-to-image or from-scratch letterform design, no upscaling, no compositing, no layout composition; image_fill_area is used strictly as solid-color flattening (the ONLY honest non-generative way to enforce flat spot inks for embroidery); the vector deliverables come from image_vectorize, and the exported masters come from document_render_vector EXPORTING a genuine authored .ai supplied as an uploaded input, not composed by the connector. The final assembly for club review uses only in-palette asset tools (create_folders + copy_assets + inline_preview) to gather and share the deliverables — no board/Express-widget tool. The embroidery stitch-

Client-supplied input assets: 2 total · 1 img · 0 video · 0 audio · 1 data

crest_sample_patch.jpg

Data & other (1)

ridgeline_crest.ai.proxy.txt
content
**Template File Description: `ridgeline_crest.ai`**

This Adobe Illustrator (.ai) document serves as the production-ready vector master for the Ridgeline Crest emblem, intended primarily for patch manufacturing. It features vector geometry hand-assembled from the traced `ridgeline_crest_master.svg` and its 4-flat-ink spot-separated counterpart, `ridgeline_crest_spot_separated.svg`.

**Structure & Content:**
The file contains multiple artboards, each precisely sized for typical 3-6 inch patch dimensions (e.g., 4x4 inches). These artboards host the complete vector paths of the emblem, presenting both the master version and its pre-separated spot color variations. No new artwork is drawn within this file; its purpose is to consolidate and arrange the traced paths for final export.

**Layers & Placeholders:**
The artwork is structured with vector paths organized for clean output and color separation. The emblem design is complete, and the file contains no empty placeholders or editable text fields for new content.

**Dimensions:**
Artboards are configured for physical output dimensions ranging from 3 to 6 inches, suitable for various patch sizes.

**Brand Styling:**
The emblem's styling is defined by a specific four-spot-color palette, critical for consistent manufacturing. These are implemented as named swatches within the document:
*   **Spot Color 1:** `#2E5A3A` (Dark Green)
*   **Spot Color 2:** `#F2E9D0` (Off-White/Cream)
*   **Spot Color 3:** `#6B7078` (Mid-Grey)
*   **Spot Color 4:** `#111111` (Black)

AO-89 — LUMIÈRE MÉNAGE monochrome beauty brand mark: vectorize the supplied concept + build the full reversible variant system (master SVG, avatar, stacked lockup, one-color/mono-tint knockouts, print PNGs) with a font recommendation, palette recipe, and usage board ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Clean and vectorize LUMIÈRE MÉNAGE's supplied flat monochrome logo concept into an editable SVG master, then assemble the full reversible variant system — black-on-white master, white-on-dark knockout, avatar/PFP crop, stacked lockup, one-color black print PNG, a mono-tint alt, a monochrome palette recipe, a font recommendation, and a usage board — all scalable from avatar to signage.

💰 $25Freelancer.com17 tool calls13 distinct toolscreatelong-horizon
grounded in a real brief: Modern Monochrome Logo Design
Full client brief
LUMIÈRE MÉNAGE is a fictional modern clean-beauty and skincare label preparing to launch across social, e-commerce, unit cartons, and large in-store signage. After an internal round we have approved ONE monochrome logo concept and now need it PRODUCTIONIZED into a fully editable, reversible brand-mark system — this is a finishing/assembly job, not a from-scratch design round.

We supply a single flat, high-contrast raster export of the approved concept: a crisp geometric monogram ("LM" resolved into an abstract, symmetric negative-space shape) sitting above the wordmark "LUMIÈRE MÉNAGE" set in a clean modern condensed sans, rendered pure black on a near-white background, no gradients, no illustration, no flourishes. The concept scan is slightly skewed and the background is an off-white (#F5F5F3), not true white.

Deliverables, all chained in the Adobe connector:
1) Ingest our supplied concept PNG and get a clean working copy — straighten the slight skew and auto-tone so the black is dense and the edges are true.
2) Knock the off-white background out to true transparency so the trace has no halo/fringe.
3) Vectorize the cleaned lockup into a clean, fully editable SVG — this is the MASTER vector (basis for the requested AI/SVG editable file + high-res PNG exports).
4) Produce the REVERSED knockout: a white-on-dark version of the mark that reads perfectly when reversed out on dark packaging and signage (the brief's key requirement).
5) Derive a tight AVATAR / social-PFP version from the monogram alone (square, centered) that stays recognizable at avatar size.
6) Build a STACKED lockup crop for narrow / vertical placements.
7) Produce a ONE-COLOR pure-black print PNG (for single-color foil/deboss and small-format print).
8) Produce a single MONOCHROMATIC brand-tint alt (one hue only — no multicolor, honoring "clean monochrome palette") for tonal applications.
9) Recommend a brand font family + heading/supporting pairing that matches the modern condensed wordmark tone.
10) Emit a MONOCHROME palette recipe (black / dark grey / off-white / paper hex callouts) as the color-palette deliverable.
11) Assemble every produced variation into a usage board deep-link the founder can review and sign off in one place.

Hard constraints (honor the brief AND connector ground truth): stay strictly monochrome — no gradients, no multicolor, no vintage/distress treatment. Everything must reverse cleanly, scale from a social-media avatar up to large signage, and arrive as an editable vector plus high-res PNGs. No from-scratch logo drawing, no generative fill, no AI object removal, no compositing — the supplied concept is the single source of truth and every deliverable is derived from it by chaining. The requested AI/EPS export and the printed spec sheet are human/format-exp
Deliverables (9)
  • lumiere_menage_master.svg — Clean, fully editable vectorized MASTER of the full monochrome lockup (LM monogram + LUMIÈRE MÉNAGE wordmark + tagline) as scalable SVG with crisp geometric paths on transparent background; the print/
  • lumiere_menage_reversed_white_on_dark.png — Reversed knockout: the mark converted to solid white (via monochromatic tint, high-lightness single hue) so it reads over dark packaging/signage, hi-res PNG — the brief's key 'must reverse out on dark
  • lumiere_menage_avatar_pfp.png — Tight square avatar / social-PFP crop of the LM monogram alone, centered with padding, transparent RGBA hi-res PNG, recognizable at small avatar size.
  • lumiere_menage_stacked_lockup.png — Stacked / vertical layout crop of the master lockup, cropped to true content bounds, transparent hi-res PNG for narrow and vertical placements.
  • lumiere_menage_oneclr_black.png — One-color pure-black (#111111) version of the lockup (via monochromatic-tint black conversion) on transparency, hi-res PNG for single-color foil/deboss and small-format print.
  • lumiere_menage_mono_tint.png — Single monochromatic brand-tint alternate of the lockup (one hue only, no multicolor) for tonal applications, hi-res PNG.
  • lumiere_menage_font_recommendation.json — Recommended brand font family + heading/supporting pairing and usage notes matched to the modern condensed wordmark tone (font_recommend output).
  • lumiere_menage_palette_recipe.md — Monochrome palette / usage recipe note (black #111111 / dark grey / off-white / paper hex callouts, clear-space + minimum-size notes) written locally as the color-palette deliverable; the printed swat
  • lumiere_menage_usage_board — Firefly board deep-link assembling the master, reversed knockout, avatar, stacked lockup, one-color and mono-tint variants for one-click founder sign-off; board notes carry the monochrome hex callouts
Workflow — 17 tool steps
  1. asset_initialize_file_upload — Begin upload of the client's supplied flat monochrome concept PNG into Adobe storage; returns an upload target/session.
  2. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the upload to a stable presigned asset URI the image_* tools can consume — the single source of truth for the chain.
  3. image_auto_straighten — Correct the ~2 degree export skew so the geometric edges are true before tracing — straight edges vectorize cleaner.
  4. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the straightened concept to normalize contrast and drive a dense, clean black-vs-white separation for the trace.
  5. image_remove_background — Knock the off-white #F5F5F3 background to true transparency (default transparent-PNG cutout, no reconstruction) so the vectorize has no halo/fringe. Hub cleaned
  6. image_vectorize — Vectorize the cleaned monochrome lockup into a clean, fully editable SVG = the MASTER vector deliverable (basis for AI/EPS/PNG).
  7. image_apply_monochromatic_tint — Monochromatic-tint the transparent mark to solid white (single hue, high lightness) -> reversed white-on-dark knockout, the brief's key reverse-out requirement
  8. image_select_by_prompt — Select the 'LUMIERE MENAGE' wordmark + tagline text region by prompt (mask only) so it can be knocked out to isolate the LM monogram for the avatar.
  9. image_fill_area — Solid-fill the selected wordmark sub-region white (solid fill only, driven by the wordmark_mask) to knock it out, leaving just the isolated LM monogram. Allowed
  10. image_crop_to_bounds — Crop the isolated monogram tight to its true content bounds so it centers cleanly for the avatar.
  11. image_crop_and_resize — Crop+resize the tight monogram to a centered square social-PFP / avatar asset that stays recognizable at small size (deliverable).
  12. image_crop_to_bounds — Crop the cleaned master to its true content bounds -> tight stacked/vertical lockup for narrow placements (deliverable).
  13. image_apply_monochromatic_tint — Monochromatic-tint to a pure-black (#111111) single-tone conversion -> one-color print version for foil/deboss/small-format (deliverable). Single tone, no multi
  14. image_apply_monochromatic_tint — Apply a single-hue monochromatic tint (one color only, no multicolor -> honors 'clean monochrome palette') -> tonal brand alt (deliverable).
  15. font_recommend — Recommend the brand font family + heading/supporting pairing matched to the modern condensed wordmark tone (deliverable).
  16. local_write_recipe_note — Emit the monochrome palette / usage recipe (black #111111 / dark grey / off-white / paper hex callouts + clear-space & minimum-size notes) as the color-palette
  17. create_firefly_board — Assemble every produced variation into a Firefly board usage/presentation deep-link for founder sign-off (board notes carry monochrome hex callouts) (deliverabl
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon (17 connector calls, 13 distinct Adobe tools plus one local step, past the >=10 / >=6 floor) with a genuine wide mix across asset ingest, the image_* raster/vector family, font, board, and a local recipe emit. Difficulty is in correct ordering (straighten + tone + bg-removal before vectorize for a clean monochrome trace), a real isolate-the-monogram sub-chain (select_by_prompt -> solid-fill knockout -> crop-to-bounds -> crop-and-resize), and reusing one cleaned hub to spawn six variations. Stays strictly inside connector limits and honors the brief's monochrome-only exclusions; the vector emerges from image_vectorize, the reversed/one-color/mono-tint variants from single-hue monochromatic tint (not multicolor, not gradient), and the palette guide from a local recipe note because a single image_fill_area call cannot author a multi-color swatch sheet. Mostly autonomous ([C] headless throughout, one [L] local recipe emit).

AO-90 — Apexguard Auto Film: vectorize the AI-drafted master + two sub-brand marks into production SVGs and assemble a multi-size die-cut vinyl decal sheet on the supplied cut-line template ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Clean and de-skew Apexguard's three AI-drafted PPF/tint logo rasters, trace them into crisp production SVG masters, derive one-color knockouts for vinyl weeding, then assemble a multi-size die-cut vinyl decal/sticker sheet by binding the vector marks and the approved-sizes CSV into the client's authored cut-line template and exporting a press-ready PDF + SVG.

💰 $500Upwork30 tool calls15 distinct toolscreatelong-horizon
grounded in a real brief: Logo and Brand Identity Designer
Full client brief
Apexguard Auto Film, a fictional performance-automotive brand selling paint protection film (PPF) and window tint, is rebranding and needs its new identity turned into production-ready vector artwork and a print-ready vinyl decal sheet for its shop glass, product boxes, and installer-kit labels. The client has drafted the concepts with an AI image tool and only has flat raster PNGs: a master wordmark lockup for 'Apexguard' (a clean modern condensed sans in matte black with a single deep-blue accent chevron, premium/minimalist, no gradients), a sub-brand mark for 'Apexguard SHIELD' (the PPF line — the wordmark plus a simple hex-shield glyph), and a sub-brand mark for 'Apexguard TINT' (the window-tint line — the wordmark plus a tint-bar glyph rendered as flat bands). All three rasters carry the usual AI-export problems: soft anti-aliased edges, a faint off-white textured card behind the art, mild luminance noise, and each is tilted a couple of degrees off level. They cannot go to a vinyl cutter/printer as-is because they do not scale, have no clean cut path, and have no transparency. The client also supplies (a) an authored die-cut decal-sheet template (decal_sheet_template.ai) laid out on a single sheet with several registered sticker cells at different sizes — small installer-kit labels up to a large window/glass piece — each cell carrying a real placement/merge field plus a registered cut-line and bleed, and (b) a CSV of the approved sticker sizes and cell labels (cell_label, size_in, which_mark, mark) that matches the template's merge fields.

The job: (1) ingest the three raster logos and get clean working copies; auto-straighten each tilted mark and tonally clean it so the black is true, the blue accent is crisp, and the near-white card is even; (2) crop each mark tight to its true artwork bounds and knock the background to transparent so the trace has no halo; (3) VECTORIZE each cleaned mark into a crisp, fully-scalable SVG master preserving the exact black + deep-blue palette — these three SVGs are the primary editable/print-ready vector deliverables (AI/EPS are the same master exported to those formats); (4) from the master wordmark, derive a single-color / knockout vector (solid-fill silhouette) for one-color window vinyl, etched glass and weeding-friendly cutter output, plus a white-on-dark knockout for dark boxes and tinted glass; (5) ASSEMBLE the multi-size vinyl decal/sticker sheet the briefs ask for: upload the cleaned vector marks, then run the client's authored decal_sheet_template.ai through a vector data-merge against the approved-sizes CSV so each registered cell is populated with the correct mark at the correct size behind its cut-line and bleed, and export the assembled sheet to a press-ready PDF and a production SVG; (6) recommen
Deliverables (9)
  • apexguard_master_wordmark.svg — Clean full-color vector trace of the Apexguard master wordmark lockup, matte-black type + single deep-blue accent chevron preserved exactly, transparent background, crisp scalable paths; the primary e
  • apexguard_shield_submark.svg — Clean vector trace of the Apexguard SHIELD sub-brand mark (wordmark + flat hex-shield glyph), palette preserved, transparent background, scalable paths; PPF-line sub-brand master.
  • apexguard_tint_submark.svg — Clean vector trace of the Apexguard TINT sub-brand mark (wordmark + flat-band tint-bar glyph), flat bands traced as discrete shapes, transparent background, scalable paths; tint-line sub-brand master.
  • apexguard_master_onecolor_knockout.svg — Single-color knockout/silhouette vector of the master wordmark (solid fill on flattened field) for one-color window vinyl, etched glass, weeding-friendly cutter output and single-color print.
  • apexguard_master_white_on_dark.png — White-on-dark one-color knockout of the master wordmark, hi-res transparent PNG (white silhouette on transparency) for dark product boxes and tinted glass panels.
  • apexguard_decal_sheet_print.pdf — Press-ready single-sheet die-cut vinyl decal PDF assembled from the authored decal_sheet_template.ai via vector data-merge: each registered cell populated with the correct cleaned vector mark at the c
  • apexguard_decal_sheet_production.svg — Production SVG export of the same assembled multi-size decal sheet (marks placed in their registered cells with cut-lines) for the vinyl cutter/plotter workflow.
  • apexguard_font_pairing.json — Recommended brand-safe font family + heading/body pairing matching the wordmark style, from font_recommend, so the client can extend the identity across packaging and web.
  • Apexguard_vector_kit_folder + review_board — Organized Creative Cloud hand-off folder containing every produced deliverable, plus a Firefly board deep-link assembling the three SVG masters, the knockout variants and the assembled decal sheet for
Workflow — 30 tool steps
  1. asset_initialize_file_upload — Begin uploading the client's three supplied raster logo concepts (master + SHIELD + TINT) into Creative Cloud storage to start the pipeline.
  2. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the upload to stable CC asset URIs for the three rasters; every later step consumes a prior output. This is the single initial asset load.
  3. image_auto_straighten — Correct the ~2.5deg clockwise tilt on the Apexguard master wordmark so the baseline is level — straight art traces cleaner.
  4. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the straightened master to normalize the mild AI lighting and recover clean black/near-white separation for a sharp trace.
  5. image_adjust_single_color_saturation — Boost only the deep-blue accent channel so the brand blue is punchy and clean-edged for the trace (black + card untouched).
  6. image_crop_to_bounds — Trim the surrounding card so the canvas hugs the master artwork, removing wasted background before knocking it out.
  7. image_remove_background — Knock the faint textured card to true transparency so the trace has no halo/fringe around the letterforms and chevron. This cleaned master is the reuse hub for
  8. image_vectorize — Vectorize the cleaned master wordmark into a crisp full-color SVG master, preserving the matte black + deep-blue chevron exactly (deliverable; AI/EPS derive fro
  9. image_auto_straighten — Straighten the SHIELD sub-mark, tilted ~3deg the OTHER way, so its baseline is level for a clean trace.
  10. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the SHIELD mark so the hex-shield glyph and type separate crisply from the off-white card.
  11. image_crop_to_bounds — Crop the SHIELD lockup tight to its true artwork bounds.
  12. image_remove_background — Knock the SHIELD card to transparency for a halo-free trace and clean decal placement.
  13. image_vectorize — Vectorize the cleaned SHIELD sub-mark into a scalable SVG master, hex-shield glyph + palette preserved (deliverable).
  14. image_auto_straighten — Level the TINT sub-mark (~2deg off) so the flat tint-bar bands and type trace true.
  15. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the TINT mark to maximize contrast between the flat tint bands so they trace as clean discrete shapes.
  16. image_crop_to_bounds — Crop the TINT lockup tight to its bounds.
  17. image_remove_background — Knock the TINT card to transparency for a halo-free trace and clean decal placement.
  18. image_vectorize — Vectorize the cleaned TINT sub-mark into a scalable SVG master, flat tint-bar bands traced as discrete shapes (deliverable).
  19. image_apply_color_overlay — Flatten the isolated master wordmark to a single solid color (color overlay over the transparent cut-out) to build the one-color knockout source for window viny
  20. image_vectorize — Vectorize the solid-color master into the one-color knockout SVG for single-color window vinyl, etched glass and weeding-friendly cutter output (deliverable).
  21. image_apply_color_overlay — Apply a solid white color overlay to the isolated master wordmark -> white-on-dark knockout PNG (white silhouette on transparency) for dark boxes and tinted gla
  22. asset_initialize_file_upload — Upload the three cleaned vector marks so the authored decal template can reference them by URN for the placement/merge.
  23. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the upload; returns the vector asset URNs the decal_sheet_template.ai merge fields will reference.
  24. document_merge_data_vector — Vector data-merge: bind each approved size/cell/mark row into the client's authored decal_sheet_template.ai (which carries real placement <<fields>> + registere
  25. document_render_vector — Export the merged decal-sheet .ai to a press-ready PDF with cut-lines, bleed and registration marks intact (deliverable).
  26. document_render_vector — Export the same merged sheet to a production SVG for the vinyl cutter/plotter workflow (deliverable).
  27. font_recommend — Recommend a brand-safe font family + heading/body pairing matching the wordmark style so the client can extend the identity across packaging and web (deliverabl
  28. asset_create_folders — Create an organized CC hand-off folder (Apexguard_Vector_Kit) to collect all deliverables neatly for the client.
  29. asset_copy_assets — Copy every produced vector + decal deliverable into the hand-off folder so the client receives one tidy, print-ready package.
  30. create_firefly_board — Assemble all produced deliverables onto a single Firefly board deep-link for one-click client sign-off on the vectors and the multi-size decal sheet.
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon and wide-mix: 30 connector calls, 15 distinct Adobe tools across four groups (asset ingest/storage, the image_* raster/vector family, the document_* vector-merge/render family, and font/board) — far above the >=10 calls / >=6 distinct-tool floor. Ordering matters — each mark must be straightened, tonally cleaned, cropped and background-knocked BEFORE vectorizing or the trace inherits the AI noise/tilt/halo and fails the 'clean, fully-scalable, print-ready vector' bar; a naive agent that vectorizes the raw PNGs fails. It runs three separate marks through their own consistent clean->trace pipelines (a master plus two sub-brands with different tilts), then reuses the cleaned master as a hub to spawn the one-color knockout (color_overlay -> re-vectorize) and the white-on-dark knockout. The genuinely hard convergence is the CREATE step: the three vector masters plus the approved-sizes CSV are bound into the client's AUTHORED die-cut template via document_merge_data_vector [T] to assemble a real multi-size decal sheet with cut-lines and bleed, then dual-exported (press PDF + production SVG) via document_render_vector [C]. It respects every hard limit: no generative fill / object-removal / text-to-image / reframe-crop / upscaling / from-scratch layout; image_fill_area is not used; there is no generative capability; the vector masters come solely from image_vectorize (rast

Data & other (2)

decal_sheet_template.ai.proxy.txt
content
**Template Description:**

**File Name:** `decal_sheet_template.ai`

**Purpose:** Adobe Illustrator template for a multi-decal die-cut vinyl sheet layout, meticulously authored for automated vector data merge and precise cutting. This template ensures proper binding of vector merge data to cut paths, critical for print-shop production.

**Dimensions:**
*   **Artboard:** 24" x 18" (standard sheet size for vinyl printing and cutting).
*   **Decal Cells:**
    *   **Large (1x):** Approx. 10" x 8" (e.g., window/glass decal).
    *   **Medium (2x):** Approx. 4" x 4" each (e.g., box labels).
    *   **Small (6x):** Approx. 1.5" x 1.5" each (e.g., installer-kit labels).

**Structure & Layers:**
The document is precisely structured with dedicated layers for production efficiency:
*   **`Artwork` Layer:** Contains all live vector data-merge fields and visual elements.
*   **`CutContour` Layer (Spot Color):** Dedicated, non-printing spot color layer (e.g., "CutContour" or "Thru-Cut") holding all vector die-cut paths for each decal.
*   **`Bleed` Layer:** Guides and visual markers indicating the required bleed allowance around each decal cell.
*   **`TrimMarks` Layer:** Standard printer's trim and registration marks, positioned for accurate sheet alignment and cutting.
*   **`Guides` Layer (Locked):** Informational guides for layout, spacing, and safe zones.

**Placeholders & Styling:**
*   **Vector Data-Merge Fields:** Each decal cell contains a unique, live vector data-merge placeholder, such as `<<large_mark>>`, `<<medium_mark>>`, and `<<small_mark>>`. These fields are configured to dynamically import and place vector assets (e.g., logos, QR codes, part numbers) from a data source directly into the corresponding decal cell.
*   **Bleed Allowance:** Each decal cell includes a standard 0.125" bleed beyond the cut line, ensuring no unprinted edges after trimming.
*   **Cut-Line Registration:** The vector cut-paths on the `CutContour` layer are precisely registered to their respective artwork and merge fields, guaranteeing accurate die-cutting of the merged artwork.
*   **Brand Styling:** Follows standard print production best practices, including clear layer naming, spot color definition for cut paths, and precise vector construction, ensuring compatibility with automated cutting equipment. The template is designed for direct integration with an external data source fo
decal_sizes.csv
gemini/gemini-2.5-flash
content

window_decal_1,10,master wordmark,master
product_decal_1,4,SHIELD sub-mark,shield
product_decal_2,4,TINT sub-mark,tint

installer_decal_2,1.5,SHIELD sub-mark,shield

installer_decal_4,1.5,master wordmark,master

AO-91 — Maison Valcere jewelry house: vector & print-readiness audit of the supplied luxury wordmark pack against the brand guideline and press spec (marked-up findings report) ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Audit the supplied Maison Valcere luxury wordmark pack (master SVG + raster export) against the brand guideline and the press/foil spec, and return a marked-up, region-referenced vector & print-readiness findings report listing every hairline-stroke, line-weight, palette, kerning and non-print-ready-path defect and the exact rule it breaks.

💰 ~$120 est.Freelancer.com16 tool calls9 distinct toolsanalyzelong-horizon
grounded in a real brief: Luxury E-commerce Brand Visuals
Full client brief
Maison Valcere is a fictional independent high-jewelry house (fine rings, pendants and bespoke pieces) that has just received its finished logo pack from an outside brand studio and, before it goes onto embossed presentation boxes, foil-stamped certificates, the e-commerce header and the video title cards, wants an independent set of eyes to confirm the mark is genuinely production-clean. The identity is meant to feel unmistakably premium — a deep-black canvas with crisp white typography, minimal ornamentation, purely typographic with no symbols or icons — and the house is strict that every asset stays consistent across packaging, print and screen. You are handed four things: (1) valcere_wordmark_master.svg, the "production" vector master the studio delivered; (2) valcere_wordmark_web.png, the flat raster export the house has been using online; (3) valcere_brand_guideline.pdf, the guideline stating the monochrome palette (Valcere Noir #0E0E10, Champagne #E8DCC2, Blanc #FAF8F3 — with the rule that the wordmark is "purely typographic, no symbols or icons, never recoloured outside the monochrome palette, minimum print width 22mm, minimum hairline stroke 0.35pt / 1.5px at favicon size"); and (4) valcere_press_spec.pdf, the printer/foil spec sheet (foil-stamp minimum stroke, CMYK/spot conversion requirement, "all strokes must be expanded to filled paths, no open contours, no RGB-only fills"). The job is an AUDIT, not a redraw — do NOT re-letter, re-trace, recolour or fix anything, and do NOT deliver a new logo. Go through the mark region by region and compare what the pack actually contains against the guideline and the press spec, then hand back a vector & print-readiness audit report that, for each issue, gives the mark region (which glyph / stroke / variant), a plain-language description of the defect, the specific guideline or press-spec clause it violates, the approved value vs. the observed value, and a severity (blocker / warning / nit). We expect you to catch things like: a hairline serif or connecting stroke thinner than the 0.35pt foil-stamp minimum that will drop out when stamped or shrunk to a favicon; a stray decorative anchor / tiny symbol that violates the "purely typography, no symbols" rule; the reverse (knockout) variant sitting on an off-palette near-black instead of Valcere Noir #0E0E10; kerning drift where the raster web export does not match the vector master's letter-spacing; and paths in the master SVG that are not print-ready (unexpanded strokes, open contours, RGB fills that were never converted for spot/foil). Deliver the report as a clean A4 portrait PDF plus the raw findings as a structured file, and include a one-page cover summary with the total count by severity and a go / no-go-for-press verdict. The report is for the hous
Deliverables (3)
  • Valcere_Vector_PrintReadiness_Audit.pdf — The primary deliverable: a clean A4 portrait vector & print-readiness audit report PDF. Opens with a one-page cover summary (total findings by severity blocker / warning / nit and a go / no-go-for-pre
  • valcere_audit_findings.json — The raw structured findings emitted by local_write_recipe_note: an ordered list of records {region, variant, defect, source_rule, approved_value, observed_value, severity} plus a summary block with co
  • valcere_inspection_rasters — The per-view raster images produced by rendering the master SVG and previewing the guideline/press PDFs and by tracing the web PNG — the inspection surface the audit reads and references (main wordmar
Workflow — 16 tool steps
  1. asset_create_folders — Stand up the CC asset workspace (e.g. /V06/logo-pack, /specs, /findings) to hold the supplied wordmark pack, the guideline + press spec, and the emitted audit r
  2. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize an upload session for the four client-supplied audit inputs: valcere_wordmark_master.svg, valcere_wordmark_web.png, valcere_brand_guideline.pdf, valc
  3. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the upload so the master SVG, web PNG, brand guideline and press spec are real CC assets with URNs the inspection steps can read. This is the single in
  4. media_summarize — Summarize valcere_brand_guideline.pdf to pull the machine-usable ground-truth brand rules the audit checks against: the monochrome palette hex values, the 'pure
  5. media_summarize — Summarize valcere_press_spec.pdf to extract the production ground truth: foil-stamp minimum reproducible stroke 0.35pt, 'expand all strokes to filled paths', 'n
  6. asset_preview_file — Render the uploaded valcere_wordmark_master.svg into per-view raster previews (main wordmark + reverse/knockout variant) so each glyph, stroke and variant can b
  7. asset_inline_preview — Inline-preview the rendered master views to read the actual on-mark state (glyph construction, stroke thinness on the thin serifs and the 'A' crossbar, the stra
  8. image_vectorize — Trace the valcere_wordmark_web.png raster export into an SVG PURELY as an audit comparison artifact — so its letter-spacing / kerning can be measured against th
  9. asset_inline_preview — Inline-preview the traced web export alongside the master views to visually confirm the kerning / letter-spacing drift between the raster export and the vector
  10. image_select_by_prompt — On the master raster views, select the 'thin serif strokes and the crossbar of the A' regions purely as an inspection LOCATOR so their stroke weight can be meas
  11. image_select_by_prompt — Select the 'small decorative diamond / non-letter mark beside the V' region so its presence can be confirmed and flagged against the 'purely typographic, no sym
  12. image_select_by_prompt — Select the 'background field of the reverse / knockout variant' region so its tone can be sampled and checked against Valcere Noir #0E0E10 (catches the off-pale
  13. asset_inline_preview — Inline-preview the isolated hairline-stroke / stray-symbol / reverse-field regions at high detail to confirm each suspected defect (sub-0.35pt stroke, stray dia
  14. asset_preview_file — Preview the brand guideline and press-spec source pages so the exact approved values (0.35pt min stroke, 22mm min width, palette hex, expand-strokes / closed-co
  15. local_write_recipe_note — Emit the structured vector & print-readiness findings: for each defect a record {region, variant, defect, source_rule, approved_value, observed_value, severity}
  16. document_convert_pdf — Render the written audit findings (cover summary + press verdict + per-finding rows) into the clean A4 portrait Vector & Print-Readiness Audit report PDF for th
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon analyze/audit: 16 connector calls, 9 distinct Adobe tools across three groups (asset workspace/upload/render/preview, media_summarize, image_vectorize + image_select_by_prompt + document_convert_pdf) plus the single [L] recipe-note emit — above the ≥10-call / ≥6-distinct-tool floor for an analyze task. Difficulty is in the analytic structure, not raster grunt-work: the agent must (a) extract two separate rule sets — the brand guideline AND the printer/foil press spec — via media_summarize rather than assuming them, and know which clause governs which defect, (b) render the vector master and inspect the main wordmark and the reverse/knockout variant view by view, (c) use image_vectorize NOT as a deliverable but as a comparison trace of the raster export to catch the kerning drift against the vector master, (d) use image_select_by_prompt purely as an inspection LOCATOR to isolate the sub-0.35pt hairline strokes, the stray non-typographic diamond glyph and the reverse-variant background field so weight, presence and tone can be measured against the ground truth, (e) distinguish clean paths from the planted defects (sub-min hairlines, open contour, unexpanded strokes, stray symbol, off-palette reverse near-black, RGB-only fill, kerning drift), and (f) emit a structured, region-referenced, severity-ranked findings report with a go / no-go-for-press verdict. It respects
