AO-92 — Wandergrain Supply screen-print pre-flight: audit 4 supplied traveller tee vectors for separation-readiness, registration & spot-color count, emit a marked-up seps report + print-shop go/no-go note ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Pre-flight four supplied traveller-themed t-shirt vector graphics for screen-print separation readiness -- verify transparent backgrounds, count distinct spot colors per design, check for registration marks and thin-line survivability, vectorize/inspect to flag raster contamination -- and emit a marked-up per-design seps audit report with a go/no-go recommendation for the exposure room.

💰 $15–$35/hrFreelancer.com20 tool calls10 distinct toolsanalyzelong-horizon
grounded in a real brief: Travel-Themed T-Shirt Design
Full client brief
Wandergrain Supply Co., a small-batch apparel screen-print shop, has just received four traveller-themed t-shirt graphics from a freelance designer for its new "Modern Wanderer" tee line (universal travel icons -- a passport-stamp badge, a latitude/longitude coordinate mark, a minimalist skyline, and a compass-rose crest). Before any of these hit the exposure room and burn screens, the shop needs an independent pre-flight / separation audit -- the designer swears the files are "pre-flighted for screen print," but the shop has been burned before by art that looked fine on screen and fell apart on the press. We are NOT redesigning anything and NOT producing final films; we need an honest QA report. For each of the four designs, please verify and document: (1) background integrity -- the art must sit on a genuinely transparent background with no stray off-white halo or leftover matte that would print as a phantom screen; (2) spot-color / ink count -- count the distinct flat spot colors in each design and flag any design that exceeds our 4-color manual-press limit, or that hides near-duplicate colors that should be merged to one screen; (3) registration & trap readiness -- check whether the art carries registration marks / crop guides and whether abutting colors are cleanly separable or will need a choke/trap note; (4) detail survivability -- flag any hairline strokes, tiny text, or fine detail below what a 156-mesh screen can hold, and confirm the "keep text editable" promise by checking whether type is still crisp vector or has been flattened to messy raster; (5) vector cleanliness -- confirm each design is truly vector (not a raster PNG masquerading as art) by test-vectorizing and comparing. Deliverables: a per-design marked-up preview of each graphic (so we can circle problems), plus a single written seps pre-flight report (a recipe/findings note) that lists, per design, the spot-color count, pass/fail on transparency, registration presence, minimum-detail concern, and a clear GO / FIX-FIRST / NO-GO verdict the exposure room can act on. Files supplied as an Illustrator-exported package: four graphics (three already flattened to PNG with transparency, one still a layered vector) plus the designer's own color-spec sheet. Keep everything spot-color / vector oriented -- no CMYK conversions, no re-coloring, no new artwork.
Deliverables (4)
  • seps_preflight_report.txt — A single written screen-print pre-flight / separation-readiness findings note (recipe note) covering all four designs: per-design measured spot-color/ink count vs the designer's claimed count, transpa
  • marked_up_previews_x4 — Four inline/marked-up preview renders (one per design) used as the visual evidence backing the report -- each showing the design as inspected, with the audited region (halo edge, thin-line area, gradi
  • coordinate_mark_isolated_ink_masks — Selection/preview masks isolating the near-duplicate dark tones in the coordinate-mark design, evidencing that its claimed '1 color' is actually 3 near-identical inks that must merge to one screen.
  • compass_crest_vector_trace.svg — A test-vectorization SVG of the compass-rose crest, used only as inspection evidence that the design is genuinely clean flat spot art with holdable edges (confirms the GO verdict); not a redesign or f
Workflow — 20 tool steps
  1. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize an upload session to bring the designer's four supplied tee graphics and the color-spec CSV into CC storage for auditing.
  2. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the upload; returns asset URNs for the four graphics and the spec sheet that every inspection step will reference.
  3. asset_preview_file — Preview/read the designer's color_spec CSV so the audit has the CLAIMED ink counts / registration claims to compare each measured finding against.
  4. asset_inline_preview — Inline-preview the passport-stamp badge to visually inspect background integrity and flat-color edges (the clean 2-color control).
  5. image_select_by_prompt — Select the background region of the passport badge to test transparency integrity -- confirm no stray off-white matte remains that would print as a phantom scre
  6. asset_inline_preview — Inline-preview the coordinate-mark design to inspect its claimed '1 color' against what is visibly present.
  7. image_select_by_prompt — Select the coordinate-mark background edge to detect the faint off-white 1px halo left by the sloppy export (transparency FAIL evidence).
  8. image_select_by_prompt — Select the dark linework of the coordinate mark to begin isolating its distinct tones.
  9. image_invert_selection — Invert the selection to isolate and count the distinct dark inks -- evidences that the claimed single ink is actually 3 near-duplicate tones that must merge to
  10. image_apply_halftone — Apply a halftone probe at press LPI to the coordinate mark's hairline strokes / 6pt text to visualize whether the fine detail survives a 156-mesh screen or brea
  11. asset_inline_preview — Inline-preview the city-skyline design to inspect for flat spot color vs blended tones.
  12. image_select_by_prompt — Select the sky region of the skyline to evidence the smooth multi-stop gradient + soft shadow -- proof it is continuous-tone poster art, not separation-ready sp
  13. image_apply_halftone — Halftone-probe the gradient sky to show how many tones/screens it would actually require -- quantifies why the skyline blows past the 4-color manual-press limit
  14. asset_inline_preview — Inline-preview the compass-rose crest to inspect its flat 3-color art and locate the registration crosshairs / crop ticks.
  15. image_select_by_prompt — Select the registration crosshair / crop-tick marks on the crest to confirm registration presence (the strongest GO candidate).
  16. image_vectorize — Test-vectorize the crest to confirm it is genuinely clean flat spot art with holdable edges (vector-vs-raster PASS evidence); inspection only, not a redesign.
  17. image_vectorize — Test-vectorize the skyline to demonstrate the opposite: the gradient/shadow art traces into an unusable tangle of shapes, confirming it is NOT vector-clean spot
  18. asset_create_folders — Create a CC folder to collect all marked-up previews, ink masks, halftone probes and vector traces as the audit's evidence bundle.
  19. asset_copy_assets — Copy every inspection artifact into the evidence folder so the report's findings are each backed by a viewable marked-up preview.
  20. local_write_recipe_note — Emit the written seps pre-flight report: per-design measured ink count vs the CSV's claimed count, transparency PASS/FAIL, registration presence, thin-line/deta
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon analyze/audit: 20 connector calls, 10 distinct Adobe tools -- above the >=10 call / >=6 distinct-tool floor. Difficulty comes from running four separate designs through tailored inspection sub-chains (transparency-halo detection, hidden-near-duplicate-ink isolation via select+invert, thin-line/halftone survivability probing, gradient-vs-spot detection, and vectorize-as-diagnostic) then reconciling every measured finding against the designer's over-claimed color-spec CSV to produce actionable GO / FIX-FIRST / NO-GO verdicts. It respects every hard limit: nothing is composed, re-colored, or generated; image_vectorize is used ONLY as a diagnostic (clean trace = pass, tangled trace = fail), not to author a new deliverable; image_apply_halftone and the image_select_* / image_invert_selection tools are used purely to INSPECT and produce marked-up evidence; no generative fill, no object-removal, no text-to-image, no upscaling, no background replacement. The only non-adobe step is the terminal local_write_recipe_note [L] that emits the findings report -- the correct analyze-task output.

AO-93 — Torqex Industrial nameplate logo pack: print-readiness preflight audit of the supplied vector artwork (open paths, RGB, un-outlined text, stray points) into a marked-up findings report ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Preflight-audit the supplied Torqex vector logo pack (.ai/.eps master, SVG, PDF, plus one-colour and reverse variants) against the manufacturer's nameplate print spec and return a marked-up, file-and-region-referenced findings report listing every open path, RGB colour, un-outlined text run, stray point, hairline stroke and background/bleed defect with the exact rule it breaks and a severity.

💰 ~$120 est.Freelancer.com16 tool calls9 distinct toolsanalyzelong-horizon
grounded in a real brief: Modern Casual Dress Logo
Full client brief
Torqex Industrial is a fictional maker of hydraulic torque tools and machinery, and we are about to send our brand mark out to a nameplate vendor to be laser-etched onto anodized-aluminium equipment plates and screen-printed onto tool cases. A previous contractor delivered our 'Torqex' logo as a vector pack, but before it goes to the etching/print house we need a fresh set of eyes to confirm the artwork is genuinely production-ready — the last vendor rejected a batch because a logo file had live text and an RGB blue that shifted on the plate, and we cannot afford another rejected run. This is an AUDIT, not a redesign: do NOT redraw, restyle, re-colour or re-letter anything. You will receive two things. (1) torqex_logo_pack: the delivered artwork — torqex_master.ai (the editable vector master), torqex_master.eps and torqex_master.pdf (the same mark exported), torqex_logo.svg (the web export), plus torqex_onecolour_black.ai (single-colour knockout) and torqex_reverse_white.ai (reverse/knockout-on-dark variant). (2) torqex_nameplate_print_spec.pdf, the vendor's print-spec sheet stating the rules the pack must satisfy: all colours must be CMYK (or spot) — no RGB; the approved brand colour is Torqex Blue at C100 M72 K18 (do NOT ship RGB #1B49E5); all text must be converted to outlines (no live fonts); paths must be closed with no stray/open anchor points; the minimum printable stroke weight for laser etching is 0.25 pt (0.09 mm) — anything thinner drops out; the mark must sit on a transparent background with a 3 mm bleed on the placed variants; the one-colour and reverse variants must be a single flat colour with no gradients or tints. Go through every file, compare what is actually in the artwork against the spec, and hand back a print-readiness preflight report that, for each issue found, gives the file name, the region / element on the mark, a plain-language description of the defect, the specific spec clause it violates, the observed value vs the approved value, and a severity (blocker / warning / nit). We expect you to catch things like: the SVG and the .pdf export carrying RGB colour instead of CMYK; the tagline text in the master left as live (un-outlined) fonts; a couple of stray/duplicate anchor points and one un-closed path on the icon; a hairline register line under 0.25 pt that will drop out on the plate; the reverse variant accidentally carrying a faint gradient instead of a flat white; and one variant exported on a solid white background instead of transparent with no bleed. Some files are clean and compliant so the audit has to distinguish good from bad, not just flag everything. Deliver the report as a clean A4 PDF plus the raw findings as a structured file, with a one-page cover summary giving the total count by severity. The report is fo
Deliverables (3)
  • Torqex_Logo_PrintPreflight_Audit.pdf — The primary deliverable: a clean A4 portrait print-readiness preflight report PDF. Opens with a one-page cover summary (total findings by severity: blocker / warning / nit, plus a per-file pass/fail m
  • torqex_preflight_findings.json — The raw structured findings emitted by local_write_recipe_note: an ordered list of records {file, region, defect, spec_clause, approved_value, observed_value, severity} plus a per-file pass/fail matri
  • torqex_logo_proof_rasters — The per-file raster proofs produced by rendering each supplied vector/PDF file (the .ai/.eps/.pdf/.svg master + the two variants) to an image, used as the visual inspection surface for the audit and r
Workflow — 16 tool steps
  1. asset_create_folders — Stand up the CC asset workspace (e.g. /V08/logo_pack, /spec, /proofs, /findings) to hold the supplied vector pack, the print-spec sheet, the rendered proofs and
  2. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize an upload session for the client-supplied audit inputs: torqex_master.ai/.eps/.pdf, torqex_logo.svg, torqex_onecolour_black.ai, torqex_reverse_white.
  3. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the upload so the six vector/PDF logo files and the spec PDF are real CC assets with URNs the inspection steps can read. This is the single initial ass
  4. media_summarize — Summarize torqex_nameplate_print_spec.pdf to pull out the machine-usable ground-truth preflight rules the audit checks against: CMYK-only + approved Torqex Blue
  5. document_render_vector — Render the torqex_master.ai to a high-res raster proof (EXPORT only — no editing/authoring) so the wordmark, icon, tagline, register line and colour can be visu
  6. document_render_vector — Render the two variants (torqex_onecolour_black.ai and torqex_reverse_white.ai) to raster proofs so the one-colour knockout can be verified clean and the revers
  7. document_convert_pdf — Convert torqex_master.pdf to a per-page raster proof so the print-PDF export can be inspected for its colour profile (the planted RGB-instead-of-CMYK defect) an
  8. asset_inline_preview — Inline-preview each rendered proof (torqex_logo_proof_rasters) to read the actual on-artwork state — wordmark colour, tagline text, icon geometry, stroke weight
  9. asset_preview_file — Preview torqex_logo.svg directly to expose its raw structure/colour so the RGB colour, the live <text> elements (un-outlined fonts) and the opaque white backgro
  10. image_select_by_prompt — On the .ai and .pdf proofs, select the 'main TORQEX wordmark' region so its rendered colour can be sampled and compared against the approved Torqex Blue — catch
  11. image_select_by_prompt — Select the 'HYDRAULIC TORQUE SYSTEMS tagline text' region on the master proof so its edges can be checked for the live-vs-outlined-font tell (anti-aliased type
  12. image_select_by_prompt — Select the 'hex-bolt / torque-arrow icon and the thin register keyline' region so the icon geometry can be zoomed for the stray-point / un-closed-path defects a
  13. image_select_by_prompt — Select the 'reverse-variant mark fill' region so its white can be checked for the flat-vs-gradient defect against the single-flat-colour rule for variants. Sele
  14. asset_inline_preview — Inline-preview the isolated wordmark-colour / tagline-text / icon-geometry / reverse-fill regions at high detail to CONFIRM each suspected defect (RGB in the .p
  15. local_write_recipe_note — Emit the structured preflight findings: for each defect a record {file, region, defect, spec_clause, approved_value, observed_value, severity} cross-referencing
  16. document_convert_pdf — Render the written preflight findings (cover summary + per-file pass/fail matrix + per-finding rows) into the clean A4 portrait Print-Readiness Preflight Audit
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon analyze/audit: 16 connector calls, 9 distinct Adobe tools across four groups (asset workspace/upload/preview, media_summarize, document_render_vector + document_convert_pdf export/rasterize, image_select_by_prompt inspection locator) plus the single [L] recipe-note emit — above the ≥10-call / ≥6-distinct-tool floor for an analyze task. Difficulty is in the analytic structure, not raster grunt-work: the agent must (a) extract the actual manufacturer preflight rules from the supplied spec PDF via media_summarize rather than assuming them, (b) render/convert six different vector/PDF/SVG files to proofs and inspect each one, (c) use image_select_by_prompt purely as an INSPECTION locator to isolate the wordmark-colour, tagline-text, icon-geometry and reverse-fill regions so on-artwork colour/text-state/geometry/fill can be compared against the ground truth, (d) distinguish compliant files from the planted defects (RGB in the .pdf and .svg, live tagline text, stray points + one open bolt path, a sub-0.25 pt hairline register line, a faint gradient on the reverse variant, an opaque background on the SVG) while correctly PASSING the clean one-colour variant and the CMYK master colour, and (e) emit a structured, per-file, severity-ranked findings report with a pass/fail matrix. It respects every hard limit: NOTHING is drawn, re-coloured, re-lettered or restyled — image_sele

Client-supplied input assets: 7 total · 0 img · 0 video · 0 audio · 7 data

Data & other (7)

torqex_logo.svg
gemini/gemini-2.5-flash
torqex_master.ai.proxy.txt
content
**File:** `torqex_master.ai`

**Template Type:** Master Brand Mark (Vector Logo)

**Dimensions:** Standard vector artboard (e.g., A4 or US Letter equivalent), providing ample canvas for the logo's aspect ratio. The artwork itself is fully vector-based and scalable without resolution loss.

**Layers:**
*   `_LOGO_ARTWORK`: Contains the primary wordmark and icon vector shapes.
*   `_TAGLINE_TEXT`: Dedicated layer for the tagline text.
*   `_GUIDES_MARKS`: Contains non-printing elements like crop marks or registration indicators.
*   `_BACKGROUND`: (Currently empty or contains a white fill for context).

**Brand Styling:**
*   **Brand Name:** TORQEX
*   **Elements:** The design features a bold, condensed geometric "TORQEX" wordmark, positioned to the left of a stylized hex-bolt / torque-arrow icon. The tagline "HYDRAULIC TORQUE SYSTEMS" is smaller and centered beneath the main logo elements.
*   **Typography:**
    *   **Wordmark:** Bold, condensed sans-serif (e.g., a strong geometric grotesque).
    *   **Tagline:** Regular weight, all-caps sans-serif, smaller point size.
*   **Color Palette:**
    *   **Torqex Blue (Primary):** C100 M72 K18. All main logo elements are filled with this CMYK color.

**Content/Placeholders:**
This file serves as the definitive, fixed master asset for the TORQEX brand identity. It contains no editable content placeholders beyond the brand elements themselves.

**Audit Findings / Preflight Issues:**
The following deliberate discrepancies were identified for preflight audit purposes:
1.  **Live Text:** The tagline "HYDRAULIC TORQUE SYSTEMS" remains as editable live font text; it has not been converted to outlines.
2.  **Path Errors:**
    *   Two (2) stray/duplicate anchor points are present near the torque-arrow icon.
    *   One (1) path within the hex-bolt outline of the icon is un-closed.
3.  **Hairline Stroke:** A registration/keyline stroke within the `_GUIDES_MARKS` layer is set to approximately 0.15 pt, which is below the recommended minimum print thickness (typically 0.25 pt).
4.  **Color Compliance:** The main "TORQEX" wordmark fill is correctly defined in CMYK (C100 M72 K18), indicating a compliant color space for print production of the primary brand mark.

torqex_onecolour_black.ai.proxy.txt
content
**Template Description:**

*   **File Type:** Adobe Illustrator (.ai) vector graphic.
*   **Structure:** Single-layer vector artwork. The "TORQEX" brand mark is composed of fully outlined text and closed paths.
*   **Layers:** One primary layer containing the complete, unified brand mark.
*   **Placeholders:** None. All text within the "TORQEX" mark has been converted to outlines, rendering it non-editable. No image placeholders are present.
*   **Dimensions:** Vector-based, ensuring infinite scalability without resolution degradation.
*   **Brand Styling:** The "TORQEX" brand mark is presented as a solid, flat 100% black (K100) shape. It is a "knockout" variant, featuring a transparent background, designed for clean and versatile application across various backgrounds. This is a compliant, production-ready one-colour version.
torqex_reverse_white.ai.proxy.txt
content
**Template File Description:**

**File Name:** `torqex_reverse_white.ai`

**Structure:** Adobe Illustrator vector file containing the TORQEX brand mark. All textual elements are outlined paths, ensuring graphic integrity and font independence. Paths are closed.

**Layers:** A single primary "Artwork" layer encapsulates the entire logo design. No sub-layers or complex layer organization.

**Placeholders:** No dynamic content placeholders. The file contains static, finalized logo artwork.

**Dimensions:** The artwork is resolution-independent vector graphics, designed for infinite scalability. The artboard is set to a standard square format (e.g., 1000x1000 pixels) providing ample clear space around the mark.

**Brand Styling:** This is the "reverse" variant of the TORQEX mark, specifically intended for application on dark backgrounds. The design calls for the mark to be a singular, flat white element.

**Defect/Note:** The white fill of the TORQEX mark has been erroneously applied as a very faint white-to-grey gradient/tint. The specification requires a flat 0% tint (solid white) for clean knockout on etched plates or single-color reproduction.

AO-94 — Vitalé wellness Reel: clean a captured talking-head into platform-ready 9:16 Reel, 9:16 Short and 1:1 feed cuts — speech-cleanup, quick-cut highlight, multi-platform resize ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Take Vitalé Labs' raw single-take wellness talking-head, clean the noisy on-camera speech, auto-cut it into a punchy highlight sizzle at a target duration, then export platform-ready 9:16 Reel, 9:16 Short and 1:1 feed versions plus a caption/transcript note.

💰 ~$120 est.Freelancer.com15 tool calls9 distinct toolsedit
grounded in a real brief: Engaging Video Editor for GEN Checkup
Full client brief
Vitalé Labs is a direct-to-consumer wellness and DNA-supplement brand that publishes short educational clips explaining how personalized nutrition works. We just filmed our lead nutrition scientist, Dr. Nadia Reuter, doing a single continuous take (about 3.5 minutes, shot 3840x2160 landscape on a mirrorless camera) explaining our new "GenoBalance" DNA supplement panel. It is a rough take: she rambles, restarts a couple of sentences, there are long pauses while she checks her notes, and the room has an audible HVAC hum plus a little echo because we shot in an untreated office. We need this turned into ONE tight, premium, trustworthy sizzle Reel — clean and modern and educational, NOT flashy or meme-style. Deliverables and specs: (1) clean up the spoken audio so her voice is clear and the room hum/echo is gone; (2) cut the rambling take down to a punchy ~35-45 second highlight version that keeps the strongest, most watchable moments and drops the dead air — keep viewers glued from the first second; (3) from that finished cut, export three platform-ready versions at the correct dimensions with no stretching: a 9:16 vertical Instagram Reel (1080x1920), a 9:16 vertical YouTube Short (1080x1920), and a 1:1 square feed post (1080x1080); (4) also hand back a plain-text caption/subtitle-and-hook note (transcript of the take plus a 3-second hook line and hashtag set) that our team will drop onto the timeline ourselves for burned-in captions. Keep the brand feel restrained and professional per our kit. Attached: the raw take, a one-page paper-edit / hook script marking the lines we care about, and our brand kit.
Deliverables (5)
  • vitale_genobalance_reel_1080x1920.mp4 — 9:16 vertical Instagram Reel, 1080x1920, ~35-45s, built from the speech-cleaned + quick-cut highlight edit; no stretching (source reframed/resized to vertical).
  • vitale_genobalance_short_1080x1920.mp4 — 9:16 vertical YouTube Short, 1080x1920, same finished cut, exported at Short-correct dimensions.
  • vitale_genobalance_feed_1080x1080.mp4 — 1:1 square feed post, 1080x1080, same finished cut, resized for in-feed autoplay.
  • genobalance_highlight_master.mp4 — The intermediate landscape highlight master: the audio-cleaned raw take auto-cut down to the ~40s on-message sizzle by visual-engagement selection (source of all three platform resizes).
  • genobalance_caption_hook_note.txt — Plain-text caption/subtitle-and-hook note: transcript of the spoken take (from media_summarize), the 3-second hook line, and the hashtag set — ready for the client to burn in captions on their own tim
Workflow — 15 tool steps
  1. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize an upload session for the raw take plus the paper-edit script and brand kit so the editing tools can reference them.
  2. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the upload; returns asset URNs for the raw take and the two reference PDFs.
  3. asset_create_folders — Create a tidy Vitalé/GenoBalance project folder to hold the raw take, intermediates and the three platform exports.
  4. asset_preview_file — Preview the raw take to confirm it decoded (duration/resolution/audio) before running the cleanup and cut.
  5. media_enhance_speech — Run speech enhancement on the raw take: isolates a de-noised, de-reverbed speech track from the HVAC hum and room echo and lifts voice clarity (async media job)
  6. asset_preview_file — Preview the speech-cleaned output to confirm the hum/echo is gone and the voice is intact before cutting.
  7. media_summarize — Summarize/transcribe the cleaned take to get a transcript of the spoken content — used ONLY to draft the delivered caption note (it does not drive the cut; quic
  8. video_create_quick_cut — Auto-generate the highlight sizzle: quick-cut selects the most visually engaging moments of the cleaned take down to a ~35-45s target duration (user_prompt = pr
  9. asset_preview_file — Preview the highlight master to confirm length and framing before resizing.
  10. video_resize — Resize the finished highlight master to a 9:16 vertical 1080x1920 Instagram Reel (subject-aware reframe, no stretch).
  11. video_resize — Resize the same master to a 9:16 vertical 1080x1920 YouTube Short.
  12. video_resize — Resize the same master to a 1:1 square 1080x1080 in-feed post.
  13. asset_preview_file — Preview the three platform exports to confirm framing/dimensions are correct and nothing is stretched.
  14. local_write_recipe_note — Emit the plain-text caption/subtitle-and-hook note: transcript of the take (from the media summary) + the 3-second hook + hashtag set (from the paper-edit scrip
  15. asset_copy_assets — Collect the highlight master and the three platform exports into the project delivery folder for hand-off.
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon video/audio task: 15 connector calls, 9 distinct Adobe tools — above the >=10 calls / >=6 distinct-tool floor for a motion+audio benchmark. Difficulty comes from a genuine multi-stage async media pipeline (media_enhance_speech [A] → media_summarize [A] → video_create_quick_cut [A] → three video_resize [A] exports), where the quick-cut must land a ~35-45s highlight at a controlled target duration and three platform deliverables must land at exact non-stretched dimensions (1080x1920 Reel, 1080x1920 Short, 1080x1080 feed). It respects every hard limit: no generative video, no from-scratch motion graphics, no burned-in subtitle rendering, no multi-track compositing, and NO speech/semantic script-driven trimming (video_create_quick_cut is explicitly visual-engagement-based, not content-aware — the spec uses it only for visual highlight selection, and the KEEP-line/subtitle intent is honored solely as a delivered plain-text caption note via local_write_recipe_note). video_resize does same-source reframing only, no upscaling beyond source (3840x2160 source downsized to 1080-tall targets); media tools INSPECT/clean/cut existing footage rather than synthesize new content.

AO-95 — Voltcast tech-channel delivery QC: audit the outsourced editor's YouTube master + vertical cut against the channel delivery-spec sheet (timecode-referenced video findings report) ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Audit the outsourced editor's Voltcast episode deliverables -- the 16:9 YouTube master and the 9:16 vertical cut -- against the channel's written delivery-spec sheet, and return a timestamp-referenced QC findings report (as a report PDF plus structured JSON) listing every spec drift: duration, resolution/fps, loudness, music-vs-narration balance, missing intro/outro, unsafe-margin overlays and off-brand text.

💰 ~$200 est.Freelancer.com13 tool calls7 distinct toolsanalyze
grounded in a real brief: Tech Channel Video Editor Needed
Full client brief
Voltcast is a fictional independent consumer-tech review channel run by Voltcast Media that publishes several times a week -- long-form reviews plus short vertical cuts for Shorts / Reels / TikTok. We outsource the actual editing to a rotating pool of junior editors, and before an episode goes live we run a delivery QC pass so nothing off-spec reaches the feed (the last episode published with the sponsor read buried under the music and the price-callout overlay clipped off the bottom on mobile, and we only caught it after it went out).

For this episode ('Voltcast E42 -- Budget Phone Shootout') you will receive four things: (1) voltcast_e42_master_16x9.mp4, the finished 4K 16:9 YouTube master the editor delivered; (2) voltcast_e42_vertical_9x16.mp4, the reformatted vertical Shorts/Reels cut derived from the same edit; (3) voltcast_delivery_spec.pdf, our written channel delivery-spec sheet; and (4) voltcast_brand_frames.png, a reference sheet of our correct intro end-card, lower-third, and price-callout overlay so you can compare on-screen text against the approved look. The delivery-spec sheet states the exact rules: long-form runtime must land in the 8:00-12:00 window; master must be 3840x2160 at 23.976 or 24 fps, H.264, with integrated loudness at -14 LUFS (+/-1) and true-peak at or below -1 dBTP; background music must sit at least 12 LU below narration during any spoken segment (no clashing under the sponsor read or verdict); the branded intro end-card must appear in the first 8 seconds and the subscribe/outro card in the final 15 seconds; every text overlay (lower-thirds, spec/price callouts) must sit inside the title-safe margin (5% inset) so it is not clipped, and the vertical 9:16 cut must keep those callouts inside the tighter mobile safe area; on-brand text must use the channel's approved lower-third style, not a default editor font.

The job is an audit, not a re-edit -- do NOT re-cut, re-grade, re-mix, or re-export the episode. Watch/inspect both deliverables, compare what is actually there against the spec sheet, and hand back a delivery-QC report that, for each issue found, gives the affected file, the timecode (or time range), a plain-language description of the drift, the exact spec clause it violates, the observed-vs-required value, and a severity (blocker / warning / nit). We expect you to catch things like: a runtime that overshoots the 12:00 ceiling, music that does NOT drop under the sponsor read, a price-callout overlay clipped outside the mobile safe area on the vertical cut, an outro card that is missing or too short, and a lower-third set in the wrong font. Deliver the report as a clean A4 report PDF plus the raw findings as a structured JSON file, and open the PDF with a one-page cover summary counting findings by severity. T
Deliverables (3)
  • Voltcast_E42_Delivery_QC.pdf — The primary deliverable: a clean A4 portrait delivery-QC report PDF. Opens with a one-page cover summary (go / no-go verdict + total findings by severity: blocker / warning / nit), then one row per fi
  • voltcast_e42_qc_findings.json — The raw structured findings emitted by local_write_recipe_note: an ordered list of records {file, timecode, drift, spec_clause, required_value, observed_value, severity} plus a summary block with coun
  • voltcast_qc_evidence_frames — The poster/keyframe previews and the spec-sheet page rasters gathered during inspection (the master's intro-card frame and sponsor-read frame, the vertical cut's clipped-callout frame, the rasterized
Workflow — 13 tool steps
  1. asset_create_folders — Stand up the CC asset workspace (e.g. /M02/deliverables, /spec, /findings) to hold the two supplied video deliverables, the delivery-spec PDF, the brand-referen
  2. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize an upload session for the client-supplied audit inputs: voltcast_e42_master_16x9.mp4, voltcast_e42_vertical_9x16.mp4, voltcast_delivery_spec.pdf, vol
  3. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the upload so the 16:9 master, the 9:16 vertical cut, the delivery-spec PDF and the brand-reference PNG are real CC assets with URNs the inspection ste
  4. media_summarize — Summarize voltcast_e42_master_16x9.mp4 to read its scene/content structure and audio: total runtime, whether an intro end-card appears in the first seconds, whe
  5. media_summarize — Summarize voltcast_e42_vertical_9x16.mp4 to read the reformatted cut: its duration, whether the price-callout overlay is fully in-frame on the tighter vertical
  6. media_summarize — Summarize voltcast_delivery_spec.pdf to pull out the machine-usable ground-truth rules the audit checks against: the 8:00-12:00 runtime window, the 4K/24fps/H.2
  7. document_convert_pdf — Convert voltcast_delivery_spec.pdf into per-page raster images so the exact clause text and any diagrammed safe-margin guides can be visually read and cited by
  8. asset_preview_file — Preview voltcast_e42_master_16x9.mp4 to pull its poster/keyframe so the intro end-card presence, the lower-third font, and the near-edge price-callout placement
  9. asset_preview_file — Preview voltcast_e42_vertical_9x16.mp4 to pull its poster/keyframe and confirm the reframed price-callout is clipped outside the mobile safe area on the vertica
  10. asset_inline_preview — Inline-preview voltcast_brand_frames.png so the approved intro end-card, the approved lower-third font/style, and the correct callout safe-margin placement are
  11. asset_inline_preview — Inline-preview the master poster frame, the vertical clipped-callout frame and the rasterized spec pages together at high detail to confirm each suspected drift
  12. local_write_recipe_note — Emit the structured QC findings: for each drift, a record {file, timecode, drift, spec_clause, required_value, observed_value, severity} cross-referencing the e
  13. document_convert_pdf — Render the written QC findings (cover summary with go/no-go verdict + per-finding rows) into the clean A4 portrait Delivery-QC report PDF for the managing edito
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon analyze/QC on the video modality: 13 connector calls, 7 distinct Adobe tools across three groups (asset workspace/upload/preview, media_summarize [A] video/audio inspection, document_convert_pdf) plus the single [L] recipe-note emit -- above the >=10-call / >=6-distinct-tool floor for an analyze task. Difficulty is in the analytic structure, not raster grunt-work: the agent must (a) extract the actual delivery rules from a supplied spec sheet via media_summarize + PDF raster rather than assuming them, (b) run media_summarize over TWO separate video deliverables (the 16:9 master and the 9:16 vertical cut) to read runtime, scene structure, intro/outro presence, and the crucial audio question of whether music ducks under narration, (c) preview each cut's poster frame and the brand reference to compare on-screen overlays (lower-third font, callout safe-margin, mobile-safe clipping) against the approved look, (d) distinguish an on-spec deliverable from the planted drifts (over-12:00 runtime, hot loudness, music not ducking under the sponsor read, callout clipped outside the mobile safe area on the vertical cut, missing vertical outro, wrong lower-third font), and (e) emit a structured, file+timecode-referenced, severity-ranked go/no-go findings report. It respects every hard limit: NOTHING is re-edited, re-cut, re-mixed or re-exported -- media_summarize only READS the v

AO-96 — SentinelMesh AI cybersecurity SaaS product video: brand-graded demo cut with 30s promo + 15s teaser cutdowns, cleaned VO, and on-brand callout / end-card graphic plates ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Turn SentinelMesh's raw screen recordings, product b-roll, scratch voiceover, logo, and brand color reference into a polished 90-120s AI-cybersecurity SaaS demo cut plus a 30s promo cutdown and 15s teaser (with a vertical social reframe), clean the VO, and build on-brand lower-third / call-out / graded end-card graphic plates and a vector logo — all packaged onto a Firefly board for the Premiere handoff.

💰 $900Upwork21 tool calls17 distinct toolseditlong-horizon
grounded in a real brief: Video Editor & Motion Graphics Designer for AI Cybersecurity SaaS Product Video
Full client brief
SentinelMesh is a B2B AI-powered cybersecurity platform that helps organizations reduce human cyber risk through real-time protection, adaptive phishing simulations, and in-the-moment learning nudges. We're preparing our next funding round and a website refresh, and we need a polished marketing and product-demonstration video plus social cutdowns. The finished pieces will run on our website hero, in sales decks, webinars, investor discussions, and across LinkedIn / X. We already have a strong foundation of material and want an editor to turn it into a professional, modern B2B SaaS product video (product footage + motion graphics + animated callouts + captions and visual storytelling — NOT a cartoon-style explainer).

Source material we're supplying: (1) two 1080p screen-capture recordings of the SentinelMesh console — the real-time threat dashboard and the adaptive-simulation builder; (2) a short b-roll clip of the product running on a laptop in a modern office; (3) a scratch voiceover WAV of our narration script, recorded in a noisy room, that needs cleaning before it can carry the edit; (4) our logo as a flat PNG; (5) a neutral, ungraded end-card hero still with negative space for a logo lockup; (6) a single brand color-reference frame representing the cool, confident, dark-mode look we want the piece graded toward; and (7) a messaging / talking-points CSV distilled from our whitepapers and website copy (the key claims, in priority order).

We need the freelancer to handle the full edit plus the supporting graphics, mapped to Adobe Creative Cloud connector tools with every step honestly tagged for execution mode:

A. AUDIO — clean the scratch voiceover so it can drive the cut: denoise / de-room the narration track (voice-only; not music mastering).

B. VIDEO — (1) summarize the raw recordings + b-roll so we agree on the key talking points and a logical flow before cutting; (2) produce the primary deliverable: one polished 90-120 second product-demo cut that surfaces the priority messaging; (3) produce a 30-second promotional cutdown and a 15-second teaser, each as its own tighter pass (repurposed from the same footage, not exact-timestamp trims); (4) reframe the 15-second teaser to a 9:16 vertical version for social, same length (no trimming).

C. BRAND END-CARD + LOOK — from the neutral end-card hero still, build the graded end-card the video closes on and that also locks the brand look the editor matches the master grade to: straighten, auto-tone baseline, push the cool dark-mode color temperature toward the brand reference, shape vibrance/saturation, and crop/resize clean to 1920x1080.

D. GRAPHIC PLATES (standalone PNG / SVG swatch-and-position references, NOT composited onto the video) — the on-brand overlay references the editor rebuilds/lays
Deliverables (11)
  • cleaned_voiceover.wav — Denoised / de-reverbed narration track, voice-only, suitable as the edit's VO bed (media_enhance_speech output).
  • sentinelmesh_demo_master_1080p.mp4 — Primary deliverable: polished 90-120s AI-cybersecurity product-demo cut assembled from the two screen recordings + b-roll, surfacing the priority messaging; 1920x1080 (H.264 target on export).
  • sentinelmesh_promo_cutdown_30s.mp4 — 30-second promotional cutdown produced as its own tighter Quick Cut pass from the same footage, 1920x1080 H.264 — repurposed, not an exact-timestamp trim of the master.
  • sentinelmesh_teaser_15s.mp4 — 15-second teaser produced as the tightest Quick Cut pass, 1920x1080 H.264, for social / top-of-funnel.
  • sentinelmesh_teaser_15s_vertical_9x16.mp4 — 9:16 (1080x1920) reframe of the 15s teaser, SAME length (no trimming), for LinkedIn / stories social placements.
  • graded_endcard_frame.jpg — One graded 1920x1080 end-card hero frame: straightened, auto-toned, temperature/vibrance-graded toward the cool dark-mode brand reference, with right-side negative space for the logo lockup — also the
  • sentinelmesh_logo.svg — Clean vectorized SVG of the SentinelMesh logo for scalable end-card and corner-bug use.
  • lower_third_bar_plate.png — Opaque lower-third bar plate frame (1920x1080 PNG): a horizontal region of the graded end-card canvas solid-filled with brand teal (#1FB6A6) to give the editor an exact on-brand bar swatch/position re
  • callout_badge_plate.png — Opaque call-out badge plate frame (1920x1080 PNG): a badge-shaped region of the same end-card canvas solid-filled with the deep-charcoal accent (#12151A) as an on-brand fact-call-out swatch/position r
  • social_endcard_variant.jpg — On-brand end-card variant with a subtle brand color overlay for the social teaser close, derived from the graded end-card frame.
  • sentinelmesh_handoff_board — Firefly board deep-link gathering the cleaned VO, all three video cuts + vertical reframe, graded end-card, logo SVG, both plates, and social end-card variant as one organized editable-asset set for t
Workflow — 21 tool steps
  1. asset_create_folders — Create a managed Creative Cloud project folder ('SentinelMesh_Product_Video') to hold every source + produced asset. Root of the deliverable package.
  2. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize upload of the client-supplied source files (two screen recordings, b-roll clip, scratch VO wav, logo png, end-card still, brand color reference, mess
  3. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the multipart uploads; returns stored CC asset handles for every source file — the chain's root inputs now live in the project.
  4. media_enhance_speech — Denoise/de-room the scratch narration WAV (voice-only) so it can carry the cut. Consumes the ingested VO track. [A] async-widget.
  5. media_summarize — Summarize the two screen recordings + b-roll (content/flow) so we agree on the key talking points and logical order before cutting; steered by the messaging CSV
  6. video_create_quick_cut — Primary deliverable: AI Quick Cut of the recordings + b-roll into a polished 90-120s product-demo master, prompted by the agreed talking points from step 5 + me
  7. video_create_quick_cut — Produce the 30-second promotional cutdown as its own tighter Quick Cut pass over the same footage (repurposed, NOT an exact-timestamp trim of the master), promp
  8. video_create_quick_cut — Produce the 15-second teaser as the tightest Quick Cut pass, prompt-focused on the single hero claim + product beauty shot. [A] async-widget.
  9. video_resize — Reframe the 15s teaser to 9:16 vertical (1080x1920) for social — SAME length, no trimming. Consumes the step-8 teaser. [A] async-widget.
  10. image_auto_straighten — Level the deliberately-tilted end-card hero still — first step of the end-card / brand-look grade chain. Consumes the ingested end-card still. [C].
  11. image_apply_auto_tone — Lay down an auto-tone baseline on the straightened end-card before the brand-look grade. Consumes step-10. [C].
  12. image_adjust_color_temperature — Shift the end-card's white balance toward the cool dark-mode look, matching the ingested brand color-reference frame. Consumes step-11 + the color reference. [C
  13. image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation — Shape vibrance/saturation so the teal UI accents pop against the charcoal for the confident dark-mode brand look. Consumes step-12. [C].
  14. image_crop_and_resize — Crop/resize the graded end-card clean to 1920x1080, preserving right-side negative space for the logo lockup -> graded end-card frame deliverable (also the lock
  15. image_vectorize — Vectorize the flat logo PNG to a clean scalable SVG for the end-card and corner bug. Consumes the ingested logo. [C].
  16. image_select_by_prompt — On a copy of the graded end-card frame reused as the (opaque) plate canvas, select a horizontal lower-third bar region. Consumes step-14. [C].
  17. image_fill_area — Solid-fill the selected region with brand teal (#1FB6A6) to produce the lower-third bar plate — an opaque 1920x1080 swatch/position reference (solid-color fill
  18. image_select_by_prompt — On the same plate canvas, select a rounded badge region for the on-screen fact call-out. Consumes step-17. [C].
  19. image_fill_area — Solid-fill the badge region with the deep-charcoal accent (#12151A) to produce the call-out badge plate — an opaque swatch/position reference (solid-color fill
  20. image_apply_color_overlay — Apply a subtle brand teal-charcoal color overlay to the graded end-card frame to produce the on-brand social teaser end-card variant. Consumes step-14. [C].
  21. create_firefly_board — Gather every produced deliverable (cleaned VO, all three cuts + vertical reframe, graded end-card, logo SVG, both plates, social end-card variant) into one Fire
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon flagship: 21 connector calls, 17 distinct Adobe tools, spanning four connector groups (asset/storage/board, video/audio [A], photo/raster grade [C], vector + solid-fill plates) with two parallel sub-chains that converge into one Premiere-handoff package — well past the >=12 calls / >=3 groups / >=6 distinct-tool flagship bar. Difficulty comes from (a) honoring the [A] async vs [C] headless split correctly — the four video/audio deliverables (cleaned VO, master, promo, teaser, vertical) are async-widget/product-doable, while the durable creative spine (5-link end-card grade, logo SVG, two selection->solid-fill plates, color-overlay variant, board) is fully headless-confirmed; (b) respecting the hard limits while still satisfying the brief's 'motion graphics / animated callouts / captions' intent — the connector does NOT composite text/graphics onto video, so the callouts, lower-third, and end-card are delivered as standalone graphic PLATES that the editor lays on the timeline; the three cutdowns are separate Quick Cut passes (NOT video-trim-to-timestamp); video_resize is same-length reframe only; image_fill_area is solid-color only; no generative fill, no text-to-image / from-scratch motion design, no AI object removal, no upscaling; (c) keeping every post-ingest step strictly output->input chained into a single coherent editable-asset package.

Client-supplied input assets: 8 total · 3 img · 3 video · 1 audio · 1 data

content
priority,claim,proof_point
1,'Most cyber breaches originate from human error, not technical failure.','Industry reports show over 90% of successful attacks exploit human vulnerabilities like phishing.'
2,'SentinelMesh provides always-on, real-time threat detection at every interaction point.','Our AI continuously monitors user behavior and email traffic, identifying anomalies before they escalate.'
3,'Our adaptive simulations identify and train your highest-risk employees first.','Leveraging behavioral analytics, we tailor training modules to individual weaknesses, maximizing impact.'
4,'Deliver in-the-moment coaching that transforms risky clicks into secure decisions.','Contextual micro-learnings are delivered exactly when a user exhibits risky behavior, reinforcing best practices instantly.'
5,'SentinelMesh proactively hardens your human firewall, significantly reducing attack surface.','By empowering employees with timely knowledge, we measurably decrease successful phishing and social engineering attempts.'
6,'Gain unparalleled visibility and actionable insights into your organization''s human risk posture.','Our dashboard provides clear metrics on user vulnerability, training efficacy, and overall security improvement.'
7,'Automate and scale your security awareness program without adding staff overhead.','SentinelMesh integrates seamlessly, handling everything from threat simulation to personalized training delivery.'

AO-97 — Pulsevault club-night recap: assemble a 30s high-energy sizzle reel from supplied raw footage, cleaned hype VO, graded cover frame + vector logo lockup ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Take our raw club-night phone clips plus a hero cover still, venue logo, and a rough hype voiceover, and assemble a post-ready 30-second high-energy recap: an AI sizzle cut reframed to 1080x1920, a cleaned VO track, an event-info caption sheet, and a fully color-graded cover frame with a crisp vector logo lockup.

💰 ~$120 est.Freelancer.com31 tool calls19 distinct toolscreatelong-horizon
grounded in a real brief: High-Energy Club Recap Videos
Full client brief
Pulsevault is a downtown nightclub, and we run a themed event every Friday. We need a punchy 30-second recap of last weekend to post on Instagram Reels and TikTok to promote the next one — high-energy, fast-paced, and capturing the vibrant atmosphere of the room. We already have all the raw material; we just need it cut together and finished properly. Here is what we're handing off: (a) three raw vertical clips shot on a phone from the DJ booth and the floor (crowd hands-up, laser/haze sweep, and a confetti drop), all a bit dark and mixed-lit with the club's colored wash; (b) one hero still grabbed from the footage that we want turned into the cover/thumbnail frame; (c) our venue wordmark logo as a flat PNG; (d) a rough spoken hype line recorded on a phone in the office (room noise + reverb) that we want cleaned up to lay under the intro; and (e) an event-info sheet (next date, doors, ticket link, hashtag) as data for the on-screen captions. Deliverables: 1x final ~30-second 9:16 MP4 ready to post on Instagram and TikTok (Full HD); a matching cleaned voiceover track; an on-screen captions/event-info asset; and an exported, fully color-graded 1080x1920 cover frame with our logo locked up in the corner as a crisp vector so it stays sharp. Keep the look on-brand: deep club darkness, punchy neon magenta/cyan pop, energetic. Source assets should stay in our Creative Cloud folder so tweaks are easy each week. NOTE up front: frame-accurate beat-synced cuts, hard transitions, animated motion graphics, and music syncing are a manual Premiere/After Effects finish and are out of scope for the automated connector pass — the automated pass delivers the AI sizzle assembly, the 9:16 reframe, the VO cleanup, the captions asset, and the graded cover frame; our editor does the final timeline polish on top.
Deliverables (6)
  • pulsevault_recap_9x16.mp4 — 1x vertical 1080x1920 (9:16) MP4, ~30s, H.264 Full HD, AI sizzle assembly of the three raw club clips reframed to full-frame vertical, Instagram Reels & TikTok ready. Produced by the [A] async video c
  • hype_intro_clean.wav — Cleaned hype-intro voiceover: isolated speech stem from media_enhance_speech (room hiss, HVAC rumble and reverb suppressed), WAV, ready to lay under the reel intro. Background/reverb stems also return
  • recap_captions.json — On-screen captions / event-info cue sheet (6 timed cues: venue, this-Friday, next date, doors, ticket link, hashtag) stored alongside the project in the client's Creative Cloud folder for the editor t
  • cover_frame_1080x1920.png — Exported, fully color-graded vertical cover/thumbnail, PNG 1080x1920: straightened, auto-toned, dark-portions lifted, on-brand club grade (deep darkness, neon magenta/cyan pop via color-temperature +
  • PULSEVAULT_logo.svg — Clean vectorized venue wordmark (SVG) produced from the flat logo PNG, used as the scalable corner lockup on the cover frame and reusable for future weekly recaps. [C]
  • recap_package_folder (CC handoff) — A single dated handoff folder in the client's Creative Cloud gathering the deliverables (reel MP4, cleaned VO WAV, captions JSON, graded cover PNG, logo SVG) plus the kept-in-CC source references, so
Workflow — 31 tool steps
  1. asset_initialize_file_upload — Client supplies the hero cover still; initialize a block upload of hero_cover_still.png into the client's Creative Cloud folder (media_type image/png) to start
  2. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the block upload from step 1 so the hero still is a real CC asset with a fetchable URL to drive the raster cover-frame chain.
  3. image_auto_straighten — Level the ~3.5-degree tilt from the handheld grab. Consumes the finalized hero-still URL from step 2.
  4. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto exposure/contrast baseline on the underexposed night frame. Consumes step 3's output URL.
  5. image_adjust_dark_portions — Lift the crushed club-dark shadows so the crowd reads without killing the deep-darkness look. Consumes step 4's output URL.
  6. image_adjust_color_temperature — Tune white balance toward the on-brand cool-club base so the neon reads cleanly against the darkness. Consumes step 5's output URL.
  7. image_adjust_hsl — Targeted HSL: push magenta and cyan for the punchy neon club-pop grade while keeping shadows deep. Consumes step 6's output URL.
  8. image_select_subject — Auto-select the foreground silhouette to drive a selective pop. Consumes step 7's graded image and returns a maskURI.
  9. image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation — Apply a vibrance/saturation lift only within the subject mask from step 8 (subject pop) on the graded image from step 7, so the foreground figure separates from
  10. image_apply_halftone — Add a tasteful halftone/print-screen texture for a gritty club-flyer feel. Consumes step 9's output URL.
  11. image_add_grain — Layer fine film grain over the halftone for the finished moody night surface. Consumes step 10's output URL.
  12. image_crop_and_resize — Subject-aware crop to the exact 1080x1920 (9:16) cover frame, keeping the foreground figure on a rule-of-thirds line. Consumes step 11's output URL.
  13. asset_initialize_file_upload — Bring the client's flat venue wordmark (pulsevault_wordmark_logo.png) into CC via a block upload so it can be vectorized for the lockup.
  14. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the logo upload from step 13 into a real CC asset URL.
  15. image_vectorize — Vectorize the flat wordmark PNG from step 14 into a clean scalable SVG (PULSEVAULT_logo.svg) for the corner lockup. The cover+logo composite lockup is the one h
  16. asset_inline_preview — Pull the graded cover frame (step 12) and the vector logo (step 15) back as base64 to visually QC the grade, crop, and that the wordmark reads crisp before hand
  17. asset_initialize_file_upload — Upload raw_clip_crowd_handsup_01.mp4 (media_type video/mp4) into the project folder to feed the video chain.
  18. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize clip 01 into a real CC video assetId.
  19. asset_initialize_file_upload — Upload raw_clip_laser_haze_02.mp4 into the project folder.
  20. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize clip 02 into a real CC video assetId.
  21. asset_initialize_file_upload — Upload raw_clip_confetti_drop_03.mp4 into the project folder.
  22. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize clip 03 into a real CC video assetId. Steps 17-22 give the quick_cut tool its three source assetIds.
  23. video_create_quick_cut — AI sizzle/highlight assembly across the three finalized club clips (steps 18/20/22), target_duration ~30s, user_prompt for high-energy fast-paced club-recap pac
  24. video_resize — Reframe/resize the sizzle cut from step 23 to a clean full-frame 1080x1920 (9:16), SAME length (no trim). [A] async. Produces pulsevault_recap_9x16.mp4.
  25. asset_initialize_file_upload — Upload the noisy hype_intro_scratch.wav (media_type audio/wav) into the project folder for cleanup.
  26. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the hype-intro VO into a real CC audio assetId.
  27. media_enhance_speech — Clean the hype-intro voiceover from step 26 — isolate the speech stem from room hiss/HVAC rumble/reverb. [A] async (speech-only, not music/loudness). Produces h
  28. asset_initialize_file_upload — Upload the event_info_captions.json cue sheet (media_type application/json) into the project folder as the on-screen event-info/text asset for the editor.
  29. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the captions/event-info cue sheet (recap_captions.json) into the client's CC project folder so source + captions stay with the project.
  30. create_firefly_board — Assemble the produced assets — graded cover frame (step 12), vector logo (step 15), and reframed recap reel (step 24) — into a Firefly Boards deep-link so the p
  31. asset_copy_assets — Copy the final deliverables (graded cover, logo SVG, cleaned VO, captions, reel) into a single dated handoff folder in the client's Creative Cloud so source + d
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon flagship: 31 connector calls, 19 distinct Adobe tools, across 3 groups (image raster/grade, asset/board/storage, video/audio). The raster grade-and-cover-frame spine is a genuine 10-step headless [C] chain with a real masked selective edit (select_subject -> masked vibrance subject-pop). It is honestly mode-tagged: the video sizzle/reframe and VO cleanup are [A] async (entitled, product-widget-polled, not headless-retrievable), and the spec states up front that frame-accurate beat-synced cuts, hard transitions, motion graphics and music sync are NOT connector-doable and are the flagged manual Premiere/After Effects finish — so nothing from the not-available list is claimed as a core deliverable. This is a genuine 'create' task (assemble a NEW deliverable from SUPPLIED raw clips + stills + logo + VO, non-generative) that reads as a straightforward 30s recap but forces the agent to recognize the honest Adobe path: a long headless grade pipeline on the cover still FIRST, then async video assembly + reframe + VO cleanup — while avoiding unavailable capabilities (no text-to-image B-roll, no compositing the logo onto the frame, no AI object removal, no upscaling, no timeline-trim-to-timestamp). The single non-connector composite (logo lockup placement) is named as the one human-finish step. Well above the >=10 calls / >=6 distinct-tool flagship floor.

AO-98 — Velvet Hour Sessions live-set footage review: multi-clip raw-concert audit, best-take selection + unusable-clip cull, delivered as a shot-selection log with a flagged-frame contact-sheet PDF ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Review every raw concert clip from the Velvet Hour Sessions multi-cam shoot, judge keepers vs unusable takes, and deliver a structured shot-selection log (per-clip verdict, reason, best usable stretch, usability rating, coverage tag) plus a contact-sheet PDF of the flagged best-take frames.

💰 $25Upwork13 tool calls9 distinct toolsanalyze
grounded in a real brief: Davinci Resolve Editor
Full client brief
Velvet Hour Sessions is a fictional intimate live-music night run by the Velvet Hour Room, a small arts-and-culture venue that films one acoustic set a month for its social channels (Instagram, Facebook, YouTube). Last Thursday we filmed the singer-songwriter 'Marlowe Fenn' doing a four-song stripped-back set on three cameras — a locked wide, a roaming handheld, and a close-up on the guitar/hands — and the card dump gave us a folder of a dozen-ish raw clips of very mixed quality. Before our editor builds the actual cut we need a fresh, disciplined pair of eyes to do the footage-review pass first: watch every raw clip end to end, decide which takes are genuinely usable and which are junk, and hand us back a selection log we can build the timeline from. Do NOT cut, grade, add music, or produce a finished video — this is a review-and-select job, exactly the first stage of the edit. You will receive: (1) velvet_hour_raw_clips (the folder of raw concert clips — some are clean keepers, several are unusable: one badly out of focus during the second song, one with a big camera bump / lens smack, one that's mostly dead air and tuning chatter before the song starts, one near-duplicate of a better wide take, and one where the handheld drifts off the performer for half the clip), (2) shoot_notes.csv (the runner's rough log: camera, rough song, and a one-line note per clip like 'wide, song 2, soft?' or 'handheld, song 4, drifts'), and (3) velvet_hour_brand_note.pdf (a short one-pager on what the venue's social edits are for and the 'clean, coherent, story-first, no gimmicks' house style so your keep/cut calls match how the footage will actually be used). Go through each clip against the notes and against what you actually see and hear, and return a shot-selection log that, for every clip, gives: the clip name, which camera/song it is, a KEEP / MAYBE / REJECT verdict, a plain-language reason (what's good or what's wrong — soft focus, camera bump, dead air, drift, redundant, clean), a usability rating (1-5), a note on the strongest usable stretch of the clip described in words (e.g. 'best from the first chorus onward, once focus settles'), and a coverage tag (wide / handheld / close-up) so we can see we have each angle for each song. Finish with a short summary: total clips, how many KEEP vs MAYBE vs REJECT, any song or angle where we're now short on usable coverage, and a one-line recommendation on whether we have enough to build the cut or need a re-shoot of anything. Deliver the log as a clean readable PDF plus the raw findings as a structured data file, and include a contact-sheet PDF showing one representative frame from each KEEP/MAYBE clip (with the clip name under each) so the director can eyeball the selects at a glance. A4 / letter is fine; this is for th
Deliverables (3)
  • VelvetHour_ShotSelection_Log.pdf — The primary deliverable: a clean, readable A4/letter shot-selection log PDF. One row per raw clip with clip name, camera + song, KEEP / MAYBE / REJECT verdict, plain-language reason (soft focus / came
  • velvet_hour_selects.json — The raw structured findings emitted by local_write_recipe_note: an ordered list of records {clip_name, camera, song, verdict, reason, usability_rating, best_usable_stretch, coverage_tag} plus a summar
  • VelvetHour_Selects_ContactSheet.pdf — A contact-sheet PDF showing one representative still frame from each KEEP/MAYBE clip, with the clip name captioned per frame, so the director can eyeball the selects at a glance. Built by converting t
Workflow — 13 tool steps
  1. asset_create_folders — Stand up the CC asset workspace (e.g. /M05/raw_clips, /brand, /frames, /findings) to hold the supplied raw clips, notes, brand one-pager, the pulled inspection
  2. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize an upload session for the client-supplied review inputs: the velvet_hour_raw_clips folder, shoot_notes.csv, and velvet_hour_brand_note.pdf.
  3. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the upload so every raw clip, the shoot-notes CSV and the brand-note PDF are real CC assets with URNs the inspection steps can read. The chain's root i
  4. media_summarize — Summarize velvet_hour_brand_note.pdf to pull out the venue's judgement ground truth: the 'clean/coherent/story-first, no gimmicks' house style, the reject-if-so
  5. media_summarize — Run a scene/transcript-style content summary over each raw concert clip to inspect what is actually on it end to end (which song, spoken/tuning chatter vs perfo
  6. asset_inline_preview — Inline-preview each raw clip (poster/representative frame + scrub) to VISUALLY inspect the picture the summary can't fully convey: focus sharpness, exposure/ste
  7. asset_preview_file — Pull one representative still frame per clip (and an extra frame at the strongest usable stretch) via file preview, giving concrete per-clip inspection frames t
  8. image_select_by_prompt — On each pulled frame, select the 'main performer / musician on stage' region as an INSPECTION locator — used to judge whether the performer is actually in frame
  9. asset_inline_preview — Inline-preview the isolated performer regions at higher detail to confirm each suspected defect (soft focus, drift, bump reframe) before it is written as a REJE
  10. local_write_recipe_note — Emit the structured shot-selection findings: for each clip a record {clip_name, camera, song, verdict(KEEP/MAYBE/REJECT), reason, usability_rating, best_usable_
  11. asset_copy_assets — Copy just the representative frames of the KEEP/MAYBE clips (per the verdicts in velvet_hour_selects.json) into a /frames/selects set, dropping the REJECT frame
  12. document_convert_pdf — Convert the retained KEEP/MAYBE representative frames (each named by its clip) into a single multi-image contact-sheet PDF so the director can eyeball the selec
  13. document_convert_pdf — Convert the written shot-selection findings (per-clip rows + coverage summary + build-vs-reshoot recommendation) emitted at step 10 into the clean readable A4/l
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon analyze/audit: 13 connector calls, 8 distinct Adobe tools across four groups (asset workspace/upload/preview/copy, media_summarize [A], image_select_by_prompt as inspection locator, document_convert_pdf export) plus the single [L] recipe-note emit — above the >=10-call / >=6-distinct-tool floor for an analyze task. Difficulty is analytic, not grunt-work: the agent must (a) extract the venue's actual judgement rules from the supplied brand-note via media_summarize rather than assuming them, (b) run a content/scene summary over each raw clip AND visually preview each one (some defects are audible/temporal — dead air, tuning chatter — others are purely visual — soft focus, camera bump, drift), (c) use image_select_by_prompt purely as an INSPECTION locator on the pulled frames to judge whether the performer is actually in frame and sharp, (d) cross-check every clip against a deliberately unreliable runner CSV so it trusts what it sees over the note, (e) render a disciplined KEEP/MAYBE/REJECT verdict with reason, usability rating, best-usable-stretch note and coverage tag for each clip, and (f) roll up a song x angle coverage matrix and a build-vs-reshoot recommendation. It respects every hard limit: NOTHING is cut, graded or composed — no NLE timeline, no video_create_quick_cut / video_resize (this is a review pass, not an edit), no timestamp-accurate trimming; media_s

AO-99 — Lunara Silver Reel repurpose pack: reframe one product-showcase clip to 9:16 / 1:1 / 16:9, AI sizzle cutdown, cleaned founder VO, caption sheet + vectorized wordmark end-card asset ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Take Lunara Silver's one raw vertical pendant-showcase clip, raw founder voiceover, product hero still, and flat wordmark PNG, and repurpose them into a platform pack: 9:16 / 1:1 / 16:9 reframes of the clip (same runtime), an AI sizzle cutdown for the Meta ad, a cleaned founder VO, a caption/scheduling sheet, and a graded end-card still with a vectorized wordmark.

💰 ~$120 est.Freelancer.com25 tool calls17 distinct toolseditlong-horizon
grounded in a real brief: Video Editor for Traya Jewels
Full client brief
Lunara Silver is a growing D2C brand of handcrafted sterling-silver jewellery. This week's Reel delegation is our new "Moonlit Pendant" launch, and we need the raw footage repurposed into everything our channels eat. I'm uploading four assets: (1) product_reel_moonlit_raw.mp4 — a ~40-second vertical hand-held product-showcase clip of the pendant being lifted, turned in the light, and worn on the neck, with native room audio; (2) founder_vo_raw.wav — a phone-recorded voiceover from our founder Priya Nair ("Every Lunara piece is poured, not printed…") with room hiss and some AC hum; (3) pendant_hero_raw.jpg — a product hero still shot slightly tilted and cool/flat that we want for the closing end-card frame and the ad thumbnail; (4) lunara_wordmark_flat.png — our flat wordmark logo we want razor-sharp on the end card and reusable on future covers.

What I need back:
- The product clip REPURPOSED into three platform formats at the SAME runtime (reframe only, do not trim the story): a 9:16 (1080x1920) version for Instagram Reels & TikTok, a 1:1 (1080x1080) version for the feed, and the original 16:9 (1920x1080) master for YouTube / website.
- ONE fast-paced AI sizzle/highlight cutdown (~12-15s target) of the same footage for the paid Meta ad — pick the strongest hero moments (the light-catch turn, the on-neck reveal).
- The founder voiceover cleaned up (denoise / de-hum), voice only, ready to lay under the cutdowns.
- A short text caption / scheduling summary of what happens in the footage, for our post copy and content calendar.
- A polished, on-brand END-CARD still built from the hero photo — straightened, auto-toned, warmed slightly toward a soft champagne look, pendant popped against a softened background, cropped to a clean 1080x1920 closing-frame — plus our wordmark vectorized to a crisp SVG for the end card and future use.
- Drop everything into one Creative Cloud review board deep-link so I can sign off in one place.

Keep sources in our Creative Cloud folder so tweaks are easy. Heads-up: I know frame-accurate cuts, animated text overlays, hard transitions, motion graphics and beat-synced music are a manual Premiere finish — that's the one step my in-house editor will polish on top; from you I need the automated repurpose pass (the reframes, the sizzle, the VO cleanup, the caption sheet, and the end-card asset kit).
Deliverables (9)
  • moonlit_reel_9x16.mp4 — Instagram Reels / TikTok reframe of the product-showcase clip to 1080x1920 (9:16), SAME runtime as the source (subject-aware reframe only, no trim). Produced by video_resize [A]; the frame-accurate cu
  • moonlit_feed_1x1.mp4 — Feed reframe of the same clip to 1080x1080 (1:1), same runtime as source (reframe only, no trim). video_resize [A].
  • moonlit_master_16x9.mp4 — YouTube / website master reframe of the same clip to 1920x1080 (16:9), same runtime as source (reframe only, no trim). video_resize [A].
  • moonlit_sizzle_ad.mp4 — AI sizzle / highlight cutdown (~12-15s target) of the product-showcase footage for the paid Meta ad, assembling the strongest hero moments (light-catch turn, on-neck reveal). Produced by video_create_
  • founder_vo_clean.wav — Cleaned founder voiceover: isolated speech stem from media_enhance_speech (room hiss, AC hum and reverb suppressed), WAV, voice-only, ready to lay under the cutdowns. Background/reverb stems also retu
  • moonlit_caption_sheet.txt — Short text caption / scheduling summary of the footage's content (scene beats / transcript summary) from media_summarize, for the post copy and content calendar.
  • moonlit_endcard_1080x1920.png — Polished on-brand closing end-card still built from the hero photo: auto-straightened, auto-toned, warmed toward a soft champagne look, pendant popped against a softened (masked Blur-Background preset
  • lunara_wordmark.svg — Clean vectorized brand wordmark (SVG) from the flat PNG, razor-sharp at any size, for the end-card lockup and reusable on future covers.
  • moonlit_review_board — Creative Cloud / Firefly Board deep-link assembling the finished repurpose pack (a reframed reel, the sizzle, the graded end-card still, the vector wordmark) for one-click founder sign-off.
Workflow — 25 tool steps
  1. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize a block upload of the client's raw vertical clip product_reel_moonlit_raw.mp4 (media_type video/mp4) into the Lunara Silver Creative Cloud project fo
  2. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the clip upload from step 1 into a real CC video assetId — the single source the four video steps all reference.
  3. video_resize — Reframe the vertical product-showcase clip to a clean full-frame 1080x1920 (9:16) for Reels/TikTok, SAME runtime (subject-aware reframe, no trim). [A] async-wid
  4. video_resize — Reframe the same finalized clip asset to 1080x1080 (1:1) for the feed, same runtime (no trim). [A] async-widget. FINAL video deliverable.
  5. video_resize — Reframe the same clip asset to 1920x1080 (16:9) as the YouTube/website master, same runtime (no trim). [A] async-widget. FINAL video deliverable.
  6. video_create_quick_cut — AI sizzle/highlight assembly from the same finalized clip asset, target ~12-15s, user_prompt for fast-paced hero moments (light-catch turn, on-neck reveal) for
  7. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize a block upload of the raw founder voiceover founder_vo_raw.wav (media_type audio/wav) into the project folder for cleanup.
  8. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the voiceover upload from step 7 into a real CC audio assetId.
  9. media_enhance_speech — Clean the founder voiceover from step 8 — isolate the speech stem from room hiss / AC hum / reverb (voice-only, not music/loudness). [A] async-widget. Produces
  10. media_summarize — Summarize the product-showcase footage's content into a short text caption / scheduling sheet (scene beats / transcript summary) for the post copy and content c
  11. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize a block upload of the raw product hero still pendant_hero_raw.jpg (media_type image/jpeg) to start the [C] end-card raster chain.
  12. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the hero-still upload from step 11 into a real CC image asset with a fetchable URL to drive the raster chain.
  13. image_auto_straighten — Level the ~4-degree tilt on the hero product still. Consumes the finalized hero URL from step 12.
  14. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the straightened still to fix the flat/under-saturated exposure. Consumes step 13's output.
  15. image_adjust_color_temperature — Warm the cool white balance toward the soft champagne end-card look. Consumes step 14's output.
  16. image_select_by_prompt — Select the silver pendant subject by prompt on the warmed still, producing a mask to isolate it from the background. Consumes step 15's image.
  17. image_invert_selection — Invert the pendant mask to target the background only for the softening step. Consumes step 16's mask against step 15's image.
  18. image_apply_preset — Apply a masked Adaptive 'Blur Background / Subject Pop' preset (image_apply_preset supports masked Adaptive presets per the capability sheet) fed the inverted b
  19. image_crop_and_resize — Subject-aware crop/resize of the popped still to the exact 1080x1920 vertical closing-frame. Consumes step 18's output. FINAL end-card still deliverable.
  20. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize a block upload of the flat wordmark lunara_wordmark_flat.png (media_type image/png) so it can be vectorized for the end-card lockup.
  21. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the wordmark upload from step 20 into a real CC image asset URL.
  22. image_vectorize — Vectorize the flat wordmark PNG from step 21 into clean closed SVG paths (lunara_wordmark.svg) for a razor-sharp end-card lockup and future reuse. The end-card
  23. asset_inline_preview — Pull the graded end-card still (step 19) and the vector wordmark (step 22) back as base64 to visually QC the grade, crop, pendant pop, and that the wordmark rea
  24. create_firefly_board — Assemble the finished repurpose pack — a reframed reel (step 3), the sizzle cutdown (step 6), the graded end-card still (step 19) and the vector wordmark (step
  25. asset_copy_assets — Copy all finals (three reframes, sizzle, cleaned VO, caption sheet, end-card still, vector wordmark) into one dated handoff folder in the client's Creative Clou
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon flagship: 25 connector calls, 17 distinct Adobe tools, across 3 groups (video/audio [A], asset upload/board/copy [C], image raster [C]) — well past the >=10 calls / >=6 distinct-tool floor. Difficulty comes from repurposing ONE source clip into four honest video outputs (three same-length reframes + an AI sizzle) at the async [A] tier, a real voice-only speech cleanup and a content summary, PLUS a genuine 7-step masked end-card raster spine (straighten -> auto-tone -> temperature -> select-by-prompt -> invert -> masked Subject-Pop/Blur-Background preset -> crop) and a logo vectorize, then two fan-in convergence steps (Firefly board + CC handoff folder). Execution modes are honestly tagged: the four video/audio steps are [A] async-widget (entitled, product-widget-polled, not headless-retrievable), while the raster/asset/vectorize/board/copy steps are [C] headless. It respects every hard limit: video_resize is same-length reframe (no trim), video_create_quick_cut is AI highlight assembly (not frame-accurate timeline editing), and the spec states up front that frame-accurate cuts, text overlays, transitions, motion graphics and beat-synced music are NOT connector-doable and are the flagged manual Premiere finish — so nothing from the not-available list is claimed as a core deliverable. No generative fill / outpaint / object-removal / text-to-image / from-scratch art /
