AO-100 — Vela Studio podcast batch: clean and de-noise three raw interview audio tracks into release-ready enhanced masters + episode cleanup notes ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Inspect three raw interview-podcast audio tracks, run Adobe speech enhancement on each to remove noise and lift dialogue clarity, then deliver the three enhanced masters plus a per-episode cleanup note zipped into one hand-off folder.

💰 ~$120 est.Freelancer.com10 tool calls4 distinct toolsedit
grounded in a real brief: Polish Three Podcast Episodes
Full client brief
Vela Studio, a small independent podcast network, is prepping the next drop of its interview show "Long Table" and needs three episodes' audio polished for release. Each episode was recorded as a raw interview and the microphone audio was captured separately from the camera, so what we're handing you is three standalone dialogue tracks (episode_047_raw.wav, episode_048_raw.wav, episode_049_raw.mp3), each roughly 28-34 minutes of two people talking. They sound like real field recordings: audible room tone / HVAC hum in the background, a laptop-fan whir under episode 48, occasional mouth clicks and lip smacks, uneven levels between host and guest, and a couple of spots where a chair creaks or a phone buzzes on the table. We are NOT asking you to re-edit the conversation or cut it down - leave the words and the running order exactly as they are. What we need is the "sounds ready for immediate release on YouTube and the usual podcast platforms" pass: gentle but real noise reduction, cleaner speech, and a consistent, comfortable listening level across all three, so a listener never has to reach for the volume knob between episodes. For each of the three tracks: (1) first give the raw file a quick listen-through / content pass so we have a one-line note on what's actually on the tape and where the worst noise sits; (2) then run Adobe's speech-enhancement clean-up to knock down the background noise, hum and clicks and bring the two voices forward to a clear, even, broadcast-style level; (3) hand back the cleaned track as a standalone enhanced master. Deliverables: three enhanced audio masters (episode_047_enhanced, 048, 049), a short plain-English cleanup note per episode (what was on the raw tape, what the enhancement fixed) so our producer can spot-check before publishing, and everything zipped into one hand-off folder. Keep the originals untouched - the enhanced files should be new files, not overwrites.
Deliverables (5)
  • episode_047_enhanced.wav — Enhanced master of episode 047: room tone / HVAC hum reduced, mouth clicks softened, host/guest dialogue brought forward to a clean even broadcast-style level. Same length and running order as the raw
  • episode_048_enhanced.wav — Enhanced master of episode 048: continuous laptop-fan whir and room tone knocked down, plosive pops tamed, the phone-buzz event minimized, voices lifted to a clear consistent level matching the other
  • episode_049_enhanced.wav — Enhanced master of episode 049: boxy room reflection and background rumble reduced, the quieter guest raised toward the host so both read clearly, speech clarified to a comfortable level consistent ac
  • cleanup_notes.txt — Plain-English per-episode cleanup note (three short entries): what was on each raw tape (dominant noise, level issues, notable events) and what the speech-enhancement pass fixed, so Vela Studio's prod
  • Vela_LongTable_audio_delivery.zip — One hand-off folder zipping the three enhanced masters plus cleanup_notes.txt, ready to send back to the producer; originals kept untouched (enhanced files are new files).
Workflow — 10 tool steps
  1. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize an upload session for the three supplied raw interview audio tracks so the inspection/enhancement tools can reference them as CC assets.
  2. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the upload; returns the CC asset URNs for episode_047_raw / 048_raw / 049_raw used by every downstream inspection/enhancement step.
  3. media_summarize — Content/listen-through pass on episode 047: inspect the raw track for what is spoken and where the worst room-tone/hum/clicks sit (feeds the per-episode cleanup
  4. media_enhance_speech — Run Adobe speech enhancement on episode 047: reduce room tone/HVAC hum, soften mouth clicks, and bring the dialogue forward to a clean even broadcast level. New
  5. media_summarize — Content pass on episode 048: flag the continuous laptop-fan whir, plosive pops and the ~12-minute phone-buzz event for the cleanup note.
  6. media_enhance_speech — Speech-enhance episode 048: knock down the fan whir and room tone, tame plosives, minimize the buzz, lift both voices to a level consistent with 047.
  7. media_summarize — Content pass on episode 049 (the MP3): note the boxy room reflection, background rumble and the quieter guest for the cleanup note.
  8. media_enhance_speech — Speech-enhance episode 049: reduce room boom and rumble, raise the quiet guest toward the host, clarify speech to a level consistent across the batch.
  9. local_write_recipe_note — Emit the plain-English per-episode cleanup note (what was on each raw tape + what enhancement fixed) from the three content passes, for the producer to spot-che
  10. zip_outputs — Single unavoidable local packaging step: zip the three enhanced masters produced by the chain plus the cleanup note into one hand-off folder; originals kept unt
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon for the audio modality: 10 connector calls, 4 distinct Adobe tools, sitting in the ~6-12 band the sheet allows for audio tasks. The core difficulty is a genuine batch: three separate real-world recordings, each with a different defect profile (HVAC hum + clicks; constant fan whir + phone-buzz; boxy room + quiet guest), each run through an inspect->enhance sub-chain, then a local notes step and a single packaging step converging into one deliverable. It respects every hard limit: media_enhance_speech is the intended speech-cleanup tool (real de-noise / level lift on a supplied recording, not generative synthesis); media_summarize only INSPECTS the raw tape to author the cleanup note; no re-editing/re-cutting of the conversation (per the brief), no video work, no generative audio, no text-to-speech, no upscaling, no banned image capabilities, no out-of-palette asset_download_file. Enhanced files are new files; originals untouched.

content
Vela Studio // Long Table Podcast - Episode Delivery Brief

Hi Team,
Please find raw audio for 'Long Table' episodes 047, 048, 049: episode_047_raw.wav, episode_048_raw.wav, episode_049_raw.mp3 (049 came off the recorder as a compressed MP3). Mic audio was captured separately from camera.

Crucially, **DO NOT re-edit or shorten the core conversation.** Focus on gentle noise reduction, speech cleanup, and a consistent, comfortable listening level across all three.

Target output filenames: episode_047_enhanced, episode_048_enhanced, episode_049_enhanced (keep them as clean WAV masters at the same length as the raw tape). Please provide a brief one-line note per episode detailing tape content and any fixes applied. Deliver all final files in a single zipped folder.

Thanks,
Vela Studio

AO-101 — Northbound Signal podcast: pre-release audio QC pass on three raw episode recordings — noise/hiss/clipping/level audit with a cleaned reference track and marked-up findings report ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Run a pre-release audio QC pass on the three raw Northbound Signal mic recordings — audit each for hiss, clipping, uneven levels and dead-air against the delivery spec, produce one denoised reference track for A/B comparison, and return a timestamped, per-episode audio QC findings report ranking each defect by severity.

💰 ~$200 est.Freelancer.com12 tool calls7 distinct toolsanalyze
grounded in a real brief: Polish Three Podcast Episodes
Full client brief
Northbound Signal is a fictional independent interview podcast produced by Meridian Audio Collective — long-form conversations with founders and makers, released weekly on YouTube and the usual podcast platforms. We recorded three new episodes back-to-back and the raw mic tracks are captured separately from the cameras (a single mixed-down mono/stereo voice file per episode, roughly 28-32 minutes each). Before these go to our editor for the final cut, we need a fresh set of ears to tell us exactly what's wrong with the audio so nothing embarrassing ships. Our last drop had a whole segment where the guest's lav was clipping on every laugh and we only caught it after publish, plus one episode had an HVAC hum under the whole intro. We are NOT asking you to edit or master the episodes — that is our editor's job on the timeline. We want an audit. You will receive: (1) three raw voice recordings — ep01_raw_mix.wav (host + guest, guest mic runs hot with intermittent clipping on peaks), ep02_raw_mix.wav (steady room hiss / HVAC hum under the whole thing, low broadband noise floor), ep03_raw_mix.wav (badly unbalanced — host is loud, guest is quiet, plus two long dead-air gaps where someone stepped away); and (2) northbound_delivery_spec.pdf, our one-page delivery standard stating the targets our editor masters to (integrated loudness target -16 LUFS for the podcast feed, true-peak ceiling -1.0 dBTP, no audible hiss/hum floor above roughly -60 dB, no clipping, speaker levels within ~3 dB of each other, no dead-air gap longer than ~2 seconds in the final cut). Go through each recording end to end and hand back a Northbound Signal audio QC report that, for every issue found, gives the episode, an approximate timestamp or time-range, a plain-language description of the defect (clipping / hiss / hum / level imbalance / dead air / plosive / sibilance), which delivery-spec target it fails, and a severity (blocker / warning / nit). We also want one thing to listen to: take the worst-affected recording (ep02, the hissy one) and run a voice-only clean-up pass on it so we have a reference track that shows how clean the dialogue CAN get once the floor is removed — clearly labelled as a reference/benchmark, NOT the final master. Deliver the QC findings as a clean PDF report plus the raw findings as a structured file, with a cover summary counting issues by severity across all three episodes, and the single cleaned reference WAV alongside. Everything is voice-only; there is no music bed to consider at this stage.
Deliverables (3)
  • NorthboundSignal_AudioQC_Report.pdf — The primary deliverable: a clean A4 audio QC report PDF. Opens with a one-page cover summary (total findings by severity: blocker / warning / nit, counted across all three episodes), then one section
  • northbound_audio_qc_findings.json — The raw structured findings emitted by local_write_recipe_note: an ordered list of records {episode, timecode, defect_type, description, spec_clause, target_value, observed_value, severity} plus a sum
  • ep02_reference_cleaned.wav — The single audio deliverable: a voice-only denoised / de-hissed reference version of ep02 (the worst-affected, hissy recording), produced via media_enhance_speech (its cleaned-speech track) to demonst
Workflow — 12 tool steps
  1. asset_create_folders — Stand up the CC asset workspace (e.g. /M08/raw, /spec, /findings) to hold the three supplied raw recordings, the delivery-spec PDF, the reference clean track an
  2. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize an upload session for the client-supplied QC inputs: ep01_raw_mix.wav, ep02_raw_mix.wav, ep03_raw_mix.wav and northbound_delivery_spec.pdf.
  3. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the upload so the three raw voice recordings and the delivery-spec PDF are real CC assets with stable URNs the inspection steps can read.
  4. media_summarize — Summarize northbound_delivery_spec.pdf to read out the machine-usable ground-truth targets the audit checks against: -16 LUFS integrated loudness, -1.0 dBTP tru
  5. media_summarize — Summarize ep01_raw_mix.wav to read its spoken content and timeline — mapping the conversational turns, laughs and emphatic peaks against runtime so the guest-mi
  6. media_summarize — Summarize ep02_raw_mix.wav to read its spoken content and timeline as the anchor for episode 2's findings; the steady HVAC hum / broadband hiss floor itself is
  7. media_summarize — Summarize ep03_raw_mix.wav to read its spoken content and timeline, surfacing the speaker turns and the two long near-silent stretches so the host-vs-guest leve
  8. asset_preview_file — Preview each raw recording asset to confirm duration and channel layout, verify the correct three files are under audit, and audibly/visually confirm the suspec
  9. media_enhance_speech — Run media_enhance_speech on ep02 (the worst-affected, hissy recording) — it separates the audio into an isolated cleaned-speech track, a background (hiss/hum) t
  10. media_summarize — Summarize the cleaned reference track to confirm the dialogue is fully intelligible and unimpaired once the floor is removed, supporting the A/B comparison note
  11. local_write_recipe_note — Emit the structured audio QC findings: for each defect, a record {episode, timecode, defect_type, description, spec_clause, target_value, observed_value, severi
  12. document_convert_pdf — Render the written QC findings (cover summary + per-episode, per-finding rows) into the clean A4 Northbound Signal Audio QC report PDF for the producer and edit
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon analyze/audit: 12 connector calls, 7 distinct Adobe tools across three groups (asset workspace/upload/preview, the [A] media pair media_summarize + media_enhance_speech, and document_convert_pdf) plus the single [L] recipe-note emit — at/above the >=10-call / >=6-distinct-tool floor for an analyze task. Difficulty is in the analytic structure, not editing grunt-work: the agent must (a) read the actual numeric delivery targets out of a supplied spec sheet via media_summarize rather than assuming them, (b) inspect three separate ~30-minute recordings and correctly attribute a DIFFERENT dominant defect class to each (clipping on ep01, continuous hiss/hum floor on ep02, level imbalance + long dead-air on ep03) by time-anchoring each on the summarized timeline and confirming it in preview / via the enhance-speech track separation, (c) run exactly one voice-only reference clean-up on the worst-affected episode — where the isolated background track is itself the hiss/hum evidence — and re-read the cleaned track to substantiate an honest A/B claim, (d) distinguish the planted defects from clean passages and rank each by severity against the spec, and (e) emit a structured, episode-and-timestamp-referenced findings report. It respects every hard limit: NOTHING is spliced, mastered, level-matched, or re-mixed — media_enhance_speech is used only for a voice-only denoise refer

AO-102 — Foxglove Records community-radio ad spot: clean the recorded VO takes + station tag, confirm the read against the guide mix, and assemble a mix-ready :30/:60 audio package ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Take the label's already-recorded voiceover takes, station tag, supplied music bed, and rough guide mix, clean every spoken recording to an isolated speech stem, confirm the read against the guide mix, and hand back one organised, mix-ready :30/:60 radio-spot audio package (final layer-mix flagged as the human-finish step).

💰 ~$120 est.Upwork18 tool calls6 distinct toolscreatelong-horizon
grounded in a real brief: Clean up and assemble already-recorded voiceover takes into a mix-ready community-radio ad-spot audio package
Full client brief
Foxglove Records, a small independent label that co-runs a volunteer community-radio arts programme, is booking a run of on-air ad spots to promote its spring 'Porch Sessions' live-music series. This is an assembly-and-clean job, not a from-scratch creative project — everything is already recorded and the label is handing it all over. Supplied: (1) a :60 voiceover read of the full spot recorded by a volunteer host on a USB mic in a spare room (warm, conversational, but with steady room hiss, a bit of HVAC hum, and light room reverb); (2) a tighter :30 cut-down read of the same script; (3) a spoken station/legal tag line ('Foxglove Community Radio, 98.3 — supported by listeners like you') recorded in the same room; (4) a supplied instrumental music bed (a gentle acoustic guitar loop the label already cleared for on-air use) as a WAV; and (5) a rough 'guide mix' the host quickly recorded on a phone with the voiceover talking over the music bed, so you can hear the intended pacing. There is also a script + timing cue sheet (CSV) listing each line, its target in/out seconds, and which take it belongs to.

What the label needs back is a clean, organised, mix-ready audio package for the two spot lengths: every spoken recording denoised and de-reverbed down to an isolated, broadcast-clear speech stem so the voice sits clean; the supplied music bed carried through untouched as the bed; the guide mix listened back and summarised so the confirmed script/timing cue sheet matches what was actually read; and all of it gathered into one dated Creative Cloud handoff folder with the cue sheet, so the label's volunteer engineer can drop the stems onto the timeline and do the final layer-mix. IMPORTANT and stated up front: the final multi-track mix itself — ducking the voiceover over the music bed, loudness/broadcast mastering to the station's target level, and the cross-fades — is a manual finish in the engineer's DAW and is out of scope for this automated audio-prep pass; this pass delivers the cleaned speech stems, the confirmed cue sheet, the carried-through bed, and the organised package. Deliverables: cleaned :60 VO speech stem, cleaned :30 VO speech stem, cleaned station-tag speech stem, the confirmed script/timing cue sheet, the supplied music bed carried into the package, and one organised mix-ready Creative Cloud handoff folder.
Deliverables (6)
  • vo_read_60s_clean.wav — Cleaned :60 voiceover speech stem: room hiss, HVAC hum, and reverb suppressed by media_enhance_speech (voice-only isolation), WAV — the broadcast-clear voice for the :60 spot. The tool's separated bac
  • vo_read_30s_clean.wav — Cleaned :30 voiceover speech stem, matched in tone to the :60 stem, WAV — the broadcast-clear voice for the :30 cut-down spot.
  • station_tag_clean.wav — Cleaned station/legal tag-line speech stem, WAV, ready to place at the tail of either spot.
  • spot_script_timing_confirmed.csv — The script/timing cue sheet confirmed against the guide-mix summary: each line, its take, target in/out seconds and mix notes verified to match what was actually read, so the engineer's timeline place
  • music_bed_acoustic_loop.wav — The supplied instrumental music bed carried into the handoff package untouched (no connector audio processing applied to music), so it sits alongside the cleaned voice stems for the manual mix.
  • foxglove_spot_package_folder — One dated Creative Cloud handoff folder gathering the cleaned :60 / :30 VO stems, the cleaned tag stem, the confirmed cue-sheet CSV, and the supplied music bed — the mix-ready audio package the volunt
Workflow — 18 tool steps
  1. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize a block upload of the client's :60 voiceover read (vo_read_60s.wav, media_type audio/wav) into the label's Creative Cloud project folder. Returns the
  2. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the :60 VO upload from step 1 into a real CC audio assetId that media_enhance_speech can consume.
  3. media_enhance_speech — Separate the :60 VO into an isolated clean speech stem (room hiss / HVAC hum / reverb suppressed) plus background/reverb stems; keep the speech stem as the :60
  4. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize a block upload of the :30 cut-down read (vo_read_30s.wav). Second source load.
  5. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the :30 VO upload from step 4 into a real CC audio assetId.
  6. media_enhance_speech — Clean the :30 read the same way so both VO stems match tonally — isolated speech stem, hiss/hum/reverb removed. [A] async. Consumes step-5 assetId.
  7. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize a block upload of the spoken station/legal tag line (station_tag_line.wav). Third source load.
  8. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the tag-line upload from step 7 into a real CC audio assetId.
  9. media_enhance_speech — Clean the short station-tag recording to an isolated speech stem so the legal/station line is crisp at the tail of either spot. [A] async. Consumes step-8 asset
  10. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize a block upload of the rough phone guide mix (guide_mix_phone_scratch.wav) — VO over the bed — used only to confirm pacing/script. Fourth source load.
  11. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the guide-mix upload from step 10 into a real CC audio assetId that media_summarize can read.
  12. media_summarize — Read back the spoken content of the guide mix (dialogue/narration) so the actual read — line order and pacing — is confirmed. [A] async. Consumes step-11 assetI
  13. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize a block upload of the script/timing cue sheet reconciled against the guide-mix summary (spot_script_timing_confirmed.csv, media_type text/csv) so the
  14. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the confirmed cue-sheet CSV into the client's CC project folder as the timing deliverable the engineer places stems against.
  15. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize a block upload of the supplied, already-cleared instrumental music bed (music_bed_acoustic_loop.wav) so it is carried into the package untouched. Fif
  16. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the music-bed upload from step 15 into a real CC audio assetId that sits alongside the cleaned voice stems for the manual mix.
  17. asset_create_folders — Create a dated handoff folder in the label's Creative Cloud (e.g. /Foxglove/PorchSessions_Spot_v1) to gather the mix-ready package into one place.
  18. asset_copy_assets — Copy the deliverables — cleaned :60 stem (3), cleaned :30 stem (6), cleaned tag stem (9), confirmed cue-sheet CSV (14), and the carried-through music bed (16) —
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon audio-CREATE task: 18 connector calls across 2 groups (asset/storage [C] and the [A] async audio media tools), 6 distinct Adobe tools — at/above the >=10-call / >=6-distinct-tool floor. It is hard for the honest reason audio work is hard on this surface: the agent must recognise that a radio-spot assembly maps to (a) three separate voice-cleanup passes via media_enhance_speech (voice-only stem isolation, run per take so the stems match), (b) a media_summarize read-back of the rough guide mix to CONFIRM the script/timing rather than invent it, and (c) carrying the supplied, cleared music bed through UNTOUCHED because the connector has no music-processing tool — then gathering everything into one organised CC handoff folder. Crucially it re-scopes the brief's 'assemble it cleanly' honestly: the final multi-track LAYER-MIX (ducking VO over bed, loudness/broadcast mastering, cross-fades) has NO connector tool and is explicitly named as the single human-finish step. It respects every hard limit: no generative audio, no text-to-audio / voice synthesis of new lines, no music generation or mastering, media_enhance_speech is used only for voice isolation, media_summarize only reads existing narration, and no banned image/video capability appears. The three media steps are correctly [A] async-widget; the rest are headless [C] asset ops.

60s_01,vo_read_60s,"This spring, Foxglove Records brings the Porch Sessions back to the neighbourhood.",0.0,5.0,"Cold VO open; music bed fades in low, duck bed under VO"
60s_02,vo_read_60s,"Six Saturday evenings of live acoustic music on the front steps of the old library.",5.0,10.0,"Bed at -18dB under VO"
60s_03,vo_read_60s,"Free, all ages, bring a folding chair. Doors and porches open at six, with the first notes ringing out shortly after.",10.0,17.0,""
60s_04,vo_read_60s,"Imagine warm evenings, incredible local talent, and friendly faces, all under the stars.",17.0,23.0,"Warmest read; let bed breathe slightly"
60s_05,vo_read_60s,"We're so excited to once again transform our beloved old library into a vibrant hub of sound and community.",23.0,30.0,""
60s_06,vo_read_60s,"Each week features a different local and regional artist, from soulful folk singers to intricate instrumentalists, bringing their unique sound to our front steps.",30.0,40.0,""
60s_07,vo_read_60s,"It's the perfect way to spend your Saturday evening, unwinding with good company and even better music.",40.0,46.0,""
60s_08,vo_read_60s,"Find out the full lineup and specific dates by visiting foxgloverecords.com, or just keep an ear out on this station for more details.",46.0,54.0,"URL is the call-to-action; keep bed clear here"
60s_09,vo_read_60s,"That's the Porch Sessions, every Saturday evening this spring, presented by Foxglove Records. We can't wait to see you there.",54.0,60.0,"Bed swells to full on tail, fade out by 60.0s; place station tag after"
30s_01,vo_read_30s,"Hey there! Get ready to experience the Foxglove Porch Sessions.",0.0,4.0,"Punchy VO open; music bed fades in low, duck bed under VO"
30s_02,vo_read_30s,"We're talking amazing live music and community vibes right on our porch. And it's all happening every Saturday evening.",4.0,11.0,"Bed at -18dB under VO"
30s_03,vo_read_30s,"Best part? It's completely free and open to all ages, so gather your friends and family.",11.0,17.0,""
30s_04,vo_read_30s,"Come discover some incredible local talent.",17.0,20.0,""
30s_05,vo_read_30s,"That's the Foxglove Porch Sessions. Saturday evenings, free and all ages.",20.0,26.0,""

AO-103 — Stillwell sleep-education course: clean up six raw lesson narration takes into broadcast-clean voiceover masters + transcript/chapter pack ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Denoise and de-hiss six raw sleep-lesson narration takes into clean voiceover masters, then inspect them to produce a transcript/chapter pack, and hand everything back as one organized, zipped course-audio package.

💰 ~$120 est.Freelancer.com19 tool calls6 distinct toolseditlong-horizon
grounded in a real brief: Relaxing Insomnia Video Production
Full client brief
Stillwell is a small sleep-and-mindfulness education platform. We are building a self-paced course module titled 'Easing Insomnia: A Six-Lesson Wind-Down', and we recorded the narration ourselves at home. The problem is the audio: our host recorded the six lessons on a USB mic in a home office, so every take has steady room hiss, a faint HVAC hum, a bit of laptop-fan whine, and uneven levels where the voice drifts loud and soft. The script is finished and the delivery is warm and calm, which we love — we just need the VOICE cleaned up so it is clear, consistent, and soothing enough for people to fall asleep to without a hiss buzzing in their ears.

What we need: (A) take the six raw lesson narration takes (lesson_01 through lesson_06, WAV) and clean each one so the room hiss, hum and fan noise are gone and the host's voice sits clear and balanced — this is voice cleanup only (denoise / de-hiss), NOT music mixing or a full master; we will lay the soft sleep-music bed under it and do the final fades and loudness pass ourselves on our side. (B) Because these become the course-player lessons, we also need a transcript-and-chapter pack: a short content summary of each cleaned lesson so our LMS team can drop in captions, chapter markers, and a one-line lesson blurb for the course syllabus. (C) Deliver everything back as one tidy, organized package — the six cleaned voiceover masters in a /clean folder, the per-lesson transcript/summary text, and a short read-me recipe note listing exactly what was done to each take (so our audio person can reproduce or extend it) — all zipped into a single download. Keep the running order lesson_01 -> lesson_06. The finished audio must sound like the same calm host, just clean.
Deliverables (9)
  • lesson_01_intro_clean.wav — Denoised/de-hissed voiceover master of lesson 1: room hiss, HVAC hum and fan whine removed, voice clear and level-balanced; WAV, voice-only enhancement (no music bed, no loudness master).
  • lesson_02_breathing_clean.wav — Cleaned voiceover master of lesson 2 (4-7-8 breathing): noise floor removed, mouth-click/clipped-breath artifacts reduced, warm calm voice preserved; WAV, voice-only.
  • lesson_03_bodyscan_clean.wav — Cleaned voiceover master of lesson 3 (body scan): steady noise floor and stray dog-bark/room-reflection reduced, voice clear; WAV, voice-only.
  • lesson_04_thoughts_clean.wav — Cleaned voiceover master of lesson 4 (quieting the mind): distant/low-gain voice lifted clear of the hiss, balanced; WAV, voice-only.
  • lesson_05_environment_clean.wav — Cleaned voiceover master of lesson 5 (sleep environment): keyboard/chair transients and steady floor reduced, voice clear; WAV, voice-only.
  • lesson_06_windown_clean.wav — Cleaned voiceover master of lesson 6 (wind-down close): low-SNR near-whisper tail recovered and de-hissed, voice clear to the soft fade-out; WAV, voice-only.
  • stillwell_course_transcript_pack.txt — Per-lesson content summary/transcript pack (lesson_01 -> lesson_06) generated from the cleaned masters: a short summary and one-line syllabus blurb per lesson to seed the course-player captions, chapt
  • stillwell_audio_recipe_note.txt — Read-me recipe note listing exactly what was done to each take (voice-only denoise/de-hiss, running order preserved) and explicitly noting the music-bed mix, fades and final loudness master are left f
  • stillwell_course_audio_package.zip — Single downloadable package: /clean folder with the six cleaned voiceover masters in running order, the transcript/chapter pack, and the recipe read-me note.
Workflow — 19 tool steps
  1. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize an upload session to bring the six raw lesson narration WAVs (lesson_01..lesson_06) into CC storage. Initial source load.
  2. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the upload, yielding stable asset URNs for the six raw takes that the enhance/summarize steps reference.
  3. asset_create_folders — Create the organized course folder tree (a /clean folder for finished masters plus a working folder) so the cleaned deliverables land in the structure the produ
  4. media_enhance_speech — Voice-only denoise/de-hiss on lesson 1: strip the room hiss, HVAC hum and fan whine so the intro voice is clear (no music, no loudness master). Async-widget; co
  5. media_enhance_speech — Voice-only enhance lesson 2 (breathing): reduce the noise floor and the mouth-click/clipped-breath transients while keeping the calm cadence. Consumes the lesso
  6. media_enhance_speech — Voice-only enhance lesson 3 (body scan): clean the steady floor and reduce the stray dog-bark/room-reflection bleed. Consumes the lesson_03 URN from step 2.
  7. media_enhance_speech — Voice-only enhance lesson 4 (quieting the mind): lift the low-gain, distant voice clear of the hiss and balance it. Consumes the lesson_04 URN from step 2.
  8. media_enhance_speech — Voice-only enhance lesson 5 (sleep environment): reduce the keyboard/chair transients and steady floor. Consumes the lesson_05 URN from step 2.
  9. media_enhance_speech — Voice-only enhance lesson 6 (wind-down close): recover and de-hiss the low-SNR near-whisper tail so the voice stays clear to the soft ending. Consumes the lesso
  10. asset_add_file — File the six cleaned voiceover masters into the /clean folder in running order (lesson_01 -> lesson_06) so they are organized for the LMS hand-off.
  11. media_summarize — Inspect the cleaned lesson-1 master and produce a short content summary/transcript to seed the course-player captions and syllabus blurb. Consumes step-4 output
  12. media_summarize — Summarize cleaned lesson 2 (breathing) for its chapter/caption blurb. Consumes step-5 output.
  13. media_summarize — Summarize cleaned lesson 3 (body scan) for its chapter/caption blurb. Consumes step-6 output.
  14. media_summarize — Summarize cleaned lesson 4 (quieting the mind) for its chapter/caption blurb. Consumes step-7 output.
  15. media_summarize — Summarize cleaned lesson 5 (sleep environment) for its chapter/caption blurb. Consumes step-8 output.
  16. media_summarize — Summarize cleaned lesson 6 (wind-down close) for its chapter/caption blurb. Consumes step-9 output.
  17. local_write_recipe_note — Assemble the six per-lesson summaries into one transcript/chapter pack (lesson_01 -> lesson_06) with a one-line syllabus blurb per lesson, ordered per the produ
  18. local_write_recipe_note — Write the read-me recipe note: what was done to each take (voice-only denoise/de-hiss, running order preserved) and an explicit note that the music-bed mix, smo
  19. zip_package — Single unavoidable local step: zip the /clean folder of six cleaned masters plus the transcript pack and recipe note into one downloadable course-audio package
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon audio-edit task: 19 connector calls, 6 distinct Adobe tools plus two local finishing tools, above the >=10-call floor for an audio task. Difficulty comes from an honest decomposition of a video-production brief down to the audio scope the connector can truly do: recognizing that 'clean audio + careful EQ + music must not compete' maps to media_enhance_speech VOICE-ONLY denoise/de-hiss (NOT a music mix, EQ or loudness master), running a consistent six-take enhancement fan-out where each take has a different noise problem (low gain, near-whisper tail, transient bleed), then INSPECTING each cleaned master with media_summarize to emit a transcript/chapter pack, and organizing + zipping the result in the exact folder structure the producer asked for. It respects every hard limit: no generative fill / text-to-image / upscaling / object-removal; no video-picture editing; no music processing or loudness mastering is claimed (explicitly flagged as the client's own finishing step); media_enhance_speech and media_summarize are [A] async-widget product steps and are tagged as such; the only non-Adobe steps are the two [L] local writes and the [L] zip, the standard local finishing tail.

Client-supplied input assets: 7 total · 0 img · 0 video · 6 audio · 1 data

openai/gpt-4o-mini-tts
lesson_04_thoughts_raw.wav
openai/gpt-4o-mini-tts

openai/gpt-4o-mini-tts

Data & other (1)

stillwell_course_brief.txt
content
Stillwell Course: Audio Cleanup Brief & Delivery Specs

Hi,

Quick production note for the Stillwell sleep-course audio cleanup. We recorded all
six lesson narrations ourselves on a USB mic in a home office, so every take has
steady room hiss, a faint HVAC hum, a little laptop-fan whine, and uneven levels.
The script and the calm delivery are exactly what we want -- we just need the VOICE
cleaned up.

Lesson Order:
Please process the following six raw takes in this exact running order:
  1. lesson_01_intro_raw.wav       (welcome + what insomnia is)
  2. lesson_02_breathing_raw.wav   (the 4-7-8 breathing technique)
  3. lesson_03_bodyscan_raw.wav    (progressive body-scan relaxation)
  4. lesson_04_thoughts_raw.wav    (quieting a racing mind at night)
  5. lesson_05_environment_raw.wav (setting up a sleep-friendly room)
  6. lesson_06_windown_raw.wav     (the closing wind-down)

Desired Audio Outcome:
A clean, calm voice throughout -- absolutely no hiss, hum or fan whine, with the
levels evened out so the host sits clear and consistent. The goal is pristine,
soothing speech. Please note this is voice cleanup only (denoise / de-hiss): we will
lay the soft sleep-music bed under it and do the smooth fades and final loudness pass
ourselves on our side. No music mixing or mastering is required from your end.

Delivery Structure:
When you deliver, please package everything with this folder structure:
  /clean            -- the six cleaned voiceover masters, in the running order above
  transcript_pack/  -- a short per-lesson transcript/summary so our LMS team can drop
                       in captions, chapter markers and a one-line syllabus blurb
  readme.txt        -- a short recipe note listing exactly what you did to each take,
                       so our audio person can reproduce or extend it

Keep the finished audio sounding like the same calm host -- just clean. Zip the whole
/clean package into a single download for hand-off.

Thanks,
Stillwell Course Producer

AO-104 — Northwind Advisory corporate headshots: batch retouch + grade a 3-frame studio session into LinkedIn/website/print deliverables with clean black-and-white alternates ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Batch-retouch and colour-grade three raw studio headshot frames of one Northwind executive into a consistent set of fully-finished colour portraits, then produce clean black-and-white alternates and the LinkedIn / website / print-ready crops, delivered in one review folder.

💰 ~$120 est.Freelancer.com43 tool calls13 distinct toolseditlong-horizon
grounded in a real brief: Traditional Corporate Headshot Session Needed
Full client brief
Hi — this is Priya Venkataraman, office manager at Northwind Advisory Partners (a boutique wealth-advisory firm). We had our new partner, Daniel Okoro, sit for a classic corporate head-shot last week and the photographer gave us the un-retouched exports, but we need someone to actually finish them. Please work only from the files I'm attaching: three high-res studio frames of Daniel against our neutral light-grey backdrop (slightly different poses/expressions), plus a one-page retouch brief with our house rules.

We want a traditional, formal look — nothing trendy. Specifically:
1. Clean up and level each of the three frames (they're a hair tilted), then apply a consistent professional studio tone so exposure and contrast look intentional across all three, not flat.
2. The studio strobes left everything slightly cool — warm the skin tone a touch and give the set a cohesive graded look so all three read as one session. Consistency across the three is the whole point; they'll sit together on the "Our Team" page.
3. Tasteful, believable retouching only: gently brighten his eyes and teeth using selective adjustments, and even out the light on his face. No plastic skin, no reshaping, no changing the backdrop — the light-grey seamless stays exactly as shot.
4. Deliver each finished colour portrait in the crops we'll actually use: a 1:1 LinkedIn/avatar crop (1200x1200), a 4:5 website "Our Team" portrait crop (1600x2000), and a 5:7 print crop (1500x2100) suitable for framing at reception.
5. For every finished colour portrait we also need a clean black-and-white version — a true, neutral monochrome conversion, not a heavy filter — in the same three crops.
6. Put everything into one delivery folder, organised so we can review the colour set and the B&W set side by side, and send me a preview link so Daniel and the partners can sign off.

Turnaround within the week, please. Deliverable: a high-resolution, print/web-ready corporate headshot package (colour + clean B&W, LinkedIn + website + print crops) for Daniel, consistent across all three source frames.
Deliverables (19)
  • okoro_color_frame01_1x1.jpg — Frame-1 finished colour portrait, retouched + graded, cropped to LinkedIn/avatar 1:1 1200x1200, subject-centred, light-grey backdrop preserved. JPEG, sRGB.
  • okoro_color_frame01_4x5.jpg — Frame-1 finished colour portrait cropped to website 'Our Team' 4:5 1600x2000. JPEG, sRGB.
  • okoro_color_frame01_5x7.jpg — Frame-1 finished colour portrait cropped to print 5:7 1500x2100 for framed reception display. JPEG, sRGB, ~300 DPI intent.
  • okoro_color_frame02_1x1.jpg — Frame-2 finished colour portrait (identical grade+retouch pipeline, colour-matched to frame 1) cropped to LinkedIn/avatar 1:1 1200x1200, subject-centred. JPEG, sRGB.
  • okoro_color_frame02_4x5.jpg — Frame-2 finished colour portrait cropped to website 'Our Team' 4:5 1600x2000. JPEG, sRGB.
  • okoro_color_frame02_5x7.jpg — Frame-2 finished colour portrait cropped to print 5:7 1500x2100. JPEG, sRGB, ~300 DPI intent.
  • okoro_color_frame03_1x1.jpg — Frame-3 finished colour portrait (identical grade+retouch pipeline plus shadow-even step, colour-matched to frame 1) cropped to LinkedIn/avatar 1:1 1200x1200, subject-centred. JPEG, sRGB.
  • okoro_color_frame03_4x5.jpg — Frame-3 finished colour portrait cropped to website 'Our Team' 4:5 1600x2000. JPEG, sRGB.
  • okoro_color_frame03_5x7.jpg — Frame-3 finished colour portrait cropped to print 5:7 1500x2100. JPEG, sRGB, ~300 DPI intent.
  • okoro_bw_frame01_1x1.jpg — Frame-1 clean neutral black-and-white monochrome conversion of the finished colour portrait, cropped to LinkedIn/avatar 1:1 1200x1200; true grayscale look (not a heavy stylised filter). JPEG.
  • okoro_bw_frame01_4x5.jpg — Frame-1 clean neutral B&W conversion cropped to website 'Our Team' 4:5 1600x2000. JPEG.
  • okoro_bw_frame01_5x7.jpg — Frame-1 clean neutral B&W conversion cropped to print 5:7 1500x2100. JPEG.
  • okoro_bw_frame02_1x1.jpg — Frame-2 clean neutral B&W conversion cropped to LinkedIn/avatar 1:1 1200x1200. JPEG.
  • okoro_bw_frame02_4x5.jpg — Frame-2 clean neutral B&W conversion cropped to website 'Our Team' 4:5 1600x2000. JPEG.
  • okoro_bw_frame02_5x7.jpg — Frame-2 clean neutral B&W conversion cropped to print 5:7 1500x2100. JPEG.
  • okoro_bw_frame03_1x1.jpg — Frame-3 clean neutral B&W conversion cropped to LinkedIn/avatar 1:1 1200x1200. JPEG.
  • okoro_bw_frame03_4x5.jpg — Frame-3 clean neutral B&W conversion cropped to website 'Our Team' 4:5 1600x2000. JPEG.
  • okoro_bw_frame03_5x7.jpg — Frame-3 clean neutral B&W conversion cropped to print 5:7 1500x2100. JPEG.
  • Okoro_Headshot_Delivery/ (folder + preview) — One CC asset folder containing all 9 finished colour crops and all 9 clean B&W crops (3 frames x 3 crops each, colour + B&W) organised for side-by-side review, plus an inline preview deep-link for par
Workflow — 43 tool steps
  1. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize an upload session for the three client-supplied raw studio frames plus the retouch brief PDF into CC storage.
  2. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the upload; yields the canonical CC asset URIs for frame01/02/03 and northwind_retouch_brief.pdf that the edit chain will consume.
  3. asset_preview_file — Preview/inspect the retouch brief PDF to confirm the required crops (1:1 1200x1200, 4:5 1600x2000, 5:7 1500x2100), the colour+clean-B&W requirement and the keep
  4. image_auto_straighten — Level the ~2-degree tilt on frame01 before any tonal work.
  5. image_apply_auto_tone — Apply a professional baseline studio tone to frame01 so the flat RAW export looks intentional (exposure/contrast).
  6. image_adjust_color_temperature — Warm the cool strobe white balance on frame01 for a premium, natural skin tone — this becomes the reference grade the other two frames match.
  7. image_select_by_prompt — Select the subject's eyes + teeth on frame01 (bodyParts Eyes,Teeth) to drive selective, believable brightening — no skin/face reshaping.
  8. image_adjust_light_portions — Lift the light portions only within the eyes/teeth mask (the sclera whites and teeth are the bright areas) on frame01 — tasteful selective retouch; consumes the
  9. image_crop_and_resize — Crop the finished frame01 colour master to the LinkedIn 1:1 1200x1200, subject-centred (downscale-only crop from the 4000x5000 source).
  10. image_crop_and_resize — Crop the finished frame01 colour master to the website 'Our Team' 4:5 1600x2000.
  11. image_crop_and_resize — Crop the finished frame01 colour master to the print 5:7 1500x2100 for framed reception display.
  12. image_apply_monochromatic_tint — Produce a clean neutral black-and-white conversion of the finished frame01 colour master (saturation 0, lightness 0 = true monochrome, not a heavy stylised filt
  13. image_crop_and_resize — Crop the frame01 B&W conversion to the LinkedIn 1:1 1200x1200.
  14. image_crop_and_resize — Crop the frame01 B&W conversion to the website 4:5 1600x2000.
  15. image_crop_and_resize — Crop the frame01 B&W conversion to the print 5:7 1500x2100.
  16. image_auto_straighten — Level the ~1.5-degree tilt on frame02, starting its matching per-frame grade+retouch sub-chain.
  17. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone frame02 to the same intentional studio baseline as frame01.
  18. image_adjust_color_temperature — Warm frame02 to match frame01's reference white balance so the set reads as one session.
  19. image_select_by_prompt — Select the subject's eyes + teeth on frame02 for the same selective brightening.
  20. image_adjust_light_portions — Selectively brighten eyes/teeth on frame02 through the step-19 mask — matching frame01's retouch. This is the frame-2 master.
  21. image_crop_and_resize — Crop the finished frame02 colour master to the LinkedIn 1:1 1200x1200.
  22. image_crop_and_resize — Crop the finished frame02 colour master to the website 4:5 1600x2000.
  23. image_crop_and_resize — Crop the finished frame02 colour master to the print 5:7 1500x2100.
  24. image_apply_monochromatic_tint — Clean neutral B&W conversion of the finished frame02 colour master (saturation 0, lightness 0).
  25. image_crop_and_resize — Crop the frame02 B&W conversion to the LinkedIn 1:1 1200x1200.
  26. image_crop_and_resize — Crop the frame02 B&W conversion to the website 4:5 1600x2000.
  27. image_crop_and_resize — Crop the frame02 B&W conversion to the print 5:7 1500x2100.
  28. image_auto_straighten — Level the ~2.5-degree tilt on frame03, starting its matching sub-chain.
  29. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone frame03 to the same studio baseline (also lifts its slightly-underexposed shadow side).
  30. image_adjust_light_portions — Lift the light portions on frame03's darker face side to even the exposure before matching the reference grade (whole-image tonal lift, no mask, no generative c
  31. image_adjust_color_temperature — Warm frame03 to match frame01's reference white balance for a consistent set.
  32. image_select_by_prompt — Select the subject's eyes + teeth on frame03 for selective brightening.
  33. image_adjust_light_portions — Selectively brighten eyes/teeth on frame03 through the step-32 mask — completing the third matched colour master.
  34. image_crop_and_resize — Crop the finished frame03 colour master to the LinkedIn 1:1 1200x1200.
  35. image_crop_and_resize — Crop the finished frame03 colour master to the website 4:5 1600x2000.
  36. image_crop_and_resize — Crop the finished frame03 colour master to the print 5:7 1500x2100.
  37. image_apply_monochromatic_tint — Clean neutral B&W conversion of the finished frame03 colour master (saturation 0, lightness 0).
  38. image_crop_and_resize — Crop the frame03 B&W conversion to the LinkedIn 1:1 1200x1200.
  39. image_crop_and_resize — Crop the frame03 B&W conversion to the website 4:5 1600x2000.
  40. image_crop_and_resize — Crop the frame03 B&W conversion to the print 5:7 1500x2100.
  41. asset_create_folders — Create the Okoro_Headshot_Delivery/ folder in CC storage, with colour and B&W subgroups for side-by-side review.
  42. asset_copy_assets — Copy all 9 finished colour crops and all 9 clean B&W crops (3 frames x 3 crops each) into the delivery folder, grouped colour vs B&W.
  43. asset_inline_preview — Render an inline preview deep-link of the populated delivery folder so Daniel and the partners can sign off on the colour and B&W sets.
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon multi-asset EDIT: 31 connector calls, 13 distinct Adobe tools, spanning two capability groups (image_* raster/tonal/selection + asset_* storage/preview) — well above the >=10 calls / >=6 distinct-tool floor. Difficulty comes from running THREE separate raw frames through a consistent, colour-matched grade+retouch pipeline (the cross-frame consistency requirement is the hard part — frame01's grade becomes the reference the others must match, and frame03 needs an extra shadow-evening step to reach parity), threading mask-driven SELECTIVE edits (select_by_prompt -> adjust_light_portions for believable eyes/teeth brightening on each frame, not a global lift), then fanning each finished master into multiple crop ratios plus a clean neutral B&W conversion, and finally folder/copy/preview packaging for side-by-side sign-off. It respects every hard limit: no generative-expand/outpaint, no generative fill, no text-to-image/from-scratch, no upscaling, no object-removal/inpainting, no compositing, no backdrop replacement (the light-grey seamless is kept exactly as shot); image_fill_area is never used; image_remove_background is never used; monochrome is a real tint conversion (saturation 0), not a heavy stylised filter. Every step is [C] headless, so it runs end-to-end autonomously.

Client-supplied input assets: 4 total · 3 img · 0 video · 0 audio · 1 data

Images (3)

AO-105 — Nocturne Sessions duotone gig poster: two-tone artist-photo FX + wordmark vectorize + InDesign template merge to print PDF/JPEG ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Give the raw band press photo a bold two-tone (duotone) FX treatment, vectorize the supplied Nocturne Sessions wordmark, then run the client's authored InDesign gig-poster template through a data-merge against the event-copy CSV to export a print-ready PDF and a high-res JPEG.

💰 $25Freelancer.com16 tool calls15 distinct toolscreatelong-horizon
grounded in a real brief: Modern Album Logo & Cover Design
Full client brief
The Nocturne Sessions, a fictional indie synth-noir act, is releasing a new EP and needs its launch gig poster finished for print and social. The band has handed off an authored InDesign poster template (nocturne_poster_template.indd), laid out A2 portrait with a full-bleed hero-photo frame, a wordmark frame, and real InDesign data-merge fields (<<title>>, <<venue>>, <<date>>, <<support>>, <<presale>>). They also supply one raw press photo of the trio (shot on a phone in a dim rehearsal room — slightly tilted, flat/dull, cool white balance, a cluttered gear-strewn background), a flat raster promo wordmark PNG that reads "THE NOCTURNE SESSIONS," and a one-row CSV holding the show copy. The band's brief calls for "a clean modern aesthetic—nothing vintage—powered by bright, bold colours that jump off the screen and still look sharp in print," and the poster's art direction is a classic bold two-tone (duotone) gig-poster look: the hero photo rendered in just two punchy brand inks (deep indigo shadows, hot magenta highlights) rather than full colour, so it reads instantly at both A2 wall size and Instagram thumbnail size. The job: (1) prep the raw press photo — straighten the tilted frame, auto-tone the flat exposure, and push contrast hard so the two-tone mapping has clean separation between shadow and highlight; (2) convert the prepped photo to a bold duotone — first flatten it to a single-ink monochromatic base tint in the indigo shadow ink across the whole frame, then lay the hot-magenta brand ink over it as a whole-image color overlay in a lighten/screen blend so only the brighter pixels shift toward magenta, producing the two-ink poster hero; add a fine grain so the flat inks don't band on large-format print; crop it to the poster frame's ratio; (3) vectorize the flat wordmark PNG into a clean SVG so the "NOCTURNE SESSIONS" word-mark stays razor-sharp at A2 and can be reused on merch/posters exactly as the brief asks; (4) recommend a modern condensed display font pairing for the show copy so the template's type reads as clean and bold; (5) upload the duotoned hero and the vector wordmark, then run the client's authored .indd through InDesign data-merge against the event-copy CSV so the title/venue/date/support/presale bind into the template around the placed hero and wordmark; (6) export the bound poster to a print-ready A2 PDF (300 DPI, CMYK-intent) and a high-resolution JPEG for social; (7) assemble the duotoned hero, the vector wordmark, and the final poster onto a Firefly board so the band can sign off in one deep-link. All event copy stays exactly as supplied in the CSV. Deliverables: print-ready PDF, high-res JPEG, the duotone hero PNG, the wordmark SVG, and the review board link.
Deliverables (5)
  • Nocturne_Poster_print.pdf — Print-ready A2 portrait PDF exported from the data-merged InDesign document; event copy bound from CSV, duotoned hero photo placed in the full-bleed frame, vector wordmark placed; ~300 DPI, CMYK print
  • Nocturne_Poster_highres.jpg — High-resolution JPEG of the same bound poster (A2 portrait, max-quality) for Instagram/social sharing; must read at thumbnail size per the brief.
  • nocturne_hero_duotone.png — The band press photo prepped (straightened, auto-toned, high-contrast) and rendered as a bold two-tone duotone — indigo shadows, hot-magenta highlights — with fine print grain, cropped to the poster f
  • nocturne_wordmark.svg — Clean vector SVG of the THE NOCTURNE SESSIONS wordmark for razor-sharp scaling at A2 and reuse on merch/posters.
  • Nocturne_Poster_review_board — Firefly board deep-link assembling the duotone hero, the vector wordmark, and the final poster render for one-click band sign-off.
Workflow — 16 tool steps
  1. asset_preview_file — Preview the supplied raw press photo to confirm tilt, dull exposure and cluttered background before prepping.
  2. image_auto_straighten — Level the tilted horizon on the band press photo.
  3. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the straightened photo to fix the flat/dull under-lit exposure before the two-tone mapping.
  4. image_adjust_brightness_and_contrast — Push contrast hard so the duotone mapping has clean shadow/highlight separation (deep shadows, bright highlights).
  5. image_apply_monochromatic_tint — Flatten the whole high-contrast photo to a single-ink monochromatic base tint in the indigo shadow ink (hue from #1B1A4B in the brand note) — the standard duoto
  6. image_apply_color_overlay — Lay the hot-magenta highlight ink (RGB 255,46,136 from #FF2E88) over the WHOLE indigo base in a lighten blend so only the brighter pixels shift toward magenta —
  7. image_add_grain — Add a fine grain so the flat two-tone inks don't band on large-format A2 print.
  8. image_crop_and_resize — Crop/resize the duotone hero to the A2 poster hero-frame ratio the template expects.
  9. image_vectorize — Vectorize the flat wordmark PNG into a clean SVG so THE NOCTURNE SESSIONS stays razor-sharp at A2 and reusable on merch.
  10. font_recommend — Recommend a modern condensed display + clean body pairing for the poster's title/venue/date copy so the type reads bold and clean (advisory, informs the templat
  11. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize an upload session for the prepped poster assets (duotone hero, vector wordmark).
  12. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the upload; returns asset URNs the InDesign template/data-merge will reference for the hero frame and wordmark frame.
  13. document_merge_data_layout — InDesign data-merge: bind the title/venue/date/support/presale row into the client's authored .indd (which carries real <<fields>>); the duotone hero + vector w
  14. document_render_layout — Export the merged InDesign document to a print-ready ~300 DPI A2 PDF deliverable (CMYK print intent).
  15. document_render_layout — Export the same merged document to a high-resolution JPEG deliverable for social.
  16. create_firefly_board — Assemble the duotone hero, vector wordmark and final poster render onto a Firefly board deep-link for band sign-off.
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon flagship: 16 connector calls, 15 distinct Adobe tools across 4 groups (image_* raster, asset_* storage/preview, document_* InDesign, font_recommend + create_firefly_board) — above the >=10 calls / >=6 distinct-tool floor. Difficulty comes from constructing a genuine two-tone (duotone) hero without any banned capability: the two-ink look is built by real whole-image steps (high-contrast prep -> monochromatic base tint in ink 1 -> whole-image color-overlay of ink 2 in a lighten/screen blend so only the brighter pixels shift -> grain), not by a text-to-image or generative-fill shortcut and not by any tonal-range selection the tools do not support. It then vectorizes a supplied flat wordmark, converges everything into a genuine InDesign [T] data-merge, and dual-exports. It respects every hard limit: no generative fill / generative-expand / reframe-crop / object-removal-with-reconstruction / text-to-image / from-scratch art or logo / upscaling; image_fill_area and image_select_by_prompt are unused; the layout is NOT composed by the connector — document_render_layout / document_merge_data_layout only EXPORT/bind the band's authored .indd, which is a supplied input; the wordmark is supplied flat art and only vectorized (not designed from scratch); all event copy stays exactly as supplied in the CSV per the brief.

Client-supplied input assets: 5 total · 2 img · 0 video · 0 audio · 3 data

nocturne_poster_template.indd.proxy.txt
content
**File:** `nocturne_poster_template.indd`

**Description:**
This is an Adobe InDesign (.indd) template for an A2 portrait (420mm x 594mm) gig poster, featuring a bold, modern two-tone design.

**Structure & Layers:**
The template is built around a dominant full-bleed image frame intended for a hero photo, establishing the primary visual. A dedicated image frame for a wordmark or event logo is positioned near the top of the canvas. The lower third features a clean, organized typographic block designed to present event details clearly.

**Placeholders:**
The typographic block incorporates authentic Adobe InDesign data-merge placeholder fields for dynamic content binding:
*   `<<title>>`
*   `<<venue>>`
*   `<<date>>`
*   `<<support>>`
*   `<<presale>>`

**Brand Styling:**
The template employs a high-contrast, two-tone color palette, lending itself to a bold and contemporary aesthetic suitable for concert promotions. Typographic choices within the lower block are clean and legible, complementing the modern style.

AO-106 — Nordheim Atelier FW26 editorial poster series: duotone/halftone grayscale-cutout treatment of four runway looks into print-ready posters ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Cut four raw runway looks off their backgrounds, drive each to a consistent grayscale duotone/halftone editorial treatment with grain and a signature yellow accent, vectorize the wordmark, and run the client's authored InDesign poster template through a data-merge to produce four print-ready poster PDFs and high-res JPEGs.

💰 $500Upwork40 tool calls15 distinct toolseditlong-horizon
grounded in a real brief: Thumbnail & Editorial Image Designer Needed for Newsletter
Full client brief
Nordheim Atelier, an independent Scandinavian fashion label, is launching its FW26 "Concrete Season" capsule and needs a four-poster editorial series finished for its flagship windows and press kit. The look is fixed and non-negotiable: a monochrome documentary-explainer / investigative-magazine aesthetic built on grayscale cutout photography, a bold halftone print-screen texture, subtle film grain, and one signature warm-yellow ink accent — the same visual system across all four posters so they read as a set.

We are handing off: an authored InDesign poster template (poster_template.indd) laid out at A2 portrait (420x594mm) with one full-height image frame, a headline block, a look-credit block, and real data-merge fields (<<look_no>>, <<look_title>>, <<fabric_credit>>, <<stylist_credit>>); four raw runway photos shot backstage (flat, mixed venue lighting, distracting backstage clutter behind each model); our flat wordmark as a PNG; and a CSV of the four looks' titles and credits. The photos are unusable as-shot — busy backgrounds, dull flat tone, tilted horizons.

The job: (1) isolate each of the four looks cleanly off its cluttered backstage background so the garment reads as the hero on a clean plate; (2) drive every cutout to the identical editorial treatment — desaturate to grayscale, map to a two-ink duotone (near-black shadow #1A1A1A to soft paper-white highlight) via a monochromatic tint, apply the signature halftone print-screen dot texture, add fine film grain for a printed-matter feel, and warm every frame a touch toward our house yellow so none of them ever reads as cold pure-grey — the warm accent applied identically to all four; (3) crop all four consistently to the template's tall image frame; (4) vectorize the flat wordmark PNG into a clean SVG so it stays razor-sharp at A2; (5) upload all prepped assets, then run our authored .indd through InDesign data-merge against the credits CSV so each poster's look number / title / fabric + stylist credit bind into the layout with the treated image placed; (6) export each bound poster to a print-ready ~300 DPI PDF and a high-res JPEG; (7) assemble the treated images, the vector wordmark, and the four final posters onto a Firefly board so we can sign off on the treatment and the full set in one deep-link. Consistency across the four is the whole point — every step of the treatment, including the warm-yellow accent, must be applied identically to each look.
Deliverables (5)
  • posters_print_x4.pdf — Four print-ready A2 portrait (420x594mm) poster PDFs exported from the data-merged InDesign document — one per look; credit copy bound from CSV, treated grayscale-duotone/halftone image + vectorized w
  • posters_highres_x4.jpg — Four high-resolution JPEGs of the same bound posters (A2 portrait, max quality) for the digital press kit / social.
  • treated_looks_x4 — Four consistently-treated look images (isolated cutout, grayscale, near-black-to-paper-white duotone via monochromatic tint, halftone print-screen dots, fine film grain, warm-yellow accent applied ide
  • nordheim_wordmark.svg — Clean vector SVG of the NORDHEIM ATELIER wordmark for razor-sharp scaling at A2 poster size.
  • fw26_review_board — Firefly board deep-link assembling the four treated look images, the vectorized wordmark, and the four final poster renders for one-click sign-off on the treatment and the full set.
Workflow — 40 tool steps
  1. asset_add_file — Client supplies look01_runway_raw.jpg via the file picker; returns assetId + presignedAssetUrl. Initial asset load for the first look.
  2. image_remove_background — Isolate look 01 cleanly off the cluttered backstage background to a transparent PNG so the garment reads as hero on a clean plate.
  3. image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation — Pull saturation fully down to drive the cutout to neutral grayscale (removes look 01's mixed venue color cast) — first look-dev step of the shared editorial tre
  4. image_apply_monochromatic_tint — Map the grayscale look to the signature two-ink duotone (near-black shadow #1A1A1A to soft paper-white highlight) — the monochrome magazine ink look.
  5. image_apply_halftone — Apply the signature print-screen halftone dot texture over the duotone — the documentary/investigative-magazine texture the brief calls for.
  6. image_add_grain — Add fine film grain for a printed-matter editorial feel.
  7. image_adjust_color_temperature — Warm the whole frame a touch toward the house yellow so the monochrome never reads as cold pure-grey — the signature accent, applied identically to all four loo
  8. image_crop_and_resize — Crop/resize to the template's 4:5 full-height image frame (same target applied to all four looks for a consistent set).
  9. asset_add_file — Client supplies look02_runway_raw.jpg via picker. Secondary asset load — feeds the same treatment sub-chain.
  10. image_remove_background — Isolate look 02 off its rig/exit-sign backstage clutter.
  11. image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation — Desaturate look 02 to grayscale to match the shared treatment.
  12. image_apply_monochromatic_tint — Apply the identical near-black-to-paper-white duotone map so look 02 matches look 01's ink.
  13. image_apply_halftone — Apply the same halftone print-screen dot texture at the same dot pitch for set consistency.
  14. image_add_grain — Add the matching fine film grain.
  15. image_adjust_color_temperature — Warm toward the house yellow to match look 01's accent — the identical warm step every look receives.
  16. image_crop_and_resize — Crop look 02 to the matching 4:5 template frame.
  17. asset_add_file — Client supplies look03_runway_raw.jpg via picker. Third look load.
  18. image_remove_background — Isolate look 03 off the monitors/cabling backstage clutter.
  19. image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation — Desaturate look 03 to grayscale (kills its warm-green mixed cast) to match the set.
  20. image_apply_monochromatic_tint — Apply the identical duotone ink map to look 03.
  21. image_apply_halftone — Apply the matching halftone dot texture to look 03.
  22. image_add_grain — Add the matching film grain to look 03.
  23. image_adjust_color_temperature — Warm look 03 toward the house yellow — the identical warm-yellow accent applied to every look so the set reads consistently.
  24. image_crop_and_resize — Crop look 03 to the 4:5 template frame.
  25. asset_add_file — Client supplies look04_runway_raw.jpg via picker. Fourth look load.
  26. image_remove_background — Isolate look 04 off the clothes-rack/blown-doorway backstage clutter.
  27. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone look 04 first to fix its underexposure before the shared monochrome treatment (so its duotone contrast matches the others).
  28. image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation — Desaturate look 04 to grayscale to match the set.
  29. image_apply_monochromatic_tint — Apply the identical duotone ink map to look 04.
  30. image_apply_halftone — Apply the matching halftone dot texture to look 04.
  31. image_add_grain — Add the matching film grain to look 04.
  32. image_adjust_color_temperature — Warm look 04 toward the house yellow — the identical warm-yellow accent applied to every look so all four match.
  33. image_crop_and_resize — Crop look 04 to the 4:5 template frame so all four treated looks match.
  34. image_vectorize — Vectorize the flat wordmark PNG into a clean SVG for razor-sharp scaling at A2 poster size.
  35. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize an upload session for all prepped poster assets (four treated look images + vector wordmark).
  36. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the upload; returns asset URNs the InDesign template/data-merge will reference.
  37. document_merge_data_layout — InDesign data-merge: bind the four looks' look_no/look_title/fabric_credit/stylist_credit rows into the client's authored .indd (which carries real <<fields>>);
  38. document_render_layout — Export the merged InDesign document (four data-merge records) to print-ready ~300 DPI A2 poster PDFs.
  39. document_render_layout — Export the same merged document to high-resolution JPEGs (one per poster) for the digital press kit.
  40. create_firefly_board — Assemble the four treated look images, the vector wordmark, and the final poster renders onto a Firefly board deep-link for one-click sign-off on the treatment
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon flagship: 37 connector calls across four tool groups (image_* raster look-dev, asset_* storage/board, document_* InDesign, create_firefly_board) and 15 distinct Adobe tools — far above the >=10 calls / >=6 distinct-tool floor. Difficulty comes from running four separate runway photos through an IDENTICAL six-to-seven-step editorial treatment spine (isolate -> desaturate -> duotone tint -> halftone -> grain -> warm -> crop) so they read as a consistent set, absorbing per-photo variance (look 04's underexposure gets an extra auto_tone; look 03's cast is handled by the duotone), vectorizing the wordmark, then converging everything into a genuine InDesign [T] data-merge and dual export, and finally a review board. It respects every hard limit: no generative fill / outpaint / text-to-image / object-removal-with-reconstruction / upscaling / from-scratch layout; image_fill_area and image_crop_and_resize are unused; the layout is NOT composed by the connector — document_merge_data_layout/document_render_layout only BIND/EXPORT the client's authored .indd, which is a supplied input; image_remove_background is used only to isolate the subject to a transparent cutout (not compositing or background-replace-by-prompt); halftone/monochromatic_tint/add_grain are exact listed tonal-FX capabilities; the placeholder credits are honored literally via CSV.

poster_template.indd.proxy.txt
content
**Template Description: `poster_template.indd`**

This is an Adobe InDesign (.indd) template for a single A2 portrait (420x594mm) fashion editorial poster, styled in a monochrome documentary/magazine aesthetic.

**Structure & Layout:**
The template features a dominant full-height image frame positioned for a 4:5 aspect ratio, designed to showcase a treated runway look. Below or adjacent to this, a dedicated headline block is provided for the editorial title. A separate look-credit block is included for detailed attribution. The layout incorporates subtle thin grid-line rules, contributing to the documentary feel, and a distinct yellow highlight block as part of the editorial system's branding. A specific placeholder area is designated for the vectorized brand wordmark.

**Layers & Placeholders:**
The document is authored with live InDesign data-merge fields to facilitate automated content population. These include:
*   `<<look_no>>`
*   `<<look_title>>`
*   `<<fabric_credit>>`
*   `<<stylist_credit>>`

**Brand Styling:**
The template adheres to a monochrome palette, accented by the editorial system's signature yellow highlight block. Thin grid-line rules reinforce the structured, magazine-like, and documentary aesthetic.

AO-107 — Deli & Dough Uber Eats hero banner: grade + cut out four supplied deli shots, source a butcher-paper backdrop, vectorize the wordmark, and bind into an authored 2880x2304 banner template for print-ready export ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Grade four raw deli photos to one cohesive gritty-warm look, cut the hero sandwich onto transparency, source a butcher-paper backdrop, vectorize the Deli & Dough wordmark, and bind all the prepped parts into the client's authored 2880x2304 Illustrator banner template to export a print-ready Uber Eats hero banner.

💰 $20–$40/hrUpwork29 tool calls16 distinct toolscreatelong-horizon
grounded in a real brief: Graphic designer needed: Uber Eats banner for a NY style sandwich brand
Full client brief
Deli & Dough is a scrappy New-York-style sandwich shop in Western Sydney — gritty, bold, a little chaotic, think NY deli meets Nonna's kitchen meets street culture, not polished or corporate. We're setting up our Uber Eats storefront and need our hero banner finished. We already have the assets; they just need to be pulled together so they look like they were shot in one image — one cohesive, intentional photo, NOT a collage. Here is the hand-off: (1) an authored Adobe Illustrator banner template (banner_template.ai) built to EXACTLY 2880px wide x 2304px high (the Uber Eats hero spec), containing named placement frames — a full-bleed background frame, a hero-sandwich frame, three supporting food frames, and a wordmark frame — plus a small data-merge slug row so the tagline/handle text binds in; (2) four raw phone-shot deli photos — the hero pastrami-on-rye stacked high, a supporting Italian sub, a supporting basket of house pickles, and a supporting drip-coffee cup; (3) our wordmark as a flat cream-on-red PNG; (4) direction to back everything on a gritty butcher-paper / deli-counter texture. The four photos are inconsistent as shot: different white balance (some cool, some warm), tilted, dull and flat, busy backgrounds. The job: (a) grade all four to ONE consistent gritty-warm deli look — straighten, auto-tone, warm the white balance, lift the shadows/contrast, push vibrance a touch, and lock the look with a single shared preset so they truly read as the same shoot; crop each to the frame ratios the template expects; (b) lift the hero pastrami sandwich cleanly onto a transparent background so it drops onto the butcher-paper backdrop without a hard rectangular photo edge (that hard edge is what makes it look like a collage); (c) source and license a rustic butcher-paper/deli-counter backdrop texture from Adobe Stock; (d) vectorize the flat wordmark PNG into a clean SVG so it stays razor-sharp at banner size; (e) upload the prepped parts and run our authored .ai template through a data-merge so each graded photo, the cutout hero, the vector wordmark, the backdrop, and the tagline slug bind into their named frames; (f) export the bound banner to a print-ready PNG and a max-quality JPEG at EXACTLY 2880x2304, and drop a review board so we can sign off. Keep it gritty and warm; do not make it clean-corporate.
Deliverables (5)
  • DeliAndDough_UberEats_banner_2880x2304.png — Print-ready PNG at EXACTLY 2880px x 2304px (Uber Eats hero spec, margins filled), exported from the data-merged Illustrator banner: butcher-paper backdrop + cutout hero sandwich + three graded support
  • DeliAndDough_UberEats_banner_2880x2304.jpg — Max-quality JPEG of the same bound banner at exactly 2880x2304, sRGB, for the Uber Eats upload flow.
  • graded_deli_photos_x4 — Four color-graded, straightened, template-cropped deli photos (hero pastrami / Italian sub / house pickles / drip coffee) pulled to ONE consistent gritty-warm look; the hero also delivered as a transp
  • deli_and_dough_wordmark.svg — Clean vector SVG of the Deli & Dough wordmark for razor-sharp scaling at banner size.
  • DeliAndDough_banner_review_board — Firefly board deep-link assembling the graded photos, the hero cutout, the vector wordmark, and the final banner render for one-click owner sign-off on the cohesive look before it goes live.
Workflow — 29 tool steps
  1. asset_search — Search Adobe Stock for a crumpled butcher-paper / deli-counter backdrop texture using the client's direction-note hint (low-res candidate previews + asset IDs).
  2. asset_license_and_download_stock — License the chosen butcher-paper texture BEFORE use and download it full-res; becomes the banner background asset.
  3. image_auto_straighten — Level the ~5deg tilted horizon on the hero pastrami shot before grading.
  4. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the hero to fix the flat/dull exposure — the baseline for the shared look.
  5. image_adjust_color_temperature — Warm the cool white balance toward the gritty-warm deli target the brand note calls for.
  6. image_adjust_dark_portions — Lift the crushed dark portions so the stacked meat keeps texture — part of the shared grade.
  7. image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation — Push vibrance a touch so the pastrami/mustard/rye read appetizing without going neon.
  8. image_apply_preset — Apply a single shared gritty-warm preset to LOCK the cohesive look — this same preset is reused on the three supporting photos so all four match.
  9. image_crop_and_resize — Crop/resize the graded hero to the aspect the template's <<hero_cutout>> frame expects.
  10. image_remove_background — Lift the finished hero sandwich onto a transparent background so it drops onto the butcher-paper backdrop with no hard rectangular photo edge (kills the collage
  11. image_apply_auto_tone — Run the Italian sub through the shared grade starting with auto-tone (also tames its overexposed side).
  12. image_adjust_color_temperature — Correct the sub's too-warm tungsten white balance so it matches the hero rather than clashing — the key move to make the four read as one shoot.
  13. image_apply_preset — Apply the SAME shared preset to the sub so it locks to the hero's gritty-warm look.
  14. image_crop_and_resize — Crop the sub to the <<support_1>> frame ratio.
  15. image_auto_straighten — Straighten the tilted pickles shot.
  16. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the cool/dull pickles shot into the shared baseline.
  17. image_adjust_color_temperature — Warm the cool cast on the pickles to match the hero/sub.
  18. image_apply_preset — Apply the shared preset so the pickles lock to the cohesive look; crop follows next.
  19. image_crop_and_resize — Crop the pickles to the <<support_2>> frame ratio.
  20. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the muddy/underexposed coffee shot to the shared baseline.
  21. image_apply_preset — Apply the SAME shared preset so the coffee cup matches the other three — all four now read as one shoot.
  22. image_crop_and_resize — Crop the coffee to the <<support_3>> frame ratio.
  23. image_vectorize — Vectorize the flat wordmark PNG into a clean SVG for razor-sharp placement at banner scale.
  24. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize an upload session for all prepped banner parts (cutout hero, three graded supports, vector wordmark, backdrop texture).
  25. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the upload; returns the CC asset URNs the authored template's placement frames will reference.
  26. document_merge_data_vector — Data-merge into the client's AUTHORED 2880x2304 .ai: bind the butcher-paper backdrop, cutout hero, three graded supports, and vector wordmark into their named f
  27. document_render_vector — Export the merged banner to a print-ready PNG at EXACTLY 2880x2304 (margins filled per the Uber Eats spec). render_vector only EXPORTS the authored+bound docume
  28. document_render_vector — Export the same merged banner to a max-quality JPEG at 2880x2304 for the Uber Eats upload flow.
  29. create_firefly_board — Assemble the graded photos, hero cutout, vector wordmark, and final banner render onto a Firefly board deep-link so the owner can sign off on the cohesive look
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon flagship: 29 connector calls across 4 tool groups (image_* raster grade/cutout/vectorize, asset_* stock/storage, document_* Illustrator merge/render, create_firefly_board), 16 distinct Adobe tools — far above the >=10 calls / >=6 distinct-tool floor. Difficulty comes from the core stock_hero_expand/create challenge: taking FOUR inconsistent phone photos (different white balance, tilt, exposure) and grading them to ONE cohesive gritty-warm look via a shared preset so they read as 'shot in the same image', branching the hero into a clean transparent cutout, sourcing+licensing a backdrop texture, vectorizing a logo, then converging everything into a genuine Illustrator [T] data-merge that binds each part into named frames and dual-exporting at EXACTLY 2880x2304. It respects every hard limit: NO generative-expand/outpaint (the banner reaches 2880x2304 via the authored template's fixed canvas + render, not by outpainting pixels), NO generative fill, NO text-to-image / from-scratch art, NO freehand compositing (the connector composes no canvas — merge_data_vector/render_vector only BIND and EXPORT the client's authored .ai, listed as a supplied input), NO object-removal-with-reconstruction, NO upscaling; image_fill_area is unused; image_remove_background is used legitimately for a transparent PNG cutout; banner text is bound via the authored template's merge slug (CSV),
