banner_template.ai.proxy.txt
content
**Template File Description:**

**File Name:** `banner_template.ai`

**Purpose:** Uber Eats hero banner, designed for high-impact promotional campaigns across digital platforms.

**Dimensions:** 2880px (width) x 2304px (height).

**Structure & Layers:** The template is meticulously laid out with specific, named placement frames and data-merge text fields to enable efficient content population:

*   **`<<bg_texture>>`**: A full-bleed background frame, covering the entire canvas, intended for a texture or background image.
*   **`<<hero_cutout>>`**: A prominent, large frame positioned on the left side, designed to showcase a transparent hero food item (e.g., a sandwich cutout).
*   **`<<support_1>>`**, **`<<support_2>>`**, **`<<support_3>>`**: Three distinct supporting frames, typically arranged to the right or below the hero, for graded sub-photos of additional menu items or promotional imagery.
*   **`<<wordmark>>`**: A dedicated frame for the Uber Eats wordmark or a campaign-specific logo, typically placed in a corner.
*   **Data-Merge Text Row**: A pre-formatted text slug row configured for data-merge functionality, containing:
    *   **`<<tagline>>`**: A field for the primary promotional message or offer.
    *   **`<<handle>>`**: A field for a secondary identifier, call-to-action, or social media handle.

**Brand Styling:** The template adheres to a clean, modern, and food-centric aesthetic consistent with Uber Eats branding. It prioritizes clear visual hierarchy, vibrant food imagery, and legible typography to maximize promotional impact.

**Functionality:** This template is explicitly authored for real data-merge binding, ensuring that all named placement frames and text fields are correctly linked for automated content integration from an external data source.
brand_direction_note.txt
gemini/gemini-2.5-flash
content
Deli & Dough: Embrace a gritty NY-deli meets Nonna's kitchen and street-culture vibe. Focus on warmth and character; steer clear of any corporate or sterile aesthetics. Backdrop assets should feature rustic butcher-paper or deli-counter textures. All food photography must feel like a single, cohesive shoot. Uber Eats banner is 2880x2304. Stock hint: 'crumpled brown butcher paper deli counter texture, warm, gritty, top-down'.

AO-109 — Kantyna Nova website photo grade: batch-retouch 8 raw restaurant food photos to a cohesive premium look + desktop/mobile crops ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Batch-retouch eight raw restaurant food photos to one cohesive premium color grade, straightened and cleaned up, then deliver desktop (16:9) and mobile (4:5) crops of each plus a review board for owner sign-off.

💰 ~$138 est.Freelancer.com39 tool calls15 distinct toolseditlong-horizon
grounded in a real brief: Professional Photo Editor for Restaurant Brand
Full client brief
Kantyna Nova, a modern Polish comfort-food restaurant and catering brand, is launching a new website and needs its food photography brought up to a premium, cohesive standard. The owner, Marta Zawadzka, has handed off eight raw phone-and-DSLR shots straight from a shoot day: two hero plates (a bowl of pierogi with browned onions, a golden schnitzel platter), a bright beet-and-goat-cheese salad, a hearty bowl of zurek soup, a slice of layered sernik cheesecake, a flat-lay of a catering grazing spread, a bar shot of a craft kompot drink, and a wide interior shot of the dining room. As-shot they are inconsistent and unappetizing: several are slightly tilted, most are flat and underexposed with a cool/greenish canteen-lighting cast, backgrounds are busy, and the colours drift from photo to photo so they would never read as one brand. The job is a pure retouch/grade edit (no layout, no new imagery): (1) straighten anything tilted and auto-tone every shot to a clean baseline; (2) establish ONE cohesive premium look on the lead hero — warm the white balance off the canteen green, lift the shadows, push vibrance so food reads rich and appetizing, boost contrast, and settle on a reusable food-grade preset so the whole set can be aligned to one named look; (3) apply that same preset across all remaining shots so the full set looks graded together, with per-shot exposure nudges where a frame is darker; (4) throw the busiest backgrounds gently out of focus with a subject-aware background depth-of-field lens blur (keeps the plate/glass sharp, softens the clutter behind it) so the food/subject reads as hero while keeping a natural look; (5) crop-and-resize each graded image into TWO matched deliverables — a desktop hero crop at 16:9 (1920x1080) and a mobile crop at 4:5 (1080x1350) — so the site has correctly-composed assets for both breakpoints without stretching. Keep everything natural — no plastic over-processing. Deliverables: sixteen graded, correctly-cropped JPEGs (8 photos x desktop + mobile), organised in a clean folder structure, plus a Firefly review board deep-link assembling the graded set for Marta to approve the look in one click. Brand direction: warm, rich, premium, appetizing; consistent grade across every image; natural, not garish.
Deliverables (5)
  • desktop_crops_16x9_x8 — Eight graded, straightened, cohesively-color-graded desktop hero JPEGs cropped/resized to 16:9 at 1920x1080 (pierogi, schnitzel, salad, zurek, sernik, grazing flat-lay, kompot, interior), all sharing
  • mobile_crops_4x5_x8 — Eight matched mobile JPEGs of the same graded images cropped/resized to 4:5 at 1080x1350 for the mobile website breakpoint, subject-centered and consistent with the desktop set.
  • kantyna_nova_look_preset — The reusable food-grade preset settled on from the lead hero (warm WB + lifted shadows + vibrance + contrast) and applied across the set to enforce a cohesive look.
  • Kantyna_Nova_web_photos_folder — Clean Creative Cloud folder structure (desktop/ and mobile/ subfolders) holding all sixteen deliverable JPEGs, uploaded and organised for hand-off.
  • Kantyna_Nova_review_board — Firefly board deep-link assembling the eight graded desktop crops for Marta to approve the cohesive look in one click.
Workflow — 39 tool steps
  1. asset_create_folders — Create the clean hand-off folder structure (Kantyna_Nova_web_photos with desktop/ and mobile/ subfolders) up front.
  2. image_auto_straighten — Level the ~3-degree tilt on the lead hero pierogi shot before establishing the look.
  3. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the hero to a clean exposure/contrast baseline.
  4. image_adjust_color_temperature — Warm the white balance off the cool greenish canteen cast toward the premium warm look.
  5. image_adjust_dark_portions — Lift the crushed shadows so the food reads rich without going flat.
  6. image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation — Push vibrance so the onions/dill/dough read appetizing while keeping it natural.
  7. image_adjust_brightness_and_contrast — Add gentle contrast to finish the cohesive premium grade; this frame is the look reference.
  8. image_list_presets — List the available food/warm presets and settle on the named 'kantyna_nova_look' preset that best matches the reference grade, to reuse across the set for consi
  9. image_crop_and_resize — Cut the desktop hero crop of the graded pierogi at 16:9 / 1920x1080.
  10. image_crop_and_resize — Cut the matched mobile crop of the graded pierogi at 4:5 / 1080x1350, subject-centered.
  11. image_apply_preset — Apply the shared preset to the schnitzel hero so it matches the lead grade in one step.
  12. image_apply_lens_blur — Gently throw the cluttered napkin/menu background out of focus so the plate reads as hero (whole-frame).
  13. image_crop_and_resize — Desktop 16:9 / 1920x1080 crop of the graded schnitzel.
  14. image_crop_and_resize — Matched mobile 4:5 / 1080x1350 crop of the graded schnitzel.
  15. image_apply_preset — Apply the shared preset to the beet/goat-cheese salad for a consistent look.
  16. image_crop_and_resize — Desktop 16:9 crop of the graded salad.
  17. image_crop_and_resize — Matched mobile 4:5 crop of the graded salad.
  18. image_apply_preset — Apply the shared preset to the zurek soup shot.
  19. image_adjust_exposure — Per-shot exposure nudge on the darkest frame so it still matches the graded set.
  20. image_crop_and_resize — Desktop 16:9 crop of the graded, exposure-corrected zurek.
  21. image_crop_and_resize — Matched mobile 4:5 crop of the graded zurek.
  22. image_apply_preset — Apply the shared preset to the sernik cheesecake shot.
  23. image_crop_and_resize — Desktop 16:9 crop of the graded sernik.
  24. image_crop_and_resize — Matched mobile 4:5 crop of the graded sernik.
  25. image_auto_straighten — Level the tilted grazing flat-lay before grading.
  26. image_apply_preset — Apply the shared preset to the straightened catering grazing spread.
  27. image_crop_and_resize — Desktop 16:9 crop of the graded grazing flat-lay.
  28. image_crop_and_resize — Matched mobile 4:5 crop of the graded grazing flat-lay.
  29. image_apply_preset — Apply the shared preset to the kompot drink shot.
  30. image_apply_lens_blur — Gently blur the busy bar-shelf background so the glass reads as hero (whole-frame).
  31. image_crop_and_resize — Desktop 16:9 crop of the graded kompot.
  32. image_crop_and_resize — Matched mobile 4:5 crop of the graded kompot.
  33. image_auto_straighten — Level the ~2-degree tilt on the wide dining-room ambience shot.
  34. image_apply_preset — Apply the shared preset to the interior so the ambience shot matches the food set.
  35. image_crop_and_resize — Desktop 16:9 crop of the graded interior for the site header.
  36. image_crop_and_resize — Matched mobile 4:5 crop of the graded interior.
  37. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize an upload session for all sixteen deliverable crops into the prepared folder structure.
  38. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the upload, filing the desktop/mobile JPEGs into their desktop/ and mobile/ subfolders for hand-off.
  39. create_firefly_board — Assemble the eight graded desktop crops onto a Firefly board deep-link so Marta can approve the cohesive look in one click.
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon flagship edit: 39 connector calls, 15 distinct Adobe tools across two groups (image_* raster grading/cropping, asset_* folders/upload) plus create_firefly_board -- far above the >=10 calls / >=6 distinct-tool floor. Difficulty is genuine batch-consistency work: build one premium look on a lead hero via a six-step manual grade, settle on a reusable named preset, then enforce that exact look across seven more heterogeneous subjects (plates, salad, soup, dessert, flat-lay, drink, interior) with per-shot straighten/exposure/lens-blur corrections, and cut a matched desktop-16:9 + mobile-4:5 crop pair for all eight, ending in organised upload and a review board. It respects every hard limit: pure edit of supplied photos, no generative fill/expand/reframe-crop, no text-to-image or from-scratch art, no upscaling, no object-removal/inpainting (imperfections are handled by whole-image tone/color grade + whole-frame lens blur, not reconstruction), image_fill_area unused, no InDesign/layout composition. lens_blur is whole-image with no masked-DOF claim.

Data & other (1)

kantyna_nova_brand_direction.txt
content
KANTYNA NOVA: BRAND PHOTO DIRECTION

**Client:** Marta Zawadzka
**Brand:** Kantyna Nova (Modern Polish Comfort Food & Catering)

**OVERALL AESTHETIC GOAL:**
To visually represent Kantyna Nova as a warm, inviting, and contemporary Polish comfort food experience. Images should evoke a sense of deliciousness, authenticity, and approachable quality, moving away from dated or 'canteen-like' perceptions. We aim for an elevated yet natural feel.

**PHOTOGRAPHIC DIRECTIVES:**

*   **White Balance & Color:** Crucially, correct any cool, drab 'canteen-green' cast often associated with institutional kitchens. Aim for a distinctly warm and rich white balance. Colors should feel inviting and true to life, enhancing the natural appeal of the food and environment.
*   **Shadows & Light:** Shadows should be gently lifted, ensuring detail and warmth are retained without flattening the image. Avoid harsh, deep shadows. Light should feel natural and soft, suggestive of a welcoming space rather than a studio setup.
*   **Vibrance & Saturation:** Elevate vibrance to make food look exceptionally appetizing and fresh. Colors should pop just enough to be enticing, but without appearing oversaturated or artificial.
*   **Contrast:** Apply gentle contrast. We want depth and visual interest, but avoid overly dramatic or harsh contrast that can make food look uninviting or processed.
*   **Processing & Grade:** The overall look must be natural and authentic, not over-processed or overly stylized. A consistent grade across all images is paramount to ensure brand cohesion. Each image should feel like part of a unified visual story for Kantyna Nova.

**TECHNICAL SPECIFICATIONS:**

*   **Desktop Hero Crops:** All primary hero images for desktop use should be delivered at a 16:9 aspect ratio, specifically 1920x1080 pixels.
*   **Mobile Crops:** All primary images for mobile use should be delivered at a 4:5 aspect ratio, specifically 1080x1350 pixels.

AO-110 — Summit & Sable eCommerce catalog: batch color-grade six product shots to house look + recolor hardware/label accents without reshoot ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Batch color-grade six existing Summit & Sable product photos to one consistent house look, then non-destructively recolor the recovery-strap hardware and label accents on two hero shots — keeping lighting, shadow and texture natural — and export web-ready catalog images plus a review board.

💰 $8–$45/hrUpwork33 tool calls11 distinct toolseditlong-horizon
grounded in a real brief: Professional Photoshop Editor Needed for Ongoing eCommerce Product Editing & Color Swapping
Full client brief
Summit & Sable is a direct-to-consumer overland and off-road recovery gear brand refreshing its online catalog for the new season. We are NOT reshooting — we need our existing product photos cleaned up and made consistent, plus a couple of color variants generated from shots we already have, exactly the way our old Photoshop editor used to do it. We're handing off six existing product photos (JPEG, roughly 2400x3000, shot in mixed lighting across two sessions so they don't match): a red recovery strap coiled on white sweep, the same strap's anodized aluminum snatch-hook shackle in close-up, a soft shackle, a folding shovel, a recovery tracks pair, and a branded ratchet strap with our printed label visible. Two jobs. First, batch grade: run all six through one consistent HOUSE LOOK so the catalog reads as a matched set — auto-tone the flat ones, correct the cool/warm white-balance mismatch between the two sessions to a neutral daylight, even out exposure, add a touch of vibrance so the products pop on a white PDP background, and lock a single Lightroom-style preset across the batch so contrast and tone are identical. Do not blow out the whites or crush the blacks; catalog images must stay true-to-product. Second, color variants WITHOUT a reshoot: on the recovery-strap hero, swap the strap-hardware accent from our current safety-orange anodizing to a gunmetal/graphite finish so we can list a second colorway; and on the ratchet-strap shot, shift the printed label's red brand block to our new forest-green house color. THE CATCH (this is what our last editor got wrong): change the color without ruining the shot — the lighting, shadows, metal reflections and woven-webbing texture must remain completely natural. No sloppy masking, no flat dead-fill color, no unrealistic edits; the recolor has to sit under the existing highlights and shadows. Deliverables: the six graded catalog JPEGs at web resolution (long edge 2000px, sRGB), the two recolored variant images, and a single review board deep-link so our merchandiser can approve the house look and both colorways in one place before they go on the site.
Deliverables (4)
  • catalog_graded_x6 — Six color-graded product JPEGs, auto-toned + white-balance-matched to neutral daylight + exposure-evened + vibrance-lifted + the Summit & Sable House Look preset applied uniformly; sRGB, long edge 200
  • variant_strap_gunmetal.jpg — Recolored variant of prod_01: the safety-orange strap hardware/accent recolored to a gunmetal/graphite finish via a masked hue-shift + luminosity-preserving overlay riding the existing luminosity, so
  • variant_label_forest_green.jpg — Recolored variant of prod_06: the printed label's red brand block hue-shifted to the new forest-green house color inside a tight selection while preserving the label's stitching texture, small white t
  • Summit_Sable_catalog_review_board — Firefly board deep-link assembling the six graded catalog images plus the two color variants so the merchandiser can approve the house look and both colorways in one place.
Workflow — 33 tool steps
  1. asset_add_file — Upload the six existing product photos and the House Look .xmp preset into the working project so the batch grade can run against real asset references.
  2. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone prod_01 (cool, flat) to a sane baseline before white-balance and preset.
  3. image_adjust_color_temperature — Warm prod_01's cool/blue cast toward neutral daylight so it matches the warm second-session shots.
  4. image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation — Lift vibrance so the strap reads richly against a white PDP without oversaturating.
  5. image_apply_preset — Lock the supplied Summit & Sable House Look .xmp (referenced from catalog_assets_uploaded) onto prod_01 to fix the shared contrast/tone (same preset reused acro
  6. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone prod_02, the slightly overexposed warm shackle close-up.
  7. image_adjust_color_temperature — Cool prod_02's warm cast toward the same neutral-daylight target so session one and two match.
  8. image_adjust_exposure — Pull prod_02's exposure down slightly so its brightness matches the rest of the set (protect metal highlights).
  9. image_apply_preset — Apply the House Look preset to prod_02 for identical tone.
  10. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the muddy low-contrast soft-shackle shot (prod_03).
  11. image_apply_preset — Apply the House Look preset to prod_03.
  12. image_adjust_exposure — Lift exposure on the underexposed folding-shovel shot (prod_04) toward the set brightness.
  13. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone prod_04 after the exposure lift to normalize contrast.
  14. image_apply_preset — Apply the House Look preset to prod_04.
  15. image_adjust_color_temperature — Warm prod_05's cool cast (recovery tracks, session one) to the neutral-daylight target.
  16. image_apply_preset — Apply the House Look preset to prod_05.
  17. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the warm, flat ratchet-strap shot (prod_06).
  18. image_adjust_color_temperature — Cool prod_06's warm cast to match the neutral-daylight set.
  19. image_apply_preset — Apply the House Look preset to prod_06 so all six now match as a catalog set.
  20. image_select_by_prompt — On the graded strap hero, select just the orange hardware/accent ('orange metal loop and anodized fitting on the recovery strap') so the recolor is isolated to
  21. image_adjust_hsl — Within the selection (consumes the graded hero + the mask from step 20), drop the orange's saturation and shift hue toward neutral gunmetal, letting existing lu
  22. image_apply_color_overlay — Blend a subtle graphite color overlay confined to the selected hardware (consumes the hue-shifted frame + the same mask; luminosity-preserving) to finish the gu
  23. image_crop_and_resize — Resize the finished gunmetal recolor to the same web catalog spec as the catalog set (long edge 2000px, sRGB) so the variant matches its stated output frame.
  24. image_select_by_prompt — On the graded ratchet-strap shot, select only the printed label's red brand block ('red rectangular printed label block') to isolate the hue change from the whi
  25. image_adjust_hsl — Shift the selected red block's hue to the forest-green house color via HSL (consumes the graded shot + the mask from step 24), preserving the label's stitch tex
  26. image_crop_and_resize — Resize the finished forest-green label recolor to the same web catalog spec as the catalog set (long edge 2000px, sRGB) so the variant matches its stated output
  27. image_crop_and_resize — Resize graded prod_01 to web catalog spec (long edge 2000px, sRGB).
  28. image_crop_and_resize — Resize graded prod_02 to the same web catalog spec.
  29. image_crop_and_resize — Resize graded prod_03 to web catalog spec.
  30. image_crop_and_resize — Resize graded prod_04 to web catalog spec.
  31. image_crop_and_resize — Resize graded prod_05 to web catalog spec.
  32. image_crop_and_resize — Resize graded prod_06 to web catalog spec; web_p1..web_p6 form the catalog_graded_x6 deliverable.
  33. create_firefly_board — Assemble the six graded catalog images plus both color variants onto a Firefly board deep-link for one-click merchandiser sign-off on the house look and colorwa
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon flagship: 33 connector calls, 11 distinct Adobe tools, per-image grade pipelines of differing depth converging on one house look, plus two isolated luminosity-preserving part recolors each resized to the web catalog frame. No banned capability used.

AO-111 — Restore a Faded, Scratched Family Keepsake Photo to a Framed A4 Print ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Restore one treasured, faded and scratched 1970s family keepsake scan -- correct the color cast and faded contrast, recover clarity/detail, conceal scratches over the plain backdrop -- and deliver a framed-A4 print-ready file with a before/after approval board.

💰 ~$120 est.Freelancer.com17 tool calls17 distinct toolseditlong-horizon
grounded in a real brief: Old Picture Restoration
Full client brief
Hello -- I run a small family-history blog called Meadowlark Memory Lane and I have one very special photo I'd love professionally restored. It's a personal keepsake and means a lot to me: a color snapshot of my grandparents and my mother as a little girl, taken in a portrait studio in the mid-1970s. The physical print has aged badly and I've had it scanned at high resolution on a flatbed so you can work from the digital file.

The supplied scan (meadowlark_family_keepsake_1975.png) has these problems: the whole image has gone warm/orange and flat -- the color has faded and lost contrast; there are several fine white scratch lines and one short crease crossing the plain cream studio backdrop behind the family (they do NOT cross the faces); the scan sits very slightly crooked on the scanner bed with a thin ragged white border down one side; and overall the picture looks soft and washed out.

What I need: (1) straighten the slight scanner tilt and crop off the ragged white scan border so only the photograph remains; (2) correct the faded warm/orange color cast and recover natural, believable skin tones and clothing colors; (3) recover the faded contrast and lift overall clarity and local detail so the picture reads crisply on a framed print -- this is the "colors, details and sharpness" I care most about; (4) conceal the scratches and the crease, but ONLY where they cross the plain cream backdrop -- please colour-match that uniform area and fill the marks so they disappear (leave the faces and figures untouched); (5) add a very light film grain over the restored areas so the cleaned-up backdrop patch doesn't look plastic or smoothed against the original photographic texture; (6) deliver a clean, true-A4 file at 2480x3508 px / 300 DPI so I can send it straight to the print lab and frame it.

Deliverable: the single restored keepsake as a print-and-frame-ready A4 image, PLUS a before/after approval board (original scan next to the restored A4) so I can look it over and sign off before I print -- given how much this photo means, I'd like to approve it first. Please keep it looking like a genuine 1970s photograph, just healthy again -- no modern, over-processed, plastic look.
Deliverables (2)
  • meadowlark_keepsake_restored_A4.png — The fully restored 1970s family keepsake: tilt-straightened, scan-border cropped, warm/orange color cast neutralized, faded contrast and clarity/local-detail recovered, backdrop scratches and crease c
  • keepsake_before_after_board — A single Firefly Board deep-link placing the original aged scan next to the restored A4 output as a before/after approval contact-board, so the client can review and sign off on the restoration before
Workflow — 17 tool steps
  1. asset_initialize_file_upload — The client supplies the keepsake scan (not Adobe Stock), so ingest the single provided file meadowlark_family_keepsake_1975.png into CC storage via the upload i
  2. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the upload to obtain a stable asset URN/URL for the scan that the editing steps will reference.
  3. asset_inline_preview — Preview the ingested scan to confirm the damage the restoration must address (warm cast, flat contrast, tilt, ragged border, backdrop scratches/crease) before e
  4. image_auto_straighten — Correct the 2-3 degree scanner tilt so the crop in the next step is clean and square to the photograph.
  5. image_crop_to_bounds — Crop off the ragged uneven white scan border down the left side (levelled by straightening), leaving only the photographic image area.
  6. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone as the first global recovery pass on the flat, faded C-print to re-spread the crushed tonal range before targeted color work.
  7. image_adjust_color_temperature — Cool the strong warm yellow-orange age cast (shift temperature toward blue) to neutralize the color so skin tones and clothing come back to natural. Consumes th
  8. image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation — Gently restore the faded color vibrance/saturation now that the cast is gone, so muted 1970s fabrics and skin regain life without oversaturating into a modern l
  9. image_adjust_highlights — Recover blown/washed highlight detail on the cream backdrop and bright clothing that the fading flattened, restoring believable tonal separation.
  10. image_adjust_dark_portions — Open up and re-anchor the muddy shadow tones so the picture has depth again -- part of the clarity/detail recovery the brief asks for.
  11. image_select_by_prompt — Select the plain cream studio backdrop region that carries the white scratch lines and the short crease -- a UNIFORM area -- so the marks over it can be conceal
  12. image_fill_area — Solid-color fill the selected backdrop with the matched cream/off-white of the seamless, covering the white scratch lines and the crease so they vanish into the
  13. image_list_presets — List the available Lightroom-style presets so a clarity/local-contrast preset can be chosen to deliver the 'sharpness/details' the client cares about (there is
  14. image_apply_preset — Apply the chosen clarity/local-contrast preset to lift perceived sharpness and micro-detail on the soft scan -- 'details and sharpness' delivered honestly via a
  15. image_add_grain — Add a very light, uniform film grain over the whole restored image so the solid-filled cream backdrop patch doesn't look plastic or smoothed against the origina
  16. image_crop_and_resize — Crop-and-resize the restored image to true A4 print dimensions 2480x3508 px at 300 DPI, yielding the framed-print-ready deliverable. A genuine downscale/re-fram
  17. create_firefly_board — Assemble the original scan URN alongside the restored A4 output into a Firefly Board before/after approval contact-board (deep-link) for the client to sign off
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon and tool-diverse for a single-image job: 17 connector calls and 17 distinct Adobe tools across 3 groups (asset ingest/preview, the image_* raster family, and the Firefly board) -- above the >=10 calls / >=6 distinct-tools bar. Difficulty comes from ordering the restoration correctly (straighten before crop; global auto-tone before targeted color; neutralize the warm cast before recovering saturation; shape highlights then shadows for depth; conceal the scratches BEFORE the clarity preset so the preset doesn't sharpen the fill seam; grain last so it overlays the solid-fill patch and re-graded areas) and from honest capability discipline: the agent must recognize the connector has NO generative heal/clone/object-removal and NO sharpen/super-resolution tool, so the brief's 'restore colors, details and sharpness' is delivered as (a) tonal/color correction for colors, (b) local-contrast/highlight/dark-portion shaping + a clarity preset for 'details and sharpness', and (c) scratch concealment scoped ONLY to the plain uniform backdrop via select-by-prompt + solid fill_area, never over the faces. A naive agent that reaches for generative fill, AI object-removal, upscaling, or a sharpen op would violate the capability sheet -- making correct, restrained tool selection the core challenge. Every step is [C] headless, so the whole workflow is autonomously runnable end to end.

Client-supplied input assets: 1 total · 1 img · 0 video · 0 audio · 0 data

Images (1)

meadowlark_family_keepsake_1975.png
gemini

AO-112 — SlabVault graded-card Shopify batch: background removal onto clean white, straighten, center, and 2048x2048 export ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Cut six iPhone-shot graded trading-card / hobby-box photos out of their cluttered backgrounds, straighten and tone them, place each on a clean pure-white background, and export a consistent 2048x2048 square set (plus transparent PNG cutouts) named to match the store's Shopify product handles.

💰 $5–$50/hrUpwork37 tool calls10 distinct toolseditlong-horizon
grounded in a real brief: Shopify Product Photo Editor — Background Removal & Centering
Full client brief
SlabVault Cards, an online collectibles store selling graded slabs and sealed hobby product, needs its new-inventory photos turned into clean, upload-ready Shopify images. The owner shot everything at the kitchen table on an iPhone, so the raw files are inconsistent: slightly tilted, dull and cool-cast under kitchen lighting, and sitting on a busy wood-grain table with a phone charger, a coffee ring, and other clutter in frame. There are six SKUs in this batch — a PSA 10 graded rookie slab, a PSA 9 vintage slab, a BGS 9.5 chrome refractor slab, a sealed hobby box, a sealed blaster box, and a three-card graded lot on a stand — each supplied as one hero photo, plus a CSV listing every SKU's Shopify product handle for file naming. The job, applied identically to all six so the storefront looks cohesive: (1) lift the product cleanly off the cluttered table so no wood grain, charger, or coffee ring survives — deliver a transparent PNG cutout of each item, and also place each cutout onto a clean, pure-white (#FFFFFF) background with no drop shadow and no grey gradient, just flat white as Shopify prefers; (2) straighten the tilt so every slab reads dead-level, and auto-tone plus gently warm the cool kitchen cast so the card faces and slab labels look true and consistent across the set; (3) center the product in frame with even margin and crop/resize every white-background image to Shopify's recommended 2048 x 2048 px square at consistent scale so the products don't jump size-to-size in the storefront grid; (4) name each exported file to match its Shopify product handle from the CSV so the owner can bulk-associate them on upload. Deliver the six finished 2048x2048 white-background JPEGs, keep the transparent PNG cutouts too (useful for marketplace cross-posting), stage everything into a dated Shopify-upload folder in Creative Cloud, and drop a short recipe note documenting the exact edit steps so the same look can be reproduced on the next inventory drop. These are collectibles worth real money, so slabs must be perfectly straight, centered, and shadow-free.
Deliverables (4)
  • shopify_whitebg_squares_x6 — Six 2048x2048 px JPEGs, each product composited onto a flat pure-white (#FFFFFF) background, straightened, tone-matched and centered at consistent scale, no drop shadow, named to match the Shopify pro
  • transparent_cutouts_x6 — Six transparent-background PNG cutouts of the same products (background removed, straightened, toned) retained for marketplace cross-posting; same handle-based naming.
  • SlabVault_Shopify_Upload_folder — A dated Creative Cloud folder (e.g. SlabVault_Shopify_2026-07-01) containing the six white-background JPEGs and six transparent PNGs, staged for bulk Shopify upload.
  • slabvault_edit_recipe.txt — A short recipe note documenting the exact per-image edit steps (straighten, auto-tone, warm, background-remove to transparent, background-remove onto #FFFFFF white, center-crop to 2048x2048, handle na
Workflow — 37 tool steps
  1. asset_create_folders — Create the dated SlabVault_Shopify_2026-07-01 Creative Cloud folder to stage all finished upload-ready assets.
  2. image_auto_straighten — Level the ~5-degree tilt on the PSA 10 hero so the slab reads dead-straight for the collectibles-grade look (done before cutout so the whole frame rotates clean
  3. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone to fix the dull/flat cool kitchen exposure so the card face and PSA label read true.
  4. image_adjust_color_temperature — Warm the cool bluish white balance toward neutral so colors are consistent across the batch (same shift reused on all six).
  5. image_select_subject — Detect and preview the slab subject region to confirm a clean cutout is possible off the cluttered wood table / charger / coffee ring before removal (inspection
  6. image_remove_background — Remove the cluttered background to a transparent PNG cutout of the graded, straightened slab (default no-backgroundColor mode) — retained deliverable for market
  7. image_remove_background — Second removal pass on the same graded frame with options.backgroundColor='#FFFFFF' — the tool-sanctioned single-call 'product on clean white' path — compositin
  8. image_crop_and_resize — Center the product (focus:subject) and crop/resize to Shopify's 2048x2048 square with even margin at the consistent batch scale; onSubjectClipping set to 'pad'
  9. image_auto_straighten — Run the identical pipeline on the PSA 9 vintage slab: level the ~3-degree tilt first.
  10. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the underexposed vintage slab to match the set.
  11. image_remove_background — Remove background to a transparent PNG cutout of the vintage slab (retained deliverable).
  12. image_remove_background — Second pass with backgroundColor='#FFFFFF' to place the vintage-slab cutout on clean flat white.
  13. image_crop_and_resize — Center and crop/resize the vintage slab to the matching 2048x2048 square (onSubjectClipping:'pad').
  14. image_auto_straighten — Straighten the reflective BGS refractor slab shot.
  15. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the dull refractor slab to the consistent brightness.
  16. image_remove_background — Remove background to a clean transparent PNG cutout of the reflective refractor slab (chrome glare / busy background handled by the cutout).
  17. image_remove_background — Second pass with backgroundColor='#FFFFFF' to place the refractor cutout on clean flat white.
  18. image_crop_and_resize — Center and crop/resize the refractor slab to 2048x2048 (onSubjectClipping:'pad').
  19. image_auto_straighten — Straighten the taller 3D sealed hobby box shot.
  20. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the hobby-box shot to the batch look.
  21. image_remove_background — Remove background to a transparent PNG of the hobby box (shrink-wrap glare handled by the cutout).
  22. image_remove_background — Second pass with backgroundColor='#FFFFFF' to place the hobby-box cutout on clean flat white.
  23. image_crop_and_resize — Center the taller box and crop/resize to 2048x2048 at consistent scale so it doesn't dwarf the slabs in the grid (onSubjectClipping:'pad').
  24. image_auto_straighten — Straighten the most-tilted (~6-degree) sealed blaster box so it reads level.
  25. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the blaster-box shot to the consistent exposure.
  26. image_remove_background — Remove background to a transparent PNG cutout of the blaster box (retained deliverable).
  27. image_remove_background — Second pass with backgroundColor='#FFFFFF' to place the blaster-box cutout on clean flat white.
  28. image_crop_and_resize — Center and crop/resize the blaster box to 2048x2048 (onSubjectClipping:'pad').
  29. image_select_by_prompt — Prompt-select 'the three graded slabs in the acrylic stand' to preview/confirm the multi-object product group holds together as one clean cutout region before r
  30. image_auto_straighten — Straighten the three-slab lot-on-stand shot.
  31. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the graded-lot shot to the batch look.
  32. image_remove_background — Remove background to a transparent PNG of the three-slab lot on its stand (retained deliverable).
  33. image_remove_background — Second pass with backgroundColor='#FFFFFF' to place the three-slab-lot cutout on clean flat white.
  34. image_crop_and_resize — Center and crop/resize the graded lot to the final 2048x2048 square (onSubjectClipping:'pad'), completing the consistent six-image set.
  35. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize an upload session for all twelve finished assets (six white-background JPEGs + six transparent PNGs).
  36. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the upload into the dated Shopify folder, naming each file per its Shopify product handle from the CSV.
  37. local_write_recipe_note — Emit a recipe note documenting the exact per-image edit steps and the handle-naming map so the same consistent white-background look reproduces on the next inve
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon batch: 37 connector calls, 10 distinct Adobe tools across three groups (image_* raster editing, asset_* storage/naming, local recipe note) — well above the >=10 calls / >=6 distinct-tool floor. Difficulty comes from running six separate SKUs (flat slabs, a reflective refractor, tall 3D boxes, a multi-object lot) through a consistent straighten -> auto-tone -> warm -> background-removal -> center-crop pipeline, holding scale and white-balance consistency across the whole set, branching each item into BOTH a transparent PNG cutout and a clean-white composite, then naming every export to its Shopify handle from a supplied CSV. It respects every hard limit: 'product on clean white' is produced the tool-sanctioned way via image_remove_background with options.backgroundColor='#FFFFFF' (a single call), NOT by feeding a transparent PNG into image_fill_area (which the tool docs explicitly warn against — wrong mask polarity — and which is the reason image_fill_area is deliberately unused here); image_crop_and_resize sets onSubjectClipping to 'pad' so it never emits a requires_expand_chain / image_crop_and_resize directive; no generative fill / generative-expand / reframe-crop, no text-to-image / from-scratch art, no upscaling, no object-removal-with-reconstruction; image_select_subject and image_select_by_prompt only INSPECT/preview the cutout region, they do not synthesize.

AO-113 — Loomhearth flat-lay apparel: batch background cut-outs + consistent mid-gray marketplace pack ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Take six already-shot raw overhead flat-lay apparel photos, straighten and color-true each, cut every garment cleanly off its cluttered surface, place them all onto one consistent flat mid-gray backdrop, and export a high-res + web-ready square marketplace pack.

💰 ~$110 est.Freelancer.com49 tool calls13 distinct toolseditlong-horizon
grounded in a real brief: Flat Lay Apparel Product Photography
Full client brief
Loomhearth Supply Co., a men's everyday-basics label refreshing its eBay and marketplace storefront, needs a clean, consistent flat-lay listing pack built from photos already shot in-studio. The owner has handed off six raw overhead flat-lay frames (a heather-gray crewneck tee, a black pullover hoodie, an olive field jacket, a navy waffle henley, a striped rugby, and a folded pair of selvedge jeans), each shot directly overhead but on a mismatched, cluttered styling surface — some on a wooden bench, some on a light linen sheet, all slightly tilted, a touch cool and flat, with stray props and shadow at the edges. Because the listings are photographed piecemeal over several sessions the backgrounds do NOT match, which looks messy across a gallery. The job: (1) straighten every frame so the garment sits square to the crop; (2) true up each garment's color and exposure so fabric reads crisp and true-to-life (correct the cool cast, lift flat exposure, gently push vibrance — subtle post only, no heavy stylizing); (3) lift each garment cleanly off its surface — select the garment as subject, then remove the background so each piece is an isolated transparent PNG with clean edges (no stray props, no surface, no shadow); (4) place every garment onto ONE consistent flat mid-gray backdrop (the owner's preferred neutral that keeps dark and light fabrics balanced on screen) by re-selecting the garment, inverting the selection and solid-filling the background region with an identical mid-gray tone behind all six, so the gallery looks like a matched set; (5) crop and resize every finished item to one uniform square (1:1) marketplace frame at high resolution (3000 px short side) plus a web-ready smaller size. Deliverables: six background-removed transparent PNGs (for reuse), six composited-on-mid-gray high-res JPEGs, six web-ready JPEGs, and a contact-sheet review board so the owner can approve the matched set in one deep-link. True-to-color, consistent, and clean — nothing stylized or invented.
Deliverables (4)
  • cutouts_transparent_png_x6 — Six background-removed transparent PNGs — one per garment (crew tee, hoodie, field jacket, henley, rugby, jeans) — color-trued and straightened, cleanly isolated with no surface/props/shadow, delivere
  • marketplace_highres_jpeg_x6 — Six high-resolution square (1:1) marketplace JPEGs, each garment sitting on ONE identical flat mid-gray backdrop (produced by inverting the garment selection and solid-filling the background region mi
  • web_ready_jpeg_x6 — Six web-ready smaller square JPEGs of the same mid-gray composites, downsized for fast marketplace listing thumbnails/galleries.
  • Loomhearth_listing_contact_board — Firefly board deep-link assembling all six mid-gray marketplace shots as a contact sheet so the owner can approve the consistent matched set in one click.
Workflow — 49 tool steps
  1. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize an upload session for the client's six raw overhead flat-lay frames into CC storage so the connector can operate on them. (Initial asset load.)
  2. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the upload; yields the six stored source asset URNs the per-garment sub-chains consume.
  3. image_auto_straighten — Level the tilted crew-tee flat-lay so the garment sits square to the crop.
  4. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the straightened tee to fix flat/dull exposure (subtle true-to-life correction).
  5. image_adjust_color_temperature — Neutralize the cool/blue cast so the heather-gray reads true-to-color.
  6. image_select_subject — Auto-select the crew tee as the subject, isolating it from the wooden bench and stray props (produces a garment mask).
  7. image_remove_background — Remove the background to a transparent PNG — clean garment edge, no surface/props/shadow. DELIVERABLE (transparent cut-out).
  8. image_invert_selection — Invert the garment mask to target the background region behind the tee (the bench + props), setting up the solid mid-gray backdrop.
  9. image_fill_area — Solid-fill the inverted background region with the owner's preferred flat mid-gray (SOLID fill only) — identical tone reused for all six so the frames read as a
  10. image_crop_and_resize — Crop/resize to a uniform 1:1 square marketplace frame at high-res (3000 px short side); same target applied to all six. DELIVERABLE.
  11. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the underexposed black hoodie flat-lay (shot on a different linen surface) to recover crushed detail.
  12. image_adjust_dark_portions — Lift the dark portions so the black fleece texture/drawstrings read (true-to-life, not stylized).
  13. image_select_subject — Select the hoodie as subject, isolating it from the linen sheet and stray tag/receipt.
  14. image_remove_background — Remove the background to a transparent PNG with a clean hood/cuff edge. DELIVERABLE.
  15. image_invert_selection — Invert the hoodie mask to target its background region for the matched mid-gray backdrop.
  16. image_fill_area — Solid-fill the inverted background with the same flat mid-gray so the black hoodie matches the tee's frame.
  17. image_crop_and_resize — Crop the hoodie to the identical 1:1 3000 px marketplace frame. DELIVERABLE.
  18. image_auto_straighten — Straighten the tilted olive field-jacket flat-lay.
  19. image_adjust_color_temperature — Correct the cool cast so the olive canvas and brass snaps read true.
  20. image_select_subject — Select the field jacket as subject (detailed pocket/snap silhouette) off the wooden bench.
  21. image_remove_background — Remove background to a clean transparent PNG. DELIVERABLE.
  22. image_invert_selection — Invert the jacket mask to target its background region.
  23. image_fill_area — Solid-fill the background with the identical mid-gray tone.
  24. image_crop_and_resize — Crop to the matching 1:1 3000 px marketplace frame. DELIVERABLE.
  25. image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation — Gently push vibrance on the navy waffle henley so the navy reads true-to-color (subtle post only; frame is square enough as shot).
  26. image_select_subject — Select the henley as subject off the linen sheet, excluding the stray thread and lint roller.
  27. image_remove_background — Remove background to a clean transparent PNG (honors the brief's stray-thread removal). DELIVERABLE.
  28. image_invert_selection — Invert the henley mask to target its background region.
  29. image_fill_area — Solid-fill the background with the identical mid-gray tone.
  30. image_crop_and_resize — Crop to the identical 1:1 3000 px marketplace frame. DELIVERABLE.
  31. image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation — Restore the desaturated green/cream rugby stripes to true-to-color (subtle).
  32. image_select_subject — Select the rugby shirt as subject off the wooden bench and hanger clutter.
  33. image_remove_background — Remove background to a clean transparent PNG. DELIVERABLE.
  34. image_invert_selection — Invert the rugby mask to target its background region.
  35. image_fill_area — Solid-fill the background with the identical mid-gray tone.
  36. image_crop_and_resize — Crop to the matching 1:1 3000 px marketplace frame. DELIVERABLE.
  37. image_auto_straighten — Straighten the tilted folded-jeans flat-lay.
  38. image_adjust_color_temperature — Correct the cool cast muting the indigo so the denim reads true.
  39. image_select_subject — Select the folded jeans as subject off the linen sheet and stray belt/clothespin.
  40. image_remove_background — Remove background to a clean transparent PNG with crisp rivet/stitch edges. DELIVERABLE.
  41. image_invert_selection — Invert the jeans mask to target its background region.
  42. image_fill_area — Solid-fill the background with the identical mid-gray tone — completes the matched six-piece set.
  43. image_crop_and_resize — Crop to the matching 1:1 3000 px marketplace frame. DELIVERABLE.
  44. image_crop_and_resize — Downsize the tee high-res shot to a web-ready square thumbnail size for listing galleries. DELIVERABLE.
  45. image_crop_and_resize — Downsize the hoodie shot to the web-ready size. DELIVERABLE.
  46. image_crop_and_resize — Downsize the jacket shot to the web-ready size. DELIVERABLE (remaining three web-ready exports follow the same downscale pattern).
  47. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize an upload session for all delivered assets (six cut-out PNGs + six mid-gray high-res JPEGs) into CC storage.
  48. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the upload; returns the CC asset URNs for the delivered listing pack.
  49. create_firefly_board — Assemble all six mid-gray marketplace shots as a contact-sheet Firefly board deep-link for one-click owner sign-off on the matched set (honest use: presenting a
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon batch flagship: 49 connector calls across 3 tool groups (image_* raster retouch/cutout/composite, asset_* CC storage, create_firefly_board), 13 distinct Adobe tools — far above the >=10 calls / >=6 distinct-tool floor. Difficulty comes from running six separate garments through a consistent multi-step pipeline (straighten -> tone/WB/vibrance/dark-portion per garment's specific defect -> subject-select -> background removal to a transparent PNG -> invert the SAME mask -> solid mid-gray fill of the background region -> uniform square crop), keeping the backdrop tone identical across all six so the gallery reads as a matched set, then branching web-ready downsizes and converging everything into upload + a contact-sheet review board. It respects every hard limit: the mid-gray backdrop is built the honest, in-corpus way (select_subject -> invert_selection -> image_fill_area SOLID fill of the inverted background region, exactly as AO-39/AO-40 do), NOT generative fill and NOT a photographic replacement background; image_remove_background outputs transparent PNGs only (no object-removal-with-reconstruction); no generative-expand/outpaint; no text-to-image/from-scratch art; no upscaling (the 3000 px target and the web-ready downsize are crop_and_resize operations on already-high-res supplied studio frames, never super-resolution); all color work is subtle true-to-life corre

AO-114 — Lumora "Dewdrop" lip-oil color-variant set: cut out on white, recolor one hero shot into 8 shade variants + spec sheet ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Clean up and cut out one raw phone shot of the Dewdrop lip-oil bottle onto a white e-commerce background, then recolor the translucent oil into 8 on-brand shade variants while keeping the metallic collar, cap, and lighting/shadow identical across all of them, and emit a variant spec sheet.

💰 ~$120 est.Upwork23 tool calls15 distinct toolseditlong-horizon
grounded in a real brief: Blind Box Packaging Design (PART A — Product Photo Retouching)
Full client brief
Lumora Beauty Co. is launching "Dewdrop," a glossy translucent lip oil in a frosted-glass dropper bottle with a brushed-gold collar and cap. We only had time to photograph ONE physical sample on an iPhone against a messy studio bench, so we need you to turn that single raw shot into a full, launch-ready shade range for our Shopify PDP and Amazon listing. First, clean up the raw photo: it's slightly tilted, a touch cool/dull, and the background is cluttered. Straighten it, correct the tone and white balance so the glass reads clean and neutral, and crop it square to our 2000x2000 px listing frame. Then cut the bottle out cleanly and place it on a pure white (#FFFFFF) e-commerce background — Amazon-compliant, no drop-shadow baked in, product centered with a little margin. Next, the important part: recolor JUST the translucent oil inside the bottle into 8 shade variants for the range — Clear Gloss (the original, ungraded), Peach Nectar, Rose Quartz, Coral Crush, Berry Tint, Amber Glow, Mauve Mist, and Cherry Sheer. The tint must look like real translucent oil: internal highlights and the light passing through the glass should shift naturally with each color, NOT a flat paint fill. Critically, the brushed-gold collar, the gold cap, the dropper glass rim, and the white background/shadow must stay IDENTICAL across all 8 — only the oil changes color. Deliverables: the 8 shade variants each as a 2000x2000 px white-background JPEG PLUS a transparent-background PNG at 300 DPI (16 product files total), and a one-page variant spec sheet (PDF) listing each shade name and its hue so our packaging team can match Pantone later. Keep everything photographic and consistent — this is a color-variant set of one product, not eight different photos.
Deliverables (3)
  • dewdrop_variants_white_x8.jpg — Eight 2000x2000 px square JPEGs, one per shade (Clear Gloss, Peach Nectar, Rose Quartz, Coral Crush, Berry Tint, Amber Glow, Mauve Mist, Cherry Sheer), product centered on pure white #FFFFFF, identica
  • dewdrop_variants_transparent_x8.png — The same eight shade variants each as a transparent-background PNG at 300 DPI for compositing into lifestyle/banner layouts; gold collar+cap preserved, only oil recolored.
  • dewdrop_variant_spec_sheet.pdf — One-page variant spec sheet (recipe note rendered/converted to PDF) listing each of the 8 shade names, its target hue, and the constant-elements note (gold collar/cap unchanged, white #FFFFFF backgrou
Workflow — 23 tool steps
  1. asset_preview_file — Inspect the supplied brand kit to confirm the white #FFFFFF background spec, the 2000x2000 frame, and which metallic elements (gold collar/cap) must stay consta
  2. image_auto_straighten — Level the ~3-degree tilt so the bottle stands vertical before any cutout or recolor.
  3. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone to fix the flat/dull exposure so the translucent oil and glass read cleanly.
  4. image_adjust_color_temperature — Neutralize the cool/blue white balance so the frosted glass and gold collar read true-to-life (baseline color before any tinting).
  5. image_crop_and_resize — Crop and resize to the 2000x2000 px square listing frame with the bottle centered and margin, per the brand kit.
  6. image_remove_background — Cut the bottle out of the cluttered bench onto a transparent background (clean e-commerce cutout, the base for both white-bg and PNG deliverables).
  7. image_fill_area — Solid-fill the transparent area with pure white #FFFFFF to produce the Amazon-compliant white-background base (SOLID fill only, no synthesized content).
  8. image_select_by_prompt — Select just the translucent oil inside the glass (prompt: 'the liquid oil inside the bottle'), excluding the gold collar, cap and glass rim — this mask is reuse
  9. image_adjust_hsl — Peach Nectar: shift the hue of the selected oil region toward the CSV peach hue; internal highlights ride along so it reads as tinted translucent oil, not flat
  10. image_adjust_hsl — Rose Quartz: re-hue the same masked oil region toward the soft-pink CSV target on the clean base; metal and background held constant.
  11. image_adjust_single_color_saturation — Boost only the recolored pink oil's saturation to hit the Rose Quartz saturation_shift from the CSV without touching the gold or white.
  12. image_adjust_hsl — Coral Crush: re-hue the masked oil toward coral-orange on the clean base; highlights preserved for the translucent look.
  13. image_adjust_hsl — Berry Tint: shift the masked oil to a deep berry hue; because it's darker, keep exposure of the highlights so the glass still reads translucent.
  14. image_adjust_hsl — Amber Glow: warm the masked oil toward amber; a natural sibling of the neutral base so the gold collar still coordinates.
  15. image_apply_color_overlay — Mauve Mist: apply a muted-mauve color overlay confined to the oil mask (overlay blends with the underlying highlights so it stays translucent, not opaque); meta
  16. image_adjust_hsl — Cherry Sheer: re-hue the masked oil to a translucent red; keeps the sheer highlights so it reads as a see-through tint over the dropper.
  17. image_remove_background — Regenerate the transparent-PNG deliverable for the Peach variant from its white-bg version (representative of the batch; each white variant is cut to a matching
  18. image_crop_and_resize — Clear Gloss variant: the ungraded original oil is passed through unchanged (per the CSV 'no recolor' row); re-affirm the 2000x2000 white-bg frame so it sits in
  19. local_write_recipe_note — Write the variant spec: each of the 8 shade names, its applied hue/saturation, and the constant-elements note (gold collar/cap unchanged, white #FFFFFF backgrou
  20. document_convert_pdf — Convert the recipe note into the one-page variant spec-sheet PDF deliverable.
  21. asset_create_folders — Create the delivery folder structure (white-bg JPEGs, transparent PNGs, spec sheet) in Creative Cloud storage.
  22. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize the upload of all 8 white-bg variants, the transparent PNG(s), and the spec sheet into the delivery folder.
  23. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the upload; returns the shareable asset URNs for the full Dewdrop variant set handed to the client.
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon product-recolor task: 23 connector calls, 15 distinct Adobe-server tools, well above the >=10-call / >=6-distinct-tool floor. Difficulty is in the discipline of a real color-variant job: prep one raw phone shot through a 6-step cleanup+cutout spine, fill to a compliant white background, derive ONE reusable oil-only mask via select_by_prompt, then fan that mask out across eight parallel recolors using hue-shift / single-color-saturation / color-overlay so the translucent oil changes shade while the gold collar, cap and white background stay pixel-identical -- the exact 'maintain consistent lighting, shadow, and metallic detail across all variants; translucent material highlights shift naturally' constraint from the source brief. It then produces dual deliverable formats (white JPEG + transparent PNG), a data-driven spec sheet, and a clean CC delivery folder. It respects every hard limit: image_fill_area is SOLID white fill only (no synthesized content); image_remove_background outputs only transparent PNGs; recoloring is hue/saturation/overlay manipulation of EXISTING pixels within a selection, never generative fill, never inpainting/object-removal, never text-to-image or upscaling; nothing is composed from scratch.

AO-115 — Rukmini Silverworks 925 jewelry catalog: batch isolate-on-pure-white with metal grade + consistent 3000px square e-com crops (transparent PNG + white-BG JPEG per SKU) ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Batch-process six raw 925-silver SKU photos into consistent, cast-free, pure-#FFFFFF e-commerce images — isolate each piece, grade the metal so it reads crisp, and deliver a transparent PNG plus a 3000x3000 white-background JPEG per SKU.

💰 ~$120 est.Freelancer.com47 tool calls14 distinct toolseditlong-horizon
grounded in a real brief: Nagpur Silver Jewelry Photoshoot
Full client brief
Rukmini Silverworks, a boutique 925 sterling-silver house, is re-listing its collection across its own store and two marketplaces and needs clean, consistent product imagery so the metalwork stays the hero. The owner has handed off six raw tabletop frames shot on a phone under mixed studio light — a filigree pendant, a pair of jhumka drop earrings, a stacking band ring, a chain bracelet, an oxidised cuff, and a solitaire-look ring. As-shot they are inconsistent: slightly tilted, framed loose with a grey sweep and prop clutter visible, dull/flat exposure, and a warm-to-cool colour cast that makes the silver look muddy or yellow rather than bright neutral. The job is a batch clean-up, not a re-shoot: for every SKU (1) straighten and level the piece; (2) neutralise the white balance and lift the exposure so the silver reads as bright, cool-neutral metal with no colour cast; (3) gently push clarity/contrast and, on the oxidised piece, hold the dark detail so the antique recesses stay legible; (4) select the jewellery as the subject, invert that selection to a background-only mask, and solid-fill it with pure #FFFFFF so each piece sits on a flat, shadow-free white backdrop (the metalwork must keep its true edge — no halo, no chopped prongs, open chain-links and prong gaps preserved); (5) crop every SKU to an identical centered square and export at 3000 x 3000 px, sRGB, web-ready; (6) from the same graded master, strip the background to a clean transparent PNG for the store's own layered layouts. Deliverables per SKU: a transparent-background PNG and a 3000 x 3000 white-#FFFFFF JPEG, all six looking like a matched set. Wrap the finished set into a single downloadable package and a recipe note listing the exact adjustment values applied so the treatment can be reproduced on the next batch. Brand constraint: pure #FFFFFF background (hex exact), no visible shadow, no colour cast, metal is the hero — the pieces must look like one consistent catalog, not six different edits.
Deliverables (4)
  • sku_isolated_transparent_png_x6 — Six background-removed transparent PNGs (one per SKU), jewellery isolated with true metal edges (no halo, prongs/chain gaps preserved), graded to bright neutral silver; for the store's own layered lay
  • sku_whitebg_3000_jpg_x6 — Six 3000 x 3000 px sRGB JPEGs, each isolated piece on a pure #FFFFFF backdrop built by select-subject -> invert -> solid-fill of the background mask, flat and shadow-free, identical centered square cr
  • Rukmini_925_batch_package.zip — Single downloadable ZIP bundling the six transparent PNGs and six white-BG JPEGs plus the recipe note, organised per SKU for hand-off.
  • Rukmini_batch_recipe_note.txt — Text recipe note recording the exact per-image adjustment values (straighten angle, WB/temperature, exposure, contrast/clarity, dark-portions lift) and the fixed crop/export/fill settings so the treat
Workflow — 47 tool steps
  1. asset_create_folders — Create a Creative Cloud project folder to hold every SKU's raw, isolated, and white-BG output so the six-piece batch stays organised.
  2. image_auto_straighten — Level the ~3-degree tilt on the hero filigree-pendant frame so the piece sits square before selection and crop.
  3. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the straightened hero to fix the flat/dull exposure.
  4. image_adjust_color_temperature — Neutralise the warm/yellow cast so the silver reads as bright cool-neutral metal (the brief's 'no colour cast' requirement); this SKU sets the master white-bala
  5. image_adjust_brightness_and_contrast — Nudge contrast/clarity so the metalwork pops and the fine filigree detail stays crisp (metal-is-hero); the values chosen here become the set's reference grade.
  6. image_select_subject — Auto-select the pendant as the subject, separating it from the grey sweep and prop clutter.
  7. image_invert_selection — Invert the subject selection to a background-only mask so the sweep/props (everything except the filigree pendant) can be filled.
  8. image_fill_area — Solid-fill the inverted background mask with pure #FFFFFF (RGB 255/255/255) so the sweep becomes a uniform, shadow-free, cast-free white backdrop behind the pen
  9. image_crop_and_resize — Crop to a centered square and export at 3000x3000 sRGB - the fixed target applied identically to all six SKUs. White-BG JPEG #1.
  10. image_remove_background — Branch off the same graded master to strip the sweep to full transparency, keeping the true metal edge (no halo). Transparent PNG #1 for the store's layered lay
  11. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the jhumka earrings to lift the underexposed cool frame.
  12. image_adjust_color_temperature — Neutralise the earrings' cool/bluish window cast (per-image WB, opposite direction to sku01) so the pair matches the set's neutral silver.
  13. image_select_subject — Select both earrings as the subject (multi-part piece) off the grey fabric.
  14. image_invert_selection — Invert the subject selection to a background-only mask so the sweep/props (everything except the jhumka earrings) can be filled.
  15. image_fill_area — Solid-fill the inverted background mask with pure #FFFFFF behind the earrings (solid fill only).
  16. image_crop_and_resize — Same 3000x3000 centered-square crop/export. White-BG JPEG #2.
  17. image_remove_background — Strip the graded earrings to a transparent PNG. Transparent PNG #2.
  18. image_adjust_color_temperature — Neutralise the flat/muddy stacking-ring frame to bright-neutral silver.
  19. image_select_subject — Select just the ring as the subject, separating it cleanly from the acrylic prop stand.
  20. image_invert_selection — Invert the subject selection to a background-only mask so the sweep and acrylic stand (everything except the stacking-band ring) can be filled.
  21. image_fill_area — Solid-fill the inverted background mask (grey sweep + acrylic stand) with pure #FFFFFF (solid fill only).
  22. image_crop_and_resize — 3000x3000 centered-square crop/export. White-BG JPEG #3.
  23. image_remove_background — Strip the graded ring to a transparent PNG. Transparent PNG #3.
  24. image_adjust_color_temperature — Neutralise the mixed warm/cool cast on the chain bracelet to match the set.
  25. image_select_subject — Select the curb chain as the subject; the open gaps between links must stay OUTSIDE the subject mask so they are treated as background.
  26. image_invert_selection — Invert the subject selection to a background-only mask that includes the sweep AND the open link-gaps, so both fill to white / go transparent rather than being
  27. image_fill_area — Solid-fill the inverted background mask with pure #FFFFFF - including the link gaps, which read as background - so the bracelet sits on clean white with its ope
  28. image_crop_and_resize — 3000x3000 centered-square crop/export. White-BG JPEG #4.
  29. image_remove_background — Strip the graded bracelet to a transparent PNG, preserving the see-through link gaps. Transparent PNG #4.
  30. image_adjust_color_temperature — Neutralise the cuff's warm cast to match the set.
  31. image_adjust_dark_portions — Lift the dark portions just enough that the blackened oxidised antique detail stays legible (no crushed blacks) while the bright silver stays neutral.
  32. image_select_subject — Select the cuff as the subject off the grey board.
  33. image_invert_selection — Invert the subject selection to a background-only mask so the sweep/props (everything except the oxidised cuff) can be filled.
  34. image_fill_area — Solid-fill the inverted background mask with pure #FFFFFF behind the cuff (solid fill only).
  35. image_crop_and_resize — 3000x3000 centered-square crop/export. White-BG JPEG #5.
  36. image_remove_background — Strip the graded cuff to a transparent PNG. Transparent PNG #5.
  37. image_adjust_color_temperature — Neutralise the solitaire ring's cool cast while keeping the CZ stone's sparkle.
  38. image_select_subject — Select the reflective solitaire ring as the subject, retaining the specular band and faceted-stone edge.
  39. image_invert_selection — Invert the subject selection to a background-only mask so the sweep/props (everything except the solitaire ring) can be filled.
  40. image_fill_area — Solid-fill the inverted background mask with pure #FFFFFF behind the ring (solid fill only).
  41. image_crop_and_resize — 3000x3000 centered-square crop/export completing the matched set. White-BG JPEG #6.
  42. image_remove_background — Strip the graded ring to a transparent PNG. Transparent PNG #6.
  43. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize an upload session for all twelve finished deliverables (six transparent PNGs + six white-BG JPEGs) into the project folder.
  44. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the upload against the project folder; returns the catalog asset URNs stored under the project folder for QA and hand-off.
  45. asset_inline_preview — Pull inline previews of the six white-BG JPEGs to QA that every background is pure #FFFFFF, shadow-free, and the set reads as one consistent catalog before pack
  46. local_write_recipe_note — Emit a recipe note recording the exact per-SKU adjustment values (straighten angle, WB/temperature, exposure/contrast, dark-portions lift) and the fixed 3000x30
  47. zip_outputs — Bundle the twelve deliverables plus the recipe note into a single per-SKU-organised ZIP for owner hand-off.
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon batch flagship: 47 connector calls, 14 distinct Adobe tools, far above the >=10 calls / >=6 distinct-tool floor. Difficulty comes from running six separate SKUs through an identical multi-step isolate-on-white pipeline while handling per-image variance (warm cast on some, cool on others; an oxidised piece needing a dark-portions lift so detail survives; a chain whose link-gaps must land in the background mask and not be painted as metal; a reflective solitaire whose specular edge must be retained), each SKU forking one graded master into a select->invert->solid-fill white-BG JPEG and a remove_background transparent PNG, then converging twelve deliverables into folder storage, an inline-preview QA pass, a reproducibility recipe note, and a packaged ZIP. It respects every hard limit: the white background is produced non-generatively by image_select_subject -> image_invert_selection -> image_fill_area (SOLID #FFFFFF fill of the background mask), exactly the reference pattern, NOT by feeding a transparent PNG into fill; image_remove_background is used only to lift a graded master to a transparent PNG; NO generative fill / expand / reframe-crop, NO upscaling / super-resolution, NO inpainting-of-occluded-pixels, NO text-to-image / from-scratch art, NO bespoke fonts, NO background-replacement-by-prompt. The pieces are inspected via asset_inline_preview, not regenerated.
