content
Rukmini Silver Catalog Batch - Spec Note:
Background pure #FFFFFF, no visible shadow, no colour cast.
Silver jewelry is the hero, ensure bright and neutral tonality.
Final crop is a centered 3000x3000 px square, sRGB profile.
Deliver two files per SKU: transparent PNG (no background).
And a white-background JPEG (using #FFFFFF fill).
All six images must maintain a consistent look and feel.

AO-116 — Umbra Loft noir concert-series poster art: three archival live shots driven to a matched two-tone amber/ink duotone + halftone treatment for print and social ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

Take three flat archival live-performance photos and drive all three to a matched noir two-tone (amber-highlight / deep-ink-shadow) duotone treatment with halftone texture and film grain, then deliver each as a print-ready poster-ratio image and a square social crop plus a packaged set and a review board.

💰 ~$120 est.Freelancer.com36 tool calls11 distinct toolseditlong-horizon
grounded in a real brief: MidSight Noir
Full client brief
Umbra Loft, a fictional basement live-jazz and spoken-word venue, is announcing its autumn "Midnight Sessions" concert series and wants a cohesive poster look built entirely from its own archive. They are handing off three raw archival performance photos pulled from past nights: a stand-up double-bass player under a single stage spot, a vocalist leaning into a vintage ribbon mic, and a wide shot of the trio mid-set on the low stage. As-shot the photos are unusable for a series: each is flat and dull, shot on a phone in near-dark with mixed warm house light and a cool spill from an EXIT sign, slightly tilted, with a cluttered background of cabling, monitors and audience heads. The venue's whole identity is dark, moody and cinematic, and they want the three photos driven to one signature NOIR TWO-TONE (duotone) treatment so they read instantly as a matched set at poster size on the venue wall and at Instagram-thumbnail size in the feed: deep ink-blue shadows lifting to a warm amber highlight, a subtle print-screen halftone texture so it feels like a screened gig poster, fine film grain so the flat inks don't band on large print, and darkened edges so the eye is pulled to the performer. The look must be applied IDENTICALLY to all three so the set is consistent. The job, per photo: (1) straighten the tilted frame; (2) auto-tone the flat near-dark exposure and push contrast hard so the two-tone mapping has clean separation between the lit performer and the dark room; (3) desaturate to a neutral grayscale base to kill the mixed warm/cool house-light cast; (4) flatten the grayscale to a single-ink monochromatic base tint in the deep ink-blue shadow ink across the whole frame; (5) lay the warm amber highlight ink over the whole ink-blue base as a whole-image color overlay in a lighten/screen blend so only the brighter (lit-performer) pixels shift toward amber, producing the two-ink noir duotone; (6) apply a subtle whole-image halftone dot texture for the screened-poster feel; (7) add fine film grain; (8) darken the frame edges toward the ink shadow with a whole-image color overlay in a multiply/darken blend so the composition vignettes down and the performer reads as hero; (9) crop to the venue's A2 poster ratio; and separately (10) crop the same treated frame to a 1:1 Instagram tile. Deliver, for each of the three photos, a print-ready high-resolution poster-ratio image (~300 DPI equivalent) and a 1:1 social crop; then package the full treated set into a single zip and assemble the treated posters onto a Firefly board so the venue can sign off on the whole series look in one deep-link. No text, logo, or layout is required in these deliverables — this is the photographic treatment only; the venue will drop the finished duotone images into their own poster te
Deliverables (4)
  • umbra_posters_print_x3 — Three print-ready high-resolution poster-ratio images (A2 portrait, ~300 DPI equivalent), one per performance photo, all carrying the identical noir two-tone treatment: deep ink-blue #10131F shadows l
  • umbra_social_tiles_x3 — Three 1:1 (1080x1080) Instagram tiles of the same three treated frames, matched to the poster treatment, cropped square with the performer centered — must still read as the same noir series at thumbna
  • umbra_midnight_series.zip — A single zip packaging the full treated set — three A2 poster-ratio images and three 1:1 social tiles — for hand-off to the venue.
  • umbra_series_review_board — Firefly board deep-link assembling the three treated poster-ratio images for one-click sign-off on the whole concert-series look.
Workflow — 36 tool steps
  1. asset_preview_file — Preview the supplied bass photo to confirm the tilt, dull underexposed tone and cluttered dark background before prepping the series.
  2. image_auto_straighten — Level the ~3-degree tilted horizon on the bass photo (first link of the shared treatment spine).
  3. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the flat near-dark exposure so the lit performer separates from the black room before the two-tone mapping.
  4. image_adjust_brightness_and_contrast — Push contrast hard so the duotone mapping has clean shadow/highlight separation (deep room shadows, bright spotlit performer).
  5. image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation — Pull saturation fully down to a neutral grayscale base, killing the mixed warm-house / cool-EXIT color cast before the two-ink map (whole-image, no mask).
  6. image_apply_monochromatic_tint — Flatten the whole grayscale frame to a single-ink monochromatic base tint in the deep ink-blue shadow ink (hue from #10131F in the brand note) — the standard du
  7. image_apply_color_overlay — Lay the warm amber highlight ink (RGB 233,162,59 from #E9A23B) over the WHOLE ink-blue base in a lighten blend so only the brighter (lit-performer) pixels shift
  8. image_apply_halftone — Apply a subtle whole-image print-screen halftone dot texture (small radius) so the duotone reads as a screened gig poster.
  9. image_add_grain — Add fine film grain so the flat two-tone inks don't band on large-format A2 print.
  10. image_apply_color_overlay — Darken the frame toward the ink shadow with a whole-image color overlay of the deep ink-blue (RGB 16,19,31) in a multiply/darken blend so the composition falls
  11. image_crop_and_resize — Crop/resize the treated bass frame to the A2 portrait poster ratio at ~300 DPI print size (same target applied to all three for a consistent set).
  12. image_crop_and_resize — Crop the same treated bass frame to a 1:1 1080x1080 Instagram tile with the player centered.
  13. image_auto_straighten — Start the vocalist photo through the identical treatment spine: level its faint tilt.
  14. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the muddy underexposed vocalist frame to match the bass photo's separation.
  15. image_adjust_brightness_and_contrast — Push the same hard contrast so the two-tone mapping matches the set.
  16. image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation — Desaturate the vocalist frame to the neutral grayscale base to kill its mixed warm-key / cool-rim cast.
  17. image_apply_monochromatic_tint — Apply the identical deep ink-blue single-ink base tint so the vocalist matches the bass photo's duotone base.
  18. image_apply_color_overlay — Lay the same warm amber highlight ink over the whole base in the same lighten blend for the matched two-ink noir look.
  19. image_apply_halftone — Apply the same subtle halftone dot texture at the same radius for set consistency.
  20. image_add_grain — Add the matching fine film grain.
  21. image_apply_color_overlay — Apply the same ink-blue multiply/darken edge vignette so the vocalist frame falls to near-black at the edges like the bass frame.
  22. image_crop_and_resize — Crop the treated vocalist frame to the matching A2 portrait poster ratio.
  23. image_crop_and_resize — Crop the same treated vocalist frame to a 1:1 1080x1080 social tile.
  24. image_auto_straighten — Start the wide trio photo through the identical spine: level its tilt.
  25. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the dark wide-frame exposure to match the set's separation.
  26. image_adjust_brightness_and_contrast — Push the same hard contrast on the busier wide frame.
  27. image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation — Desaturate the trio frame to the neutral grayscale base to kill its mixed warm/cool cast.
  28. image_apply_monochromatic_tint — Apply the identical deep ink-blue single-ink base tint to the trio frame.
  29. image_apply_color_overlay — Lay the same amber highlight ink over the whole base in the same lighten blend for the matched two-ink noir look.
  30. image_apply_halftone — Apply the same subtle halftone dot texture at the same radius.
  31. image_add_grain — Add the matching fine film grain.
  32. image_apply_color_overlay — Apply the same ink-blue multiply/darken edge vignette so the trio frame matches the others' near-black falloff.
  33. image_crop_and_resize — Crop the treated trio frame to the matching A2 portrait poster ratio so all three posters match.
  34. image_crop_and_resize — Crop the same treated trio frame to a 1:1 1080x1080 social tile so all three tiles match.
  35. zip_outputs — Package the full treated set (three A2 poster-ratio images + three 1:1 social tiles) into a single zip for hand-off to the venue.
  36. create_firefly_board — Assemble the three treated poster-ratio images onto a Firefly board deep-link so the venue can sign off on the whole concert-series look in one place.
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon flagship: 36 connector calls across three Adobe groups (image_* raster look-dev, asset_* preview, create_firefly_board) plus one local packaging step, and 11 distinct Adobe tools — above the >=10 calls / >=6 distinct-tool floor. Difficulty comes from running three separate low-light concert photos through an IDENTICAL nine-step noir-duotone treatment spine (straighten -> auto-tone -> contrast -> desaturate -> ink-blue tint -> amber overlay -> halftone -> grain -> edge-darken) so they read as one matched concert-series set, then forking each treated frame into an A2 poster crop and a 1:1 social crop, and finally packaging + boarding the set. Constructing a genuine two-tone (duotone) hero without any banned capability is the core challenge: the two-ink look is built by real whole-image steps (grayscale base -> monochromatic base tint in ink 1 -> whole-image color-overlay of ink 2 in a lighten/screen blend so only brighter pixels shift -> halftone -> grain -> a second darken-blend overlay for the vignette), not by a text-to-image or generative-fill shortcut and not by any tonal-range selection the tools do not support. It respects every hard limit: no generative fill / generative-expand / reframe-crop / object-removal-with-reconstruction / text-to-image / from-scratch art or logo / upscaling; image_fill_area, image_crop_and_resize, image_remove_background and image_se

umbra_brand_inks.txt
gemini/gemini-2.5-flash
content
Umbra Loft - Brand Ink Specifications

For noir duotone posters and promotional imagery, please use the following brand inks:

Shadow Ink: deep ink-blue #10131F (RGB 16,19,31)
Highlight Ink: warm amber #E9A23B (RGB 233,162,59)

Mapping Instructions: The performer should read as amber highlights emerging from deep ink-blue shadows; keep it dark and moody, edges falling to near-black.

Consistency Note: All three photos must be treated identically for a matched series.

AO-117 — Marisol Cove boutique-hotel homepage hero: license widescreen stock, crop desktop + mobile banners, grade to warm coastal look, place vectorized logo, InDesign export to web JPEG/PNG + print PDF ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

License a large widescreen coastal stock photo, crop it into desktop and mobile homepage hero frames, grade both to a warm coastal look, place the client's vectorized Marisol Cove logo, and export web-ready JPEG/PNG banners plus a print-ready PDF proof from the client's authored InDesign banner template.

💰 $15Upwork18 tool calls15 distinct toolscreatelong-horizon
grounded in a real brief: Shopify banner design
Full client brief
Marisol Cove is a fictional boutique clifftop hotel on the Amalfi-style coast, refreshing its homepage. We need a premium, luxury-style homepage hero banner in two sizes — a Desktop hero (1920x720 px, wide cinematic band) and a Mobile hero (1080x1350 px, tall portrait) — that share one consistent image and brand identity. We do NOT have our own hero photography yet, so please license a suitable high-resolution widescreen coastal shot from Adobe Stock (think: turquoise sea, terraced clifftop villas, warm late-afternoon light, generous empty sky on one side so our headline and logo have room to breathe) — it must be large enough to crop both the wide desktop band and the tall mobile frame without any upscaling. Grade the licensed image to our warm "golden-hour coastal" look: lift the exposure a touch, warm the white balance, push vibrance so the sea and terracotta pop, and keep it clean and premium (no heavy filters). For both crops leave clear open space on the left third for our headline "Escape to the Cove" and our logo lockup — do not let the buildings or horizon crowd that zone. We are handing you our exact wordmark as a flat PNG (cream "Marisol Cove" with a small wave mark) — please preserve it exactly, vectorize it to a crisp SVG, and place it top-left in both banners; do not redraw or restyle it. We are also handing you our authored InDesign banner template (marisol_hero_template.indd) that already contains the desktop and mobile artboards with a background image frame, the logo frame, and a <<headline>> / <<tagline>> data field pair, plus a one-row CSV with our copy — please run the merge so the copy and placed assets bind, then export: a web JPEG of the desktop hero, a web PNG of the mobile hero, and a print-ready PDF proof of both artboards for our sign-off deck. Finally, drop the two graded crops, the vectorized logo, and the final banners onto a review board so our founder can approve in one link. Brand colors are deep teal (#0E5A62), terracotta (#C4652E) and cream (#F4EBDD).
Deliverables (6)
  • Marisol_Hero_desktop.jpg — Web-ready 1920x720 px JPEG of the desktop homepage hero: warm golden-hour coastal crop, headline/tagline bound from CSV, vectorized Marisol Cove logo placed top-left, left-third clear for copy. Max qu
  • Marisol_Hero_mobile.png — Web-ready 1080x1350 px PNG of the mobile homepage hero: same graded image reframed to the tall portrait crop, headline/tagline bound, logo placed top-left.
  • Marisol_Hero_proof.pdf — Print-ready PDF proof containing both artboards (desktop + mobile) exported from the merged InDesign document for the founder sign-off deck; ~300 DPI.
  • graded_hero_crops_x2 — The licensed coastal stock image, warm-graded and cropped into the two hero frames: a 1920x720 desktop band and a 1080x1350 mobile portrait, both with the focal subject kept off the left third.
  • marisol_wordmark.svg — Clean vector SVG of the preserved Marisol Cove wordmark for crisp scaling across both banner sizes.
  • Marisol_Hero_review_board — Firefly review board deep-link assembling the two graded crops, the vectorized logo, and the final desktop/mobile banners for one-click founder sign-off.
Workflow — 18 tool steps
  1. asset_search — Search Adobe Stock for a high-resolution widescreen coastal shot (turquoise sea, terraced clifftop villas, warm light, open sky on one side). Returns low-res ca
  2. asset_license_and_download_stock — License and download the chosen coastal hero at full resolution (license BEFORE use). Must be large enough to crop both the wide desktop and tall mobile frames
  3. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the licensed stock to normalize exposure/contrast before the warm grade (applied once on the shared master so both crops match).
  4. image_adjust_exposure — Lift the exposure a touch for the bright, airy premium coastal feel the brief asks for.
  5. image_adjust_color_temperature — Warm the white balance toward the golden-hour coastal look (sun-warm terracotta and sand).
  6. image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation — Push vibrance so the turquoise sea and terracotta villas pop while staying clean/premium. This is the shared graded master both crops derive from.
  7. image_crop_and_resize — Reframe the graded master to the 1920x720 desktop band, aligning the focal villas to the right so the left third stays clear for the headline/logo (align x~0.67
  8. image_crop_and_resize — Reframe the SAME graded master to the 1080x1350 tall mobile frame, again keeping the subject off the left third — same source so both banners are visually consi
  9. image_vectorize — Vectorize the preserved flat wordmark PNG into a crisp SVG so the logo stays razor-sharp at both banner sizes. Logo is preserved, not redrawn.
  10. asset_create_folders — Create a Creative Cloud project folder (Marisol Cove / homepage-hero) to hold the prepped assets for the merge.
  11. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize an upload session for the prepped banner assets (two graded crops + vector logo) into the project folder.
  12. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the upload; returns the asset URNs the InDesign template/data-merge will reference for the background and logo frames.
  13. document_merge_data_layout — InDesign data-merge: bind the headline/tagline row into the client's authored .indd (real <<headline>>/<<tagline>> fields); the desktop/mobile graded crops sit
  14. document_render_layout — Export the desktop artboard of the merged document to a max-quality web JPEG deliverable (1920x720).
  15. document_render_layout — Export the mobile artboard of the merged document to a web PNG deliverable (1080x1350).
  16. document_render_layout — Export both artboards of the merged document to a print-ready ~300 DPI PDF proof for the founder sign-off deck.
  17. asset_inline_preview — Generate inline previews of the two finished banners to eyeball the logo placement and left-third clearance before assembling the review board.
  18. create_firefly_board — Assemble the two graded crops, the vector logo, and both final banners onto a Firefly board deep-link for one-click founder sign-off.
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon: 18 connector calls across 3 tool groups (image_* raster grading/crop/vectorize, asset_* stock+storage, document_* InDesign merge/export) plus create_firefly_board, with 15 distinct Adobe tools — well above the >=10 calls / >=6 distinct-tool floor. Difficulty comes from sourcing and licensing a correctly-sized stock hero, running a 4-step warm grade on a shared master, forking it into two aspect-distinct hero crops (wide desktop band + tall mobile portrait) that must stay visually consistent and keep the left third clear, vectorizing and preserving the client logo, then converging everything into a genuine InDesign [T] data-merge with a triple export (JPEG/PNG/PDF). It respects every hard limit: this is the GEN-FREE O8 stock-hero pattern — a large stock image is licensed then cropped DOWN, never a small image expanded up; NO image_crop_and_resize / reframe-crop / generative fill / text-to-image / upscaling; image_fill_area is unused; the InDesign layout is authored by the client (supplied .indd input), and document_render_layout / document_merge_data_layout only bind and EXPORT it, not compose it.

gemini/gemini-2.5-flash
content
headline,tagline
Escape to the Cove,A clifftop retreat above the Amalfi blue.
marisol_hero_template.indd.proxy.txt
content
**Template File:** `marisol_hero_template.indd`

**Description:**
This is an Adobe InDesign document containing two artboards for the Marisol Cove homepage hero section:

1.  **Desktop Artboard:** 1920x720 px
2.  **Mobile Artboard:** 1080x1350 px

**Structure & Layers (per artboard):**
Each artboard features a layered composition designed for responsiveness and brand consistency:
*   **Background Image Frame:** A full-bleed image frame positioned behind all other elements, intended for a "graded coastal crop" visual.
*   **Logo Frame:** Anchored to the top-left corner of the artboard.
*   **Text Area:** Dynamically populated via real InDesign data-merge fields.

**Placeholders & Data-Merge Fields:**
*   `<<headline>>`: Placeholder for the primary hero text.
*   `<<tagline>>`: Placeholder for secondary hero text.
*   **Background Image:** Image frame for the coastal visual.
*   **Logo:** Image frame for the brand logo.

**Brand Styling & Layout:**
The design ensures a consistent brand presence and clear messaging:
*   The left third of both the Desktop and Mobile artboards is intentionally kept clear of the background image's focal subject.
*   This designated clear area is reserved for the `<<headline>>` and `<<tagline>>` text, along with the logo, forming a distinct "headline/logo lockup."

AO-118 — Fizzwild kombucha website hero + banner set: license oversized stock beverage shots, reframe to exact hero/header/CTA/banner sizes, grade to one cohesive family, deliver web-ready pack + style reference ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

License oversized beverage/lifestyle stock photos, reframe them DOWN to Fizzwild's desktop-hero / mobile-hero / section-header / CTA-card / promo-banner slots at exact pixel sizes, grade them into one cohesive vibrant family, vectorize and cut out the supplied wordmark, and deliver a web-ready pack plus a style reference so future assets stay consistent.

💰 ~$138 est.Freelancer.com29 tool calls11 distinct toolscreatelong-horizon
grounded in a real brief: Vibrant Social & Web Graphics
Full client brief
Fizzwild Kombucha, a fictional small-batch craft kombucha brand relaunching its website, needs a cohesive set of website hero and banner graphics built entirely from licensed stock — no from-scratch illustration, no canvas expansion. The client has handed off a brand kit: a flat wordmark PNG (fizzwild_wordmark.png), a hex palette (brand teal #0E8C7F, sunburst amber #F5A623, off-white cream #FAF3E0), and a short text list of the section headers and promo copy that the dev team will typeset live (the freelancer lays in imagery + prepares the reusable wordmark, not body copy). The ask, quoting the brief, is "matching website graphics—hero images, section headers, and call-to-action spots—plus occasional banner ads for promotional campaigns," and every asset "should feel part of one cohesive family while being perfectly sized, compressed, and formatted for its destination." Deliverables and exact dimensions: (1) a desktop homepage hero at 1920x1080; (2) a mobile homepage hero at 1080x1920 built from a SECOND licensed shot (portrait crop) so the phone view is not just a squeezed desktop image; (3) an "Our Flavors" section-header strip at 1600x600; (4) a call-to-action card at 1080x1350; (5) a promotional banner at 1456x180 (leaderboard-style). Brand constraints: the whole set must read as ONE vibrant, warm, energetic family — push the color the same way across every image, warm the white balance slightly, and keep the amber/teal palette feeling present; the wordmark must stay razor-sharp wherever the dev team later places it, so vectorize it and also deliver a transparent cutout PNG. Hard sourcing rule (per the brief and the web context): because these are website heroes, each stock photo MUST be licensed at a resolution LARGER than its destination slot and then cropped/reframed DOWN to fit — never scaled up or canvas-expanded. Required content: bright bottles-of-kombucha / pouring-fizz / fresh-fruit-and-glass lifestyle imagery; a consistent warm vibrant grade across all five; all five photographic slots stay clean of baked-in text and logo so the dev team can overlay the live headline, CTA button, and wordmark on top (matching how a real web hand-off keeps text as live layers). Final handoff: a web-ready pack (correctly-sized JPEGs for the five slots, the wordmark SVG, a transparent wordmark PNG) plus a one-page style/recipe note listing the palette and the exact grade recipe so future assets stay consistent, all zipped, and a Firefly board deep-link so the founder can approve the look in one click.
Deliverables (10)
  • hero_desktop_1920x1080.jpg — Desktop homepage hero, exactly 1920x1080, cropped/reframed DOWN from an oversized licensed landscape kombucha stock shot; warm vibrant grade matching the family; clean of baked-in text/logo for live o
  • hero_mobile_1080x1920.jpg — Mobile homepage hero, exactly 1080x1920 portrait, cropped from a SECOND oversized licensed shot (not a squeezed desktop crop); same grade; clean for live overlay; web-ready JPEG.
  • section_header_1600x600.jpg — 'Our Flavors' section-header strip, exactly 1600x600, cropped from an oversized licensed fruit/glass shot; same warm vibrant grade; clean (no text/logo) for live overlay; web-ready JPEG.
  • cta_card_1080x1350.jpg — Call-to-action card, exactly 1080x1350, cropped from an oversized licensed shot with the subject reframed to leave breathing room at the base for the dev team's live CTA button + wordmark overlay; sam
  • promo_banner_1456x180.jpg — Leaderboard-style promo banner, exactly 1456x180, cropped from an oversized licensed shot with the subject reframed to leave the left region clear for the dev team's live wordmark overlay; same grade;
  • fizzwild_wordmark.svg — Clean vector SVG of the Fizzwild wordmark for razor-sharp placement/scaling by the dev team on the CTA and banner.
  • fizzwild_wordmark_transparent.png — Transparent-background PNG of the wordmark (background removed from the supplied flat PNG) for the dev team's live overlays.
  • fizzwild_web_pack.zip — Zipped web-ready pack: the five correctly-sized JPEGs, the wordmark SVG, the transparent wordmark PNG, and the style/recipe note.
  • fizzwild_style_recipe_note.txt — One-page style/recipe note listing the palette (teal/amber/cream) and the exact grade recipe (auto-tone -> warm temperature -> vibrance step values) so future assets stay consistent with this family.
  • Fizzwild_Web_review_board — Firefly board deep-link assembling the five graded slots + vector wordmark for one-click founder sign-off.
Workflow — 29 tool steps
  1. asset_search — Search Adobe Stock for vibrant oversized kombucha/beverage hero photos (landscape + portrait + fruit/glass + lifestyle candidates) per the wishlist; low-res com
  2. asset_license_and_download_stock — License and download the chosen landscape kombucha shot at full-res (oversized vs 1920x1080 so it can be cropped DOWN, never expanded).
  3. asset_license_and_download_stock — License a second, portrait-oriented beverage shot at full-res for the mobile hero (oversized vs 1080x1920).
  4. asset_license_and_download_stock — License the fresh-fruit-and-glass shot at full-res for the section header (oversized vs 1600x600).
  5. asset_license_and_download_stock — License the iced-glass/condensation lifestyle shot at full-res to source both the CTA card and the promo banner (oversized vs both slots).
  6. image_apply_auto_tone — Auto-tone the desktop hero source to establish the base exposure/contrast for the whole family's grade.
  7. image_adjust_color_temperature — Warm the white balance slightly toward the brand's energetic amber feel (recipe step reused across all slots).
  8. image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation — Push vibrance so bottles/fruit/fizz read bold and appetizing; this locks the family grade recipe.
  9. image_crop_and_resize — Crop/reframe DOWN from the oversized graded landscape shot to exactly 1920x1080 desktop hero (output width/height set explicitly; no upscaling).
  10. image_apply_auto_tone — Run the portrait mobile source through the same grade recipe, starting with auto-tone.
  11. image_adjust_color_temperature — Apply the matching warm white-balance step so the mobile hero belongs to the same family.
  12. image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation — Match the vibrance push used on the desktop hero.
  13. image_crop_and_resize — Crop/reframe DOWN to exactly 1080x1920 portrait mobile hero (distinct source, not a squeezed desktop crop).
  14. image_apply_auto_tone — Grade the section-header source with the same recipe: auto-tone first.
  15. image_adjust_color_temperature — Warm the header shot to match the family.
  16. image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation — Apply the matching vibrance so the 'Our Flavors' strip is cohesive with the heroes.
  17. image_crop_and_resize — Crop/reframe DOWN to exactly 1600x600; kept clean (no text/logo) for the dev team's live overlay.
  18. image_apply_auto_tone — Grade the shared CTA/banner source with the same recipe: auto-tone.
  19. image_adjust_color_temperature — Warm the shared source so CTA and banner match the family.
  20. image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation — Vibrance push on the shared CTA/banner source (final graded master before the two crops branch off).
  21. image_crop_and_resize — Crop/reframe DOWN to exactly 1080x1350, reframing the subject with breathing room at the base so the dev team can overlay the live CTA button + wordmark on top
  22. image_crop_and_resize — Crop/reframe DOWN a wide leaderboard slice to exactly 1456x180 from the same graded master, reframing the subject so the left region stays clear for the dev tea
  23. image_vectorize — Vectorize the flat wordmark PNG into a clean SVG for razor-sharp placement/scaling by the dev team on the CTA and banner.
  24. image_remove_background — Produce a transparent-background PNG of the wordmark (-> transparent PNG only) for the dev team's live overlays.
  25. local_write_recipe_note — Emit the one-page style/recipe note: palette (teal/amber/cream) + the exact grade recipe (auto-tone -> warm temperature -> vibrance) so future assets stay consi
  26. zip_outputs — Zip the web-ready pack: five correctly-sized JPEGs + wordmark SVG + transparent PNG + style/recipe note.
  27. asset_initialize_file_upload — Initialize an upload session to push the finished slots + vector wordmark to CC storage for the review board.
  28. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the upload; returns the CC asset URNs the Firefly board will reference.
  29. create_firefly_board — Assemble the five graded slots + vector wordmark onto a Firefly board deep-link for one-click founder sign-off.
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon flagship: 29 connector calls, 10 distinct Adobe-server tools plus two local steps — above the >=10 calls / >=6 distinct-tool floor. Difficulty comes from sourcing FOUR separately-licensed oversized stock heroes, running each (and a shared fifth master) through an identical three-step grade recipe so five differently-sized web slots read as one cohesive family, cropping each DOWN to an EXACT pixel size (never expanded), reframing the CTA and banner subjects to leave clear regions for the dev team's live overlays, forking the wordmark into a vector SVG and a transparent PNG, emitting a style/recipe note, zipping a web-ready pack, and converging into a Firefly review board. It respects every hard limit and the slot's replacement note: NO image_crop_and_resize / reframe-crop / reframe-crop (every hero is licensed larger than its slot and only cropped down), NO generative fill or text-to-image or from-scratch art, NO upscaling / super-resolution, NO object-removal-with-reconstruction (inpainting), NO fake image-compositing (the wordmark is delivered as reusable SVG + transparent PNG for live overlay rather than baked onto a photographic slot via a color-overlay tool that cannot composite a second image). image_remove_background yields only a transparent PNG; image_vectorize traces the supplied flat PNG.

gemini/gemini-2.5-flash
content
Fizzwild Brand Kit Notes

**Brand Name:** Fizzwild
**Product:** Craft Kombucha

**Brand Voice:** Bold, warm, energetic. Our tone should feel adventurous and inviting, reflecting the wild and vibrant flavors of our kombucha.

**Primary Color Palette (Hex Codes):**
*   **Brand Teal:** #0E8C7F (Our core brand color, representing natural refreshment)
*   **Sunburst Amber:** #F5A623 (Accent color, evoking warmth, energy, and flavor)
*   **Off-white Cream:** #FAF3E0 (Neutral background, providing a clean canvas)

**Website Asset Slots & Pixel Dimensions (W x H):**
Please ensure all assets are provided at these exact dimensions.

*   **Desktop Hero Image:** 1920x1080px
*   **Mobile Hero Image:** 1080x1920px
*   **"Our Flavors" Section Header Image:** 1600x600px
*   **Call to Action (CTA) Card Image:** 1080x1350px
*   **Promotional Banner Image:** 1456x180px

**Important Note on Photography & Overlays:**
All photographic assets submitted for the above slots *must* be clean images, free of any baked-in text or logos. Our development team will be responsible for overlaying headlines, CTA buttons, and the Fizzwild wordmark live on the website. This allows for dynamic content updates and optimal responsiveness across devices.

AO-119 — Harbor Crest Realty website visual refresh: license one large Adobe Stock architecture hero, brand-grade it, crop/reframe into desktop + tablet + mobile heroes, section headers and banner ads, and vectorize the logo for placement ↑ top

ready brand-kit ✓

License one large Adobe Stock architecture photograph, brand-grade it to the Harbor Crest palette, then crop/reframe that single graded master into a desktop hero, tablet hero, mobile hero, two section-header strips, and two promo banner sizes, vectorize the client logo, and package the cohesive web-ready set with a font recommendation and a review board.

💰 ~$200 est.Freelancer.com22 tool calls16 distinct toolscreatelong-horizon
grounded in a real brief: Vibrant Social & Web Graphics
Full client brief
Harbor Crest Realty is a boutique waterfront-and-city brokerage whose website structure and copy already work, but the imagery feels dated and generic and no longer signals the premium, trustworthy feel we want. Refresh the visual layer into ONE cohesive, web-ready graphics family, all cut from a single hero look so the site feels intentional. (1) HERO SOURCE: source a high-quality, aspirational real-estate architecture photograph from Adobe Stock — a modern light-filled open living space or a clean contemporary home exterior at golden hour, no recognizable faces, no readable signage or brand marks — and license it at LARGE resolution (we need it big enough to cut multiple crops from, roughly 5000px on the long edge; do NOT stretch or AI-expand a small file). (2) BRAND GRADE: color-grade the licensed photo to our palette — primary deep harbor navy #17324B, warm sand accent #C9A66B, off-white #F5F1E8: auto-tone for a clean baseline, warm the white balance slightly toward the golden-hour sand tone, lift the shadows so interiors read open and airy, recover highlights so windows keep detail, and push vibrance just enough that the scene looks premium without being over-cooked. This graded file is the single MASTER every web slot is cut from. (3) RESPONSIVE HEROES (crop/reframe only, no expansion): from the graded master, produce a desktop hero 2560x1080 (~21:9 band), a tablet hero 1536x1024, and a mobile hero 1080x1350 (4:5 portrait) — each framed so the focal architecture survives the reframe. (4) SECTION HEADERS: cut two low-band section-header strips 1920x480 from the master for the 'Featured Listings' and 'About Us' sections. (5) PROMO BANNERS: cut two banner-ad sizes for our seasonal open-house campaign — a leaderboard 1200x628 and a square 1080x1080 — and give the square banner a subtle navy monochromatic treatment so it reads as the stylized promo variant rather than a plain photo. (6) LOGO: we'll supply our flat wordmark as a PNG; knock the background to transparency and vectorize it into a clean SVG so it stays razor-sharp placed over any of these graphics at any size. (7) HAND-OFF: recommend an on-brand heading/label typeface pairing for the mini style reference, upload the finished web-ready set back to a shared Creative Cloud folder, and assemble everything onto a single Firefly board deep-link so I can review the whole cohesive family in one place. Deliverables: brand-graded master, 3 responsive hero crops, 2 section-header strips, 2 promo banners (leaderboard + square navy variant), 1 transparent logo SVG, a font-pairing note, a shared CC folder, and a review board link. Everything must be perfectly sized to the dimensions above and feel part of one family.
Deliverables (11)
  • hero_master_graded.jpg — The brand-graded full-resolution master (~5000px landscape) — auto-toned, warmed toward the sand tone, shadows lifted, highlights recovered, vibrance pushed to the Harbor Crest look. Single source eve
  • hero_desktop_2560x1080.png — Desktop hero band, 2560x1080 (~21:9) PNG, cropped/reframed from the graded master with the focal architecture preserved. Web-ready.
  • hero_tablet_1536x1024.png — Tablet hero, 1536x1024 PNG, cropped/reframed from the same graded master.
  • hero_mobile_1080x1350.png — Mobile portrait hero, 1080x1350 (4:5) PNG, cropped/reframed from the graded master, focal area preserved.
  • section_header_featured_1920x480.png — 'Featured Listings' section-header strip, 1920x480 PNG, low band cropped from the graded master.
  • section_header_about_1920x480.png — 'About Us' section-header strip, 1920x480 PNG, a different low band cropped from the graded master so the two headers vary but stay in-family.
  • banner_openhouse_leaderboard_1200x628.png — Open-house promo leaderboard banner, 1200x628 PNG, cropped/reframed from the graded master.
  • banner_openhouse_square_navy.png — Open-house promo square banner, 1080x1080 PNG, cropped from the master then given a subtle navy (#17324B) monochromatic treatment so it reads as the stylized branded promo variant.
  • harbor_crest_logo.svg — Transparent-background vectorized Harbor Crest wordmark, clean scalable SVG for crisp placement over any hero/banner at any size.
  • font_pairing_recommendation.txt — Recommended heading/label typeface pairing matching the premium coastal look, from font_recommend, for the mini style reference.
  • harbor_web_review_board.url — Firefly board deep-link assembling the graded master, all hero crops, section headers, banners and the logo SVG so Priya can review the whole cohesive family in one link; the finished set also lands i
Workflow — 22 tool steps
  1. asset_search — Search Adobe Stock for a modern, aspirational real-estate architecture hero (light-filled interior / golden-hour exterior, no faces, no signage) using the clien
  2. asset_license_and_download_stock — License the chosen candidate BEFORE editing and download the full-resolution (~5000px) presigned file. This large licensed photo is the single seed the rest of
  3. image_apply_auto_tone — Neutral auto-tone baseline on the licensed photo before the manual brand grade.
  4. image_adjust_color_temperature — Warm the white balance slightly toward the golden-hour sand tone (#C9A66B accent direction) so the scene reads premium and inviting.
  5. image_adjust_dark_portions — Lift the shadows/dark portions so the interior reads open and airy rather than heavy.
  6. image_adjust_highlights — Recover the bright window/sunlit highlights so the glass keeps detail in the crops.
  7. image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation — Push vibrance just enough for a premium look without over-cooking — completes the GRADED MASTER that every web slot is cropped from.
  8. image_crop_and_resize — Crop/reframe the graded master to the 2560x1080 desktop hero band, keeping the focal architecture. No expansion — the master is large enough to crop directly.
  9. image_crop_and_resize — Crop/reframe the graded master to the 1536x1024 tablet hero.
  10. image_crop_and_resize — Crop/reframe the graded master to the 1080x1350 (4:5) mobile portrait hero, focal area preserved.
  11. image_crop_and_resize — Cut a 1920x480 low-band strip from the graded master for the 'Featured Listings' section header.
  12. image_crop_and_resize — Cut a different 1920x480 low band from the master for the 'About Us' section header so the two headers vary while staying in-family.
  13. image_crop_and_resize — Crop/reframe the master to the 1200x628 open-house leaderboard promo banner.
  14. image_crop_and_resize — Crop the master to a 1080x1080 square base for the open-house square promo banner.
  15. image_apply_monochromatic_tint — Lay a subtle navy #17324B monochromatic treatment over the square base so it reads as the stylized branded promo variant rather than a plain photo (deliverable)
  16. image_remove_background — Knock the flat gray background of the client wordmark out to transparency before vectorizing (-> transparent PNG only, no compositing).
  17. image_vectorize — Vectorize the transparent wordmark into a clean, infinitely-scalable single-color SVG for crisp placement over any hero/banner.
  18. font_recommend — Recommend an on-brand premium/coastal heading + label typeface pairing (existing typefaces only) for the mini style reference, driven by the brand kit voice and
  19. asset_create_folders — Create the shared CC delivery folder that will hold the full web-ready family for Priya's team.
  20. asset_initialize_file_upload — Open an upload session into the delivery folder to push the finished deliverables back to shared CC storage.
  21. asset_finalize_file_upload — Finalize the upload of the master, all hero crops, both section headers, both banners and the logo SVG to the CC delivery folder; returns the asset URNs for the
  22. create_firefly_board — Assemble the produced asset URNs the chain itself uploaded into one Firefly board deep-link Priya reviews for cohesive-family sign-off — the hand-off surface (h
Why it's non-trivial
Long-horizon and wide-mix: 22 connector calls, all server="adobe", spanning 16 DISTINCT Adobe tools across three capability groups (asset/stock/storage/board, raster photo-edit, and vector). It is a genuine multi-stage grade-and-reframe pipeline, not a one-shot edit: stock sourcing + licensing at large resolution -> a five-step brand tonal grade (auto-tone -> color-temperature -> dark-portions -> highlights -> vibrance) producing a single master -> SEVEN cohesive web slots cropped/reframed from that one master at exact required dimensions -> a navy monochromatic promo variant -> logo knockout + vectorize -> font recommendation -> CC folder upload + Firefly board hand-off. Hard parts that test the agent: (a) ordering correctly (license before edit; auto-tone before manual adjustments); (b) treating the crops as REFRAMES of one graded master so the whole set reads as one family at correct destination dimensions; (c) confining the promo treatment to the square variant only; (d) reusing one licensed hero across seven output geometries. This is the GEN-FREE O8 stock-hero pattern the slot's replacement_note prescribes: the hero is a LICENSED large stock photo that is CROPPED to each slot, NOT expanded from a small file — image_crop_and_resize is deliberately NOT used. Every step is [C] headless-confirmed. It avoids all banned capabilities: no generative expand/outpaint, no generative

gemini/gemini-2.5-flash
content
{
  "brand": "Harbor Crest Realty",
  "contact": "Priya Deshmukh, Marketing Lead",
